Root: root/pli/ms/vinaya/pli-tv-bu-vb/pli-tv-bu-vb-as1-7_root-pli-ms.json

pli-tv-bu-vb-as1:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as1:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as1:0.3
Pali: Adhikaraṇasamatha 
English (brahmali, published): The settling of legal issues 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as1:1.1
Pali: Ime kho panāyasmanto satta adhikaraṇasamathā dhammā uddesaṁ āgacchanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, these seven principles for the settling of legal issues come up for recitation. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as1:2.1
Pali: Uppannuppannānaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ samathāya vūpasamāya 
English (brahmali, published): For the settling and resolving of legal issues whenever they arise there is: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as1:2.2
Pali: sammukhāvinayo dātabbo, 
English (brahmali, published): Resolution face-to-face to be applied; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as2:1.1
Pali: sativinayo dātabbo, 
English (brahmali, published): Resolution through recollection to be granted; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as3:1.1
Pali: amūḷhavinayo dātabbo, 
English (brahmali, published): Resolution because of past insanity to be granted; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as4:1.1
Pali: paṭiññāya kāretabbaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): Acting according to what has been admitted; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as5:1.1
Pali: yebhuyyasikā, 
English (brahmali, published): Majority decision; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as6:1.1
Pali: tassapāpiyasikā, 
English (brahmali, published): Further penalty; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as7:1.1
Pali: tiṇavatthārakoti. 
English (brahmali, published): Covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as7:2.1
Pali: Uddiṭṭhā kho, āyasmanto, satta adhikaraṇasamathā dhammā. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerables, the seven principles for the settling of legal issues have been recited. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as7:2.2
Pali: Tatthāyasmante pucchāmi—
English (brahmali, published): In regard to this I ask you, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as7:2.3
Pali: “kaccittha parisuddhā”? 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as7:2.4
Pali: Dutiyampi pucchāmi—
English (brahmali, published): A second time I ask, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as7:2.5
Pali: “kaccittha parisuddhā”? 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as7:2.6
Pali: Tatiyampi pucchāmi—
English (brahmali, published): A third time I ask, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as7:2.7
Pali: “kaccittha parisuddhā”? 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as7:2.8
Pali: Parisuddhetthāyasmanto, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmīti. 
English (brahmali, published): You are pure in this and therefore silent. I will remember it thus.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as7:3.1
Pali: Adhikaraṇasamathā niṭṭhitā. 
English (brahmali, published): The seven principles for the settling of legal issues are finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as7:4.1
Pali: Uddiṭṭhaṁ kho, āyasmanto, nidānaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, the introduction has been recited; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as7:4.2
Pali: uddiṭṭhā cattāro pārājikā dhammā; 
English (brahmali, published): the four rules on expulsion have been recited; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as7:4.3
Pali: uddiṭṭhā terasa saṅghādisesā dhammā; 
English (brahmali, published): the thirteen rules on suspension have been recited; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as7:4.4
Pali: uddiṭṭhā dve aniyatā dhammā; 
English (brahmali, published): the two undetermined rules have been recited; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as7:4.5
Pali: uddiṭṭhā tiṁsa nissaggiyā pācittiyā dhammā; 
English (brahmali, published): the thirty rules on relinquishment and confession have been recited; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as7:4.6
Pali: uddiṭṭhā dvenavuti pācittiyā dhammā; 
English (brahmali, published): the ninety-two rules on confession have been recited; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as7:4.7
Pali: uddiṭṭhā cattāro pāṭidesanīyā dhammā; 
English (brahmali, published): the four rules on acknowledgment have been recited; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as7:4.8
Pali: uddiṭṭhā sekhiyā dhammā; 
English (brahmali, published): the rules of training have been recited; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as7:4.9
Pali: uddiṭṭhā satta adhikaraṇasamathā dhammā. 
English (brahmali, published): the seven principles for the settling of legal issues have been recited. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as7:4.10
Pali: Ettakaṁ tassa bhagavato suttāgataṁ suttapariyāpannaṁ anvaddhamāsaṁ uddesaṁ āgacchati. 
English (brahmali, published): This much has come down and is included in the Monastic Code of the Buddha and comes up for recitation every half-month. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as7:4.11
Pali: Tattha sabbeheva samaggehi sammodamānehi avivadamānehi sikkhitabbanti. 
English (brahmali, published): In regard to this everyone should train in unity, in concord, without dispute.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-as7:5.1
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅgo niṭṭhito. 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:0.3
Pali: Aniyatakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on undetermined offenses 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:0.4
Pali: 1. Paṭhamaaniyatasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 1. Sitting with a woman in a concealed place 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:0.5
Pali: Ime kho panāyasmanto dve aniyatā dhammā uddesaṁ āgacchanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerables, these two undetermined rules come up for recitation. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā udāyī sāvatthiyaṁ kulūpako hoti, bahukāni kulāni upasaṅkamati. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time Venerable Udāyī was associating with and visiting a number of families in Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.3
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmato udāyissa upaṭṭhākakulassa kumārikā aññatarassa kulassa kumārakassa dinnā hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion one of the families that supported him gave their daughter in marriage to the son of another family. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.4
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā udāyī pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena taṁ kulaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā manusse pucchi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards Venerable Udāyī robed up in the morning, took his bowl and robe, and went to the first of those families. When he arrived, he asked 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.5
Pali: “kahaṁ itthannāmā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): where the daughter was, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.6
Pali: Te evamāhaṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): and he was told 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.7
Pali: “dinnā, bhante, amukassa kulassa kumārakassā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): that she had been given to another family. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.8
Pali: Tampi kho kulaṁ āyasmato udāyissa upaṭṭhākaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): That family too supported Udāyī. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.9
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā udāyī yena taṁ kulaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā manusse pucchi—
English (brahmali, published): He then went there and again asked 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.10
Pali: “kahaṁ itthannāmā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): where the girl was. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.11
Pali: Te evamāhaṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): They said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.12
Pali: “esāyya, ovarake nisinnā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “She’s sitting in her room.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.13
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā udāyī yena sā kumārikā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā tassā kumārikāya saddhiṁ eko ekāya raho paṭicchanne āsane alaṅkammaniye nisajjaṁ kappesi kālayuttaṁ samullapanto kālayuttaṁ dhammaṁ bhaṇanto. 
English (brahmali, published): He went up to that girl, and the two of them sat down alone in private on a concealed seat suitable for the deed. When they were able, they chatted; otherwise he gave her a teaching. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.14
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena visākhā migāramātā bahuputtā hoti bahunattā arogaputtā aroganattā abhimaṅgalasammatā. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time Visākhā Migāramātā had many healthy children and grandchildren. As a consequence, she was considered auspicious. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.15
Pali: Manussā yaññesu chaṇesu ussavesu visākhaṁ migāramātaraṁ paṭhamaṁ bhojenti. 
English (brahmali, published): At sacrifices, ceremonies, and celebrations people would feed Visākhā first. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.16
Pali: Atha kho visākhā migāramātā nimantitā taṁ kulaṁ agamāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): Just then she had been invited to that family that supported Udāyī. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.17
Pali: Addasā kho visākhā migāramātā āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ tassā kumārikāya saddhiṁ ekaṁ ekāya raho paṭicchanne āsane alaṅkammaniye nisinnaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): When she arrived, she saw him sitting alone with that girl, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.18
Pali: Disvāna āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): and she said to him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.19
Pali: “idaṁ, bhante, nacchannaṁ nappatirūpaṁ yaṁ ayyo mātugāmena saddhiṁ eko ekāya raho paṭicchanne āsane alaṅkammaniye nisajjaṁ kappeti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, it’s not appropriate for you to sit down in private alone with a woman on a concealed seat suitable for the deed. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.20
Pali: Kiñcāpi, bhante, ayyo anatthiko tena dhammena, api ca dussaddhāpayā appasannā manussā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): You may not be aiming at that act, but it’s hard to convince people with little confidence.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.21
Pali: Evampi kho āyasmā udāyī visākhāya migāramātuyā vuccamāno nādiyi. 
English (brahmali, published): But Udāyī did not listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.22
Pali: Atha kho visākhā migāramātā nikkhamitvā bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): After leaving, Visākhā told the monks what had happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.23
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.24
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā udāyī mātugāmena saddhiṁ eko ekāya raho paṭicchanne āsane alaṅkammaniye nisajjaṁ kappessatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Udāyī sit down in private alone with a woman on a concealed seat suitable for the deed?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.25
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking Udāyī in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned Udāyī: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.26
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, udāyi, mātugāmena saddhiṁ eko ekāya raho paṭicchanne āsane alaṅkammaniye nisajjaṁ kappesī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Udāyī, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.27
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.28
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.29
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, mātugāmena saddhiṁ eko ekāya raho paṭicchanne āsane alaṅkammaniye nisajjaṁ kappessasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.30
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.31
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.32.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu mātugāmena saddhiṁ eko ekāya raho paṭicchanne āsane alaṅkammaniye nisajjaṁ kappeyya, tamenaṁ saddheyyavacasā upāsikā disvā 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk sits down in private alone with a woman on a concealed seat suitable for the deed, and a trustworthy female lay follower sees him 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.33
Pali: tiṇṇaṁ dhammānaṁ aññatarena vadeyya—pārājikena vā saṅghādisesena vā pācittiyena vā. 
English (brahmali, published): and accuses him of an offense entailing expulsion, an offense entailing suspension, or an offense entailing confession, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.34
Pali: Nisajjaṁ bhikkhu paṭijānamāno tiṇṇaṁ dhammānaṁ aññatarena kāretabbo—pārājikena vā saṅghādisesena vā pācittiyena vā. 
English (brahmali, published): then, if he admits to the sitting, he is to be dealt with according to one of these three 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.35
Pali: Yena vā sā saddheyyavacasā upāsikā vadeyya, tena so bhikkhu kāretabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): or according to what that trustworthy female lay follower has said. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:1.36
Pali: Ayaṁ dhammo aniyato”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): This rule is undetermined.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.5
Pali: Mātugāmo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A woman: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.6
Pali: manussitthī, na yakkhī na petī na tiracchānagatā. Antamaso tadahujātāpi dārikā, pageva mahattarī. 
English (brahmali, published): a human female, not a female spirit, not a female ghost, not a female animal; even a girl born that very day, let alone an older one. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.7
Pali: Saddhinti 
English (brahmali, published): With: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.8
Pali: ekato. 
English (brahmali, published): together. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.9
Pali: Eko ekāyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Alone: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.10
Pali: bhikkhu ceva hoti mātugāmo ca. 
English (brahmali, published): just the monk and the woman. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.11
Pali: Raho nāma 
English (brahmali, published): In private: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.12
Pali: cakkhussa raho, sotassa raho. 
English (brahmali, published): private to the eye and private to the ear. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.13
Pali: Cakkhussa raho nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Private to the eye: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.14
Pali: na sakkā hoti akkhiṁ vā nikhaṇīyamāne bhamukaṁ vā ukkhipīyamāne sīsaṁ vā ukkhipīyamāne passituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one is unable to see them winking, raising an eyebrow, or nodding. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.15
Pali: Sotassa raho nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Private to the ear: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.16
Pali: na sakkā hoti pakatikathā sotuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one is unable to hear ordinary speech. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.17
Pali: Paṭicchannaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Concealed seat: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.18
Pali: āsanaṁ kuṭṭena vā kavāṭena vā kilañjena vā sāṇipākārena vā rukkhena vā thambhena vā kotthaḷiyā vā yena kenaci paṭicchannaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): it is concealed by a wall, a screen, a door, a cloth screen, a tree, a pillar, a grain container, or anything else. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.19
Pali: Alaṅkammaniyeti 
English (brahmali, published): Suitable for the deed: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.20
Pali: sakkā hoti methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one is able to have sexual intercourse. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.21
Pali: Nisajjaṁ kappeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Sits down: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.22
Pali: mātugāme nisinne bhikkhu upanisinno vā hoti upanipanno vā. 
English (brahmali, published): the monk sits down or lies down next to the seated woman. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.23
Pali: Bhikkhu nisinne mātugāmo upanisinno vā hoti upanipanno vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The woman sits down or lies down next to the seated monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.24
Pali: Ubho vā nisinnā honti ubho vā nipannā. 
English (brahmali, published): Both are seated or both are lying down. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.25
Pali: Saddheyyavacasā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Trustworthy: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.26
Pali: āgataphalā abhisametāvinī viññātasāsanā. 
English (brahmali, published): she has attained the fruit, she has broken through, she has understood the instruction. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.27
Pali: Upāsikā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Female lay follower: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.28
Pali: buddhaṁ saraṇaṁ gatā, dhammaṁ saraṇaṁ gatā, saṅghaṁ saraṇaṁ gatā. 
English (brahmali, published): she has gone for refuge to the Buddha, the Teaching, and the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.29
Pali: Disvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Sees: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.1.30
Pali: passitvā. 
English (brahmali, published): having seen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.2.1
Pali: Tiṇṇaṁ dhammānaṁ aññatarena vadeyya— pārājikena vā saṅghādisesena vā pācittiyena vā. 
English (brahmali, published): If she accuses him of an offense entailing expulsion, an offense entailing suspension, or an offense entailing confession, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.2.2
Pali: Nisajjaṁ bhikkhu paṭijānamāno tiṇṇaṁ dhammānaṁ aññatarena kāretabbo— pārājikena vā saṅghādisesena vā pācittiyena vā. 
English (brahmali, published): then, if he admits to the sitting, he is to be dealt with according to one of these three 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.2.3
Pali: Yena vā sā saddheyyavacasā upāsikā vadeyya tena so bhikkhu kāretabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): or according to what that trustworthy female lay follower has said: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.2.4
Pali: Sā ce evaṁ vadeyya—
English (brahmali, published): If she accuses him like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:2.2.5
Pali: “ayyo mayā diṭṭho nisinno mātugāmassa methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevanto”ti, 
English (brahmali, published): “I’ve seen you seated, having sexual intercourse with a woman,”

Pali: na niyato, pārājikaṁ vā saṅghādiseso vā pācittiyaṁ vā. 
English (brahmali, published): not determined. It is either an offense entailing expulsion, an offense entailing suspension, or an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:3.1
Pali: Gamanaṁ paṭijānāti, nisajjaṁ paṭijānāti, āpattiṁ paṭijānāti, āpattiyā kāretabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): If he admits to going, and he admits to sitting, and he admits to an offense, he is to be dealt with for the offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:3.2
Pali: Gamanaṁ paṭijānāti, nisajjaṁ na paṭijānāti, āpattiṁ paṭijānāti, āpattiyā kāretabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): If he admits to going, but he does not admit to sitting, yet he admits to an offense, he is to be dealt with for the offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:3.3
Pali: Gamanaṁ paṭijānāti, nisajjaṁ paṭijānāti, āpattiṁ na paṭijānāti, nisajjāya kāretabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): If he admits to going, and he admits to sitting, but he does not admit to an offense, he is to be dealt with for the sitting. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:3.4
Pali: Gamanaṁ paṭijānāti, nisajjaṁ na paṭijānāti, āpattiṁ na paṭijānāti, na kāretabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): If he admits to going, but he does not admit to sitting, nor does he admit to an offense, he is not to be dealt with. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:3.5
Pali: Gamanaṁ na paṭijānāti, nisajjaṁ paṭijānāti, āpattiṁ paṭijānāti, āpattiyā kāretabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not admit to going, but he admits to sitting, and he admits to an offense, he is to be dealt with for the offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:3.6
Pali: Gamanaṁ na paṭijānāti, nisajjaṁ na paṭijānāti, āpattiṁ paṭijānāti, āpattiyā kāretabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not admit to going, nor does he admit to sitting, but he admits to an offense, he is to be dealt with for the offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:3.7
Pali: Gamanaṁ na paṭijānāti, nisajjaṁ paṭijānāti, āpattiṁ na paṭijānāti, nisajjāya kāretabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not admit to going, but he admits to sitting, yet he does not admit to an offense, he is to be dealt with for the sitting. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:3.8
Pali: Gamanaṁ na paṭijānāti, nisajjaṁ na paṭijānāti, āpattiṁ na paṭijānāti, na kāretabboti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not admit to going, nor does he admit to sitting, nor does he admit to an offense, he is not to be dealt with. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay1:3.9
Pali: Paṭhamo aniyato niṭṭhito. 
English (brahmali, published): The first undetermined offense on sitting with a woman in a concealed place is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2
Root: root/pli/ms/vinaya/pli-tv-bu-vb/pli-tv-bu-vb-ay/pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2_root-pli-ms.json

English translation sources:
  - brahmali [published]: translation/en/brahmali/vinaya/pli-tv-bu-vb/pli-tv-bu-vb-ay/pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2_translation-en-brahmali.json

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:0.3
Pali: Aniyatakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on undetermined offenses 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:0.4
Pali: 2. Dutiyaaniyatasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 2. Sitting alone with a woman 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā udāyī—“bhagavatā paṭikkhittaṁ mātugāmena saddhiṁ eko ekāya raho paṭicchanne āsane alaṅkammaniye nisajjaṁ kappetun”ti tassāyeva kumārikāya saddhiṁ eko ekāya raho nisajjaṁ kappesi kālayuttaṁ samullapanto kālayuttaṁ dhammaṁ bhaṇanto. 
English (brahmali, published): At this time Venerable Udāyī heard that the Buddha had prohibited sitting alone with a woman on a private and concealed seat suitable for the deed, and so instead he sat down in private alone with the same girl. When they were able, they just chatted; otherwise he gave her a teaching. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:1.3
Pali: Dutiyampi kho visākhā migāramātā nimantitā taṁ kulaṁ agamāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): A second time Visākhā had been invited to that family. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:1.4
Pali: Addasā kho visākhā migāramātā āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ tassāyeva kumārikāya saddhiṁ ekaṁ ekāya raho nisinnaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): When she arrived, she saw Udāyī sitting in private alone with the same girl, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:1.5
Pali: Disvāna āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): and she said to Udāyī, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:1.6
Pali: “idaṁ, bhante, nacchannaṁ nappatirūpaṁ yaṁ ayyo mātugāmena saddhiṁ eko ekāya raho nisajjaṁ kappeti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, it’s not appropriate for you to sit down in private alone with a woman. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:1.7
Pali: Kiñcāpi, bhante, ayyo anatthiko tena dhammena, api ca dussaddhāpayā appasannā manussā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): You may not be aiming at that act, but it’s hard to convince people with little confidence.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:1.8
Pali: Evampi kho āyasmā udāyī visākhāya migāramātuyā vuccamāno nādiyi. 
English (brahmali, published): But Udāyī did not listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:1.9
Pali: Atha kho visākhā migāramātā nikkhamitvā bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): After leaving, Visākhā told the monks what had happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:1.10
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:1.11
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā udāyī mātugāmena saddhiṁ eko ekāya raho nisajjaṁ kappessatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Udāyī sit down in private alone with a woman?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:1.12
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking Udāyī in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned Udāyī: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:1.13
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, udāyi, mātugāmena saddhiṁ eko ekāya raho nisajjaṁ kappesī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Udāyī, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:1.14
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:1.15
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:1.16
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, mātugāmena saddhiṁ eko ekāya raho nisajjaṁ kappessasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:1.17
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:1.18
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:1.19.1
Pali: “Na heva kho pana paṭicchannaṁ āsanaṁ hoti nālaṅkammaniyaṁ, alañca kho hoti mātugāmaṁ duṭṭhullāhi vācāhi obhāsituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Although a seat is not concealed, nor suitable for the deed, it may be suitable for speaking indecently to a woman. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:1.20
Pali: Yo pana bhikkhu tathārūpe āsane mātugāmena saddhiṁ eko ekāya raho nisajjaṁ kappeyya, tamenaṁ saddheyyavacasā upāsikā disvā 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk sits down on such a seat in private alone with a woman, and a trustworthy female lay follower sees him 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:1.21
Pali: dvinnaṁ dhammānaṁ aññatarena vadeyya—saṅghādisesena vā pācittiyena vā. 
English (brahmali, published): and accuses him of an offense entailing suspension or an offense entailing confession, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:1.22
Pali: Nisajjaṁ bhikkhu paṭijānamāno dvinnaṁ dhammānaṁ aññatarena kāretabbo— saṅghādisesena vā pācittiyena vā. 
English (brahmali, published): then, if he admits to the sitting, he is to be dealt with according to one of these two 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:1.23
Pali: Yena vā sā saddheyyavacasā upāsikā vadeyya tena so bhikkhu kāretabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): or according to what that trustworthy female lay follower has said. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:1.24
Pali: Ayampi dhammo aniyato”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): This rule too is undetermined.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.1
Pali: Na heva kho pana paṭicchannaṁ āsanaṁ hotīti 
English (brahmali, published): Although a seat is not concealed: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.2
Pali: appaṭicchannaṁ hoti kuṭṭena vā kavāṭena vā kilañjena vā sāṇipākārena vā rukkhena vā thambhena vā kotthaḷiyā vā yena kenaci appaṭicchannaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): it is not concealed by a wall, a screen, a door, a cloth screen, a tree, a pillar, a grain container, or anything else. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.3
Pali: Nālaṅkammaniyanti 
English (brahmali, published): Nor suitable for the deed: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.4
Pali: na sakkā hoti methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one is not able to have sexual intercourse. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.5
Pali: Alañca kho hoti mātugāmaṁ duṭṭhullāhi vācāhi obhāsitunti 
English (brahmali, published): It may be suitable for speaking indecently to a woman: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.6
Pali: sakkā hoti mātugāmaṁ duṭṭhullāhi vācāhi obhāsituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one is able to speak indecently to a woman. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.7
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.8
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.9
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.10
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.11
Pali: Tathārūpe āsaneti 
English (brahmali, published): On such a seat: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.12
Pali: evarūpe āsane. 
English (brahmali, published): on that sort of seat. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.13
Pali: Mātugāmo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A woman: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.14
Pali: manussitthī, na yakkhī na petī na tiracchānagatā, viññū paṭibalā subhāsitadubbhāsitaṁ duṭṭhullāduṭṭhullaṁ ājānituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): a human female, not a female spirit, not a female ghost, not a female animal. She understands and is capable of discerning good and bad speech, what is decent and what is indecent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.15
Pali: Saddhinti 
English (brahmali, published): With: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.16
Pali: ekato. 
English (brahmali, published): together. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.17
Pali: Eko ekāyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Alone: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.18
Pali: bhikkhu ceva hoti mātugāmo ca. 
English (brahmali, published): just the monk and the woman. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.19
Pali: Raho nāma 
English (brahmali, published): In private: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.20
Pali: cakkhussa raho, sotassa raho. 
English (brahmali, published): private to the eye and private to the ear. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.21
Pali: Cakkhussa raho nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Private to the eye: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.22
Pali: na sakkā hoti akkhiṁ vā nikhaṇīyamāne bhamukaṁ vā ukkhipīyamāne sīsaṁ vā ukkhipīyamāne passituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one is unable to see them winking, raising an eyebrow, or nodding. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.23
Pali: Sotassa raho nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Private to the ear: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.24
Pali: na sakkā hoti pakatikathā sotuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one is unable to hear ordinary speech. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.25
Pali: Nisajjaṁ kappeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Sits down: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.26
Pali: mātugāme nisinne bhikkhu upanisinno vā hoti upanipanno vā. 
English (brahmali, published): the monk sits down or lies down next to the seated woman. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.27
Pali: Bhikkhu nisinne mātugāmo upanisinno vā hoti upanipanno vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The woman sits down or lies down next to the seated monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.28
Pali: Ubho vā nisinnā honti ubho vā nipannā. 
English (brahmali, published): Both are seated or both are lying down. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.29
Pali: Saddheyyavacasā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Trustworthy: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.30
Pali: āgataphalā abhisametāvinī viññātasāsanā. 
English (brahmali, published): she has attained the fruit, she has broken through, she has understood the instruction. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.31
Pali: Upāsikā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Female lay follower: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.32
Pali: buddhaṁ saraṇaṁ gatā, dhammaṁ saraṇaṁ gatā, saṅghaṁ saraṇaṁ gatā. 
English (brahmali, published): she has gone for refuge to the Buddha, the Teaching, and the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.33
Pali: Disvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Sees: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.1.34
Pali: passitvā. 
English (brahmali, published): having seen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.2.1
Pali: Dvinnaṁ dhammānaṁ aññatarena vadeyya saṅghādisesena vā pācittiyena vā. 
English (brahmali, published): If she accuses him of an offense entailing suspension or an offense entailing confession, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.2.2
Pali: Nisajjaṁ bhikkhu paṭijānamāno dvinnaṁ dhammānaṁ aññatarena kāretabbo— saṅghādisesena vā pācittiyena vā. 
English (brahmali, published): then, if he admits to the sitting, he is to be dealt with according to one of these two 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.2.3
Pali: Yena vā sā saddheyyavacasā upāsikā vadeyya, tena so bhikkhu kāretabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): or according to what that trustworthy female lay follower has said. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.2.4
Pali: Sā ce evaṁ vadeyya—
English (brahmali, published): If she accuses him like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.2.5
Pali: “ayyo mayā diṭṭho nisinno mātugāmena saddhiṁ kāyasaṁsaggaṁ samāpajjanto”ti, 
English (brahmali, published): “I’ve seen you seated, making physical contact with a woman,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.2.6
Pali: so ca taṁ paṭijānāti, āpattiyā kāretabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): and he admits to that, then he is to be dealt with for the offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.2.7
Pali: Sā ce evaṁ vadeyya—
English (brahmali, published): If she accuses him like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.2.8
Pali: “ayyo mayā diṭṭho nisinno mātugāmena saddhiṁ kāyasaṁsaggaṁ samāpajjanto”ti, 
English (brahmali, published): “I’ve seen you seated, making physical contact with a woman,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.2.9
Pali: so ce evaṁ vadeyya—
English (brahmali, published): but he says, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.2.10
Pali: “saccāhaṁ nisinno, no ca kho kāyasaṁsaggaṁ samāpajjin”ti, 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true that I was seated, but I didn’t make physical contact,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.2.11
Pali: nisajjāya kāretabbo …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): then he is to be dealt with for the sitting. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.2.12
Pali: “nāhaṁ nisinno, api ca kho nipanno”ti, 
English (brahmali, published): “I wasn’t seated, but lying down,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.2.13

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:2.2.63
Pali: na niyato, saṅghādiseso vā pācittiyaṁ vā. 
English (brahmali, published): not determined. It is either an offense entailing suspension or an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:3.1
Pali: Gamanaṁ paṭijānāti nisajjaṁ paṭijānāti āpattiṁ paṭijānāti, āpattiyā kāretabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): If he admits to going, and he admits to sitting, and he admits to an offense, he is to be dealt with for the offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:3.2
Pali: Gamanaṁ paṭijānāti nisajjaṁ na paṭijānāti āpattiṁ paṭijānāti, āpattiyā kāretabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): If he admits to going, but he does not admit to sitting, yet he admits to an offense, he is to be dealt with for the offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:3.3
Pali: Gamanaṁ paṭijānāti nisajjaṁ paṭijānāti āpattiṁ na paṭijānāti, nisajjāya kāretabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): If he admits to going, and he admits to sitting, but he does not admit to an offense, he is to be dealt with for the sitting. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:3.4
Pali: Gamanaṁ paṭijānāti nisajjaṁ na paṭijānāti āpattiṁ na paṭijānāti, na kāretabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): If he admits to going, but he does not admit to sitting, nor does he admit to an offense, he is not to be dealt with. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:3.5
Pali: Gamanaṁ na paṭijānāti nisajjaṁ paṭijānāti āpattiṁ paṭijānāti, āpattiyā kāretabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not admit to going, but he admits to sitting, and he admits to an offense, he is to be dealt with for the offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:3.6
Pali: Gamanaṁ na paṭijānāti nisajjaṁ na paṭijānāti āpattiṁ paṭijānāti, āpattiyā kāretabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not admit to going, nor does he admit to sitting, but he admits to an offense, he is to be dealt with for the offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:3.7
Pali: Gamanaṁ na paṭijānāti nisajjaṁ paṭijānāti āpattiṁ na paṭijānāti, nisajjāya kāretabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not admit to going, but he admits to sitting, yet he does not admit to an offense, he is to be dealt with for the sitting. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:3.8
Pali: Gamanaṁ na paṭijānāti nisajjaṁ na paṭijānāti āpattiṁ na paṭijānāti, na kāretabboti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not admit to going, nor does he admit to sitting, nor does he admit to an offense, he is not to be dealt with. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:3.9
Pali: Dutiyo aniyato niṭṭhito. 
English (brahmali, published): The second undetermined offense on sitting alone with a woman is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:3.10
Pali: Uddiṭṭhā kho āyasmanto dve aniyatā dhammā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, the two undetermined rules have been recited. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:3.11
Pali: Tatthāyasmante pucchāmi—
English (brahmali, published): In regard to this I ask you, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:3.12
Pali: “kaccittha parisuddhā”? 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:3.13
Pali: Dutiyampi pucchāmi—
English (brahmali, published): A second time I ask, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:3.14
Pali: “kaccittha parisuddhā”? 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:3.15
Pali: Tatiyampi pucchāmi—
English (brahmali, published): A third time I ask, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:3.16
Pali: “kaccittha parisuddhā”? 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:3.17
Pali: Parisuddhetthāyasmanto; 
English (brahmali, published): You are pure in this 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:3.18
Pali: tasmā tuṇhī, 
English (brahmali, published): and therefore silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:3.19
Pali: evametaṁ dhārayāmīti. 
English (brahmali, published): I will remember it thus.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:3.20
Pali: Tassuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:3.21
Pali: Alaṁ kammaniyañceva, 
English (brahmali, published): “Suitable for the deed, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:3.22
Pali: tatheva ca naheva kho; 
English (brahmali, published): And then not so—

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:3.23
Pali: Aniyatā supaññattā, 
English (brahmali, published): The undetermined offenses have been well laid down, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:3.24
Pali: buddhaseṭṭhena tādināti. 
English (brahmali, published): By the Stable One, the Buddha who is the best.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ay2:3.25
Pali: Aniyatakaṇḍaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on undetermined offenses is finished.

English (brahmali, published): Venerables, these thirty rules on relinquishment and confession come up for recitation.

Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati gotamake cetiye. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Vesālī at the Gotamaka Shrine,

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:1.3

English (brahmali, published): When they heard about this, the monks from the group of six went to the village in one set of three robes, stayed in the monastery in another set, and went to bathe in yet another set.

Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:1.5

English (brahmali, published): “How can the monks from the group of six keep extra robes?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:1.6

English (brahmali, published): After rebuking those monks in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks:

English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this:

English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk keeps an extra robe, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:1.14
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:2.1.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmato ānandassa atirekacīvaraṁ uppannaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards Venerable Ānanda was offered an extra robe.

English (brahmali, published): He wanted to give it to Venerable Sāriputta 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:2.3
Pali: Āyasmā ca sāriputto sākete viharati. 
English (brahmali, published): who was staying at Sāketa. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:2.4
Pali: Atha kho āyasmato ānandassa etadahosi—
English (brahmali, published): Knowing that the Buddha had laid down a rule against having extra robes, Ānanda thought,

English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:2.6
Pali: ‘na atirekacīvaraṁ dhāretabban’ti.

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:2.7
Pali: Idañca me atirekacīvaraṁ uppannaṁ.

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:2.8

Pali: Āyasmā ca sāriputto sākete viharati. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:2.10

English (brahmali, published): “What should I do now?”

Pali: Atha kho āyasmā ānando bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): He told the Buddha, who said,

Pali: “Kīvaciraṁ panānanda, sāriputto āgacchissatī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How long is it, Ānanda, before Sāriputta returns?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:2.13
Pali: “Navamaṁ vā, bhagavā, divasaṁ dasamaṁ vā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Nine or ten days.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:2.14
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:2.15
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, dasāhaparamaṁ atirekacīvaraṁ dhāretuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, you should keep an extra robe for ten days at the most. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:2.16
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:2.17.1
Pali: “Niṭṭhitacīvarasmiṁ bhikkhunā ubbhatasmiṁ kathine dasāhaparamaṁ atirekacīvaraṁ dhāretabbaṁ. Taṁ atikkāmayato nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘When his robe is finished and the robe season has ended, a monk should keep an extra robe for ten days at the most. If he keeps it longer than that, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.1.1
Pali: Niṭṭhitacīvarasminti 
English (brahmali, published): When his robe is finished: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.1.2
Pali: bhikkhuno cīvaraṁ kataṁ vā hoti naṭṭhaṁ vā vinaṭṭhaṁ vā daḍḍhaṁ vā cīvarāsā vā upacchinnā. 
English (brahmali, published): the monk has made a robe; or the robe-cloth has been lost, destroyed, or burned; or his expectation of receiving further robe-cloth is disappointed. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.1.3
Pali: Ubbhatasmiṁ kathineti 
English (brahmali, published): The robe season has ended: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.1.4
Pali: aṭṭhannaṁ mātikānaṁ aññatarāya mātikāya ubbhataṁ hoti, saṅghena vā antarā ubbhataṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): it has ended according to one of the eight key phrases or the Sangha has ended it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.1.5
Pali: Dasāhaparamanti 
English (brahmali, published): For ten days at the most: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.1.6
Pali: dasāhaparamatā dhāretabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): it should be kept ten days at a maximum. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.1.7
Pali: Atirekacīvaraṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): An extra robe: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.1.8
Pali: anadhiṭṭhitaṁ avikappitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): it has not been determined nor assigned to another. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.1.9
Pali: Cīvaraṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Robe: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.1.10
Pali: channaṁ cīvarānaṁ aññataraṁ cīvaraṁ, vikappanupagaṁ pacchimaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one of the six kinds of robe-cloth, but not smaller than what can be assigned to another. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.2.1
Pali: Taṁ atikkāmayato nissaggiyaṁ hotīti 
English (brahmali, published): If he keeps it longer than that, it becomes subject to relinquishment: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.2.2
Pali: ekādase aruṇuggamane nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): it becomes subject to relinquishment at dawn on the eleventh day. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.2.3
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā puggalassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The robe-cloth should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.2.4
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.2.5
Pali: Tena bhikkhunā saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā vuḍḍhānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ pāde vanditvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): After approaching the Sangha, that monk should arrange his upper robe over one shoulder and pay respect at the feet of the senior monks. He should then squat on his heels, raise his joined palms, and say: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.2.6
Pali: “idaṁ me, bhante, cīvaraṁ dasāhātikkantaṁ nissaggiyaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Venerables, this robe-cloth, which I have kept over ten days, is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.2.7
Pali: imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to the Sangha.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.2.8
Pali: Nissajjitvā āpatti desetabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): After relinquishing it, he should confess the offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.2.9
Pali: Byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena āpatti paṭiggahetabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): The confession should be received by a competent and capable monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.2.10
Pali: Nissaṭṭhacīvaraṁ dātabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): The relinquished robe-cloth is then to be given back: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.2.11
Pali: “Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.2.12
Pali: Idaṁ cīvaraṁ itthannāmassa bhikkhuno nissaggiyaṁ saṅghassa nissaṭṭhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): This robe-cloth, which was to be relinquished by monk so-and-so, has been relinquished to the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.2.13
Pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho imaṁ cīvaraṁ itthannāmassa bhikkhuno dadeyyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If the Sangha is ready, it should give this robe-cloth back to monk so-and-so.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.2.14
Pali: Tena bhikkhunā sambahule bhikkhū upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā vuḍḍhānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ pāde vanditvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassu vacanīyā—
English (brahmali, published): Or: after approaching several monks, that monk should arrange his upper robe over one shoulder and pay respect at the feet of the senior monks. He should then squat on his heels, raise his joined palms, and say: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.2.15
Pali: “idaṁ me, bhante, cīvaraṁ dasāhātikkantaṁ nissaggiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this robe-cloth, which I have kept over ten days, is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.2.16
Pali: Imāhaṁ āyasmantānaṁ nissajjāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.2.17
Pali: Nissajjitvā āpatti desetabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): After relinquishing it, he should confess the offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.2.18
Pali: Byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena āpatti paṭiggahetabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): The confession should be received by a competent and capable monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.2.19
Pali: Nissaṭṭhacīvaraṁ dātabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): The relinquished robe-cloth is then to be given back: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.2.20
Pali: “Suṇantu me āyasmantā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please, venerables, I ask you to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.2.21
Pali: Idaṁ cīvaraṁ itthannāmassa bhikkhuno nissaggiyaṁ āyasmantānaṁ nissaṭṭhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): This robe-cloth, which was to be relinquished by monk so-and-so, has been relinquished to you. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.2.22
Pali: Yadāyasmantānaṁ pattakallaṁ, āyasmantā imaṁ cīvaraṁ itthannāmassa bhikkhuno dadeyyun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If the venerables are ready, you should give this robe-cloth back to monk so-and-so.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.2.23
Pali: Tena bhikkhunā ekaṁ bhikkhuṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): Or: after approaching a single monk, that monk should arrange his upper robe over one shoulder, squat on his heels, raise his joined palms, and say: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.2.24
Pali: “idaṁ me, āvuso, cīvaraṁ dasāhātikkantaṁ nissaggiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “This robe-cloth, which I have kept over ten days, is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.2.25
Pali: Imāhaṁ āyasmato nissajjāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.2.26
Pali: Nissajjitvā āpatti desetabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): After relinquishing it, he should confess the offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.2.27
Pali: Tena bhikkhunā āpatti paṭiggahetabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): The confession should be received by that monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.2.28
Pali: Nissaṭṭhacīvaraṁ dātabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): The relinquished robe-cloth is then to be given back: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:3.2.29
Pali: “imaṁ cīvaraṁ āyasmato dammī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this robe-cloth back to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:4.1
Pali: Dasāhātikkante atikkantasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than ten days and he perceives it as more, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:4.2
Pali: Dasāhātikkante vematiko, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than ten days, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:4.3
Pali: Dasāhātikkante anatikkantasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than ten days, but he perceives it as less, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:4.4
Pali: Anadhiṭṭhite adhiṭṭhitasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been determined, but he perceives that it has, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:4.5
Pali: Avikappite vikappitasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been assigned to another, but he perceives that it has, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:4.6
Pali: Avissajjite vissajjitasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been given away, but he perceives that it has, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:4.7
Pali: Anaṭṭhe naṭṭhasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been lost, but he perceives that it has, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:4.8
Pali: Avinaṭṭhe vinaṭṭhasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been destroyed, but he perceives that it has, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:4.9
Pali: Adaḍḍhe daḍḍhasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been burned, but he perceives that it has, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:4.10
Pali: Avilutte viluttasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been stolen, but he perceives that it has, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:4.11
Pali: Nissaggiyaṁ cīvaraṁ anissajjitvā paribhuñjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he uses robe-cloth that should be relinquished without first relinquishing it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:4.12
Pali: Dasāhānatikkante atikkantasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than ten days, but he perceives it as more, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:4.13
Pali: Dasāhānatikkante vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than ten days, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:4.14
Pali: Dasāhānatikkante anatikkantasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than ten days and he perceives it as less, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:4.15.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:4.16
Pali: antodasāhaṁ adhiṭṭheti, vikappeti, vissajjeti, nassati, vinassati, ḍayhati, acchinditvā gaṇhanti, vissāsaṁ gaṇhanti, 
English (brahmali, published): if within ten days it has been determined, assigned to another, given away, lost, destroyed, burned, stolen, or taken on trust; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:4.17
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:4.18
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:5.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū nissaṭṭhacīvaraṁ na denti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the monks from the group of six did not give back relinquished robe-cloth. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:5.2
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:5.3
Pali: (…) “Na, bhikkhave, nissaṭṭhacīvaraṁ na dātabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, relinquished robe-cloth should be given back. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:5.4
Pali: Yo na dadeyya, āpatti dukkaṭassā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If you don’t give it back, you commit an offense of wrong conduct.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np1:5.5
Pali: Kathinasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ paṭhamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on keeping extra robe-cloth beyond ten days, the first, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:0.3
Pali: Nissaggiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on relinquishment 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:0.4
Pali: Cīvaravagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on robes 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:0.5
Pali: 2. Udosita(dutiyakathina)sikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 2. Staying apart from one’s robes 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū bhikkhūnaṁ hatthe cīvaraṁ nikkhipitvā santaruttarena janapadacārikaṁ pakkamanti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the monks stored one of their robes with other monks and then left to wander the country in a sarong and an upper robe. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:1.3
Pali: Tāni cīvarāni ciraṁ nikkhittāni kaṇṇakitāni honti. 
English (brahmali, published): Because they were stored for a long time, the robes became moldy. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:1.4
Pali: Tāni bhikkhū otāpenti. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks put them out in the sun. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:1.5
Pali: Addasā kho āyasmā ānando senāsanacārikaṁ āhiṇḍanto te bhikkhū tāni cīvarāni otāpente. 
English (brahmali, published): Then, while walking about the dwellings, Venerable Ānanda noticed that the monks were sunning those robes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:1.6
Pali: Disvāna yena te bhikkhū tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā te bhikkhū etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): He asked them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:1.7
Pali: “kassimāni, āvuso, cīvarāni kaṇṇakitānī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whose moldy robes are these?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:1.8
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū āyasmato ānandassa etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): And they told him what had happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:1.9
Pali: Āyasmā ānando ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Ānanda complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:1.10
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhū bhikkhūnaṁ hatthe cīvaraṁ nikkhipitvā santaruttarena janapadacārikaṁ pakkamissantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can those monks store a robe with other monks and then leave to wander the country in a sarong and an upper robe?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:1.11
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā ānando te bhikkhū anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesi …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking those monks in many ways, Venerable Ānanda told the Buddha. Soon afterwards the Buddha had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:1.12
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhū bhikkhūnaṁ hatthe cīvaraṁ nikkhipitvā santaruttarena janapadacārikaṁ pakkamantī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that there are monks who do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:1.13
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:1.14
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:1.15
Pali: kathañhi nāma te, bhikkhave, moghapurisā bhikkhūnaṁ hatthe cīvaraṁ nikkhipitvā santaruttarena janapadacārikaṁ pakkamissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can those foolish men do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:1.16
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:1.17
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:1.18.1
Pali: “Niṭṭhitacīvarasmiṁ bhikkhunā ubbhatasmiṁ kathine ekarattampi ce bhikkhu ticīvarena vippavaseyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘When his robe is finished and the robe season has ended, if a monk stays apart from his three robes even for a single day, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:1.19
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu kosambiyaṁ gilāno hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time a certain monk at Kosambī was sick. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.2
Pali: Ñātakā tassa bhikkhuno santike dūtaṁ pāhesuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): His relatives sent him a message, saying, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.3
Pali: “āgacchatu bhadanto, mayaṁ, upaṭṭhahissāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Come, venerable, we’ll nurse you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.4
Pali: Bhikkhūpi evamāhaṁsu—“gacchāvuso, ñātakā taṁ upaṭṭhahissantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks urged him to go, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.5
Pali: So evamāha—
English (brahmali, published): but he said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.6
Pali: “bhagavatāvuso, sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): “The Buddha has laid down a training rule 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.7
Pali: ‘na ticīvarena vippavasitabban’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): that you can’t be apart from your three robes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.8
Pali: Ahañcamhi gilāno. 
English (brahmali, published): Now because I’m sick, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.9
Pali: Na sakkomi ticīvaraṁ ādāya pakkamituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): I’m unable to travel with my three robes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.10
Pali: Nāhaṁ gamissāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): So I can’t go.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.11
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.12
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards he gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.13
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, gilānassa bhikkhuno ticīvarena avippavāsasammutiṁ dātuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to give permission to a sick monk to stay apart from his three robes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.14
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, dātabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): And it should be given like this. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.15
Pali: Tena gilānena bhikkhunā saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā vuḍḍhānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ pāde vanditvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): After approaching the Sangha, the sick monk should arrange his upper robe over one shoulder and pay respect at the feet of the senior monks. He should then squat on his heels, raise his joined palms, and say, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.16
Pali: ‘ahaṁ, bhante, gilāno. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Venerables, I’m sick. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.17
Pali: Na sakkomi ticīvaraṁ ādāya pakkamituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): I’m unable to travel with my three robes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.18
Pali: Sohaṁ, bhante, saṅghaṁ ticīvarena avippavāsasammutiṁ yācāmī’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I ask the Sangha for permission to stay apart from my three robes.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.19
Pali: Dutiyampi yācitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): And he should ask a second 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.20
Pali: Tatiyampi yācitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): and a third time. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.21
Pali: Byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena saṅgho ñāpetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): A competent and capable monk should then inform the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.22
Pali: ‘Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.23
Pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmo bhikkhu gilāno. 
English (brahmali, published): The monk so-and-so is sick. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.24
Pali: Na sakkoti ticīvaraṁ ādāya pakkamituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): He is unable to travel with his three robes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.25
Pali: So saṅghaṁ ticīvarena avippavāsasammutiṁ yācati. 
English (brahmali, published): He is asking the Sangha for permission to stay apart from his three robes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.26
Pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmassa bhikkhuno ticīvarena avippavāsasammutiṁ dadeyya. 
English (brahmali, published): If the Sangha is ready, it should give permission to monk so-and-so to stay apart from his three robes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.27
Pali: Esā ñatti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the motion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.28
Pali: Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.29
Pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmo bhikkhu gilāno. 
English (brahmali, published): The monk so-and-so is sick. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.30
Pali: Na sakkoti ticīvaraṁ ādāya pakkamituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): He is unable to travel with his three robes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.31
Pali: So saṅghaṁ ticīvarena avippavāsasammutiṁ yācati. 
English (brahmali, published): He is asking the Sangha for permission to stay apart from his three robes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.32
Pali: Saṅgho itthannāmassa bhikkhuno ticīvarena avippavāsasammutiṁ deti. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha gives permission to monk so-and-so to stay apart from his three robes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.33
Pali: Yassāyasmato khamati itthannāmassa bhikkhuno ticīvarena avippavāsasammutiyā dānaṁ, so tuṇhassa; 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who approves of giving permission to monk so-and-so to stay apart from his three robes should remain silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.34
Pali: yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.35
Pali: Dinnā saṅghena itthannāmassa bhikkhuno ticīvarena avippavāsasammuti. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha has given permission to monk so-and-so to stay apart from his three robes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.36
Pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.37
Pali: evametaṁ dhārayāmī’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I will remember it thus.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.38
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:2.39.1
Pali: “Niṭṭhitacīvarasmiṁ bhikkhunā ubbhatasmiṁ kathine ekarattampi ce bhikkhu ticīvarena vippavaseyya, aññatra bhikkhusammutiyā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘When his robe is finished and the robe season has ended, if a monk stays apart from his three robes even for a single day, except if the monks have agreed, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.1.1
Pali: Niṭṭhitacīvarasminti 
English (brahmali, published): When his robe is finished: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.1.2
Pali: bhikkhuno cīvaraṁ kataṁ vā hoti naṭṭhaṁ vā vinaṭṭhaṁ vā daḍḍhaṁ vā cīvarāsā vā upacchinnā. 
English (brahmali, published): the monk has made a robe; or the robe-cloth has been lost, destroyed, or burned; or his expectation of receiving further robe-cloth is disappointed. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.1.3
Pali: Ubbhatasmiṁ kathineti 
English (brahmali, published): The robe season has ended: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.1.4
Pali: aṭṭhannaṁ mātikānaṁ aññatarāya mātikāya ubbhataṁ hoti, saṅghena vā antarā ubbhataṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): it has ended according to one of the eight key phrases or the Sangha has ended it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.1.5
Pali: Ekarattampi ce bhikkhu ticīvarena vippavaseyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk stays apart from his three robes even for a single day: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.1.6
Pali: saṅghāṭiyā vā uttarāsaṅgena vā antaravāsakena vā. 
English (brahmali, published): from the outer robe, the upper robe, or the sarong. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.1.7
Pali: Aññatra bhikkhusammutiyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Except if the monks have agreed: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.1.8
Pali: ṭhapetvā bhikkhusammutiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): unless the monks have agreed. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.1.9
Pali: Nissaggiyaṁ hotīti 
English (brahmali, published): Entailing relinquishment: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.1.10
Pali: saha aruṇuggamanā nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): it becomes subject to relinquishment at dawn. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.1.11
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā puggalassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The robe should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.1.12
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.1.13
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bu-vb-np1#3.2.5'>Bu NP 1:3.2.5</a>–3.2.29, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.1.14
Pali: “idaṁ me, bhante, cīvaraṁ rattivippavutthaṁ aññatra bhikkhusammutiyā nissaggiyaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this robe, which I have stayed apart from for one day without the agreement of the monks, is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.1.15
Pali: imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.1.16
Pali: dadeyyāti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.1.17
Pali: dadeyyunti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.1.18
Pali: āyasmato dammīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this robe back to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.2.1
Pali: Gāmo ekūpacāro nānūpacāro. 
English (brahmali, published): An inhabited area may have a single access or many; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.2.2
Pali: Nivesanaṁ ekūpacāraṁ nānūpacāraṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): a house may have a single access or many; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.2.3
Pali: Udosito ekūpacāro nānūpacāro. 
English (brahmali, published): a storehouse may have a single access or many; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.2.4
Pali: Aṭṭo ekūpacāro nānūpacāro. 
English (brahmali, published): a watchtower may have a single access or many; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.2.5
Pali: Māḷo ekūpacāro nānūpacāro. 
English (brahmali, published): a stilt house may have a single access or many; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.2.6
Pali: Pāsādo ekūpacāro nānūpacāro. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.2.7
Pali: Hammiyaṁ ekūpacāraṁ nānūpacāraṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.2.8
Pali: Nāvā ekūpacārā nānūpacārā. 
English (brahmali, published): a boat may have a single access or many; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.2.9
Pali: Sattho ekūpacāro nānūpacāro. 
English (brahmali, published): a caravan may have a single access or many; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.2.10
Pali: Khettaṁ ekūpacāraṁ nānūpacāraṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): a field may have a single access or many; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.2.11
Pali: Dhaññakaraṇaṁ ekūpacāraṁ nānūpacāraṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): a threshing floor may have a single access or many; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.2.12
Pali: Ārāmo ekūpacāro nānūpacāro. 
English (brahmali, published): a monastery may have a single access or many; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.2.13
Pali: Vihāro ekūpacāro nānūpacāro. 
English (brahmali, published): a dwelling may have a single access or many; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.2.14
Pali: Rukkhamūlaṁ ekūpacāraṁ nānūpacāraṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): the foot of a tree may have a single access or many; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.2.15
Pali: Ajjhokāso ekūpacāro nānūpacāro. 
English (brahmali, published): out-in-the-open may have a single access or many. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.3.1
Pali: Gāmo ekūpacāro nāma 
English (brahmali, published): “An inhabited area with a single access” refers to the following. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.3.2
Pali: ekakulassa gāmo hoti parikkhitto ca. Antogāme cīvaraṁ nikkhipitvā antogāme vatthabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): An enclosed inhabited area belonging to one clan: if the robe is kept within the inhabited area, one must stay within the inhabited area. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.3.3
Pali: Aparikkhitto hoti, yasmiṁ ghare cīvaraṁ nikkhittaṁ hoti tasmiṁ ghare vatthabbaṁ, hatthapāsā vā na vijahitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): An unenclosed inhabited area belonging to one clan: one must stay in the house where the robe is kept, or not go beyond arm’s reach of the house. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.3.4
Pali: Nānākulassa gāmo hoti parikkhitto ca. Yasmiṁ ghare cīvaraṁ nikkhittaṁ hoti tasmiṁ ghare vatthabbaṁ sabhāye vā dvāramūle vā, hatthapāsā vā na vijahitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): An enclosed inhabited area belonging to many clans: if the robe is kept in a house, one must stay in that house, in the public meeting hall, or at the gateway to the inhabited area, or not go beyond arm’s reach of the public meeting hall or the gateway. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.3.5
Pali: Sabhāyaṁ gacchantena hatthapāse cīvaraṁ nikkhipitvā sabhāye vā vatthabbaṁ dvāramūle vā, hatthapāsā vā na vijahitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If one puts aside the robe within arm’s reach while going to the public meeting hall, one must stay in the public meeting hall, or at the gateway to the inhabited area, or not go beyond arm’s reach of either. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.3.6
Pali: Sabhāye cīvaraṁ nikkhipitvā sabhāye vā vatthabbaṁ dvāramūle vā, hatthapāsā vā na vijahitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If the robe is kept in the public meeting hall, one must stay in the public meeting hall, or at the gateway to the inhabited area, or not go beyond arm’s reach of either. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.3.7
Pali: Aparikkhitto hoti, yasmiṁ ghare cīvaraṁ nikkhittaṁ hoti tasmiṁ ghare vatthabbaṁ, hatthapāsā vā na vijahitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): An unenclosed inhabited area belonging to many clans: one must stay in the house where the robe is kept, or not go beyond arm’s reach of the house. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.4.1
Pali: Ekakulassa nivesanaṁ hoti parikkhittañca, nānāgabbhā nānāovarakā. Antonivesane cīvaraṁ nikkhipitvā antonivesane vatthabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): An enclosed house belonging to one clan and having many rooms: if the robe is kept in the house, one must stay within the house. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.4.2
Pali: Aparikkhittaṁ hoti, yasmiṁ gabbhe cīvaraṁ nikkhittaṁ hoti tasmiṁ gabbhe vatthabbaṁ, hatthapāsā vā na vijahitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): An unenclosed house belonging to one clan and having many rooms: one must stay in the room where the robe is kept, or not go beyond arm’s reach of the room. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.4.3
Pali: Nānākulassa nivesanaṁ hoti parikkhittañca, nānāgabbhā nānāovarakā. Yasmiṁ gabbhe cīvaraṁ nikkhittaṁ hoti tasmiṁ gabbhe vatthabbaṁ dvāramūle vā, hatthapāsā vā na vijahitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): An enclosed house belonging to many clans and having many rooms: if the robe is kept in a room, one must stay in that room, or at the main entrance, or not go beyond arm’s reach of either. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.4.4

English (brahmali, published): An unenclosed house belonging to many clans and having many rooms: one must stay in the room where the robe is kept, or not go beyond arm’s reach of the room.

English (brahmali, published): A caravan belonging to one clan: if the robe is kept within the caravan, one must not go further than 80 meters in front of or behind the caravan, and no further than 11 meters from either side.

Pali: Ekakulassa rukkhamūlaṁ hoti, yaṁ majjhanhike kāle samantā chāyā pharati, antochāyāya cīvaraṁ nikkhipitvā antochāyāya vatthabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At the foot of a tree belonging to one clan: if the robe is kept within the area of the midday shadow of the tree, one must stay within that area.

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.17.1
Pali: Ajjhokāso ekūpacāro nāma agāmake araññe samantā sattabbhantarā ekūpacāro, 
English (brahmali, published): Out-in-the-open with a single access: in an uninhabited area, in the wilderness, the area covered by a circle with 80 meters radius has a single access. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.17.2
Pali: tato paraṁ nānūpacāro. 
English (brahmali, published): Whatever lies beyond that has many accesses. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.18.1
Pali: Vippavutthe vippavutthasaññī aññatra bhikkhusammutiyā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has stayed apart and he perceives that he has, then, except if the monks have agreed, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.18.2
Pali: Vippavutthe vematiko, aññatra bhikkhusammutiyā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has stayed apart, but he is unsure of it, then, except if the monks have agreed, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.18.3
Pali: Vippavutthe avippavutthasaññī, aññatra bhikkhusammutiyā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has stayed apart, but he does not perceive that he has, then, except if the monks have agreed, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.18.4
Pali: Appaccuddhaṭe paccuddhaṭasaññī …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been relinquished, but he perceives that it has, then, except if the monks have agreed, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.18.5
Pali: avissajjite vissajjitasaññī … 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been given away, but he perceives that it has, then, except if the monks have agreed, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.18.6
Pali: anaṭṭhe naṭṭhasaññī … 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been lost, but he perceives that it has, then, except if the monks have agreed, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.18.7
Pali: avinaṭṭhe vinaṭṭhasaññī … 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been destroyed, but he perceives that it has, then, except if the monks have agreed, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.18.8
Pali: adaḍḍhe daḍḍhasaññī … 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been burned, but he perceives that it has, then, except if the monks have agreed, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.18.9
Pali: avilutte viluttasaññī, aññatra bhikkhusammutiyā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been stolen, but he perceives that it has, then, except if the monks have agreed, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.18.10
Pali: Nissaggiyaṁ cīvaraṁ anissajjitvā paribhuñjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he uses a robe that should be relinquished without first relinquishing it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.18.11
Pali: Avippavutthe vippavutthasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has not stayed apart, but he perceives that he has, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.18.12
Pali: Avippavutthe vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has not stayed apart, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.18.13
Pali: Avippavutthe avippavutthasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has not stayed apart and he does not perceive that he has, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.19.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.19.2
Pali: antoaruṇe paccuddharati, vissajjeti, nassati, vinassati, ḍayhati, acchinditvā gaṇhanti, vissāsaṁ gaṇhanti, 
English (brahmali, published): if before dawn it has been relinquished, given away, lost, destroyed, burned, stolen, or taken on trust; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.19.3
Pali: bhikkhusammutiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he has the permission of the monks; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.19.4
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.19.5
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np2:3.19.6
Pali: Udosita(dutiyakathina)sikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ dutiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on staying apart from one’s robes, the second, is finished.
