pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:0.3
Pali: Nissaggiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on relinquishment 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:0.4
Pali: Cīvaravagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on robes 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:0.5
Pali: 3. Tatiyakathinasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 3. Keeping extra robe-cloth beyond a month 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarassa bhikkhuno akālacīvaraṁ uppannaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): a monk had been given robe-cloth outside the robe season. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.1.3
Pali: Tassa taṁ cīvaraṁ kayiramānaṁ nappahoti. 
English (brahmali, published): While he was making the robe, he realized there was not enough cloth. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.1.4
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu taṁ cīvaraṁ ussāpetvā punappunaṁ vimajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): Lifting it up, he smoothed it out again and again. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.1.5
Pali: Addasā kho bhagavā senāsanacārikaṁ āhiṇḍanto taṁ bhikkhuṁ taṁ cīvaraṁ ussāpetvā punappunaṁ vimajjantaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): While walking about the dwellings, the Buddha saw that monk acting in this way. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.1.6
Pali: Disvāna yena so bhikkhu tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): He went up to him and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.1.7
Pali: “kissa tvaṁ, bhikkhu, imaṁ cīvaraṁ ussāpetvā punappunaṁ vimajjasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What are you doing, monk?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.1.8
Pali: “Idaṁ me, bhante, akālacīvaraṁ uppannaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, I’ve been given this out-of-season robe-cloth, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.1.9
Pali: Kayiramānaṁ nappahoti. 
English (brahmali, published): but it’s not enough to make a robe. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.1.10
Pali: Tenāhaṁ imaṁ cīvaraṁ ussāpetvā punappunaṁ vimajjāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): That’s why I lift it up and smooth it out again and again.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.1.11
Pali: “Atthi pana te, bhikkhu, cīvarapaccāsā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Are you expecting to receive more cloth?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.1.12
Pali: “Atthi, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I am.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.1.13
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā, bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.1.14
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, akālacīvaraṁ paṭiggahetvā cīvarapaccāsā nikkhipitun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to keep out-of-season robe-cloth if you are expecting to receive more.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū—“bhagavatā anuññātaṁ akālacīvaraṁ paṭiggahetvā cīvarapaccāsā nikkhipitun”ti akālacīvarāni paṭiggahetvā atirekamāsaṁ nikkhipanti. 
English (brahmali, published): When they heard about this, some monks kept out-of-season robe-cloth for more than a month, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.2.2
Pali: Tāni cīvarāni cīvaravaṁse bhaṇḍikābaddhāni tiṭṭhanti. 
English (brahmali, published): keeping them in bundles on a bamboo robe rack. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.2.3
Pali: Addasā kho āyasmā ānando senāsanacārikaṁ āhiṇḍanto tāni cīvarāni cīvaravaṁse bhaṇḍikābaddhāni tiṭṭhante. 
English (brahmali, published): While walking about the dwellings, Venerable Ānanda saw that robe-cloth, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.2.4
Pali: Disvāna bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): and he asked the monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.2.5
Pali: “kassimāni, āvuso, cīvarāni cīvaravaṁse bhaṇḍikābaddhāni tiṭṭhantī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whose cloth is this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.3.1
Pali: “Amhākaṁ, āvuso, akālacīvarāni cīvarapaccāsā nikkhittānī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s our out-of-season robe-cloth, which we’re keeping because we’re expecting more.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.3.2
Pali: “Kīvaciraṁ panāvuso, imāni cīvarāni nikkhittānī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “But how long have you kept it?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.3.3
Pali: “Atirekamāsaṁ, āvuso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “More than a month.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.3.4
Pali: Āyasmā ānando ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Ānanda complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.3.5
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhū akālacīvaraṁ paṭiggahetvā atirekamāsaṁ nikkhipissantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can these monks keep out-of-season robe-cloth for more than a month?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.3.6
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā ānando te bhikkhū anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesi …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking those monks in many ways, Venerable Ānanda told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.3.7
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhū akālacīvaraṁ paṭiggahetvā atirekamāsaṁ nikkhipantī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that there are monks who do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.3.8
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.3.9
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.3.10
Pali: kathañhi nāma te, bhikkhave, moghapurisā akālacīvaraṁ paṭiggahetvā atirekamāsaṁ nikkhipissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can those foolish men keep out-of-season robe-cloth for more than a month? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.3.11
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.3.12
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.3.13.1
Pali: “Niṭṭhitacīvarasmiṁ bhikkhunā ubbhatasmiṁ kathine bhikkhuno paneva akālacīvaraṁ uppajjeyya, ākaṅkhamānena bhikkhunā paṭiggahetabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘When his robe is finished and the robe season has ended, if out-of-season robe-cloth is offered to a monk, he may receive it if he wishes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.3.14
Pali: Paṭiggahetvā khippameva kāretabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he receives it, he should quickly make a robe. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.3.15
Pali: No cassa pāripūri, māsaparamaṁ tena bhikkhunā taṁ cīvaraṁ nikkhipitabbaṁ ūnassa pāripūriyā satiyā paccāsāya. 
English (brahmali, published): If there is not enough cloth, but he is expecting more, he should keep it at most one month to make up the lack. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:1.3.16
Pali: Tato ce uttari nikkhipeyya, satiyāpi paccāsāya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he keeps it longer than that, then even if he expects more cloth, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.1.1
Pali: Niṭṭhitacīvarasminti 
English (brahmali, published): When his robe is finished: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.1.2
Pali: bhikkhuno cīvaraṁ kataṁ vā hoti naṭṭhaṁ vā vinaṭṭhaṁ vā daḍḍhaṁ vā cīvarāsā vā upacchinnā. 
English (brahmali, published): the monk has made a robe; or the robe-cloth has been lost, destroyed, or burned; or his expectation of receiving further robe-cloth is disappointed. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.1.3
Pali: Ubbhatasmiṁ kathineti 
English (brahmali, published): The robe season has ended: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.1.4
Pali: aṭṭhannaṁ mātikānaṁ aññatarāya mātikāya ubbhataṁ hoti, saṅghena vā antarā ubbhataṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): it has ended according to one of the eight key phrases or the Sangha has ended it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.1.5
Pali: Akālacīvaraṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Out-of-season robe-cloth: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.1.6
Pali: anatthate kathine ekādasamāse uppannaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): for one who has not participated in the robe-making ceremony, it is robe-cloth given during the eleven months. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.1.7
Pali: atthate kathine sattamāse uppannaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): for one who has participated in the robe-making ceremony, it is robe-cloth given during the seven months. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.1.8
Pali: kālepi ādissa dinnaṁ, etaṁ akālacīvaraṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): Also, if it is given in the robe season, but the cloth is designated, it is called “out-of-season robe-cloth”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.1.9
Pali: Uppajjeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): If it is offered: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.1.10
Pali: uppajjeyya saṅghato vā gaṇato vā ñātito vā mittato vā paṁsukūlaṁ vā attano vā dhanena. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is offered by a sangha, by a group, by a relative, or by a friend, or if it is rags, or if he got it by means of his own property. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.1.11
Pali: Ākaṅkhamānenāti 
English (brahmali, published): If he wishes: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.1.12
Pali: icchamānena paṭiggahetabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if he desires, he may receive it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.1.13
Pali: Paṭiggahetvā khippameva kāretabbanti 
English (brahmali, published): If he receives it, he should quickly make a robe: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.1.14
Pali: dasāhā kāretabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): it should be made within ten days. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.1.15
Pali: No cassa pāripūrīti 
English (brahmali, published): If there is not enough cloth: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.1.16
Pali: kayiramānaṁ nappahoti. 
English (brahmali, published): if there is not enough cloth when the robe is being made. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.1.17
Pali: Māsaparamaṁ tena bhikkhunā taṁ cīvaraṁ nikkhipitabbanti 
English (brahmali, published): He should keep it at most one month: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.1.18
Pali: māsaparamatā nikkhipitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): he should keep it one month at a maximum. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.1.19
Pali: Ūnassa pāripūriyāti 
English (brahmali, published): To make up the lack: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.1.20
Pali: ūnassa pāripūratthāya. 
English (brahmali, published): for the purpose of making up the lack. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.1.21
Pali: Satiyā paccāsāyāti 
English (brahmali, published): But he is expecting more: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.1.22
Pali: paccāsā hoti saṅghato vā gaṇato vā ñātito vā mittato vā paṁsukūlaṁ vā attano vā dhanena. 
English (brahmali, published): he is expecting more from a sangha, from a group, from a relative, or from a friend, or he is expecting to get rags, or he is expecting to get it by means of his own property. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.1
Pali: Tato ce uttari nikkhipeyya satiyāpi paccāsāyāti 
English (brahmali, published): If he keeps it longer than that, then even if he expects more cloth: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.2
Pali: tadahuppanne mūlacīvare paccāsācīvaraṁ uppajjati, dasāhā kāretabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is given the expected robe-cloth on the same day as he was given the original robe-cloth, it must be made into a robe within ten days. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.3
Pali: Dvīhuppanne mūlacīvare paccāsācīvaraṁ uppajjati, dasāhā kāretabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is given the expected robe-cloth the day after he was given the original robe-cloth, it must be made into a robe within ten days. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.4
Pali: Tīhuppanne mūlacīvare …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If he is given the expected robe-cloth two days after … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.5
Pali: catūhuppanne … 
English (brahmali, published): three days after … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.6
Pali: pañcāhuppanne … 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.7
Pali: chāhuppanne … 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.8
Pali: sattāhuppanne … 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.9
Pali: aṭṭhāhuppanne … 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.10
Pali: navāhuppanne … 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.11
Pali: dasāhuppanne mūlacīvare paccāsācīvaraṁ uppajjati, dasāhā kāretabbaṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.12
Pali: Ekādase uppanne …pe… 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.13

English:

English:

English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.18
Pali: sattarase uppanne … 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.19
Pali: aṭṭhārase uppanne … 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.20
Pali: ekūnavīse uppanne … 
English (brahmali, published): eighteen days after … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.21
Pali: vīse uppanne mūlacīvare paccāsācīvaraṁ uppajjati, dasāhā kāretabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): nineteen days after he was given the original robe-cloth, it must be made into a robe within ten days. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.22
Pali: Ekavīse uppanne mūlacīvare paccāsācīvaraṁ uppajjati, navāhā kāretabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is given the expected robe-cloth twenty days after he was given the original robe-cloth, it must be made into a robe within nine days. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.23
Pali: Dvāvīse uppanne …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If he is given the expected robe-cloth twenty-one days after he was given the original robe-cloth, it must be made into a robe within eight days. …

Pali: tevīse uppanne … 
English (brahmali, published): twenty-two days after …

Pali: aṭṭhavīse uppanne … 
English (brahmali, published): twenty-seven days after … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.30
Pali: ekūnatiṁse uppanne mūlacīvare paccāsācīvaraṁ uppajjati, ekāhaṁ kāretabbaṁ … 
English (brahmali, published): If he is given the expected robe-cloth twenty-eight days after he was given the original robe-cloth, it must be made into a robe within one day. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.31
Pali: tiṁse uppanne mūlacīvare paccāsācīvaraṁ uppajjati, tadaheva adhiṭṭhātabbaṁ vikappetabbaṁ vissajjetabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is given the expected robe-cloth twenty-nine days after he was given the original robe-cloth, it must be determined, assigned to another, or given away on that very day. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.32
Pali: No ce adhiṭṭheyya vā vikappeyya vā vissajjeyya vā, ekatiṁse aruṇuggamane nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not determine it, assign it to another, or give it away, it becomes subject to relinquishment at dawn on the thirtieth day. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.33
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā puggalassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The robe-cloth should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.34
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.35
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bu-vb-np1#3.2.5'>Bu NP 1:3.2.5</a>–3.2.29, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.36
Pali: idaṁ me, bhante, akālacīvaraṁ māsātikkantaṁ nissaggiyaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this out-of-season robe-cloth, which I have kept for more than a month, is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.37
Pali: imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmīti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.38
Pali: dadeyyāti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.39
Pali: dadeyyunti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.40
Pali: āyasmato dammīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this robe-cloth back to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.2.41
Pali: Visabhāge uppanne mūlacīvare paccāsācīvaraṁ uppajjati, rattiyo ca sesā honti, na akāmā kāretabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is given the expected robe-cloth, but it is different from the robe-cloth originally given to him, and there are days remaining, he does not have to make a robe if he does not want to. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.3.1
Pali: Māsātikkante atikkantasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than a month and he perceives it as more, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.3.2
Pali: Māsātikkante vematiko nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than a month, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.3.3
Pali: Māsātikkante anatikkantasaññī nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than a month, but he perceives it as less, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.3.4
Pali: Anadhiṭṭhite adhiṭṭhitasaññī …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been determined, but he perceives that it has, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.3.5
Pali: avikappite vikappitasaññī … 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been assigned to another, but he perceives that it has, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.3.6
Pali: avissajjite vissajjitasaññī … 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been given away, but he perceives that it has, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.3.7
Pali: anaṭṭhe naṭṭhasaññī … 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been lost, but he perceives that it has, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.3.8
Pali: avinaṭṭhe vinaṭṭhasaññī … 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been destroyed, but he perceives that it has, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.3.9
Pali: adaḍḍhe daḍḍhasaññī … 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been burned, but he perceives that it has, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.3.10
Pali: avilutte viluttasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been stolen, but he perceives that it has, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.3.11
Pali: Nissaggiyaṁ cīvaraṁ anissajjitvā paribhuñjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he uses robe-cloth that should be relinquished without first relinquishing it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.3.12
Pali: Māsānatikkante atikkantasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than a month, but he perceives it as more, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.3.13
Pali: Māsānatikkante vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than a month, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.3.14
Pali: Māsānatikkante anatikkantasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than a month and he perceives it as less, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.4.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.4.2
Pali: antomāse adhiṭṭheti, vikappeti, vissajjeti, nassati, vinassati, ḍayhati, acchinditvā gaṇhanti, vissāsaṁ gaṇhanti, 
English (brahmali, published): if within a month it has been determined, assigned to another, given away, lost, destroyed, burned, stolen, or taken on trust; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.4.3
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.4.4
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np3:2.4.5
Pali: Tatiyakathinasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ tatiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The third training rule on keeping extra robe-cloth beyond a month, the third, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:0.5
Pali: 4. Purāṇacīvarasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 4. Having a nun wash a used robe 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmato udāyissa purāṇadutiyikā bhikkhunīsu pabbajitā hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Udāyī’s ex-wife became a nun. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.3
Pali: Sā āyasmato udāyissa santike abhikkhaṇaṁ āgacchati. 
English (brahmali, published): She frequently visited Udāyī, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.4
Pali: Āyasmāpi udāyī tassā bhikkhuniyā santike abhikkhaṇaṁ gacchati. 
English (brahmali, published): and he frequently visited her. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.5
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā udāyī tassā bhikkhuniyā santike bhattavissaggaṁ karoti. 
English (brahmali, published): And Udāyī shared his meals with that nun. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.6
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā udāyī pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena sā bhikkhunī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā tassā bhikkhuniyā purato aṅgajātaṁ vivaritvā āsane nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): One morning Udāyī robed up, took his bowl and robe, and went to her. He then uncovered his genitals in front of her and sat down on a seat. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.7
Pali: Sāpi kho bhikkhunī āyasmato udāyissa purato aṅgajātaṁ vivaritvā āsane nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): She too uncovered her genitals in front of him and sat down on a seat. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.8
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā udāyī sāratto tassā bhikkhuniyā aṅgajātaṁ upanijjhāyi. Tassa asuci mucci. 
English (brahmali, published): Lustfully staring at her genitals, he emitted semen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.9
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā udāyī taṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): He then said to that nun: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.10
Pali: “gaccha, bhagini, udakaṁ āhara, antaravāsakaṁ dhovissāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sister, get some water. I’ll wash the robe.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.11
Pali: “Āharayya, ahameva dhovissāmī”ti 
English (brahmali, published): “Give it to me, venerable, I’ll wash it.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.12
Pali: taṁ asuciṁ ekadesaṁ mukhena aggahesi ekadesaṁ aṅgajāte pakkhipi. 
English (brahmali, published): She then took some of the semen in her mouth and inserted some into her vagina. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.13
Pali: Sā tena gabbhaṁ gaṇhi. 
English (brahmali, published): Because of that she became pregnant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.14
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo evamāhaṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.15
Pali: “abrahmacārinī ayaṁ bhikkhunī, gabbhinī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “This nun doesn’t abstain from sex. She’s pregnant.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.16
Pali: “Nāhaṁ, ayye, abrahmacārinī”ti bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): She said, “Venerables, I do abstain from sex,” and she told them what had happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.17
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns complained and criticized Udāyī, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.18
Pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyo udāyī bhikkhuniyā purāṇacīvaraṁ dhovāpessatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Udāyī get a nun to wash a used robe?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.19
Pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They then told the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.20
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.21
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā udāyī bhikkhuniyā purāṇacīvaraṁ dhovāpessatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Udāyī get a nun to wash a used robe?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.22
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking him in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned Udāyī: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.23
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, udāyi, bhikkhuniyā purāṇacīvaraṁ dhovāpesī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Udāyī, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.24
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.25
Pali: “Ñātikā te, udāyi, aññātikā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is she a relative of yours?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.26
Pali: “Aññātikā, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “No.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.27
Pali: “Aññātako, moghapurisa, aññātikāya na jānāti patirūpaṁ vā appatirūpaṁ vā pāsādikaṁ vā apāsādikaṁ vā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, a man and a woman who are unrelated don’t know what’s appropriate and inappropriate, what’s inspiring and uninspiring, in dealing with each other. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.28
Pali: Tattha nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, aññātikāya bhikkhuniyā purāṇacīvaraṁ dhovāpessasi. 
English (brahmali, published): And still you did this. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.29
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.30
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:1.31.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu aññātikāya bhikkhuniyā purāṇacīvaraṁ dhovāpeyya vā rajāpeyya vā ākoṭāpeyya vā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk has an unrelated nun wash, dye, or beat a used robe, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.1.5
Pali: Aññātikā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Unrelated: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.1.6
Pali: mātito vā pitito vā yāva sattamā pitāmahayugā asambaddhā. 
English (brahmali, published): anyone who is not a descendant of one’s male ancestors going back eight generations, either on the mother’s side or on the father’s side. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.1.7
Pali: Bhikkhunī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A nun: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.1.8
Pali: ubhatosaṅghe upasampannā. 
English (brahmali, published): she has been given the full ordination by both Sanghas. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.1.9
Pali: Purāṇacīvaraṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A used robe: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.1.10
Pali: sakiṁ nivatthampi sakiṁ pārutampi. 
English (brahmali, published): a sarong or an upper robe, even worn once. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.1.11
Pali: Dhovāti āṇāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he tells her to wash it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.1.12
Pali: Dhotaṁ nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): When it has been washed, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.1.13
Pali: Rajāti āṇāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he tells her to dye it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.1.14
Pali: Rattaṁ nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): When it has been dyed, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.1.15
Pali: Ākoṭehīti āṇāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he tells her to beat it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.1.16
Pali: Sakiṁ pāṇippahāraṁ vā muggarappahāraṁ vā dinne nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): When she has struck it once with her hand or with an implement, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.1.17
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā puggalassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The robe should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.1.18
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.1.19
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bu-vb-np1#3.2.5'>Bu NP 1:3.2.5</a>–3.2.29, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.1.20
Pali: idaṁ me, bhante, purāṇacīvaraṁ aññātikāya bhikkhuniyā dhovāpitaṁ nissaggiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this used robe, which I got an unrelated nun to wash, is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.1.21
Pali: Imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmīti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.1.22
Pali: dadeyyāti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.1.23
Pali: dadeyyunti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.1.24
Pali: āyasmato dammīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this robe back to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.1
Pali: Aññātikāya aññātikasaññī purāṇacīvaraṁ dhovāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated and he perceives her as such, and he has her wash a used robe, he commits one offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.2
Pali: Aññātikāya aññātikasaññī purāṇacīvaraṁ dhovāpeti rajāpeti, nissaggiyena āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated and he perceives her as such, and he has her wash and dye a used robe, he commits one offense entailing relinquishment and one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.3
Pali: Aññātikāya aññātikasaññī purāṇacīvaraṁ dhovāpeti ākoṭāpeti, nissaggiyena āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated and he perceives her as such, and he has her wash and beat a used robe, he commits one offense entailing relinquishment and one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.4
Pali: Aññātikāya aññātikasaññī purāṇacīvaraṁ dhovāpeti rajāpeti ākoṭāpeti, nissaggiyena āpatti dvinnaṁ dukkaṭānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated and he perceives her as such, and he has her wash, dye, and beat a used robe, he commits one offense entailing relinquishment and two offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.5
Pali: Aññātikāya aññātikasaññī purāṇacīvaraṁ rajāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated and he perceives her as such, and he has her dye a used robe, he commits one offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.6
Pali: Aññātikāya aññātikasaññī purāṇacīvaraṁ rajāpeti ākoṭāpeti, nissaggiyena āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated and he perceives her as such, and he has her dye and beat a used robe, he commits one offense entailing relinquishment and one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.7
Pali: Aññātikāya aññātikasaññī purāṇacīvaraṁ rajāpeti dhovāpeti, nissaggiyena āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated and he perceives her as such, and he has her dye and wash a used robe, he commits one offense entailing relinquishment and one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.8
Pali: Aññātikāya aññātikasaññī purāṇacīvaraṁ rajāpeti ākoṭāpeti dhovāpeti, nissaggiyena āpatti dvinnaṁ dukkaṭānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated and he perceives her as such, and he has her dye, beat, and wash a used robe, he commits one offense entailing relinquishment and two offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.9
Pali: Aññātikāya aññātikasaññī purāṇacīvaraṁ ākoṭāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated and he perceives her as such, and he has her beat a used robe, he commits one offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.10
Pali: Aññātikāya aññātikasaññī purāṇacīvaraṁ ākoṭāpeti dhovāpeti, nissaggiyena āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated and he perceives her as such, and he has her beat and wash a used robe, he commits one offense entailing relinquishment and one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.11
Pali: Aññātikāya aññātikasaññī purāṇacīvaraṁ ākoṭāpeti rajāpeti, nissaggiyena āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated and he perceives her as such, and he has her beat and dye a used robe, he commits one offense entailing relinquishment and one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.12
Pali: Aññātikāya aññātikasaññī purāṇacīvaraṁ ākoṭāpeti dhovāpeti rajāpeti, nissaggiyena āpatti dvinnaṁ dukkaṭānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated and he perceives her as such, and he has her beat, wash, and dye a used robe, he commits one offense entailing relinquishment and two offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.13
Pali: Aññātikāya vematiko …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated, but he is unsure of it … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.14
Pali: aññātikāya ñātikasaññī …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated, but he perceives her as related … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.15
Pali: aññassa purāṇacīvaraṁ dhovāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has her wash a used robe belonging to someone else, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.16
Pali: Nisīdanapaccattharaṇaṁ dhovāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has her wash a sitting mat or a sheet, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.17
Pali: Ekatoupasampannāya dhovāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has a nun who is fully ordained only on one side do the washing, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.18
Pali: Ñātikāya aññātikasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is related, but he perceives her as unrelated, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.19
Pali: Ñātikāya vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is related, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.20
Pali: Ñātikāya ñātikasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is related and he perceives her as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.21.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.22
Pali: ñātikāya dhovantiyā aññātikā dutiyā hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if a related nun does the washing and an unrelated nun helps her; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.23
Pali: avuttā dhovati, 
English (brahmali, published): if a nun does the washing without being asked; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.24
Pali: aparibhuttaṁ dhovāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he has a nun wash an unused robe; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.25
Pali: cīvaraṁ ṭhapetvā aññaṁ parikkhāraṁ dhovāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he has a nun wash any requisite apart from a robe; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.26
Pali: sikkhamānāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a trainee nun; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.27
Pali: sāmaṇeriyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a novice nun; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.28
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.29
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np4:2.2.30
Pali: Purāṇacīvarasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ catutthaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on having a nun wash a used robe, the fourth, is finished.

English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

English (brahmali, published): The chapter on relinquishment

English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on robes 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:0.5
Pali: 5. Cīvarapaṭiggahaṇasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 5. Receiving a robe from a nun 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Rājagaha in the Bamboo Grove, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena uppalavaṇṇā bhikkhunī sāvatthiyaṁ viharati. 
English (brahmali, published): the nun Uppalavaṇṇā was staying at Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.1.3
Pali: Atha kho uppalavaṇṇā bhikkhunī pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya sāvatthiṁ piṇḍāya pāvisi. 
English (brahmali, published): One morning she robed up, took her bowl and robe, and entered Sāvatthī to collect almsfood. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.1.4
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ piṇḍāya caritvā pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkantā yena andhavanaṁ tenupasaṅkami divāvihārāya. 
English (brahmali, published): When she had completed her almsround, eaten her meal, and returned, she went to the Blind Men’s Grove 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.1.5
Pali: Andhavanaṁ ajjhogāhetvā aññatarasmiṁ rukkhamūle divāvihāraṁ nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): where she sat down at the foot of a tree for the day’s meditation. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.1.6
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena corā katakammā gāviṁ vadhitvā maṁsaṁ gahetvā andhavanaṁ pavisiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): Just then some bandits who had stolen and slaughtered a cow were taking the meat to the Blind Men’s Grove. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.1.7
Pali: Addasā kho coragāmaṇiko uppalavaṇṇaṁ bhikkhuniṁ aññatarasmiṁ rukkhamūle divāvihāraṁ nisinnaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The head bandit saw Uppalavaṇṇā sitting at the foot of that tree. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.1.8
Pali: Disvānassa etadahosi—
English (brahmali, published): He thought, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.1.9
Pali: “sace me puttabhātukā passissanti viheṭhissanti imaṁ bhikkhunin”ti 
English (brahmali, published): “If my sons and brothers see this nun, they’ll harass her,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.1.10
Pali: aññena maggena agamāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): and he took a different path. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.1.11
Pali: Atha kho so coragāmaṇiko maṁse pakke varamaṁsāni gahetvā paṇṇapuṭaṁ bandhitvā uppalavaṇṇāya bhikkhuniyā avidūre rukkhe ālaggetvā—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards when the meat was cooked, he took the best part, tied it up with a palm-leaf wrap, hung it from a tree not far from Uppalavaṇṇā, and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.1.12
Pali: “yo passati samaṇo vā brāhmaṇo vā dinnaṁyeva haratū”ti, vatvā pakkāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Whatever ascetic or brahmin sees this gift, please take it.” And he left. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.1.13
Pali: Assosi kho uppalavaṇṇā bhikkhunī samādhimhā vuṭṭhahitvā tassa coragāmaṇikassa imaṁ vācaṁ bhāsamānassa. 
English (brahmali, published): Uppalavaṇṇā had just come out from the stillness of meditation when she heard the head bandit making that statement. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.1.14
Pali: Atha kho uppalavaṇṇā bhikkhunī taṁ maṁsaṁ gahetvā upassayaṁ agamāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): She took the meat and returned to the nunnery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.1.15
Pali: Atha kho uppalavaṇṇā bhikkhunī tassā rattiyā accayena taṁ maṁsaṁ sampādetvā uttarāsaṅgena bhaṇḍikaṁ bandhitvā vehāsaṁ abbhuggantvā veḷuvane paccuṭṭhāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): The following morning she prepared the meat and made it into a bundle with her upper robe. She then rose into the air and landed in the Bamboo Grove. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā gāmaṁ piṇḍāya paviṭṭho hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): When she arrived, the Buddha had already gone to the village for alms, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.2
Pali: Āyasmā udāyī ohiyyako hoti vihārapālo. 
English (brahmali, published): but Venerable Udāyī had been left behind to look after the dwellings. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.3
Pali: Atha kho uppalavaṇṇā bhikkhunī yenāyasmā udāyī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Uppalavaṇṇā approached Udāyī and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.4
Pali: “kahaṁ, bhante, bhagavā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, where’s the Buddha?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.5
Pali: “Paviṭṭho, bhagini, bhagavā gāmaṁ piṇḍāyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “He’s gone to the village for alms.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.6
Pali: “Imaṁ, bhante, maṁsaṁ bhagavato dehī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please give this meat to the Buddha.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.7
Pali: “Santappito tayā, bhagini, bhagavā maṁsena. 
English (brahmali, published): “You’ll please the Buddha with that meat. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.8
Pali: Sace me tvaṁ antaravāsakaṁ dadeyyāsi, evaṁ ahampi santappito bhaveyyaṁ antaravāsakenā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If you give me your sarong, you’ll please me too.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.9
Pali: “Mayaṁ kho, bhante, mātugāmā nāma kicchalābhā. Idañca me antimaṁ pañcamaṁ cīvaraṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s hard for women to get material support, and this is one of my five robes. I don’t have another. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.10
Pali: Nāhaṁ dassāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I can’t give it away.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.11
Pali: “Seyyathāpi, bhagini, puriso hatthiṁ datvā kacche sajjeyya; 
English (brahmali, published): “Sister, just as a man giving an elephant might decorate it with a girdle, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.12
Pali: evameva kho tvaṁ, bhagini, bhagavato maṁsaṁ datvā mayi antaravāsake sajjasī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): so should you, when giving meat to the Buddha, decorate me with your sarong.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.13
Pali: Atha kho uppalavaṇṇā bhikkhunī āyasmatā udāyinā nippīḷiyamānā antaravāsakaṁ datvā upassayaṁ agamāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): Being pressured by Udāyī, Uppalavaṇṇā gave him her sarong and then returned to the nunnery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.14
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo uppalavaṇṇāya bhikkhuniyā pattacīvaraṁ paṭiggaṇhantiyo uppalavaṇṇaṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns who received Uppalavaṇṇā’s bowl and robe asked her 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.15
Pali: “kahaṁ te, ayye, antaravāsako”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): where her sarong was. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.16
Pali: Uppalavaṇṇā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): And she told them what had happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.17
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns complained and criticized Udāyī, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.18
Pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyo udāyī bhikkhuniyā cīvaraṁ paṭiggahessati kicchalābho mātugāmo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Udāyī receive a robe from a nun? It’s hard for women to get material support.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.19
Pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns told the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.20
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized Udāyī, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.21
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā udāyī bhikkhuniyā cīvaraṁ paṭiggahessatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Udāyī receive a robe from a nun?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.22
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking him in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned Udāyī: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.23
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, udāyi, bhikkhuniyā cīvaraṁ paṭiggahesī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Udāyī, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.24
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.25
Pali: “Ñātikā te, udāyi, aññātikā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is she a relative of yours?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.26
Pali: “Aññātikā, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “No.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.27
Pali: “Aññātako, moghapurisa, aññātikāya na jānāti patirūpaṁ vā appatirūpaṁ vā santaṁ vā asantaṁ vā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, a man and a woman who are unrelated don’t know what’s appropriate and inappropriate, what’s good and bad, in dealing with each other. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.28
Pali: Tattha nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, aññātikāya bhikkhuniyā hatthato cīvaraṁ paṭiggahessasi. 
English (brahmali, published): And still you did this. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.29
Pali: Netaṁ moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.30
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.31.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu aññātikāya bhikkhuniyā hatthato cīvaraṁ paṭiggaṇheyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk receives a robe directly from an unrelated nun, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:1.2.32
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū kukkuccāyantā bhikkhunīnaṁ pārivattakacīvaraṁ na paṭiggaṇhanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Then, being afraid of wrongdoing, the monks did not receive robes from nuns even in exchange. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:2.2
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:2.3
Pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā amhākaṁ pārivattakacīvaraṁ na paṭiggahessantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can they not receive robes from us in exchange?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:2.4
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tāsaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ ujjhāyantīnaṁ khiyyantīnaṁ vipācentīnaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the criticism of those nuns 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:2.5
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): and they told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:2.6
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:2.7
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, pañcannaṁ pārivattakaṁ paṭiggahetuṁ— bhikkhussa, bhikkhuniyā, sikkhamānāya, sāmaṇerassa, sāmaṇeriyā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to receive things in exchange from five kinds of people: monks, nuns, trainee nuns, novice monks, and novice nuns. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:2.8
Pali: Anujānāmi, bhikkhave, imesaṁ pañcannaṁ pārivattakaṁ paṭiggahetuṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:2.9
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:2.10.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu aññātikāya bhikkhuniyā hatthato cīvaraṁ paṭiggaṇheyya, aññatra pārivattakā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk receives a robe directly from an unrelated nun, except in exchange, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.1.5
Pali: Aññātikā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Unrelated: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.1.6
Pali: mātito vā pitito vā yāva sattamā pitāmahayugā asambaddhā. 
English (brahmali, published): anyone who is not a descendant of one’s male ancestors going back eight generations, either on the mother’s side or on the father’s side. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.1.7
Pali: Bhikkhunī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A nun: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.1.8
Pali: ubhatosaṅghe upasampannā. 
English (brahmali, published): she has been given the full ordination by both Sanghas. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.1.9
Pali: Cīvaraṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A robe: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.1.10
Pali: channaṁ cīvarānaṁ aññataraṁ cīvaraṁ vikappanupagaṁ pacchimaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one of the six kinds of robe-cloth, but not smaller than what can be assigned to another. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.1.11
Pali: Aññatra pārivattakāti 
English (brahmali, published): Except in exchange: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.1.12
Pali: ṭhapetvā pārivattakaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): unless there is an exchange. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.1.13
Pali: Paṭiggaṇhāti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he accepts, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.1.14
Pali: Paṭilābhena nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): When he gets the robe-cloth, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.1.15
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā puggalassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The robe-cloth should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.1.16
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.1.17
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bu-vb-np1#3.2.5'>Bu NP 1:3.2.5</a>–3.2.29, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.1.18
Pali: idaṁ me, bhante, cīvaraṁ aññātikāya bhikkhuniyā hatthato paṭiggahitaṁ, aññatra pārivattakā, nissaggiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this robe-cloth, which I received directly from an unrelated nun without anything in exchange, is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.1.19
Pali: Imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmīti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.1.20
Pali: dadeyyāti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.1.21
Pali: dadeyyunti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.1.22
Pali: āyasmato dammīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this robe-cloth back to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.2.1
Pali: Aññātikāya aññātikasaññī cīvaraṁ paṭiggaṇhāti, aññatra pārivattakā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated and he perceives her as such, and he receives robe-cloth from her, except in exchange, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.2.2
Pali: Aññātikāya vematiko cīvaraṁ paṭiggaṇhāti, aññatra pārivattakā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated, but he is unsure of it, and he receives robe-cloth from her, except in exchange, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.2.3
Pali: Aññātikāya ñātikasaññī cīvaraṁ paṭiggaṇhāti, aññatra pārivattakā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated, but he perceives her as related, and he receives robe-cloth from her, except in exchange, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.2.4
Pali: Ekatoupasampannāya hatthato cīvaraṁ paṭiggaṇhāti, aññatra pārivattakā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he receives robe-cloth directly from a nun who is fully ordained only on one side, except in exchange, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.2.5
Pali: Ñātikāya aññātikasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is related, but he perceives her as unrelated, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.2.6
Pali: Ñātikāya vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is related, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.2.7
Pali: Ñātikāya ñātikasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is related and he perceives her as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.3.2
Pali: ñātikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if the nun is related; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.3.3
Pali: pārivattakaṁ parittena vā vipulaṁ, vipulena vā parittaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if much is exchanged with little or little is exchanged with much; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.3.4
Pali: bhikkhu vissāsaṁ gaṇhāti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he takes it on trust; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.3.5
Pali: tāvakālikaṁ gaṇhāti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he borrows it; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.3.6
Pali: cīvaraṁ ṭhapetvā aññaṁ parikkhāraṁ gaṇhāti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he receives any requisite apart from robe-cloth; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.3.7
Pali: sikkhamānāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a trainee nun; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.3.8
Pali: sāmaṇeriyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a novice nun; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.3.9
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.3.10
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np5:3.3.11
Pali: Cīvarapaṭiggahaṇasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ pañcamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on receiving a robe from a nun, the fifth, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:0.5
Pali: 6. Aññātakaviññattisikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 6. Asking an unrelated person for a robe 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā upanando sakyaputto paṭṭo hoti dhammiṁ kathaṁ kātuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time Venerable Upananda the Sakyan was skilled at teaching. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.1.3
Pali: Atha kho aññataro seṭṭhiputto yenāyasmā upanando sakyaputto tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ upanandaṁ sakyaputtaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion the son of a wealthy merchant went to Upananda, bowed, and sat down. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.1.4
Pali: Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho taṁ seṭṭhiputtaṁ āyasmā upanando sakyaputto dhammiyā kathāya sandassesi samādapesi samuttejesi sampahaṁsesi. 
English (brahmali, published): And Upananda instructed, inspired, and gladdened him with a teaching. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.1.5
Pali: Atha kho so seṭṭhiputto āyasmatā upanandena sakyaputtena dhammiyā kathāya sandassito samādapito samuttejito sampahaṁsito āyasmantaṁ upanandaṁ sakyaputtaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Afterwards that merchant’s son said to Upananda: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.1.6
Pali: “vadeyyātha, bhante, yena attho. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, please tell me what you need. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.1.7
Pali: Paṭibalā mayaṁ ayyassa dātuṁ yadidaṁ cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhāran”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I can give you robe-cloth, almsfood, a dwelling, and medicinal supplies.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.1.8
Pali: “Sace me tvaṁ, āvuso, dātukāmosi, ito ekaṁ sāṭakaṁ dehī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “If you wish to give me something, give me one of your wrap garments.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.1.9
Pali: “Amhākaṁ kho, bhante, kulaputtānaṁ kismiṁ viya ekasāṭakaṁ gantuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s shameful, venerable, for a gentleman to walk around in only one wrap. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.1.10
Pali: Āgamehi, bhante, yāva gharaṁ gacchāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): Please wait until I get back home. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.1.11
Pali: Gharaṁ gato ito vā ekaṁ sāṭakaṁ pahiṇissāmi ito vā sundarataran”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I’ll send you this wrap or a better one.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.1.12
Pali: Dutiyampi kho āyasmā upanando sakyaputto taṁ seṭṭhiputtaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): A second time 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.1.13
Pali: “sace me tvaṁ, āvuso, dātukāmosi ito ekaṁ sāṭakaṁ dehī”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.1.14
Pali: Amhākaṁ kho bhante kulaputtānaṁ kismiṁ viya ekasāṭakaṁ gantuṁ, āgamehi, bhante yāva gharaṁ gacchāmi, gharaṁ gato ito vā ekaṁ sāṭakaṁ pahiṇissāmi ito vā sundarataranti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.1.15
Pali: Tatiyampi kho āyasmā upanando sakyaputto taṁ seṭṭhiputtaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): and a third time Upananda said 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.1.16
Pali: “sace me tvaṁ, āvuso, dātukāmosi, ito ekaṁ sāṭakaṁ dehī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): the same thing to that merchant’s son, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.1.17
Pali: Amhākaṁ kho, bhante, kulaputtānaṁ kismiṁ viya ekasāṭakaṁ gantuṁ, āgamehi, bhante yāva gharaṁ gacchāmi, gharaṁ gato ito vā ekaṁ sāṭakaṁ pahiṇissāmi ito vā sundarataranti. 
English (brahmali, published): and he got the same reply. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.1.18
Pali: “Kiṁ pana tayā, āvuso, adātukāmena pavāritena yaṁ tvaṁ pavāretvā na desī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): He then said, “What’s the point of inviting me if you don’t want to give?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.1.19
Pali: Atha kho so seṭṭhiputto āyasmatā upanandena sakyaputtena nippīḷiyamāno ekaṁ sāṭakaṁ datvā agamāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): Being pressured by Upananda, that merchant’s son gave him one of his wraps and left. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.2.1
Pali: Manussā taṁ seṭṭhiputtaṁ etadavocuṁ—“kissa tvaṁ, ayyo, ekasāṭako āgacchasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): People asked him why he was walking around in only one wrap, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.2.2
Pali: Atha kho so seṭṭhiputto tesaṁ manussānaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): and he told them what had happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.2.3
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized Upananda, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.2.4
Pali: “mahicchā ime samaṇā sakyaputtiyā asantuṭṭhā. 
English (brahmali, published): “These Sakyan monastics have great desires. They’re not content. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.2.5
Pali: Nayimesaṁ sukarā dhammanimantanāpi kātuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Even to make them an appropriate offer isn’t easy. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.2.6
Pali: Kathañhi nāma seṭṭhiputtena dhammanimantanāya kayiramānāya sāṭakaṁ gahessantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): How could they take his wrap when the merchant’s son was making an appropriate offer?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.2.7
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.2.8
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized him too, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.2.9
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā upanando sakyaputto seṭṭhiputtaṁ cīvaraṁ viññāpessatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Upananda ask the merchant’s son for a robe?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.2.10
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ upanandaṁ sakyaputtaṁ anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking him in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned Upananda: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.2.11
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, upananda, seṭṭhiputtaṁ cīvaraṁ viññāpesī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Upananda, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.2.12
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.2.13
Pali: “Ñātako te, upananda, aññātako”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is he a relative of yours?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.2.14
Pali: “Aññātako, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “No.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.2.15
Pali: “Aññātako, moghapurisa, aññātakassa na jānāti patirūpaṁ vā appatirūpaṁ vā santaṁ vā asantaṁ vā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, people who are unrelated don’t know what’s appropriate and inappropriate, what’s good and bad, in dealing with each other. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.2.16
Pali: Tattha nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, aññātakaṁ seṭṭhiputtaṁ cīvaraṁ viññāpessasi. 
English (brahmali, published): And still you did this. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.2.17
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.2.18
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.2.19.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu aññātakaṁ gahapatiṁ vā gahapatāniṁ vā cīvaraṁ viññāpeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk asks an unrelated male or female householder for a robe, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:1.2.20
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā bhikkhū sāketā sāvatthiṁ addhānamaggappaṭipannā honti. Antarāmagge corā nikkhamitvā te bhikkhū acchindiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards a number of monks who were traveling from Sāketa to Sāvatthī were robbed by bandits. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:2.2
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū—“bhagavatā paṭikkhittaṁ aññātakaṁ gahapatiṁ vā gahapatāniṁ vā cīvaraṁ viññāpetun”ti, kukkuccāyantā na viññāpesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Knowing that the Buddha had laid down this training rule and being afraid of wrongdoing, they did not ask for robes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:2.3
Pali: Yathānaggāva sāvatthiṁ gantvā bhikkhū abhivādenti. 
English (brahmali, published): As a consequence, they walked naked to Sāvatthī, where they bowed down to the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:2.4
Pali: Bhikkhū evamāhaṁsu—“sundarā kho ime, āvuso, ājīvakā ye ime bhikkhūsu abhivādentī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks there said, “These Ājīvaka ascetics are good people, as they bow down to the monks.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:2.5
Pali: Te evamāhaṁsu—“na mayaṁ, āvuso, ājīvakā, bhikkhū mayan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “We’re not Ājīvakas! We’re monks!” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:2.6
Pali: Bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ upāliṁ etadavocuṁ—“iṅghāvuso upāli, ime anuyuñjāhī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks asked Venerable Upāli to examine them. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:2.7
Pali: Atha kho āyasmatā upālinā anuyuñjiyamānā te bhikkhū etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. Atha kho āyasmā upāli te bhikkhū anuyuñjitvā bhikkhū etadavoca—“bhikkhū ime, āvuso. Detha nesaṁ cīvarānī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): When the naked monks told him what had happened, Upāli said to the monks, “They are monks. Please give them robes.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:2.8
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:2.9
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhū naggā āgacchissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can monks go naked? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:2.10
Pali: Nanu nāma tiṇena vā paṇṇena vā paṭicchādetvā āgantabban”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Shouldn’t they have covered up with grass and leaves?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:2.11
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū te anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking those monks in many ways, they told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:2.12
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards he gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:2.13
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, acchinnacīvarassa vā naṭṭhacīvarassa vā aññātakaṁ gahapatiṁ vā gahapatāniṁ vā cīvaraṁ viññāpetuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, if a monk’s robes are stolen or lost, I allow him to ask an unrelated householder for robes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:2.14
Pali: Yaṁ āvāsaṁ paṭhamaṁ upagacchati, sace tattha hoti saṅghassa vihāracīvaraṁ vā uttarattharaṇaṁ vā bhūmattharaṇaṁ vā bhisicchavi vā, taṁ gahetvā pārupituṁ ‘labhitvā odahissāmī’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): At the first monastery where he arrives, if the Sangha has a communal robe, a bedspread, a floor cover, or a mattress cover, he should take that and put it on, thinking, ‘When I get a robe, I’ll return it.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:2.15
Pali: No ce hoti saṅghassa vihāracīvaraṁ vā uttarattharaṇaṁ vā bhūmattharaṇaṁ vā bhisicchavi vā tiṇena vā paṇṇena vā paṭicchādetvā āgantabbaṁ; na tveva naggena āgantabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If there’s none of these things, he should cover up with grass and leaves before going on. He should not go on while naked. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:2.16
Pali: Yo āgaccheyya, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:2.17
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:2.18.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu aññātakaṁ gahapatiṁ vā gahapatāniṁ vā cīvaraṁ viññāpeyya, aññatra samayā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk asks an unrelated male or female householder for a robe, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:2.19
Pali: Tatthāyaṁ samayo—acchinnacīvaro vā hoti bhikkhu naṭṭhacīvaro vā. 
English (brahmali, published): These are the appropriate occasions: his robes are stolen or his robes are lost.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:2.20
Pali: Ayaṁ tattha samayo”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.1.5
Pali: Aññātako nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Unrelated: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.1.6
Pali: mātito vā pitito vā yāva sattamā pitāmahayugā asambaddho. 
English (brahmali, published): anyone who is not a descendant of one’s male ancestors going back eight generations, either on the mother’s side or on the father’s side. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.1.7
Pali: Gahapati nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A male householder: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.1.8
Pali: yo koci agāraṁ ajjhāvasati. 
English (brahmali, published): any man who lives at home. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.1.9
Pali: Gahapatānī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A female householder: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.1.10
Pali: yā kāci agāraṁ ajjhāvasati. 
English (brahmali, published): any woman who lives at home. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.1.11
Pali: Cīvaraṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A robe: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.1.12
Pali: channaṁ cīvarānaṁ aññataraṁ cīvaraṁ vikappanupagaṁ pacchimaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one of the six kinds of robe-cloth, but not smaller than what can be assigned to another. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.1.13
Pali: Aññatra samayāti 
English (brahmali, published): Except on an appropriate occasion: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.1.14
Pali: ṭhapetvā samayaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): unless it is an appropriate occasion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.1.15
Pali: Acchinnacīvaro nāma 
English (brahmali, published): His robes are stolen: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.1.16
Pali: bhikkhussa cīvaraṁ acchinnaṁ hoti rājūhi vā corehi vā dhuttehi vā, yehi kehici vā acchinnaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): a monk’s robe is taken by kings, bandits, scoundrels, or whoever. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.1.17
Pali: Naṭṭhacīvaro nāma 
English (brahmali, published): His robes are lost: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.1.18
Pali: bhikkhussa cīvaraṁ agginā vā daḍḍhaṁ hoti, udakena vā vūḷhaṁ hoti, undūrehi vā upacikāhi vā khāyitaṁ hoti, paribhogajiṇṇaṁ vā hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): a monk’s robe is burned by fire, carried away by flooding, eaten by rats or termites, or worn through use. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.2.1
Pali: Aññatra samayā viññāpeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he asks, except on an appropriate occasion, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.2.2
Pali: Paṭilābhena nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): When he gets the robe-cloth, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.2.3
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā puggalassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The robe-cloth should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.2.4
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.2.5
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bu-vb-np1#3.2.5'>Bu NP 1:3.2.5</a>–3.2.29, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.2.6
Pali: idaṁ me, bhante, cīvaraṁ aññātakaṁ gahapatikaṁ, aññatra samayā viññāpitaṁ, nissaggiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this robe-cloth, which I received after asking an unrelated householder, but not on an appropriate occasion, is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.2.7
Pali: Imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmīti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.2.8
Pali: dadeyyāti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.2.9
Pali: dadeyyunti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.2.10
Pali: āyasmato dammīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this robe-cloth back to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.3.1
Pali: Aññātake aññātakasaññī aññatra samayā cīvaraṁ viññāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If the person is unrelated and the monk perceives them as such, and he asks them for robe-cloth, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.3.2
Pali: Aññātake vematiko aññatra samayā cīvaraṁ viññāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If the person is unrelated, but the monk is unsure of it, and he asks them for robe-cloth, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.3.3
Pali: Aññātake ñātakasaññī aññatra samayā cīvaraṁ viññāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If the person is unrelated, but the monk perceives them as related, and he asks them for robe-cloth, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.3.4
Pali: Ñātake aññātakasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the person is related, but the monk perceives them as unrelated, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.3.5
Pali: Ñātake vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the person is related, but the monk is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.3.6
Pali: Ñātake ñātakasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If the person is related and the monk perceives them as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.4.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.4.2
Pali: samaye, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is an appropriate occasion; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.4.3
Pali: ñātakānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if he asks relatives; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.4.4
Pali: pavāritānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if he asks those who have given an invitation; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.4.5
Pali: aññassatthāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if he asks for the benefit of someone else; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.4.6
Pali: attano dhanena, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is by means of his own property; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.4.7
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.4.8
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np6:3.4.9
Pali: Aññātakaviññattisikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ chaṭṭhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on asking an unrelated person for a robe, the sixth, is finished.
