pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:0.5
Pali: 7. Tatuttarisikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 7. Accepting too many robes 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū acchinnacīvarake bhikkhū upasaṅkamitvā evaṁ vadanti—
English (brahmali, published): the monks from the group of six said to the monks whose robes had been stolen, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.3
Pali: “bhagavatā, āvuso, anuññātaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): “The Buddha has allowed 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.4
Pali: ‘acchinnacīvarassa vā naṭṭhacīvarassa vā aññātakaṁ gahapatiṁ vā gahapatāniṁ vā cīvaraṁ viññāpetuṁ’; 
English (brahmali, published): a monk whose robes have been stolen or lost to ask an unrelated householder for a robe. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.5
Pali: viññāpetha, āvuso, cīvaran”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): You should ask for a robe.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.6
Pali: “Alaṁ, āvuso, laddhaṁ amhehi cīvaran”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s not necessary. We’ve already gotten robes.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.7
Pali: “Mayaṁ āyasmantānaṁ viññāpemā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “We’ll ask for you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.8
Pali: “Viññāpetha, āvuso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Do as you please.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.9
Pali: Atha kho chabbaggiyā bhikkhū gahapatike upasaṅkamitvā etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): The monks from the group of six then went to householders and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.10
Pali: “acchinnacīvarakā, āvuso, bhikkhū āgatā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks have arrived whose robes have been stolen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.11
Pali: Detha nesaṁ cīvarānī”ti, 
English (brahmali, published): Please give them robes.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.12
Pali: bahuṁ cīvaraṁ viññāpesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): And they asked for many robes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.13
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro puriso sabhāyaṁ nisinno aññataraṁ purisaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards in the public meeting hall a certain man said to another, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.14
Pali: “acchinnacīvarakā ayyo bhikkhū āgatā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, monks have arrived whose robes have been stolen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.15
Pali: Tesaṁ mayā cīvaraṁ dinnan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I’ve given them robes.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.16
Pali: Sopi evamāha—“mayāpi dinnan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The other replied, “So have I.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.17
Pali: Aparopi evamāha—“mayāpi dinnan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): And another said the same. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.18
Pali: Te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): They complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.19
Pali: “kathañhi nāma samaṇā sakyaputtiyā na mattaṁ jānitvā bahuṁ cīvaraṁ viññāpessanti, 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the Sakyan monastics ask for many robes without moderation? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.20
Pali: dussavāṇijjaṁ vā samaṇā sakyaputtiyā karissanti, paggāhikasālaṁ vā pasāressantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Are they going to trade in cloth or set up shop?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.21
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.22
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.23
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū na mattaṁ jānitvā bahuṁ cīvaraṁ viññāpessantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the monks from the group of six ask for many robes without moderation?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.24
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū chabbaggiye bhikkhū anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking those monks in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.25
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, na mattaṁ jānitvā bahuṁ cīvaraṁ viññāpethā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.26
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.27
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.28
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, na mattaṁ jānitvā bahuṁ cīvaraṁ viññāpessatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.29
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.30
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:1.31.1
Pali: “Tañce aññātako gahapati vā gahapatānī vā bahūhi cīvarehi abhihaṭṭhuṁ pavāreyya santaruttaraparamaṁ tena bhikkhunā tato cīvaraṁ sāditabbaṁ. Tato ce uttari sādiyeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If an unrelated male or female householder invites that monk to take many robes, he should accept at most one sarong and one upper robe. If he accepts more than that, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.1.1
Pali: Tañceti 
English (brahmali, published): That monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.1.2
Pali: acchinnacīvarakaṁ bhikkhuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): the monk whose robes have been stolen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.1.3
Pali: Aññātako nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Unrelated: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.1.4
Pali: mātito vā pitito vā yāva sattamā pitāmahayugā asambaddho. 
English (brahmali, published): anyone who is not a descendant of one’s male ancestors going back eight generations, either on the mother’s side or on the father’s side. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.1.5
Pali: Gahapati nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A male householder: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.1.6
Pali: yo koci agāraṁ ajjhāvasati. 
English (brahmali, published): any man who lives at home. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.1.7
Pali: Gahapatānī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A female householder: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.1.8
Pali: yā kāci agāraṁ ajjhāvasati. 
English (brahmali, published): any woman who lives at home. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.1.9
Pali: Bahūhi cīvarehīti 
English (brahmali, published): Many robes: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.1.10
Pali: bahukehi cīvarehi. 
English (brahmali, published): a lot of robes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.1.11
Pali: Abhihaṭṭhuṁ pavāreyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Invites to take: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.1.12
Pali: yāvatakaṁ icchasi tāvatakaṁ gaṇhāhīti. 
English (brahmali, published): saying, “Take as many as you like.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.1.13
Pali: Santaruttaraparamaṁ tena bhikkhunā tato cīvaraṁ sāditabbanti 
English (brahmali, published): He should accept at most one sarong and one upper robe: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.1.14
Pali: sace tīṇi naṭṭhāni honti dve sāditabbāni, dve naṭṭhāni ekaṁ sāditabbaṁ, ekaṁ naṭṭhaṁ na kiñci sāditabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if three robes are lost, he should accept two; if two robes are lost, he should accept one; if one robe is lost, he should not accept any. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.1.15
Pali: Tato ce uttari sādiyeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): If he accepts more than that: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.1.16
Pali: tatuttari viññāpeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if he asks for more than that, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.1.17
Pali: Paṭilābhena nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): When he gets the robe, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.1.18
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā puggalassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The robe should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.1.19
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.1.20
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bu-vb-np1#3.2.5'>Bu NP 1:3.2.5</a>–3.2.29, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.1.21
Pali: “idaṁ me, bhante, cīvaraṁ aññātakaṁ gahapatikaṁ upasaṅkamitvā tatuttari viññāpitaṁ nissaggiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this robe, which I received after asking an unrelated householder for too many, is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.1.22
Pali: Imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.1.23
Pali: dadeyyāti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.1.24
Pali: dadeyyunti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.1.25
Pali: āyasmato dammīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this robe back to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.2.1.1
Pali: Aññātake aññātakasaññī tatuttari cīvaraṁ viññāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If the person is unrelated and the monk perceives them as such, and he asks them for too many robes, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.2.2
Pali: Aññātake vematiko tatuttari cīvaraṁ viññāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If the person is unrelated, but the monk is unsure of it, and he asks them for too many robes, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.2.3
Pali: Aññātake ñātakasaññī tatuttari cīvaraṁ viññāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If the person is unrelated, but the monk perceives them as related, and he asks them for too many robes, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.2.4
Pali: Ñātake aññātakasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the person is related, but the monk perceives them as unrelated, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.2.5
Pali: Ñātake vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the person is related, but the monk is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.2.6
Pali: Ñātake ñātakasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If the person is related and the monk perceives them as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.3.2
Pali: “sesakaṁ āharissāmī”ti haranto gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he takes too much, but with the intention of returning the remainder; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.3.3
Pali: “sesakaṁ tuyheva hotū”ti denti, 
English (brahmali, published): if they give, saying, “The remainder is for you;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.3.4
Pali: na acchinnakāraṇā denti, 
English (brahmali, published): if they give, but not because his robes were stolen; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.3.5
Pali: na naṭṭhakāraṇā denti, 
English (brahmali, published): if they give, but not because his robes were lost; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.3.6
Pali: ñātakānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is from relatives; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.3.7
Pali: pavāritānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is from those who have given an invitation; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.3.8
Pali: attano dhanena, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is by means of his own property; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.3.9
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.3.10
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np7:2.3.11
Pali: Tatuttarisikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ sattamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on accepting too many robes, the seventh, is finished.

Pali: 8. Upakkhaṭasikkhāpada

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

English (brahmali, published): a certain man said to his wife,

Pali: “ayyaṁ upanandaṁ cīvarena acchādessāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I’m going to give robe-cloth to Venerable Upananda.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.4
Pali: Assosi kho aññataro piṇḍacāriko bhikkhu tassa purisassa imaṁ vācaṁ bhāsamānassa. 
English (brahmali, published): An alms-collecting monk heard that man making that statement. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.5
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu yenāyasmā upanando sakyaputto tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ upanandaṁ sakyaputtaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): He then went to Upananda the Sakyan and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.6
Pali: “mahāpuññosi tvaṁ, āvuso upananda. 
English (brahmali, published): “Upananda, you have much merit.

Pali: Amukasmiṁ okāse aññataro puriso pajāpatiṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): In such-and-such a place I heard a man tell his wife 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.8
Pali: ‘ayyaṁ upanandaṁ cīvarena acchādessāmī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): that he’s going to give you robe-cloth.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.9
Pali: “Atthāvuso, maṁ so upaṭṭhāko”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “He’s my supporter.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.10

English (brahmali, published): Upananda then went to that man and said,

Pali: “saccaṁ kira maṁ tvaṁ, āvuso, cīvarena acchādetukāmosī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true that you want to give me robe-cloth?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.12
Pali: “Api meyya, evaṁ hoti—
English (brahmali, published): “Yes, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.13
Pali: ‘ayyaṁ upanandaṁ cīvarena acchādessāmī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): that’s just what I was thinking.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.14
Pali: “Sace kho maṁ tvaṁ, āvuso, cīvarena acchādetukāmosi, evarūpena cīvarena acchādehi. 
English (brahmali, published): “If that’s the case, give me such-and-such robe-cloth. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.15
Pali: Kyāhaṁ tena acchannopi karissāmi yāhaṁ na paribhuñjissāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): For what’s the point of giving robe-cloth that I won’t use?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.16
Pali: Atha kho so puriso ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—
English (brahmali, published): That man complained and criticized him,

Pali: “mahicchā ime samaṇā sakyaputtiyā asantuṭṭhā. 
English (brahmali, published): “These Sakyan monastics have great desires. They’re not content. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.18
Pali: Nayime sukarā cīvarena acchādetuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): It’s no easy matter to give them robe-cloth. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.19
Pali: Kathañhi nāma ayyo upanando mayā pubbe appavārito maṁ upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): How could Venerable Upananda come to me and say what kind of robe-cloth he wants without first being invited by me to do so?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.20
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tassa purisassa ujjhāyantassa khiyyantassa vipācentassa. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of that man, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.21
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.22
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā upanando sakyaputto pubbe appavārito gahapatikaṁ upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Upananda go to a householder and say what kind of robe-cloth he wants without first being invited to do so?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.23
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ upanandaṁ sakyaputtaṁ anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking him in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned Upananda: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.24
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, upananda, pubbe appavārito gahapatikaṁ upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Upananda, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.25
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.26
Pali: “Ñātako te, upananda, aññātako”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is he a relative of yours?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.27
Pali: “Aññātako, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “No.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.28
Pali: “Aññātako, moghapurisa, aññātakassa na jānāti patirūpaṁ vā appatirūpaṁ vā santaṁ vā asantaṁ vā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, people who are unrelated don’t know what’s appropriate and inappropriate, what’s good and bad, in dealing with each other. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.29
Pali: Tattha nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, pubbe appavārito aññātakaṁ gahapatikaṁ upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjissasi. 
English (brahmali, published): And still you did this. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.30
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.31
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.32.1
Pali: “Bhikkhuṁ paneva uddissa aññātakassa gahapatissa vā gahapatāniyā vā cīvaracetāpannaṁ upakkhaṭaṁ hoti—
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a male or female householder has set aside a robe fund for an unrelated monk, thinking, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.33
Pali: ‘iminā cīvaracetāpannena cīvaraṁ cetāpetvā itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuṁ cīvarena acchādessāmī’ti; 
English (brahmali, published): “With this robe fund I will buy robe-cloth and give it to monk so-and-so;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.34
Pali: tatra ce so bhikkhu pubbe appavārito upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjeyya—
English (brahmali, published): and if that monk, without first being invited, goes to them and specifies the kind of robe-cloth he wants, saying, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.35
Pali: ‘sādhu vata maṁ āyasmā iminā cīvaracetāpannena evarūpaṁ vā evarūpaṁ vā cīvaraṁ cetāpetvā acchādehī’ti, 
English (brahmali, published): “Please use this robe fund to buy such-and-such robe-cloth and then give it to me,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:1.1.36
Pali: kalyāṇakamyataṁ upādāya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): and he does so because he wants something fine, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.1
Pali: Bhikkhuṁ paneva uddissāti 
English (brahmali, published): For a monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.2
Pali: bhikkhussatthāya, bhikkhuṁ ārammaṇaṁ karitvā, bhikkhuṁ acchādetukāmo. 
English (brahmali, published): for the benefit of a monk; making a monk the object of consideration, one wants to give to him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.3
Pali: Aññātako nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Unrelated: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.4
Pali: mātito vā pitito vā yāva sattamā pitāmahayugā asambaddho. 
English (brahmali, published): anyone who is not a descendant of one’s male ancestors going back eight generations, either on the mother’s side or on the father’s side. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.5
Pali: Gahapati nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A male householder: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.6
Pali: yo koci agāraṁ ajjhāvasati. 
English (brahmali, published): any man who lives at home. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.7
Pali: Gahapatānī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A female householder: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.8
Pali: yā kāci agāraṁ ajjhāvasati. 
English (brahmali, published): any woman who lives at home. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.9
Pali: Cīvaracetāpannaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A robe fund: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.10
Pali: hiraññaṁ vā suvaṇṇaṁ vā muttā vā maṇi vā pavāḷo vā phaliko vā paṭako vā suttaṁ vā kappāso vā. 
English (brahmali, published): money, gold, a pearl, a gem, a coral, a crystal, cloth, thread, or cotton wool. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.11
Pali: Iminā cīvaracetāpannenāti 
English (brahmali, published): With this robe fund: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.12
Pali: paccupaṭṭhitena. 
English (brahmali, published): with that which is at one’s disposal. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.13
Pali: Cetāpetvāti 
English (brahmali, published): I will buy: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.14
Pali: parivattetvā. 
English (brahmali, published): having traded. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.15
Pali: Acchādessāmīti 
English (brahmali, published): I will give: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.16
Pali: dassāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): I will donate. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.17
Pali: Tatra ce so bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): If that monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.18
Pali: yaṁ bhikkhuṁ uddissa cīvaracetāpannaṁ upakkhaṭaṁ hoti so bhikkhu. 
English (brahmali, published): the monk the robe fund has been set aside for. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.19
Pali: Pubbe appavāritoti 
English (brahmali, published): Without first being invited: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.20
Pali: pubbe avutto hoti— “kīdisena te, bhante, cīvarena attho, kīdisaṁ te cīvaraṁ cetāpemī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): he or she has not said beforehand: “Venerable, what kind of robe-cloth do you need? What kind of robe-cloth can I buy for you?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.21
Pali: Upasaṅkamitvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Goes to them: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.22
Pali: gharaṁ gantvā yattha katthaci upasaṅkamitvā. 
English (brahmali, published): having gone to their house or having gone wherever. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.23
Pali: Cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Specifies the kind of robe-cloth he wants: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.24
Pali: āyataṁ vā hotu vitthataṁ vā appitaṁ vā saṇhaṁ vā. 
English (brahmali, published): please make it long or wide or closely woven or soft. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.25
Pali: Iminā cīvaracetāpannenāti 
English (brahmali, published): This robe fund: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.26
Pali: paccupaṭṭhitena. 
English (brahmali, published): that which is at one’s disposal. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.27
Pali: Evarūpaṁ vā evarūpaṁ vāti. 
English (brahmali, published): Such-and-such: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.28
Pali: Āyataṁ vā vitthataṁ vā appitaṁ vā saṇhaṁ vā. 
English (brahmali, published): long or wide or closely woven or soft. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.29
Pali: Cetāpetvāti 
English (brahmali, published): To buy: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.30
Pali: parivattetvā. 
English (brahmali, published): having traded. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.31
Pali: Acchādehīti 
English (brahmali, published): Give: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.32
Pali: dajjehi. 
English (brahmali, published): donate. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.33
Pali: Kalyāṇakamyataṁ upādāyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Because he wants something fine: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.34
Pali: sādhatthiko mahagghatthiko. 
English (brahmali, published): wanting something good, wanting something expensive. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.35
Pali: Tassa vacanena āyataṁ vā vitthataṁ vā appitaṁ vā saṇhaṁ vā cetāpeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If that householder buys robe-cloth that is long, wide, closely woven, or soft because of the monk’s statement, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.36
Pali: Paṭilābhena nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): When he gets the robe-cloth, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.37
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā puggalassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The robe-cloth should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.38
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.39
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bu-vb-np1#3.2.5'>Bu NP 1:3.2.5</a>–3.2.29, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.40
Pali: “idaṁ me, bhante, cīvaraṁ pubbe appavārito aññātakaṁ gahapatikaṁ upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpannaṁ nissaggiyaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this robe-cloth, which I received after going to an unrelated householder and saying what kind of robe-cloth I wanted without first being invited, is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.41
Pali: imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.42
Pali: dadeyyāti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.43
Pali: dadeyyunti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.1.44
Pali: āyasmato dammīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this robe-cloth back to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.2.1
Pali: Aññātake aññātakasaññī pubbe appavārito gahapatikaṁ upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjati, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If the householder is unrelated and the monk perceives them as such, and, without first being invited, he goes to them and specifies the kind of robe-cloth he wants, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.2.2
Pali: Aññātake vematiko pubbe appavārito gahapatikaṁ upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjati, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If the householder is unrelated, but the monk is unsure of it, and, without first being invited, he goes to them and specifies the kind of robe-cloth he wants, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.2.3
Pali: Aññātake ñātakasaññī pubbe appavārito gahapatikaṁ upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjati, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If the householder is unrelated, but the monk perceives them as related, and, without first being invited, he goes to them and specifies the kind of robe-cloth he wants, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.2.4
Pali: Ñātake aññātakasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the householder is related, but the monk perceives them as unrelated, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.2.5
Pali: Ñātake vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the householder is related, but the monk is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.2.6
Pali: Ñātake ñātakasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If the householder is related and the monk perceives them as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.3.2
Pali: ñātakānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is from relatives; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.3.3
Pali: pavāritānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is from those who have given an invitation; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.3.4
Pali: aññassatthāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is for the benefit of another; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.3.5
Pali: attano dhanena, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is by means of his own property; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.3.6
Pali: mahagghaṁ cetāpetukāmassa appagghaṁ cetāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if the householder wishes to buy something expensive, but the monk has them buy something inexpensive; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.3.7
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.3.8
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np8:2.3.9
Pali: Upakkhaṭasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ aṭṭhamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on requesting a fine robe from a fund, the eighth, is finished.

English (brahmali, published): 9. Requesting a fine robe from a combined fund 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro puriso aññataraṁ purisaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): a certain man said to another man, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.3
Pali: “ayyaṁ upanandaṁ cīvarena acchādessāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I’m going to give robe-cloth to Venerable Upananda.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.4
Pali: Sopi evamāha—
English (brahmali, published): And he replied, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.5
Pali: “ahampi ayyaṁ upanandaṁ cīvarena acchādessāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “So am I.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.6
Pali: Assosi kho aññataro piṇḍacāriko bhikkhu tesaṁ purisānaṁ imaṁ kathāsallāpaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): An alms-collecting monk heard that conversation. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.7
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu yenāyasmā upanando sakyaputto tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ upanandaṁ sakyaputtaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): He then went to Upananda the Sakyan and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.8
Pali: “mahāpuññosi tvaṁ, āvuso upananda. 
English (brahmali, published): “Upananda, you have much merit. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.9
Pali: Amukasmiṁ okāse aññataro puriso aññataraṁ purisaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): In such-and-such a place I heard two men telling each other 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.10
Pali: ‘ayyaṁ upanandaṁ cīvarena acchādessāmī’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): that they’re each going to give you robe-cloth.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.11
Pali: Sopi evamāha—
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.12
Pali: ‘ahampi ayyaṁ upanandaṁ cīvarena acchādessāmī’”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.13
Pali: “Atthāvuso, maṁ te upaṭṭhākā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “They are my supporters.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.14
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā upanando sakyaputto yena te purisā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā te purise etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Upananda then went to those men and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.15
Pali: “saccaṁ kira maṁ tumhe, āvuso, cīvarehi acchādetukāmātthā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true that you want to give me robe-cloth?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.16
Pali: “Api nayya, evaṁ hoti—
English (brahmali, published): “Yes, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.17
Pali: ‘ayyaṁ upanandaṁ cīvarehi acchādessāmā’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): that’s just what we were thinking.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.18
Pali: “Sace kho maṁ tumhe, āvuso, cīvarehi acchādetukāmāttha, evarūpena cīvarena acchādetha, 
English (brahmali, published): “If that’s the case, then give me such-and-such robe-cloth. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.19
Pali: kyāhaṁ tehi acchannopi karissāmi, yānāhaṁ na paribhuñjissāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): For what’s the point of giving robe-cloth that I won’t use?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.20
Pali: Atha kho te purisā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): Those men complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.21
Pali: “mahicchā ime samaṇā sakyaputtiyā asantuṭṭhā. 
English (brahmali, published): “These Sakyan monastics have great desires. They’re not content. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.22
Pali: Nayime sukarā cīvarehi acchādetuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): It’s no easy matter to give them robe-cloth. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.23
Pali: Kathañhi nāma ayyo upanando amhehi pubbe appavārito upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): How could Venerable Upananda come to us and say what kind of robe-cloth he wants without first being invited by us to do so?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.24
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ purisānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those men, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.25
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.26
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā upanando sakyaputto pubbe appavārito gahapatike upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Upananda go to householders and say what kind of robe-cloth he wants without first being invited to do so?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.27
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ upanandaṁ sakyaputtaṁ anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking him in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned Upananda: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.28
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, upananda, pubbe appavārito gahapatike upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Upananda, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.29
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.30
Pali: “Ñātakā te, upananda, aññātakā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Are they relatives of yours?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.31
Pali: “Aññātakā, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “No.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.32
Pali: “Aññātako, moghapurisa, aññātakānaṁ na jānāti patirūpaṁ vā appatirūpaṁ vā santaṁ vā asantaṁ vā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, people who are unrelated don’t know what’s appropriate and inappropriate, what’s good and bad, in dealing with each other. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.33
Pali: Tattha nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, pubbe appavārito aññātake gahapatike upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjissasi. 
English (brahmali, published): And still you did this. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.34
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.35
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.36.1
Pali: “Bhikkhuṁ paneva uddissa ubhinnaṁ aññātakānaṁ gahapatīnaṁ vā gahapatānīnaṁ vā paccekacīvaracetāpannāni upakkhaṭāni honti—
English (brahmali, published): ‘If two male or female householders have set aside separate robe funds for an unrelated monk, thinking, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.37
Pali: ‘imehi mayaṁ paccekacīvaracetāpannehi paccekacīvarāni cetāpetvā itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuṁ cīvarehi acchādessāmā’ti; 
English (brahmali, published): “With these separate robe funds we’ll buy separate robe-cloths and give them to monk so-and-so;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.38
Pali: tatra ce so bhikkhu pubbe appavārito upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjeyya—
English (brahmali, published): and if that monk, without first being invited, goes to them and specifies the kind of robe-cloth he wants, saying, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.39
Pali: ‘sādhu vata maṁ āyasmanto imehi paccekacīvaracetāpannehi evarūpaṁ vā evarūpaṁ vā cīvaraṁ cetāpetvā acchādetha, ubhova santā ekenā’ti, 
English (brahmali, published): “Please put these separate robe funds together to buy such-and-such robe-cloth and then give it to me,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:1.40
Pali: kalyāṇakamyataṁ upādāya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): and he does so because he wants something fine, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.1
Pali: Bhikkhuṁ paneva uddissāti 
English (brahmali, published): For a monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.2
Pali: bhikkhussatthāya, bhikkhuṁ ārammaṇaṁ karitvā, bhikkhuṁ acchādetukāmā. 
English (brahmali, published): for the benefit of a monk; making a monk the object of consideration, they want to give to him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.3
Pali: Ubhinnanti 
English (brahmali, published): Two: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.4
Pali: dvinnaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): a pair. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.5
Pali: Aññātakā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Unrelated: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.6
Pali: mātito vā pitito vā yāva sattamā pitāmahayugā asambaddhā. 
English (brahmali, published): anyone who is not a descendant of one’s male ancestors going back eight generations, either on the mother’s side or on the father’s side. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.7
Pali: Gahapatī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Male householders: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.8
Pali: ye keci agāraṁ ajjhāvasanti. 
English (brahmali, published): any men who live at home. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.9
Pali: Gahapatāniyo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Female householders: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.10
Pali: yā kāci agāraṁ ajjhāvasanti. 
English (brahmali, published): any women who live at home. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.11
Pali: Cīvaracetāpannāni nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Robe funds: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.12
Pali: hiraññā vā suvaṇṇā vā muttā vā maṇī vā pavāḷā vā phalikā vā paṭakā vā suttā vā kappāsā vā. 
English (brahmali, published): money, gold, pearls, gems, corals, crystals, cloth, thread, or cotton wool. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.13
Pali: Imehi paccekacīvaracetāpannehīti 
English (brahmali, published): With these separate robe funds: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.14
Pali: paccupaṭṭhitehi. 
English (brahmali, published): with that which is at their disposal. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.15
Pali: Cetāpetvāti 
English (brahmali, published): We’ll buy: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.16
Pali: parivattetvā. 
English (brahmali, published): having traded. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.17
Pali: Acchādessāmāti 
English (brahmali, published): We’ll give: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.18
Pali: dassāma. 
English (brahmali, published): we’ll donate. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.19
Pali: Tatra ce so bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): If that monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.20
Pali: yaṁ bhikkhuṁ uddissa cīvaracetāpannāni upakkhaṭāni honti so bhikkhu. 
English (brahmali, published): the monk the robe funds have been set aside for. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.21
Pali: Pubbe appavāritoti 
English (brahmali, published): Without first being invited: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.22
Pali: pubbe avutto hoti— “kīdisena te, bhante, cīvarena attho, kīdisaṁ te cīvaraṁ cetāpemā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): they have not said beforehand: “Venerable, what kind of robe-cloth do you need? What kind of robe-cloth can we buy for you?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.23
Pali: Upasaṅkamitvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Goes to them: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.24
Pali: gharaṁ gantvā yattha katthaci upasaṅkamitvā. 
English (brahmali, published): having gone to their house or having gone wherever. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.25
Pali: Cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Specifies the kind of robe-cloth he wants: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.26
Pali: āyataṁ vā hotu vitthataṁ vā appitaṁ vā saṇhaṁ vā. 
English (brahmali, published): please make it long or wide or closely woven or soft. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.27
Pali: Imehi paccekacīvaracetāpannehīti 
English (brahmali, published): These separate robe funds: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.28
Pali: paccupaṭṭhitehi. 
English (brahmali, published): that which is at their disposal. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.29
Pali: Evarūpaṁ vā evarūpaṁ vāti 
English (brahmali, published): Such-and-such: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.30
Pali: āyataṁ vā vitthataṁ vā appitaṁ vā saṇhaṁ vā. 
English (brahmali, published): long or wide or closely woven or soft. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.31
Pali: Cetāpetvāti 
English (brahmali, published): To buy: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.32
Pali: parivattetvā. 
English (brahmali, published): having traded. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.33
Pali: Acchādethāti 
English (brahmali, published): Give: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.34
Pali: dajjetha. 
English (brahmali, published): donate. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.35
Pali: Ubhova santā ekenāti 
English (brahmali, published): Put together: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.36
Pali: dvepi janā ekena. 
English (brahmali, published): two people supplying him with one robe-cloth. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.37
Pali: Kalyāṇakamyataṁ upādāyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Because he wants something fine: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.38
Pali: sādhatthiko mahagghatthiko. 
English (brahmali, published): wanting something good, wanting something expensive. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.39
Pali: Tassa vacanena āyataṁ vā vitthataṁ vā appitaṁ vā saṇhaṁ vā cetāpenti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If those householders buy robe-cloth that is long, wide, closely woven, or soft because of his statement, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.40
Pali: Paṭilābhena nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): When he gets the robe-cloth, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.41
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā puggalassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The robe-cloth should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.42
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.43
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bu-vb-np1#3.2.5'>Bu NP 1:3.2.5</a>–3.2.29, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.44
Pali: “idaṁ me, bhante, cīvaraṁ pubbe appavārito aññātake gahapatike upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpannaṁ nissaggiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this robe-cloth, which I received after going to unrelated householders and saying what kind of robe-cloth I wanted without first being invited, is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.45
Pali: Imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.46
Pali: dadeyyāti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.47
Pali: dadeyyunti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.48
Pali: āyasmato dammīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this robe-cloth back to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.49.1
Pali: Aññātake aññātakasaññī pubbe appavārito gahapatike upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjati, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If the householders are unrelated and he perceives them as such, and, without first being invited, he goes to them and specifies the kind of robe-cloth he wants, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.50
Pali: Aññātake vematiko pubbe appavārito gahapatike upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjati, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If the householders are unrelated, but he is unsure of it, and, without first being invited, he goes to them and specifies the kind of robe-cloth he wants, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.51
Pali: Aññātake ñātakasaññī pubbe appavārito gahapatike upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjati, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If the householders are unrelated, but he perceives them as related, and, without first being invited, he goes to them and specifies the kind of robe-cloth he wants, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.52
Pali: Ñātake aññātakasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the householders are related, but he perceives them as unrelated, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.53
Pali: Ñātake vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the householders are related, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.54
Pali: Ñātake ñātakasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If the householders are related and he perceives them as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.55.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.56
Pali: ñātakānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is from relatives; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.57
Pali: pavāritānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is from those who have given an invitation; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.58
Pali: aññassatthāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is for the benefit of another; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.59
Pali: attano dhanena, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is by means of his own property; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.60
Pali: mahagghaṁ cetāpetukāmānaṁ appagghaṁ cetāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if the householders wish to buy something expensive, but he has them buy something inexpensive; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.61
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.62
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np9:2.63
Pali: Dutiyaupakkhaṭasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ navamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The second training rule on requesting a fine robe from a combined fund, the ninth, is finished.

Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis

English (brahmali, published): The chapter on relinquishment 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:0.4
Pali: Cīvaravagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on robes 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:0.5
Pali: 10. Rājasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 10. Excessive prompting to get a robe 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmato upanandassa sakyaputtassa upaṭṭhāko mahāmatto āyasmato upanandassa sakyaputtassa dūtena cīvaracetāpannaṁ pāhesi—
English (brahmali, published): At that time a certain government official was a supporter of Venerable Upananda the Sakyan. On one occasion that official sent a robe fund by messenger, saying, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.1.3
Pali: “iminā cīvaracetāpannena cīvaraṁ cetāpetvā ayyaṁ upanandaṁ cīvarena acchādehī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Buy robe-cloth with this fund and give it to Venerable Upananda.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.1.4
Pali: Atha kho so dūto yenāyasmā upanando sakyaputto tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ upanandaṁ sakyaputtaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): The messenger went to Upananda and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.1.5
Pali: “idaṁ kho, bhante, āyasmantaṁ uddissa cīvaracetāpannaṁ ābhataṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, I’ve brought a robe fund for you. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.1.6
Pali: Paṭiggaṇhātu āyasmā cīvaracetāpannan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Please receive it.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.1.7
Pali: Evaṁ vutte āyasmā upanando sakyaputto taṁ dūtaṁ etadavoca—
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.1.8
Pali: “na kho mayaṁ, āvuso, cīvaracetāpannaṁ paṭiggaṇhāma, cīvarañca kho mayaṁ paṭiggaṇhāma kālena kappiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “We don’t receive robe funds, but we do receive allowable robe-cloth at the right time.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.1.9
Pali: Evaṁ vutte, so dūto āyasmantaṁ upanandaṁ sakyaputtaṁ etadavoca—
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.1.10
Pali: “atthi panāyasmato koci veyyāvaccakaro”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is there anyone who provides services for you?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.1.11
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro upāsako ārāmaṁ agamāsi kenacideva karaṇīyena. 
English (brahmali, published): Just then a lay follower had come to the monastery on some business. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.1.12
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā upanando sakyaputto taṁ dūtaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Upananda told the messenger, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.1.13
Pali: “eso kho, āvuso, upāsako bhikkhūnaṁ veyyāvaccakaro”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “This lay follower provides services for the monks.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.1.14
Pali: Atha kho so dūto taṁ upāsakaṁ saññāpetvā yenāyasmā upanando sakyaputto tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ upanandaṁ sakyaputtaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): The messenger instructed that lay follower and then returned to Upananda, saying, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.1.15
Pali: “yaṁ kho, bhante, āyasmā veyyāvaccakaraṁ niddisi saññatto so mayā. 
English (brahmali, published): “I’ve instructed the lay follower you pointed out to me. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.1.16
Pali: Upasaṅkamatu āyasmā kālena, cīvarena taṁ acchādessatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Please go to them at the right time and they’ll give you robe-cloth.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.1.17
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena so mahāmatto āyasmato upanandassa sakyaputtassa santike dūtaṁ pāhesi—
English (brahmali, published): Later on that government official sent a message to Upananda, saying, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.1.18
Pali: “paribhuñjatu ayyo taṁ cīvaraṁ, icchāma mayaṁ ayyena taṁ cīvaraṁ paribhuttan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please use the robe-cloth. I would like you to use the robe-cloth.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.1.19
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā upanando sakyaputto taṁ upāsakaṁ na kiñci avacāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): When Upananda did not say anything to that lay follower, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.1.20
Pali: Dutiyampi kho so mahāmatto āyasmato upanandassa sakyaputtassa santike dūtaṁ pāhesi—
English (brahmali, published): that government official sent him a second message 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.1.21
Pali: “paribhuñjatu ayyo taṁ cīvaraṁ, icchāma mayaṁ ayyena taṁ cīvaraṁ paribhuttan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): saying the same thing. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.1.22
Pali: Dutiyampi kho āyasmā upanando sakyaputto taṁ upāsakaṁ na kiñci avacāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): When Upananda still did not say anything to that lay follower, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.1.23
Pali: Tatiyampi kho so mahāmatto āyasmato upanandassa sakyaputtassa santike dūtaṁ pāhesi—
English (brahmali, published): that government official sent him a third message. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.1.24
Pali: “paribhuñjatu ayyo taṁ cīvaraṁ, icchāma mayaṁ ayyena taṁ cīvaraṁ paribhuttan”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena negamassa samayo hoti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.2.2
Pali: Negamena ca katikā katā hoti— “yo pacchā āgacchati paññāsaṁ baddho”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the householder association had made an agreement that whoever came late to a meeting would be fined fifty coins. And now they were having a meeting. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.2.3
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā upanando sakyaputto yena so upāsako tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ upāsakaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Just then Upananda went to that lay follower and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.2.4
Pali: “attho me, āvuso, cīvarenā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I need robe-cloth.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.2.5
Pali: “Ajjaṇho, bhante, āgamehi, ajja negamassa samayo. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please wait one day, venerable, for today there’s a meeting of the householder association. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.2.6
Pali: Negamena ca katikā katā hoti—‘yo pacchā āgacchati paññāsaṁ baddho’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): They’ve made an agreement that whoever comes late gets fined fifty coins.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.2.7
Pali: “Ajjeva me, āvuso, cīvaraṁ dehī”ti ovaṭṭikāya parāmasi. 
English (brahmali, published): Saying, “Give me the robe-cloth today,” he grabbed him by the belt. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.2.8
Pali: Atha kho so upāsako āyasmatā upanandena sakyaputtena nippīḷiyamāno āyasmato upanandassa sakyaputtassa cīvaraṁ cetāpetvā pacchā agamāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): Being pressured by Upananda, the lay follower bought him robe-cloth, and as a consequence he was late for the meeting. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.2.9
Pali: Manussā taṁ upāsakaṁ etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): People asked him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.2.10
Pali: “kissa tvaṁ, ayyo, pacchā āgato, paññāsaṁ jīnosī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, why are you late? You’ve just lost fifty coins.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.2.11
Pali: Atha kho so upāsako tesaṁ manussānaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): When that lay follower told them what had happened, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.2.12
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): they complained and criticized Upananda, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.2.13
Pali: “mahicchā ime samaṇā sakyaputtiyā asantuṭṭhā. 
English (brahmali, published): “These Sakyan monastics have great desires. They’re not content. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.2.14
Pali: Nayimesaṁ sukaraṁ veyyāvaccampi kātuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): It’s no easy matter to provide them with a service. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.2.15
Pali: Kathañhi nāma āyasmā upanando upāsakena—
English (brahmali, published): How could Venerable Upananda not agree when asked by a lay follower 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.2.16
Pali: ‘ajjaṇho, bhante, āgamehī’ti vuccamāno nāgamessatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): to wait for a day?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.2.17
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.2.18
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.2.19
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā upanando sakyaputto upāsakena—
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Upananda not agree when asked by a lay follower 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.2.20
Pali: ‘ajjaṇho, bhante, āgamehī’ti vuccamāno nāgamessatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): to wait for a day?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.2.21
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ upanandaṁ sakyaputtaṁ anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking him in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned Upananda: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.2.22
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, upananda, upāsakena—
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Upananda, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.2.23
Pali: ‘ajjaṇho, bhante, āgamehī’ti vuccamāno nāgamesī”ti? 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.2.24
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.2.25
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.2.26
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, upāsakena—
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.2.27
Pali: ‘ajjaṇho, bhante, āgamehī’ti vuccamāno nāgamessasi. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.2.28
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.2.29
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.3.1
Pali: “Bhikkhuṁ paneva uddissa rājā vā rājabhoggo vā brāhmaṇo vā gahapatiko vā dūtena cīvaracetāpannaṁ pahiṇeyya—
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a king, a king’s employee, a brahmin, or a householder sends a robe fund for a monk by messenger, saying, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.3.2
Pali: ‘iminā cīvaracetāpannena cīvaraṁ cetāpetvā itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuṁ cīvarena acchādehī’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Buy robe-cloth with this robe fund and give it to monk so-and-so,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.3.3
Pali: So ce dūto taṁ bhikkhuṁ upasaṅkamitvā evaṁ vadeyya—
English (brahmali, published): and the messenger goes to that monk and says, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.3.4
Pali: ‘idaṁ kho, bhante, āyasmantaṁ uddissa cīvaracetāpannaṁ ābhataṁ, paṭiggaṇhātu āyasmā cīvaracetāpannan’ti, 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, I have brought a robe fund for you. Please receive it,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.3.5
Pali: tena bhikkhunā so dūto evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): then that monk should reply, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.3.6
Pali: ‘na kho mayaṁ, āvuso, cīvaracetāpannaṁ paṭiggaṇhāma. Cīvarañca kho mayaṁ paṭiggaṇhāma, kālena kappiyan’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “We don’t receive robe funds, but we do receive allowable robe-cloth at the right time.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.3.7
Pali: So ce dūto taṁ bhikkhuṁ evaṁ vadeyya—
English (brahmali, published): If that messenger says, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.3.8
Pali: ‘atthi panāyasmato koci veyyāvaccakaro’ti, 
English (brahmali, published): “Is there anyone who provides services for you?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.3.9
Pali: cīvaratthikena, bhikkhave, bhikkhunā veyyāvaccakaro niddisitabbo ārāmiko vā upāsako vā—
English (brahmali, published): the monk, if he needs robe-cloth, should point out a monastery worker or a lay follower and say, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.3.10
Pali: ‘eso kho, āvuso, bhikkhūnaṁ veyyāvaccakaro’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “They provide services for the monks.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.3.11
Pali: So ce dūto taṁ veyyāvaccakaraṁ saññāpetvā taṁ bhikkhuṁ upasaṅkamitvā evaṁ vadeyya—
English (brahmali, published): If the messenger instructs that service provider and then returns to the monk and says, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.3.12
Pali: ‘yaṁ kho, bhante, āyasmā veyyāvaccakaraṁ niddisi saññatto so mayā, upasaṅkamatu āyasmā kālena, cīvarena taṁ acchādessatī’ti, 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, I have instructed the service provider you pointed out. Please go to them at the right time and they’ll give you robe-cloth,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.3.13
Pali: cīvaratthikena, bhikkhave, bhikkhunā veyyāvaccakaro upasaṅkamitvā dvattikkhattuṁ codetabbo sāretabbo—
English (brahmali, published): then, if that monk needs robe-cloth, he should go to that service provider and prompt them and remind them two or three times, saying, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.3.14
Pali: ‘attho me, āvuso, cīvarenā’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I need robe-cloth.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.3.15
Pali: Dvattikkhattuṁ codayamāno sārayamāno taṁ cīvaraṁ abhinipphādeyya, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): If he then gets robe-cloth, all is well. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.3.16
Pali: no ce abhinipphādeyya, catukkhattuṁ pañcakkhattuṁ chakkhattuparamaṁ tuṇhībhūtena uddissa ṭhātabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not get it, he should stand in silence for it at most six times. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.3.17
Pali: Catukkhattuṁ pañcakkhattuṁ chakkhattuparamaṁ tuṇhībhūto uddissa tiṭṭhamāno taṁ cīvaraṁ abhinipphādeyya, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): If he then gets robe-cloth, all is well. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.3.18
Pali: tato ce uttari vāyamamāno taṁ cīvaraṁ abhinipphādeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes any further effort and then gets robe-cloth, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.3.19
Pali: No ce abhinipphādeyya, yatassa cīvaracetāpannaṁ ābhataṁ, tattha sāmaṁ vā gantabbaṁ dūto vā pāhetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): If he does not get robe-cloth, he should go to the owners of that robe fund, or send a message, saying, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.3.20
Pali: ‘yaṁ kho tumhe āyasmanto bhikkhuṁ uddissa cīvaracetāpannaṁ pahiṇittha, na taṁ tassa bhikkhuno kiñci atthaṁ anubhoti, 
English (brahmali, published): “That monk hasn’t received any benefit from the robe fund you sent for him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.3.21
Pali: yuñjantāyasmanto sakaṁ, mā vo sakaṁ vinassā’ti, 
English (brahmali, published): Please recover what’s yours, or it might perish.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:1.3.22
Pali: ayaṁ tattha sāmīcī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the proper procedure.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.1
Pali: Bhikkhuṁ paneva uddissāti 
English (brahmali, published): For a monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.2
Pali: bhikkhussatthāya, bhikkhuṁ ārammaṇaṁ karitvā, bhikkhuṁ acchādetukāmo. 
English (brahmali, published): for the benefit of a monk; making a monk the object of consideration, one wants to give to him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.3
Pali: Rājā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A king: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.4
Pali: yo koci rajjaṁ kāreti. 
English (brahmali, published): whoever rules. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.5
Pali: Rājabhoggo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A king’s employee: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.6
Pali: yo koci rañño bhattavetanāhāro. 
English (brahmali, published): whoever gets food and wages from a king. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.7
Pali: Brāhmaṇo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A brahmin: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.8
Pali: jātiyā brāhmaṇo. 
English (brahmali, published): a brahmin by birth. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.9
Pali: Gahapatiko nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A householder: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.10
Pali: ṭhapetvā rājaṁ rājabhoggaṁ brāhmaṇaṁ avaseso gahapatiko nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): anyone apart from a king, a king’s employee, and a brahmin. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.11
Pali: Cīvaracetāpannaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A robe fund: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.12
Pali: hiraññaṁ vā suvaṇṇaṁ vā muttā vā maṇi vā. 
English (brahmali, published): money, gold, a pearl, or a gem. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.13
Pali: Iminā cīvaracetāpannenāti 
English (brahmali, published): With this robe fund: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.14
Pali: paccupaṭṭhitena. 
English (brahmali, published): with that which is at one’s disposal. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.15
Pali: Cetāpetvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Buy: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.16
Pali: parivattetvā. 
English (brahmali, published): having traded. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.17
Pali: Acchādehīti 
English (brahmali, published): Give: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.18
Pali: dajjehi. 
English (brahmali, published): donate. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.19
Pali: So ce dūto taṁ bhikkhuṁ upasaṅkamitvā evaṁ vadeyya—
English (brahmali, published): And the messenger goes to that monk and says, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.20
Pali: “idaṁ kho, bhante, āyasmantaṁ uddissa cīvaracetāpannaṁ ābhataṁ. Paṭiggaṇhātu āyasmā cīvaracetāpannan”ti, 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, I have brought a robe fund for you. Please receive it,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.21
Pali: tena bhikkhunā so dūto evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): then that monk should reply, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.22
Pali: “na kho mayaṁ, āvuso, cīvaracetāpannaṁ paṭiggaṇhāma. Cīvarañca kho mayaṁ paṭiggaṇhāma, kālena kappiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “We don’t receive robe funds, but we do receive allowable robe-cloth at the right time.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.23
Pali: So ce dūto taṁ bhikkhuṁ evaṁ vadeyya—
English (brahmali, published): If that messenger says, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.24
Pali: “atthi panāyasmato koci veyyāvaccakaro”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is there anyone who provides services for you?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.25
Pali: Cīvaratthikena, bhikkhave, bhikkhunā veyyāvaccakaro niddisitabbo ārāmiko vā upāsako vā—
English (brahmali, published): the monk, if he needs robe-cloth, should point out a monastery worker or a lay follower and say, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.26
Pali: “eso kho, āvuso, bhikkhūnaṁ veyyāvaccakaro”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “They provides services for the monks”: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.27
Pali: Na vattabbo— “tassa dehīti vā, so vā nikkhipissati, so vā parivattessati, so vā cetāpessatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): he should not say, “Give it to them,” “They’ll put it aside,” “They’ll trade it,” “They’ll buy it.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.28
Pali: So ce dūto taṁ veyyāvaccakaraṁ saññāpetvā taṁ bhikkhuṁ upasaṅkamitvā evaṁ vadeyya—
English (brahmali, published): If the messenger instructs that service provider and then returns to the monk and says, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.29
Pali: “yaṁ kho, bhante, āyasmā veyyāvaccakaraṁ niddisi saññatto so mayā. Upasaṅkamatu āyasmā kālena, cīvarena taṁ acchādessatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, I have instructed the service provider you pointed out. Please go to them at the right time and they’ll give you robe-cloth,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.30
Pali: Cīvaratthikena, bhikkhave, bhikkhunā veyyāvaccakaro upasaṅkamitvā dvattikkhattuṁ codetabbo sāretabbo—
English (brahmali, published): then, if that monk needs robe-cloth, he should go to that service provider and prompt them and remind them two or three times, saying, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.31
Pali: “attho me, āvuso, cīvarenā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I need robe-cloth”: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.32
Pali: Na vattabbo— “dehi me cīvaraṁ, āhara me cīvaraṁ, parivattehi me cīvaraṁ, cetāpehi me cīvaran”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): he should not say, “Give me robe-cloth,” “Get me robe-cloth,” “Trade me robe-cloth,” “Buy me robe-cloth.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.33
Pali: Dutiyampi vattabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): He should say it a second 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.34
Pali: Tatiyampi vattabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): and a third time. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.35
Pali: Sace abhinipphādeti, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): If he gets it, all is well. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.36
Pali: no ce abhinipphādeti, tattha gantvā tuṇhībhūtena uddissa ṭhātabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not get it, he should go there and stand in silence for it: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.37
Pali: Na āsane nisīditabbaṁ. Na āmisaṁ paṭiggahetabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): he should not sit down on a seat; he should not receive food; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.38
Pali: Na dhammo bhāsitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): he should not give a teaching. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.39
Pali: “Kiṁ kāraṇā āgatosī”ti pucchiyamāno “jānāhi, āvuso”ti vattabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is asked, “Why have you come?” he should say, “Think about it.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.40
Pali: Sace āsane vā nisīdati, āmisaṁ vā paṭiggaṇhāti, dhammaṁ vā bhāsati, ṭhānaṁ bhañjati. 
English (brahmali, published): If he sits down on a seat, or he receives food, or he gives a teaching, he loses one allowance to stand. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.41
Pali: Dutiyampi ṭhātabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): He should stand a second 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.42
Pali: Tatiyampi ṭhātabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): and a third time. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.43
Pali: Catukkhattuṁ codetvā catukkhattuṁ ṭhātabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he prompts four times, he can stand four times. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.44
Pali: Pañcakkhattuṁ codetvā dvikkhattuṁ ṭhātabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he prompts five times, he can stand twice. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.1.45
Pali: Chakkhattuṁ codetvā na ṭhātabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he prompts six times, he cannot stand at all. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.2.1
Pali: Tato ce uttari vāyamamāno taṁ cīvaraṁ abhinipphādeti, 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes any further effort and then gets robe-cloth: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.2.2
Pali: payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.2.3
Pali: Paṭilābhena nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): When he gets the robe-cloth, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.2.4
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā puggalassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The robe-cloth should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.2.5
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.2.6
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bu-vb-np1#3.2.5'>Bu NP 1:3.2.5</a>–3.2.29, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.2.7
Pali: “idaṁ me, bhante, cīvaraṁ atirekatikkhattuṁ codanāya atirekachakkhattuṁ ṭhānena abhinipphāditaṁ nissaggiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this robe-cloth, which I got after prompting more than three times and standing more than six times, is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.2.8
Pali: Imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.2.9
Pali: dadeyyāti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.2.10
Pali: dadeyyunti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.2.11
Pali: āyasmato dammīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this robe-cloth back to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.2.12
Pali: No ce abhinipphādeyya, yatassa cīvaracetāpannaṁ ābhataṁ tattha sāmaṁ vā gantabbaṁ dūto vā pāhetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): If he does not get robe-cloth, he should go to the owner of that robe fund, or send a message, saying, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.2.13
Pali: “yaṁ kho tumhe, āyasmanto, bhikkhuṁ uddissa cīvaracetāpannaṁ pahiṇittha na taṁ tassa bhikkhuno kiñci atthaṁ anubhoti. 
English (brahmali, published): “That monk hasn’t received any benefit from the robe fund you sent for him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.2.14
Pali: Yuñjantāyasmanto sakaṁ, mā vo sakaṁ vinassā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Please recover what’s yours, or it might perish.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.2.15
Pali: Ayaṁ tattha sāmīcīti 
English (brahmali, published): This is the proper procedure: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.2.16
Pali: ayaṁ tattha anudhammatā. 
English (brahmali, published): this is the right method. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.3.1
Pali: Atirekatikkhattuṁ codanāya atirekachakkhattuṁ ṭhānena atirekasaññī abhinipphādeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he prompts more than three times and stands more than six times, and he perceives it as more, and he gets robe-cloth, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.3.2
Pali: Atirekatikkhattuṁ codanāya atirekachakkhattuṁ ṭhānena vematiko abhinipphādeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he prompts more than three times and stands more than six times, but he is unsure of it, and he gets robe-cloth, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.3.3
Pali: Atirekatikkhattuṁ codanāya atirekachakkhattuṁ ṭhānena ūnakasaññī abhinipphādeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he prompts more than three times and stands more than six times, but he perceives it as less, and he gets robe-cloth, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.3.4
Pali: Ūnakatikkhattuṁ codanāya ūnakachakkhattuṁ ṭhānena atirekasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he prompts less than three times and stands less than six times, but he perceives it as more, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.3.5
Pali: Ūnakatikkhattuṁ codanāya ūnakachakkhattuṁ ṭhānena vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he prompts less than three times and stands less than six times, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.3.6
Pali: Ūnakatikkhattuṁ codanāya ūnakachakkhattuṁ ṭhānena ūnakasaññī anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he prompts less than three times and stands less than six times, and he perceives it as less, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.4.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.4.2
Pali: tikkhattuṁ codanāya, chakkhattuṁ ṭhānena, 
English (brahmali, published): if he prompts three times and stands six times; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.4.3
Pali: ūnakatikkhattuṁ codanāya, ūnakachakkhattuṁ ṭhānena, 
English (brahmali, published): if he prompts less than three times and stands less than six times; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.4.4
Pali: acodiyamāno deti, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is given without prompting; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.4.5
Pali: sāmikā codetvā denti, 
English (brahmali, published): if the owners prompt and then it is given; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.4.6
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.4.7
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.4.8
Pali: Rājasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ dasamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on excessive prompting to get a robe, the tenth, is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.4.9
Pali: Kathinavaggo paṭhamo. 
English (brahmali, published): The first subchapter on the robe season is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.4.10
Pali: Tassuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.4.11
Pali: Ubbhataṁ kathinaṁ tīṇi, 
English (brahmali, published): “Three on the ended robe season, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.4.12
Pali: dhovanañca paṭiggaho; 
English (brahmali, published): And washing, receiving; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.4.13
Pali: Aññātakāni tīṇeva, 
English (brahmali, published): Three on those who are unrelated, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np10:2.4.14
Pali: ubhinnaṁ dūtakena cāti. 
English (brahmali, published): Of both, and with messenger.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-np11:1.23.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu kosiyamissakaṁ santhataṁ kārāpeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk has a blanket made that contains silk, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’”

Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A:

Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np11:2.1.5
Pali: Santhataṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A blanket: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np11:2.1.6
Pali: santharitvā kataṁ hoti avāyimaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): it is made by strewing, not by weaving. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np11:2.1.7
Pali: Kārāpeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Has made: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np11:2.1.8
Pali: ekenapi kosiyaṁsunā missitvā karoti vā kārāpeti vā, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes a blanket that contains even one thread of silk, or he has one made, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np11:2.1.9
Pali: Paṭilābhena nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): When he gets the blanket, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np11:2.1.10
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā puggalassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The blanket should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual.

Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …”

Pali: …pe…

pli-tv-bu-vb-np11:2.1.13
Pali: idaṁ me, bhante, kosiyamissakaṁ santhataṁ kārāpitaṁ nissaggiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this blanket containing silk, which I got made, is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np11:2.1.14
Pali: Imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmīti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np11:2.1.15
Pali: dadeyyāti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np11:2.1.16
Pali: dadeyyunti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np11:2.1.17
Pali: āyasmato dammīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this blanket back to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np11:2.2.1
Pali: Attanā vippakataṁ attanā pariyosāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he finishes what he began himself, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np11:2.2.2
Pali: Attanā vippakataṁ parehi pariyosāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has others finish what he began himself, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np11:2.2.3
Pali: Parehi vippakataṁ attanā pariyosāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he finishes himself what was begun by others, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np11:2.2.4
Pali: Parehi vippakataṁ parehi pariyosāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has others finish what was begun by others, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np11:2.2.5
Pali: Aññassatthāya karoti vā kārāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes one, or has one made, for someone else, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np11:2.2.6
Pali: Aññena kataṁ paṭilabhitvā paribhuñjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he gets one that was made by someone else and then uses it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np11:2.2.7.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np11:2.2.8
Pali: vitānaṁ vā bhūmattharaṇaṁ vā sāṇipākāraṁ vā bhisiṁ vā bibbohanaṁ vā karoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes a canopy, a floor cover, a cloth screen, a mattress, or a pillow; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np11:2.2.9
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np11:2.2.10
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np11:2.2.11
Pali: Kosiyasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ paṭhamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on making a blanket with silk, the first, is finished.

Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np12:2.30
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np12:2.31
Pali: Suddhakāḷakasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ dutiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on making a blanket entirely of black wool, the second, is finished.
