pli-tv-bu-vb-np13
Root: root/pli/ms/vinaya/pli-tv-bu-vb/pli-tv-bu-vb-np/pli-tv-bu-vb-np13_root-pli-ms.json

English translation sources:
  - brahmali [published]: translation/en/brahmali/vinaya/pli-tv-bu-vb/pli-tv-bu-vb-np/pli-tv-bu-vb-np13_translation-en-brahmali.json

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:0.3
Pali: Nissaggiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on relinquishment 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:0.4
Pali: Kosiyavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on silk 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:0.5
Pali: 13. Dvebhāgasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 13. Making a new blanket with too much black wool 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū—“bhagavatā paṭikkhittaṁ suddhakāḷakānaṁ eḷakalomānaṁ santhataṁ kārāpetun”ti, te thokaññeva odātaṁ ante ādiyitvā tatheva suddhakāḷakānaṁ eḷakalomānaṁ santhataṁ kārāpenti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time, when the monks from the group of six knew that the Buddha had prohibited having a blanket made entirely of black wool, they added just a little bit of white on the edge, effectively having a blanket made entirely of black wool just as before. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:1.3
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:1.4
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū thokaññeva odātaṁ ante ādiyitvā tatheva suddhakāḷakānaṁ eḷakalomānaṁ santhataṁ kārāpessantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the monks from the group of six do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:1.5
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū chabbaggiye bhikkhū anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking those monks in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:1.6
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, thokaññeva odātaṁ ante ādiyitvā tatheva suddhakāḷakānaṁ eḷakalomānaṁ santhataṁ kārāpethā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:1.7
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:1.8
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:1.9
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, thokaññeva odātaṁ ante ādiyitvā tatheva suddhakāḷakānaṁ eḷakalomānaṁ santhataṁ kārāpessatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:1.10
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:1.11
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:1.12.1
Pali: “Navaṁ pana bhikkhunā santhataṁ kārayamānena dve bhāgā suddhakāḷakānaṁ eḷakalomānaṁ ādātabbā tatiyaṁ odātānaṁ catutthaṁ gocariyānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk is having a new blanket made, he should use two parts of entirely black wool, a third part of white, and a fourth part of brown. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:1.13
Pali: Anādā ce bhikkhu dve bhāge suddhakāḷakānaṁ eḷakalomānaṁ tatiyaṁ odātānaṁ catutthaṁ gocariyānaṁ navaṁ santhataṁ kārāpeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has a new blanket made without using two parts of entirely black wool, a third part of white, and a fourth part of brown, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.1
Pali: Navaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): New: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.2
Pali: karaṇaṁ upādāya vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): newly made is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.3
Pali: Santhataṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A blanket: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.4
Pali: santharitvā kataṁ hoti avāyimaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): it is made by strewing, not by weaving. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.5
Pali: Kārayamānenāti 
English (brahmali, published): Is having made: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.6
Pali: karonto vā kārāpento vā. 
English (brahmali, published): making it himself or having it made. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.7
Pali: Dve bhāgā suddhakāḷakānaṁ eḷakalomānaṁ ādātabbāti 
English (brahmali, published): He should use two parts of entirely black wool: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.8
Pali: dhārayitvā dve tulā ādātabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): having weighed it, he should use two measures. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.9
Pali: Tatiyaṁ odātānanti 
English (brahmali, published): A third part of white: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.10
Pali: tulaṁ odātānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one measure of white. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.11
Pali: Catutthaṁ gocariyānanti 
English (brahmali, published): A fourth part of brown: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.12
Pali: tulaṁ gocariyānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one measure of brown. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.13
Pali: Anādā ce bhikkhu dve bhāge suddhakāḷakānaṁ eḷakalomānaṁ tatiyaṁ odātānaṁ catutthaṁ gocariyānanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Without using two parts of entirely black wool, a third part of white, and a fourth part of brown: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.14
Pali: Anādiyitvā dve tule suddhakāḷakānaṁ eḷakalomānaṁ tulaṁ odātānaṁ tulaṁ gocariyānaṁ navaṁ santhataṁ karoti vā kārāpeti vā payoge dukkaṭaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes it, or has it made, without using two measures of entirely black wool, one measure of white, and one measure of brown, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.15
Pali: paṭilābhena nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): When he gets the blanket, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.16
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā puggalassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The blanket should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.17
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.18
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bu-vb-np1#3.2.5'>Bu NP 1:3.2.5</a>–3.2.29, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.19
Pali: “idaṁ me, bhante, santhataṁ anādiyitvā dve tule suddhakāḷakānaṁ eḷakalomānaṁ tulaṁ odātānaṁ tulaṁ gocariyānaṁ kārāpitaṁ nissaggiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this blanket, which I got made without using two measures of entirely black wool, one measure of white, and one measure of brown, is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.20
Pali: Imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.21
Pali: dadeyyāti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.22
Pali: dadeyyunti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.23
Pali: āyasmato dammīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this blanket back to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.24.1
Pali: Attanā vippakataṁ attanā pariyosāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he finishes what he began himself, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.25
Pali: Attanā vippakataṁ parehi pariyosāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has others finish what he began himself, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.26
Pali: Parehi vippakataṁ attanā pariyosāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he finishes himself what was begun by others, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.27
Pali: Parehi vippakataṁ parehi pariyosāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has others finish what was begun by others, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.28
Pali: Aññassatthāya karoti vā kārāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes one, or has one made, for someone else, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.29
Pali: Aññena kataṁ paṭilabhitvā paribhuñjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he gets one that was made by someone else and then uses it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.30.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.31
Pali: tulaṁ odātānaṁ tulaṁ gocariyānaṁ ādiyitvā karoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes one using one measure of white and one measure of brown; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.32
Pali: bahutaraṁ odātānaṁ bahutaraṁ gocariyānaṁ ādiyitvā karoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes one using more than one measure of white and more than one measure of brown; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.33
Pali: suddhaṁ odātānaṁ suddhaṁ gocariyānaṁ ādiyitvā karoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes one using just white and brown; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.34
Pali: vitānaṁ vā bhūmattharaṇaṁ vā sāṇipākāraṁ vā bhisiṁ vā bibbohanaṁ vā karoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes a canopy, a floor cover, a cloth screen, a mattress, or a pillow; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.35
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.36
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np13:2.37
Pali: Dvebhāgasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ tatiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on making a new blanket with too much black wool, the third, is finished.

Pali: Theravāda Vinaya

English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on silk 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:0.5
Pali: 14. Chabbassasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 14. Keeping a new blanket less than six years 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū anuvassaṁ santhataṁ kārāpenti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time monks were having blankets made every year. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:1.3
Pali: Te yācanabahulā viññattibahulā viharanti—
English (brahmali, published): They kept on begging and asking, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:1.4
Pali: “eḷakalomāni detha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please give wool! 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:1.5
Pali: Eḷakalomehi attho”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): We need wool!” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:1.6
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:1.7
Pali: “kathañhi nāma samaṇā sakyaputtiyā anuvassaṁ santhataṁ kārāpessanti, yācanabahulā viññattibahulā viharissanti—
English (brahmali, published): “How can the Sakyan monastics have blankets made every year, begging and asking, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:1.8
Pali: ‘eḷakalomāni detha, eḷakalomehi attho’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Please give wool! We need wool!’? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:1.9
Pali: Amhākaṁ pana sakiṁ katāni santhatāni pañcapi chapi vassāni honti, yesaṁ no dārakā uhadantipi ummihantipi undūrehipi khajjanti. 
English (brahmali, published): We only make blankets for ourselves every five or six years, even though our children defecate and urinate on them and they’re eaten by rats.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:1.10
Pali: Ime pana samaṇā sakyaputtiyā anuvassaṁ santhataṁ kārāpenti, yācanabahulā viññattibahulā viharanti—
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:1.11
Pali: ‘eḷakalomāni detha, eḷakalomehi attho’”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:1.12
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:1.13
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:1.17

English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that there are monks who do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:1.18
Pali: ‘eḷakalomāni detha, eḷakalomehi attho’”ti? 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:1.19

English (brahmali, published): “How can those foolish men do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:1.22
Pali: ‘eḷakalomāni detha, eḷakalomehi attho’ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:1.23
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:1.24
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:1.25.1
Pali: “Navaṁ pana bhikkhunā santhataṁ kārāpetvā chabbassāni dhāretabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk has had a new blanket made, he should keep it for six years. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:1.26
Pali: Orena ce channaṁ vassānaṁ taṁ santhataṁ vissajjetvā vā avissajjetvā vā aññaṁ navaṁ santhataṁ kārāpeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Whether that blanket has been given away or not, if he has another new blanket made in less than six years, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:1.27
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu kosambiyaṁ gilāno hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time a certain monk at Kosambī was sick. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.2
Pali: Ñātakā tassa bhikkhuno santike dūtaṁ pāhesuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): His relatives sent him a message, saying, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.3
Pali: “āgacchatu bhadanto mayaṁ upaṭṭhahissāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Come, venerable, we’ll nurse you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.4
Pali: Bhikkhūpi evamāhaṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): The monks 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.5
Pali: “gacchāvuso, ñātakā taṁ upaṭṭhahissantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): urged him to go, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.6
Pali: So evamāha—
English (brahmali, published): but he said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.7
Pali: “bhagavatā, āvuso, sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): “The Buddha has laid down a training rule 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.8
Pali: ‘navaṁ pana bhikkhunā santhataṁ kārāpetvā chabbassāni dhāretabban’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): that a monk who has had a new blanket made must keep it for six years. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.9
Pali: Ahañcamhi gilāno, na sakkomi santhataṁ ādāya pakkamituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Now because I’m sick, I’m unable to travel with my blanket. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.10
Pali: Mayhañca vinā santhatā na phāsu hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): And because I’m not comfortable without it, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.11
Pali: Nāhaṁ gamissāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I can’t go.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.12
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.13
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards he gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.14
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, gilānassa bhikkhuno santhatasammutiṁ dātuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to give permission to a sick monk to make a blanket. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.15
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, dātabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): And it should be given like this. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.16
Pali: Tena gilānena bhikkhunā saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā vuḍḍhānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ pāde vanditvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): After approaching the Sangha, the sick monk should arrange his upper robe over one shoulder and pay respect at the feet of the senior monks. He should then squat on his heels, raise his joined palms, and say, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.17
Pali: ‘ahaṁ, bhante, gilāno 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Venerables, I’m sick. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.18
Pali: na sakkomi santhataṁ ādāya pakkamituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): I’m unable to travel with my blanket. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.19
Pali: Sohaṁ, bhante, saṅghaṁ santhatasammutiṁ yācāmī’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I ask the Sangha for permission to make a blanket.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.20
Pali: Dutiyampi yācitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): And he should ask a second 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.21
Pali: Tatiyampi yācitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): and a third time. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.22
Pali: Byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena saṅgho ñāpetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): A competent and capable monk should then inform the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.23
Pali: ‘Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.24
Pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmo bhikkhu gilāno na sakkoti santhataṁ ādāya pakkamituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monk so-and-so is sick. He’s unable to travel with his blanket. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.25
Pali: So saṅghaṁ santhatasammutiṁ yācati. 
English (brahmali, published): He’s asking the Sangha for permission to make a blanket. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.26
Pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmassa bhikkhuno santhatasammutiṁ dadeyya. 
English (brahmali, published): If the Sangha is ready, it should give permission to monk so-and-so to make a blanket. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.27
Pali: Esā ñatti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the motion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.28
Pali: Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.29
Pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmo bhikkhu gilāno na sakkoti santhataṁ ādāya pakkamituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monk so-and-so is sick. He’s unable to travel with his blanket. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.30
Pali: So saṅghaṁ santhatasammutiṁ yācati. 
English (brahmali, published): He’s asking the Sangha for permission to make a blanket. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.31
Pali: Saṅgho itthannāmassa bhikkhuno santhatasammutiṁ deti. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha gives permission to monk so-and-so to make a blanket. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.32
Pali: Yassāyasmato khamati itthannāmassa bhikkhuno santhatasammutiyā dānaṁ, so tuṇhassa; 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who approves of giving permission to monk so-and-so to make a blanket should remain silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.33
Pali: yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.34
Pali: Dinnā saṅghena itthannāmassa bhikkhuno santhatasammuti. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha has given permission to monk so-and-so to make a blanket. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.35
Pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.36
Pali: evametaṁ dhārayāmī’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I will remember it thus.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.37
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.38.1
Pali: “Navaṁ pana bhikkhunā santhataṁ kārāpetvā chabbassāni dhāretabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk has had a new blanket made, he should keep it for six years. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:2.39
Pali: Orena ce channaṁ vassānaṁ taṁ santhataṁ vissajjetvā vā avissajjetvā vā aññaṁ navaṁ santhataṁ kārāpeyya, aññatra bhikkhusammutiyā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Whether that blanket has been given away or not, if he has another new blanket made in less than six years, except if the monks have agreed, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.1
Pali: Navaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): New: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.2
Pali: karaṇaṁ upādāya vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): newly made is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.3
Pali: Santhataṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A blanket: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.4
Pali: santharitvā kataṁ hoti avāyimaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): it is made by strewing, not by weaving. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.5
Pali: Kārāpetvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Has had made: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.6
Pali: karitvā vā kārāpetvā vā. 
English (brahmali, published): has made or has had made. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.7
Pali: Chabbassāni dhāretabbanti 
English (brahmali, published): He should keep it for six years: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.8
Pali: chabbassaparamatā dhāretabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): he should keep it for six years at a minimum. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.9
Pali: Orena ce channaṁ vassānanti 
English (brahmali, published): In less than six years: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.10
Pali: ūnakachabbassāni. 
English (brahmali, published): fewer than six years. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.11
Pali: Taṁ santhataṁ vissajjetvāti 
English (brahmali, published): That blanket has been given away: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.12
Pali: aññesaṁ datvā. 
English (brahmali, published): it has been given to others. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.13
Pali: Avissajjetvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Not: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.14
Pali: na kassaci datvā. 
English (brahmali, published): it has not been given to anyone. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.15
Pali: Aññatra bhikkhusammutiyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Except if the monks have agreed: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.16
Pali: ṭhapetvā bhikkhusammutiṁ aññaṁ navaṁ santhataṁ karoti vā kārāpeti vā, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes another new blanket, or has one made, unless the monks have agreed, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.17
Pali: Paṭilābhena nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): When he gets the blanket, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.18
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā puggalassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The blanket should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.19
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.20
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bu-vb-np1#3.2.5'>Bu NP 1:3.2.5</a>–3.2.29, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.21
Pali: “idaṁ me, bhante, santhataṁ ūnakachabbassāni kārāpitaṁ, aññatra bhikkhusammutiyā, nissaggiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this blanket, which I got made after less than six years without the agreement of the monks, is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.22
Pali: Imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.23
Pali: dadeyyāti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.24
Pali: dadeyyunti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.25
Pali: āyasmato dammīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this blanket back to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.26.1
Pali: Attanā vippakataṁ attanā pariyosāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he finishes what he began himself, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.27
Pali: Attanā vippakataṁ parehi pariyosāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has others finish what he began himself, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.28
Pali: Parehi vippakataṁ attanā pariyosāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he finishes himself what was begun by others, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.29
Pali: Parehi vippakataṁ parehi pariyosāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has others finish what was begun by others, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.30.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.31
Pali: chabbassāni karoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes one after six years; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.32
Pali: atirekachabbassāni karoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes one after more than six years; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.33
Pali: aññassatthāya karoti vā kārāpeti vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes one, or has one made, for the sake of another; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.34
Pali: aññena kataṁ paṭilabhitvā paribhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he gets what was made by another and then uses it; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.35
Pali: vitānaṁ vā bhūmattharaṇaṁ vā sāṇipākāraṁ vā bhisiṁ vā bibbohanaṁ vā karoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes a canopy, a floor cover, a cloth screen, a mattress, or a pillow; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.36
Pali: bhikkhusammutiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he has the permission of the monks; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.37
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.38
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np14:3.39
Pali: Chabbassasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ catutthaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on keeping a new blanket less than six years, the fourth, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:0.5
Pali: 15. Nisīdanasanthatasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 15. Making an inappropriate sitting blanket 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.2
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā bhikkhū āmantesi—“icchāmahaṁ, bhikkhave, temāsaṁ paṭisallīyituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): There the Buddha addressed the monks: “Monks, I wish to do a solitary retreat for three months. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.3
Pali: Namhi kenaci upasaṅkamitabbo, aññatra ekena piṇḍapātanīhārakenā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): No-one should visit me except the one who brings me almsfood.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.4
Pali: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho te bhikkhū bhagavato paṭissuṇitvā nāssudha koci bhagavantaṁ upasaṅkamati, aññatra ekena piṇḍapātanīhārakena. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes, sir,” they replied, and no-one visited him except the one who brought him almsfood. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.5
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sāvatthiyā saṅghena katikā katā hoti—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Sangha at Sāvatthī made the following agreement: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.6
Pali: “icchatāvuso, bhagavā temāsaṁ paṭisallīyituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “The Buddha wishes to be on solitary retreat for three months. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.7
Pali: Na bhagavā kenaci upasaṅkamitabbo, aññatra ekena piṇḍapātanīhārakena. 
English (brahmali, published): No-one should visit him except the one who brings him almsfood. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.8
Pali: Yo bhagavantaṁ upasaṅkamati so pācittiyaṁ desāpetabbo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Anyone who does must confess an offense entailing confession.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.9
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā upaseno vaṅgantaputto, sapariso yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): Just then Venerable Upasena of Vaṅganta and his followers went to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.10
Pali: Āciṇṇaṁ kho panetaṁ buddhānaṁ bhagavantānaṁ āgantukehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ paṭisammodituṁ. Atha kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ upasenaṁ vaṅgantaputtaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Since it is the custom for Buddhas to greet newly-arrived monks, the Buddha said to Upasena, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.11
Pali: “kacci vo, upasena, khamanīyaṁ kacci yāpanīyaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): “I hope you’re keeping well, Upasena, I hope you’re getting by? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.12
Pali: kaccittha appakilamathena addhānaṁ āgatā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): I hope you’re not tired from traveling?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.13
Pali: “Khamanīyaṁ, bhagavā, yāpanīyaṁ, bhagavā. 
English (brahmali, published): “We’re keeping well, sir, we’re getting by. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.14
Pali: Appakilamathena ca mayaṁ, bhante, addhānaṁ āgatā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): We’re not tired from traveling.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.15
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmato upasenassa vaṅgantaputtassa saddhivihāriko bhikkhu bhagavato avidūre nisinno hoti. Atha kho bhagavā taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): One of Upasena’s students was seated not far from the Buddha, and the Buddha said to him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.16
Pali: “manāpāni te, bhikkhu, paṁsukūlānī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Do you like rag-robes, monk?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.17
Pali: “Na kho me, bhante, manāpāni paṁsukūlānī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I don’t like rag-robes, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.18
Pali: “Kissa pana tvaṁ, bhikkhu, paṁsukūliko”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Why then do you wear them?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.19
Pali: “Upajjhāyo me, bhante, paṁsukūliko. Evaṁ ahampi paṁsukūliko”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “My preceptor wears them, and so I do it too.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.20
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ upasenaṁ vaṅgantaputtaṁ etadavoca—“pāsādikā kho tyāyaṁ, upasena, parisā. 
English (brahmali, published): And the Buddha said to Upasena, “Upasena, your followers are inspiring. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.21
Pali: Kathaṁ tvaṁ, upasena, parisaṁ vinesī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): How do you train them?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.22
Pali: “Yo maṁ, bhante, upasampadaṁ yācati tamahaṁ evaṁ vadāmi—
English (brahmali, published): “When anyone asks me for the full ordination, I tell him this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.23
Pali: ‘ahaṁ kho, āvuso, āraññiko piṇḍapātiko paṁsukūliko. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘I stay in the wilderness, I eat only almsfood, and I wear rag-robes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.24
Pali: Sace tvampi āraññiko bhavissasi piṇḍapātiko paṁsukūliko, evāhaṁ taṁ upasampādessāmī’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If you do the same, I’ll give you the full ordination.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.25
Pali: Sace me paṭissuṇāti upasampādemi, no ce me paṭissuṇāti na upasampādemi. 
English (brahmali, published): If he agrees, I ordain him. Otherwise I don’t. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.26
Pali: Yo maṁ nissayaṁ yācati tamahaṁ evaṁ vadāmi—
English (brahmali, published): And I do the same when anyone asks me for support. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.27
Pali: ‘ahaṁ kho, āvuso, āraññiko piṇḍapātiko paṁsukūliko. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.28
Pali: Sace tvampi āraññiko bhavissasi piṇḍapātiko paṁsukūliko, evāhaṁ te nissayaṁ dassāmī’ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.29
Pali: Sace me paṭissuṇāti nissayaṁ demi, no ce me paṭissuṇāti na nissayaṁ demi. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.1.30
Pali: Evaṁ kho ahaṁ, bhante, parisaṁ vinemī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): It’s in this way that I train my followers.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.2.1
Pali: “Sādhu sādhu, upasena. Sādhu kho tvaṁ, upasena, parisaṁ vinesi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Good, Upasena, you train your followers well. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.2.2
Pali: Jānāsi pana tvaṁ, upasena, sāvatthiyā saṅghassa katikan”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): But do you know the agreement made by the Sangha at Sāvatthī?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.2.3
Pali: “Na kho ahaṁ, bhante, jānāmi sāvatthiyā saṅghassa katikan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “No.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.2.4
Pali: “Sāvatthiyā kho, upasena, saṅghena katikā katā—
English (brahmali, published): “The Sangha at Sāvatthī has made the following agreement: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.2.5
Pali: ‘icchatāvuso, bhagavā temāsaṁ paṭisallīyituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘The Buddha wishes to be on solitary retreat for three months. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.2.6
Pali: Na bhagavā kenaci upasaṅkamitabbo, aññatra ekena piṇḍapātanīhārakena. 
English (brahmali, published): No-one should visit him except the one who brings him almsfood. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.2.7
Pali: Yo bhagavantaṁ upasaṅkamati so pācittiyaṁ desāpetabbo’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Anyone who does must confess an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.2.8
Pali: “Paññāyissati, bhante, sāvatthiyā saṅgho sakāya katikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, let the Sangha at Sāvatthī be known for this agreement. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.2.9
Pali: na mayaṁ apaññattaṁ paññapessāma paññattaṁ vā na samucchindissāma, 
English (brahmali, published): We, however, don’t lay down new rules, nor do we get rid of the existing ones. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.2.10
Pali: yathāpaññattesu sikkhāpadesu samādāya vattissāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): We practice and undertake the training rules as they are.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.2.11
Pali: “Sādhu sādhu, upasena, 
English (brahmali, published): “Good, Upasena. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.2.12
Pali: apaññattaṁ na paññapetabbaṁ, paññattaṁ vā na samucchinditabbaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): One should not lay down new rules, nor should one get rid of the existing ones. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.2.13
Pali: yathāpaññattesu sikkhāpadesu samādāya vattitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): One should practice and undertake the training rules as they are. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.2.14
Pali: Anujānāmi, upasena, ye te bhikkhū āraññikā piṇḍapātikā paṁsukūlikā yathāsukhaṁ maṁ dassanāya upasaṅkamantū”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): And, Upasena, I allow those monks who stay in the wilderness, who eat only almsfood, and who wear rag-robes to visit me whenever they please.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.2.15
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā bhikkhū bahidvārakoṭṭhake ṭhitā honti—“mayaṁ āyasmantaṁ upasenaṁ vaṅgantaputtaṁ pācittiyaṁ desāpessāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Upasena and his followers got up from their seats, bowed down, circumambulated the Buddha with their right sides toward him, and left. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.2.16
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā upaseno vaṅgantaputto sapariso uṭṭhāyāsanā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): Just then a number of monks were standing outside the gateway, thinking, “We’ll make Venerable Upasena confess an offense entailing confession.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.2.17
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ upasenaṁ vaṅgantaputtaṁ etadavocuṁ—“jānāsi tvaṁ, āvuso upasena, sāvatthiyā saṅghassa katikan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): And they said to Upasena, “Upasena, do you know the agreement of the Sangha at Sāvatthī?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.2.18
Pali: “Bhagavāpi maṁ, āvuso, evamāha—‘jānāsi pana tvaṁ, upasena, sāvatthiyā saṅghassa katikan’ti? ‘Na kho ahaṁ, bhante, jānāmi sāvatthiyā saṅghassa katikan’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “The Buddha asked me the same question, and I replied that I didn’t. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.2.19
Pali: ‘Sāvatthiyā kho, upasena, saṅghena katikā katā—
English (brahmali, published): He then told me what it was, and I said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.2.20
Pali: icchatāvuso, bhagavā temāsaṁ paṭisallīyituṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.2.21
Pali: Na bhagavā kenaci upasaṅkamitabbo, aññatra ekena piṇḍapātanīhārakena. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.2.22
Pali: Yo bhagavantaṁ upasaṅkamati so pācittiyaṁ desāpetabbo’ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.2.23
Pali: ‘Paññāyissati, bhante, sāvatthiyā saṅgho sakāya katikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Sir, let the Sangha at Sāvatthī be known for this agreement. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.2.24
Pali: na mayaṁ apaññattaṁ paññapessāma paññattaṁ vā na samucchindissāma, 
English (brahmali, published): We, however, don’t lay down new rules, nor do we get rid of the existing ones. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.2.25
Pali: yathāpaññattesu sikkhāpadesu samādāya vattissāmā’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): We practice and undertake the training rules as they are.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.2.26
Pali: Anuññātāvuso, bhagavatā—‘ye te bhikkhū āraññikā piṇḍapātikā paṁsukūlikā yathāsukhaṁ maṁ dassanāya upasaṅkamantū’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Also, the Buddha has allowed those monks who stay in the wilderness, who eat only almsfood, and who wear rag-robes to visit him whenever they please.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.2.27
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū—“saccaṁ kho āyasmā upaseno āha—
English (brahmali, published): Those monks thought, “It’s true what Venerable Upasena says.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.2.28
Pali: ‘na apaññattaṁ paññapetabbaṁ, paññattaṁ vā na samucchinditabbaṁ, yathāpaññattesu sikkhāpadesu samādāya vattitabban’”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.3.1
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū—“anuññātā kira bhagavatā—‘ye te bhikkhū āraññikā piṇḍapātikā paṁsukūlikā yathāsukhaṁ maṁ dassanāya upasaṅkamantū’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard that the Buddha had allowed those monks who stay in the wilderness, who eat only almsfood, and who wear rag-robes to visit him whenever they please. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.3.2
Pali: Te bhagavantaṁ dassanaṁ pihentā santhatāni ujjhitvā āraññikaṅgaṁ piṇḍapātikaṅgaṁ paṁsukūlikaṅgaṁ samādiyiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): Longing to see the Buddha, they discarded their blankets and undertook the practice of staying in the wilderness, of eating only almsfood, and of wearing rag-robes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.3.3
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā sambahulehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ senāsanacārikaṁ āhiṇḍanto addasa santhatāni tahaṁ tahaṁ ujjhitāni. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards, when the Buddha and a number of monks were walking about the dwellings, he saw discarded blankets here and there. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.3.4
Pali: Passitvā bhikkhū āmantesi—“kassimāni, bhikkhave, santhatāni tahaṁ tahaṁ ujjhitānī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): He asked the monks, “Who owns these discarded blankets?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.3.5
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks told him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.3.6
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards he gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.3.7
Pali: “tena hi, bhikkhave, bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññapessāmi dasa atthavase paṭicca—
English (brahmali, published): “Well then, monks, I will lay down a training rule for the following ten reasons: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.3.8
Pali: saṅghasuṭṭhutāya, saṅghaphāsutāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): for the well-being of the Sangha, for the comfort of the Sangha, for the restraint of bad people, for the ease of good monks, for the restraint of the corruptions relating to the present life, for the restraint of the corruptions relating to future lives, to give rise to confidence in those without it, to increase the confidence of those who have it, for the longevity of the true Teaching, and for supporting the training. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.3.9
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.3.10.1
Pali: “Nisīdanasanthataṁ pana bhikkhunā kārayamānena purāṇasanthatassa sāmantā sugatavidatthi ādātabbā dubbaṇṇakaraṇāya, 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk is having a sitting blanket made, he must incorporate a piece of one standard handspan from the border of an old blanket in order to make it ugly. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:1.3.11
Pali: anādā ce bhikkhu purāṇasanthatassa sāmantā sugatavidatthiṁ navaṁ nisīdanasanthataṁ kārāpeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has a new sitting blanket made without incorporating a piece of one standard handspan from the border of an old blanket, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.1
Pali: Nisīdanaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Sitting (blanket): 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.2
Pali: sadasaṁ vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): one with a border is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.3
Pali: Santhataṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A blanket: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.4
Pali: santharitvā kataṁ hoti avāyimaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): it is made by strewing, not by weaving. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.5
Pali: Kārayamānenāti 
English (brahmali, published): Is having made: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.6
Pali: karonto vā kārāpento vā. 
English (brahmali, published): making it himself or having it made. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.7
Pali: Purāṇasanthataṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): An old blanket: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.8
Pali: sakiṁ nivatthampi sakiṁ pārutampi. 
English (brahmali, published): even worn once. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.9
Pali: Sāmantā sugatavidatthi ādātabbā dubbaṇṇakaraṇāyāti 
English (brahmali, published): He must incorporate a piece of one standard handspan from the border in order to make it ugly: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.10
Pali: thirabhāvāya vaṭṭaṁ vā caturassaṁ vā chinditvā ekadese vā santharitabbaṁ vijaṭetvā vā santharitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): to make it strong, he cuts out a circular or a rectangular piece, and he then incorporates it in one place or he strews it on after pulling it apart. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.11
Pali: Anādā ce bhikkhu purāṇasanthatassa sāmantā sugatavidatthinti 
English (brahmali, published): Without incorporating a piece of one standard handspan from the border of an old blanket: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.12
Pali: anādiyitvā purāṇasanthatassa sāmantā sugatavidatthiṁ navaṁ nisīdanasanthataṁ karoti vā kārāpeti vā, payoge dukkaṭaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes a new sitting blanket, or has one made, without incorporating a piece of one standard handspan from the border of an old blanket, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.13
Pali: paṭilābhena nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): When he gets the sitting blanket, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.14
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā puggalassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The sitting blanket should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.15
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.16
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bu-vb-np1#3.2.5'>Bu NP 1:3.2.5</a>–3.2.29, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.17
Pali: “idaṁ me, bhante, nisīdanasanthataṁ anādiyitvā purāṇasanthatassa sāmantā sugatavidatthiṁ kārāpitaṁ nissaggiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this sitting blanket, which I got made without incorporating a piece of one standard handspan from the border of an old blanket, is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.18
Pali: Imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.19
Pali: dadeyyāti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.20
Pali: dadeyyunti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.21
Pali: āyasmato dammīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this sitting blanket back to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.22.1
Pali: Attanā vippakataṁ attanā pariyosāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he finishes what he began himself, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.23
Pali: Attanā vippakataṁ parehi pariyosāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has others finish what he began himself, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.24
Pali: Parehi vippakataṁ attanā pariyosāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he finishes himself what was begun by others, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.25
Pali: Parehi vippakataṁ parehi pariyosāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has others finish what was begun by others, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.26
Pali: Aññassatthāya karoti vā kārāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes one, or has one made, for the sake of another, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.27.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.28
Pali: purāṇasanthatassa sāmantā sugatavidatthiṁ ādiyitvā karoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes one incorporating a piece of one standard handspan from the border of an old blanket; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.29
Pali: alabhanto thokataraṁ ādiyitvā karoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is unable to get hold of such a piece and he makes one incorporating a smaller piece; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.30
Pali: alabhanto anādiyitvā karoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is unable to get hold of such a smaller piece and he makes one without; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.31
Pali: aññena kataṁ paṭilabhitvā paribhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he gets what was made by another and then uses it; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.32
Pali: vitānaṁ vā bhūmattharaṇaṁ vā sāṇipākāraṁ vā bhisiṁ vā bibbohanaṁ vā karoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes a canopy, a floor cover, a cloth screen, a mattress, or a pillow; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.33
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.34
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np15:2.35
Pali: Nisīdanasanthatasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ pañcamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on making an inappropriate sitting blanket, the fifth, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis

Pali: Nissaggiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on relinquishment 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:0.4
Pali: Kosiyavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on silk 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:0.5
Pali: 16. Eḷakalomasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 16. Carrying wool too far 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarassa bhikkhuno kosalesu janapade sāvatthiṁ gacchantassa antarāmagge eḷakalomāni uppajjiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): a certain monk was given wool as he was walking through the Kosalan country on his way to Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:1.3
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu tāni eḷakalomāni uttarāsaṅgena bhaṇḍikaṁ bandhitvā agamāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): He bound it into a bundle with his upper robe and carried on. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:1.4
Pali: Manussā taṁ bhikkhuṁ passitvā uppaṇḍesuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): People who saw him teased him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:1.5
Pali: “kittakena te, bhante, kītāni? 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, how much did it cost you? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:1.6
Pali: Kittako udayo bhavissatī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): How much will the profit be?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:1.7
Pali: So bhikkhu tehi manussehi uppaṇḍiyamāno maṅku ahosi. 
English (brahmali, published): As a result he was humiliated. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:1.8
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu sāvatthiṁ gantvā tāni eḷakalomāni ṭhitakova āsumbhi. 
English (brahmali, published): When he arrived at Sāvatthī, he threw the wool to the ground. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:1.9
Pali: Bhikkhū taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): The monks asked him why. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:1.10
Pali: “kissa tvaṁ, āvuso, imāni eḷakalomāni ṭhitakova āsumbhasī”ti? 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:1.11
Pali: “Tathā hi panāhaṁ, āvuso, imesaṁ eḷakalomānaṁ kāraṇā manussehi uppaṇḍito”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “People have been teasing me because of this wool.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:1.12
Pali: “Kīva dūrato pana tvaṁ, āvuso, imāni eḷakalomāni āharī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “But how far have you carried it?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:1.13
Pali: “Atirekatiyojanaṁ, āvuso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Over 40 kilometers.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:1.14
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:1.15
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhu atirekatiyojanaṁ eḷakalomāni āharissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could a monk carry wool more than 40 kilometers?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:1.16
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū taṁ bhikkhuṁ anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking that monk in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:1.17
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, bhikkhu, atirekatiyojanaṁ eḷakalomāni āharī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monk, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:1.18
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:1.19
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:1.20
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, atirekatiyojanaṁ eḷakalomāni āharissasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:1.21
Pali: Netaṁ moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:1.22
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:1.23.1
Pali: “Bhikkhuno paneva addhānamaggappaṭipannassa eḷakalomāni uppajjeyyuṁ. Ākaṅkhamānena bhikkhunā paṭiggahetabbāni. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If wool is given to a monk who is traveling, he may receive it if he wishes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:1.24
Pali: Paṭiggahetvā tiyojanaparamaṁ sahatthā haritabbāni, asante hārake. 
English (brahmali, published): If he receives it and there is no-one else to carry it, he may carry it himself for at most 40 kilometers. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:1.25
Pali: Tato ce uttari hareyya, asantepi hārake, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he carries it further than that, even if there is no-one else to carry it, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.1
Pali: Bhikkhuno paneva addhānamaggappaṭipannassāti 
English (brahmali, published): To a monk who is traveling: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.2
Pali: panthaṁ gacchantassa. 
English (brahmali, published): to one who is walking on a road. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.3
Pali: Eḷakalomāni uppajjeyyunti 
English (brahmali, published): If wool is given: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.4
Pali: uppajjeyyuṁ saṅghato vā gaṇato vā ñātito vā mittato vā paṁsukūlaṁ vā attano vā dhanena. 
English (brahmali, published): if it is given by a sangha, by a group, by a relative, by a friend, or if it is discarded wool, or if he got it by means of his own property. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.5
Pali: Ākaṅkhamānenāti 
English (brahmali, published): If he wishes: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.6
Pali: icchamānena paṭiggahetabbāni. 
English (brahmali, published): if he desires, he may receive it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.7
Pali: Paṭiggahetvā tiyojanaparamaṁ sahatthā haritabbānīti 
English (brahmali, published): If he receives it, he may carry it himself for at most 40 kilometers: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.8
Pali: tiyojanaparamatā sahatthā haritabbāni. 
English (brahmali, published): he may carry it himself a maximum of 40 kilometers. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.9
Pali: Asante hāraketi 
English (brahmali, published): There is no-one else to carry it: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.10
Pali: nāñño koci hārako hoti itthī vā puriso vā gahaṭṭho vā pabbajito vā. 
English (brahmali, published): there is no other person who can carry it, either a woman or a man, either a householder or a monastic. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.11
Pali: Tato ce uttari hareyya, asantepi hāraketi 
English (brahmali, published): If he carries it further than that, even if there is no-one else to carry it: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.12
Pali: paṭhamaṁ pādaṁ tiyojanaṁ atikkāmeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when he goes beyond 40 kilometers with the first foot, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.13
Pali: Dutiyaṁ pādaṁ atikkāmeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): When he goes beyond with the second foot, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.14
Pali: Antotiyojane ṭhito bahitiyojanaṁ pāteti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he stands within the 40 kilometer limit, but drops it beyond the 40 kilometer limit, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.15
Pali: Aññassa yāne vā bhaṇḍe vā ajānantassa pakkhipitvā tiyojanaṁ atikkāmeti, nissaggiyāni honti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he places it in the vehicle or among the goods of another without their knowledge, and it goes more than 40 kilometers, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.16
Pali: Nissajjitabbāni saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā puggalassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The wool should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.17
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbāni. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.18
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bu-vb-np1#3.2.5'>Bu NP 1:3.2.5</a>–3.2.29, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.19
Pali: “imāni me, bhante, eḷakalomāni tiyojanaṁ atikkāmitāni nissaggiyāni. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this wool, which I have taken more than 40 kilometers, is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.20
Pali: Imānāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.21
Pali: dadeyyāti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.22
Pali: dadeyyunti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.23
Pali: āyasmato dammīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this wool back to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.24.1
Pali: Atirekatiyojane atirekasaññī atikkāmeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he takes it more than 40 kilometers and he perceives it as more, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.25
Pali: Atirekatiyojane vematiko atikkāmeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he takes it more than 40 kilometers, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.26
Pali: Atirekatiyojane ūnakasaññī atikkāmeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he takes it more than 40 kilometers, but he perceives it as less, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.27
Pali: Ūnakatiyojane atirekasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he takes it less than 40 kilometers, but he perceives it as more, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.28
Pali: Ūnakatiyojane vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he takes it less than 40 kilometers, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.29
Pali: Ūnakatiyojane ūnakasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he takes it less than 40 kilometers and he perceives it as less, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.30.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.31
Pali: tiyojanaṁ harati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he carries it 40 kilometers; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.32
Pali: ūnakatiyojanaṁ harati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he carries it less than 40 kilometers; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.33
Pali: tiyojanaṁ haratipi paccāharatipi, 
English (brahmali, published): if he carries it 40 kilometers and then carries it back; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.34
Pali: tiyojanaṁ vāsādhippāyo gantvā tato paraṁ harati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he takes it 40 kilometers with the intention of staying there, but then takes it further; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.35
Pali: acchinnaṁ paṭilabhitvā harati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he gets back what had been taken from him and then takes it further; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.36
Pali: nissaṭṭhaṁ paṭilabhitvā harati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he gets back what he had given up and then takes it further; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.37
Pali: aññaṁ harāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he gets someone else to carry it; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.38
Pali: katabhaṇḍaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a finished article; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.39
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.40
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np16:2.41
Pali: Eḷakalomasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ chaṭṭhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on carrying wool too far, the sixth, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:0.5
Pali: 17. Eḷakalomadhovāpanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 17. Getting a nun to wash wool 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sakkesu viharati kapilavatthusmiṁ nigrodhārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying in the Sakyan country in the Banyan Tree Monastery at Kapilavatthu. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū bhikkhunīhi eḷakalomāni dhovāpentipi rajāpentipi vijaṭāpentipi. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the monks from the group of six had the nuns wash, dye, and comb wool. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:1.3
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo eḷakalomāni dhovantiyo rajantiyo vijaṭentiyo riñcanti uddesaṁ paripucchaṁ adhisīlaṁ adhicittaṁ adhipaññaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Because of this, the nuns neglected recitation, testing, the higher morality, the higher mind, and the higher wisdom. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:1.4
Pali: Atha kho mahāpajāpati gotamī yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): Mahāpajāpati Gotamī then went to the Buddha and bowed down to him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:1.5
Pali: Ekamantaṁ ṭhitaṁ kho mahāpajāpatiṁ gotamiṁ bhagavā etadavoca—“kacci, gotami, bhikkhuniyo appamattā ātāpiniyo pahitattā viharantī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): And the Buddha said to her, “Gotamī, I hope the nuns are heedful, energetic, and diligent?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:1.6
Pali: “Kuto, bhante, bhikkhunīnaṁ appamādo. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the nuns be heedful, venerable sir?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:1.7
Pali: Ayyā chabbaggiyā bhikkhunīhi eḷakalomāni dhovāpentipi rajāpentipi vijaṭāpentipi. 
English (brahmali, published): And she told him what was happening. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:1.8
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo eḷakalomāni dhovantiyo rajantiyo vijaṭentiyo riñcanti uddesaṁ paripucchaṁ adhisīlaṁ adhicittaṁ adhipaññan”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:1.9
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā mahāpajāpatiṁ gotamiṁ dhammiyā kathāya sandassesi samādapesi samuttejesi sampahaṁsesi. 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha then instructed, inspired, and gladdened her with a teaching. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:1.10
Pali: Atha kho mahāpajāpati gotamī bhagavatā dhammiyā kathāya sandassitā samādapitā samuttejitā sampahaṁsitā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): She bowed down, circumambulated him with her right side toward him, and left. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:1.11
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe bhikkhusaṅghaṁ sannipātāpetvā chabbaggiye bhikkhū paṭipucchi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks from the group of six: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:1.12
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, bhikkhunīhi eḷakalomāni dhovāpethapi rajāpethapi vijaṭāpethapī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:1.13
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:1.14
Pali: “Ñātikāyo tumhākaṁ, bhikkhave, aññātikāyo”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Are they your relatives?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:1.15
Pali: “Aññātikāyo, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “No.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:1.16
Pali: “Aññātakā, moghapurisā, aññātikānaṁ na jānanti patirūpaṁ vā appatirūpaṁ vā pāsādikaṁ vā apāsādikaṁ vā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, people who are unrelated don’t know what’s appropriate and inappropriate, what’s inspiring and uninspiring, in dealing with each other. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:1.17
Pali: Tattha nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, aññātikāhi bhikkhunīhi eḷakalomāni dhovāpessathapi rajāpessathapi vijaṭāpessathapi. 
English (brahmali, published): And still you do this. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:1.18
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:1.19
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:1.20.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu aññātikāya bhikkhuniyā eḷakalomāni dhovāpeyya vā rajāpeyya vā vijaṭāpeyya vā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk has an unrelated nun wash, dye, or comb wool, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.5
Pali: Aññātikā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Unrelated: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.6
Pali: mātito vā pitito vā yāva sattamā pitāmahayugā asambaddhā. 
English (brahmali, published): anyone who is not a descendant of one’s male ancestors going back eight generations, either on the mother’s side or on the father’s side. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.7
Pali: Bhikkhunī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A nun: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.8
Pali: ubhatosaṅghe upasampannā. 
English (brahmali, published): she has been given the full ordination by both Sanghas. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.9
Pali: Dhovāti āṇāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he tells her to wash it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.10
Pali: Dhotāni nissaggiyāni honti. 
English (brahmali, published): When it has been washed, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.11
Pali: Rajāti āṇāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he tells her to dye it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.12
Pali: Rattāni nissaggiyāni honti. 
English (brahmali, published): When it has been dyed, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.13
Pali: Vijaṭehīti āṇāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he tells her to comb it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.14
Pali: Vijaṭitāni nissaggiyāni honti. 
English (brahmali, published): When it has been combed, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.15
Pali: Nissajjitabbāni saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā puggalassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The wool should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.16
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbāni. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …”

Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bu-vb-np1#3.2.5'>Bu NP 1:3.2.5</a>–3.2.29, with appropriate substitutions.)

English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this wool, which I got washed by an unrelated nun, is to be relinquished.

English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to the Sangha.” …

English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.22
Pali: āyasmato dammīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this wool back to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.23.1
Pali: Aññātikāya aññātikasaññī eḷakalomāni dhovāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated and he perceives her as such, and he has her wash wool, he commits one offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.24
Pali: Aññātikāya aññātikasaññī eḷakalomāni dhovāpeti rajāpeti, nissaggiyena āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated and he perceives her as such, and he has her wash and dye wool, he commits one offense entailing relinquishment and one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.25
Pali: Aññātikāya aññātikasaññī eḷakalomāni dhovāpeti vijaṭāpeti, nissaggiyena āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated and he perceives her as such, and he has her wash and comb wool, he commits one offense entailing relinquishment and one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.26
Pali: Aññātikāya aññātikasaññī eḷakalomāni dhovāpeti rajāpeti vijaṭāpeti, nissaggiyena āpatti dvinnaṁ dukkaṭānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated and he perceives her as such, and he has her wash, dye, and comb wool, he commits one offense entailing relinquishment and two offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.27
Pali: Aññātikāya aññātikasaññī eḷakalomāni rajāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated and he perceives her as such, and he has her dye wool, he commits one offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.28
Pali: Aññātikāya aññātikasaññī eḷakalomāni rajāpeti vijaṭāpeti, nissaggiyena āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated and he perceives her as such, and he has her dye and comb wool, he commits one offense entailing relinquishment and one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.29
Pali: Aññātikāya aññātikasaññī eḷakalomāni rajāpeti dhovāpeti, nissaggiyena āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated and he perceives her as such, and he has her dye and wash wool, he commits one offense entailing relinquishment and one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.30

English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated and he perceives her as such, and he has her dye, comb, and wash wool, he commits one offense entailing relinquishment and two offenses of wrong conduct.

Pali: Aññātikāya aññātikasaññī eḷakalomāni vijaṭāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated and he perceives her as such, and he has her comb wool, he commits one offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.32
Pali: Aññātikāya aññātikasaññī eḷakalomāni vijaṭāpeti dhovāpeti, nissaggiyena āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated and he perceives her as such, and he has her comb and wash wool, he commits one offense entailing relinquishment and one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.33
Pali: Aññātikāya aññātikasaññī eḷakalomāni vijaṭāpeti rajāpeti, nissaggiyena āpatti dukkaṭassa.

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.34

English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated and he perceives her as such, and he has her comb, wash, and dye wool, he commits one offense entailing relinquishment and two offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.35
Pali: Aññātikāya vematiko …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated, but he is unsure of it … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.36
Pali: aññātikāya ñātikasaññī …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated, but he perceives her as related … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.37
Pali: aññassa eḷakalomāni dhovāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has her wash wool belonging to someone else, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.38
Pali: Ekato upasampannāya dhovāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has a nun who is fully ordained only on one side do the washing, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.39
Pali: Ñātikāya aññātikasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is related, but he perceives her as unrelated, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.40
Pali: Ñātikāya vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is related, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.41
Pali: Ñātikāya ñātikasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is related and he perceives her as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.42.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.43
Pali: ñātikāya dhovantiyā aññātikā dutiyā hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if a related nun does the washing and an unrelated nun helps her; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.44
Pali: avuttā dhovati, 
English (brahmali, published): if a nun does the washing without being asked; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.45
Pali: aparibhuttaṁ katabhaṇḍaṁ dhovāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he has a nun wash an unused and finished article; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.46
Pali: sikkhamānāya 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a trainee nun; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.47
Pali: sāmaṇeriyā 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a novice nun; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.48
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.49
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np17:2.50
Pali: Eḷakalomadhovāpanasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ sattamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on getting a nun to wash wool, the seventh, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:1.2

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:1.3

English (brahmali, published): Whenever that family obtained food, they put aside a portion for Upananda.

English (brahmali, published): But then in the morning our son got up early and cried, ‘Give me meat!’ and we gave him your portion.

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:1.28.1

English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk takes, has someone else take, or consents to gold, silver, or money being deposited for him, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’”

English (brahmali, published): A:

English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.5

English (brahmali, published): Gold:

Pali: Uggaṇheyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Takes:

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.13
Pali: Upanikkhittaṁ vā sādiyeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Consents to … being deposited for him:

English (brahmali, published): if someone says, “This is for you,” and he consents to it being deposited for him, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.15
Pali: Saṅghamajjhe nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): It should be relinquished in the midst of the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.16

English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.17

English (brahmali, published): After approaching the Sangha, that monk should arrange his upper robe over one shoulder and pay respect at the feet of the senior monks. He should then squat on his heels, raise his joined palms, and say: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.18
Pali: “ahaṁ, bhante, rūpiyaṁ paṭiggahesiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Venerables, I have received money.

Pali: Idaṁ me nissaggiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): It is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.20
Pali: Imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to the Sangha.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.21
Pali: Nissajjitvā āpatti desetabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): After relinquishing it, he is to confess the offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.22
Pali: Byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena āpatti paṭiggahetabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): The confession should be received by a competent and capable monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.23
Pali: Sace tattha āgacchati ārāmiko vā upāsako vā so vattabbo—
English (brahmali, published): If a monastery worker or a lay follower is available, you should tell him,

Pali: “āvuso, imaṁ jānāhī”ti.

English (brahmali, published): If he says,

Pali: na vattabbo—
English (brahmali, published): one should not say,

English (brahmali, published): If he makes a purchase and brings back what is allowable, everyone may enjoy it except the one who received the money. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.32
Pali: Evañcetaṁ labhetha, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): If this is what happens, all is well.

Pali: no ce labhetha, so vattabbo—
English (brahmali, published): If not, he should be told,

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.35
Pali: Sace so chaḍḍeti, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): If he discards it, all is well. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.36

English (brahmali, published): If he does not, a monk who has five qualities should be appointed as the money discarder: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.37
Pali: yo na chandāgatiṁ gaccheyya, na dosāgatiṁ gaccheyya, na mohāgatiṁ gaccheyya, na bhayāgatiṁ gaccheyya, chaḍḍitāchaḍḍitañca jāneyya. 
English (brahmali, published): he’s not biased by desire, ill will, confusion, or fear, and he knows what has and has not been discarded. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.38
Pali: “Evañca pana, bhikkhave, sammannitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, he should be appointed like this.

Pali: Paṭhamaṁ bhikkhu yācitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): First the monk should be asked
