English (brahmali, published): and then a competent and capable monk should inform the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.41
Pali: ‘Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.42
Pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuṁ rūpiyachaḍḍakaṁ sammanneyya. 
English (brahmali, published): If the Sangha is ready, it should appoint monk so-and-so as the money discarder. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.43
Pali: Esā ñatti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the motion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.44
Pali: Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.45
Pali: Saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuṁ rūpiyachaḍḍakaṁ sammannati. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha appoints monk so-and-so as the money discarder. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.46
Pali: Yassāyasmato khamati itthannāmassa bhikkhuno rūpiyachaḍḍakassa sammuti, so tuṇhassa; 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who agrees to appointing monk so-and-so as the money discarder should remain silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.47
Pali: yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who doesn’t agree should speak up. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.48
Pali: Sammato saṅghena itthannāmo bhikkhu rūpiyachaḍḍako. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha has appointed monk so-and-so as the money discarder. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.49
Pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.50
Pali: evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I will remember it thus.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.51
Pali: Tena sammatena bhikkhunā animittaṁ katvā pātetabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The appointed monk should throw it away without taking note of where. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.52
Pali: Sace nimittaṁ katvā pāteti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he takes note of where he throws it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.53.1
Pali: Rūpiye rūpiyasaññī rūpiyaṁ paṭiggaṇhāti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is money, and he perceives it as such, and he receives it, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.54
Pali: Rūpiye vematiko rūpiyaṁ paṭiggaṇhāti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is money, but he is unsure of it, and he receives it, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.55
Pali: Rūpiye arūpiyasaññī rūpiyaṁ paṭiggaṇhāti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is money, but he does not perceive it as such, and he receives it, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.56
Pali: Arūpiye rūpiyasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not money, but he perceives it as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.57
Pali: Arūpiye vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not money, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.58
Pali: Arūpiye arūpiyasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not money, and he does not perceive it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.59.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.60
Pali: ajjhārāme vā ajjhāvasathe vā uggahetvā vā uggahāpetvā vā nikkhipati—
English (brahmali, published): if, within a monastery or a lodging, he takes it or has someone take it, and he then puts it aside with the thought, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.61
Pali: yassa bhavissati so harissatīti, 
English (brahmali, published): “Whoever owns it will come and get it;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.62
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.63
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np18:2.64
Pali: Rūpiyasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ aṭṭhamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on becoming the owner of money, the eighth, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:0.5
Pali: 19. Rūpiyasaṁvohārasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 19. Trading with money 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū nānappakārakaṁ rūpiyasaṁvohāraṁ samāpajjanti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the monks from the group of six engaged in various kinds of trades involving money. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:1.3
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:1.4
Pali: “kathañhi nāma samaṇā sakyaputtiyā nānappakārakaṁ rūpiyasaṁvohāraṁ samāpajjissanti, seyyathāpi gihī kāmabhogino”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the Sakyan monastics engage in trades that involve money? They’re just like householders who indulge in worldly pleasures!” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:1.5
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:1.6
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:1.7
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū nānappakārakaṁ rūpiyasaṁvohāraṁ samāpajjissantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the monks from the group of six do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:1.8
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū chabbaggiye bhikkhū anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking those monks in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned those monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:1.9
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, nānappakārakaṁ rūpiyasaṁvohāraṁ samāpajjathā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:1.10
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:1.11
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:1.12
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, nānappakārakaṁ rūpiyasaṁvohāraṁ samāpajjissatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:1.13
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:1.14
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:1.15.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu nānappakārakaṁ rūpiyasaṁvohāraṁ samāpajjeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk engages in various kinds of trades involving money, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.5
Pali: Nānappakārakaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Various kinds: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.6
Pali: katampi akatampi katākatampi. 
English (brahmali, published): what is shaped, what is not shaped, and what is both shaped and not shaped. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.7
Pali: Kataṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): What is shaped: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.8
Pali: sīsūpagaṁ gīvūpagaṁ hatthūpagaṁ pādūpagaṁ kaṭūpagaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): what is meant for the head, what is meant for the neck, what is meant for the hands, what is meant for the feet, what is meant for the waist. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.9
Pali: Akataṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): What is not shaped: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.10
Pali: ghanakataṁ vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): what is shaped in a lump is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.11
Pali: Katākataṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): What is both shaped and not shaped: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.12
Pali: tadubhayaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): both of them. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.13
Pali: Rūpiyaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Money: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.14
Pali: satthuvaṇṇo kahāpaṇo, lohamāsako, dārumāsako, jatumāsako ye vohāraṁ gacchanti. 
English (brahmali, published): a golden <i lang='pi' translate='no'>kahāpaṇa</i> coin, a copper <i lang='pi' translate='no'>māsaka</i> coin, a wooden <i lang='pi' translate='no'>māsaka</i> coin, a resin <i lang='pi' translate='no'>māsaka</i> coin—whatever is used in commerce. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.15
Pali: Samāpajjeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Engages: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.16
Pali: katena kataṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges what is shaped with what is shaped, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.17
Pali: Katena akataṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges what is not shaped with what is shaped, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.18
Pali: Katena katākataṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges what is both shaped and not shaped with what is shaped, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.19
Pali: Akatena kataṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges what is shaped with what is not shaped, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.20
Pali: Akatena akataṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges what is not shaped with what is not shaped, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.21
Pali: Akatena katākataṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges what is both shaped and not shaped with what is not shaped, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.22
Pali: Katākatena kataṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges what is shaped with what is both shaped and not shaped, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.23
Pali: Katākatena akataṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges what is not shaped with what is both shaped and not shaped, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.24
Pali: Katākatena katākataṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges what is both shaped and not shaped with what is both shaped and not shaped, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.25
Pali: Saṅghamajjhe nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): It should be relinquished in the midst of the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.26
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.27
Pali: Tena bhikkhunā saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā vuḍḍhānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ pāde vanditvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): After approaching the Sangha, that monk should arrange his upper robe over one shoulder and pay respect at the feet of the senior monks. He should then squat on his heels, raise his joined palms, and say, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.28
Pali: “ahaṁ, bhante, nānappakārakaṁ rūpiyasaṁvohāraṁ samāpajjiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Venerables, I have engaged in various kinds of trades involving money. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.29
Pali: Idaṁ me nissaggiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): This is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.30
Pali: Imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to the Sangha.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.31
Pali: Nissajjitvā āpatti desetabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): After relinquishing it, he is to confess the offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.32
Pali: Byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena āpatti paṭiggahetabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): The confession should be received by a competent and capable monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.33
Pali: Sace tattha āgacchati ārāmiko vā upāsako vā so vattabbo—
English (brahmali, published): If a monastery worker or a lay follower is available, you should tell him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.34
Pali: “āvuso, imaṁ jānāhī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Look into this.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.35
Pali: Sace so bhaṇati—
English (brahmali, published): If he says, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.36
Pali: “iminā kiṁ āhariyyatū”ti, 
English (brahmali, published): “What can I get you with this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.37
Pali: na vattabbo—
English (brahmali, published): one should not say, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.38
Pali: “imaṁ vā imaṁ vā āharā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Get this or that;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.39
Pali: Kappiyaṁ ācikkhitabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): one should point out what is allowable: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.40
Pali: sappi vā telaṁ vā madhu vā phāṇitaṁ vā. 
English (brahmali, published): ghee, oil, honey, or syrup. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.41
Pali: Sace so tena parivattetvā kappiyaṁ āharati, rūpiyacetāpakaṁ ṭhapetvā, sabbeheva paribhuñjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes a purchase and brings back what is allowable, everyone may enjoy it except the one who did the exchange involving money. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.42
Pali: Evañcetaṁ labhetha, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): If this is what happens, all is well. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.43
Pali: no ce labhetha, so vattabbo—
English (brahmali, published): If not, he should be told, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.44
Pali: “āvuso, imaṁ chaḍḍehī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Discard this.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.45
Pali: Sace so chaḍḍeti, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): If he discards it, all is well. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.46
Pali: no ce chaḍḍeti, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu rūpiyachaḍḍako sammannitabbo—
English (brahmali, published): If he does not, a monk who has five qualities should be appointed as the money discarder: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.47
Pali: yo na chandāgatiṁ gaccheyya, na dosāgatiṁ gaccheyya, na mohāgatiṁ gaccheyya, na bhayāgatiṁ gaccheyya, chaḍḍitāchaḍḍitañca jāneyya. 
English (brahmali, published): he’s not biased by desire, ill will, confusion, or fear, and he knows what has and has not been discarded. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.48
Pali: “Evañca pana, bhikkhave, sammannitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, he should be appointed like this. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.49
Pali: Paṭhamaṁ bhikkhu yācitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): First the monk should be asked 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.50
Pali: Yācitvā byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena saṅgho ñāpetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): and then a competent and capable monk should inform the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.51
Pali: ‘Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.52
Pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuṁ rūpiyachaḍḍakaṁ sammanneyya. 
English (brahmali, published): If the Sangha is ready, it should appoint monk so-and-so as the money discarder. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.53
Pali: Esā ñatti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the motion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.54
Pali: Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.55
Pali: Saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuṁ rūpiyachaḍḍakaṁ sammannati. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha appoints monk so-and-so as the money discarder. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.56
Pali: Yassāyasmato khamati itthannāmassa bhikkhuno rūpiyachaḍḍakassa sammuti, so tuṇhassa; 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who agrees to appointing monk so-and-so as the money discarder should remain silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.57
Pali: yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who doesn’t agree should speak up. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.58
Pali: Sammato saṅghena itthannāmo bhikkhu rūpiyachaḍḍako. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha has appointed monk so-and-so as the money discarder. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.59
Pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.60
Pali: evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I will remember it thus.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.61
Pali: Tena sammatena bhikkhunā animittaṁ katvā pātetabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The appointed monk should throw it away without taking note of where. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.62
Pali: Sace nimittaṁ katvā pāteti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he takes note of where he throws it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.63.1
Pali: Rūpiye rūpiyasaññī rūpiyaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is money, and he perceives it as such, and he exchanges it for money, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.64
Pali: Rūpiye vematiko rūpiyaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is money, but he is unsure of it, and he exchanges it for money, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.65
Pali: Rūpiye arūpiyasaññī rūpiyaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is money, but he does not perceive it as such, and he exchanges it for money, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.66
Pali: Arūpiye rūpiyasaññī rūpiyaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not money, but he perceives it as such, and he exchanges it for money, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.67
Pali: Arūpiye vematiko rūpiyaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not money, but he is unsure of it, and he exchanges it for money, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.68
Pali: Arūpiye arūpiyasaññī rūpiyaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not money, and he does not perceive it as such, but he exchanges it for money, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.69
Pali: Arūpiye rūpiyasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not money, but he perceives it as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.70
Pali: Arūpiye vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not money, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.71
Pali: Arūpiye arūpiyasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not money, and he does not perceive it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.72.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.73
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.74
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np19:2.75
Pali: Rūpiyasaṁvohārasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ navamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on trading with money, the ninth, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:0.5
Pali: 20. Kayavikkayasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 20. Bartering 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā upanando sakyaputto paṭṭo hoti cīvarakammaṁ kātuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time Venerable Upananda the Sakyan had become skilled at making robes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.3
Pali: So paṭapilotikānaṁ saṅghāṭiṁ karitvā surattaṁ suparikammakataṁ katvā pārupi. 
English (brahmali, published): He made an upper robe of old cloth, well-dyed and beautifully made, and he wore it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.4
Pali: Atha kho aññataro paribbājako mahagghaṁ paṭaṁ pārupitvā yenāyasmā upanando sakyaputto tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ upanandaṁ sakyaputtaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Just then a certain wanderer who was wearing an expensive robe went up to Upananda and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.5
Pali: “sundarā kho tyāyaṁ, āvuso, saṅghāṭi; 
English (brahmali, published): “Your upper robe is beautiful. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.6
Pali: dehi me paṭenā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Please give it to me in exchange for my robe.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.7
Pali: “Jānāhi, āvuso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Are you sure?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.8
Pali: “Āmāvuso, jānāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I am.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.9
Pali: “Handāvuso”ti, adāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): Saying, “Alright, then,” he gave it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.10
Pali: Atha kho so paribbājako taṁ saṅghāṭiṁ pārupitvā paribbājakārāmaṁ agamāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): The wanderer put on the upper robe and went to the wanderers’ monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.11
Pali: Paribbājakā taṁ paribbājakaṁ etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): The wanderers said to him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.12
Pali: “sundarā kho tyāyaṁ, āvuso, saṅghāṭi; 
English (brahmali, published): “This upper robe of yours is beautiful. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.13
Pali: kuto tayā laddhā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): Where did you get it?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.14
Pali: “Tena me, āvuso, paṭena parivattitā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I traded it for my robe.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.15
Pali: “Katihipi tyāyaṁ, āvuso, saṅghāṭi bhavissati, 
English (brahmali, published): “But how long will it last? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.16
Pali: soyeva te paṭo varo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Your other robe was better.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.17
Pali: Atha kho so paribbājako—“saccaṁ kho paribbājakā āhaṁsu—‘katihipi myāyaṁ saṅghāṭi bhavissati. Soyeva me paṭo varo’”ti 
English (brahmali, published): The wanderer realized they were right, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.18
Pali: yenāyasmā upanando sakyaputto tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ upanandaṁ sakyaputtaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): and so he returned to Upananda and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.19
Pali: “handa te, āvuso, saṅghāṭi; dehi me paṭan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Here’s your upper robe. Please give me back mine.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.20
Pali: “Nanu tvaṁ, āvuso, mayā vutto— ‘jānāhi, āvuso’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “But didn’t I ask you if you were sure? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.21
Pali: Nāhaṁ dassāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I won’t give it back.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.22
Pali: Atha kho so paribbājako ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—
English (brahmali, published): Then that wanderer complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.23
Pali: “gihīpi naṁ gihissa vippaṭisārissa denti, 
English (brahmali, published): “Even householders give back to each other when they regret a trade. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.24
Pali: kiṁ pana pabbajito pabbajitassa na dassatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): How, then, can a monastic not do the same?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.25
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tassa paribbājakassa ujjhāyantassa khiyyantassa vipācentassa. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of that wanderer, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.26
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized Upananda, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.27
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā upanando sakyaputto paribbājakena saddhiṁ kayavikkayaṁ samāpajjissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Upananda barter with a wanderer?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.28
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ upanandaṁ sakyaputtaṁ anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking him in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned Upananda: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.29
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, upananda, paribbājakena saddhiṁ kayavikkayaṁ samāpajjasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Upananda, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.30
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.31
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.32
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, paribbājakena saddhiṁ kayavikkayaṁ samāpajjissasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.33
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.34
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:1.35.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu nānappakārakaṁ kayavikkayaṁ samāpajjeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk engages in various kinds of barter, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.5
Pali: Nānappakārakaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Various kinds: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.6
Pali: cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārā, antamaso cuṇṇapiṇḍopi dantakaṭṭhampi dasikasuttampi. 
English (brahmali, published): robes, almsfood, a dwelling, or medicinal supplies; even a bit of bath powder, a tooth cleaner, or a piece of string. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.7
Pali: Kayavikkayaṁ samāpajjeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Engages in barter: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.8
Pali: iminā imaṁ dehi, iminā imaṁ āhara, iminā imaṁ parivattehi, iminā imaṁ cetāpehīti. Ajjhācarati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he misbehaves, saying, “Give that for this,” “Bring that for this,” “Trade that with this,” “Exchange that for this,” he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.9
Pali: Yato kayitañca hoti vikkayitañca attano bhaṇḍaṁ parahatthagataṁ parabhaṇḍaṁ attano hatthagataṁ, nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): When it has been bartered—his own goods are in the hands of the other and the other’s goods are in his own hands—it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.10
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā puggalassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The goods should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.11
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, they should be relinquished like this. …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.12
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bu-vb-np1#3.2.5'>Bu NP 1:3.2.5</a>–3.2.29, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.13
Pali: “ahaṁ, bhante, nānappakārakaṁ kayavikkayaṁ samāpajjiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, I have engaged in various kinds of barter. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.14
Pali: Idaṁ me nissaggiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): This is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.15
Pali: Imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.16
Pali: dadeyyāti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.17
Pali: dadeyyunti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.18
Pali: āyasmato dammīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this back to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.19.1
Pali: Kayavikkaye kayavikkayasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is bartering, and he perceives it as such, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.20
Pali: Kayavikkaye vematiko, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is bartering, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.21
Pali: Kayavikkaye nakayavikkayasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is bartering, but he does not perceive it as such, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.22
Pali: Nakayavikkaye kayavikkayasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not bartering, but he perceives it as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.23
Pali: Nakayavikkaye vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not bartering, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.24
Pali: Nakayavikkaye nakayavikkayasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not bartering, and he does not perceive it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.25.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.26
Pali: agghaṁ pucchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he asks about the value; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.27
Pali: kappiyakārakassa ācikkhati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he tells an attendant; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.28
Pali: “idaṁ amhākaṁ atthi, amhākañca iminā ca iminā ca attho”ti bhaṇati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he says, “I have this and I have need of such-and-such;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.29
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.30
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.31
Pali: Kayavikkayasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ dasamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on bartering, the tenth, is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.32
Pali: Kosiyavaggo dutiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): The second subchapter on silk is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.33
Pali: Tassuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.34
Pali: Kosiyā suddhadvebhāgā, 
English (brahmali, published): “Silk, entirely, two parts, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.35
Pali: chabbassāni nisīdanaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): Six years, sitting blanket; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.36
Pali: Dve ca lomāni uggaṇhe, 
English (brahmali, published): And two on wool, should take, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np20:2.37
Pali: ubho nānappakārakāti. 
English (brahmali, published): Two on various kinds.”

English:

English (brahmali, published): He told the Buddha, who said,

English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:2.15
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, dasāhaparamaṁ atirekapattaṁ dhāretuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, you should keep an extra almsbowl for ten days at the most. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:2.16
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:2.17.1
Pali: “Dasāhaparamaṁ atirekapatto dhāretabbo. Taṁ atikkāmayato nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘A monk should keep an extra almsbowl for ten days at the most. If he keeps it longer than that, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.1
Pali: Dasāhaparamanti 
English (brahmali, published): For ten days at the most: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.2
Pali: dasāhaparamatā dhāretabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): it should be kept ten days at a maximum. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.3

English (brahmali, published): An extra almsbowl: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.4
Pali: anadhiṭṭhito avikappito. 
English (brahmali, published): a bowl that is not determined, nor assigned to another. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.5
Pali: Patto nāma 
English (brahmali, published): An almsbowl: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.6
Pali: dve pattā ayopatto mattikāpattoti. 
English (brahmali, published): there are two kinds of bowls: the iron bowl and the ceramic bowl. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.7
Pali: Tayo pattassa vaṇṇā ukkaṭṭho patto majjhimo patto omako patto. 
English (brahmali, published): And there are three sizes of bowls: the large bowl, the medium bowl, and the small bowl. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.8
Pali: Ukkaṭṭho nāma patto aḍḍhāḷhakodanaṁ gaṇhāti catubhāgaṁ khādanaṁ tadupiyaṁ byañjanaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The large bowl: it holds half an <i lang='pi' translate='no'>āḷhaka</i> measure of cooked rice, a fourth part of fresh food, and a suitable amount of curry. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.9
Pali: Majjhimo nāma patto nāḷikodanaṁ gaṇhāti catubhāgaṁ khādanaṁ tadupiyaṁ byañjanaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The medium bowl: it holds a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>nāḷika</i> measure of cooked rice, a fourth part of fresh food, and a suitable amount of curry. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.10
Pali: Omako nāma patto patthodanaṁ gaṇhāti catubhāgaṁ khādanaṁ tadupiyaṁ byañjanaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The small bowl: it holds a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>pattha</i> measure of cooked rice, a fourth part of fresh food, and a suitable amount of curry. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.11
Pali: Tato ukkaṭṭho apatto, omako apatto. 
English (brahmali, published): Anything larger than this is not a bowl, nor anything smaller. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.12
Pali: Taṁ atikkāmayato nissaggiyo hotīti 
English (brahmali, published): If he keeps it longer than that, it becomes subject to relinquishment: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.13
Pali: ekādase aruṇuggamane nissaggiyo hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): it becomes subject to relinquishment at dawn on the eleventh day. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.14
Pali: Nissajjitabbo saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā puggalassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The bowl should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.15
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.16
Pali: Tena bhikkhunā saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā vuḍḍhānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ pāde vanditvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): After approaching the Sangha, that monk should arrange his upper robe over one shoulder and pay respect at the feet of the senior monks. He should then squat on his heels, raise his joined palms, and say, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.17
Pali: “ayaṁ me, bhante, patto dasāhātikkanto nissaggiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Venerables, this almsbowl, which I have kept over ten days, is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.18
Pali: Imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to the Sangha.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.19
Pali: Nissajjitvā āpatti desetabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): After relinquishing it, he should confess the offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.20
Pali: Byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena āpatti paṭiggahetabbā, 
English (brahmali, published): The confession should be received by a competent and capable monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.21
Pali: nissaṭṭhapatto dātabbo—
English (brahmali, published): The relinquished bowl is then to be given back: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.22
Pali: “Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.23
Pali: Ayaṁ patto itthannāmassa bhikkhuno nissaggiyo saṅghassa nissaṭṭho. 
English (brahmali, published): This almsbowl, which was to be relinquished by monk so-and-so, has been relinquished to the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.24
Pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho imaṁ pattaṁ itthannāmassa bhikkhuno dadeyyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If the Sangha is ready, it should give this bowl back to monk so-and-so.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.25
Pali: Tena bhikkhunā sambahule bhikkhū upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā vuḍḍhānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ pāde vanditvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassu vacanīyā—
English (brahmali, published): Or: after approaching several monks, that monk should arrange his upper robe over one shoulder and pay respect at the feet of the senior monks. He should then squat on his heels, raise his joined palms, and say: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.26
Pali: “ayaṁ me, bhante, patto dasāhātikkanto nissaggiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this almsbowl, which I have kept over ten days, is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.27
Pali: Imāhaṁ āyasmantānaṁ nissajjāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.28
Pali: Nissajjitvā āpatti desetabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): After relinquishing it, he should confess the offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.29
Pali: Byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena āpatti paṭiggahetabbā, 
English (brahmali, published): The confession should be received by a competent and capable monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.30
Pali: nissaṭṭhapatto dātabbo—
English (brahmali, published): The relinquished bowl is then to be given back: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.31
Pali: “Suṇantu me āyasmantā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please, venerables, I ask you to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.32
Pali: Ayaṁ patto itthannāmassa bhikkhuno nissaggiyo āyasmantānaṁ nissaṭṭho. 
English (brahmali, published): This almsbowl, which was to be relinquished by monk so-and-so, has been relinquished to you. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.33
Pali: Yadāyasmantānaṁ pattakallaṁ, āyasmantā imaṁ pattaṁ itthannāmassa bhikkhuno dadeyyun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If the venerables are ready, you should give this bowl back to monk so-and-so.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.34
Pali: Tena bhikkhunā ekaṁ bhikkhuṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): Or: after approaching a single monk, that monk should arrange his upper robe over one shoulder, squat on his heels, raise his joined palms, and say: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.35
Pali: “ayaṁ me, āvuso, patto dasāhātikkanto nissaggiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): “This almsbowl, which I have kept over ten days, is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.36
Pali: Imāhaṁ āyasmato nissajjāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.37
Pali: Nissajjitvā āpatti desetabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): After relinquishing it, he should confess the offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.38
Pali: Tena bhikkhunā āpatti paṭiggahetabbā, 
English (brahmali, published): The confession should be received by that monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.39
Pali: nissaṭṭhapatto dātabbo—
English (brahmali, published): The relinquished bowl is then to be given back: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.40
Pali: “imaṁ pattaṁ āyasmato dammī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this almsbowl back to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.41.1
Pali: Dasāhātikkante atikkantasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than ten days and he perceives it as more, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.42
Pali: Dasāhātikkante vematiko, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than ten days, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.43
Pali: Dasāhātikkante anatikkantasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than ten days, but he perceives it as less, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.44
Pali: Anadhiṭṭhite adhiṭṭhitasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been determined, but he perceives that it has, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.45
Pali: Avikappite vikappitasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been assigned to another, but he perceives that it has, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.46
Pali: Avissajjite vissajjitasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been given away, but he perceives that it has, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.47
Pali: Anaṭṭhe naṭṭhasaññī nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been lost, but he perceives that it has, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.48
Pali: Avinaṭṭhe vinaṭṭhasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been destroyed, but he perceives that it has, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.49
Pali: Abhinne bhinnasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been broken, but he perceives that it has, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.50
Pali: Avilutte viluttasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been stolen, but he perceives that it has, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.51
Pali: Nissaggiyaṁ pattaṁ anissajjitvā paribhuñjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he uses an almsbowl that should be relinquished without first relinquishing it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.52
Pali: Dasāhānatikkante atikkantasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than ten days, but he perceives it as more, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.53
Pali: Dasāhānatikkante vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than ten days, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.54
Pali: Dasāhānatikkante anatikkantasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than ten days and he perceives it as less, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.55.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.56
Pali: antodasāhaṁ adhiṭṭheti, vikappeti, vissajjeti, nassati, vinassati, bhijjati, acchinditvā gaṇhanti, vissāsaṁ gaṇhanti, 
English (brahmali, published): if, within ten days, it has been determined, assigned to another, given away, lost, destroyed, broken, stolen, or taken on trust; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.57
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:3.58
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:4.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū nissaṭṭhapattaṁ na denti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the monks from the group of six did not give back a relinquished bowl. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:4.2
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:4.3
Pali: “Na, bhikkhave, nissaṭṭhapatto na dātabbo. Yo na dadeyya, āpatti dukkaṭassā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, a relinquished almsbowl should be given back. If you don’t give it back, you commit an offense of wrong conduct.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np21:4.4
Pali: Pattasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ paṭhamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on keeping an extra almsbowl beyond ten days, the first, is finished.

English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on almsbowls 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:0.5
Pali: 22. Ūnapañcabandhanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 22. Exchanging an almsbowl with fewer than five mends 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sakkesu viharati kapilavatthusmiṁ nigrodhārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying in the Sakyan country in the Banyan Tree Monastery at Kapilavatthu. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarena kumbhakārena bhikkhū pavāritā honti—
English (brahmali, published): At that time a potter had invited the monks, saying, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.1.3
Pali: “yesaṁ ayyānaṁ pattena attho ahaṁ pattenā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “If any of you needs an almsbowl, I’ll provide it.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.1.4
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū na mattaṁ jānitvā bahū patte viññāpenti. 
English (brahmali, published): But the monks had no sense of moderation, and they asked for many bowls. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.1.5
Pali: Yesaṁ khuddakā pattā te mahante patte viññāpenti. 
English (brahmali, published): Those who had small bowls asked for large ones, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.1.6
Pali: Yesaṁ mahantā pattā te khuddake patte viññāpenti. 
English (brahmali, published): and those who had large bowls asked for small ones. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.1.7
Pali: Atha kho so kumbhakāro bhikkhūnaṁ bahū patte karonto na sakkoti aññaṁ vikkāyikaṁ bhaṇḍaṁ kātuṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): The potter was so busy making bowls for the monks that he was unable to make goods for sale. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.1.8
Pali: attanāpi na yāpeti, puttadārāpissa kilamanti. 
English (brahmali, published): He could not make a living for himself, and his wives and children suffered. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.1.9
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.1.10
Pali: “kathañhi nāma samaṇā sakyaputtiyā na mattaṁ jānitvā bahū patte viññāpessanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the Sakyan monastics have no sense of moderation and ask for many bowls? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.1.11
Pali: Ayaṁ imesaṁ bahū patte karonto na sakkoti aññaṁ vikkāyikaṁ bhaṇḍaṁ kātuṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): This potter is so busy making bowls for them that he is unable to make goods for sale. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.1.12
Pali: attanāpi na yāpeti, puttadārāpissa kilamantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): He is unable to make a living for himself, and his wives and children are suffering.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.1.13
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.1.14
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.1.15
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhū na mattaṁ jānitvā bahū patte viññāpessantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can those monks have no sense of moderation and ask for many bowls?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.1.16
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū te anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking those monks in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.1.17
Pali: saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhū na mattaṁ jānitvā bahū patte viññāpentīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that there are monks who do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.1.18
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.1.19
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.1.20
Pali: kathañhi nāma te, bhikkhave, moghapurisā na mattaṁ jānitvā bahū patte viññāpessanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can those foolish men do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.1.21
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.1.22
Pali: vigarahitvā dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking them, he gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.1.23
Pali: “na, bhikkhave, patto viññāpetabbo. Yo viññāpeyya, āpatti dukkaṭassā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, a monk should not ask for an almsbowl. If he does, he commits an offense of wrong conduct.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarassa bhikkhuno patto bhinno hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the bowl of a certain monk broke. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.2.2
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu—“bhagavatā paṭikkhittaṁ pattaṁ viññāpetun”ti kukkuccāyanto na viññāpeti. 
English (brahmali, published): Knowing that the Buddha had prohibited asking for a bowl and being afraid of wrongdoing, he did not ask for a new one. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.2.3
Pali: Hatthesu piṇḍāya carati. 
English (brahmali, published): As a consequence, he collected almsfood with his hands. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.2.4
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.2.5
Pali: “kathañhi nāma samaṇā sakyaputtiyā hatthesu piṇḍāya carissanti, seyyathāpi titthiyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the Sakyan monastics collect almsfood with their hands, just like the monastics of other religions?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.2.6
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those people 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.2.7
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): and they told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.2.8
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.2.9
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, naṭṭhapattassa vā bhinnapattassa vā pattaṁ viññāpetun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to ask for a new almsbowl if your bowl has been lost or is broken.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.3.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū—“bhagavatā anuññātaṁ naṭṭhapattassa vā bhinnapattassa vā pattaṁ viññāpetun”ti appamattakenapi bhinnena appamattakenapi khaṇḍena vilikhitamattenapi bahū patte viññāpenti. 
English (brahmali, published): When they heard about the Buddha’s allowance, the monks from the group of six asked for many bowls even when their existing bowls only had a minor chip or scratch. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.3.2
Pali: Atha kho so kumbhakāro bhikkhūnaṁ tatheva bahū patte karonto na sakkoti aññaṁ vikkāyikaṁ bhaṇḍaṁ kātuṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): Once again that potter was so busy making bowls for the monks that he was unable to make goods for sale. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.3.3
Pali: attanāpi na yāpeti, puttadārāpissa kilamanti. 
English (brahmali, published): He could not make a living for himself, and his wives and children suffered. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.3.4
Pali: Manussā tatheva ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): And people complained and criticized them as before. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.3.5
Pali: “kathañhi nāma samaṇā sakyaputtiyā na mattaṁ jānitvā bahū patte viññāpessanti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.3.6
Pali: Ayaṁ imesaṁ bahū patte karonto na sakkoti aññaṁ vikkāyikaṁ bhaṇḍaṁ kātuṁ, attanāpi na yāpeti, puttadārāpissa kilamantī”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.3.7
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.3.8
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.3.9
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū appamattakenapi bhinnena appamattakenapi khaṇḍena vilikhitamattenapi bahū patte viññāpessantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the monks from the group of six ask for many bowls even when their existing bowls only have a minor chip or scratch?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.3.10
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū chabbaggiye bhikkhū anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking those monks in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.3.11
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, appamattakenapi bhinnena appamattakenapi khaṇḍena vilikhitamattenapi bahū patte viññāpethā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.3.12
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.3.13
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.3.14
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, appamattakenapi bhinnena appamattakenapi khaṇḍena vilikhitamattenapi bahū patte viññāpessatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.3.15
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.3.16
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.3.17.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu ūnapañcabandhanena pattena aññaṁ navaṁ pattaṁ cetāpeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk exchanges an almsbowl with fewer than five mends for a new almsbowl, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.3.18
Pali: Tena bhikkhunā so patto bhikkhuparisāya nissajjitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): That monk should relinquish that almsbowl to a gathering of monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.3.19
Pali: Yo ca tassā bhikkhuparisāya pattapariyanto so tassa bhikkhuno padātabbo— ‘ayaṁ te, bhikkhu, patto yāva bhedanāya dhāretabbo’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): He should then be given the last almsbowl belonging to that gathering: “Monk, this bowl is yours. Keep it until it breaks.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:1.3.20
Pali: Ayaṁ tattha sāmīcī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the proper procedure.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.5
Pali: Ūnapañcabandhano nāma patto 
English (brahmali, published): An almsbowl with fewer than five mends: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.6
Pali: abandhano vā ekabandhano vā dvibandhano vā tibandhano vā catubandhano vā. 
English (brahmali, published): it has no mends, one mend, two mends, three mends, or four mends. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.7
Pali: Abandhanokāso nāma patto 
English (brahmali, published): An almsbowl with a mend that does not count: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.8
Pali: yassa dvaṅgulā rāji na hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): one that does not have a fracture of 3.5 cm. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.9
Pali: Bandhanokāso nāma patto 
English (brahmali, published): An almsbowl with a mend that counts: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.10
Pali: yassa dvaṅgulā rāji hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): one that does have a fracture of 3.5 cm. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.11
Pali: Navo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): New almsbowl: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.12
Pali: patto viññattiṁ upādāya vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): asked for is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.13
Pali: Cetāpeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Exchanges: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.14
Pali: viññāpeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if he asks, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.15
Pali: Paṭilābhena nissaggiyo hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): When he gets the bowl, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.16
Pali: Saṅghamajjhe nissajjitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): It should be relinquished in the midst of the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.17
Pali: Sabbeheva adhiṭṭhitapattaṁ gahetvā sannipatitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): All determined bowls should be brought together. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.18
Pali: Na lāmako patto adhiṭṭhātabbo—
English (brahmali, published): One should not determine an inferior bowl, thinking, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.19
Pali: “mahagghaṁ pattaṁ gahessāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I’ll get a valuable one.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.20
Pali: Sace lāmakaṁ pattaṁ adhiṭṭheti— “mahagghaṁ pattaṁ gahessāmī”ti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one determines an inferior bowl, thinking, “I’ll get a valuable one,” one commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.21
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.22
Pali: Tena bhikkhunā saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā vuḍḍhānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ pāde vanditvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): After approaching the Sangha, that monk should arrange his upper robe over one shoulder and pay respect at the feet of the senior monks. He should then squat on his heels, raise his joined palms, and say: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.23
Pali: “ayaṁ me, bhante, patto ūnapañcabandhanena pattena cetāpito nissaggiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Venerables, this almsbowl, which I got in exchange for a bowl that had fewer than five mends, is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.24
Pali: Imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to the Sangha.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.25
Pali: Nissajjitvā āpatti desetabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): After relinquishing it, he should confess the offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.26
Pali: Byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena āpatti paṭiggahetabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): The confession should be received by a competent and capable monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.27
Pali: Pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu pattaggāhāpako sammannitabbo—
English (brahmali, published): A monk who has five qualities should be appointed as the distributor of almsbowls: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.28
Pali: yo na chandāgatiṁ gaccheyya, na dosāgatiṁ gaccheyya, na mohāgatiṁ gaccheyya, na bhayāgatiṁ gaccheyya, gāhitāgāhitañca jāneyya. 
English (brahmali, published): he’s not biased by favoritism, ill will, confusion, or fear, and he knows what has and has not been distributed. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.29
Pali: “Evañca pana, bhikkhave, sammannitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, he should be appointed like this. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.30
Pali: Paṭhamaṁ bhikkhu yācitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): First the monk should be asked, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.31
Pali: Yācitvā byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena saṅgho ñāpetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): and then a competent and capable monk should inform the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.32
Pali: ‘Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.33
Pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuṁ pattaggāhāpakaṁ sammanneyya. 
English (brahmali, published): If the Sangha is ready, it should appoint monk so-and-so as the distributor of almsbowls. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.34
Pali: Esā ñatti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the motion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.35
Pali: Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.36
Pali: Saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuṁ pattaggāhāpakaṁ sammannati. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha appoints monk so-and-so as the distributor of almsbowls. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.37
Pali: Yassāyasmato khamati itthannāmassa bhikkhuno pattaggāhāpakassa sammuti, so tuṇhassa; 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who agrees to appointing monk so-and-so as the distributor of almsbowls should remain silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.38
Pali: yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who doesn’t agree should speak up. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.39
Pali: Sammato saṅghena itthannāmo bhikkhu pattaggāhāpako. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha has appointed monk so-and-so as the distributor of almsbowls. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.40
Pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.41
Pali: evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I will remember it thus.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.42
Pali: Tena sammatena bhikkhunā patto gāhetabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): The appointed monk should give away that relinquished bowl. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.43
Pali: Thero vattabbo—
English (brahmali, published): He should ask the most senior monk, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.44
Pali: “gaṇhātu, bhante, thero pattan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, would you like this bowl?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.45
Pali: Sace thero gaṇhāti, therassa patto dutiyassa gāhetabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): If the most senior monk takes it, his old bowl should be offered to the next monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.46
Pali: Na ca tassa anuddayatāya na gahetabbo. Yo na gaṇheyya, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): He should not not take that bowl out of sympathy. If he does, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.47
Pali: Apattakassa na gāhetabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): It is not to be offered to anyone who does not have bowl. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.48
Pali: Eteneva upāyena yāva saṅghanavakā gāhetabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): In this manner it should be offered all the way to the most junior monk in the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.49
Pali: Yo ca tassā bhikkhuparisāya pattapariyanto, so tassa bhikkhuno padātabbo— “ayaṁ te, bhikkhu, patto yāva bhedanāya dhāretabbo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): He should then be given the last almsbowl belonging to that gathering: “Monk, this bowl is yours. Keep it until it breaks”: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.50
Pali: Tena bhikkhunā so patto na adese nikkhipitabbo, na abhogena bhuñjitabbo, na vissajjetabbo— “kathāyaṁ patto nasseyya vā vinasseyya vā bhijjeyya vā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): That monk is not to store that bowl in an unsuitable place, use it in an unsuitable way, or give it away, thinking, “How may this bowl be lost, destroyed, or broken?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.51
Pali: Sace adese vā nikkhipati abhogena vā bhuñjati vissajjeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he stores it in an unsuitable place, uses it in an unsuitable way, or gives it away, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.52
Pali: Ayaṁ tattha sāmīcīti 
English (brahmali, published): This is the proper procedure: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.1.53
Pali: ayaṁ tattha anudhammatā. 
English (brahmali, published): this is the right method. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.1
Pali: Abandhanena pattena abandhanaṁ pattaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges a bowl without mends for a bowl without mends, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.2
Pali: Abandhanena pattena ekabandhanaṁ pattaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges a bowl without mends for a bowl with one mend, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.3
Pali: Abandhanena pattena dvibandhanaṁ pattaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges a bowl without mends for a bowl with two mends, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.4
Pali: Abandhanena pattena tibandhanaṁ pattaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges a bowl without mends for a bowl with three mends, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.5
Pali: Abandhanena pattena catubandhanaṁ pattaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges a bowl without mends for a bowl with four mends, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.6
Pali: Ekabandhanena pattena abandhanaṁ pattaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges a bowl with one mend for a bowl without mends, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.7
Pali: Ekabandhanena pattena ekabandhanaṁ pattaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges a bowl with one mend for a bowl with one mend, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.8
Pali: Ekabandhanena pattena dvibandhanaṁ pattaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges a bowl with one mend for a bowl with two mends, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.9
Pali: Ekabandhanena pattena tibandhanaṁ pattaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges a bowl with one mend for a bowl with three mends, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.10
Pali: Ekabandhanena pattena catubandhanaṁ pattaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges a bowl with one mend for a bowl with four mends, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.11
Pali: Dvibandhanena pattena abandhanaṁ pattaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges a bowl with two mends for a bowl without mends, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.12
Pali: Dvibandhanena pattena ekabandhanaṁ pattaṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges a bowl with two mends for a bowl with one mend … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.13
Pali: dvibandhanaṁ pattaṁ … 
English (brahmali, published): for a bowl with two mends … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.14
Pali: tibandhanaṁ pattaṁ … 
English (brahmali, published): for a bowl with three mends … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.15
Pali: catubandhanaṁ pattaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges a bowl with two mends for a bowl with four mends, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.16
Pali: Tibandhanena pattena abandhanaṁ pattaṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges a bowl with three mends for a bowl without mends … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.17
Pali: ekabandhanaṁ pattaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): for a bowl with one mend … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.18
Pali: dvibandhanaṁ pattaṁ … 
English (brahmali, published): for a bowl with two mends … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.19
Pali: tibandhanaṁ pattaṁ … 
English (brahmali, published): for a bowl with three mends … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.20
Pali: catubandhanaṁ pattaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges a bowl with three mends for a bowl with four mends, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.21
Pali: Catubandhanena pattena abandhanaṁ pattaṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges a bowl with four mends for a bowl without mends … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.22
Pali: ekabandhanaṁ pattaṁ … 
English (brahmali, published): for a bowl with one mend … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.23
Pali: dvibandhanaṁ pattaṁ … 
English (brahmali, published): for a bowl with two mends … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.24
Pali: tibandhanaṁ pattaṁ … 
English (brahmali, published): for a bowl with three mends … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.25
Pali: catubandhanaṁ pattaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges a bowl with four mends for a bowl with four mends, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.26
Pali: Abandhanena pattena abandhanokāsaṁ pattaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges a bowl without mends for a bowl without mends that count, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.27
Pali: Abandhanena pattena ekabandhanokāsaṁ pattaṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges a bowl without mends for a bowl with one mend that counts …

Pali: dvibandhanokāsaṁ pattaṁ … 
English (brahmali, published): for a bowl with two mends that count … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.29
Pali: tibandhanokāsaṁ pattaṁ … 
English (brahmali, published): for a bowl with three mends that count … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.30
Pali: catubandhanokāsaṁ pattaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges a bowl without mends for a bowl with four mends that count, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.31
Pali: Ekabandhanena pattena abandhanokāsaṁ pattaṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges a bowl with one mend for a bowl without mends that count …

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.38

English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges a bowl with three mends for a bowl without mends that count … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.39
Pali: catubandhanokāsaṁ pattaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he exchanges a bowl with three mends for a bowl with four mends that count, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.65.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.66
Pali: naṭṭhapattassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if his almsbowl is lost; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.67
Pali: bhinnapattassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if his almsbowl is broken; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.68
Pali: ñātakānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): if it is from relatives; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.69
Pali: pavāritānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is from those who have given an invitation; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.70
Pali: aññassatthāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is for the benefit of someone else; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.71
Pali: attano dhanena, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is by means of his own property; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.72
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.73
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np22:2.2.74
Pali: Ūnapañcabandhanasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ dutiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on exchanging an almsbowl with fewer than five mends, the second, is finished.
