Pali: 23. Bhesajjasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 23. Storing the five tonics too long 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā pilindavaccho rājagahe pabbhāraṁ sodhāpeti leṇaṁ kattukāmo. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Pilindavaccha was clearing a slope near Rājagaha, intending to build a shelter. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.1.3
Pali: Atha kho rājā māgadho seniyo bimbisāro yenāyasmā pilindavaccho tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ pilindavacchaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho rājā māgadho seniyo bimbisāro āyasmantaṁ pilindavacchaṁ etadavoca—“kiṁ, bhante, thero kārāpetī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): Just then King Seniya Bimbisāra of Magadha went to Pilindavaccha, bowed, sat down, and said, “Venerable, what are you having made?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.1.4
Pali: “Pabbhāraṁ, mahārāja, sodhāpemi leṇaṁ kattukāmo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I’m clearing a slope, great king. I want to build a shelter.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.1.5
Pali: “Attho, bhante, ayyassa ārāmikenā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Do you need a monastery worker?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.1.6
Pali: “Na kho, mahārāja, bhagavatā ārāmiko anuññāto”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “The Buddha hasn’t allowed monastery workers.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.1.7
Pali: “Tena hi, bhante, bhagavantaṁ paṭipucchitvā mama āroceyyāthā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Well then, sir, please ask the Buddha and tell me the outcome.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.1.8
Pali: “Evaṁ, mahārājā”ti kho āyasmā pilindavaccho rañño māgadhassa seniyassa bimbisārassa paccassosi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes, great king.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.1.9
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā pilindavaccho rājānaṁ māgadhaṁ seniyaṁ bimbisāraṁ dhammiyā kathāya sandassesi samādapesi samuttejesi sampahaṁsesi. 
English (brahmali, published): Pilindavaccha then instructed, inspired, and gladdened King Bimbisāra with a teaching, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.1.10
Pali: Atha kho rājā māgadho seniyo bimbisāro āyasmatā pilindavacchena dhammiyā kathāya sandassito samādapito samuttejito sampahaṁsito uṭṭhāyāsanā āyasmantaṁ pilindavacchaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): after which the king got up from his seat, bowed down, circumambulated Pilindavaccha with his right side toward him, and left. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.1.11
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā pilindavaccho bhagavato santike dūtaṁ pāhesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards Pilindavaccha sent a message to the Buddha: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.1.12
Pali: “rājā, bhante, māgadho seniyo bimbisāro ārāmikaṁ dātukāmo. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, King Seniya Bimbisāra of Magadha wishes to provide a monastery worker. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.1.13
Pali: Kathaṁ nu kho, bhante, mayā paṭipajjitabban”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): What should I tell him?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.1.14
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha then gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.1.15
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, ārāmikan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow monastery workers.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.1.16
Pali: Dutiyampi kho rājā māgadho seniyo bimbisāro yenāyasmā pilindavaccho tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ pilindavacchaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho rājā māgadho seniyo bimbisāro āyasmantaṁ pilindavacchaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Once again King Bimbisāra went to Pilindavaccha, bowed, sat down, and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.1.17
Pali: “anuññāto, bhante, bhagavatā ārāmiko”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, has the Buddha allowed monastery workers?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.1.18
Pali: “Evaṁ, mahārājā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes, great king.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.1.19
Pali: “Tena hi, bhante, ayyassa ārāmikaṁ dammī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Well then, I’ll provide you with a monastery worker.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.1.20
Pali: Atha kho rājā māgadho seniyo bimbisāro āyasmato pilindavacchassa ārāmikaṁ paṭissuṇitvā vissaritvā cirena satiṁ paṭilabhitvā aññataraṁ sabbatthakaṁ mahāmattaṁ āmantesi—“yo mayā, bhaṇe, ayyassa ārāmiko paṭissuto, dinno so ārāmiko”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): But after making this promise, he forgot, and only remembered after a long time. He then addressed the official in charge of all practical affairs: “Listen, has the monastery worker I promised been provided?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.1.21
Pali: “Na kho, deva, ayyassa ārāmiko dinno”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “No, sir, he hasn’t.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.1.22
Pali: “Kīvaciraṁ nu kho, bhaṇe, ito hi taṁ hotī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How long has it been since we made that promise?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.1.23
Pali: Atha kho so mahāmatto rattiyo gaṇetvā rājānaṁ māgadhaṁ seniyaṁ bimbisāraṁ etadavoca—“pañca, deva, rattisatānī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The official counted the days and said, “It’s been five hundred days.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.1.24
Pali: “Tena hi, bhaṇe, ayyassa pañca ārāmikasatāni dehī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Well then, provide him with five hundred monastery workers.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.1.25
Pali: “Evaṁ, devā”ti 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.1.26
Pali: kho so mahāmatto rañño māgadhassa seniyassa bimbisārassa paṭissuṇitvā āyasmato pilindavacchassa pañca ārāmikasatāni pādāsi, pāṭiyekko gāmo nivisi. 
English (brahmali, published): The official provided Pilindavaccha with those monastery workers and a separate village was established. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.1.27
Pali: Ārāmikagāmakotipi naṁ āhaṁsu, pilindagāmakotipi naṁ āhaṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): They called it “The Monastery Workers’ Village” and “Pilinda Village”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā pilindavaccho tasmiṁ gāmake kulūpako hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): And Pilindavaccha began associating with the families in that village. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.2.2
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā pilindavaccho pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaraṁ ādāya pilindagāmakaṁ piṇḍāya pāvisi. 
English (brahmali, published): After robing up one morning, he took his bowl and robe and went to Pilinda Village for alms. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.2.3
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena tasmiṁ gāmake ussavo hoti. Dārakā alaṅkatā mālākitā kīḷanti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time they were holding a celebration in that village and the children were dressed up with ornaments and garlands. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.2.4
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā pilindavaccho pilindagāmake sapadānaṁ piṇḍāya caramāno yena aññatarassa ārāmikassa nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): As Pilindavaccha was walking on continuous almsround, he came to the house of a certain monastery worker where he sat down on the prepared seat. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.2.5
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena tassā ārāmikiniyā dhītā aññe dārake alaṅkate mālākite passitvā rodati—“mālaṁ me detha, alaṅkāraṁ me dethā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Just then the daughter of that house had seen the other children dressed up in ornaments and garlands. She cried, saying, “Give me a garland! Give me ornaments!” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.2.6
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā pilindavaccho taṁ ārāmikiniṁ etadavoca—“kissāyaṁ dārikā rodatī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): Pilindavaccha asked her mother why the girl was crying. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.2.7
Pali: “Ayaṁ, bhante, dārikā aññe dārake alaṅkate mālākite passitvā rodati—‘mālaṁ me detha, alaṅkāraṁ me dethā’ti. Kuto amhākaṁ duggatānaṁ mālā kuto, alaṅkāro”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): She told him, adding, “Poor people like us can’t afford garlands and ornaments.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.2.8
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā pilindavaccho aññataraṁ tiṇaṇḍupakaṁ gahetvā taṁ ārāmikiniṁ etadavoca—“handimaṁ tiṇaṇḍupakaṁ tassā dārikāya sīse paṭimuñcā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Pilindavaccha took a pad of grass and said to the mother, “Here, place this on the girl’s head.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.2.9
Pali: Atha kho sā ārāmikinī taṁ tiṇaṇḍupakaṁ gahetvā tassā dārikāya sīse paṭimuñci. Sā ahosi suvaṇṇamālā abhirūpā dassanīyā pāsādikā. 
English (brahmali, published): She did, and it turned into a beautiful golden garland. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.2.10
Pali: Natthi tādisā raññopi antepure suvaṇṇamālā. 
English (brahmali, published): Even the royal compound had nothing like it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.2.11
Pali: Manussā rañño māgadhassa seniyassa bimbisārassa ārocesuṁ—“amukassa, deva, ārāmikassa ghare suvaṇṇamālā abhirūpā dassanīyā pāsādikā. 
English (brahmali, published): People told King Bimbisāra, “In the house of such-and-such a monastery worker there’s a beautiful golden garland. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.2.12
Pali: Natthi tādisā devassāpi antepure suvaṇṇamālā. 
English (brahmali, published): Even in your court, sir, there’s nothing like it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.2.13
Pali: Kuto tassa duggatassa. 
English (brahmali, published): So how did those poor people get it? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.2.14
Pali: Nissaṁsayaṁ corikāya ābhatā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): They must have stolen it.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.2.15
Pali: Atha kho rājā māgadho seniyo bimbisāro taṁ ārāmikakulaṁ bandhāpesi. 
English (brahmali, published): King Bimbisāra had that family imprisoned. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.2.16
Pali: Dutiyampi kho āyasmā pilindavaccho pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaraṁ ādāya pilindagāmakaṁ piṇḍāya pāvisi. 
English (brahmali, published): Once again Pilindavaccha robed up in the morning, took his bowl and robe, and went to Pilinda Village for alms. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.2.17
Pali: Pilindagāmake sapadānaṁ piṇḍāya caramāno yena tassa ārāmikassa nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paṭivissake pucchi—“kahaṁ imaṁ ārāmikakulaṁ gatan”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): As he was walking on continuous almsround, he came to the house of that monastery worker. He then asked the neighbors what had happened to that family. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.2.18
Pali: “Etissā, bhante, suvaṇṇamālāya kāraṇā raññā bandhāpitan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “The king has jailed them, venerable, because of that golden garland.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.1
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā pilindavaccho yena rañño māgadhassa seniyassa bimbisārassa nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): Pilindavaccha then went to King Bimbisāra’s house and sat down on the prepared seat. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.2
Pali: Atha kho rājā māgadho seniyo bimbisāro yenāyasmā pilindavaccho tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ pilindavacchaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): King Bimbisāra approached Pilindavaccha, bowed, and sat down. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.3
Pali: Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho rājānaṁ māgadhaṁ seniyaṁ bimbisāraṁ āyasmā pilindavaccho etadavoca—“kissa, mahārāja, ārāmikakulaṁ bandhāpitan”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): Pilindavaccha said, “Great king, why have you jailed the family of that monastery worker?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.4
Pali: “Tassa, bhante, ārāmikassa ghare suvaṇṇamālā abhirūpā dassanīyā pāsādikā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, in the house of that monastery worker there was a beautiful golden garland. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.5
Pali: Natthi tādisā amhākampi antepure suvaṇṇamālā. 
English (brahmali, published): Even the royal compound has nothing like it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.6
Pali: Kuto tassa duggatassa. 
English (brahmali, published): So how did those poor people get it? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.7
Pali: Nissaṁsayaṁ corikāya ābhatā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): They must have stolen it.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.8
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā pilindavaccho rañño māgadhassa seniyassa bimbisārassa pāsādaṁ suvaṇṇanti adhimucci. 
English (brahmali, published): Pilindavaccha then focused his mind on turning King Bimbisāra’s stilt house into gold. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.9
Pali: So ahosi sabbasovaṇṇamayo. 
English (brahmali, published): As a result, the whole house became gold. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.10
Pali: “Idaṁ pana te, mahārāja, tāva bahuṁ suvaṇṇaṁ kuto”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): He said, “Great king, how did you get so much gold?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.11
Pali: “Aññātaṁ, bhante, ayyasseveso iddhānubhāvo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Understood, sir! It’s your supernormal power.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.12
Pali: Taṁ ārāmikakulaṁ muñcāpesi. 
English (brahmali, published): He then released that family. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.13
Pali: Manussā—“ayyena kira pilindavacchena sarājikāya parisāya uttarimanussadhammaṁ iddhipāṭihāriyaṁ dassitan”ti, 
English (brahmali, published): People said, “They say Venerable Pilindavaccha has performed a superhuman feat, a wonder of supernormal power, for the king and his court!” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.14
Pali: attamanā abhippasannā āyasmato pilindavacchassa pañca bhesajjāni abhihariṁsu, seyyathidaṁ—sappi navanītaṁ telaṁ madhu phāṇitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Being delighted and gaining confidence in Pilindavaccha, they brought him the five tonics: ghee, butter, oil, honey, and syrup. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.15
Pali: Pakatiyāpi ca āyasmā pilindavaccho lābhī hoti pañcannaṁ bhesajjānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Ordinarily, too, Pilindavaccha was getting the five tonics. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.16
Pali: Laddhaṁ laddhaṁ parisāya vissajjeti. 
English (brahmali, published): Since he was getting so much, he gave it away to his followers, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.17
Pali: Parisā cassa hoti bāhullikā. 
English (brahmali, published): who ended up with an abundance of tonics. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.18
Pali: Laddhaṁ laddhaṁ kolambepi ghaṭepi pūretvā paṭisāmeti, parissāvanānipi thavikāyopi pūretvā vātapānesu laggeti. 
English (brahmali, published): After filling up basins and waterpots and setting these aside, they filled their water filters and bags and hung these in the windows. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.19
Pali: Tāni olīnavilīnāni tiṭṭhanti. Undūrehipi vihārā okiṇṇavikiṇṇā honti. 
English (brahmali, published): But the tonics were dripping, and the dwellings became infested with rats. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.20
Pali: Manussā vihāracārikaṁ āhiṇḍantā passitvā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): When people walking about the dwellings saw this, they complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.21
Pali: “antokoṭṭhāgārikā ime samaṇā sakyaputtiyā, seyyathāpi rājā māgadho seniyo bimbisāro”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “These Sakyan monastics are hoarding things indoors, just like King Seniya Bimbisāra of Magadha.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.22
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those people 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.23
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.24
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhū evarūpāya bāhullāya cetessantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can these monks choose to live with such abundance?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.25
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū te anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking those monks in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.26
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhū evarūpāya bāhullāya cetentī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that there are monks who live like this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.27
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.28
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.29
Pali: kathañhi nāma te, bhikkhave, moghapurisā evarūpāya bāhullāya cetessanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can those foolish men live like this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.30
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.31
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.32.1
Pali: “Yāni kho pana tāni gilānānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ paṭisāyanīyāni bhesajjāni, seyyathidaṁ— sappi navanītaṁ telaṁ madhu phāṇitaṁ, tāni paṭiggahetvā sattāhaparamaṁ sannidhikārakaṁ paribhuñjitabbāni. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘After being received, the tonics allowable for sick monks—that is, ghee, butter, oil, honey, and syrup—should be used from storage for at most seven days. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:1.3.33
Pali: Taṁ atikkāmayato nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If one uses them longer than that, one commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.1
Pali: Yāni kho pana tāni gilānānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ paṭisāyanīyāni bhesajjānīti 
English (brahmali, published): The tonics allowable for sick monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.2
Pali: sappi nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Ghee: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.3
Pali: gosappi vā ajikāsappi vā mahiṁsasappi vā yesaṁ maṁsaṁ kappati tesaṁ sappi. 
English (brahmali, published): ghee from cows, ghee from goats, ghee from buffaloes, or ghee from whatever animal whose meat is allowable. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.4
Pali: Navanītaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Butter: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.5
Pali: tesaṁyeva navanītaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): butter from the same animals. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.6
Pali: Telaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Oil: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.7
Pali: tilatelaṁ sāsapatelaṁ madhukatelaṁ eraṇḍatelaṁ vasātelaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): sesame oil, mustard oil, honey-tree oil, castor oil, oil from fat. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.8
Pali: Madhu nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Honey: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.9
Pali: makkhikāmadhu. 
English (brahmali, published): honey from bees. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.10
Pali: Phāṇitaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Syrup: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.11
Pali: ucchumhā nibbattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): from sugarcane. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.12
Pali: Tāni paṭiggahetvā sattāhaparamaṁ sannidhikārakaṁ paribhuñjitabbānīti 
English (brahmali, published): After being received, they should be used from storage for at most seven days: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.13
Pali: sattāhaparamatā paribhuñjitabbāni. 
English (brahmali, published): they are to be used for seven days at a maximum. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.14
Pali: Taṁ atikkāmayato nissaggiyaṁ hotīti 
English (brahmali, published): If one uses them longer than that, one commits an offense entailing relinquishment: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.15
Pali: aṭṭhame aruṇuggamane nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): it becomes subject to relinquishment at dawn on the eighth day. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.16
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā puggalassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The tonics should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.17
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.18
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bu-vb-np1#3.2.5'>Bu NP 1:3.2.5</a>–3.2.29, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.19
Pali: idaṁ me, bhante, bhesajjaṁ sattāhātikkantaṁ nissaggiyaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, these tonics, which I have kept over seven days, are to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.20
Pali: imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmīti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.21
Pali: dadeyyāti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.22
Pali: dadeyyunti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.23
Pali: āyasmato dammīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give these tonics back to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.24.1
Pali: Sattāhātikkante atikkantasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than seven days and he perceives it as more, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.25
Pali: Sattāhātikkante vematiko, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than seven days, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.26
Pali: Sattāhātikkante anatikkantasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than seven days, but he perceives it as less, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.27
Pali: Anadhiṭṭhite adhiṭṭhitasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If they have not been determined, but he perceives that they have, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.28
Pali: Avissajjite vissajjitasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If they have not been given away, but he perceives that they have, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.29
Pali: Anaṭṭhe naṭṭhasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If they have not been lost, but he perceives that they have, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.30
Pali: Avinaṭṭhe vinaṭṭhasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If they have not been destroyed, but he perceives that they have, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.31
Pali: Adaḍḍhe daḍḍhasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If they have not been burned, but he perceives that they have, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.32
Pali: Avilutte viluttasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If they have not been stolen, but he perceives that they have, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.33
Pali: Nissaṭṭhaṁ paṭilabhitvā na kāyikena paribhogena paribhuñjitabbaṁ, na ajjhoharitabbaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): After the relinquished tonics have been returned, they are not to be used on the body, nor are they to be eaten. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.34
Pali: padīpe vā kāḷavaṇṇe vā upanetabbaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): They may be used in lamps or as a black coloring agent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.35
Pali: aññena bhikkhunā kāyikena paribhogena paribhuñjitabbaṁ, na ajjhoharitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Other monks may use them on the body, but they may not eat them. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.36
Pali: Sattāhānatikkante atikkantasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than seven days, but he perceives it as more, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.37
Pali: Sattāhānatikkante vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than seven days, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.38
Pali: Sattāhānatikkante anatikkantasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than seven days and he perceives it as less, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.39.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.40
Pali: antosattāhaṁ adhiṭṭheti, vissajjeti, nassati, vinassati, ḍayhati, acchinditvā gaṇhanti, vissāsaṁ gaṇhanti, 
English (brahmali, published): if within seven days they have been determined, given away, lost, destroyed, burned, stolen, or taken on trust; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.41
Pali: anupasampannassa cattena vantena muttena anapekkho datvā paṭilabhitvā paribhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if, without any desire for them, he gives them up to a person who is not fully ordained, and he then obtains them again and then uses them; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.42
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.43
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np23:2.44
Pali: Bhesajjasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ tatiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on storing the five tonics too long, the third, is finished.

English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on almsbowls 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:0.5
Pali: 24. Vassikasāṭikasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 24. Making a rainy-season robe at the wrong time 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ vassikasāṭikā anuññātā hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): he allowed the rainy-season robe for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:1.3
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhū—“bhagavatā vassikasāṭikā anuññātā”ti, 
English (brahmali, published): Knowing that this was the case, the monks from the group of six 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:1.4
Pali: paṭikacceva vassikasāṭikacīvaraṁ pariyesanti, 
English (brahmali, published): went looking for cloth for their rainy-season robes in advance. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:1.5
Pali: paṭikacceva katvā nivāsenti, 
English (brahmali, published): And after sewing them in advance, they wore them. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:1.6
Pali: jiṇṇāya vassikasāṭikāya naggā kāyaṁ ovassāpenti. 
English (brahmali, published): Then, because their rainy-season robes were worn out, they bathed naked in the rain. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:1.7
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:1.8
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū paṭikacceva vassikasāṭikacīvaraṁ pariyesissanti, paṭikacceva katvā nivāsessanti, jiṇṇāya vassikasāṭikāya naggā kāyaṁ ovassāpessantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the monks from the group of six go looking for cloth for their rainy-season robes in advance, sew them in advance, and then wear them, and then, because their rainy-season robes are worn out, bathe naked in the rain?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:1.9
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū chabbaggiye bhikkhū anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking the monks from the group of six in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:1.10
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, paṭikacceva vassikasāṭikacīvaraṁ pariyesatha? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:1.11
Pali: Paṭikacceva katvā nivāsetha? 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:1.12
Pali: Jiṇṇāya vassikasāṭikāya naggā kāyaṁ ovassāpethā”ti? 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:1.13
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:1.14
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:1.15
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe moghapurisā, paṭikacceva vassikasāṭikacīvaraṁ pariyesissatha, paṭikacceva katvā nivāsessatha, jiṇṇāya vassikasāṭikāya naggā kāyaṁ ovassāpessatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:1.16
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:1.17
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:1.18.1
Pali: “‘Māso seso gimhānan’ti bhikkhunā vassikasāṭikacīvaraṁ pariyesitabbaṁ; ‘addhamāso seso gimhānan’ti katvā nivāsetabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘When there is a month left of summer, a monk may go looking for cloth for his rainy-season robe. When there is a half-month left, he may sew it and then wear it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:1.19
Pali: ‘Orena ce māso seso gimhānan’ti vassikasāṭikacīvaraṁ pariyeseyya, ‘orenaddhamāso seso gimhānan’ti katvā nivāseyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he goes looking for cloth for his rainy-season robe when there is more than a month left of summer, or if he sews it and then wears it when there is more than a half-month left, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.1
Pali: “Māso seso gimhānan”ti bhikkhunā vassikasāṭikacīvaraṁ pariyesitabbanti. 
English (brahmali, published): When there is a month left of summer, a monk may go looking for cloth for his rainy-season robe: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.2
Pali: Ye manussā pubbepi vassikasāṭikacīvaraṁ denti te upasaṅkamitvā evamassu vacanīyā— “kālo vassikasāṭikāya, samayo vassikasāṭikāya, aññepi manussā vassikasāṭikacīvaraṁ dentī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): after going to those people who previously, too, have given cloth for the rainy-season robes, he should say, “It’s time for the rainy-season robe,” “It’s the occasion for the rainy-season robe,” “Other people, too, are giving cloth for the rainy-season robe.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.3
Pali: Na vattabbā— “detha me vassikasāṭikacīvaraṁ, āharatha me vassikasāṭikacīvaraṁ, parivattetha me vassikasāṭikacīvaraṁ, cetāpetha me vassikasāṭikacīvaran”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): He should not say, “Give me cloth for the rainy-season robe,” “Bring me cloth for the rainy-season robe,” “Trade me cloth for the rainy-season robe,” “Buy me cloth for the rainy-season robe.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.4
Pali: “Addhamāso seso gimhānan”ti katvā nivāsetabbanti. 
English (brahmali, published): When there is a half-month left, he may sew it and then wear it: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.5
Pali: Addhamāse sese gimhāne katvā nivāsetabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): after sewing it during the last half-month of summer, he may wear it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.6
Pali: “Orena ce māso seso gimhānan”ti 
English (brahmali, published): When there is more than a month left of summer: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.7
Pali: atirekamāse sese gimhāne vassikasāṭikacīvaraṁ pariyesati, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if he goes looking for cloth for the rainy-season robe when there is more than a month left of summer, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.8
Pali: “Orenaddhamāso seso gimhānan”ti 
English (brahmali, published): When there is more than a half-month left: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.9
Pali: atirekaddhamāse sese gimhāne katvā nivāseti, nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he wears it after sewing it when there is more than a half-month left of summer, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.10
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā puggalassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The rainy-season robe should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.11
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.12
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bu-vb-np1#3.2.5'>Bu NP 1:3.2.5</a>–3.2.29, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.13
Pali: “idaṁ me, bhante, vassikasāṭikacīvaraṁ atirekamāse sese gimhāne pariyiṭṭhaṁ atirekaddhamāse sese gimhāne katvā paridahitaṁ nissaggiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this cloth for the rainy-season robe, which I went looking for when there was more than a month left of summer or which I wore after sewing it when there was more than a half-month left of summer, is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.14
Pali: Imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.15
Pali: dadeyyāti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.16
Pali: dadeyyunti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.17
Pali: āyasmato dammīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this cloth for the rainy-season robe back to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.18.1
Pali: Atirekamāse sese gimhāne atirekasaññī vassikasāṭikacīvaraṁ pariyesati, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If there is more than a month left of summer, and he perceives it as more, and he goes looking for cloth for a rainy-season robe, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.19
Pali: Atirekamāse sese gimhāne vematiko vassikasāṭikacīvaraṁ pariyesati, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If there is more than a month left of summer, but he is unsure of it, and he goes looking for cloth for a rainy-season robe, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.20
Pali: Atirekamāse sese gimhāne ūnakasaññī vassikasāṭikacīvaraṁ pariyesati, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If there is more than a month left of summer, but he perceives it as less, and he goes looking for cloth for a rainy-season robe, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.21
Pali: Atirekaddhamāse sese gimhāne atirekasaññī katvā nivāseti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If there is more than a half-month left of summer, and he perceives it as more, and he wears the rainy-season robe after sewing it, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.22
Pali: Atirekaddhamāse sese gimhāne vematiko katvā nivāseti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If there is more than a half-month left of summer, but he is unsure of it, and he wears the rainy-season robe after sewing it, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.23
Pali: Atirekaddhamāse sese gimhāne ūnakasaññī katvā nivāseti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If there is more than a half-month left of summer, but he perceives it as less, and he wears the rainy-season robe after sewing it, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.24
Pali: Satiyā vassikasāṭikāya naggo kāyaṁ ovassāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has a rainy-season robe, but he bathes naked in the rain, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.25
Pali: Ūnakamāse sese gimhāne atirekasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If there is less than a month left of summer, but he perceives it as more, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.26
Pali: Ūnakamāse sese gimhāne vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If there is less than a month left of summer, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.27
Pali: Ūnakamāse sese gimhāne ūnakasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If there is less than a month left of summer, and he perceives it as less, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.28
Pali: Ūnakaddhamāse sese gimhāne atirekasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If there is less than a half-month left of summer, but he perceives it as more, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.29
Pali: Ūnakaddhamāse sese gimhāne vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If there is less than a half-month left of summer, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.30
Pali: Ūnakaddhamāse sese gimhāne ūnakasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If there is less than a half-month left of summer, and he perceives it as less, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.31.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.32
Pali: “māso seso gimhānan”ti vassikasāṭikacīvaraṁ pariyesati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he goes looking for cloth for the rainy-season robe when there is a month left of summer; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.33
Pali: “addhamāso seso gimhānan”ti katvā nivāseti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he wears the rainy-season robe after sewing it when there is a half-month left of summer; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.34
Pali: “ūnakamāso seso gimhānan”ti vassikasāṭikacīvaraṁ pariyesati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he goes looking for cloth for the rainy-season robe when there is less than a month left of summer; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.35
Pali: “ūnakaddhamāso seso gimhānan”ti katvā nivāseti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he wears the rainy-season robe after sewing it when there is less than a half-month left of summer; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.36
Pali: pariyiṭṭhāya vassikasāṭikāya vassaṁ ukkaḍḍhiyyati, 
English (brahmali, published): if, after looking for a rainy-season robe, he postpones the rainy-season residence; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.37
Pali: nivatthāya vassikasāṭikāya vassaṁ ukkaḍḍhiyyati, dhovitvā nikkhipitabbaṁ; samaye nivāsetabbaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if, after wearing a rainy-season robe, he postpones the rainy-season residence (in which case he should wash it and store it and then use it at the right time); 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.38
Pali: acchinnacīvarassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if his robe has been stolen; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.39
Pali: naṭṭhacīvarassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if his robe has been lost; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.40
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.41
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.42
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np24:2.43
Pali: Vassikasāṭikasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ catutthaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on making a rainy-season robe at the wrong time, the fourth, is finished.

Pali: 25. Cīvaraacchindanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 25. Taking back robe-cloth in anger 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā upanando sakyaputto bhātuno saddhivihārikaṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Upananda the Sakyan said to his brother’s student, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:1.3
Pali: “ehāvuso, janapadacārikaṁ pakkamissāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Come, let’s go wandering the country.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:1.4
Pali: “Nāhaṁ, bhante, gamissāmi; 
English (brahmali, published): “I can’t, venerable, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:1.5
Pali: dubbalacīvaromhī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): my robes are worn out.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:1.6
Pali: “Ehāvuso, ahaṁ te cīvaraṁ dassāmī”ti 
English (brahmali, published): “I’ll give you a robe.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:1.7
Pali: tassa cīvaraṁ adāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): And he gave him a robe. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:1.8
Pali: Assosi kho so bhikkhu—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards that monk heard 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:1.9
Pali: “bhagavā kira janapadacārikaṁ pakkamissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): that the Buddha was about to go wandering the country. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:1.10
Pali: Atha kho tassa bhikkhuno etadahosi—
English (brahmali, published): He thought, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:1.11
Pali: “na dānāhaṁ āyasmatā upanandena sakyaputtena saddhiṁ janapadacārikaṁ pakkamissāmi, bhagavatā saddhiṁ janapadacārikaṁ pakkamissāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Now I’ll go wandering with the Buddha instead.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:1.12
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā upanando sakyaputto taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Then, when Upananda said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:1.13
Pali: “ehi dāni, āvuso, janapadacārikaṁ pakkamissāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Let’s go,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:1.14
Pali: “Nāhaṁ, bhante, tayā saddhiṁ janapadacārikaṁ pakkamissāmi, bhagavatā saddhiṁ janapadacārikaṁ pakkamissāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): he replied, “I’m not going with you, but with the Buddha.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:1.15

English (brahmali, published): “Well, that robe I gave you is going with me,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:1.16
Pali: kupito anattamano acchindi. 
English (brahmali, published): and he just took it back in anger. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:1.17
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): That monk told other monks what had happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:1.18
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): And the monks of few desires complained and criticized Upananda, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:1.19
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā upanando sakyaputto bhikkhussa sāmaṁ cīvaraṁ datvā kupito anattamano acchindissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Upananda give away a robe and then take it back in anger?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:1.20
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ upanandaṁ sakyaputtaṁ anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking him in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:1.21
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, upananda, bhikkhussa sāmaṁ cīvaraṁ datvā kupito anattamano acchindī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Upananda, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:1.22
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:1.23
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:1.24
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, bhikkhussa sāmaṁ cīvaraṁ datvā kupito anattamano acchindissasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:1.25
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” …

Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:1.27.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhussa sāmaṁ cīvaraṁ datvā kupito anattamano acchindeyya vā acchindāpeyya vā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk himself gives a robe to a monk, but then, in anger, takes it back or has it taken back, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.5
Pali: Bhikkhussāti 
English (brahmali, published): To a monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.6
Pali: aññassa bhikkhussa. 
English (brahmali, published): to another monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.7
Pali: Sāmanti 
English (brahmali, published): Himself:

Pali: sayaṁ datvā.

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.9

English (brahmali, published): A robe: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.10
Pali: channaṁ cīvarānaṁ aññataraṁ cīvaraṁ, vikappanupagaṁ pacchimaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one of the six kinds of robe-cloth, but not smaller than what can be assigned to another. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.11
Pali: Kupito anattamanoti 
English (brahmali, published): In anger: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.12
Pali: anabhiraddho āhatacitto khilajāto. 
English (brahmali, published): discontent, having hatred, hostile. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.13
Pali: Acchindeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Takes back: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.14
Pali: sayaṁ acchindati, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if he takes it back himself, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.15
Pali: Acchindāpeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Has taken back: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.16
Pali: aññaṁ āṇāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he asks another, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.17
Pali: Sakiṁ āṇatto bahukampi acchindati, nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he only asks once, then even if the other takes back many, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.18
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā puggalassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The robe-cloth should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual.

Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ.

Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bu-vb-np1#3.2.5'>Bu NP 1:3.2.5</a>–3.2.29, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.21
Pali: “idaṁ me, bhante, cīvaraṁ bhikkhussa sāmaṁ datvā acchinnaṁ nissaggiyaṁ imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this robe-cloth, which I took back after giving it to a monk, is to be relinquished. I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.22
Pali: dadeyyāti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.23

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.24
Pali: āyasmato dammīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this robe-cloth back to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.25.1
Pali: Upasampanne upasampannasaññī cīvaraṁ datvā kupito anattamano acchindati vā acchindāpeti vā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained and he perceives them as such, and after giving them robe-cloth, he takes it back in anger or has it taken back, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.26
Pali: Upasampanne vematiko cīvaraṁ datvā kupito anattamano acchindati vā acchindāpeti vā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, but he is unsure of it, and after giving them robe-cloth, he takes it back in anger or has it taken back, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.27
Pali: Upasampanne anupasampannasaññī cīvaraṁ datvā kupito anattamano acchindati vā acchindāpeti vā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, but he does not perceive them as such, and after giving them robe-cloth, he takes it back in anger or has it taken back, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.28
Pali: Aññaṁ parikkhāraṁ datvā kupito anattamano acchindati vā acchindāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, after giving them another requisite, he takes it back in anger or has it taken back, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.29
Pali: Anupasampannassa cīvaraṁ vā aññaṁ vā parikkhāraṁ datvā kupito anattamano acchindati vā acchindāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, after giving robe-cloth or another requisite to a person who is not fully ordained, he takes it back in anger or has it taken back, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.30
Pali: Anupasampanne upasampannasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, but he perceives them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.31
Pali: Anupasampanne vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.32
Pali: Anupasampanne anupasampannasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, and he does not perceive them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.33.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.34
Pali: so vā deti, 
English (brahmali, published): if the other person gives it back; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.35
Pali: tassa vā vissasanto gaṇhāti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he takes it on trust from them; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.36
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.37
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np25:2.38
Pali: Cīvaraacchindanasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ pañcamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on taking back robe-cloth in anger, the fifth, is finished.

Pali: 26. Suttaviññattisikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 26. Asking for thread 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Rājagaha in the Bamboo Grove, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū cīvarakārasamaye bahuṁ suttaṁ viññāpesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): the monks from the group of six were making robes and they asked for a large amount of thread. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:1.3
Pali: Katepi cīvare bahuṁ suttaṁ avasiṭṭhaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): But when their robes were finished, there was much thread left over. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:1.4
Pali: Atha kho chabbaggiyānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ etadahosi—
English (brahmali, published): They said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:1.5
Pali: “handa mayaṁ, āvuso, aññampi suttaṁ viññāpetvā tantavāyehi cīvaraṁ vāyāpemā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Well, let’s ask for even more thread and get weavers to weave us robe-cloth.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:1.6
Pali: Atha kho chabbaggiyā bhikkhū aññampi suttaṁ viññāpetvā tantavāyehi cīvaraṁ vāyāpesuṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:1.7
Pali: Vītepi cīvare bahuṁ suttaṁ avasiṭṭhaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): Yet even when that robe-cloth had been woven, there was much thread left over. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:1.8
Pali: Dutiyampi kho chabbaggiyā bhikkhū aññampi suttaṁ viññāpetvā tantavāyehi cīvaraṁ vāyāpesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): A second time they asked for more thread and had weavers weave them robe-cloth. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:1.9
Pali: Vītepi cīvare bahuṁ suttaṁ avasiṭṭhaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): Once again there was much thread left over. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:1.10
Pali: Tatiyampi kho chabbaggiyā bhikkhū aññampi suttaṁ viññāpetvā tantavāyehi cīvaraṁ vāyāpesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): A third time they asked for more thread and had weavers weave them robe-cloth. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:1.11
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:1.12
Pali: “kathañhi nāma samaṇā sakyaputtiyā sāmaṁ suttaṁ viññāpetvā tantavāyehi cīvaraṁ vāyāpessantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the Sakyan monastics ask for thread and then get weavers to weave them robe-cloth?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:1.13
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:1.14
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:1.15
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū sāmaṁ suttaṁ viññāpetvā tantavāyehi cīvaraṁ vāyāpessantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the monks from the group of six ask for thread and then get weavers to weave them robe-cloth?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:1.16
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū chabbaggiye bhikkhū anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking those monks in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned those monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:1.17
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, sāmaṁ suttaṁ viññāpetvā tantavāyehi cīvaraṁ vāyāpethā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:1.18
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:1.19
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:1.20
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, sāmaṁ suttaṁ viññāpetvā tantavāyehi cīvaraṁ vāyāpessatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:1.21
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:1.22
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:1.23.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu sāmaṁ suttaṁ viññāpetvā tantavāyehi cīvaraṁ vāyāpeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk himself asks for thread, and then has weavers weave him robe-cloth, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.5
Pali: Sāmanti 
English (brahmali, published): Himself: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.6
Pali: sayaṁ viññāpetvā. 
English (brahmali, published): he himself has asked. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.7
Pali: Suttaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Thread: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.8
Pali: cha suttāni—
English (brahmali, published): there are six kinds of thread: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.9
Pali: khomaṁ kappāsikaṁ koseyyaṁ kambalaṁ sāṇaṁ bhaṅgaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): linen, cotton, silk, wool, sunn hemp, and hemp. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.10
Pali: Tantavāyehīti 
English (brahmali, published): Weavers: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.11
Pali: pesakārehi vāyāpeti, payoge payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if he has it woven by weavers, then for every effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.12
Pali: Paṭilābhena nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): When he gets the robe-cloth, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.13
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā puggalassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The robe-cloth should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.14
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.15
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bu-vb-np1#3.2.5'>Bu NP 1:3.2.5</a>–3.2.29, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.16
Pali: “idaṁ me, bhante, cīvaraṁ sāmaṁ suttaṁ viññāpetvā tantavāyehi vāyāpitaṁ nissaggiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this robe-cloth, which I got weavers to weave after asking for the thread myself, is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.17
Pali: Imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.18
Pali: dadeyyāti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.19
Pali: dadeyyunti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.20
Pali: āyasmato dammīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this robe-cloth back to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.21.1
Pali: Vāyāpite vāyāpitasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he had it woven, and he perceives that he did, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.22
Pali: Vāyāpite vematiko, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he had it woven, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.23
Pali: Vāyāpite avāyāpitasaññī, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he had it woven, but he does not perceive that he did, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.24
Pali: Avāyāpite vāyāpitasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he did not have it woven, but he perceives that he did, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.25
Pali: Avāyāpite vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he did not have it woven, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.26
Pali: Avāyāpite avāyāpitasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he did not have it woven, and he does not perceive that he did, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.27.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.28
Pali: cīvaraṁ sibbetuṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is to sew a robe; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.29
Pali: āyoge, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is for a back-and-knee strap; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.30
Pali: kāyabandhane, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is for a belt; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.31
Pali: aṁsabaddhake, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is for a shoulder strap; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.32
Pali: pattatthavikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is for a bowl bag; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.33
Pali: parissāvane, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is for a water filter; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.34
Pali: ñātakānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is from relatives; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.35
Pali: pavāritānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is from those who have given an invitation; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.36
Pali: aññassatthāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if it for the benefit of someone else; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.37
Pali: attano dhanena, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is by means of his own property; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.38
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.39
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np26:2.40
Pali: Suttaviññattisikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ chaṭṭhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on asking for thread, the sixth, is finished.
