  - brahmali [published]: translation/en/brahmali/vinaya/pli-tv-bu-vb/pli-tv-bu-vb-np/pli-tv-bu-vb-np27_translation-en-brahmali.json

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:0.3
Pali: Nissaggiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on relinquishment 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:0.4
Pali: Pattavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on almsbowls 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:0.5
Pali: 27. Mahāpesakārasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 27. Bribing weavers 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro puriso pavāsaṁ gacchanto pajāpatiṁ etadavoca—“suttaṁ dhārayitvā amukassa tantavāyassa dehi, cīvaraṁ vāyāpetvā nikkhipa, 
English (brahmali, published): a man who was going away said to his wife, “Please weigh some thread, take it to the weavers, get them to weave robe-cloth, and put the robe-cloth aside. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.3
Pali: āgato ayyaṁ upanandaṁ cīvarena acchādessāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): When I return, I’ll give it to Venerable Upananda.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.4
Pali: Assosi kho aññataro piṇḍacāriko bhikkhu tassa purisassa imaṁ vācaṁ bhāsamānassa. 
English (brahmali, published): An alms-collecting monk heard that man speaking those words. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.5
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu yenāyasmā upanando sakyaputto tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ upanandaṁ sakyaputtaṁ etadavoca—“mahāpuññosi tvaṁ, āvuso upananda, 
English (brahmali, published): He then went to Upananda the Sakyan and said, “Upananda, you have much merit. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.6
Pali: amukasmiṁ okāse aññataro puriso pavāsaṁ gacchanto pajāpatiṁ etadavoca—‘suttaṁ dhārayitvā amukassa tantavāyassa dehi, cīvaraṁ vāyāpetvā nikkhipa, āgato ayyaṁ upanandaṁ cīvarena acchādessāmī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): In such-and-such a place I heard a man, as he was going away, tell his wife to get robe-cloth woven so that he could give it to you when he returned.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.7
Pali: “Atthāvuso, maṁ so upaṭṭhāko”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “He’s my supporter.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.8
Pali: Sopi kho tantavāyo āyasmato upanandassa sakyaputtassa upaṭṭhāko hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): And the weaver was Upananda’s supporter too. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.9
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā upanando sakyaputto yena so tantavāyo tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ tantavāyaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Upananda then went to that weaver and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.10
Pali: “idaṁ kho, āvuso, cīvaraṁ maṁ uddissa viyyati; āyatañca karohi vitthatañca. Appitañca suvītañca suppavāyitañca suvilekhitañca suvitacchitañca karohī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “This robe-cloth that you’re weaving for me, make it long and wide. And make it closely woven, well-woven, well-stretched, well-scraped, and well-combed.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.11
Pali: “Ete kho me, bhante, suttaṁ dhārayitvā adaṁsu; 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, they’ve already weighed the thread and given it to me, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.12
Pali: iminā suttena cīvaraṁ vināhī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): telling me to weave the robe-cloth with that. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.13
Pali: “Na, bhante, sakkā āyataṁ vā vitthataṁ vā appitaṁ vā kātuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): I won’t be able to make it long, wide, or closely woven. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.14
Pali: Sakkā ca kho, bhante, suvītañca suppavāyitañca suvilekhitañca suvitacchitañca kātun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): But I’m able to make it well-woven, well-stretched, well-scraped, and well-combed.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.15
Pali: “Iṅgha tvaṁ, āvuso, āyatañca karohi vitthatañca appitañca. 
English (brahmali, published): “Just make it long, wide, and closely woven. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.16
Pali: Na tena suttena paṭibaddhaṁ bhavissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): There’ll be enough thread.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.17
Pali: Atha kho so tantavāyo yathābhataṁ suttaṁ tante upanetvā yena sā itthī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ itthiṁ etadavoca—“suttena, ayye, attho”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Then, when all the thread had been used up, that weaver went to that woman and said, “Ma’am, I need more thread.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.18
Pali: “Nanu tvaṁ ayyo mayā vutto—‘iminā suttena cīvaraṁ vināhī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “But didn’t I tell you to weave the robe-cloth with that thread?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.19
Pali: “Saccāhaṁ, ayye, tayā vutto—‘iminā suttena cīvaraṁ vināhī’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “You did. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.20
Pali: Api ca maṁ ayyo upanando evamāha—‘iṅgha tvaṁ, āvuso, āyatañca karohi vitthatañca appitañca, 
English (brahmali, published): But Venerable Upananda told me to make it long, wide, and closely woven. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.21
Pali: na tena suttena paṭibaddhaṁ bhavissatī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): And he said there would be enough thread.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.22
Pali: Atha kho sā itthī yattakaṁyeva suttaṁ paṭhamaṁ adāsi tattakaṁ pacchā adāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): That woman then gave him as much thread again as she had done the first time. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.23
Pali: Assosi kho āyasmā upanando sakyaputto—“so kira puriso pavāsato āgato”ti. Atha kho āyasmā upanando sakyaputto yena tassa purisassa nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): When Upananda heard that her husband had returned from his travels, he went to his house and sat down on the prepared seat. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.24
Pali: Atha kho so puriso yenāyasmā upanando sakyaputto tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ upanandaṁ sakyaputtaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): That man approached him, bowed, and sat down. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.25
Pali: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so puriso pajāpatiṁ etadavoca—“vītaṁ taṁ cīvaran”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): He then said to his wife, “Has the robe-cloth been woven?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.26
Pali: “Āmāyya, vītaṁ taṁ cīvaran”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes, it has.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.27
Pali: “Āhara, ayyaṁ upanandaṁ cīvarena acchādessāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please bring it. I’ll give it to Venerable Upananda.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.28
Pali: Atha kho sā itthī taṁ cīvaraṁ nīharitvā sāmikassa datvā etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): She then got the robe-cloth, gave it to her husband, and told him what had happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.29
Pali: Atha kho so puriso āyasmato upanandassa sakyaputtassa cīvaraṁ datvā ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—
English (brahmali, published): After giving the robe-cloth to Upananda, he complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.30
Pali: “mahicchā ime samaṇā sakyaputtiyā asantuṭṭhā. 
English (brahmali, published): “These Sakyan monastics have great desires; they’re not content. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.31
Pali: Nayime sukarā cīvarena acchādetuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): It’s no easy matter to give them robe-cloth. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.32
Pali: Kathañhi nāma ayyo upanando mayā pubbe appavārito tantavāye upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): How could Venerable Upananda go to the weavers and say what kind of robe-cloth he wanted without first being invited by me?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.33
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tassa purisassa ujjhāyantassa khiyyantassa vipācentassa. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of that man, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.34
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized Upananda, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.35
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā upanando sakyaputto pubbe appavārito gahapatikassa tantavāye upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Upananda go to a householder’s weavers and say what kind of robe-cloth he wants without first being invited?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.36
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ upanandaṁ sakyaputtaṁ anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking him in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned Upananda: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.37
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, upananda, pubbe appavārito gahapatikassa tantavāye upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Upananda, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.38
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.39
Pali: “Ñātako te, upananda, aññātako”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is he a relative of yours?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.40
Pali: “Aññātako, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “No, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.41
Pali: “Aññātako, moghapurisa, aññātakassa na jānāti patirūpaṁ vā appatirūpaṁ vā santaṁ vā asantaṁ vā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, people who are unrelated don’t know what’s appropriate and inappropriate, what’s good and bad, in dealing with each other. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.42
Pali: Tattha nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, pubbe appavārito aññātakassa gahapatikassa tantavāye upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjissasi. 
English (brahmali, published): And still you did this. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.43
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.44
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.45.1
Pali: “Bhikkhuṁ paneva uddissa aññātako gahapati vā gahapatānī vā tantavāyehi cīvaraṁ vāyāpeyya, tatra ce so bhikkhu pubbe appavārito tantavāye upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjeyya—
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a male or female householder is having robe-cloth woven by weavers for an unrelated monk and, without first being invited, that monk goes to those weavers and specifies the kind of robe-cloth he wants, saying, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.46
Pali: ‘idaṁ kho, āvuso, cīvaraṁ maṁ uddissa viyyati. Āyatañca karotha vitthatañca. Appitañca suvītañca suppavāyitañca suvilekhitañca suvitacchitañca karotha. 
English (brahmali, published): “This robe-cloth that you are weaving for me, make it long and wide; make it closely woven, well-woven, well-stretched, well-scraped, and well-combed, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.47
Pali: Appeva nāma mayampi āyasmantānaṁ kiñcimattaṁ anupadajjeyyāmā’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): and perhaps I will even give you a small gift,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:1.48
Pali: Evañca so bhikkhu vatvā kiñcimattaṁ anupadajjeyya antamaso piṇḍapātamattampi, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): then, in saying that and afterwards giving them a small gift, even a bit of almsfood, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.1
Pali: Bhikkhuṁ paneva uddissāti 
English (brahmali, published): For a monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.2
Pali: bhikkhussatthāya bhikkhuṁ ārammaṇaṁ karitvā bhikkhuṁ acchādetukāmo. 
English (brahmali, published): for the benefit of a monk; making a monk the object of consideration, one wants to give to him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.3
Pali: Aññātako nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Unrelated: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.4
Pali: mātito vā pitito vā yāva sattamā pitāmahayugā asambaddho. 
English (brahmali, published): anyone who is not a descendant of one’s male ancestors going back eight generations, either on the mother’s side or on the father’s side. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.5
Pali: Gahapati nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A male householder: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.6
Pali: yo koci agāraṁ ajjhāvasati. 
English (brahmali, published): any man who lives at home. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.7
Pali: Gahapatānī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A female householder: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.8
Pali: yā kāci agāraṁ ajjhāvasati. 
English (brahmali, published): any woman who lives at home. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.9
Pali: Tantavāyehīti 
English (brahmali, published): By weavers: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.10
Pali: pesakārehi. 
English (brahmali, published): by those who weave. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.11
Pali: Cīvaraṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Robe-cloth: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.12
Pali: channaṁ cīvarānaṁ aññataraṁ cīvaraṁ vikappanupagaṁ pacchimaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one of the six kinds of robe-cloth, but not smaller than what can be assigned to another. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.13
Pali: Vāyāpeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Is having woven: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.14
Pali: vināpeti. 
English (brahmali, published): is causing to weave. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.15
Pali: Tatra ce so bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): If that monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.16
Pali: yaṁ bhikkhuṁ uddissa cīvaraṁ viyyati so bhikkhu. 
English (brahmali, published): the monk the robe-cloth is being woven for. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.17
Pali: Pubbe appavāritoti 
English (brahmali, published): Without first being invited: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.18
Pali: pubbe avutto hoti— “kīdisena te, bhante, cīvarena attho, kīdisaṁ te cīvaraṁ vāyāpemī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): without it first being said, “Venerable, what kind of robe-cloth do you need? What kind of robe-cloth should I get woven for you?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.19
Pali: Tantavāye upasaṅkamitvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Goes to those weavers: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.20
Pali: gharaṁ gantvā yattha katthaci upasaṅkamitvā. 
English (brahmali, published): having gone to their house, having gone up to them wherever. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.21
Pali: Cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjeyyāti—
English (brahmali, published): Specifies the kind of robe-cloth he wants: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.22
Pali: “idaṁ kho, āvuso, cīvaraṁ maṁ uddissa viyyati, āyatañca karotha vitthatañca. 
English (brahmali, published): “This robe-cloth that you are weaving for me, make it long and wide; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.23
Pali: Appitañca suvītañca suppavāyitañca suvilekhitañca suvitacchitañca karotha. 
English (brahmali, published): make it closely woven, well-woven, well-stretched, well-scraped, and well-combed; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.24
Pali: Appeva nāma mayampi āyasmantānaṁ kiñcimattaṁ anupadajjeyyāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): and perhaps I will even give you a small gift.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.25
Pali: Evañca so bhikkhu vatvā kiñcimattaṁ anupadajjeyya antamaso piṇḍapātamattampīti. 
English (brahmali, published): Then in saying that and afterwards giving them a small gift, even a bit of almsfood—

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.26
Pali: Piṇḍapāto nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Almsfood: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.27
Pali: yāgupi bhattampi khādanīyampi cuṇṇapiṇḍopi dantakaṭṭhampi dasikasuttampi, antamaso dhammampi bhaṇati. 
English (brahmali, published): congee, a meal, fresh food, a bit of bath powder, a tooth cleaner, a piece of string, and even if he gives a teaching. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.28
Pali: Tassa vacanena āyataṁ vā vitthataṁ vā appitaṁ vā karoti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If the weaver makes it long or wide or closely woven because of the monk’s statement, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.29
Pali: Paṭilābhena nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): When he gets the robe-cloth, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.30
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā puggalassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The robe-cloth should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.31
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.32
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bu-vb-np1#3.2.5'>Bu NP 1:3.2.5</a>–3.2.29, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.33
Pali: “idaṁ me, bhante, cīvaraṁ pubbe appavārito aññātakassa gahapatikassa tantavāye upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpannaṁ nissaggiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this robe-cloth, for which I went to the weavers of an unrelated householder and specified what kind of robe-cloth I wanted without first being invited, is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.34
Pali: Imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.35
Pali: dadeyyāti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.36
Pali: dadeyyunti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.37
Pali: āyasmato dammīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this robe-cloth back to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.38.1
Pali: Aññātake aññātakasaññī pubbe appavārito gahapatikassa tantavāye upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjati, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If the householder is unrelated and the monk perceives them as such and, without first being invited, he goes to their weavers and specifies the kind of robe-cloth he wants, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.39
Pali: Aññātake vematiko pubbe appavārito gahapatikassa tantavāye upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjati, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If the householder is unrelated, but the monk is unsure of it and, without first being invited, he goes to their weavers and specifies the kind of robe-cloth he wants, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.40
Pali: Aññātake ñātakasaññī pubbe appavārito gahapatikassa tantavāye upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjati, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If the householder is unrelated, but the monk perceives them as related and, without first being invited, he goes to their weavers and specifies the kind of robe-cloth he wants, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.41
Pali: Ñātake añātakasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the householder is related, but the monk perceives them as unrelated, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.42
Pali: Ñātake vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the householder is related, but the monk is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.43
Pali: Ñātake ñātakasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If the householder is related and the monk perceives them as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.44.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.45
Pali: ñātakānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is from relatives; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.46
Pali: pavāritānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is from those who have given an invitation; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.47
Pali: aññassatthāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is for the benefit of someone else; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.48
Pali: attano dhanena, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is by means of his own property; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.49
Pali: mahagghaṁ vāyāpetukāmassa appagghaṁ vāyāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if someone wants to have expensive robe-cloth woven, but he has them weave inexpensive robe-cloth instead; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.50
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.51
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np27:2.52
Pali: Mahāpesakārasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ sattamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The long training rule on bribing weavers, the seventh, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:0.5
Pali: 28. Accekacīvarasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 28. Storing haste-cloth too long 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro mahāmatto pavāsaṁ gacchanto bhikkhūnaṁ santike dūtaṁ pāhesi—
English (brahmali, published): a government official who was about to set out on a journey sent a message to the monks, saying, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.1.3
Pali: “āgacchantu bhadantā vassāvāsikaṁ dassāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Come, venerables, I wish to give robe-cloth to those who have completed the rainy-season residence.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.1.4
Pali: Bhikkhū—
English (brahmali, published): The monks thought, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.1.5
Pali: “vassaṁvuṭṭhānaṁ bhagavatā vassāvāsikaṁ anuññātan”ti, 
English (brahmali, published): “The Buddha has allowed such robes only for those who have completed the rains residence,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.1.6
Pali: kukkuccāyantā nāgamaṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): and being afraid of wrongdoing they did not go. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.1.7
Pali: Atha kho so mahāmatto ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—
English (brahmali, published): That government official complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.1.8
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhadantā mayā dūte pahite nāgacchissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could they not come when I send a message? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.1.9
Pali: Ahañhi senāya gacchāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): I’m about to set out with the army. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.1.10
Pali: Dujjānaṁ jīvitaṁ dujjānaṁ maraṇan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): It’s hard to know whether I’ll live or die.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.1.11
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tassa mahāmattassa ujjhāyantassa khiyyantassa vipācentassa. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of that government official, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.1.12
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): and they told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.1.13
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.1.14
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, accekacīvaraṁ paṭiggahetvā nikkhipitun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to receive a haste-cloth, and then store it.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū—
English (brahmali, published): When they heard about this, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.2.2
Pali: “bhagavatā anuññātaṁ accekacīvaraṁ paṭiggahetvā nikkhipitun”ti, 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.2.3
Pali: accekacīvarāni paṭiggahetvā cīvarakālasamayaṁ atikkāmenti. 
English (brahmali, published): monks received haste-cloths and stored them beyond the robe season, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.2.4
Pali: Tāni cīvarāni cīvaravaṁse bhaṇḍikābaddhāni tiṭṭhanti. 
English (brahmali, published): keeping them in bundles on a bamboo robe rack. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.2.5
Pali: Addasa kho āyasmā ānando senāsanacārikaṁ āhiṇḍanto tāni cīvarāni cīvaravaṁse bhaṇḍikābaddhāni tiṭṭhante disvā bhikkhū etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): While walking about the dwellings, Venerable Ānanda saw those cloths, and he asked the monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.2.6
Pali: “kassimāni, āvuso, cīvarāni cīvaravaṁse bhaṇḍikābaddhāni tiṭṭhantī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whose cloths are these?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.2.7
Pali: “Amhākaṁ, āvuso, accekacīvarānī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “They’re our haste-cloths.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.2.8
Pali: “Kīvaciraṁ panāvuso, imāni cīvarāni nikkhittānī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “But how long have you stored them?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.2.9
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū āyasmato ānandassa yathānikkhittaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They told him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.2.10
Pali: Āyasmā ānando ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—
English (brahmali, published): Ānanda then complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.2.11
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhū accekacīvaraṁ paṭiggahetvā cīvarakālasamayaṁ atikkāmessantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could these monks receive haste-cloths and then store them beyond the robe season?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.2.12
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā ānando te bhikkhū anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesi …pe…—
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking those monks in many ways, Ānanda told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.2.13
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhū accekacīvaraṁ paṭiggahetvā cīvarakālasamayaṁ atikkāmentī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that there are monks who do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.2.14
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.2.15
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.2.16
Pali: kathañhi nāma te, bhikkhave, moghapurisā accekacīvaraṁ paṭiggahetvā cīvarakālasamayaṁ atikkāmessanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could those foolish men do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.2.17
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.2.18
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.2.19.1
Pali: “Dasāhānāgataṁ kattikatemāsikapuṇṇamaṁ bhikkhuno paneva accekacīvaraṁ uppajjeyya, accekaṁ maññamānena bhikkhunā paṭiggahetabbaṁ, paṭiggahetvā yāva cīvarakālasamayaṁ nikkhipitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘When there are ten days left to the Kattika full moon that ends the first rainy-season residence and a haste-cloth is given to a monk, he may receive it if he regards it as urgent. He may then store it until the end of the robe season. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:1.2.20
Pali: Tato ce uttari nikkhipeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he stores it beyond that, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.1
Pali: Dasāhānāgatanti 
English (brahmali, published): There are ten days left: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.2
Pali: dasāhānāgatāya pavāraṇāya. 
English (brahmali, published): the invitation ceremony is ten days in the future. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.3
Pali: Kattikatemāsikapuṇṇamanti 
English (brahmali, published): The Kattika full moon that ends the first rainy-season residence: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.4
Pali: pavāraṇā kattikā vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): the Kattika full moon of the invitation ceremony is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.5
Pali: Accekacīvaraṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Haste-cloth: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.6
Pali: senāya vā gantukāmo hoti, pavāsaṁ vā gantukāmo hoti, gilāno vā hoti, gabbhinī vā hoti, assaddhassa vā saddhā uppannā hoti, appasannassa vā pasādo uppanno hoti, so ce bhikkhūnaṁ santike dūtaṁ pahiṇeyya— “āgacchantu bhadantā vassāvāsikaṁ dassāmī”ti, etaṁ accekacīvaraṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): when someone intends to set out with the army, when someone intends to set out on a journey, when someone is sick, when someone is pregnant, when someone without faith acquires faith, when someone without confidence acquires confidence—if that person sends a message to the monks, saying, “Come, venerables, I wish to give a robe to those who have completed the rainy-season residence,” this is called “haste-cloth”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.7
Pali: Accekaṁ maññamānena bhikkhunā paṭiggahetabbaṁ paṭiggahetvā yāva cīvarakālasamayaṁ nikkhipitabbanti 
English (brahmali, published): He may receive it if he regards it as urgent. He may then store it until the end of the robe season: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.8
Pali: saññāṇaṁ katvā nikkhipitabbaṁ—“idaṁ accekacīvaran”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): establishing the perception of it as a haste-cloth, he may store it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.9
Pali: Cīvarakālasamayo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The robe season: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.10
Pali: anatthate kathine vassānassa pacchimo māso, 
English (brahmali, published): for one who has not participated in the robe-making ceremony, it is the last month of the rainy season; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.11
Pali: atthate kathine pañcamāsā. 
English (brahmali, published): for one who has participated in the robe-making ceremony, it is the five month period. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.12
Pali: Tato ce uttari nikkhipeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): If he stores it beyond that: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.13
Pali: anatthate kathine vassānassa pacchimaṁ divasaṁ atikkāmeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): for one who has not participated in the robe-making ceremony, if he stores it beyond the last day of the rainy season, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.14
Pali: Atthate kathine kathinuddhāradivasaṁ atikkāmeti, nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): For one who has participated in the robe-making ceremony, if he stores it beyond the day on which the robe season ends, the cloth becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.15
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā puggalassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The cloth should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.16
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.17
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bu-vb-np1#3.2.5'>Bu NP 1:3.2.5</a>–3.2.29, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.18
Pali: idaṁ me, bhante, accekacīvaraṁ cīvarakālasamayaṁ atikkāmitaṁ nissaggiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this haste-cloth, which I have stored beyond the robe season, is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.19
Pali: Imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmīti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.20
Pali: dadeyyāti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.21
Pali: dadeyyunti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.22
Pali: āyasmato dammīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this cloth back to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.23.1
Pali: Accekacīvare accekacīvarasaññī cīvarakālasamayaṁ atikkāmeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a haste-cloth and he perceives it as such, and he stores it beyond the robe season, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.24
Pali: Accekacīvare vematiko cīvarakālasamayaṁ atikkāmeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a haste-cloth, but he is unsure of it, and he stores it beyond the robe season, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.25
Pali: Accekacīvare anaccekacīvarasaññī cīvarakālasamayaṁ atikkāmeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a haste-cloth, but he does not perceive it as such, and he stores it beyond the robe season, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.26
Pali: Anadhiṭṭhite adhiṭṭhitasaññī …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been determined, but he perceives that it has … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.27
Pali: avikappite vikappitasaññī … 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been assigned to another, but he perceives that it has … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.28
Pali: avissajjite vissajjitasaññī … 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been given away, but he perceives that it has … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.29
Pali: anaṭṭhe naṭṭhasaññī … 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been lost, but he perceives that it has … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.30
Pali: avinaṭṭhe vinaṭṭhasaññī … 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been destroyed, but he perceives that it has … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.31
Pali: adaḍḍhe daḍḍhasaññī … 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been burned, but he perceives that it has … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.32
Pali: avilutte viluttasaññī cīvarakālasamayaṁ atikkāmeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been stolen, but he perceives that it has, and he stores it beyond the robe season, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.33
Pali: Nissaggiyaṁ cīvaraṁ anissajjitvā paribhuñjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he uses cloth that should be relinquished without first relinquishing it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.34
Pali: Anaccekacīvare accekacīvarasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a haste-cloth, but he perceives it as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.35
Pali: Anaccekacīvare vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a haste-cloth, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.36
Pali: Anaccekacīvare anaccekacīvarasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a haste-cloth and he does not perceive it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.37.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.38
Pali: antosamaye adhiṭṭheti, vikappeti, vissajjeti, nassati, vinassati, ḍayhati, acchinditvā gaṇhanti, vissāsaṁ gaṇhanti, 
English (brahmali, published): if within the robe season the haste-cloth has been determined, assigned to another, given away, lost, destroyed, burned, stolen, or taken on trust; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.39
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.40
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np28:2.41
Pali: Accekacīvarasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ aṭṭhamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on storing haste-cloth too long, the eighth, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:0.5
Pali: 29. Sāsaṅkasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 29. Storing a robe for more than six days in an inhabited area 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:1.1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:1.1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū vutthavassā āraññakesu senāsanesu viharanti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time monks who had completed the rainy-season residence were staying in wilderness dwellings. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:1.1.3
Pali: Kattikacorakā bhikkhū—
English (brahmali, published): Thieves who were active during the month of Kattika attacked those monks, thinking, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:1.1.4
Pali: “laddhalābhā”ti paripātenti. 
English (brahmali, published): “They have been given things.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:1.1.5
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:1.1.6
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:1.1.7
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, āraññakesu senāsanesu viharantena tiṇṇaṁ cīvarānaṁ aññataraṁ cīvaraṁ antaraghare nikkhipitun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow monks who are staying in wilderness dwellings to store one of their three robes in an inhabited area.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:1.2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū—“bhagavatā anuññātaṁ āraññakesu senāsanesu viharantena tiṇṇaṁ cīvarānaṁ aññataraṁ cīvaraṁ antaraghare nikkhipitun”ti tiṇṇaṁ cīvarānaṁ aññataraṁ cīvaraṁ antaraghare nikkhipitvā atirekachārattaṁ vippavasanti. 
English (brahmali, published): When they heard about this, monks stored one of their three robes in inhabited areas, staying apart from them for more than six days. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:1.2.2
Pali: Tāni cīvarāni nassantipi vinassantipi ḍayhantipi undūrehipi khajjanti. 
English (brahmali, published): The robes were lost, destroyed, burned, and eaten by rats. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:1.2.3
Pali: Bhikkhū duccoḷā honti lūkhacīvarā. 
English (brahmali, published): As a consequence, those monks became poorly dressed. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:1.2.4
Pali: Bhikkhū evamāhaṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): Other monks asked them why, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:1.2.5
Pali: “kissa tumhe, āvuso, duccoḷā lūkhacīvarā”ti? 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:1.2.6
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): and they told them what had happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:1.2.7
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:1.2.8
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhū tiṇṇaṁ cīvarānaṁ aññataraṁ cīvaraṁ antaraghare nikkhipitvā atirekachārattaṁ vippavasissantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could those monks store one of their three robes in an inhabited area and then stay apart from it for more than six days?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:1.2.9
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū te anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking those monks in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:1.2.10
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhū tiṇṇaṁ cīvarānaṁ aññataraṁ cīvaraṁ antaraghare nikkhipitvā atirekachārattaṁ vippavasantī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that there are monks who do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:1.2.11
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:1.2.12
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:1.2.13
Pali: kathañhi nāma te, bhikkhave, moghapurisā tiṇṇaṁ cīvarānaṁ aññataraṁ cīvaraṁ antaraghare nikkhipitvā atirekachārattaṁ vippavasissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could those foolish men do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:1.2.14
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:1.2.15
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:1.2.16.1
Pali: “Upavassaṁ kho pana kattikapuṇṇamaṁ yāni kho pana tāni āraññakāni senāsanāni sāsaṅkasammatāni sappaṭibhayāni tathārūpesu bhikkhu senāsanesu viharanto ākaṅkhamāno tiṇṇaṁ cīvarānaṁ aññataraṁ cīvaraṁ antaraghare nikkhipeyya, siyā ca tassa bhikkhuno kocideva paccayo tena cīvarena vippavāsāya. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘There are wilderness dwellings that are considered risky and dangerous. After observing the Kattika full moon that ends the rainy season, a monk who is staying in such a dwelling may, if he so desires, store one of his three robes in an inhabited area so long as he has a reason for staying apart from that robe. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:1.2.17
Pali: Chārattaparamaṁ tena bhikkhunā tena cīvarena vippavasitabbaṁ. Tato ce uttari vippavaseyya, aññatra bhikkhusammutiyā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): He should stay apart from that robe for six days at the most. If he stays apart from it longer than that, except if the monks have agreed, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.1
Pali: Upavassaṁ kho panāti 
English (brahmali, published): After observing: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.2
Pali: vuṭṭhavassānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): after completing the rainy season. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.3
Pali: Kattikapuṇṇamanti 
English (brahmali, published): The Kattika full moon that ends the rainy season: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.4
Pali: kattikacātumāsinī vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): the fourth full moon of the rainy season in the month of Kattika is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.5
Pali: Yāni kho pana tāni āraññakāni senāsanānīti 
English (brahmali, published): There are wilderness dwellings: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.6
Pali: āraññakaṁ nāma senāsanaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): a wilderness dwelling: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.7
Pali: pañcadhanusatikaṁ pacchimaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): it is at least 800 meters away from any inhabited area. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.8
Pali: Sāsaṅkaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Risky: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.9
Pali: ārāme ārāmūpacāre corānaṁ niviṭṭhokāso dissati, bhuttokāso dissati, ṭhitokāso dissati, nisinnokāso dissati, nipannokāso dissati. 
English (brahmali, published): in the monastery, or in the vicinity of the monastery, thieves have been seen camping, eating, standing, sitting, or lying down. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.10
Pali: Sappaṭibhayaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Dangerous: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.11
Pali: ārāme ārāmūpacāre corehi manussā hatā dissanti, viluttā dissanti, ākoṭitā dissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): in the monastery, or in the vicinity of the monastery, thieves have been seen injuring, robbing, or beating people. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.12
Pali: Tathārūpesu bhikkhu senāsanesu viharantoti 
English (brahmali, published): A monk who is staying in such a dwelling: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.13
Pali: evarūpesu bhikkhu senāsanesu viharanto. 
English (brahmali, published): a monk who is staying in that kind of dwelling. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.14
Pali: Ākaṅkhamānoti 
English (brahmali, published): If he so desires: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.15
Pali: icchamāno. 
English (brahmali, published): if he so wishes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.16
Pali: Tiṇṇaṁ cīvarānaṁ aññataraṁ cīvaranti 
English (brahmali, published): One of his three robes: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.17
Pali: saṅghāṭiṁ vā uttarāsaṅgaṁ vā antaravāsakaṁ vā. 
English (brahmali, published): the outer robe, the upper robe, or the sarong. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.18
Pali: Antaraghare nikkhipeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): May store in an inhabited area: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.19
Pali: samantā gocaragāme nikkhipeyya. 
English (brahmali, published): may store it anywhere in his alms village. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.20
Pali: Siyā ca tassa bhikkhuno kocideva paccayo tena cīvarena vippavāsāyāti 
English (brahmali, published): So long as he has a reason for staying apart from that robe: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.21
Pali: siyā paccayo siyā karaṇīyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if there is a reason, if there is something to be done. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.22
Pali: Chārattaparamaṁ tena bhikkhunā tena cīvarena vippavasitabbanti 
English (brahmali, published): He should stay apart from that robe for six days at the most: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.23
Pali: chārattaparamatā vippavasitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): he should stay apart from it for six days at a maximum. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.24
Pali: Aññatra bhikkhusammutiyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Except if the monks have agreed: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.25
Pali: ṭhapetvā bhikkhusammutiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): unless the monks have agreed. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.26
Pali: Tato ce uttari vippavaseyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): If he stays apart from it longer than that: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.27
Pali: sattame aruṇuggamane nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): the robe becomes subject to relinquishment at dawn on the seventh day. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.28
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā puggalassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The robe should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.29
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.30
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bu-vb-np1#3.2.5'>Bu NP 1:3.2.5</a>–3.2.29, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.31
Pali: idaṁ me, bhante, cīvaraṁ atirekachārattaṁ vippavutthaṁ, aññatra bhikkhusammutiyā, nissaggiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this robe, which I have stayed apart from for more than six days without the agreement of the monks, is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.32
Pali: Imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmīti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.33
Pali: dadeyyāti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.34
Pali: dadeyyunti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.35
Pali: āyasmato dammīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this robe back to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.36.1
Pali: Atirekachāratte atirekasaññī vippavasati, aññatra bhikkhusammutiyā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than six days and he perceives it as more, and he is staying apart from it, then, except if the monks have agreed, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.37
Pali: Atirekachāratte vematiko vippavasati, aññatra bhikkhusammutiyā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than six days, but he is unsure of it, and he is staying apart from it, then, except if the monks have agreed, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.38
Pali: Atirekachāratte ūnakasaññī vippavasati, aññatra bhikkhusammutiyā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than six days, but he perceives it as less, and he is staying apart from it, then, except if the monks have agreed, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.39
Pali: Appaccuddhaṭe paccuddhaṭasaññī …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If the determination has not been given up, but he perceives that it has … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.40
Pali: avissajjite vissajjitasaññī … 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been given away, but he perceives that it has … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.41
Pali: anaṭṭhe naṭṭhasaññī … 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been lost, but he perceives that it has … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.42
Pali: avinaṭṭhe vinaṭṭhasaññī … 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been destroyed, but he perceives that it has … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.43
Pali: adaḍḍhe daḍḍhasaññī … 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been burned, but he perceives that it has … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.44
Pali: avilutte viluttasaññī vippavasati, aññatra bhikkhusammutiyā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been stolen, but he perceives that it has, and he is staying apart from it, then, except if the monks have agreed, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.45
Pali: Nissaggiyaṁ cīvaraṁ anissajjitvā paribhuñjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he uses a robe that should be relinquished without first relinquishing it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.46
Pali: Ūnakachāratte atirekasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than six days, but he perceives it as more, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.47
Pali: Ūnakachāratte vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than six days, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.48
Pali: Ūnakachāratte ūnakasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than six days and he perceives it as less, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.49.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.50
Pali: chārattaṁ vippavasati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he stays apart from the robe for six days; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.51
Pali: ūnakachārattaṁ vippavasati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he stays apart from the robe for less than six days; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.52
Pali: chārattaṁ vippavasitvā puna gāmasīmaṁ okkamitvā vasitvā pakkamati, 
English (brahmali, published): if, after staying apart from it for six days, he stays overnight within the village zone and then leaves; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.53
Pali: antochārattaṁ paccuddharati, vissajjeti, nassati, vinassati, ḍayhati, acchinditvā gaṇhanti, vissāsaṁ gaṇhanti, 
English (brahmali, published): if within the six days he gives up the determination, or the robe has been given away, lost, destroyed, burned, stolen, or taken on trust; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.54
Pali: bhikkhusammutiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he has the permission of the monks; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.55
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.56
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np29:2.57
Pali: Sāsaṅkasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ navamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on storing a robe for more than six days in an inhabited area, the ninth, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:0.5
Pali: 30. Pariṇatasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 30. Diverting an offering intended for the Sangha 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sāvatthiyaṁ aññatarassa pūgassa saṅghassa sacīvarabhattaṁ paṭiyattaṁ hoti—
English (brahmali, published): an association had prepared a meal together with robe-cloth for the Sangha, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:1.3
Pali: “bhojetvā cīvarena acchādessāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): intending to offer the robe-cloth after giving the meal. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:1.4
Pali: Atha kho chabbaggiyā bhikkhū yena so pūgo tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; 
English (brahmali, published): But the monks from the group of six went to that association 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:1.5
Pali: upasaṅkamitvā taṁ pūgaṁ etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:1.6
Pali: “dethāvuso, amhākaṁ imāni cīvarānī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please give this robe-cloth to us.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:1.7
Pali: “Na mayaṁ, bhante, dassāma. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, we can’t do that. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:1.8
Pali: Amhākaṁ saṅghassa anuvassaṁ sacīvarabhikkhā paññattā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): We’ve prepared our annual alms-offering together with robe-cloth for the Sangha.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:1.9
Pali: “Bahū, āvuso, saṅghassa dāyakā, bahū saṅghassa bhattā. 
English (brahmali, published): “The Sangha has many donors and supporters. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:1.10
Pali: Mayaṁ tumhe nissāya tumhe sampassantā idha viharāma. 
English (brahmali, published): But since we’re staying here, we look to you for support. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:1.11
Pali: Tumhe ce amhākaṁ na dassatha, atha ko carahi amhākaṁ dassati? 
English (brahmali, published): If you don’t give to us, who will? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:1.12
Pali: Dethāvuso, amhākaṁ imāni cīvarānī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): So give us the robe-cloth.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:1.13
Pali: Atha kho so pūgo chabbaggiyehi bhikkhūhi nippīḷiyamāno yathāpaṭiyattaṁ cīvaraṁ chabbaggiyānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ datvā saṅghaṁ bhattena parivisi. 
English (brahmali, published): Being pressured by the monks from the group of six, that association gave the prepared robe-cloth to them and served the food to the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:1.14
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū jānanti saṅghassa sacīvarabhattaṁ paṭiyattaṁ, na ca jānanti chabbaggiyānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ dinnanti, te evamāhaṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): The monks who knew that a meal together with robe-cloth had been prepared for the Sangha, but who did not know that the robe-cloth had been given to the monks from the group of six, said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:1.15
Pali: “oṇojethāvuso, saṅghassa cīvaran”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please dedicate the robe-cloth.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:1.16
Pali: “Natthi, bhante. 
English (brahmali, published): “There isn’t any. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:1.17
Pali: Yathāpaṭiyattaṁ cīvaraṁ ayyā chabbaggiyā attano pariṇāmesun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks from the group of six have diverted to themselves the robe-cloth we had prepared.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:1.18
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:1.19
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū jānaṁ saṅghikaṁ lābhaṁ pariṇataṁ attano pariṇāmessantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the monks from the group of six divert to themselves things they knew were intended for the Sangha?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:1.20
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū chabbaggiye bhikkhū anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking those monks in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:1.21
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, jānaṁ saṅghikaṁ lābhaṁ pariṇataṁ attano pariṇāmethā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:1.22
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:1.23
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:1.24
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, jānaṁ saṅghikaṁ lābhaṁ pariṇataṁ attano pariṇāmessatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:1.25
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:1.26
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:1.27.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu jānaṁ saṅghikaṁ lābhaṁ pariṇataṁ attano pariṇāmeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk diverts to himself material support that he knows was intended for the Sangha, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.5
Pali: Jānāti nāma 
English (brahmali, published): He knows: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.6
Pali: sāmaṁ vā jānāti aññe vā tassa ārocenti so vā āroceti. 
English (brahmali, published): he knows by himself or others have told him or the donor has told him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.7
Pali: Saṅghikaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): For the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.8
Pali: saṅghassa dinnaṁ hoti pariccattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): given to the Sangha, given up to the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.9
Pali: Lābho nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Material support: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.10
Pali: cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārā. Antamaso cuṇṇapiṇḍopi, dantakaṭṭhampi, dasikasuttampi. 
English (brahmali, published): robe-cloth, almsfood, a dwelling, and medicinal supplies; even a bit of bath powder, a tooth cleaner, or a piece of string. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.11
Pali: Pariṇataṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Intended: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.12
Pali: dassāma karissāmāti vācā bhinnā hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): they have said, “We’ll give,” “We’ll prepare.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.13
Pali: Attano pariṇāmeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he diverts it to himself, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.14
Pali: Paṭilābhena nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): When he gets it, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.15
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā puggalassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): It should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.16
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.17
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bu-vb-np1#3.2.5'>Bu NP 1:3.2.5</a>–3.2.29, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.18
Pali: “idaṁ me, bhante, jānaṁ saṅghikaṁ lābhaṁ pariṇataṁ attano pariṇāmitaṁ nissaggiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this thing, which I diverted to myself knowing that it was intended for the Sangha, is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.19
Pali: Imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.20
Pali: dadeyyāti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.21
Pali: dadeyyunti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.22
Pali: āyasmato dammīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this back to you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.23.1
Pali: Pariṇate pariṇatasaññī attano pariṇāmeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it was intended for the Sangha and he perceives it as such, and he diverts it to himself, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.24
Pali: Pariṇate vematiko attano pariṇāmeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it was intended for the Sangha, but he is unsure of it, and he diverts it to himself, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.25
Pali: Pariṇate apariṇatasaññī attano pariṇāmeti, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it was intended for the Sangha, but he does not perceive it as such, and he diverts it to himself, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.26
Pali: Saṅghassa pariṇataṁ aññasaṅghassa vā cetiyassa vā pariṇāmeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it was intended for one Sangha and he diverts it to another Sangha or to a shrine, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.27
Pali: Cetiyassa pariṇataṁ aññacetiyassa vā saṅghassa vā puggalassa vā pariṇāmeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it was intended for one shrine and he diverts it to another shrine or to a sangha or to an individual, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.28
Pali: Puggalassa pariṇataṁ aññapuggalassa vā saṅghassa vā cetiyassa vā pariṇāmeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it was intended for an individual and he diverts it to another individual or to a sangha or to a shrine, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.29
Pali: Apariṇate pariṇatasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it was not intended for the Sangha, but he perceives it as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.30
Pali: Apariṇate vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it was not intended for the Sangha, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.31
Pali: Apariṇate apariṇatasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it was not intended for the Sangha and he does not perceive it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.32.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.33
Pali: kattha demāti pucchiyamāno yattha tumhākaṁ deyyadhammo paribhogaṁ vā labheyya paṭisaṅkhāraṁ vā labheyya ciraṭṭhitiko vā assa yattha vā pana tumhākaṁ cittaṁ pasīdati tattha dethāti bhaṇati, 
English (brahmali, published): if being asked, “Where may we give?” he says, “Give where your gift will be useful;” “Give where it goes toward repairs;” “Give where it will last for a long time;” “Give where you feel inspired;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.34
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.35
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.36
Pali: Pariṇatasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ dasamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on diverting an offering intended for the Sangha, the tenth, is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.37
Pali: Pattavaggo tatiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): The third subchapter on almsbowls is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.38
Pali: Tassuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.39
Pali: Dve ca pattāni bhesajjaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): “Two on bowls, and tonics, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.40
Pali: vassikā dānapañcamaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): Rainy season, the fifth on a gift; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.41
Pali: Sāmaṁ vāyāpanacceko, 
English (brahmali, published): Oneself, having woven, haste, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.42
Pali: sāsaṅkaṁ saṅghikena cāti. 
English (brahmali, published): Risky, and with the Sangha.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.43
Pali: “Uddiṭṭhā kho, āyasmanto, tiṁsa nissaggiyā pācittiyā dhammā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, the thirty rules on relinquishment and confession have been recited. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.44
Pali: Tatthāyasmante pucchāmi—
English (brahmali, published): In regard to this I ask you, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.45
Pali: ‘kaccittha parisuddhā’? 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.46
Pali: Dutiyampi pucchāmi—
English (brahmali, published): A second time I ask, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.47
Pali: ‘kaccittha parisuddhā’? 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.48
Pali: Tatiyampi pucchāmi—
English (brahmali, published): A third time I ask, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.49
Pali: ‘kaccittha parisuddhā’? 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.50
Pali: Parisuddhetthāyasmanto, tasmā tuṇhī, 
English (brahmali, published): You are pure in this and therefore silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.51
Pali: evametaṁ dhārayāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I will remember it thus.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.52
Pali: Nissaggiyakaṇḍaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing relinquishment is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-np30:2.53
Pali: Pārājikapāḷi niṭṭhitā. 
English (brahmali, published): The canonical text beginning with the offenses entailing expulsion is finished.

Pali: 1. Musāvādasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 1. The training rule on lying 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:1.0.1
Pali: Namo tassa Bhagavato Arahato Sammāsambuddhassa. 
English (brahmali, published): Homage to the Buddha, the Perfected One, the fully Awakened One 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:1.0.2
Pali: Ime kho panāyasmanto dvenavuti pācittiyā dhammā uddesaṁ āgacchanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerables, these ninety-two rules on confession come up for recitation. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:1.1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena hatthako sakyaputto vādakkhitto hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): Hatthaka the Sakyan was beaten in debate. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:1.3
Pali: So titthiyehi saddhiṁ sallapanto avajānitvā paṭijānāti, paṭijānitvā avajānāti, aññenaññaṁ paṭicarati, sampajānamusā bhāsati, saṅketaṁ katvā visaṁvādeti. 
English (brahmali, published): While talking with the monastics of other religions, he would assert things after denying them, and he would deny things after asserting them. He evaded the issues, lied, and made sham appointments. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:1.4
Pali: Titthiyā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—“kathañhi nāma hatthako sakyaputto amhehi saddhiṁ sallapanto avajānitvā paṭijānissati, paṭijānitvā avajānissati, aññenaññaṁ paṭicarissati, sampajānamusā bhāsissati, saṅketaṁ katvā visaṁvādessatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The monastics of other religions complained and criticized him, “When Hatthaka talks with us, how can he assert things after denying them, deny things after asserting them, evade the issues, lie, and make sham appointments?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:1.5
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ titthiyānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those monastics of other religions. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:1.6
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū yena hatthako sakyaputto tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā hatthakaṁ sakyaputtaṁ etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): They then went to Hatthaka and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:1.7
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, āvuso hatthaka, titthiyehi saddhiṁ sallapanto avajānitvā paṭijānāsi, paṭijānitvā avajānāsi, aññenaññaṁ paṭicarasi, sampajānamusā bhāsasi, saṅketaṁ katvā visaṁvādesī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Hatthaka, that you’re doing this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:1.8
Pali: “Ete kho, āvuso, titthiyā nāma yena kenaci jetabbā; 
English (brahmali, published): “These monastics of other religions should be beaten, whatever it takes! 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:1.9
Pali: neva tesaṁ jayo dātabbo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): They shouldn’t be allowed to win.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:1.10
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:1.11
Pali: “kathañhi nāma hatthako sakyaputto titthiyehi saddhiṁ sallapanto avajānitvā paṭijānissati, paṭijānitvā avajānissati, aññenaññaṁ paṭicarissati, sampajānamusā bhāsissati, saṅketaṁ katvā visaṁvādessatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “When Hatthaka talks with the monastics of other religions, how can he assert things after denying them, deny things after asserting them, evade the issues, lie, and make sham appointments?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:1.12
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū hatthakaṁ sakyaputtaṁ anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking Hatthaka in many ways, they told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:1.13
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe bhikkhusaṅghaṁ sannipātāpetvā hatthakaṁ sakyaputtaṁ paṭipucchi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha had the Sangha gathered and questioned Hatthaka: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:1.14
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, hatthaka, titthiyehi saddhiṁ sallapanto avajānitvā paṭijānāsi, paṭijānitvā avajānāsi, aññenaññaṁ paṭicarasi, sampajānamusā bhāsasi, saṅketaṁ katvā visaṁvādesī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Hatthaka, that you’re doing this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:1.15
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:1.16
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:1.17
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, titthiyehi saddhiṁ sallapanto avajānitvā paṭijānissasi, paṭijānitvā avajānissasi, aññenaññaṁ paṭicarissasi, sampajānamusā bhāsissasi, saṅketaṁ katvā visaṁvādessasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, how can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:1.18
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:1.19
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:1.20.1
Pali: “Sampajānamusāvāde pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk lies in full awareness, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.1.1
Pali: Sampajānamusāvādo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Lies in full awareness: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.1.2
Pali: visaṁvādanapurekkhārassa vācā, girā, byappatho, vacībhedo, vācasikā viññatti, aṭṭha anariyavohārā—adiṭṭhaṁ diṭṭhaṁ meti, assutaṁ sutaṁ meti, amutaṁ mutaṁ meti, aviññātaṁ viññātaṁ meti, diṭṭhaṁ adiṭṭhaṁ meti, sutaṁ assutaṁ meti, mutaṁ amutaṁ meti, viññātaṁ aviññātaṁ meti. 
English (brahmali, published): the speech of one who is aiming to deceive—his words, his way of speaking, his breaking into speech, his verbal expression, his eight kinds of ignoble speech: he says that he has seen what he has not seen; he says that he has heard what he has not heard; he says that he has sensed what he has not sensed; he says that he has mentally experienced what he has not mentally experienced; he says that he has not seen what he has seen; he says that he has not heard what he has heard; he says that he has not sensed what he has sensed; he says that he has not mentally experienced what he has mentally experienced. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.1.3.1
Pali: Adiṭṭhaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Not seen: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.1.4
Pali: na cakkhunā diṭṭhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): not seen with the eye. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.1.5
Pali: Assutaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Not heard: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.1.6
Pali: na sotena sutaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): not heard with the ear. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.1.7
Pali: Amutaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Not sensed: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.1.8
Pali: na ghānena ghāyitaṁ, na jivhāya sāyitaṁ, na kāyena phuṭṭhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): not smelled with the nose, not tasted with the tongue, not touched with the body. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.1.9
Pali: Aviññātaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Not mentally experienced: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.1.10
Pali: na manasā viññātaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): not mentally experienced with the mind. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.1.11
Pali: Diṭṭhaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Seen: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.1.12
Pali: cakkhunā diṭṭhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): seen with the eye. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.1.13
Pali: Sutaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Heard: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.1.14
Pali: sotena sutaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): heard with the ear. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.1.15
Pali: Mutaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Sensed: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.1.16
Pali: ghānena ghāyitaṁ, jivhāya sāyitaṁ, kāyena phuṭṭhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): smelled with the nose, tasted with the tongue, touched with the body. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.1.17
Pali: Viññātaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Mentally experienced: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.1.18
Pali: manasā viññātaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): mentally experienced with the mind. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.2.1
Pali: Tīhākārehi “adiṭṭhaṁ diṭṭhaṁ me”ti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pācittiyassa—
English (brahmali, published): If he lies in full awareness, saying that he has seen what he has not seen, he commits an offense entailing confession when three conditions are fulfilled: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.2.2
Pali: pubbevassa hoti “musā bhaṇissan”ti, bhaṇantassa hoti “musā bhaṇāmī”ti, bhaṇitassa hoti “musā mayā bhaṇitan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): before he has lied, he knows he is going to lie; while lying, he knows he is lying; after he has lied, he knows he has lied. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.2.3
Pali: Catūhākārehi “adiṭṭhaṁ diṭṭhaṁ me”ti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pācittiyassa—
English (brahmali, published): If he lies in full awareness, saying that he has seen what he has not seen, he commits an offense entailing confession when four conditions are fulfilled: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.2.4
Pali: pubbevassa hoti “musā bhaṇissan”ti, bhaṇantassa hoti “musā bhaṇāmī”ti, bhaṇitassa hoti “musā mayā bhaṇitan”ti, vinidhāya diṭṭhiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): before he has lied, he knows he is going to lie; while lying, he knows he is lying; after he has lied, he knows he has lied; he misrepresents his view of what is true. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.2.5
Pali: Pañcahākārehi “adiṭṭhaṁ diṭṭhaṁ me”ti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pācittiyassa—
English (brahmali, published): If he lies in full awareness, saying that he has seen what he has not seen, he commits an offense entailing confession when five conditions are fulfilled: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.2.6
Pali: pubbevassa hoti “musā bhaṇissan”ti, bhaṇantassa hoti “musā bhaṇāmī”ti, bhaṇitassa hoti “musā mayā bhaṇitan”ti, vinidhāya diṭṭhiṁ, vinidhāya khantiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): before he has lied, he knows he is going to lie; while lying, he knows he is lying; after he has lied, he knows he has lied; he misrepresents his view of what is true; he misrepresents his belief of what is true. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.2.7
Pali: Chahākārehi “adiṭṭhaṁ diṭṭhaṁ me”ti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pācittiyassa—
English (brahmali, published): If he lies in full awareness, saying that he has seen what he has not seen, he commits an offense entailing confession when six conditions are fulfilled: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.2.8
Pali: pubbevassa hoti “musā bhaṇissan”ti, bhaṇantassa hoti “musā bhaṇāmī”ti, bhaṇitassa hoti “musā mayā bhaṇitan”ti, vinidhāya diṭṭhiṁ, vinidhāya khantiṁ, vinidhāya ruciṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): before he has lied, he knows he is going to lie; while lying, he knows he is lying; after he has lied, he knows he has lied; he misrepresents his view of what is true; he misrepresents his belief of what is true; he misrepresents his acceptance of what is true. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.2.9

Pali: Tīhākārehi “assutaṁ sutaṁ me”ti …pe…

English (brahmali, published): is confused about what he has mentally experienced, but he lies in full awareness, saying that he has mentally experienced and seen and heard and sensed, he commits an offense entailing confession when seven conditions are fulfilled: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.6.14
Pali: pubbevassa hoti “musā bhaṇissan”ti, bhaṇantassa hoti “musā bhaṇāmī”ti, bhaṇitassa hoti “musā mayā bhaṇitan”ti, vinidhāya diṭṭhiṁ, vinidhāya khantiṁ, vinidhāya ruciṁ, vinidhāya bhāvaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): before he has lied, he knows he is going to lie; while lying, he knows he is lying; after he has lied, he knows he has lied; he misrepresents his view of what is true; he misrepresents his belief of what is true; he misrepresents his acceptance of what is true; he misrepresents his sentiment of what is true.

Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense:

English (brahmali, published): (Speaks playfully means:

Pali: sahasā bhaṇati. 
English (brahmali, published): speaking quickly.

Pali: Ravā bhaṇati nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Speaks too fast means: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.7.7
Pali: ‘aññaṁ bhaṇissāmī’ti aññaṁ bhaṇati”. 
English (brahmali, published): meaning to say one thing, he says something else.) 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.7.8
Pali: Ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.7.9
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc1:2.7.10
Pali: Musāvādasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ paṭhamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on lying, the first, is finished.
