English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on lying 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:0.5
Pali: 3. Pesuññasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 3. Malicious talebearing 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū bhikkhūnaṁ bhaṇḍanajātānaṁ kalahajātānaṁ vivādāpannānaṁ pesuññaṁ upasaṁharanti; 
English (brahmali, published): the monks from the group of six were engaged in malicious talebearing between monks who were arguing. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:1.3
Pali: imassa sutvā amussa akkhāyanti, imassa bhedāya; amussa sutvā imassa akkhāyanti, amussa bhedāya. 
English (brahmali, published): After hearing something on one side they reported it to the other side, and vice versa, in order to create division between them. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:1.4
Pali: Tena anuppannāni ceva bhaṇḍanāni uppajjanti, uppannāni ca bhaṇḍanāni bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya saṁvattanti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way they started new quarrels and made existing quarrels worse. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:1.5
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:1.6
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū bhikkhūnaṁ bhaṇḍanajātānaṁ kalahajātānaṁ vivādāpannānaṁ pesuññaṁ upasaṁharissanti, imassa sutvā amussa akkhāyissanti, imassa bhedāya; 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the monks from the group of six engage in malicious talebearing between monks who are arguing? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:1.7
Pali: amussa sutvā imassa akkhāyissanti, amussa bhedāya. 
English (brahmali, published): How can they report to one side what they have heard on the other side, and vice versa, in order to create division, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:1.8
Pali: Tena anuppannāni ceva bhaṇḍanāni uppajjanti, uppannāni ca bhaṇḍanāni bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya saṁvattantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): and in this way start new quarrels and make existing quarrels worse?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:1.9
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū chabbaggiye bhikkhū anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking those monks in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned those monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:1.10
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, bhikkhūnaṁ bhaṇḍanajātānaṁ kalahajātānaṁ vivādāpannānaṁ pesuññaṁ upasaṁharatha, imassa sutvā amussa akkhāyatha, imassa bhedāya, amussa sutvā imassa akkhāyatha, amussa bhedāya? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:1.11
Pali: Tena anuppannāni ceva bhaṇḍanāni uppajjanti, uppannāni ca bhaṇḍanāni bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya saṁvattantī”ti? 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:1.12
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:1.13
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:1.14
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, bhikkhūnaṁ bhaṇḍanajātānaṁ kalahajātānaṁ vivādāpannānaṁ pesuññaṁ upasaṁharissatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:1.15
Pali: Imassa sutvā amussa akkhāyissatha, imassa bhedāya. Amussa sutvā imassa akkhāyissatha, amussa bhedāya. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:1.16
Pali: Tena anuppannāni ceva bhaṇḍanāni uppajjanti, uppannāni ca bhaṇḍanāni bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya saṁvattanti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:1.17
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya. 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:1.18
Pali: Pasannānaṁ vā bhiyyobhāvāya …pe… 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:1.19
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:1.20.1
Pali: “Bhikkhupesuññe pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk engages in malicious talebearing between monks, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.1.1
Pali: Pesuññaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Malicious talebearing: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.1.2
Pali: dvīhākārehi pesuññaṁ hoti— piyakamyassa vā bhedādhippāyassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): there is malicious talebearing in two ways: for one wanting to endear himself and for one aiming at division. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.1.3
Pali: Dasahākārehi pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati— jātitopi, nāmatopi, gottatopi, kammatopi, sippatopi, ābādhatopi, liṅgatopi, kilesatopi, āpattitopi, akkosatopi. 
English (brahmali, published): One engages in malicious talebearing in ten ways: about caste, about name, about family, about occupation, about profession, about illnesses, about physical traits, about defilements, about offenses, and by name-calling. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.1.4.1
Pali: Jāti nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Caste: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.1.5
Pali: dve jātiyo— hīnā ca jāti ukkaṭṭhā ca jāti. 
English (brahmali, published): there are two kinds of castes: low castes and high castes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.1.6
Pali: Hīnā nāma jāti—
English (brahmali, published): Low castes: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.1.7
Pali: caṇḍālajāti venajāti nesādajāti rathakārajāti pukkusajāti. Esā hīnā nāma jāti. 
English (brahmali, published): outcasts, bamboo workers, hunters, carriage makers, waste removers—these are called “low castes”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.1.8
Pali: Ukkaṭṭhā nāma jāti—
English (brahmali, published): High castes: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.1.9
Pali: khattiyajāti brāhmaṇajāti. Esā ukkaṭṭhā nāma jāti. 
English (brahmali, published): aristocrats and brahmins—these are called “high castes”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.1.10
Pali: …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in previous rule.) 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.1.11
Pali: Akkoso nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Name-calling: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.1.12
Pali: dve akkosā— hīno ca akkoso ukkaṭṭho ca akkoso. 
English (brahmali, published): there are two kinds of name-calling: low name-calling and high name-calling. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.1.13
Pali: Hīno nāma akkoso—
English (brahmali, published): Low name-calling: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.1.14
Pali: “oṭṭhosi, meṇḍosi, goṇosi, gadrabhosi, tiracchānagatosi, nerayikosi; natthi tuyhaṁ sugati; duggatiyeva tuyhaṁ pāṭikaṅkhā”ti, yakārena vā bhakārena vā kāṭakoṭacikāya vā. Eso hīno nāma akkoso. 
English (brahmali, published): “You’re a camel,” “You’re a ram,” “You’re an ox,” “You’re a donkey,” “You’re an animal,” “You’re bound for hell,” “You’re not going to a good destination,” “You can only expect a bad destination,” or adding disparaging endings to someone’s name, or calling someone words for the male and female genitals—this is called “low name-calling”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.1.15
Pali: Ukkaṭṭho nāma akkoso—
English (brahmali, published): High name-calling: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.1.16
Pali: “paṇḍitosi, byattosi, medhāvīsi, bahussutosi, dhammakathikosi; natthi tuyhaṁ duggati; sugatiyeva tuyhaṁ pāṭikaṅkhā”ti. Eso ukkaṭṭho nāma akkoso. 
English (brahmali, published): “You’re wise,” “You’re competent,” “You’re intelligent,” “You’re learned,” “You’re an expounder of the Teaching,” “You’re not going to a bad destination,” “You can only expect a good destination”—this is called “high name-calling”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.2.1
Pali: Upasampanno upasampannassa sutvā upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati— “itthannāmo taṁ ‘caṇḍālo veno nesādo rathakāro pukkuso’ti bhaṇatī”ti. Āpatti vācāya, vācāya pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from another who is fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing by saying to yet another who is fully ordained, “So-and-so says this about you, ‘He’s an outcast,’ ‘He’s a bamboo worker,’ ‘He’s a hunter,’ ‘He’s a carriage maker,’ ‘He’s a waste remover,’” then for every statement, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.2.2
Pali: Upasampanno upasampannassa sutvā upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati— “itthannāmo taṁ ‘khattiyo brāhmaṇo’ti bhaṇatī”ti. Āpatti vācāya, vācāya pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from another who is fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing by saying to yet another who is fully ordained, “So-and-so says this about you, ‘He’s an aristocrat,’ ‘He’s a brahmin,’” then for every statement, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.2.3
Pali: Upasampanno upasampannassa sutvā upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati— “itthannāmo taṁ ‘avakaṇṇako javakaṇṇako dhaniṭṭhako saviṭṭhako kulavaḍḍhako’ti bhaṇatī”ti. Āpatti vācāya, vācāya pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from another who is fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing by saying to yet another who is fully ordained, “So-and-so says this about you, ‘He’s an Avakaṇṇaka,’ ‘He’s a Javakaṇṇaka,’ ‘He’s a Dhaniṭṭhaka,’ ‘He’s a Saviṭṭhaka,’ ‘He’s a Kulavaḍḍhaka,’” then for every statement, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.2.4
Pali: Upasampanno upasampannassa sutvā upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati— “itthannāmo taṁ ‘buddharakkhito dhammarakkhito saṅgharakkhito’ti bhaṇatī”ti. Āpatti vācāya, vācāya pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from another who is fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing by saying to yet another who is fully ordained, “So-and-so says this about you, ‘He’s a Buddharakkhita,’ ‘He’s a Dhammarakkhita,’ ‘He’s a Sangharakkhita,’” then for every statement, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.2.5
Pali: Upasampanno upasampannassa sutvā upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati— “itthannāmo taṁ ‘kosiyo bhāradvājo’ti bhaṇatī”ti. Āpatti vācāya, vācāya pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from another who is fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing by saying to yet another who is fully ordained, “So-and-so says this about you, ‘He’s a Kosiya,’ ‘He’s a Bhāradvāja,’” then for every statement, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.2.6
Pali: Upasampanno upasampannassa sutvā upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati— “itthannāmo taṁ ‘gotamo moggallāno kaccāno vāsiṭṭho’ti bhaṇatī”ti. Āpatti vācāya, vācāya pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from another who is fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing by saying to yet another who is fully ordained, “So-and-so says this about you, ‘He’s a Gotama,’ ‘He’s a Moggallāna,’ ‘He’s a Kaccāna,’ ‘He’s a Vāsiṭṭha,’” then for every statement, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.2.7
Pali: Upasampanno upasampannassa sutvā upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati— “itthannāmo taṁ ‘koṭṭhako pupphachaḍḍako’ti bhaṇatī”ti. Āpatti vācāya, vācāya pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from another who is fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing by saying to yet another who is fully ordained, “So-and-so says this about you, ‘He’s a carpenter,’ ‘He’s a waste remover,’” then for every statement, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.2.8
Pali: Upasampanno upasampannassa sutvā upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati— “itthannāmo taṁ ‘kassako vāṇijo gorakkho’ti bhaṇatī”ti. Āpatti vācāya, vācāya pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from another who is fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing by saying to yet another who is fully ordained, “So-and-so says this about you, ‘He’s a farmer,’ ‘He’s a trader,’ ‘He’s a cattle keeper,’” then for every statement, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.2.9
Pali: Upasampanno upasampannassa sutvā upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati— “itthannāmo taṁ ‘naḷakāro kumbhakāro pesakāro cammakāro nahāpito’ti bhaṇatī”ti. Āpatti vācāya, vācāya pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from another who is fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing by saying to yet another who is fully ordained, “So-and-so says this about you, ‘He’s a reed worker,’ ‘He’s a potter,’ ‘He’s a weaver,’ ‘He’s a leather worker,’ ‘He’s a barber,’” then for every statement, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.2.10
Pali: Upasampanno upasampannassa sutvā upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati— “itthannāmo taṁ ‘muddiko gaṇako lekhako’ti bhaṇatī”ti. Āpatti vācāya, vācāya pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from another who is fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing by saying to yet another who is fully ordained, “So-and-so says this about you, ‘He’s an arithmetician,’ ‘He’s an accountant,’ ‘He’s a clerk,’” then for every statement, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.2.11
Pali: Upasampanno upasampannassa sutvā upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati— “itthannāmo taṁ ‘kuṭṭhiko gaṇḍiko kilāsiko sosiko apamāriko’ti bhaṇatī”ti. Āpatti vācāya, vācāya pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from another who is fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing by saying to yet another who is fully ordained, “So-and-so says this about you, ‘He’s a leper,’ ‘He has abscesses,’ ‘He has mild leprosy,’ ‘He has tuberculosis,’ ‘He’s an epileptic,’” then for every statement, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.2.12
Pali: Upasampanno upasampannassa sutvā upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati— “itthannāmo taṁ ‘madhumehiko’ti bhaṇatī”ti. Āpatti vācāya, vācāya pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from another who is fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing by saying to yet another who is fully ordained, “So-and-so says this about you, ‘He’s a diabetic,’” then for every statement, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.2.13
Pali: Upasampanno upasampannassa sutvā upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati— “itthannāmo taṁ ‘atidīgho atirasso atikaṇho accodāto’ti bhaṇatī”ti. Āpatti vācāya, vācāya pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from another who is fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing by saying to yet another who is fully ordained, “So-and-so says this about you, ‘He’s too tall,’ ‘He’s too short,’ ‘He’s too dark,’ ‘He’s too fair,’” then for every statement, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.2.14
Pali: Upasampanno upasampannassa sutvā upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati— “itthannāmo taṁ ‘nātidīgho nātirasso nātikaṇho nāccodāto’ti bhaṇatī”ti. Āpatti vācāya, vācāya pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from another who is fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing by saying to yet another who is fully ordained, “So-and-so says this about you, ‘He’s not too tall,’ ‘He’s not too short,’ ‘He’s not too dark,’ ‘He’s not too fair,’” then for every statement, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.2.15
Pali: Upasampanno upasampannassa sutvā upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati— “itthannāmo taṁ ‘rāgapariyuṭṭhito dosapariyuṭṭhito mohapariyuṭṭhito’ti bhaṇatī”ti. Āpatti vācāya, vācāya pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from another who is fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing by saying to yet another who is fully ordained, “So-and-so says this about you, ‘He’s full of sensual desire,’ ‘He’s full of ill will,’ ‘He’s full of confusion,’” then for every statement, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.2.16
Pali: Upasampanno upasampannassa sutvā upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati— “itthannāmo taṁ ‘vītarāgo vītadoso vītamoho’ti bhaṇatī”ti. Āpatti vācāya, vācāya pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from another who is fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing by saying to yet another who is fully ordained, “So-and-so says this about you, ‘He’s without sensual desire,’ ‘He’s without ill will,’ ‘He’s without confusion,’” then for every statement, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.2.17
Pali: Upasampanno upasampannassa sutvā upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati— “itthannāmo taṁ ‘pārājikaṁ ajjhāpanno, saṅghādisesaṁ ajjhāpanno, thullaccayaṁ ajjhāpanno, pācittiyaṁ ajjhāpanno, pāṭidesanīyaṁ ajjhāpanno, dukkaṭaṁ ajjhāpanno, dubbhāsitaṁ ajjhāpanno’ti bhaṇatī”ti. Āpatti vācāya, vācāya pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from another who is fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing by saying to yet another who is fully ordained, “So-and-so says this about you, ‘He has committed an offense entailing expulsion,’ ‘He has committed an offense entailing suspension,’ ‘He has committed a serious offense,’ ‘He has committed an offense entailing confession,’ ‘He has committed an offense entailing acknowledgment,’ ‘He has committed an offense of wrong conduct,’ ‘He has committed an offense of wrong speech,’” then for every statement, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.2.18
Pali: Upasampanno upasampannassa sutvā upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati— “itthannāmo taṁ ‘sotāpanno’ti bhaṇatī”ti. Āpatti vācāya, vācāya pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from another who is fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing by saying to yet another who is fully ordained, “So-and-so says this about you, ‘He’s a streamenterer,’” then for every statement, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.2.19
Pali: Upasampanno upasampannassa sutvā upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati— “itthannāmo taṁ ‘oṭṭho meṇḍo goṇo gadrabho tiracchānagato nerayiko, natthi tassa sugati, duggatiyeva tassa pāṭikaṅkhā’ti bhaṇatī”ti. Āpatti vācāya, vācāya pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from another who is fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing by saying to yet another who is fully ordained, “So-and-so says this about you, ‘He’s a camel,’ ‘He’s a ram,’ ‘He’s an ox,’ ‘He’s a donkey,’ ‘He’s an animal,’ ‘He’s bound for hell,’ ‘He’s not going to a good destination,’ ‘He can only expect a bad destination,’” then for every statement, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.2.20
Pali: Upasampanno upasampannassa sutvā upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati— “itthannāmo taṁ ‘paṇḍito byatto medhāvī bahussuto dhammakathiko, natthi tassa duggati, sugatiyeva tassa pāṭikaṅkhā’ti bhaṇatī”ti. Āpatti vācāya, vācāya pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from another who is fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing by saying to yet another who is fully ordained, “So-and-so says this about you, ‘He’s wise,’ ‘He’s competent,’ ‘He’s intelligent,’ ‘He’s learned,’ ‘He’s an expounder of the Teaching,’ ‘He’s not going to a bad destination,’ ‘He can only expect a good destination,’” then for every statement, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.2.21.1
Pali: Upasampanno upasampannassa sutvā upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati— “itthannāmo ‘santi idhekacce caṇḍālā venā nesādā rathakārā, pukkusā’ti bhaṇati, na so aññaṁ bhaṇati, taññeva bhaṇatī”ti. Āpatti vācāya, vācāya dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from another who is fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing by saying to yet another who is fully ordained, “So-and-so says, ‘There are outcasts right here,’ ‘There are bamboo workers right here,’ ‘There are hunters right here,’ ‘There are carriage makers right here,’ ‘There are waste removers right here,’ and he’s not speaking about someone else, he’s speaking about you,” then for every statement, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.2.22
Pali: Upasampanno upasampannassa sutvā upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati— “itthannāmo ‘santi idhekacce khattiyā brāhmaṇā’ti bhaṇati, na so aññaṁ bhaṇati, taññeva bhaṇatī”ti. Āpatti vācāya, vācāya dukkaṭassa …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from another who is fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing by saying to yet another who is fully ordained, “So-and-so says, ‘There are aristocrats right here,’ ‘There are brahmins right here,’ and he’s not speaking about someone else, he’s speaking about you,” then for every statement, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.2.23
Pali: Upasampanno upasampannassa sutvā upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati— “itthannāmo ‘santi idhekacce paṇḍitā byattā medhāvī bahussutā dhammakathikā, natthi tesaṁ duggati, sugatiyeva tesaṁ pāṭikaṅkhā’ti bhaṇati, na so aññaṁ bhaṇati, taññeva bhaṇatī”ti. Āpatti vācāya, vācāya dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from another who is fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing by saying to yet another who is fully ordained, “So-and-so says, ‘There are wise ones right here,’ ‘There are competent ones right here,’ ‘There are intelligent ones right here,’ ‘There are learned ones right here,’ ‘There are expounders of the Teaching right here,’ ‘There are those not going to a bad destination right here,’ ‘There are those who can only expect a good destination right here,’ and he’s not speaking about someone else, he’s speaking about you,” then for every statement, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.2.24
Pali: Upasampanno upasampannassa sutvā upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati— “itthannāmo ‘ye nūna caṇḍālā venā nesādā rathakārā pukkusā’ti bhaṇati, na so aññaṁ bhaṇati, taññeva bhaṇatī”ti. Āpatti vācāya, vācāya dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from another who is fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing by saying to yet another who is fully ordained, “So-and-so says, ‘Perhaps these are outcasts,’ ‘Perhaps these are bamboo workers,’ ‘Perhaps these are hunters,’ ‘Perhaps these are carriage makers,’ ‘Perhaps these are waste removers,’ and he’s not speaking about someone else, he’s speaking about you,” then for every statement, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.2.25
Pali: Upasampanno upasampannassa sutvā upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati— “itthannāmo ‘ye nūna paṇḍitā byattā medhāvī bahussutā dhammakathikā’ti bhaṇati, na so aññaṁ bhaṇati, taññeva bhaṇatī”ti. Āpatti vācāya, vācāya dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from another who is fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing by saying to yet another who is fully ordained, “So-and-so says, ‘Perhaps these are wise ones,’ ‘Perhaps these are competent ones,’ ‘Perhaps these are intelligent ones,’ ‘Perhaps these are learned ones,’ ‘Perhaps these are expounders of the Teaching,’ and he’s not speaking about someone else, he’s speaking about you,” then for every statement, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.2.26
Pali: Upasampanno upasampannassa sutvā upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati— “itthannāmo ‘na mayaṁ caṇḍālā venā nesādā rathakārā pukkusā’ti bhaṇati, na so aññaṁ bhaṇati, taññeva bhaṇatī”ti. Āpatti vācāya, vācāya dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from another who is fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing by saying to yet another who is fully ordained, “So-and-so says, ‘We’re not outcasts,’ ‘We’re not bamboo workers,’ ‘We’re not hunters,’ ‘We’re not carriage makers,’ ‘We’re not waste removers,’ and he’s not speaking about someone else, he’s speaking about you,” then for every statement, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.2.27
Pali: Upasampanno upasampannassa sutvā upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati— “itthannāmo ‘na mayaṁ paṇḍitā byattā medhāvī bahussutā dhammakathikā, natthamhākaṁ duggati, sugatiyeva amhākaṁ pāṭikaṅkhā’ti bhaṇati, na so aññaṁ bhaṇati, taññeva bhaṇatī”ti. Āpatti vācāya, vācāya dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from another who is fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing by saying to yet another who is fully ordained, “So-and-so says, ‘We’re not wise ones,’ ‘We’re not competent ones,’ ‘We’re not intelligent ones,’ ‘We’re not learned ones,’ ‘We’re not expounders of the Teaching,’ ‘We’re not going to a bad destination,’ ‘We can only expect a good destination,’ and he’s not speaking about someone else, he’s speaking about you,” then for every statement, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.3.1
Pali: Upasampanno upasampannassa sutvā upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati; āpatti vācāya, vācāya pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from another who is fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing to yet another who is fully ordained, then for every statement, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.3.2
Pali: Upasampanno upasampannassa sutvā anupasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from another who is fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing to one who is not fully ordained, then for every statement, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.3.3
Pali: Upasampanno anupasampannassa sutvā upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from one who is not fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing to another who is fully ordained, then for every statement, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.3.4
Pali: Upasampanno anupasampannassa sutvā anupasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one who is fully ordained, after hearing it from one who is not fully ordained, engages in malicious talebearing to another who is not fully ordained, then for every statement, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.4.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.4.2
Pali: napiyakamyassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not want to endear himself 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.4.3
Pali: nabhedādhippāyassa, 
English (brahmali, published): and he is not aiming at division; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.4.4
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.4.5
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc3:2.4.6
Pali: Pesuññasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ tatiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on malicious talebearing, the third, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:0.5
Pali: 4. Padasodhammasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 4. Instructing a householder to memorize the Teaching 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū upāsake padaso dhammaṁ vācenti. 
English (brahmali, published): the monks from the group of six were instructing lay followers to memorize the Teaching. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:1.3
Pali: Upāsakā bhikkhūsu agāravā appatissā asabhāgavuttikā viharanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Those lay followers became disrespectful, undeferential, and rude toward the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:1.4
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:1.5
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū upāsake padaso dhammaṁ vācessanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the monks from the group of six instruct lay followers to memorize the Teaching?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:1.6
Pali: Upāsakā bhikkhūsu agāravā appatissā asabhāgavuttikā viharantī”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:1.7
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū chabbaggiye bhikkhū anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking those monks in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:1.8
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, upāsake padaso dhammaṁ vācetha; 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:1.9
Pali: upāsakā bhikkhūsu agāravā appatissā asabhāgavuttikā viharantī”ti? 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:1.10
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:1.11
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:1.12
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, upāsake padaso dhammaṁ vācessatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how can you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:1.13
Pali: Upāsakā bhikkhūsu agāravā appatissā asabhāgavuttikā viharanti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:1.14
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya pasannānaṁ vā bhiyyobhāvāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:1.15
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:1.16.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu anupasampannaṁ padaso dhammaṁ vāceyya pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk instructs a person who is not fully ordained to memorize the Teaching, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.1.5
Pali: Anupasampanno nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A person who is not fully ordained: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.1.6
Pali: bhikkhuñca bhikkhuniñca ṭhapetvā avaseso anupasampanno nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): anyone except a fully ordained monk or a fully ordained nun. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.1.7
Pali: Padaso nāma 
English (brahmali, published): To memorize: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.1.8
Pali: padaṁ, anupadaṁ, anvakkharaṁ, anubyañjanaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): a line, the next line, the next syllable, the next phrase. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.1.9
Pali: Padaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A line: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.1.10
Pali: ekato paṭṭhapetvā ekato osāpenti. 
English (brahmali, published): they start together and finish together. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.1.11
Pali: Anupadaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The next line: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.1.12
Pali: pāṭekkaṁ paṭṭhapetvā ekato osāpenti. 
English (brahmali, published): one of them starts, but they finish together. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.1.13
Pali: Anvakkharaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The next syllable: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.1.14
Pali: “rūpaṁ aniccan”ti vuccamāno, “run”ti opāteti. 
English (brahmali, published): when ‘<i lang='pi' translate='no'>Rūpaṁ aniccaṁ</i>’ is being said, he prompts him, saying, ‘<i lang='pi' translate='no'>rū</i>’. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.1.15
Pali: Anubyañjanaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The next phrase: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.1.16
Pali: “rūpaṁ aniccan”ti vuccamāno, “vedanā aniccā”ti saddaṁ nicchāreti. 
English (brahmali, published): when ‘<i lang='pi' translate='no'>Rūpaṁ aniccaṁ</i>,’ is being said, the other says, ‘<i lang='pi' translate='no'>Vedanā aniccā</i>.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.1.17
Pali: Yañca padaṁ, yañca anupadaṁ, yañca anvakkharaṁ, yañca anubyañjanaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): And whatever line there is, whatever next line, whatever next syllable, whatever next phrase—

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.1.18
Pali: sabbametaṁ padaso nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): this is all called “to memorize”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.1.19
Pali: Dhammo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The Teaching: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.1.20
Pali: buddhabhāsito, sāvakabhāsito, isibhāsito, devatābhāsito, atthūpasañhito, dhammūpasañhito. 
English (brahmali, published): what has been spoken by the Buddha, what has been spoken by disciples, what has been spoken by sages, what has been spoken by gods, what is connected with what is beneficial, what is connected with the Teaching. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.1.21
Pali: Vāceyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Instructs: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.1.22
Pali: padena vāceti, pade pade āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he instructs by the line, then for every line he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.1.23
Pali: Akkharāya vāceti, akkharakkharāya āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he instructs by the syllable, then for every syllable he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.2.1
Pali: Anupasampanne anupasampannasaññī padaso dhammaṁ vāceti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the person is not fully ordained, and the monk does not perceive them as such, and he instructs them to memorize the Teaching, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.2.2
Pali: Anupasampanne vematiko padaso dhammaṁ vāceti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the person is not fully ordained, but the monk is unsure of it, and he instructs them to memorize the Teaching, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.2.3
Pali: Anupasampanne upasampannasaññī padaso dhammaṁ vāceti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the person is not fully ordained, but the monk perceives them as such, and he instructs them to memorize the Teaching, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.2.4
Pali: Upasampanne anupasampannasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the person is fully ordained, but the monk does not perceive them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.2.5
Pali: Upasampanne vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the person is fully ordained, but the monk is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.2.6
Pali: Upasampanne upasampannasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If the person is fully ordained, and the monk perceives them as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.3.2
Pali: ekato uddisāpento, 
English (brahmali, published): if the recitation is done together; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.3.3
Pali: ekato sajjhāyaṁ karonto, 
English (brahmali, published): if they practice together; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.3.4
Pali: yebhuyyena paguṇaṁ ganthaṁ bhaṇantaṁ opāteti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he prompts one who is speaking a mostly familiar text; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.3.5
Pali: osārentaṁ opāteti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he prompts one who is reciting; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.3.6
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.3.7
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc4:2.3.8
Pali: Padasodhammasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ catutthaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on instructing a householder to memorize the Teaching, the fourth, is finished.

Pali: 5. Sahaseyyasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 5. Lying down with one not fully ordained 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā āḷaviyaṁ viharati aggāḷave cetiye. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Āḷavī at the Aggāḷava Shrine. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena upāsakā ārāmaṁ āgacchanti dhammassavanāya. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the lay followers were coming to the monastery to listen to the Teaching. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:1.3
Pali: Dhamme bhāsite therā bhikkhū yathāvihāraṁ gacchanti. 
English (brahmali, published): When the instruction was over, the senior monks went to their own dwellings, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:1.4
Pali: Navakā bhikkhū tattheva upaṭṭhānasālāyaṁ upāsakehi saddhiṁ muṭṭhassatī, asampajānā, naggā, vikūjamānā, kākacchamānā seyyaṁ kappenti. 
English (brahmali, published): but the newly ordained monks lay down right there in the assembly hall together with the lay followers—absentminded, heedless, naked, muttering, and snoring. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:1.5
Pali: Upāsakā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The lay followers complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:1.6

English (brahmali, published): “How can the venerables lie down absentminded, heedless, naked, muttering, and snoring?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:1.7
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ upāsakānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those lay followers, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:1.8
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:1.9
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhū anupasampannena sahaseyyaṁ kappessantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can monks lie down in the same sleeping place as people who are not fully ordained?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:1.10
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū te navake bhikkhū anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe…

English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” …

English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:1.17.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu anupasampannena sahaseyyaṁ kappeyya pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk lies down in the same sleeping place as a person who is not fully ordained, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:1.18
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:2.1
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā āḷaviyaṁ yathābhirantaṁ viharitvā yena kosambī tena cārikaṁ pakkāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): After staying at Āḷavī for as long as he wanted, the Buddha set out wandering toward Kosambī. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:2.2
Pali: Anupubbena cārikaṁ caramāno yena kosambī tadavasari. Tatra sudaṁ bhagavā kosambiyaṁ viharati badarikārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): When he eventually arrived, he stayed at the Badarikā Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:2.3
Pali: Bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ rāhulaṁ etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): Just then the monks there said to Venerable Rāhula, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:2.4
Pali: “bhagavatā, āvuso rāhula, sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ—‘na anupasampannena sahaseyyā kappetabbā’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Rāhula, the Buddha has laid down a training rule that we can’t lie down in the same sleeping place as a person who’s not fully ordained. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:2.5
Pali: Seyyaṁ, āvuso rāhula, jānāhī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Please find another sleeping place.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:2.6
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā rāhulo seyyaṁ alabhamāno vaccakuṭiyā seyyaṁ kappesi. 
English (brahmali, published): Since Rāhula was not able to find a sleeping place, he lay down in the restroom. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:2.7
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā rattiyā paccūsasamayaṁ paccuṭṭhāya yena vaccakuṭi tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā ukkāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): Then, after rising early in the morning, the Buddha went to the restroom where he cleared his throat. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:2.8
Pali: Āyasmāpi rāhulo ukkāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): Rāhula, too, cleared his throat. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:2.9
Pali: “Ko etthā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Who’s there?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:2.10
Pali: “Ahaṁ, bhagavā, rāhulo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s me, sir, Rāhula.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:2.11
Pali: “Kissa tvaṁ, rāhula, idha nisinnosī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Why are you sitting here, Rāhula?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:2.12
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā rāhulo bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): Rāhula told the Buddha what had happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:2.13
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:2.14
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, anupasampannena dirattatirattaṁ sahaseyyaṁ kappetuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to lie down in the same sleeping place as a person who isn’t fully ordained for two or three nights. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:2.15
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:2.16.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu anupasampannena uttaridirattatirattaṁ sahaseyyaṁ kappeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk lies down more than two or three nights in the same sleeping place as a person who is not fully ordained, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.1.5
Pali: Anupasampanno nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A person who is not fully ordained: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.1.6
Pali: bhikkhuṁ ṭhapetvā avaseso anupasampanno nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): anyone except a fully ordained monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.1.7
Pali: Uttaridirattatirattanti 
English (brahmali, published): More than two or three nights: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.1.8
Pali: atirekadirattatirattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): in excess of two or three nights. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.1.9
Pali: Sahāti 
English (brahmali, published): Same: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.1.10
Pali: ekato. 
English (brahmali, published): together. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.1.11
Pali: Seyyā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Sleeping place: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.1.12
Pali: sabbacchannā, sabbaparicchannā, yebhuyyenacchannā, yebhuyyena paricchannā. 
English (brahmali, published): fully roofed, fully walled; mostly roofed, mostly walled. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.1.13
Pali: Seyyaṁ kappeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Lies down in the same sleeping place: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.1.14
Pali: catutthe divase atthaṅgate sūriye, anupasampanne nipanne, bhikkhu nipajjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): at dawn on the fourth day: if he lies down when the person who is not fully ordained is already lying down, he commits an offense entailing confession; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.1.15
Pali: Bhikkhu nipanne, anupasampanno nipajjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if the person who is not fully ordained lies down when he is already lying down, he commits an offense entailing confession; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.1.16
Pali: Ubho vā nipajjanti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if they both lie down together, he commits an offense entailing confession; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.1.17
Pali: Uṭṭhahitvā punappunaṁ nipajjanti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): every time they get up and then lie down again, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.2.1
Pali: Anupasampanne anupasampannasaññī uttaridirattatirattaṁ sahaseyyaṁ kappeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If they are not fully ordained, and the monk does not perceive them as such, and he lies down more than two or three nights in the same sleeping place as them, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.2.2
Pali: Anupasampanne vematiko uttaridirattatirattaṁ sahaseyyaṁ kappeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If they are not fully ordained, but the monk is unsure of it, and he lies down more than two or three nights in the same sleeping place as them, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.2.3
Pali: Anupasampanne upasampannasaññī uttaridirattatirattaṁ sahaseyyaṁ kappeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If they are not fully ordained, but the monk perceives them as such, and he lies down more than two or three nights in the same sleeping place as them, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.2.4
Pali: Upaḍḍhacchanne upaḍḍhaparicchanne, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is half-roofed and half-walled, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.2.5
Pali: Upasampanne anupasampannasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If they are fully ordained, but the monk does not perceive them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.2.6
Pali: Upasampanne vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If they are fully ordained, but the monk is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.2.7
Pali: Upasampanne upasampannasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If they are fully ordained, and the monk perceives them as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.3.2
Pali: dvetisso rattiyo vasati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he stays together with them for two or three nights; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.3.3
Pali: ūnakadvetisso rattiyo vasati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he stays together with them for less than two or three nights; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.3.4
Pali: dve rattiyo vasitvā tatiyāya rattiyā purāruṇā nikkhamitvā puna vasati, 
English (brahmali, published): if, after staying together for two nights, he leaves before dawn on the third night and then stays together again; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.3.5
Pali: sabbacchanne sabbaaparicchanne, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is fully roofed, but not walled; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.3.6
Pali: sabbaparicchanne sabbaacchanne, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is fully walled, but not roofed; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.3.7
Pali: yebhuyyena acchanne, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is mostly not roofed; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.3.8
Pali: yebhuyyena aparicchanne, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is mostly not walled; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.3.9
Pali: anupasampanne nipanne bhikkhu nisīdati, 
English (brahmali, published): if the monk sits when the person who is not fully ordained is lying down; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.3.10
Pali: bhikkhu nipanne anupasampanno nisīdati, 
English (brahmali, published): if the person who is not fully ordained sits when the monk is lying down; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.3.11
Pali: ubho vā nisīdanti, 
English (brahmali, published): if they both sit; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.3.12
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.3.13
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc5:3.3.14
Pali: Sahaseyyasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ pañcamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on lying down with one not fully ordained, the fifth, is finished.

Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis

Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:0.4
Pali: Musāvādavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on lying 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:0.5
Pali: 6. Dutiyasahaseyyasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 6. Lying down with a woman 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā anuruddho kosalesu janapade sāvatthiṁ gacchanto sāyaṁ aññataraṁ gāmaṁ upagacchi. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Anuruddha was walking through the Kosalan country on his way to Sāvatthī, when one evening he arrived at a certain village. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.3
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena tasmiṁ gāme aññatarissā itthiyā āvasathāgāraṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): Just then a woman in that village had prepared her guesthouse. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.4
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā anuruddho yena sā itthī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ itthiṁ etadavoca—“sace te, bhagini, agaru, vaseyyāma ekarattaṁ āvasathāgāre”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Anuruddha went to that woman and said, “If it’s not inconvenient for you, I’d like to stay in your guesthouse for one night.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.5
Pali: “Vaseyyātha, bhante”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please stay, venerable.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.6
Pali: Aññepi addhikā yena sā itthī tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ itthiṁ etadavocuṁ—“sace te, ayye, agaru vaseyyāma ekarattaṁ āvasathāgāre”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Other travelers also went to that woman and said, “Ma’am, if it’s not troublesome for you, we’d like to stay in your guesthouse for one night.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.7
Pali: “Eso kho ayyo samaṇo paṭhamaṁ upagato; sace so anujānāti, vaseyyāthā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sirs, a monastic is already staying there. If he agrees, you may stay.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.8
Pali: Atha kho te addhikā yenāyasmā anuruddho tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ anuruddhaṁ etadavocuṁ—“sace te, bhante, agaru, vaseyyāma ekarattaṁ āvasathāgāre”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Those travelers then approached Anuruddha and said, “If you don’t mind, venerable, we’d like to stay one night in the guesthouse.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.9
Pali: “Vaseyyātha, āvuso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “No problem.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.10
Pali: Atha kho sā itthī āyasmante anuruddhe saha dassanena paṭibaddhacittā ahosi. 
English (brahmali, published): Now as soon as that woman had seen Anuruddha, she had fallen in love with him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.11
Pali: Atha kho sā itthī yenāyasmā anuruddho tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ anuruddhaṁ etadavoca—“ayyo, bhante, imehi manussehi ākiṇṇo na phāsu viharissati. 
English (brahmali, published): She now went to him and said, “Sir, you won’t be comfortable surrounded by these people. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.12
Pali: Sādhāhaṁ, bhante, ayyassa mañcakaṁ abbhantaraṁ paññapeyyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Why don’t I prepare a bed for you in the main house?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.13
Pali: Adhivāsesi kho āyasmā anuruddho tuṇhībhāvena. 
English (brahmali, published): Anuruddha consented by remaining silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.14
Pali: Atha kho sā itthī āyasmato anuruddhassa mañcakaṁ abbhantaraṁ paññapetvā alaṅkatappaṭiyattā gandhagandhinī yenāyasmā anuruddho tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ anuruddhaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): After preparing a bed in the main house, she put on jewelery and perfume, and she went to Anuruddha and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.15
Pali: “ayyo, bhante, abhirūpo dassanīyo pāsādiko, ahañcamhi abhirūpā dassanīyā pāsādikā. 
English (brahmali, published): “You’re attractive, sir, and so am I. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.16
Pali: Sādhāhaṁ, bhante, ayyassa pajāpati bhaveyyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Why don’t you take me as your wife?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.17
Pali: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā anuruddho tuṇhī ahosi. 
English (brahmali, published): But Anuruddha remained silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.18
Pali: Dutiyampi kho …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): She said the same thing a second time, but again got no response. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.19
Pali: tatiyampi kho sā itthī āyasmantaṁ anuruddhaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): And a third time she said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.20
Pali: “ayyo, bhante, abhirūpo dassanīyo pāsādiko, ahañcamhi abhirūpā dassanīyā pāsādikā. 
English (brahmali, published): “You’re attractive, sir, and so am I. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.21
Pali: Sādhu, bhante, ayyo mañceva paṭicchatu sabbañca sāpateyyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Why don’t you take me and all this property?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.22
Pali: Tatiyampi kho āyasmā anuruddho tuṇhī ahosi. 
English (brahmali, published): Once again Anuruddha remained silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.23
Pali: Atha kho sā itthī sāṭakaṁ nikkhipitvā āyasmato anuruddhassa purato caṅkamatipi tiṭṭhatipi nisīdatipi seyyampi kappeti. 
English (brahmali, published): She then threw off her wrap, and she walked back and forth, stood, sat down, and lay down in front of him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.24
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā anuruddho indriyāni okkhipitvā taṁ itthiṁ neva olokesi napi ālapi. 
English (brahmali, published): But Anuruddha controlled his senses and neither looked at nor spoke to her. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.25
Pali: Atha kho sā itthī—“acchariyaṁ vata bho, abbhutaṁ vata bho. 
English (brahmali, published): Then that woman said, “It’s astonishing and amazing. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.26
Pali: Bahū me manussā satenapi sahassenapi pahiṇanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Many people pay a hundred or a thousand coins to be with me. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.27
Pali: Ayaṁ pana samaṇo—mayā sāmaṁ yāciyamāno—na icchati mañceva paṭicchituṁ sabbañca sāpateyyan”ti 
English (brahmali, published): But this monastic doesn’t want me and all this property, even when I beg him!” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.28
Pali: sāṭakaṁ nivāsetvā āyasmato anuruddhassa pādesu sirasā nipatitvā āyasmantaṁ anuruddhaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): After dressing, she bowed down with her head at Anuruddha’s feet and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.29
Pali: “accayo maṁ, bhante, accagamā yathābālaṁ yathāmūḷhaṁ yathāakusalaṁ yāhaṁ evamakāsiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, I’ve made a mistake. I’ve been foolish, confused, and unskillful. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.30
Pali: Tassā me, bhante, ayyo accayaṁ accayato paṭiggaṇhātu āyatiṁ saṁvarāyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Please forgive me so that I may restrain myself in the future.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.31
Pali: “Taggha tvaṁ, bhagini, accayo accagamā yathābālaṁ yathāmūḷhaṁ yathāakusalaṁ yā tvaṁ evamakāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): “You have certainly made a mistake. You’ve been foolish, confused, and unskillful. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.32
Pali: Yato ca kho tvaṁ, bhagini, accayaṁ accayato disvā yathādhammaṁ paṭikarosi, taṁ te mayaṁ paṭiggaṇhāma. 
English (brahmali, published): But since you acknowledge your mistake and make proper amends, I forgive you. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.33
Pali: Vuddhi hesā, bhagini, ariyassa vinaye yo accayaṁ accayato disvā yathādhammaṁ paṭikaroti āyatiñca saṁvaraṁ āpajjatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): For this is called growth in the training of the noble ones: acknowledging a mistake, making proper amends, and undertaking restraint for the future.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.34
Pali: Atha kho sā itthī tassā rattiyā accayena āyasmantaṁ anuruddhaṁ paṇītena khādanīyena bhojanīyena sahatthā santappetvā sampavāretvā, āyasmantaṁ anuruddhaṁ bhuttāviṁ onītapattapāṇiṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): The following morning that woman personally served and satisfied Anuruddha with various kinds of fine foods. When he had finished his meal, she bowed and sat down to one side. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.35
Pali: Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho taṁ itthiṁ āyasmā anuruddho dhammiyā kathāya sandassesi samādapesi samuttejesi sampahaṁsesi. 
English (brahmali, published): And Anuruddha instructed, inspired, and gladdened her with a teaching. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.36
Pali: Atha kho sā itthī—āyasmatā anuruddhena dhammiyā kathāya sandassitā samādapitā samuttejitā sampahaṁsitā—āyasmantaṁ anuruddhaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): She then said to him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.37
Pali: “abhikkantaṁ, bhante, abhikkantaṁ, bhante. 
English (brahmali, published): “Wonderful, sir, wonderful! 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.38
Pali: Seyyathāpi, bhante, nikkujjitaṁ vā ukkujjeyya, paṭicchannaṁ vā vivareyya, mūḷhassa vā maggaṁ ācikkheyya, andhakāre vā telapajjotaṁ dhāreyya—‘cakkhumanto rūpāni dakkhantī’ti; 
English (brahmali, published): Just as one might set upright what’s overturned, or reveal what’s hidden, or show the way to one who’s lost, or bring a lamp into the dark so that one with eyes might see what’s there—

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.39
Pali: evamevaṁ ayyena anuruddhena anekapariyāyena dhammo pakāsito. 
English (brahmali, published): just so have you made the Teaching clear in many ways. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.40
Pali: Esāhaṁ, bhante, taṁ bhagavantaṁ saraṇaṁ gacchāmi dhammañca bhikkhusaṅghañca. 
English (brahmali, published): I go for refuge to the Buddha, the Teaching, and the Sangha of monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.41
Pali: Upāsikaṁ maṁ ayyo dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Please accept me as a lay follower who’s gone for refuge for life.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.42
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā anuruddho sāvatthiṁ gantvā bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards, after arriving at Sāvatthī, Anuruddha told the monks what had happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.43
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.44
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā anuruddho mātugāmena sahaseyyaṁ kappessatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Anuruddha lie down in the same sleeping place as a woman?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.45
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ anuruddhaṁ anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking him in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned Anuruddha: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.46
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, anuruddha, mātugāmena sahaseyyaṁ kappesī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Anuruddha, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.47
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.48
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, anuruddha, mātugāmena sahaseyyaṁ kappessasi. 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … “Anuruddha, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.49
Pali: Netaṁ, anuruddha, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.50
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:1.51.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu mātugāmena sahaseyyaṁ kappeyya pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk lies down in the same sleeping place as a woman, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.1.5
Pali: Mātugāmo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A woman: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.1.6
Pali: manussitthī, na yakkhī, na petī, na tiracchānagatā; antamaso tadahujātāpi dārikā, pageva mahattarī. 
English (brahmali, published): a human female, not a female spirit, not a female ghost, not a female animal; even a girl born on that very day, let alone an older one. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.1.7
Pali: Sahāti 
English (brahmali, published): Same: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.1.8
Pali: ekato. 
English (brahmali, published): together. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.1.9
Pali: Seyyā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Sleeping place: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.1.10
Pali: sabbacchannā, sabbaparicchannā, yebhuyyenacchannā, yebhuyyena paricchannā. 
English (brahmali, published): fully roofed, fully walled; mostly roofed, mostly walled. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.1.11
Pali: Seyyaṁ kappeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Lies down in the same sleeping place: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.1.12
Pali: atthaṅgate sūriye—
English (brahmali, published): when the sun has set: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.1.13
Pali: Mātugāme nipanne bhikkhu nipajjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if the monk lies down when the woman is already lying down, he commits an offense entailing confession; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.1.14
Pali: Bhikkhu nipanne mātugāmo nipajjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if the woman lies down when the monk is already lying down, he commits an offense entailing confession; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.1.15
Pali: Ubho vā nipajjanti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if they both lie down together, he commits an offense entailing confession; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.1.16
Pali: Uṭṭhahitvā punappunaṁ nipajjanti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): every time they get up and then lie down again, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.2.1
Pali: Mātugāme mātugāmasaññī sahaseyyaṁ kappeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a woman, and he perceives her as such, and he lies down in the same sleeping place as her, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.2.2
Pali: Mātugāme vematiko sahaseyyaṁ kappeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a woman, but he is unsure of it, and he lies down in the same sleeping place as her, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.2.3
Pali: Mātugāme amātugāmasaññī sahaseyyaṁ kappeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a woman, but he does not perceive her as such, and he lies down in the same sleeping place as her, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.2.4
Pali: Upaḍḍhacchanne upaḍḍhaparicchanne, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is half-roofed and half-walled, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.2.5
Pali: Yakkhiyā vā petiyā vā paṇḍakena vā tiracchānagatitthiyā vā sahaseyyaṁ kappeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he lies down in the same sleeping place as a female spirit, a female ghost, a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>paṇḍaka</i>, or a female animal, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.2.6
Pali: Amātugāme mātugāmasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a woman, but he perceives them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.2.7
Pali: Amātugāme vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a woman, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.2.8
Pali: Amātugāme amātugāmasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a woman, and he does not perceive them as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.3.2
Pali: sabbacchanne sabbaaparicchanne, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is fully roofed, but not walled; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.3.3
Pali: sabbaparicchanne sabbaacchanne, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is fully walled, but not roofed; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.3.4
Pali: yebhuyyena acchanne, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is mostly not roofed; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.3.5
Pali: yebhuyyena aparicchanne, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is mostly not walled; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.3.6
Pali: mātugāme nipanne bhikkhu nisīdati, 
English (brahmali, published): if the monk sits when the woman is lying down; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.3.7
Pali: bhikkhu nipanne mātugāmo nisīdati, 
English (brahmali, published): if the woman sits when the monk is lying down; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.3.8
Pali: ubho vā nisīdanti, 
English (brahmali, published): if they both sit down together; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.3.9
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.3.10
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc6:2.3.11
Pali: Dutiyasahaseyyasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ chaṭṭhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on lying down with a woman, the sixth, is finished.

English (brahmali, published): “Just teach five or six sentences. That might be enough for us to understand.”

English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk gives a teaching of more than five or six sentences to a woman, except in the presence of a man who understands, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A:

English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): …The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.1.5
Pali: Mātugāmo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A woman: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.1.6
Pali: manussitthī; na yakkhī na petī na tiracchānagatā; viññū, paṭibalā subhāsitadubbhāsitaṁ duṭṭhullāduṭṭhullaṁ ājānituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): a human female, not a female spirit, not a female ghost, not a female animal. She understands and is capable of discerning good and bad speech, what is decent and what is indecent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.1.7
Pali: Uttarichappañcavācāhīti 
English (brahmali, published): More than five or six sentences:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.1.9
Pali: Dhammo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A teaching: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.1.10
Pali: buddhabhāsito, sāvakabhāsito, isibhāsito, devatābhāsito, atthūpasañhito, dhammūpasañhito. 
English (brahmali, published): what has been spoken by the Buddha, what has been spoken by disciples, what has been spoken by sages, what has been spoken by gods, what is connected with what is beneficial, what is connected with the Teaching. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.1.11
Pali: Deseyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Gives: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.1.12
Pali: padena deseti, pade pade āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he teaches by the line, then for every line he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.1.13
Pali: Akkharāya deseti, akkharakkharāya āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he teaches by the syllable, then for every syllable he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.1.14
Pali: Aññatra viññunā purisaviggahenāti 
English (brahmali, published): Except in the presence of a man who understands: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.1.15
Pali: ṭhapetvā viññuṁ purisaviggahaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): unless a man who understands is present. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.1.16
Pali: Viññū nāma purisaviggaho, 
English (brahmali, published): A man who understands: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.1.17
Pali: paṭibalo hoti subhāsitadubbhāsitaṁ duṭṭhullāduṭṭhullaṁ ājānituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one who is capable of discerning good and bad speech, what is decent and what is indecent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.2.1
Pali: Mātugāme mātugāmasaññī uttarichappañcavācāhi dhammaṁ deseti, aññatra viññunā purisaviggahena, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a woman, and he perceives her as such, and he teaches her more than five or six sentences, except in the presence of a man who understands, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.2.2
Pali: Mātugāme vematiko uttarichappañcavācāhi dhammaṁ deseti, aññatra viññunā purisaviggahena, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a woman, but he is unsure of it, and he teaches her more than five or six sentences, except in the presence of a man who understands, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.2.3
Pali: Mātugāme amātugāmasaññī uttarichappañcavācāhi dhammaṁ deseti, aññatra viññunā purisaviggahena, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a woman, but he does not perceive her as such, and he teaches her more than five or six sentences, except in the presence of a man who understands, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.2.4
Pali: Yakkhiyā vā petiyā vā paṇḍakassa vā tiracchānagatamanussaviggahitthiyā vā uttarichappañcavācāhi dhammaṁ deseti, aññatra viññunā purisaviggahena, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he teaches more than five or six sentences to a female spirit, a female ghost, a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>paṇḍaka</i>, or a female animal in the form of a woman, except in the presence of a man who understands, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.2.5
Pali: Amātugāme mātugāmasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a woman, but he perceives them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.2.6
Pali: Amātugāme vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a woman, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.2.7
Pali: Amātugāme amātugāmasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a woman, and he does not perceive them as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.3.2
Pali: viññunā purisaviggahena, 
English (brahmali, published): if a man who understands is present; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.3.3
Pali: chappañcavācāhi dhammaṁ deseti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he teaches five or six sentences; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.3.4
Pali: ūnakachappañcavācāhi dhammaṁ deseti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he teaches fewer than five or six sentences; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.3.5
Pali: uṭṭhahitvā puna nisīditvā deseti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he gets up, sits down again, and then teaches; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.3.6
Pali: mātugāmo uṭṭhahitvā puna nisīdati tasmiṁ deseti, 
English (brahmali, published): if the woman gets up and sits down again, and he then teaches her; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.3.7
Pali: aññassa mātugāmassa deseti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he teaches another woman; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.3.8
Pali: pañhaṁ pucchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he asks a question; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.3.9
Pali: pañhaṁ puṭṭho katheti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is asked a question and then speaks; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.3.10
Pali: aññassatthāya bhaṇantaṁ mātugāmo suṇāti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is speaking for the benefit of someone else and a woman listens in; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.3.11
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.3.12
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc7:4.3.13
Pali: Dhammadesanāsikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ sattamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on teaching a woman alone, the seventh, is finished.
