pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:0.5
Pali: 8. Bhūtārocanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 8. Truthfully announcing a superhuman quality 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying in the house with the peaked roof in the Great Wood near Vesālī, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā sandiṭṭhā sambhattā bhikkhū vaggumudāya nadiyā tīre vassaṁ upagacchiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): a number of monks who were friends had entered the rainy-season residence on the banks of the river Vaggumudā. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.1.3
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena vajjī dubbhikkhā hoti—dvīhitikā setaṭṭhikā salākāvuttā, na sukarā uñchena paggahena yāpetuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time, the country of Vajjī was short of food and afflicted with hunger, with crops affected by whiteheads and turned to straw. It was not easy to get by on almsfood. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.1.4
Pali: Atha kho tesaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ etadahosi—“etarahi kho vajjī dubbhikkhā—dvīhitikā setaṭṭhikā salākāvuttā, na sukarā uñchena paggahena yāpetuṁ. Kena nu kho mayaṁ upāyena samaggā sammodamānā avivadamānā phāsukaṁ vassaṁ vaseyyāma, na ca piṇḍakena kilameyyāmā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): The monks considered the difficult circumstances, and they thought, “How can we live together in peace and harmony, have a comfortable rains, and get almsfood without trouble?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.1.5
Pali: Ekacce evamāhaṁsu—“handa mayaṁ, āvuso, gihīnaṁ kammantaṁ adhiṭṭhema. Evaṁ te amhākaṁ dātuṁ maññissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Some said, “We could work for the householders, and they’ll support us in return.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.1.6
Pali: Evaṁ mayaṁ samaggā sammodamānā avivadamānā phāsukaṁ vassaṁ vasissāma, na ca piṇḍakena kilamissāmā”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.1.7
Pali: Ekacce evamāhaṁsu—“alaṁ, āvuso, kiṁ gihīnaṁ kammantaṁ adhiṭṭhitena? 
English (brahmali, published): Others said, “There’s no need to work for the householders. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.1.8
Pali: Handa mayaṁ, āvuso, gihīnaṁ dūteyyaṁ harāma. Evaṁ te amhākaṁ dātuṁ maññissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Let’s instead take messages for them, and they’ll support us in return.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.1.9
Pali: Evaṁ mayaṁ samaggā sammodamānā avivadamānā phāsukaṁ vassaṁ vasissāma, na ca piṇḍakena kilamissāmā”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.1.10
Pali: Ekacce evamāhaṁsu—“alaṁ, āvuso; kiṁ gihīnaṁ kammantaṁ adhiṭṭhitena. Kiṁ gihīnaṁ dūteyyaṁ haṭena. 
English (brahmali, published): Still others said, “There’s no need to work or take messages for them. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.1.11
Pali: Handa mayaṁ, āvuso, gihīnaṁ aññamaññassa uttarimanussadhammassa vaṇṇaṁ bhāsissāma—
English (brahmali, published): Let’s instead talk up one another’s superhuman qualities to the householders: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.1.12
Pali: ‘asuko bhikkhu paṭhamassa jhānassa lābhī, asuko bhikkhu dutiyassa jhānassa lābhī, asuko bhikkhu tatiyassa jhānassa lābhī, asuko bhikkhu catutthassa jhānassa lābhī, asuko bhikkhu sotāpanno, asuko bhikkhu sakadāgāmī, asuko bhikkhu anāgāmī, asuko bhikkhu arahā, asuko bhikkhu tevijjo, asuko bhikkhu chaḷabhiñño’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘That monk has the first absorption, that monk the second absorption, that monk the third, that monk the fourth; that monk is a streamenterer, that monk a once-returner, that a non-returner, that a perfected one; that monk has the three true insights, and that the six direct knowledges.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.1.13
Pali: Evaṁ te amhākaṁ dātuṁ maññissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Then they’ll support us. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.1.14
Pali: Evaṁ mayaṁ samaggā sammodamānā avivadamānā phāsukaṁ vassaṁ vasissāma, na ca piṇḍakena kilamissāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way we’ll have a comfortable rains, live together in peace and harmony, and get almsfood without trouble. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.1.15
Pali: “Eso yeva kho, āvuso, seyyo, yo amhākaṁ gihīnaṁ aññamaññassa uttarimanussadhammassa vaṇṇo bhāsito”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the way to go.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.1.16
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū gihīnaṁ aññamaññassa uttarimanussadhammassa vaṇṇaṁ bhāsiṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): Then those monks did just that. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.1.17
Pali: “asuko bhikkhu paṭhamassa jhānassa lābhī …pe… asuko bhikkhu chaḷabhiñño”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.1.18
Pali: Atha kho te manussā—“lābhā vata no, suladdhaṁ vata no, yesaṁ no evarūpā bhikkhū vassaṁ upagatā, na vata no ito pubbe evarūpā bhikkhū vassaṁ upagatā, yathayime bhikkhū sīlavanto kalyāṇadhammā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): And the people there thought, “We’re so fortunate that such monks have come to us for the rainy-season residence. Such virtuous and good monks have never before entered the rains residence with us.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.1.19
Pali: Te na tādisāni bhojanāni attanā bhuñjanti, mātāpitūnaṁ denti puttadārassa denti dāsakammakaraporisassa denti mittāmaccānaṁ denti ñātisālohitānaṁ denti yādisāni bhikkhūnaṁ denti. 
English (brahmali, published): And they gave such food and drink to those monks that they did not even eat and drink themselves, or give to their parents, to their wives and children, to their slaves, servants, and workers, to their friends and companions, or to their relatives. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.1.20
Pali: Na tādisāni khādanīyāni sāyanīyāni pānāni attanā khādanti sāyanti pivanti mātāpitūnaṁ denti puttadārassa denti dāsakammakaraporisassa denti mittāmaccānaṁ denti ñātisālohitānaṁ denti, yādisāni bhikkhūnaṁ denti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.1.21
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū vaṇṇavā ahesuṁ pīṇindriyā pasannamukhavaṇṇā vippasannachavivaṇṇā. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon those monks had a good color, bright faces, clear skin, and sharp senses. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.2.1
Pali: Āciṇṇaṁ kho panetaṁ vassaṁvuṭṭhānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ bhagavantaṁ dassanāya upasaṅkamituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Now it was the custom for monks who had completed the rainy-season residence to go and visit the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.2.2
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū vassaṁvuṭṭhā temāsaccayena senāsanaṁ saṁsāmetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena vesālī tena pakkamiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): And so, when the three months were over and they had completed the rains residence, those monks put their dwellings in order, took their bowls and robes, and set out for Vesālī. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.2.3
Pali: Anupubbena yena vesālī mahāvanaṁ kūṭāgārasālā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; 
English (brahmali, published): When they eventually arrived, they went to the house with the peaked roof in the Great Wood. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.2.4
Pali: upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): There they approached the Buddha, bowed, and sat down. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.2.5
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena disāsu vassaṁvuṭṭhā bhikkhū kisā honti lūkhā dubbaṇṇā uppaṇḍuppaṇḍukajātā dhamanisanthatagattā. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the monks who had completed the rains residence in that region were thin, haggard and pale, with veins protruding all over their bodies. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.2.6
Pali: Vaggumudātīriyā pana bhikkhū vaṇṇavā honti pīṇindriyā pasannamukhavaṇṇā vippasannachavivaṇṇā. 
English (brahmali, published): Yet the monks from the banks of the Vaggumudā had a good color, bright faces, clear skin, and  sharp senses. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.2.7
Pali: Āciṇṇaṁ kho panetaṁ buddhānaṁ bhagavantānaṁ āgantukehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ paṭisammodituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Since it is the custom for Buddhas to greet newly-arrived monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.2.8
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā vaggumudātīriye bhikkhū etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): the Buddha said to them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.2.9
Pali: “kacci, bhikkhave, khamanīyaṁ, kacci yāpanīyaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): “I hope you’re keeping well, monks, I hope you’re getting by? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.2.10
Pali: kacci samaggā sammodamānā avivadamānā phāsukaṁ vassaṁ vasittha, na ca piṇḍakena kilamitthā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): I hope you had a comfortable and harmonious rains, and got almsfood without trouble?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.2.11
Pali: “Khamanīyaṁ, bhagavā, yāpanīyaṁ, bhagavā. 
English (brahmali, published): “We’re keeping well, sir, we’re getting by. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.2.12
Pali: Samaggā ca mayaṁ, bhante, sammodamānā avivadamānā phāsukaṁ vassaṁ vasimhā, na ca piṇḍakena kilamimhā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): We had a comfortable and harmonious rains, and got almsfood without trouble.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.2.13
Pali: Jānantāpi tathāgatā pucchanti, jānantāpi na pucchanti. 
English (brahmali, published): When Buddhas know what is going on, sometimes they ask and sometimes not. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.2.14
Pali: Kālaṁ viditvā pucchanti, kālaṁ viditvā na pucchanti. 
English (brahmali, published): They know the right time to ask and when not to ask. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.2.15
Pali: Atthasañhitaṁ tathāgatā pucchanti, no anatthasañhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Buddhas ask when it is beneficial, otherwise not, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.2.16
Pali: Anatthasañhite setughāto tathāgatānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): for Buddhas are incapable of doing what is unbeneficial. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.2.17
Pali: Dvīhākārehi buddhā bhagavanto bhikkhū paṭipucchanti—“dhammaṁ vā desessāma, sāvakānaṁ vā sikkhāpadaṁ paññapessāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Buddhas question the monks for two reasons: to give a teaching or to lay down a training rule. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.2.18
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā vaggumudātīriye bhikkhū etadavoca—“yathā kathaṁ pana tumhe, bhikkhave, samaggā sammodamānā avivadamānā phāsukaṁ vassaṁ vasittha, na ca piṇḍakena kilamitthā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): And the Buddha said to those monks, “In what way, monks, did you have a comfortable and harmonious rains? And how did you get almsfood without trouble?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.2.19
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They then told him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.2.20
Pali: “Kacci pana vo, bhikkhave, bhūtan”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “But did you really have those superhuman qualities?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.2.21
Pali: “Bhūtaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.2.22
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.2.23

English (brahmali, published): “How could you for the sake of your stomachs talk up one another’s superhuman qualities to householders? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.2.24
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.2.25
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:1.2.26.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu anupasampannassa uttarimanussadhammaṁ āroceyya bhūtasmiṁ, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk truthfully tells a person who is not fully ordained of a superhuman quality, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A:

English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.1.5
Pali: Anupasampanno nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A person who is not fully ordained: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.1.6
Pali: bhikkhuñca bhikkhuniñca ṭhapetvā, avaseso anupasampanno nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): anyone except a fully ordained monk or a fully ordained nun. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.1.7
Pali: Uttarimanussadhammo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A superhuman quality: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.1.8
Pali: jhānaṁ, vimokkho, samādhi, samāpatti, ñāṇadassanaṁ, maggabhāvanā, phalasacchikiriyā, kilesappahānaṁ, vinīvaraṇatā cittassa, suññāgāre abhirati. 
English (brahmali, published): absorption, release, stillness, attainment, knowledge and vision, development of the path, realization of the fruits, abandoning the defilements, a mind without hindrances, delighting in solitude. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.1.9.1
Pali: Jhānanti 
English (brahmali, published): Absorption: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.1.10
Pali: paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ, dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ, tatiyaṁ jhānaṁ, catutthaṁ jhānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): the first absorption, the second absorption, the third absorption, the fourth absorption. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.1.11
Pali: Vimokkhoti 
English (brahmali, published): Release: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.1.12
Pali: suññato vimokkho, animitto vimokkho, appaṇihito vimokkho. 
English (brahmali, published): emptiness release, signless release, desireless release. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.1.13
Pali: Samādhīti 
English (brahmali, published): Stillness: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.1.14
Pali: suññato samādhi, animitto samādhi, appaṇihito samādhi. 
English (brahmali, published): emptiness stillness, signless stillness, desireless stillness. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.1.15
Pali: Samāpattīti 
English (brahmali, published): Attainment: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.1.16
Pali: suññatā samāpatti, animittā samāpatti, appaṇihitā samāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): emptiness attainment, signless attainment, desireless attainment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.1.17
Pali: Ñāṇadassananti 
English (brahmali, published): Knowledge and vision: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.1.18
Pali: tisso vijjā. 
English (brahmali, published): the three true insights. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.1.19
Pali: Maggabhāvanāti 
English (brahmali, published): Development of the path: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.1.20
Pali: cattāro satipaṭṭhānā, cattāro sammappadhānā, cattāro iddhipādā, pañcindriyāni, pañca balāni, satta bojjhaṅgā, ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo. 
English (brahmali, published): the four applications of mindfulness, the four right efforts, the four foundations for supernormal power, the five spiritual faculties, the five spiritual powers, the seven factors of awakening, the noble eightfold path. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.1.21
Pali: Phalasacchikiriyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Realization of the fruits: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.1.22
Pali: sotāpattiphalassa sacchikiriyā, sakadāgāmiphalassa sacchikiriyā, anāgāmiphalassa sacchikiriyā, arahattassa sacchikiriyā. 
English (brahmali, published): realization of the fruit of streamentry, realization of the fruit of once-returning, realization of the fruit of non-returning, realization of perfection. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.1.23
Pali: Kilesappahānanti 
English (brahmali, published): Abandoning the defilements: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.1.24
Pali: rāgassa pahānaṁ, dosassa pahānaṁ, mohassa pahānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): the abandoning of sensual desire, the abandoning of ill will, the abandoning of confusion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.1.25
Pali: Vinīvaraṇatā cittassāti 
English (brahmali, published): A mind without hindrances: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.1.26
Pali: rāgā cittaṁ vinīvaraṇatā, dosā cittaṁ vinīvaraṇatā, mohā cittaṁ vinīvaraṇatā. 
English (brahmali, published): a mind without sensual desire, a mind without ill will, a mind without confusion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.1.27
Pali: Suññāgāre abhiratīti 
English (brahmali, published): Delighting in solitude: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.1.28
Pali: paṭhamena jhānena suññāgāre abhirati, dutiyena jhānena suññāgāre abhirati, tatiyena jhānena suññāgāre abhirati, catutthena jhānena suññāgāre abhirati. 
English (brahmali, published): because of the first absorption there is delight in solitude, because of the second absorption there is delight in solitude, because of the third absorption there is delight in solitude, because of the fourth absorption there is delight in solitude. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.2.1
Pali: Āroceyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Tells: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.2.2
Pali: anupasampannassa—“paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjin”ti bhaṇantassa āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if a monk says to a person who is not fully ordained, “I attained the first absorption,” he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.2.3
Pali: Āroceyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Tells: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.2.4
Pali: anupasampannassa—“paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjāmī”ti bhaṇantassa āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if a monk says to a person who is not fully ordained, “I’m attaining the first absorption,” he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.2.5
Pali: Āroceyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Tells: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.2.6
Pali: anupasampannassa—“paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpanno”ti bhaṇantassa āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if a monk says to a person who is not fully ordained, “I’ve attained the first absorption,” he commits an offense entailing confession.

Pali: Āroceyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Tells: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.2.8
Pali: anupasampannassa—“paṭhamassa jhānassa lābhimhī”ti bhaṇantassa āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if a monk says to a person who is not fully ordained, “I obtain the first absorption,” he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.2.9
Pali: Āroceyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Tells: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.2.10
Pali: anupasampannassa—“paṭhamassa jhānassa vasimhī”ti bhaṇantassa āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if a monk says to a person who is not fully ordained, “I master the first absorption,” he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.2.11
Pali: Āroceyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Tells: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.2.12
Pali: anupasampannassa—“paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ sacchikataṁ mayā”ti bhaṇantassa āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if a monk says to a person who is not fully ordained, “I’ve realized the first absorption,” he commits an offense entailing confession.

Pali: tatiyaṁ jhānaṁ … 
English (brahmali, published): the third absorption … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.2.16

English (brahmali, published): the fourth absorption … I’m attaining … I’ve attained … I obtain … I master … I’ve realized the fourth absorption,” he commits an offense entailing confession.

English (brahmali, published): the desireless release …

English (brahmali, published): the desireless stillness … I’m attaining … I’ve attained … I obtain … I master … I’ve realized the desireless stillness,” he commits an offense entailing confession.

English (brahmali, published): the desireless attainment … I’m attaining … I’ve attained … I obtain … I master … I’ve realized the desireless attainment,” he commits an offense entailing confession.

English (brahmali, published): if a monk says to a person who is not fully ordained, “I attained the three true insights … I’m attaining … I’ve attained … I obtain … I master … I’ve realized the three true insights,” he commits an offense entailing confession.

English (brahmali, published): if a monk says to a person who is not fully ordained, “I attained the four applications of mindfulness … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.2.32
Pali: cattāro sammappadhāne … 
English (brahmali, published): the four right efforts … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.2.33

English (brahmali, published): the four foundations for supernormal power … I’m attaining … I’ve attained … I obtain … I master … I’ve realized the four foundations for supernormal power,” he commits an offense entailing confession.

English (brahmali, published): the five spiritual powers … I’m attaining … I’ve attained … I obtain … I master … I’ve realized the five spiritual powers,” he commits an offense entailing confession.

English (brahmali, published): if a monk says to a person who is not fully ordained, “I attained the seven factors of awakening … I’m attaining … I’ve attained … I obtain … I master … I’ve realized the seven factors of awakening,” he commits an offense entailing confession.

English (brahmali, published): if a monk says to a person who is not fully ordained, “I attained the noble eightfold path … I’m attaining … I’ve attained … I obtain … I master … I’ve realized the noble eightfold path,” he commits an offense entailing confession.

English (brahmali, published): if a monk says to a person who is not fully ordained, “I attained the fruit of streamentry … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.2.43
Pali: sakadāgāmiphalaṁ … 
English (brahmali, published): the fruit of once-returning … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.2.44
Pali: anāgāmiphalaṁ … 
English (brahmali, published): the fruit of non-returning … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.2.45
Pali: arahattaṁ samāpajjiṁ, samāpajjāmi, samāpanno; arahattassa lābhimhi, vasimhi; arahattaṁ sacchikataṁ mayā”ti bhaṇantassa āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): perfection … I’m attaining … I’ve attained … I obtain … I master … I’ve realized perfection,” he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.2.46
Pali: Āroceyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Tells: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.2.47
Pali: anupasampannassa— “rāgo me catto …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): if a monk says to a person who is not fully ordained, “I’ve given up sensual desire … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.2.48
Pali: doso me catto … 
English (brahmali, published): I’ve given up ill will … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.2.49
Pali: moho me catto, vanto, mutto, pahīno, paṭinissaṭṭho, ukkheṭito, samukkheṭito”ti bhaṇantassa āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): I’ve given up confusion, I’ve renounced it, I’ve let it go, I’ve abandoned it, I’ve relinquished it, I’ve forsaken it, I’ve thrown it aside,” he commits an offense entailing confession.

English (brahmali, published): if a monk says to a person who is not fully ordained, “My mind is free from the hindrance of sensual desire … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.2.52
Pali: dosā me cittaṁ vinīvaraṇaṁ … 
English (brahmali, published): My mind is free from the hindrance of ill will … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.2.53
Pali: mohā me cittaṁ vinīvaraṇan”ti bhaṇantassa āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): My mind is free from the hindrance of confusion,” he commits an offense entailing confession.

Pali: dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ … 
English (brahmali, published): the second absorption …

Pali: tatiyaṁ jhānaṁ … 
English (brahmali, published): the third absorption …

English (brahmali, published): the fourth absorption … I’m attaining … I’ve attained … I obtain … I master … I’ve realized the fourth absorption in solitude,” he commits an offense entailing confession.

Pali: Āroceyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Tells:

English (brahmali, published): Tells: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.3.31
Pali: anupasampannassa— “paṭhamañca jhānaṁ sotāpattiphalañca …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): if a monk says to a person who is not fully ordained, “I attained the first absorption and the fruit of streamentry … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.3.32
Pali: sakadāgāmiphalañca … 
English (brahmali, published): and the fruit of once-returning … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.3.33
Pali: anāgāmiphalañca … 
English (brahmali, published): and the fruit of non-returning … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.3.34
Pali: arahattañca samāpajjiṁ, samāpajjāmi, samāpanno; paṭhamassa ca jhānassa arahattassa ca lābhimhi, vasimhi; paṭhamañca jhānaṁ arahattañca sacchikataṁ mayā”ti bhaṇantassa āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): and perfection … I’m attaining … I’ve attained … I obtain … I master … I’ve realized the first absorption and perfection,” he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.3.35
Pali: Āroceyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Tells: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.3.36
Pali: anupasampannassa— “paṭhamañca jhānaṁ samāpajjiṁ, samāpajjāmi, samāpanno …pe… rāgo ca me catto … 
English (brahmali, published): if a monk says to a person who is not fully ordained, “I attained the first absorption and I’ve given up sensual desire … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.3.37
Pali: doso ca me catto … 
English (brahmali, published): and I’ve given up ill will … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.3.38
Pali: moho ca me catto, vanto, mutto, pahīno, paṭinissaṭṭho, ukkheṭito, samukkheṭito”ti bhaṇantassa āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): and I’ve given up confusion … I’m attaining … I’ve attained … I obtain … I master … I’ve realized the first absorption and I’ve given up confusion,” he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.3.39
Pali: Āroceyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Tells: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.3.40

English (brahmali, published): if a monk says to a person who is not fully ordained, “I attained the first absorption and my mind is free from the hindrance of sensual desire … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.3.41

English (brahmali, published): and my mind is free from the hindrance of ill will …

English (brahmali, published): if a monk says to a person who is not fully ordained, “I attained the second absorption and the third absorption … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.3.45
Pali: dutiyañca jhānaṁ catutthañca jhānaṁ samāpajjiṁ, samāpajjāmi, samāpanno; dutiyassa ca jhānassa catutthassa ca jhānassa lābhimhi, vasimhi; dutiyañca jhānaṁ catutthañca jhānaṁ sacchikataṁ mayā”ti bhaṇantassa āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): the second absorption and the fourth absorption … I’m attaining … I’ve attained … I obtain … I master … I’ve realized the second absorption and the fourth absorption,” he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.3.46
Pali: Āroceyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Tells: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.3.47
Pali: anupasampannassa— “dutiyañca jhānaṁ suññatañca vimokkhaṁ …pe… mohā ca me cittaṁ vinīvaraṇan”ti bhaṇantassa āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if a monk says to a person who is not fully ordained, “I attained the second absorption and the emptiness release … and my mind is free from the hindrance of confusion,” he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.3.48
Pali: Āroceyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Tells: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.3.49
Pali: anupasampannassa— “dutiyañca jhānaṁ paṭhamañca jhānaṁ samāpajjiṁ, samāpajjāmi, samāpanno; dutiyassa ca jhānassa paṭhamassa ca jhānassa lābhimhi, vasimhi; dutiyañca jhānaṁ paṭhamañca jhānaṁ sacchikataṁ mayā”ti bhaṇantassa āpatti pācittiyassa …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): if a monk says to a person who is not fully ordained, “I attained the second absorption and the first absorption … I’m attaining … I’ve attained … I obtain … I master … I’ve realized the second absorption and the first absorption,” he commits an offense entailing confession. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.3.50
Pali: Mūlaṁ saṅkhittaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The basis in brief is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.3.51
Pali: Āroceyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Tells: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.3.52
Pali: anupasampannassa— “mohā ca me cittaṁ vinīvaraṇaṁ, paṭhamañca jhānaṁ samāpajjiṁ, samāpajjāmi, samāpanno; mohā ca me cittaṁ vinīvaraṇaṁ, paṭhamassa ca jhānassa lābhimhi, vasimhi; mohā ca me cittaṁ vinīvaraṇaṁ, paṭhamañca jhānaṁ sacchikataṁ mayā”ti bhaṇantassa āpatti pācittiyassa …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): if a monk says to a person who is not fully ordained, “My mind is free from the hindrance of confusion and I attained the first absorption … I’m attaining … I’ve attained … I obtain … I master … My mind is free from the hindrance of confusion and I’ve realized the first absorption,” he commits an offense entailing confession. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.3.53
Pali: Āroceyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Tells: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.3.54

English (brahmali, published): if a monk says to a person who is not fully ordained, “My mind is free from the hindrance of confusion and my mind is free from the hindrance of ill will,” he commits an offense entailing confession. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.3.55.1
Pali: Āroceyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Tells: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.3.56
Pali: anupasampannassa— “paṭhamañca jhānaṁ dutiyañca jhānaṁ tatiyañca jhānaṁ catutthañca jhānaṁ suññatañca vimokkhaṁ animittañca vimokkhaṁ appaṇihitañca vimokkhaṁ suññatañca samādhiṁ animittañca samādhiṁ appaṇihitañca samādhiṁ suññatañca samāpattiṁ animittañca samāpattiṁ appaṇihitañca samāpattiṁ tisso ca vijjā cattāro ca satipaṭṭhāne cattāro ca sammappadhāne cattāro ca iddhipāde pañca ca indriyāni pañca ca balāni satta ca bojjhaṅge ariyañca aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ sotāpattiphalañca sakadāgāmiphalañca anāgāmiphalañca arahattañca samāpajjiṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): if a monk says to a person who is not fully ordained, “I attained the first absorption and the second absorption and the third absorption and the fourth absorption and the emptiness release and the signless release and the desireless release and the emptiness stillness and the signless stillness and the desireless stillness and the emptiness attainment and the signless attainment and the desireless attainment and the three true insights and the four applications of mindfulness and the four right efforts and the four foundations for supernormal power and the five spiritual faculties and the five spiritual powers and the seven factors of awakening and the noble eightfold path and the fruit of stream entry and the fruit of once-returning and the fruit of non-returning and perfection … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.3.57
Pali: rāgo ca me catto, doso ca me catto, moho ca me catto, vanto, mutto, pahīno, paṭinissaṭṭho, ukkheṭito samukkheṭito, rāgā ca me cittaṁ vinīvaraṇaṁ, dosā ca me cittaṁ vinīvaraṇaṁ, mohā ca me cittaṁ vinīvaraṇan”ti bhaṇantassa āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): and I’ve given up sensual desire, renounced it, let it go, abandoned it, relinquished it, forsaken it, thrown it aside; and I’ve given up ill will, renounced it, let it go, abandoned it, relinquished it, forsaken it, thrown it aside; and I’ve given up confusion, renounced it, let it go, abandoned it, relinquished it, forsaken it, thrown it aside; and my mind is free from the hindrance of sensual desire; and my mind is free from the hindrance of ill will; and my mind is free from the hindrance of confusion,” he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.4.1
Pali: Āroceyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Tells: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.4.2
Pali: anupasampannassa— “paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjin”ti vattukāmo—“dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjin”ti bhaṇantassa paṭivijānantassa āpatti pācittiyassa, na paṭivijānantassa āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if a monk means to say to a person who is not fully ordained, “I attained the first absorption,” but actually says, “I attained the second absorption,” then, if the listener understands, he commits an offense entailing confession; if the listener does not understand, he commits an offense of wrong conduct.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.5.1

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc8:2.5.2

English (brahmali, published): if a monk says to a person who is not fully ordained, “The monk who stayed in your dwelling attained the first absorption … is attaining … has attained … obtains … masters … has realized the first absorption,” he commits an offense of wrong conduct.

Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense:

English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender.

Pali: Bhūtārocanasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ aṭṭhamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on truthfully announcing a superhuman quality, the eighth, is finished.

English (brahmali, published): 9. Announcing a monk’s grave offense 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā upanando sakyaputto chabbaggiyehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ bhaṇḍanakato hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Upananda the Sakyan was quarreling with the monks from the group of six. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:1.3
Pali: So sañcetanikaṁ sukkavissaṭṭhiṁ āpattiṁ āpajjitvā saṅghaṁ tassā āpattiyā parivāsaṁ yāci. 
English (brahmali, published): He then committed an offense of intentional emission of semen. He asked the Sangha for probation for that offense, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:1.4
Pali: Tassa saṅgho tassā āpattiyā parivāsaṁ adāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): which he received. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:1.5
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sāvatthiyaṁ aññatarassa pūgassa saṅghabhattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): Just then an association in Sāvatthī was offering a meal to the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:1.6
Pali: So parivasanto bhattagge āsanapariyante nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): Because Upananda was on probation, he sat on the last seat in the dining hall. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:1.7
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhū te upāsake etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): The monks from the group of six then told those lay followers, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:1.8
Pali: “eso, āvuso, āyasmā upanando sakyaputto tumhākaṁ sambhāvito kulūpako; yeneva hatthena saddhādeyyaṁ bhuñjati teneva hatthena upakkamitvā asuciṁ mocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): “This Venerable Upananda, the esteemed associate of your families, eats the food given in faith with the same hand he uses to masturbate. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:1.9
Pali: So sañcetanikaṁ sukkavissaṭṭhiṁ āpattiṁ āpajjitvā saṅghaṁ tassā āpattiyā parivāsaṁ yāci. 
English (brahmali, published): After committing an offense of intentional emission of semen, he asked the Sangha for probation for that offense, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:1.10
Pali: Tassa saṅgho tassā āpattiyā parivāsaṁ adāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): which he received. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:1.11
Pali: So parivasanto āsanapariyante nisinno”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): And because he’s on probation, he now sits on the last seat.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:1.12
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:1.13
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū bhikkhussa duṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ anupasampannassa ārocessantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the monks from the group of six tell a person who’s not fully ordained about a monk’s grave offense?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:1.14

English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you did this?”

Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:1.16
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:1.17
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, bhikkhussa duṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ anupasampannassa ārocessatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:1.18
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:1.19
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:1.20.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhussa duṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ anupasampannassa āroceyya, aññatra bhikkhusammutiyā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk tells a person who is not fully ordained about a monk’s grave offense, except if the monks have agreed, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.1.5

English (brahmali, published): A monk’s:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.1.7

English (brahmali, published): A grave offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.1.8
Pali: cattāri ca pārājikāni, terasa ca saṅghādisesā. 
English (brahmali, published): the four offenses entailing expulsion and the thirteen entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.1.9
Pali: Anupasampanno nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A person who is not fully ordained: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.1.10
Pali: bhikkhuñca bhikkhuniñca ṭhapetvā avaseso anupasampanno nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): anyone except a fully ordained monk or a fully ordained nun. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.1.11
Pali: Āroceyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Tells:

Pali: āroceyya itthiyā vā purisassa vā gahaṭṭhassa vā pabbajitassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): tells a woman or a man, a householder or a monastic. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.1.13
Pali: Aññatra bhikkhusammutiyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Except if the monks have agreed: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.1.14
Pali: ṭhapetvā bhikkhusammutiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): unless the monks have agreed. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.1.15.1
Pali: Atthi bhikkhusammuti āpattipariyantā, na kulapariyantā. 
English (brahmali, published): There is agreement of the monks with a limit on offenses, but not on families. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.1.16
Pali: Atthi bhikkhusammuti kulapariyantā, na āpattipariyantā, atthi bhikkhusammuti āpattipariyantā ca kulapariyantā ca, atthi bhikkhusammuti neva āpattipariyantā na kulapariyantā. 
English (brahmali, published): There is agreement of the monks with a limit on families, but not on offenses. There is agreement of the monks with a limit both on offenses and on families. There is agreement of the monks with neither a limit on offenses nor on families. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.1.17.1
Pali: Āpattipariyantā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): With a limit on offenses: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.1.18
Pali: āpattiyo pariggahitāyo honti— “ettakāhi āpattīhi ārocetabbo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): offenses are specified: “These particular offenses can be told about.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.1.19
Pali: Kulapariyantā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): With limit on families: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.1.20
Pali: kulāni pariggahitāni honti— “ettakesu kulesu ārocetabbo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): families are specified: “These particular families can be told.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.1.21
Pali: Āpattipariyantā ca kulapariyantā ca nāma 
English (brahmali, published): With a limit both on offenses and on families: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.1.22
Pali: āpattiyo ca pariggahitāyo honti, kulāni ca pariggahitāni honti— “ettakāhi āpattīhi ettakesu kulesu ārocetabbo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): both offenses and families are specified: “These particular offenses can be told about, and these particular families can be told.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.1.23
Pali: Neva āpattipariyantā na kulapariyantā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): With neither a limit on offenses nor on families: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.1.24
Pali: āpattiyo ca apariggahitāyo honti, kulāni ca apariggahitāni honti— “ettakāhi āpattīhi ettakesu kulesu ārocetabbo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): neither offenses nor families are specified in this way. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.1.25.1
Pali: Āpattipariyante yā āpattiyo pariggahitāyo honti, tā āpattiyo ṭhapetvā aññāhi āpattīhi āroceti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When there is a limit on offenses, if he tells about other offenses than those that are specified, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.1.26
Pali: Kulapariyante yāni kulāni pariggahitāni honti, tāni kulāni ṭhapetvā aññesu kulesu āroceti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When there is a limit on families, if he tells other families than those that are specified, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.1.27
Pali: Āpattipariyante ca kulapariyante ca yā āpattiyo pariggahitāyo honti, tā āpattiyo ṭhapetvā yāni kulāni pariggahitāni honti, tāni kulāni ṭhapetvā aññāhi āpattīhi aññesu kulesu āroceti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When there is a limit both on offenses and on families, if he tells about other offenses than those that are specified or he tells other families than those that are specified, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.1.28
Pali: Neva āpattipariyante na kulapariyante, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): When there is neither a limit on offenses nor on families, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.2.1
Pali: Duṭṭhullāya āpattiyā duṭṭhullāpattisaññī anupasampannassa āroceti, aññatra bhikkhusammutiyā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the offense is grave, and he perceives it as such, and he tells a person who is not fully ordained, then, except if the monks have agreed, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.2.2
Pali: Duṭṭhullāya āpattiyā vematiko anupasampannassa āroceti, aññatra bhikkhusammutiyā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the offense is grave, but he is unsure of it, and he tells a person who is not fully ordained, then, except if the monks have agreed, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.2.3
Pali: Duṭṭhullāya āpattiyā aduṭṭhullāpattisaññī anupasampannassa āroceti, aññatra bhikkhusammutiyā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the offense is grave, but he perceives it as minor, and he tells a person who is not fully ordained, then, except if the monks have agreed, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.2.4
Pali: Aduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ āroceti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he tells about a minor offense, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.2.5
Pali: Anupasampannassa duṭṭhullaṁ vā aduṭṭhullaṁ vā ajjhācāraṁ āroceti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he tells about the misconduct of a person who is not fully ordained, whether grave or minor, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.2.6
Pali: Aduṭṭhullāya āpattiyā duṭṭhullāpattisaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the offense is minor, but he perceives it as grave, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.2.7
Pali: Aduṭṭhullāya āpattiyā vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the offense is minor, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.2.8
Pali: Aduṭṭhullāya āpattiyā aduṭṭhullāpattisaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the offense is minor, and he perceives it as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.3.2
Pali: vatthuṁ āroceti no āpattiṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if he tells about the action that was the basis for the offense, but not the class of offense; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.3.3
Pali: āpattiṁ āroceti no vatthuṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if he tells about the class of offense, but not the action that was the basis for the offense; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.3.4
Pali: bhikkhusammutiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if the monks have agreed; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.3.5
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.3.6
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc9:2.3.7
Pali: Duṭṭhullārocanasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ navamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on announcing a monk’s grave offense, the ninth, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis

English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:0.4
Pali: Musāvādavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on lying 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:0.5
Pali: 10. Pathavīkhaṇanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 10. Digging the earth 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā āḷaviyaṁ viharati aggāḷave cetiye. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Āḷavī at the Aggāḷava Shrine. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āḷavakā bhikkhū navakammaṁ karontā pathaviṁ khaṇantipi khaṇāpentipi. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the monks of Āḷavī were doing building work, and they dug the earth and had it dug. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:1.3
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:1.4
Pali: “kathañhi nāma samaṇā sakyaputtiyā pathaviṁ khaṇissantipi khaṇāpessantipi. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the Sakyan monastics dig the earth and have it dug? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:1.5
Pali: Ekindriyaṁ samaṇā sakyaputtiyā jīvaṁ viheṭhentī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): They are hurting one-sensed life.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:1.6
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:1.7
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:1.8
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āḷavakā bhikkhū pathaviṁ khaṇissantipi khaṇāpessantipī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can those monks at Āḷavī dig the earth and have it dug?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:1.9
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, pathaviṁ khaṇathapi khaṇāpethapī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:1.10
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:1.11
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:1.12
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, pathaviṁ khaṇissathapi khaṇāpessathapi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:1.13
Pali: Jīvasaññino hi, moghapurisā, manussā pathaviyā. 
English (brahmali, published): People regard the earth as conscious. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:1.14
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:1.15
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:1.16.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu pathaviṁ khaṇeyya vā khaṇāpeyya vā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk digs the earth or has it dug, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.1.5
Pali: Pathavī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The earth: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.1.6
Pali: dve pathaviyo— jātā ca pathavī ajātā ca pathavī. 
English (brahmali, published): there are two kinds of earth: productive earth and unproductive earth. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.1.7
Pali: Jātā nāma pathavī—
English (brahmali, published): Productive earth: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.1.8
Pali: suddhapaṁsu suddhamattikā appapāsāṇā appasakkharā appakaṭhalā appamarumbā appavālikā, yebhuyyenapaṁsukā, yebhuyyenamattikā. 
English (brahmali, published): pure soil, pure clay, with few stones, with few pebbles, with few potsherds, with little gravel, with little sand; mostly soil, mostly clay. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.1.9
Pali: Adaḍḍhāpi vuccati jātā pathavī. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is unburned, it is also called “productive earth”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.1.10
Pali: Yopi paṁsupuñjo vā mattikāpuñjo vā atirekacātumāsaṁ ovaṭṭho, ayampi vuccati jātā pathavī. 
English (brahmali, published): A pile of soil or clay that has been rained on for more than four months—this too is called “productive earth”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.1.11
Pali: Ajātā nāma pathavī—
English (brahmali, published): Unproductive earth: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.1.12
Pali: suddhapāsāṇā suddhasakkharā suddhakaṭhalā suddhamarumbā suddhavālikā appapaṁsukā appamattikā, yebhuyyenapāsāṇā, yebhuyyenasakkharā, yebhuyyenakaṭhalā, yebhuyyenamarumbā, yebhuyyenavālikā. 
English (brahmali, published): just stones, just pebbles, just potsherds, just gravel, just sand, with little soil, with little clay; mostly stones, mostly pebbles, mostly potsherds, mostly gravel, mostly sand. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.1.13
Pali: Daḍḍhāpi vuccati ajātā pathavī. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is burned, it is also called “unproductive earth”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.1.14
Pali: Yopi paṁsupuñjo vā mattikāpuñjo vā omakacātumāsaṁ ovaṭṭho, ayampi vuccati ajātā pathavī. 
English (brahmali, published): A pile of soil or clay that has been rained on for less than four months—this too is called “unproductive earth”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.1.15
Pali: Khaṇeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Digs: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.1.16
Pali: sayaṁ khaṇati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he digs it himself, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.1.17
Pali: Khaṇāpeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Has dug: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.1.18
Pali: aññaṁ āṇāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he asks another, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.1.19
Pali: Sakiṁ āṇatto bahukampi khaṇati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he only asks once, then even if the other digs a lot, he commits one offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.2.1
Pali: Pathaviyā pathavisaññī khaṇati vā khaṇāpeti vā, bhindati vā bhedāpeti vā, dahati vā dahāpeti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is earth, and he perceives it as such, and he digs it or has it dug, or he breaks it or has it broken, or he burns it or has it burned, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.2.2
Pali: Pathaviyā vematiko khaṇati vā khaṇāpeti vā, bhindati vā bhedāpeti vā, dahati vā dahāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is earth, but he is unsure of it, and he digs it or has it dug, or he breaks it or has it broken, or he burns it or has it burned, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.2.3
Pali: Pathaviyā apathavisaññī khaṇati vā khaṇāpeti vā, bhindati vā bhedāpeti vā, dahati vā dahāpeti vā, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is earth, but he does not perceive it as such, and he digs it or has it dug, or he breaks it or has it broken, or he burns it or has it burned, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.2.4
Pali: Apathaviyā pathavisaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not earth, but he perceives it as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.2.5
Pali: Apathaviyā vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not earth, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.2.6
Pali: Apathaviyā apathavisaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not earth, and he does not perceive it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.3.2
Pali: “imaṁ jāna, imaṁ dehi, imaṁ āhara, iminā attho, imaṁ kappiyaṁ karohī”ti bhaṇati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he says, “Consider this,” “Give this,” “Bring this,” “There’s need for this,” “Make this allowable;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.3.3
Pali: asañcicca, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is unintentional; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.3.4
Pali: asatiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is not mindful; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.3.5
Pali: ajānantassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not know; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.3.6
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.3.7
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.3.8
Pali: Pathavīkhaṇanasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ dasamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on digging the earth, the tenth, is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.3.9
Pali: Musāvādavaggo paṭhamo. 
English (brahmali, published): The first subchapter on lying is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.3.10
Pali: Tassuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.3.11
Pali: Musā omasapesuññaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): “Falsely, abusive, and malicious talebearing, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.3.12
Pali: padaseyyāya ve duve; 
English (brahmali, published): Memorizing, and two on beds; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.3.13
Pali: Aññatra viññunā bhūtā, 
English (brahmali, published): Except with one who understands, true, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc10:2.3.14
Pali: duṭṭhullāpatti khaṇanāti. 
English (brahmali, published): Grave offense, digging.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:0.4
Pali: Bhūtagāmavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on plants 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:0.5
Pali: 11. Bhūtagāmasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 11. Destroying plants 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā āḷaviyaṁ viharati aggāḷave cetiye. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Āḷavī at the Aggāḷava Shrine. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āḷavakā bhikkhū navakammaṁ karontā rukkhaṁ chindantipi chedāpentipi. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the monks of Āḷavī were doing building work, and they cut down trees and had them cut down. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:1.3
Pali: Aññataropi āḷavako bhikkhu rukkhaṁ chindati. 
English (brahmali, published): Then, when a certain monk was cutting down a tree, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:1.4
Pali: Tasmiṁ rukkhe adhivatthā devatā taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): the deity that lived in it said to him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:1.5
Pali: “mā, bhante, attano bhavanaṁ kattukāmo mayhaṁ bhavanaṁ chindī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, don’t cut down our dwelling because you want to build a dwelling for yourself.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:1.6
Pali: So bhikkhu anādiyanto chindiyeva, tassā ca devatāya dārakassa bāhuṁ ākoṭesi. 
English (brahmali, published): Not taking any heed, he just cut it down, and he hurt the arm of that deity’s child. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:1.7
Pali: Atha kho tassā devatāya etadahosi—
English (brahmali, published): The deity thought, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:1.8
Pali: “yannūnāhaṁ imaṁ bhikkhuṁ idheva jīvitā voropeyyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Why don’t I just kill this monk?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:1.9
Pali: Atha kho tassā devatāya etadahosi—
English (brahmali, published): But then it reconsidered, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:1.10
Pali: “na kho metaṁ patirūpaṁ yāhaṁ imaṁ bhikkhuṁ idheva jīvitā voropeyyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “It wouldn’t be right to just kill this monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:1.11
Pali: Yannūnāhaṁ bhagavato etamatthaṁ āroceyyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Let me instead tell the Buddha about this matter.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:1.12
Pali: Atha kho sā devatā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): And it approached the Buddha and told him what had happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:1.13
Pali: “Sādhu sādhu, devate. 
English (brahmali, published): “Well done, deity! 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:1.14
Pali: Sādhu kho tvaṁ, devate, taṁ bhikkhuṁ jīvitā na voropesi. 
English (brahmali, published): It’s good that you didn’t kill that monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:1.15
Pali: Sacajja tvaṁ, devate, taṁ bhikkhuṁ jīvitā voropeyyāsi, bahuñca tvaṁ, devate, apuññaṁ pasaveyyāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): If you had killed that monk, you would have made much demerit. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:1.16
Pali: Gaccha tvaṁ, devate, amukasmiṁ okāse rukkho vivitto tasmiṁ upagacchā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The tree over there is empty. Take that as your dwelling.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:1.17
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized the monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:1.18
Pali: “kathañhi nāma samaṇā sakyaputtiyā rukkhaṁ chindissantipi chedāpessantipi ekindriyaṁ samaṇā sakyaputtiyā jīvaṁ viheṭhentī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the Sakyan monastics cut down trees and have them cut down? They are hurting life with one sense.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:1.19
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:1.20
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:1.21
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āḷavakā bhikkhū rukkhaṁ chindissantipi chedāpessantipī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can those monks at Āḷavī cut down trees and have them cut down?”… 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:1.22
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, rukkhaṁ chindathāpi chedāpethāpī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:1.23
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:1.24
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:1.25
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, rukkhaṁ chindissathāpi, chedāpessathāpi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:1.26
Pali: Jīvasaññino hi, moghapurisā, manussā rukkhasmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): People regard trees as conscious. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:1.27
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:1.28
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:1.29.1
Pali: “Bhūtagāmapātabyatāya pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk destroys a plant, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:2.1.1
Pali: Bhūtagāmo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Plant: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:2.1.2
Pali: pañca bījajātāni— mūlabījaṁ, khandhabījaṁ, phaḷubījaṁ, aggabījaṁ, bījabījameva pañcamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): there are five kinds of propagation: propagation from roots, propagation from stems, propagation from joints, propagation from cuttings, propagation from seeds. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:2.1.3
Pali: Mūlabījaṁ nāma—
English (brahmali, published): Propagation from roots: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:2.1.4
Pali: haliddi, siṅgiveraṁ, vacā, vacattaṁ, ativisā, kaṭukarohiṇī, usīraṁ, bhaddamuttakaṁ, yāni vā panaññānipi atthi mūle jāyanti, mūle sañjāyanti, etaṁ mūlabījaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): turmeric, ginger, sweet flag, white sweet flag, atis root, black hellebore, vetiver root, nutgrass, or any other plant produced from roots, that grows from roots—this is called “propagation from roots”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:2.1.5
Pali: Khandhabījaṁ nāma—
English (brahmali, published): Propagation from stems: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:2.1.6
Pali: assattho, nigrodho, pilakkho, udumbaro, kacchako, kapitthano, yāni vā panaññānipi atthi khandhe jāyanti, khandhe sañjāyanti, etaṁ khandhabījaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): the Bodhi tree, the banyan tree, the Indian rock fig, the cluster fig, the Indian cedar, the portia tree, or any other plant produced from stems, that grows from stems—this is called “propagation from stems”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:2.1.7
Pali: Phaḷubījaṁ nāma—
English (brahmali, published): Propagation from joints: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:2.1.8
Pali: ucchu, veḷu, naḷo, yāni vā panaññānipi atthi pabbe jāyanti, pabbe sañjāyanti, etaṁ phaḷubījaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): sugarcane, bamboo, reed, or any other plant produced from joints, that grows from joints—this is called “propagation from joints”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:2.1.9
Pali: Aggabījaṁ nāma—
English (brahmali, published): Propagation from cuttings: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:2.1.10
Pali: ajjukaṁ, phaṇijjakaṁ, hiriveraṁ, yāni vā panaññānipi atthi agge jāyanti, agge sañjāyanti, etaṁ aggabījaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): shrubby basil, rajmahal hemp, Vicks plant, or any other plant produced from cuttings, that grows from cuttings—this is called “propagation from cuttings”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:2.1.11
Pali: Bījabījaṁ nāma—
English (brahmali, published): Propagation from seeds: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:2.1.12
Pali: pubbaṇṇaṁ, aparaṇṇaṁ, yāni vā panaññānipi atthi bīje jāyanti, bīje sañjāyanti, etaṁ bījabījaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): grains, vegetables, or any other plant produced from seeds, that grows from seeds—this is called “propagation from seeds”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:2.2.1
Pali: Bīje bījasaññī chindati vā chedāpeti vā, bhindati vā bhedāpeti vā, pacati vā pacāpeti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is capable of propagation, and he perceives that it is, and he cuts it down or has it cut down, or he breaks it or has it broken, or he cooks it or has it cooked, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:2.2.2
Pali: Bīje vematiko chindati vā chedāpeti vā, bhindati vā bhedāpeti vā, pacati vā pacāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is capable of propagation, but he is unsure of it, and  he cuts it down or has it cut down, or he breaks it or has it broken, or he cooks it or has it cooked, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:2.2.3
Pali: Bīje abījasaññī chindati vā chedāpeti vā, bhindati vā bhedāpeti vā, pacati vā pacāpeti vā, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is capable of propagation, but he perceives that it is not, and he cuts it down or has it cut down, or he breaks it or has it broken, or he cooks it or has it cooked, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:2.2.4
Pali: Abīje bījasaññī āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not capable of propagation, but he perceives that it is, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:2.2.5
Pali: Abīje vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not capable of propagation, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:2.2.6
Pali: Abīje abījasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not capable of propagation, and he perceives that it is not, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:2.3.2
Pali: “imaṁ jāna, imaṁ dehi, imaṁ āhara, iminā attho, imaṁ kappiyaṁ karohī”ti bhaṇati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he says, “Consider this”, “Give this”, “Bring this”, “There’s need for this”, “Make this allowable;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:2.3.3
Pali: asañcicca, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is unintentional; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:2.3.4
Pali: assatiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is not mindful; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:2.3.5
Pali: ajānantassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not know; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:2.3.6
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:2.3.7
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc11:2.3.8
Pali: Bhūtagāmasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ paṭhamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on destroying plants, the first, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā kosambiyaṁ viharati ghositārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Kosambī in Ghosita’s Monastery,

English (brahmali, published): Venerable Channa was misbehaving. Then, when he was examined about an offense in the midst of the Sangha, he spoke evasively,

English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized him,

English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:1.7

English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Channa, that you did this?”

English (brahmali, published): This is the motion.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:1.25
Pali: Saṅgho channassa bhikkhuno aññavādakaṁ ropeti. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha is charging the monk Channa with evasive speech. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:1.26
Pali: Yassāyasmato khamati channassa bhikkhuno aññavādakassa ropanā, so tuṇhassa; 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who approves of charging the monk Channa with evasive speech should remain silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:1.27
Pali: yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:1.28
Pali: Ropitaṁ saṅghena channassa bhikkhuno aññavādakaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha has charged the monk Channa with evasive speech. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:1.29
Pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’”

English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā channo saṅghamajjhe āpattiyā anuyuñjīyamāno aññenaññaṁ paṭicaranto—
English (brahmali, published): Later, when Channa was again being examined about an offense in the midst of the Sangha, he thought,

Pali: “āpattiṁ āpajjissāmī”ti tuṇhībhūto saṅghaṁ viheseti. 
English (brahmali, published): “By speaking evasively I’ll commit an offense,” and he instead harassed the Sangha by remaining silent.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:2.28.1
Pali: “Aññavādake vihesake pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk speaks evasively or harasses, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:3.1.1
Pali: Aññavādako nāma 
English (brahmali, published): One who speaks evasively: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:3.1.2
Pali: saṅghamajjhe vatthusmiṁ vā āpattiyā vā anuyuñjīyamāno taṁ na kathetukāmo taṁ na ugghāṭetukāmo aññenaññaṁ paṭicarati— “ko āpanno, kiṁ āpanno, kismiṁ āpanno, kathaṁ āpanno, kaṁ bhaṇatha, kiṁ bhaṇathā”ti. Eso aññavādako nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): when being examined in the midst of the Sangha about an action that was the basis for an offense or about the class of an offense, he speaks evasively because he does not want to talk about it or reveal it, saying, “Who has committed an offense? What offense was committed? In regard to what was it committed? How was it committed? Who are you talking about? What are you talking about?”—this is called “one who speaks evasively”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:3.1.3
Pali: Vihesako nāma 
English (brahmali, published): One who harasses: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:3.1.4
Pali: saṅghamajjhe vatthusmiṁ vā āpattiyā vā anuyuñjīyamāno taṁ na kathetukāmo taṁ na ugghāṭetukāmo tuṇhībhūto saṅghaṁ viheseti. Eso vihesako nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): when being examined in the midst of the Sangha about an action that was the basis for an offense or about the class of an offense, he harasses the Sangha by remaining silent because he does not want to talk about it or reveal it—this is called “one who harasses”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:3.1.5.1
Pali: Aropite aññavādake saṅghamajjhe vatthusmiṁ vā āpattiyā vā anuyuñjīyamāno taṁ na kathetukāmo taṁ na ugghāṭetukāmo aññenaññaṁ paṭicarati—
English (brahmali, published): If he has not been charged with evasive speech, but he is being examined in the midst of the Sangha about an action that was the basis for an offense or about the class of an offense, and he then speaks evasively because he does not want to talk about it or reveal it, saying, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:3.1.6
Pali: “ko āpanno, kiṁ āpanno, kismiṁ āpanno, kathaṁ āpanno, kaṁ bhaṇatha, kiṁ bhaṇathā”ti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): “Who has committed an offense? What offense was committed? In regard to what was it committed? How was it committed? Who are you talking about? What are you talking about?” then he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:3.1.7
Pali: Aropite vihesake saṅghamajjhe vatthusmiṁ vā āpattiyā vā anuyuñjīyamāno taṁ na kathetukāmo taṁ na ugghāṭetukāmo tuṇhībhūto saṅghaṁ viheseti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has not been charged with harassment, but he is being examined in the midst of the Sangha about an action that was the basis for an offense or about the class of an offense, and he then harasses the Sangha by remaining silent because he does not want to talk about it or reveal it, then he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:3.1.8
Pali: Ropite aññavādake saṅghamajjhe vatthusmiṁ vā āpattiyā vā anuyuñjīyamāno taṁ na kathetukāmo taṁ na ugghāṭetukāmo aññenaññaṁ paṭicarati—
English (brahmali, published): If he has been charged with evasive speech, and he is being examined in the midst of the Sangha about an action that was the basis for an offense or about the class of an offense, and he then speaks evasively because he does not want to talk about it or reveal it, saying, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:3.1.9
Pali: “ko āpanno, kiṁ āpanno, kismiṁ āpanno, kathaṁ āpanno, kaṁ bhaṇatha, kiṁ bhaṇathā”ti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): “Who has committed an offense? What offense was committed? In regard to what was it committed? How was it committed? Who are you talking about? What are you talking about?” then he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:3.1.10
Pali: Ropite vihesake saṅghamajjhe vatthusmiṁ vā āpattiyā vā anuyuñjīyamāno taṁ na kathetukāmo taṁ na ugghāṭetukāmo tuṇhībhūto saṅghaṁ viheseti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has been charged with harassment, and he is being examined in the midst of the Sangha about an action that was the basis for an offense or about the class of an offense, and he then harasses the Sangha by remaining silent because he does not want to talk about it or reveal it, then he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:3.2.1
Pali: Dhammakamme dhammakammasaññī aññavādake vihesake, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and he perceives it as such, and he speaks evasively or he harasses, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:3.2.2
Pali: Dhammakamme vematiko aññavādake vihesake, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but he is unsure of it, and he speaks evasively or he harasses, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:3.2.3
Pali: Dhammakamme adhammakammasaññī aññavādake vihesake, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but he perceives it as illegitimate, and he speaks evasively or he harasses, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:3.2.4
Pali: Adhammakamme dhammakammasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but he perceives it as legitimate, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:3.2.5
Pali: Adhammakamme vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:3.2.6
Pali: Adhammakamme adhammakammasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and he perceives it as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:3.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:3.3.2
Pali: ajānanto pucchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he asks because he does not know; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:3.3.3
Pali: gilāno vā na katheti; 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not speak because he is sick; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:3.3.4
Pali: “saṅghassa bhaṇḍanaṁ vā kalaho vā viggaho vā vivādo vā bhavissatī”ti na katheti; 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not speak because he thinks there will be quarrels or disputes in the Sangha; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:3.3.5
Pali: “saṅghabhedo vā saṅgharāji vā bhavissatī”ti na katheti; 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not speak because he thinks there will be a fracture or schism in the Sangha; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:3.3.6
Pali: “adhammena vā vaggena vā nakammārahassa vā kammaṁ karissatī”ti na katheti; 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not speak because he thinks the legal procedure will be illegitimate, done by an incomplete assembly, or done to one who does not deserve a legal procedure; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:3.3.7
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:3.3.8
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc12:3.3.9
Pali: Aññavādakasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ dutiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on speaking evasively, the second, is finished.
