pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:0.4
Pali: Bhūtagāmavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on plants 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:0.5
Pali: 13. Ujjhāpanakasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 13. Complaining 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Rājagaha in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrel sanctuary. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā dabbo mallaputto saṅghassa senāsanañca paññapeti bhattāni ca uddisati. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time Venerable Dabba the Mallian was assigning the dwellings and designating the meals, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:1.3
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena mettiyabhūmajakā bhikkhū navakā ceva honti appapuññā ca. 
English (brahmali, published): and the monks Mettiya and Bhūmajaka were newly ordained. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:1.4
Pali: Yāni saṅghassa lāmakāni senāsanāni tāni tesaṁ pāpuṇanti lāmakāni ca bhattāni. 
English (brahmali, published): They had little merit, getting inferior dwellings and meals. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:1.5
Pali: Te āyasmantaṁ dabbaṁ mallaputtaṁ bhikkhū ujjhāpenti—
English (brahmali, published): They then complained about Dabba to other monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:1.6
Pali: “chandāya dabbo mallaputto senāsanaṁ paññapeti, chandāya ca bhattāni uddisatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Dabba the Mallian assigns dwellings and designates meals based on favoritism.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:1.7
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:1.8
Pali: “kathañhi nāma mettiyabhūmajakā bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ dabbaṁ mallaputtaṁ bhikkhū ujjhāpessantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the monks Mettiya and Bhūmajaka complain about Venerable Dabba to other monks?”… 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:1.9
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, dabbaṁ mallaputtaṁ bhikkhū ujjhāpethā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:1.10
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:1.11
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:1.12
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, dabbaṁ mallaputtaṁ bhikkhū ujjhāpessatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:1.13
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:1.14
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:1.15.1
Pali: “Ujjhāpanake pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk complains, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:1.16
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena mettiyabhūmajakā bhikkhū—
English (brahmali, published): Knowing that the Buddha had prohibited complaining, the monks Mettiya and Bhūmajaka thought of other ways of getting the monks to hear about their grievances. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:2.2
Pali: “‘bhagavatā ujjhāpanakaṁ paṭikkhittan’ti ‘ettāvatā bhikkhū sossantī’”ti bhikkhūnaṁ sāmantā āyasmantaṁ dabbaṁ mallaputtaṁ khiyyanti—
English (brahmali, published): They then criticized Dabba the Mallian in the vicinity of other monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:2.3
Pali: “chandāya dabbo mallaputto senāsanaṁ paññapeti, chandāya ca bhattāni uddisatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Dabba assigns dwellings and designates meals based on favoritism.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:2.4
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:2.5
Pali: “kathañhi nāma mettiyabhūmajakā bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ dabbaṁ mallaputtaṁ khiyyissantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the monks Mettiya and Bhūmajaka criticize Venerable Dabba?”… 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:2.6
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, dabbaṁ mallaputtaṁ khiyyathā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:2.7
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:2.8
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:2.9
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, dabbaṁ mallaputtaṁ khiyyissatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:2.10
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:2.11
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:2.12.1
Pali: “Ujjhāpanake khiyyanake pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk complains or criticizes, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:3.1.1
Pali: Ujjhāpanakaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Complaining: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:3.1.2
Pali: upasampannaṁ saṅghena sammataṁ senāsanapaññāpakaṁ vā bhattuddesakaṁ vā yāgubhājakaṁ vā phalabhājakaṁ vā khajjabhājakaṁ vā appamattakavissajjakaṁ vā avaṇṇaṁ kattukāmo, ayasaṁ kattukāmo, maṅkukattukāmo, upasampannaṁ ujjhāpeti vā khiyyati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when someone who is fully ordained is the assigner of dwellings or the designator of meals or the distributor of congee or the distributor of fruit or the distributor of fresh foods or the distributor of minor requisites, and he has been appointed by the Sangha as such, then if a monk complains about him or criticizes him to one who is fully ordained—desiring to disparage him, desiring to give him a bad reputation, desiring to humiliate him—he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:3.1.3.1
Pali: Dhammakamme dhammakammasaññī ujjhāpanake khiyyanake āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and he perceives it as such, and he complains or criticizes, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:3.1.4
Pali: Dhammakamme vematiko ujjhāpanake khiyyanake āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but he is unsure of it, and he complains or criticizes, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:3.1.5
Pali: Dhammakamme adhammakammasaññī ujjhāpanake khiyyanake āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but he perceives it as illegitimate, and he complains or criticizes, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:3.2.1
Pali: Anupasampannaṁ ujjhāpeti vā khiyyati vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he complains or criticizes him to one who is not fully ordained, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:3.2.2
Pali: Upasampannaṁ saṅghena asammataṁ senāsanapaññāpakaṁ vā bhattuddesakaṁ vā yāgubhājakaṁ vā phalabhājakaṁ vā khajjabhājakaṁ vā appamattakavissajjakaṁ vā avaṇṇaṁ kattukāmo, ayasaṁ kattukāmo, maṅkukattukāmo, upasampannaṁ vā anupasampannaṁ vā ujjhāpeti vā khiyyati vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When someone who is fully ordained is the assigner of dwellings or the designator of meals or the distributor of congee or the distributor of fruit or the distributor of fresh foods or the distributor of minor requisites, but he has not been appointed by the Sangha as such, then if a monk complains about him or criticizes him to one who is fully ordained or to one who is not fully ordained—desiring to disparage him, desiring to give him a bad reputation, desiring to humiliate him—he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:3.2.3
Pali: Anupasampannaṁ saṅghena sammataṁ vā asammataṁ vā senāsanapaññāpakaṁ vā bhattuddesakaṁ vā yāgubhājakaṁ vā phalabhājakaṁ vā khajjabhājakaṁ vā appamattakavissajjakaṁ vā avaṇṇaṁ kattukāmo, ayasaṁ kattukāmo, maṅkukattukāmo, upasampannaṁ vā anupasampannaṁ vā ujjhāpeti vā khiyyati vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When someone who is not fully ordained is the assigner of dwellings or the designator of meals or the distributor of congee or the distributor of fruit or the distributor of fresh foods or the distributor of minor requisites, whether he has been appointed by the Sangha as such or not, then if a monk complains about him or criticizes him to one who is fully ordained or to one who is not fully ordained—desiring to disparage him, desiring to give him a bad reputation, desiring to humiliate him—he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:3.2.4
Pali: Adhammakamme dhammakammasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but he perceives it as legitimate, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:3.2.5
Pali: Adhammakamme vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:3.2.6
Pali: Adhammakamme adhammakammasaññī āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and he perceives it as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:3.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:3.3.2
Pali: pakatiyā chandā dosā mohā bhayā karontaṁ ujjhāpeti vā khiyyati vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he complains about or criticizes one who regularly acts out of favoritism, ill will, confusion, or fear; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:3.3.3
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:3.3.4
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc13:3.3.5
Pali: Ujjhāpanakasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ tatiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on complaining, the third, is finished.

Root: root/pli/ms/vinaya/pli-tv-bu-vb/pli-tv-bu-vb-pc/pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14_root-pli-ms.json

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:0.4
Pali: Bhūtagāmavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on plants 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:0.5
Pali: 14. Senāsanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 14. Leaving furniture outside 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:1.1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery during winter, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:1.1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū hemantike kāle ajjhokāse senāsanaṁ paññapetvā kāyaṁ otāpentā kāle ārocite taṁ pakkamantā neva uddhariṁsu na uddharāpesuṁ, anāpucchā pakkamiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): the monks put furniture out in the open in order to warm themselves in the sun. But when the time for departure was announced, they departed without putting it away, having it put away, or informing anyone. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:1.1.3
Pali: Senāsanaṁ ovaṭṭhaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): The furniture was rained on. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:1.1.4
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:1.1.5
Pali: “kathaṁ hi nāma bhikkhū ajjhokāse senāsanaṁ paññapetvā taṁ pakkamantā neva uddharissanti na uddharāpessanti, anāpucchā pakkamissanti, senāsanaṁ ovaṭṭhan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could those monks put furniture out in the open and then depart without putting it away, having it put away, or informing anyone? The furniture was rained on.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:1.1.6
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū te anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking those monks in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:1.1.7
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhū ajjhokāse …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you did this?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:1.1.8
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:1.1.9.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu saṅghikaṁ mañcaṁ vā pīṭhaṁ vā bhisiṁ vā kocchaṁ vā ajjhokāse santharitvā vā santharāpetvā vā taṁ pakkamanto neva uddhareyya na uddharāpeyya, anāpucchaṁ vā gaccheyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk takes a bed, a bench, a mattress, or a stool belonging to the Sangha and puts it out in the open or has it put out in the open, and he then departs without putting it away, having it put away, or informing anyone, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:1.1.10
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:1.2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū ajjhokāse vasitvā kālasseva senāsanaṁ abhiharanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards, monks who had stayed out in the open brought the furniture back even though it was not the rainy season. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:1.2.2
Pali: Addasā kho bhagavā te bhikkhū kālasseva senāsanaṁ abhiharante. 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha saw this. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:1.2.3
Pali: Disvāna etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): After giving a teaching, he addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:1.2.4
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, aṭṭha māse avassikasaṅkete maṇḍape vā rukkhamūle vā yattha kākā vā kulalā vā na ūhadanti tattha senāsanaṁ nikkhipitun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, during the eight months outside of the rainy season, I allow you to store furniture under a roof cover or at the foot of a tree or wherever crows or ravens don’t leave droppings.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): …The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.1.5
Pali: Saṅghikaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Belonging to the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.1.6
Pali: saṅghassa dinnaṁ hoti pariccattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): given to the Sangha, given up to the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.1.7
Pali: Mañco nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A bed: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.1.8
Pali: cattāro mañcā—
English (brahmali, published): there are four kinds of beds: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.1.9
Pali: masārako, bundikābaddho, kuḷīrapādako, āhaccapādako. 
English (brahmali, published): one with legs and frame, called <i lang='pi' translate='no'>masāraka</i>; one with legs and frame, called <i lang='pi' translate='no'>bundikābaddha</i>; one with crooked legs; one with detachable legs. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.1.10
Pali: Pīṭhaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A bench: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.1.11
Pali: cattāri pīṭhāni— masārakaṁ, bundikābaddhaṁ, kuḷīrapādakaṁ, āhaccapādakaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): there are four kinds of benches: one with legs and frame, called <i lang='pi' translate='no'>masāraka</i>; one with legs and frame, called <i lang='pi' translate='no'>bundikābaddha</i>; one with crooked legs; one with detachable legs. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.1.12
Pali: Bhisi nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A mattress: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.1.13
Pali: pañca bhisiyo— uṇṇabhisi, coḷabhisi, vākabhisi, tiṇabhisi, paṇṇabhisi. 
English (brahmali, published): there are five kinds of mattresses: a mattress stuffed with wool, a mattress stuffed with cloth, a mattress stuffed with bark, a mattress stuffed with grass, a mattress stuffed with leaves. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.1.14
Pali: Kocchaṁ nāma—
English (brahmali, published): A stool: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.1.15
Pali: vākamayaṁ vā usīramayaṁ vā muñjamayaṁ vā pabbajamayaṁ vā anto saṁveṭhetvā baddhaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): one made of bark, one made of vetiver grass, one made of reed. It is upholstered and then bound together. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.1.16
Pali: Santharitvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Puts it: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.1.17
Pali: sayaṁ santharitvā. 
English (brahmali, published): puts it oneself. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.1.18
Pali: Santharāpetvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Has it put: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.1.19
Pali: aññaṁ santharāpetvā. 
English (brahmali, published): gets another to put it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.1.20
Pali: Anupasampannaṁ santharāpeti, tassa palibodho. 
English (brahmali, published): If he gets one who is not fully ordained to put it, it is the responsibility of the monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.1.21
Pali: Upasampannaṁ santharāpeti, santhārakassa palibodho. 
English (brahmali, published): If he gets one who is fully ordained to put it, it is the responsibility of the one who puts it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.1.22
Pali: Taṁ pakkamanto neva uddhareyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Departs without putting it away: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.1.23
Pali: na sayaṁ uddhareyya. 
English (brahmali, published): he does not put it away himself. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.1.24
Pali: Na uddharāpeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Having it put away: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.1.25
Pali: na aññaṁ uddharāpeyya. 
English (brahmali, published): he does not get another to put it away. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.1.26
Pali: Anāpucchaṁ vā gaccheyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Or informing anyone: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.1.27
Pali: bhikkhuṁ vā sāmaṇeraṁ vā ārāmikaṁ vā anāpucchā majjhimassa purisassa leḍḍupātaṁ atikkamantassa āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not inform a monk, a novice monk, or a monastery worker, then when he goes beyond the distance of a stone’s throw of an average man, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.2.1
Pali: Saṅghike saṅghikasaññī ajjhokāse santharitvā vā santharāpetvā vā taṁ pakkamanto neva uddhareyya na uddharāpeyya anāpucchaṁ vā gaccheyya, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to the Sangha, and he perceives it as such, and he puts it out in the open or has it put out in the open, and he then departs without putting it away or having it put away or informing anyone, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.2.2
Pali: Saṅghike vematiko …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to the Sangha, but he is unsure of it … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.2.3
Pali: saṅghike puggalikasaññī ajjhokāse santharitvā vā santharāpetvā vā taṁ pakkamanto neva uddhareyya na uddharāpeyya, anāpucchaṁ vā gaccheyya, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to the Sangha, but he perceives it as belonging to an individual, and he puts it out in the open or has it put out in the open, and he then departs without putting it away or having it put away or informing anyone, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.2.4
Pali: Cimilikaṁ vā uttarattharaṇaṁ vā bhūmattharaṇaṁ vā taṭṭikaṁ vā cammakhaṇḍaṁ vā pādapuñchaniṁ vā phalakapīṭhaṁ vā ajjhokāse santharitvā vā santharāpetvā vā taṁ pakkamanto neva uddhareyya na uddharāpeyya, anāpucchaṁ vā gaccheyya, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a mat underlay, a bedspread, a floor cover, a straw mat, a hide, a foot-wiping cloth, or a plank bench, and he puts it out in the open or has it put out in the open, and he then departs without putting it away or having it put away or informing anyone, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.2.5
Pali: Puggalike saṅghikasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to an individual, but he perceives it as belonging to the Sangha, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.2.6
Pali: Puggalike vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to an individual, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.2.7
Pali: Puggalike puggalikasaññī aññassa puggalike, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to an individual, and he perceives it as such, but that individual is not himself, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.2.8
Pali: Attano puggalike anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to himself, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.3.2
Pali: uddharitvā gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he departs after putting it away; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.3.3
Pali: uddharāpetvā gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he departs after having it put away; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.3.4
Pali: āpucchaṁ gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he departs after informing someone; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.3.5
Pali: otāpento gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he departs while he is sunning it; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.3.6
Pali: kenaci palibuddhaṁ hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if the furniture is obstructed; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.3.7
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.3.8
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.3.9
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc14:2.3.10
Pali: Senāsanasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ catutthaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on leaving furniture outside, the fourth, is finished.

Pali: 15. Dutiyasenāsanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 15. Not putting away bedding 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sattarasavaggiyā bhikkhū sahāyakā honti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the monks from the group of seventeen were friends. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:1.3
Pali: Te vasantāpi ekatova vasanti, pakkamantāpi ekatova pakkamanti. 
English (brahmali, published): The lived together, and when going somewhere they would leave together. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:1.4
Pali: Te aññatarasmiṁ saṅghike vihāre seyyaṁ santharitvā taṁ pakkamantā neva uddhariṁsu na uddharāpesuṁ, anāpucchā pakkamiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion they put out bedding in a dwelling belonging to the Sangha, but then departed without putting it away, having it put away, or informing anyone. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:1.5
Pali: Senāsanaṁ upacikāhi khāyitaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): The furniture was eaten by termites. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:1.6
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:1.7
Pali: “kathañhi nāma sattarasavaggiyā bhikkhū saṅghike vihāre seyyaṁ santharitvā taṁ pakkamantā neva uddharissanti na uddharāpessanti, anāpucchā pakkamissanti, senāsanaṁ upacikāhi khāyitan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could those monks from the group of seventeen put out bedding in a dwelling belonging to the Sangha, and then depart without putting it away, getting it put away, or informing anyone? The furniture was eaten by termites.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:1.8
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū sattarasavaggiye bhikkhū anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking those monks in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:1.9
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, sattarasavaggiyā bhikkhū saṅghike vihāre seyyaṁ santharitvā taṁ pakkamantā neva uddhariṁsu na uddharāpesuṁ, anāpucchā pakkamiṁsu, senāsanaṁ upacikāhi khāyitan”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that those monks did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:1.10
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:1.11
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:1.12
Pali: kathañhi nāma te, bhikkhave, moghapurisā saṅghike vihāre seyyaṁ santharitvā taṁ pakkamantā neva uddharissanti na uddharāpessanti, anāpucchā pakkamissanti, senāsanaṁ upacikāhi khāyitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could those foolish men do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:1.13
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” …

English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this:

English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk puts out bedding in a dwelling belonging to the Sangha, or has it put out, and he then departs without putting it away, having it put away, or informing anyone, he commits an offense entailing confession.’”

English (brahmali, published): A dwelling belonging to the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.1.6

English (brahmali, published): given to the Sangha, given up to the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.1.7
Pali: Seyyaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Bedding: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.1.8
Pali: bhisi, cimilikā uttarattharaṇaṁ, bhūmattharaṇaṁ, taṭṭikā, cammakhaṇḍo, nisīdanaṁ, paccattharaṇaṁ, tiṇasanthāro, paṇṇasanthāro. 
English (brahmali, published): a mattress, a mat underlay, a bedspread, a floor cover, a straw mat, a hide, a sitting mat, a sheet, a spread of grass, a spread of leaves. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.1.9
Pali: Santharitvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Puts out: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.1.10
Pali: sayaṁ santharitvā. 
English (brahmali, published): puts out oneself.

English (brahmali, published): he does not put it away himself. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.1.15
Pali: Na uddharāpeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Having it put away: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.1.16

English (brahmali, published): he does not get another to put it away. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.1.17
Pali: Anāpucchaṁ vā gaccheyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Or informing anyone: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.1.18
Pali: bhikkhuṁ vā sāmaṇeraṁ vā ārāmikaṁ vā anāpucchā parikkhittassa ārāmassa parikkhepaṁ atikkamantassa āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not inform a monk, a novice monk, or a monastery worker, and he crosses the boundary of an enclosed monastery, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.1.19
Pali: Aparikkhittassa ārāmassa upacāraṁ atikkamantassa āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he goes beyond the vicinity of an unenclosed monastery, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.2.1
Pali: Saṅghike saṅghikasaññī seyyaṁ santharitvā vā santharāpetvā vā taṁ pakkamanto neva uddhareyya na uddharāpeyya, anāpucchaṁ vā gaccheyya, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to the Sangha, and he perceives it as such, and he puts out bedding there or has it put out, and he then departs without putting it away or having it put away or informing anyone, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.2.2
Pali: Saṅghike vematiko seyyaṁ santharitvā vā santharāpetvā vā taṁ pakkamanto neva uddhareyya na uddharāpeyya, anāpucchaṁ vā gaccheyya, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to the Sangha, but he is unsure of it, and he puts out bedding there or has it put out, and he then departs without putting it away or having it put away or informing anyone, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.2.3
Pali: Saṅghike puggalikasaññī seyyaṁ santharitvā vā santharāpetvā vā taṁ pakkamanto neva uddhareyya na uddharāpeyya, anāpucchaṁ vā gaccheyya, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to the Sangha, but he perceives it as belonging to an individual, and he puts out bedding there or has it put out, and he then departs without putting it away or having it put away or informing anyone, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.2.4
Pali: Vihārassa upacāre vā upaṭṭhānasālāyaṁ vā maṇḍape vā rukkhamūle vā seyyaṁ santharitvā vā santharāpetvā vā taṁ pakkamanto neva uddhareyya na uddharāpeyya, anāpucchaṁ vā gaccheyya, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he puts out bedding, or has it put out, in the vicinity of a dwelling, in the assembly hall, under a roof-cover, or at the foot of a tree, and he then departs without putting it away or having it put away or informing anyone, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.2.5
Pali: Mañcaṁ vā pīṭhaṁ vā vihāre vā vihārassūpacāre vā upaṭṭhānasālāyaṁ vā maṇḍape vā rukkhamūle vā santharitvā vā santharāpetvā vā taṁ pakkamanto neva uddhareyya na uddharāpeyya, anāpucchaṁ vā gaccheyya, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he puts out a bed or a bench, or has it put out, in a dwelling, in the vicinity of a dwelling, in the assembly hall, under a roof cover, or at the foot of a tree, and he then departs without putting it away or having it put away or informing anyone, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.2.6
Pali: Puggalike saṅghikasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to an individual, but he perceives it as belonging to the Sangha, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.2.7
Pali: Puggalike vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to an individual, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.2.8
Pali: Puggalike puggalikasaññī aññassa puggalike āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to an individual, and he perceives it as such, but that individual is not himself, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.2.9
Pali: Attano puggalike anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to himself, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.3.2
Pali: uddharitvā gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he departs after putting it away; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.3.3
Pali: uddharāpetvā gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he departs after having it put away; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.3.4
Pali: āpucchaṁ gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he departs after informing someone; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.3.5
Pali: kenaci palibuddhaṁ hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if the bedding is obstructed; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.3.6
Pali: sāpekkho gantvā tattha ṭhito āpucchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he abandons his intention to return, and at that spot informs anyone; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.3.7
Pali: kenaci palibuddho hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is obstructed; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.3.8
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.3.9
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.3.10
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc15:2.3.11
Pali: Dutiyasenāsanasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ pañcamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on not putting away bedding, the fifth, is finished.

English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on plants 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:0.5
Pali: 16. Anupakhajjasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 16. Encroaching on a monk 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū varaseyyāyo palibundhenti, therā bhikkhū vuṭṭhāpenti. 
English (brahmali, published): the monks from the group of six had taken possession of the best sleeping places. When the the senior monks evicted them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:1.3
Pali: Atha kho chabbaggiyānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ etadahosi—
English (brahmali, published): they thought, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:1.4
Pali: “kena nu kho mayaṁ upāyena idheva vassaṁ vaseyyāmā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How can we get to stay here during the rainy season?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:1.5
Pali: Atha kho chabbaggiyā bhikkhū there bhikkhū anupakhajja seyyaṁ kappenti—
English (brahmali, published): They then arranged their sleeping places so as to encroach on the senior monks, thinking, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:1.6
Pali: “yassa sambādho bhavissati so pakkamissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Whoever feels crowded will leave.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:1.7
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:1.8
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū there bhikkhū anupakhajja seyyaṁ kappessantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the monks from the group of six arrange their sleeping places so as to encroach on the senior monks?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:1.9
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū chabbaggiye bhikkhū anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking those monks in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:1.10
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, there bhikkhū anupakhajja seyyaṁ kappethā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:1.11
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:1.12
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:1.13
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, there bhikkhū anupakhajja seyyaṁ kappessatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:1.14
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:1.15
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:1.16.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu saṅghike vihāre jānaṁ pubbupagataṁ bhikkhuṁ anupakhajja seyyaṁ kappeyya— yassa sambādho bhavissati so pakkamissatī’ti, etadeva paccayaṁ karitvā anaññaṁ, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If, in a dwelling belonging to the Sangha, a monk arranges his sleeping place in a way that encroaches on a monk that he knows arrived there before him, with the intention that anyone who feels crowded will leave, and he does so only for this reason and no other, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): …The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.1.5
Pali: Saṅghiko nāma vihāro 
English (brahmali, published): A dwelling belonging to the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.1.6
Pali: saṅghassa dinno hoti pariccatto. 
English (brahmali, published): given to the Sangha, given up to the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.1.7
Pali: Jānāti nāma 
English (brahmali, published): He knows: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.1.8
Pali: vuḍḍhoti jānāti, gilānoti jānāti, saṅghena dinnoti jānāti. 
English (brahmali, published): he knows that he is senior, he knows that he is sick, he knows that it was given to him by the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.1.9
Pali: Anupakhajjāti 
English (brahmali, published): To encroach on: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.1.10
Pali: anupavisitvā. 
English (brahmali, published): enters after. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.1.11
Pali: Seyyaṁ kappeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Arranges his sleeping place: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.1.12
Pali: mañcassa vā pīṭhassa vā pavisantassa vā nikkhamantassa vā upacāre seyyaṁ santharati vā santharāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he puts out his sleeping place at the access to the bed, the bench, the entrance, or the exit, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.1.13
Pali: Abhinisīdati vā abhinipajjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he sits down or lies down on it, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.1.14
Pali: Etadeva paccayaṁ karitvā anaññanti 
English (brahmali, published): He does so only for this reason and no other: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.1.15
Pali: na añño koci paccayo hoti anupakhajja seyyaṁ kappetuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): there is no other reason for arranging his sleeping place in a way that encroaches. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.2.1
Pali: Saṅghike saṅghikasaññī anupakhajja seyyaṁ kappeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to the Sangha, and he perceives it as such, and he arranges his sleeping place there in a way that encroaches, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.2.2
Pali: Saṅghike vematiko anupakhajja seyyaṁ kappeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to the Sangha, but he is unsure of it, and he arranges his sleeping place there in a way that encroaches, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.2.3
Pali: Saṅghike puggalikasaññī anupakhajja seyyaṁ kappeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to the Sangha, but he perceives it as belonging to an individual, and he arranges his sleeping place there in a way that encroaches, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.2.4
Pali: Mañcassa vā pīṭhassa vā pavisantassa vā nikkhamantassa vā upacāraṁ ṭhapetvā seyyaṁ santharati vā santharāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he puts out his sleeping place, or has it put out, anywhere apart from the access to the bed, the bench, the entrance, or the exit, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.2.5
Pali: Abhinisīdati vā abhinipajjati vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he sits down or lies down on it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.2.6
Pali: Vihārassa upacāre vā upaṭṭhānasālāyaṁ vā maṇḍape vā rukkhamūle vā ajjhokāse vā seyyaṁ santharati vā santharāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he puts out his sleeping place, or has it put out, in the vicinity of a dwelling, in an assembly hall, under a roof cover, at the foot of a tree, or out in the open, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.2.7
Pali: Abhinisīdati vā abhinipajjati vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he sits down or lies down on it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.2.8
Pali: Puggalike saṅghikasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to an individual, but he perceives it as belonging to the Sangha, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.2.9
Pali: Puggalike vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to an individual, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.2.10
Pali: Puggalike puggalikasaññī aññassa puggalike āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to an individual, and he perceives it as such, but that individual is not himself, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.2.11
Pali: Attano puggalike anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to himself, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.3.2
Pali: gilāno pavisati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he enters because he is sick; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.3.3
Pali: sītena vā uṇhena vā pīḷito pavisati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he enters because he is feeling cold or hot; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.3.4
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.3.5
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.3.6
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc16:2.3.7
Pali: Anupakhajjasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ chaṭṭhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on encroaching on a monk, the sixth, is finished.

Pali: 17. Nikkaḍḍhanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 17. Evicting a monk 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sattarasavaggiyā bhikkhū aññataraṁ paccantimaṁ mahāvihāraṁ paṭisaṅkharonti—
English (brahmali, published): At that time the monks from the group of seventeen were repairing a large dwelling nearby, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.3
Pali: “idha mayaṁ vassaṁ vasissāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): intending to stay there for the rainy season. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.4
Pali: Addasaṁsu kho chabbaggiyā bhikkhū sattarasavaggiye bhikkhū vihāraṁ paṭisaṅkharonte. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks from the group of six saw this 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.5
Pali: Disvāna evamāhaṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.6
Pali: “ime, āvuso, sattarasavaggiyā bhikkhū vihāraṁ paṭisaṅkharonti. 
English (brahmali, published): “These monks from the group of seventeen are repairing a dwelling. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.7
Pali: Handa ne vuṭṭhāpessāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Let’s throw them out.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.8
Pali: Ekacce evamāhaṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): But some of them said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.9
Pali: “āgamethāvuso, yāva paṭisaṅkharonti; paṭisaṅkhate vuṭṭhāpessāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Let’s wait until they’ve finished repairing it.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.10
Pali: Atha kho chabbaggiyā bhikkhū sattarasavaggiye bhikkhū etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the monks from the group of six said to those from the group of seventeen, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.11
Pali: “uṭṭhethāvuso, amhākaṁ vihāro pāpuṇātī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Leave, this dwelling is ours.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.12
Pali: “Nanu, āvuso, paṭikacceva ācikkhitabbaṁ, mayañcaññaṁ paṭisaṅkhareyyāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Shouldn’t you have told us beforehand? We would’ve repaired another one.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.13
Pali: “Nanu, āvuso, saṅghiko vihāro”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Doesn’t this dwelling belong to the Sangha?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.14
Pali: “Āmāvuso, saṅghiko vihāro”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes, it does.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.15
Pali: “Uṭṭhethāvuso, amhākaṁ vihāro pāpuṇātī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Well then, leave! This dwelling is ours.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.16
Pali: “Mahallako, āvuso, vihāro. 
English (brahmali, published): “The dwelling is large. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.17
Pali: Tumhepi vasatha, mayampi vasissāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): You can stay here and so can we.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.18
Pali: “Uṭṭhethāvuso, amhākaṁ vihāro pāpuṇātī”ti kupitā anattamanā gīvāyaṁ gahetvā nikkaḍḍhanti. 
English (brahmali, published): But they said, “Leave, this dwelling is ours,” and they grabbed them by the necks and threw them out in anger. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.19
Pali: Te nikkaḍḍhīyamānā rodanti. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks from the group of seventeen cried. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.20
Pali: Bhikkhū evamāhaṁsu—“kissa tumhe, āvuso, rodathā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): When other monks asked them why, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.21
Pali: “Ime, āvuso, chabbaggiyā bhikkhū kupitā anattamanā amhe saṅghikā vihārā nikkaḍḍhantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): they told them what had happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.22
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.23
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū kupitā anattamanā bhikkhū saṅghikā vihārā nikkaḍḍhissantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the monks from the group of six angrily throw other monks out of a dwelling belonging to the Sangha?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.24
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū chabbaggiye bhikkhū anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking those monks in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.25
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, kupitā anattamanā bhikkhū saṅghikā vihārā nikkaḍḍhathā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.26
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.27
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.28
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, kupitā anattamanā bhikkhū saṅghikā vihārā nikkaḍḍhissatha? 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.29
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.30
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:1.31.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhuṁ kupito anattamano saṅghikā vihārā nikkaḍḍheyya vā nikkaḍḍhāpeyya vā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk, in anger, throws a monk out of a dwelling belonging to the Sangha, or has him thrown out, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): …The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.1.5
Pali: Bhikkhunti 
English (brahmali, published): A monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.1.6
Pali: aññaṁ bhikkhuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): another monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.1.7
Pali: Kupito anattamanoti 
English (brahmali, published): In anger: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.1.8
Pali: anabhiraddho āhatacitto khilajāto. 
English (brahmali, published): discontent, having hatred, hostile. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.1.9
Pali: Saṅghiko nāma vihāro 
English (brahmali, published): A dwelling belonging to the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.1.10
Pali: saṅghassa dinno hoti pariccatto. 
English (brahmali, published): given to the Sangha, given up to the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.1.11
Pali: Nikkaḍḍheyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Throws out: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.1.12
Pali: gabbhe gahetvā pamukhaṁ nikkaḍḍhati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he takes hold of him in a room and throws him out to the entryway, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.1.13
Pali: Pamukhe gahetvā bahi nikkaḍḍhati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he takes hold of him in the entryway and throws him outside, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.1.14
Pali: Ekena payogena bahukepi dvāre atikkāmeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): Even if he makes him go through many doors with a single effort, he commits one offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.1.15
Pali: Nikkaḍḍhāpeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Has thrown out: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.1.16
Pali: aññaṁ āṇāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he asks another, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.1.17
Pali: Sakiṁ āṇatto bahukepi dvāre atikkāmeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he only asks once, then even if the other makes him go through many doors, he commits one offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.2.1
Pali: Saṅghike saṅghikasaññī kupito anattamano nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to the Sangha, and he perceives it as such, and in anger he throws him out, or has him thrown out, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.2.2
Pali: Saṅghike vematiko kupito anattamano nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to the Sangha, but he is unsure of it, and in anger he throws him out, or has him thrown out, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.2.3
Pali: Saṅghike puggalikasaññī kupito anattamano nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to the Sangha, but he perceives it as belonging to an individual, and in anger he throws him out, or has him thrown out, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.2.4
Pali: Tassa parikkhāraṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he throws out one of his requisites, or he has it thrown out, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.2.5
Pali: Vihārassa upacārā vā upaṭṭhānasālāya vā maṇḍapā vā rukkhamūlā vā ajjhokāsā vā nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he throws him out, or has him thrown out, from the vicinity of a dwelling, from an assembly hall, from under a roof cover, from the foot of a tree, or from a space out in the open, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.2.6
Pali: Tassa parikkhāraṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he throws out one of his requisites from any of these places, or he has it thrown out, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.2.7
Pali: Anupasampannaṁ vihārā vā vihārassa upacārā vā upaṭṭhānasālāya vā maṇḍapā vā rukkhamūlā vā ajjhokāsā vā nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he throws out one who is not fully ordained, or has them thrown out, from a dwelling, from the vicinity of a dwelling, from an assembly hall, from under a roof cover, from the foot of a tree, or from a space out in the open, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.2.8
Pali: Tassa parikkhāraṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he throws out one of their requisites from any of these places, or has it thrown out, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.2.9
Pali: Puggalike saṅghikasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to an individual, but he perceives it as belonging to the Sangha, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.2.10
Pali: Puggalike vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to an individual, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.2.11
Pali: Puggalike puggalikasaññī aññassa puggalike āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to an individual, and he perceives it as such, but that individual is not himself, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.2.12
Pali: Attano puggalike anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to himself, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.3.2
Pali: alajjiṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he throws out one who is shameless, or has him thrown out; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.3.3
Pali: tassa parikkhāraṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he throws out one of the requisites belonging to that person, or has them thrown out; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.3.4
Pali: ummattakaṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he throws out one who is insane, or has him thrown out; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.3.5
Pali: tassa parikkhāraṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he throws out one of the requisites belonging to that person, or has them thrown out; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.3.6
Pali: bhaṇḍanakārakaṁ kalahakārakaṁ vivādakārakaṁ bhassakārakaṁ saṅghe adhikaraṇakārakaṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he throws out one who is quarrelsome and argumentative, who creates legal issues in the Sangha, or has them thrown out; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.3.7
Pali: tassa parikkhāraṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he throws out one of the requisites belonging to that person, or has them thrown out; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.3.8
Pali: antevāsikaṁ vā saddhivihārikaṁ vā na sammā vattantaṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he throws out a pupil or student who is not conducting himself properly, or has him thrown out; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.3.9
Pali: tassa parikkhāraṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he throws out one of the requisites belonging to such a person, or has them thrown out; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.3.10
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.3.11
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc17:2.3.12
Pali: Nikkaḍḍhanasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ sattamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on evicting a monk, the seventh, is finished.

Pali: 18. Vehāsakuṭisikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 18. Sitting down on an upper story 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena dve bhikkhū saṅghike vihāre uparivehāsakuṭiyā eko heṭṭhā viharati, eko upari. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time two monks were staying in a dwelling with an upper story belonging to the Sangha, one staying below and one above. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:1.3
Pali: Uparimo bhikkhu āhaccapādakaṁ mañcaṁ sahasā abhinisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): The monk above sat down hastily on a bed with detachable legs. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:1.4
Pali: Mañcapādo nippatitvā heṭṭhimassa bhikkhuno matthake avatthāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): A leg fell off and hit the monk below on the head. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:1.5
Pali: So bhikkhu vissaramakāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): He cried out. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:1.6
Pali: Bhikkhū upadhāvitvā taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): Monks rushed up 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:1.7
Pali: “kissa tvaṁ, āvuso, vissaramakāsī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): and asked him why, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:1.8
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): and he told them what had happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:1.9
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:1.10
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhu saṅghike vihāre uparivehāsakuṭiyā āhaccapādakaṁ mañcaṁ sahasā abhinisīdissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could a monk sit down hastily on a bed with detachable legs on an upper story in a dwelling belonging to the Sangha?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:1.11
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū taṁ bhikkhuṁ anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking that monk in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:1.12
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, bhikkhu, saṅghike vihāre uparivehāsakuṭiyā āhaccapādakaṁ mañcaṁ sahasā abhinisīdasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monk, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:1.13
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:1.14
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:1.15
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, saṅghike vihāre uparivehāsakuṭiyā āhaccapādakaṁ mañcaṁ sahasā abhinisīdissasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:1.16
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:1.17
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:1.18.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu saṅghike vihāre uparivehāsakuṭiyā āhaccapādakaṁ mañcaṁ vā pīṭhaṁ vā abhinisīdeyya vā abhinipajjeyya vā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk sits down or lies down on a bed or a bench with detachable legs on an upper story in a dwelling belonging to the Sangha, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): …The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.1.5
Pali: Saṅghiko nāma vihāro 
English (brahmali, published): A dwelling belonging to the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.1.6
Pali: saṅghassa dinno hoti pariccatto. 
English (brahmali, published): given to the Sangha, given up to the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.1.7
Pali: Vehāsakuṭi nāma 
English (brahmali, published): An upper story: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.1.8
Pali: majjhimassa purisassa asīsaghaṭṭā. 
English (brahmali, published): a man of average height does not hit his head. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.1.9
Pali: Āhaccapādako nāma mañco 
English (brahmali, published): A bed with detachable legs: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.1.10
Pali: aṅge vijjhitvā ṭhito hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): it stands after inserting the limbs. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.1.11
Pali: Āhaccapādakaṁ nāma pīṭhaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): A bench with detachable legs: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.1.12
Pali: aṅge vijjhitvā ṭhitaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): it stands after inserting the limbs. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.1.13
Pali: Abhinisīdeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Sits down: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.1.14
Pali: tasmiṁ abhinisīdati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he sits down on it, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.1.15
Pali: Abhinipajjeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Lies down: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.1.16
Pali: tasmiṁ abhinipajjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he lies down on it, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.2.1
Pali: Saṅghike saṅghikasaññī uparivehāsakuṭiyā āhaccapādakaṁ mañcaṁ vā pīṭhaṁ vā abhinisīdati vā abhinipajjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to the Sangha, and he perceives it as such, and he sits down or lies down on a bed or a bench with detachable legs on an upper story, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.2.2
Pali: Saṅghike vematiko …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to the Sangha, but he is unsure of it … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.2.3
Pali: saṅghike puggalikasaññī uparivehāsakuṭiyā āhaccapādakaṁ mañcaṁ vā pīṭhaṁ vā abhinisīdati vā abhinipajjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to the Sangha, but he perceives it as belonging to an individual, and he sits down or lies down on a bed or a bench with detachable legs on an upper story, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.2.4
Pali: Puggalike saṅghikasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to an individual, but he perceives it as belonging to the Sangha, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.2.5
Pali: Puggalike vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to an individual, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.2.6
Pali: Puggalike puggalikasaññī aññassa puggalike, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to an individual, and he perceives it as such, but that individual is not himself, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.2.7
Pali: Attano puggalike, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it belongs to himself, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.3.2
Pali: avehāsakuṭiyā 
English (brahmali, published): if there is no upper story; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.3.3
Pali: sīsaghaṭṭāya 
English (brahmali, published): if the upper story is so low that one hits the head; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.3.4
Pali: heṭṭhā aparibhogaṁ hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if the lower story is not in use; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.3.5
Pali: padarasañcitaṁ hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if the upper story has floorboards; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.3.6
Pali: paṭāṇi dinnā hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if the legs are fastened by bolts; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.3.7
Pali: tasmiṁ ṭhito gaṇhati vā laggeti vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he stands on it to get hold of something or to put something up; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.3.8
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.3.9
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc18:2.3.10
Pali: Vehāsakuṭisikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ aṭṭhamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on sitting down on an upper story, the eighth, is finished.

Pali: 19. Mahallakavihārasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 19. Applying excessive materials to a large dwelling 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā kosambiyaṁ viharati ghositārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Kosambī in Ghosita’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmato channassa upaṭṭhāko mahāmatto āyasmato channassa vihāraṁ kārāpeti. 
English (brahmali, published): a government official who was Venerable Channa’s supporter was making him a dwelling. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:1.3
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā channo katapariyositaṁ vihāraṁ punappunaṁ chādāpeti, punappunaṁ lepāpeti. 
English (brahmali, published): When the dwelling was finished, Channa had it roofed and plastered over and over. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:1.4
Pali: Atibhārito vihāro paripati. 
English (brahmali, published): Being overloaded, the dwelling collapsed. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:1.5
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā channo tiṇañca kaṭṭhañca saṅkaḍḍhanto aññatarassa brāhmaṇassa yavakhettaṁ dūsesi. 
English (brahmali, published): Then, while collecting grass and sticks, Channa spoiled the barley field belonging to a certain brahmin. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:1.6
Pali: Atha kho so brāhmaṇo ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—
English (brahmali, published): That brahmin complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:1.7
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhadantā amhākaṁ yavakhettaṁ dūsessantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the venerables spoil my barley field?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:1.8
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tassa brāhmaṇassa ujjhāyantassa khiyyantassa vipācentassa. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of that brahmin, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:1.9
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized Channa, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:1.10
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā channo katapariyositaṁ vihāraṁ punappunaṁ chādāpessati, punappunaṁ lepāpessati, atibhārito vihāro paripatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Channa have a finished dwelling roofed and plastered over and over until it collapsed from overloading?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:1.11
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ channaṁ anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking him in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned Channa: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:1.12
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, channa, katapariyositaṁ vihāraṁ punappunaṁ chādāpesi, punappunaṁ lepāpesi, atibhārito vihāro paripatī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Channa, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:1.13
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:1.14
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:1.15
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, katapariyositaṁ vihāraṁ punappunaṁ chādāpessasi, punappunaṁ lepāpessasi, atibhārito vihāro paripati. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:1.16
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:1.17
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:1.18.1
Pali: “Mahallakaṁ pana bhikkhunā vihāraṁ kārayamānena yāva dvārakosā aggaḷaṭṭhapanāya ālokasandhiparikammāya dvatticchadanassa pariyāyaṁ appaharite ṭhitena adhiṭṭhātabbaṁ. Tato ce uttari appaharitepi ṭhito adhiṭṭhaheyya pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘When a monk is building a large dwelling, then standing where there are no cultivated plants, he may apply two or three layers of roofing material, taking it as far as the doorcase and using it for fixing the door and for treating the window openings. If he applies more than that, even if he stands where there are no cultivated plants, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.1.1
Pali: Mahallako nāma vihāro 
English (brahmali, published): A large dwelling: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.1.2
Pali: sassāmiko vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): one with an owner is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.1.3
Pali: Vihāro nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A dwelling: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.1.4
Pali: ullitto vā hoti avalitto vā ullittāvalitto vā. 
English (brahmali, published): plastered inside or plastered outside or plastered both inside and outside. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.1.5
Pali: Kārayamānenāti 
English (brahmali, published): Is building: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.1.6
Pali: karonto vā kārāpento vā. 
English (brahmali, published): building it himself or having it built. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.1.7
Pali: Yāva dvārakosāti 
English (brahmali, published): As far as the doorcase: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.1.8
Pali: piṭṭhasaṅghāṭassa samantā hatthapāsā. 
English (brahmali, published): a distance of an arm’s reach next to the door frame. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.1.9
Pali: Aggaḷaṭṭhapanāyāti 
English (brahmali, published): For fixing the door: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.1.10
Pali: dvāraṭṭhapanāya. 
English (brahmali, published): for the fixing of the door. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.1.11
Pali: Ālokasandhiparikammāyāti 
English (brahmali, published): For treating the window openings: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.1.12
Pali: vātapānaparikammāya setavaṇṇaṁ kāḷavaṇṇaṁ gerukaparikammaṁ mālākammaṁ latākammaṁ makaradantakaṁ pañcapaṭikaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): for treating the window openings there is white color, black color, and treating with red ocher; and there is making a garland pattern, a creeper pattern, a shark-teeth pattern, and the fivefold pattern. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.1.13
Pali: Dvatticchadanassa pariyāyaṁ appaharite ṭhitena adhiṭṭhātabbanti—
English (brahmali, published): Standing where there are no cultivated plants, he may apply two or three layers of roofing material: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.1.14
Pali: haritaṁ nāma pubbaṇṇaṁ aparaṇṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): cultivated plants: grain and vegetables; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.1.15
Pali: Sace harite ṭhito adhiṭṭhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he applies it while standing where there are cultivated plants, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.1.16
Pali: Maggena chādentassa dve magge adhiṭṭhahitvā tatiyaṁ maggaṁ āṇāpetvā pakkamitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): For someone covering by the line, after covering with two layers, he may ask for a third layer, and he should then leave. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.1.17
Pali: Pariyāyena chādentassa dve pariyāye adhiṭṭhahitvā tatiyaṁ pariyāyaṁ āṇāpetvā pakkamitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): For someone covering by the layer, after covering with two layers, he may ask for a third layer, and he should then leave. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.1.18
Pali: Tato ce uttari appaharitepi ṭhito adhiṭṭhaheyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): If he applies more than that, even if he stands where there are no cultivated plants: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.1.19
Pali: iṭṭhakāya chādentassa iṭṭhakiṭṭhakāya āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is covering with bricks, then for every brick, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.1.20
Pali: Silāya chādentassa silāya silāya āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is covering with slate, then for every piece of slate, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.1.21
Pali: Sudhāya chādentassa piṇḍe piṇḍe āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is covering with plaster, then for every lump, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.1.22
Pali: Tiṇena chādentassa karaḷe karaḷe āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is covering with grass, then for every handful, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.1.23
Pali: Paṇṇena chādentassa paṇṇe paṇṇe āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is covering with leaves, then for every leaf, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.2.1
Pali: Atirekadvattipariyāye atirekasaññī adhiṭṭhāti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than two or three layers, and he perceives it as more, and he applies it, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.2.2
Pali: Atirekadvattipariyāye vematiko adhiṭṭhāti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than two or three layers, but he is unsure of it, and he applies it, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.2.3
Pali: Atirekadvattipariyāye ūnakasaññī adhiṭṭhāti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than two or three layers, but he perceives it as less, and he applies it, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.2.4
Pali: Ūnakadvattipariyāye atirekasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than two or three layers, but he perceives it as more, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.2.5
Pali: Ūnakadvattipariyāye vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than two or three layers, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.2.6
Pali: Ūnakadvattipariyāye ūnakasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than two or three layers, and he perceives it as less, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.3.2
Pali: dvattipariyāye, 
English (brahmali, published): if he applies two or three layers; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.3.3
Pali: ūnakadvattipariyāye, 
English (brahmali, published): if he applies less than two or three layers; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.3.4
Pali: leṇe, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a shelter; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.3.5
Pali: guhāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a cave; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.3.6
Pali: tiṇakuṭikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a grass hut; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.3.7
Pali: aññassatthāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is for the benefit of someone else; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.3.8
Pali: attano dhanena, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is by means of his own property; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.3.9
Pali: vāsāgāraṁ ṭhapetvā sabbattha anāpatti, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is anything apart from a dwelling; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.3.10
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.3.11
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc19:2.3.12
Pali: Mahallakavihārasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ navamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on applying excessive materials to a large dwelling, the ninth, is finished.

Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law
