Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:0.4
Pali: Bhūtagāmavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on plants 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:0.5
Pali: 20. Sappāṇakasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 20. Pouring water with living beings in it 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā āḷaviyaṁ viharati aggāḷave cetiye. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Āḷavī at the Aggāḷava Shrine, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āḷavakā bhikkhū navakammaṁ karontā jānaṁ sappāṇakaṁ udakaṁ tiṇampi mattikampi siñcantipi siñcāpentipi. 
English (brahmali, published): the monks there were doing building work. They poured water that they knew contained living beings onto grass and clay, and they had others do the same. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:1.3
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:1.4
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āḷavakā bhikkhū jānaṁ sappāṇakaṁ udakaṁ tiṇampi mattikampi siñcissantipi siñcāpessantipī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the monks at Āḷavī pour water that they know contains living beings onto grass and clay, and have others do the same?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:1.5
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū āḷavake bhikkhū anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking those monks in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:1.6
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, jānaṁ sappāṇakaṁ udakaṁ tiṇampi mattikampi siñcathapi siñcāpethapī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:1.7
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:1.8
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:1.9
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, jānaṁ sappāṇakaṁ udakaṁ tiṇampi mattikampi siñcissathapi siñcāpessathapi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:1.10
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:1.11
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:1.12.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu jānaṁ sappāṇakaṁ udakaṁ tiṇaṁ vā mattikaṁ vā siñceyya vā siñcāpeyya vā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk pours water that he knows contains living beings onto grass or clay, or has it poured, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): …The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.1.5
Pali: Jānāti nāma 
English (brahmali, published): He knows: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.1.6
Pali: sāmaṁ vā jānāti. Aññe vā tassa ārocenti. 
English (brahmali, published): he knows by himself or others have told him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.1.7
Pali: Siñceyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Pours: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.1.8
Pali: sayaṁ siñcati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he pours it himself, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.1.9
Pali: Siñcāpeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Has poured: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.1.10
Pali: aññaṁ āṇāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he asks another, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.1.11
Pali: Sakiṁ āṇatto bahukampi siñcati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he only asks once, then even if the other pours a lot, he commits one offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.2.1
Pali: Sappāṇake sappāṇakasaññī tiṇaṁ vā mattikaṁ vā siñcati vā siñcāpeti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it contains living beings, and he perceives it as such, and he pours it onto grass or clay, or he has it poured, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.2.2
Pali: Sappāṇake vematiko tiṇaṁ vā mattikaṁ vā siñcati vā siñcāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it contains living beings, but he is unsure of it, and he pours it onto grass or clay, or he has it poured, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.2.3
Pali: Sappāṇake appāṇakasaññī tiṇaṁ vā mattikaṁ vā siñcati vā siñcāpeti vā, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it contains living beings, but he does not perceive it as such, and he pours it onto grass or clay, or he has it poured, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.2.4
Pali: Appāṇake sappāṇakasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it does not contain living beings, but he perceives it as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.2.5
Pali: Appāṇake vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it does not contain living beings, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.2.6
Pali: Appāṇake appāṇakasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it does not contain living beings, and he does not perceive it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.3.2
Pali: asañcicca, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is unintentional; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.3.3
Pali: assatiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is not mindful; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.3.4
Pali: ajānantassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not know; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.3.5
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.3.6
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.3.7
Pali: Sappāṇakasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ dasamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on pouring water with living beings in it, the tenth, is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.3.8
Pali: Bhūtagāmavaggo dutiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): The second subchapter on plants is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.3.9
Pali: Tassuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.3.10
Pali: Bhūtaṁ aññāya ujjhāyaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): “Plant, with evasion, complaining, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.3.11
Pali: pakkamantena te duve; 
English (brahmali, published): The two with departing; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.3.12
Pali: Pubbe nikkaḍḍhanāhacca, 
English (brahmali, published): Before, throwing out, detachable, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc20:2.3.13
Pali: dvāraṁ sappāṇakena cāti. 
English (brahmali, published): Door, and containing living beings.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:0.4
Pali: Ovādavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on the instruction 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:0.5
Pali: 21. Ovādasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 21. Instructing nuns without approval 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena therā bhikkhū bhikkhuniyo ovadantā lābhino honti cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the senior monks who were instructing the nuns received robe-cloth, almsfood, dwellings, and medicinal supplies. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.3
Pali: Atha kho chabbaggiyānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ etadahosi—
English (brahmali, published): When the monks from the group of six found out about this, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.4
Pali: “etarahi kho, āvuso, therā bhikkhū bhikkhuniyo ovadantā lābhino honti cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārānaṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.5
Pali: Handāvuso, mayampi bhikkhuniyo ovadāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): they thought, “Well then, let’s instruct the nuns.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.6
Pali: Atha kho chabbaggiyā bhikkhū bhikkhuniyo upasaṅkamitvā etadavocuṁ—“amhepi, bhaginiyo, upasaṅkamatha; mayampi ovadissāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): They then went to the nuns and said, “Come to us, sisters, and we too will instruct you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.7
Pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo yena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards those nuns went to the monks from the group of six, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.8
Pali: upasaṅkamitvā chabbaggiye bhikkhū abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): bowed, and sat down. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.9
Pali: Atha kho chabbaggiyā bhikkhū bhikkhunīnaṁ parittaññeva dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā divasaṁ tiracchānakathāya vītināmetvā uyyojesuṁ—“gacchatha, bhaginiyo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): But after giving just a short teaching, those monks spent the day on worldly talk. They then dismissed the nuns, saying, “Go, sisters.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.10
Pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhaṁsu. Ekamantaṁ ṭhitā kho tā bhikkhuniyo bhagavā etadavoca—“kacci, bhikkhuniyo, ovādo iddho ahosī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns went to the Buddha and bowed, and the Buddha said to them, “Nuns, I hope the instruction was effective?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.11
Pali: “Kuto, bhante, ovādo iddho bhavissati. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, how could the instruction be effective? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.12
Pali: Ayyā chabbaggiyā parittaññeva dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā divasaṁ tiracchānakathāya vītināmetvā uyyojesun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): After giving just a short teaching, the monks from the group of six spent the day on worldly talk, and then dismissed us.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.13
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā tā bhikkhuniyo dhammiyā kathāya sandassesi samādapesi samuttejesi sampahaṁsesi. 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha instructed, inspired, and gladdened those nuns with a teaching. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.14
Pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo bhagavatā dhammiyā kathāya sandassitā samādapitā samuttejitā sampahaṁsitā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkamiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns bowed, circumambulated the Buddha with their right sides toward him, and left. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.15
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe bhikkhusaṅghaṁ sannipātāpetvā chabbaggiye bhikkhū paṭipucchi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks from the group of six: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.16
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, bhikkhunīnaṁ parittaññeva dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā divasaṁ tiracchānakathāya vītināmetvā uyyojethā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you acted like this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.17
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.18
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.19
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, bhikkhunīnaṁ parittaññeva dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā divasaṁ tiracchānakathāya vītināmetvā uyyojessatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how could you act like this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.20
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.21
Pali: vigarahitvā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.22
Pali: dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): he gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.23
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, bhikkhunovādakaṁ sammannituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, you should appoint an instructor of the nuns. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.24
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, sammannitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): And he should be appointed like this. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.25
Pali: Paṭhamaṁ bhikkhu yācitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): First a monk should be asked 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.26
Pali: Yācitvā byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena saṅgho ñāpetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): and then a competent and capable monk should inform the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.27
Pali: ‘Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.28
Pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuṁ bhikkhunovādakaṁ sammanneyya. 
English (brahmali, published): If the Sangha is ready, it should appoint monk so-and-so as an instructor of the nuns. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.29
Pali: Esā ñatti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the motion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.30
Pali: Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.31
Pali: Saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuṁ bhikkhunovādakaṁ sammannati. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha appoints monk so-and-so as an instructor of the nuns. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.32
Pali: Yassāyasmato khamati itthannāmassa bhikkhuno bhikkhunovādakassa sammuti, so tuṇhassa; 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who approves of appointing monk so-and-so as an instructor of the nuns should remain silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.33
Pali: yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.34
Pali: Dutiyampi etamatthaṁ vadāmi …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): For the second time, I speak on this matter. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.35
Pali: tatiyampi etamatthaṁ vadāmi—
English (brahmali, published): For the third time, I speak on this matter. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.36
Pali: suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.37
Pali: Saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuṁ bhikkhunovādakaṁ sammannati. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha appoints monk so-and-so as an instructor of the nuns. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.38
Pali: Yassāyasmato khamati itthannāmassa bhikkhuno bhikkhunovādakassa sammuti, so tuṇhassa; 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who approves of appointing monk so-and-so as an instructor of the nuns should remain silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.39
Pali: yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.40
Pali: Sammato saṅghena itthannāmo bhikkhu bhikkhunovādako. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha has appointed monk so-and-so as an instructor of the nuns. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.41
Pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.42
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā chabbaggiye bhikkhū anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā dubbharatāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): Then, after rebuking the monks from the group of six in many ways, the Buddha spoke in dispraise of being difficult to support … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.43
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.44.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu asammato bhikkhuniyo ovadeyya pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk who has not been appointed instructs the nuns, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:1.45
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena therā bhikkhū sammatā bhikkhuniyo ovadantā tatheva lābhino honti cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): After being appointed to do so, the senior monks who instructed the nuns still received robe-cloth, almsfood, dwellings, and medicinal supplies. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.2
Pali: Atha kho chabbaggiyānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ etadahosi—
English (brahmali, published): When they found out about this, the monks from the group of six said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.3
Pali: “etarahi kho, āvuso, therā bhikkhū sammatā bhikkhuniyo ovadantā tatheva lābhino honti cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārānaṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.4
Pali: Handāvuso, mayampi nissīmaṁ gantvā aññamaññaṁ bhikkhunovādakaṁ sammannitvā bhikkhuniyo ovadāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Well then, let’s go outside the monastery zone, appoint each other as instructors of the nuns, and then instruct them.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.5
Pali: Atha kho chabbaggiyā bhikkhū nissīmaṁ gantvā aññamaññaṁ bhikkhunovādakaṁ sammannitvā bhikkhuniyo upasaṅkamitvā etadavocuṁ—“mayampi, bhaginiyo, sammatā. 
English (brahmali, published): After doing just that, they again went to the nuns and said, “Sisters, we too have been appointed. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.6
Pali: Amhepi upasaṅkamatha. Mayampi ovadissāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Come to us, and we will instruct you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.7
Pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo yena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā chabbaggiye bhikkhū abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): Once again the nuns went to the monks from the group of six and everything unfolded as before. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.8
Pali: Atha kho chabbaggiyā bhikkhū bhikkhunīnaṁ parittaññeva dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā divasaṁ tiracchānakathāya vītināmetvā uyyojesuṁ—
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.9
Pali: “gacchatha, bhaginiyo”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.10
Pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.11
Pali: upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhaṁsu. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.12
Pali: Ekamantaṁ ṭhitā kho tā bhikkhuniyo bhagavā etadavoca—
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.13
Pali: “kacci, bhikkhuniyo, ovādo iddho ahosī”ti? 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.14
Pali: “Kuto, bhante, ovādo iddho bhavissati. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.15
Pali: Ayyā chabbaggiyā parittaññeva dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā divasaṁ tiracchānakathāya vītināmetvā uyyojesun”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.16
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā tā bhikkhuniyo dhammiyā kathāya sandassesi …pe… 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.17
Pali: atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo bhagavatā dhammiyā kathāya sandassitā samādapitā samuttejitā sampahaṁsitā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkamiṁsu. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.18
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe bhikkhusaṅghaṁ sannipātāpetvā chabbaggiye bhikkhū paṭipucchi—
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha then had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks from the group of six: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.19
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, bhikkhunīnaṁ parittaññeva dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā divasaṁ tiracchānakathāya vītināmetvā uyyojethā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you acted like this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.20
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.21
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.22
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, bhikkhunīnaṁ parittaññeva dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā divasaṁ tiracchānakathāya vītināmetvā uyyojessatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how could you act like this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.23
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.24
Pali: vigarahitvā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.25
Pali: dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): he gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.26
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, aṭṭhahaṅgehi samannāgataṁ bhikkhuṁ bhikkhunovādakaṁ sammannituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, you may appoint a monk who possesses eight qualities as an instructor of the nuns: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.27
Pali: Sīlavā hoti, pātimokkhasaṁvarasaṁvuto viharati ācāragocarasampanno aṇumattesu vajjesu bhayadassāvī, samādāya sikkhati sikkhāpadesu; 
English (brahmali, published): He is virtuous and restrained by the Monastic Code. His conduct is good, he associates with the right people, and he sees danger in minor faults. He undertakes and trains in the training rules. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.28
Pali: bahussuto hoti sutadharo sutasannicayo, ye te dhammā ādikalyāṇā majjhekalyāṇā pariyosānakalyāṇā sātthaṁ sabyañjanaṁ kevalaparipuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ abhivadanti tathārūpāssa dhammā bahussutā honti dhātā vacasā paricitā manasā anupekkhitā diṭṭhiyā suppaṭividdhā; 
English (brahmali, published): He has learned much, and he retains and accumulates what he has learned. Those teachings that are good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, that have a true goal and are well articulated, and that set out the perfectly complete and pure spiritual life—he has learned many such teachings, retained them in mind, recited them verbally, mentally investigated them, and penetrated them well by view. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.29
Pali: ubhayāni kho panassa pātimokkhāni vitthārena svāgatāni honti suvibhattāni suppavattīni suvinicchitāni suttaso anubyañjanaso; 
English (brahmali, published): He has properly learned both Monastic Codes in detail. He has analyzed them well, thoroughly mastered them, and investigated them well, both in terms of the rules and their detailed exposition. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.30
Pali: kalyāṇavāco hoti kalyāṇavākkaraṇo; 
English (brahmali, published): He is well-spoken and has a good voice. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.31
Pali: yebhuyyena bhikkhunīnaṁ piyo hoti manāpo; 
English (brahmali, published): He is generally liked by and pleasing to the nuns. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.32
Pali: paṭibalo hoti bhikkhuniyo ovadituṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): He is capable of instructing the nuns. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.33
Pali: na kho panetaṁ bhagavantaṁ uddissa pabbajitāya kāsāyavatthavasanāya garudhammaṁ ajjhāpannapubbo hoti; 
English (brahmali, published): He has never committed a heavy offense against a Buddhist nun. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.34
Pali: vīsativasso vā hoti atirekavīsativasso vā—
English (brahmali, published): He has been fully ordained for at least twenty years. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:2.35
Pali: anujānāmi, bhikkhave, imehi aṭṭhahaṅgehi samannāgataṁ bhikkhuṁ bhikkhunovādakaṁ sammannitun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Monks, you may appoint a monk who possesses these eight qualities as an instructor of the nuns.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.5
Pali: Asammato nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Who has not been appointed: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.6
Pali: ñatticatutthena kammena asammato. 
English (brahmali, published): who has not been appointed through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.7
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.8
Pali: ubhatosaṅghe upasampannā. 
English (brahmali, published): they have been given the full ordination by both Sanghas. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.9
Pali: Ovadeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Instructs: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.10
Pali: aṭṭhahi garudhammehi ovadati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he instructs in the eight important principles, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.11
Pali: Aññena dhammena ovadati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he instructs in any other teaching, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.12
Pali: Ekatoupasampannaṁ ovadati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he instructs a nun who is fully ordained only on one side, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.13
Pali: Tena sammatena bhikkhunā pariveṇaṁ sammajjitvā pānīyaṁ paribhojanīyaṁ upaṭṭhāpetvā āsanaṁ paññapetvā dutiyaṁ gahetvā nisīditabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monk who has been appointed should sweep the yard, set out water for drinking and water for washing, prepare a seat, get hold of a companion, and then sit down. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.14
Pali: Bhikkhunīhi tattha gantvā taṁ bhikkhuṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīditabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns should go there, bow down to the monk, and sit down. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.15
Pali: Tena bhikkhunā pucchitabbā—
English (brahmali, published): The monk should then ask them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.16
Pali: “samaggāttha, bhaginiyo”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Are you all here, sisters?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.17
Pali: Sace “samaggāmhāyyā”ti bhaṇanti, “vattanti, bhaginiyo, aṭṭha garudhammā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): If they say, “We’re all here, venerable,” he should say, “Are you keeping the eight important principles?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.18
Pali: Sace “vattantāyyā”ti bhaṇanti, “eso, bhaginiyo, ovādo”ti niyyādetabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): If they say, “We are,” he should say, “This is the instruction.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.19
Pali: Sace “na vattantāyyā”ti bhaṇanti, osāretabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): If they say, “We’re not,” he should recite the important principles: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.20
Pali: “Vassasatūpasampannāya bhikkhuniyā tadahupasampannassa bhikkhuno abhivādanaṁ paccuṭṭhānaṁ añjalikammaṁ sāmīcikammaṁ kātabbaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): “A nun who has been fully ordained for a hundred years should bow down to a monk who was given the full ordination on that very day, and she should stand up for him, raise her joined palms to him, and do acts of respect toward him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.21
Pali: ayampi dhammo sakkatvā garukatvā mānetvā pūjetvā yāvajīvaṁ anatikkamanīyo. 
English (brahmali, published): This principle is to be honored and respected all one’s life, and is not to be breached. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.22
Pali: Na bhikkhuniyā abhikkhuke āvāse vassaṁ vasitabbaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): A nun shouldn’t spend the rainy-season residence in a monastery without monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.23
Pali: ayampi dhammo sakkatvā garukatvā mānetvā pūjetvā yāvajīvaṁ anatikkamanīyo. 
English (brahmali, published): This principle too is to be honored and respected all one’s life, and is not to be breached. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.24
Pali: Anvaddhamāsaṁ bhikkhuniyā bhikkhusaṅghato dve dhammā paccāsīsitabbā 
English (brahmali, published): Every half-month a nun should seek two things from the Sangha of monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.25
Pali: uposathapucchakañca ovādūpasaṅkamanañca, 
English (brahmali, published): asking it about the observance day and going to it for the instruction. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.26
Pali: ayampi dhammo …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This principle too is to be honored and respected all one’s life, and is not to be breached. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.27
Pali: vassaṁ vuṭṭhāya bhikkhuniyā ubhatosaṅghe tīhi ṭhānehi pavāretabbaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): A nun who has completed the rainy-season residence should invite correction from both Sanghas in regard to three things: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.28
Pali: diṭṭhena vā sutena vā parisaṅkāya vā; 
English (brahmali, published): what has been seen, heard, or suspected. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.29
Pali: ayampi dhammo …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This principle too is to be honored and respected all one’s life, and is not to be breached. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.30
Pali: garudhammaṁ ajjhāpannāya bhikkhuniyā ubhatosaṅghe pakkhamānattaṁ caritabbaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): A nun who has committed a heavy offense must undertake a trial period for a half-month toward both Sanghas. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.31
Pali: ayampi dhammo …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This principle too is to be honored and respected all one’s life, and is not to be breached. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.32
Pali: dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhāya sikkhamānāya ubhatosaṅghe upasampadā pariyesitabbā; 
English (brahmali, published): A trainee nun who has trained for two years in the six rules may seek for full ordination in both Sanghas. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.33
Pali: ayampi dhammo …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This principle too is to be honored and respected all one’s life, and is not to be breached. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.34
Pali: na bhikkhuniyā kenaci pariyāyena bhikkhu akkositabbo paribhāsitabbo; 
English (brahmali, published): A nun may not in any way abuse or revile a monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.35
Pali: ayampi dhammo …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This principle too is to be honored and respected all one’s life, and is not to be breached. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.36
Pali: ajjatagge ovaṭo bhikkhunīnaṁ bhikkhūsu vacanapatho, anovaṭo bhikkhūnaṁ bhikkhunīsu vacanapatho; 
English (brahmali, published): From today onwards, nuns may not correct monks, but monks may correct nuns. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.37
Pali: ayampi dhammo sakkatvā garukatvā mānetvā pūjetvā yāvajīvaṁ anatikkamanīyo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): This principle too is to be honored and respected all one’s life, and is not to be breached.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.38
Pali: Sace “samaggāmhāyyā”ti bhaṇantaṁ aññaṁ dhammaṁ bhaṇati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If they say, “We’re all here, venerable,” and he instructs them with another teaching, then he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.39
Pali: Sace “vaggāmhāyyā”ti bhaṇantaṁ aṭṭha garudhamme bhaṇati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If they say, “We’re not all here, venerable,” and he instructs them in the eight important principles, then he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.1.40
Pali: Ovādaṁ aniyyādetvā aññaṁ dhammaṁ bhaṇati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not give the instruction, but he gives them another teaching, then he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.2.1
Pali: Adhammakamme adhammakammasaññī vaggaṁ bhikkhunisaṅghaṁ vaggasaññī ovadati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure and he perceives it as such, and the Sangha of nuns is incomplete and he perceives it as such, then if he instructs those nuns, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.2.2
Pali: Adhammakamme adhammakammasaññī vaggaṁ bhikkhunisaṅghaṁ vematiko ovadati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure and he perceives it as such, and the Sangha of nuns is incomplete but he is unsure of it, then if he instructs those nuns, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.2.3
Pali: Adhammakamme adhammakammasaññī vaggaṁ bhikkhunisaṅghaṁ samaggasaññī ovadati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure and he perceives it as such, and the Sangha of nuns is complete and he perceives it as such, then if he instructs those nuns, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.3.2
Pali: uddesaṁ dento, 
English (brahmali, published): if he recites; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.3.3
Pali: paripucchaṁ dento, 
English (brahmali, published): if he tests them; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.3.4
Pali: “osārehi ayyā”ti vuccamāno, osāreti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he recites when asked to do so; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.3.5
Pali: pañhaṁ pucchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he asks a question; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.3.6
Pali: pañhaṁ puṭṭho katheti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he replies when asked a question; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.3.7
Pali: aññassatthāya bhaṇantaṁ bhikkhuniyo suṇanti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is speaking for the benefit of someone else and the nuns listen in; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.3.8
Pali: sikkhamānāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is to a trainee nun; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.3.9
Pali: sāmaṇeriyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is to a novice nun; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.3.10
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.3.11
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc21:3.3.12
Pali: Ovādasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ paṭhamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on instructing nuns without approval, the first, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:0.5
Pali: 22. Atthaṅgatasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 22. Instructing nuns after sunset 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena therā bhikkhū bhikkhuniyo ovadanti pariyāyena. Tena kho pana samayena āyasmato cūḷapanthakassa pariyāyo hoti bhikkhuniyo ovadituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): the senior monks were taking turns instructing the nuns. Then, on one occasion, it was Venerable Cūḷapanthaka’s turn. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.3
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo evamāhaṁsu—“na dāni ajja ovādo iddho bhavissati, taññeva dāni udānaṁ ayyo cūḷapanthako punappunaṁ bhaṇissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns said, “Today the instruction won’t be effective. Venerable Cūḷapanthaka will just be repeating the same thing over and over.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.4
Pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo yenāyasmā cūḷapanthako tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ cūḷapanthakaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards those nuns went to Cūḷapanthaka, bowed, and sat down. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.5
Pali: Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho tā bhikkhuniyo āyasmā cūḷapanthako etadavoca—“samaggāttha, bhaginiyo”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): Cūḷapanthaka then said to them, “Are you all here, sisters?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.6
Pali: “Samaggāmhāyyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “We’re all here, venerable.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.7
Pali: “Vattanti, bhaginiyo, aṭṭha garudhammā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Are you keeping the eight important principles?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.8
Pali: “Vattantāyyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “We are.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.9
Pali: “Eso, bhaginiyo, ovādo”ti niyyādetvā imaṁ udānaṁ punappunaṁ abhāsi—
English (brahmali, published): After saying, “This is the instruction,” he uttered the same heartfelt exclamation again and again: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.10
Pali: “Adhicetaso appamajjato, 
English (brahmali, published): “For the heedful one possessed of the higher mind, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.11
Pali: Munino monapathesu sikkhato; 
English (brahmali, published): For the sage training on the path to sagacity—

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.12
Pali: Sokā na bhavanti tādino, 
English (brahmali, published): There are no sorrows for such a one, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.13
Pali: Upasantassa sadā satīmato”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The peaceful one, who is always mindful.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.14
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo evamāhaṁsu—“nanu avocumhā—na dāni ajja ovādo iddho bhavissati, taññeva dāni udānaṁ ayyo cūḷapanthako punappunaṁ bhaṇissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): And the nuns said, “Didn’t we say that the instruction wouldn’t be effective, that Venerable Cūḷapanthaka would just be repeating the same thing over and over?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.15
Pali: Assosi kho āyasmā cūḷapanthako tāsaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ imaṁ kathāsallāpaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Cūḷapanthaka overheard that conversation between the nuns. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.16
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā cūḷapanthako vehāsaṁ abbhuggantvā ākāse antalikkhe caṅkamatipi tiṭṭhatipi nisīdatipi seyyampi kappeti dhūmāyatipi pajjalatipi antaradhāyatipi, tañceva udānaṁ bhaṇati aññañca bahuṁ buddhavacanaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): He then rose up into the air, walked back and forth in space, and he stood, sat down, and lay down there. He emitted smoke and fire, and he disappeared, all the while uttering the same heartfelt exclamation and many other sayings by the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.17
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo evamāhaṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.18
Pali: “acchariyaṁ vata bho, abbhutaṁ vata bho. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s astonishing and amazing! 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.19
Pali: Na vata no ito pubbe ovādo evaṁ iddho bhūtapubbo yathā ayyassa cūḷapanthakassā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): No previous instruction has been as effective as this one from Venerable Cūḷapanthaka!” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.20
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā cūḷapanthako tā bhikkhuniyo yāva samandhakārā ovaditvā uyyojesi—“gacchatha, bhaginiyo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): And Cūḷapanthaka kept on instructing those nuns until the dark of night and then dismissed them, saying, “Go, sisters.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.21
Pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo nagaradvāre thakite bahinagare vasitvā kālasseva nagaraṁ pavisanti. 
English (brahmali, published): But because the gates to town were closed, those nuns spent the night outside and only entered early in the morning. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.22
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—“abrahmacāriniyo imā bhikkhuniyo; 
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized them, “These nuns aren’t celibate. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.23
Pali: ārāme bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ vasitvā idāni nagaraṁ pavisantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): They spend the night in the monastery with the monks and only now do they enter town.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.24
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.25
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized Cūḷapanthaka, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.26
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā cūḷapanthako atthaṅgate sūriye bhikkhuniyo ovadissatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Cūḷapanthaka instruct the nuns after the sun has set?”… 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.27
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, cūḷapanthaka, atthaṅgate sūriye bhikkhuniyo ovadasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Cūḷapanthaka, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.28
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.29
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.30
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, cūḷapanthaka, atthaṅgate sūriye bhikkhuniyo ovadissasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Cūḷapanthaka, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.31
Pali: Netaṁ, cūḷapanthaka, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.32
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:1.33.1
Pali: “Sammatopi ce bhikkhu atthaṅgate sūriye bhikkhuniyo ovadeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Even if he has been appointed, if a monk instructs the nuns after sunset, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:2.1.1
Pali: Sammato nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Has been appointed: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:2.1.2
Pali: ñatticatutthena kammena sammato. 
English (brahmali, published): has been appointed through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:2.1.3
Pali: Atthaṅgate sūriyeti 
English (brahmali, published): After sunset: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:2.1.4
Pali: oggate sūriye. 
English (brahmali, published): after the sun has gone down. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:2.1.5
Pali: Bhikkhunī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:2.1.6
Pali: ubhatosaṅghe upasampannā. 
English (brahmali, published): they have been given the full ordination by both Sanghas. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:2.1.7
Pali: Ovadeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Instructs: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:2.1.8
Pali: aṭṭhahi vā garudhammehi aññena vā dhammena ovadati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he instructs in the eight important principles or he gives any other teaching, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:2.2.1
Pali: Atthaṅgate atthaṅgatasaññī ovadati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the sun has set, and he perceives that it has, and he instructs the nuns, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:2.2.2
Pali: Atthaṅgate vematiko ovadati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the sun has set, but he is unsure of it, and he instructs the nuns, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:2.2.3
Pali: Atthaṅgate anatthaṅgatasaññī ovadati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the sun has set, but he perceives that it has not, and he instructs the nuns, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:2.2.4
Pali: Ekatoupasampannāya ovadati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he instructs a nun who is fully ordained only on one side, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:2.2.5
Pali: Anatthaṅgate atthaṅgatasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the sun has not set, but he perceives that it has, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:2.2.6
Pali: Anatthaṅgate vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the sun has not set, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:2.2.7
Pali: Anatthaṅgate anatthaṅgatasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If the sun has not set, and he perceives that it has not, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:2.3.2
Pali: uddesaṁ dento, 
English (brahmali, published): if he recites; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:2.3.3
Pali: paripucchaṁ dento, 
English (brahmali, published): if he tests them; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:2.3.4
Pali: “osārehi ayyā”ti vuccamāno, osāreti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he recites when asked to do so; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:2.3.5
Pali: pañhaṁ pucchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he asks a question; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:2.3.6
Pali: pañhaṁ puṭṭho katheti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he replies when asked a question; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:2.3.7
Pali: aññassatthāya bhaṇantaṁ bhikkhuniyo suṇanti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is speaking for the benefit of someone else and the nuns listen in; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:2.3.8
Pali: sikkhamānāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is to a trainee nun; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:2.3.9
Pali: sāmaṇeriyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is to a novice nun; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:2.3.10
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:2.3.11
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc22:2.3.12
Pali: Atthaṅgatasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ dutiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on instructing nuns after sunset, the second, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:0.5
Pali: 23. Bhikkhunupassayasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 23. Instructing nuns in a nunnery 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sakkesu viharati kapilavatthusmiṁ nigrodhārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying in the Sakyan country in the Banyan Tree Monastery at Kapilavatthu, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū bhikkhunupassayaṁ upasaṅkamitvā chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo ovadanti. 
English (brahmali, published): the monks from the group of six went to the nunnery and instructed the nuns from the group of six. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:1.3
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards, other nuns said to the nuns from the group of six, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:1.4
Pali: “ethāyye, ovādaṁ gamissāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Come, venerables, let’s go to the instruction.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:1.5
Pali: “Yampi mayaṁ, ayye, gaccheyyāma ovādassa kāraṇā, ayyā chabbaggiyā idheva āgantvā amhe ovadantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “There’s no need. The monks from the group of six came and instructed us right here.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:1.6
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns complained and criticized the monks from the group of six, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:1.7
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū bhikkhunupassayaṁ upasaṅkamitvā bhikkhuniyo ovadissantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the monks from the group of six go to the nunnery and instruct the nuns?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:1.8
Pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Then those nuns told the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:1.9
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:1.10
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū bhikkhunupassayaṁ upasaṅkamitvā bhikkhuniyo ovadissantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could those monks do this?”… 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:1.11
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, bhikkhunupassayaṁ upasaṅkamitvā bhikkhuniyo ovadathā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:1.12
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:1.13
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:1.14
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, bhikkhunupassayaṁ upasaṅkamitvā bhikkhuniyo ovadissatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:1.15
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:1.16
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:1.17.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhunupassayaṁ upasaṅkamitvā bhikkhuniyo ovadeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk goes to a nunnery and instructs the nuns, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:1.18
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena mahāpajāpati gotamī gilānā hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards Mahāpajāpati Gotamī became sick. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:2.2
Pali: Therā bhikkhū yena mahāpajāpati gotamī tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; 
English (brahmali, published): The senior monks went to see her 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:2.3
Pali: upasaṅkamitvā mahāpajāpatiṁ gotamiṁ etadavocuṁ—“ 
English (brahmali, published): and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:2.4
Pali: kacci, gotami, khamanīyaṁ, kacci yāpanīyan”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “We hope you’re bearing up, Gotamī, we hope you’re getting better?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:2.5
Pali: “Na me, ayyā, khamanīyaṁ na yāpanīyaṁ”. 
English (brahmali, published): “I’m not bearing up, venerables, and I’m not getting better. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:2.6
Pali: “Iṅghayyā, dhammaṁ desethā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Please give me a teaching.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:2.7
Pali: “Na, bhagini, kappati bhikkhunupassayaṁ upasaṅkamitvā bhikkhuniyo dhammaṁ desetun”ti kukkuccāyantā na desesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s not allowable for us to go a nunnery and teach the nuns.” And being afraid of wrongdoing, they did not teach her. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:2.8
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena mahāpajāpati gotamī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards, after robing up in the morning, the Buddha took his bowl and robe and went to Mahāpajāpati Gotamī where he sat down on the prepared seat. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:2.9
Pali: Nisajja kho bhagavā mahāpajāpatiṁ gotamiṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): He said to her, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:2.10
Pali: “kacci, gotami, khamanīyaṁ, kacci yāpanīyan”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “I hope you’re bearing up, Gotamī, I hope you’re getting better?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:2.11
Pali: “Pubbe me, bhante, therā bhikkhū āgantvā dhammaṁ desenti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Previously, sir, the senior monks would come and teach me, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:2.12
Pali: Tena me phāsu hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): and because of that I would be comfortable. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:2.13
Pali: Idāni pana—‘bhagavatā paṭikkhittan’ti, kukkuccāyantā na desenti. 
English (brahmali, published): But now that this has been prohibited by the Buddha, they don’t teach because they’re afraid of wrongdoing. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:2.14
Pali: Tena me na phāsu hotī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Because of that I’m not comfortable.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:2.15
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā mahāpajāpatiṁ gotamiṁ dhammiyā kathāya sandassetvā samādapetvā samuttejetvā sampahaṁsetvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): After instructing, inspiring, and gladdening her with a teaching, the Buddha got up from his seat and left. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:2.16
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:2.17
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, bhikkhunupassayaṁ upasaṅkamitvā gilānaṁ bhikkhuniṁ ovadituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to go a nunnery and instruct a sick nun. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:2.18
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:2.19.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhunupassayaṁ upasaṅkamitvā bhikkhuniyo ovadeyya, aññatra samayā, pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk goes to a nunnery and instructs the nuns, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:2.20
Pali: Tatthāyaṁ samayo. Gilānā hoti bhikkhunī—ayaṁ tattha samayo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the appropriate occasion: a nun is sick.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.1.5
Pali: Bhikkhunupassayo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A nunnery: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.1.6
Pali: yattha bhikkhuniyo ekarattampi vasanti. 
English (brahmali, published): wherever nuns stay, even for a single night. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.1.7
Pali: Upasaṅkamitvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Goes to: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.1.8
Pali: tattha gantvā. 
English (brahmali, published): goes there. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.1.9
Pali: Bhikkhunī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.1.10
Pali: ubhatosaṅghe upasampannā. 
English (brahmali, published): they have been given the full ordination by both Sanghas. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.1.11
Pali: Ovadeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Instructs: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.1.12
Pali: aṭṭhahi garudhammehi ovadati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he instructs about the eight important principles, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.1.13
Pali: Aññatra samayāti 
English (brahmali, published): Except on an appropriate occasion: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.1.14
Pali: ṭhapetvā samayaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): unless it is an appropriate occasion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.1.15
Pali: Gilānā nāma bhikkhunī 
English (brahmali, published): A sick nun: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.1.16
Pali: na sakkoti ovādāya vā saṁvāsāya vā gantuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): she is not able to go to the instruction or to a formal meeting of the community. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.2.1
Pali: Upasampannāya upasampannasaññī bhikkhunupassayaṁ upasaṅkamitvā aññatra samayā ovadati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is fully ordained, and he perceives her as such, and he goes to the nunnery and instructs her, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.2.2
Pali: Upasampannāya vematiko bhikkhunupassayaṁ upasaṅkamitvā aññatra samayā ovadati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is fully ordained, but he is unsure of it, and he goes to the nunnery and instructs her, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.2.3
Pali: Upasampannāya anupasampannasaññī bhikkhunupassayaṁ upasaṅkamitvā aññatra samayā ovadati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is fully ordained, but he does not perceive her as such, and he goes to the nunnery and instructs her, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.2.4
Pali: Aññena dhammena ovadati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he instructs her with another teaching, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.2.5
Pali: Ekatoupasampannāya ovadati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he instructs a nun who is fully ordained only on one side, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.2.6
Pali: Anupasampannāya upasampannasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is not fully ordained, but he perceives her as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.2.7
Pali: Anupasampannāya vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is not fully ordained, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.2.8
Pali: Anupasampannāya anupasampannasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is not fully ordained, and he does not perceive her as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.3.2
Pali: samaye, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is an appropriate occasion; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.3.3
Pali: uddesaṁ dento, 
English (brahmali, published): if he recites; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.3.4
Pali: paripucchaṁ dento, 
English (brahmali, published): if he tests them; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.3.5
Pali: “osārehi ayyā”ti vuccamāno osāreti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he recites when asked to do so; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.3.6
Pali: pañhaṁ pucchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he asks a question; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.3.7
Pali: pañhaṁ puṭṭho katheti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he replies when asked a question; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.3.8
Pali: aññassatthāya bhaṇantaṁ bhikkhuniyo suṇanti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is speaking for the benefit of someone else and the nuns listen in; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.3.9
Pali: sikkhamānāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is to a trainee nun; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.3.10
Pali: sāmaṇeriyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is to a novice nun; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.3.11
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.3.12
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc23:3.3.13
Pali: Bhikkhunupassayasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ tatiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on instructing nuns in a nunnery, the third, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:0.4
Pali: Ovādavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on the instruction 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:0.5
Pali: 24. Āmisasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 24. Accusing monks of teaching nuns for profit 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena therā bhikkhū bhikkhuniyo ovadantā lābhino honti cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the senior monks who were instructing the nuns received robe-cloth, almsfood, dwellings, and medicinal supplies. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:1.3
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhū evaṁ vadanti—
English (brahmali, published): And the monks from the group of six said this about them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:1.4
Pali: “na bahukatā therā bhikkhū bhikkhuniyo ovadituṁ; āmisahetu therā bhikkhū bhikkhuniyo ovadantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “The senior monks aren’t instructing the nuns as a service, but for the sake of worldly gain.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:1.5
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:1.6
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū evaṁ vakkhanti—
English (brahmali, published): “How can the monks from the group of six say that 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:1.7
Pali: ‘na bahukatā therā bhikkhū bhikkhuniyo ovadituṁ; āmisahetu therā bhikkhū bhikkhuniyo ovadantī’”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): the senior monks aren’t instructing the nuns to render a service, but for the sake of worldly gain?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:1.8
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, evaṁ vadetha—
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you say this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:1.9
Pali: ‘na bahukatā therā bhikkhū bhikkhuniyo ovadituṁ; āmisahetu therā bhikkhū bhikkhuniyo ovadantī’”ti? 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:1.10
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:1.11
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:1.12
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, evaṁ vakkhatha—
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how can you say this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:1.13
Pali: ‘na bahukatā therā bhikkhū bhikkhuniyo ovadituṁ; āmisahetu therā bhikkhū bhikkhuniyo ovadantī’ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:1.14
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:1.15
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:1.16.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu evaṁ vadeyya—‘āmisahetu therā bhikkhū bhikkhuniyo ovandantī’ti, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk says that the senior monks are instructing the nuns for the sake of worldly gain, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:2.1.5
Pali: Āmisahetūti 
English (brahmali, published): For the sake of worldly gain: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:2.1.6
Pali: cīvarahetu piṇḍapātahetu senāsanahetu gilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārahetu sakkārahetu garukārahetu mānanahetu vandanahetu pūjanahetu. 
English (brahmali, published): for the sake of robe-cloth, for the sake of almsfood, for the sake of a dwelling, for the sake of medicinal supplies, for the sake of honor, for the sake of respect, for the sake of deference, for the sake of veneration, for the sake of worship. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:2.1.7
Pali: Evaṁ vadeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Says: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:2.1.8
Pali: upasampannaṁ saṅghena sammataṁ bhikkhunovādakaṁ avaṇṇaṁ kattukāmo ayasaṁ kattukāmo maṅkukattukāmo evaṁ vadeti— “cīvarahetu piṇḍapātahetu senāsanahetu gilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārahetu sakkārahetu garukārahetu mānanahetu vandanahetu pūjanahetu ovadatī”ti bhaṇati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if, concerning one who is fully ordained and who has been appointed by the Sangha as an instructor of nuns—desiring to disparage him, desiring to give him a bad reputation, desiring to humiliate him—he says, “He instructs for the sake of robe-cloth,” “… for the sake of almsfood,” “… for the sake of a dwelling,” “… for the sake of medicinal supplies,” “… for the sake of honor,” “… for the sake of respect,” “… for the sake of deference,” “… for the sake of veneration,” “… for the sake of worship,” he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:2.2.1
Pali: Dhammakamme dhammakammasaññī evaṁ vadeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and he perceives it as such, and he says such a thing, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:2.2.2
Pali: Dhammakamme vematiko evaṁ vadeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but he is unsure of it, and he says such a thing, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:2.2.3
Pali: Dhammakamme adhammakammasaññī evaṁ vadeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but he perceives it as illegitimate, and he says such a thing, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:2.2.4
Pali: Upasampannaṁ saṅghena asammataṁ bhikkhunovādakaṁ avaṇṇaṁ kattukāmo ayasaṁ kattukāmo maṅkukattukāmo evaṁ vadeti—
English (brahmali, published): When someone who is fully ordained is an instructor of nuns, but he has not been appointed by the Sangha as such, and a monk—desiring to disparage him, desiring to give him a bad reputation, desiring to humiliate him—says, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:2.2.5
Pali: “cīvarahetu …pe… pūjanahetu ovadatī”ti bhaṇati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): “He instructs for the sake of robe-cloth,” “… for the sake of almsfood,” “… for the sake of a dwelling,” “… for the sake of medicines,” “… for the sake of honor,” “… for the sake of respect,” “… for the sake of deference,” “… for the sake of veneration,” “… for the sake of worship,” he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:2.2.6
Pali: Anupasampannaṁ saṅghena sammataṁ vā asammataṁ vā bhikkhunovādakaṁ avaṇṇaṁ kattukāmo ayasaṁ kattukāmo maṅkukattukāmo evaṁ vadeti—
English (brahmali, published): When someone who is not fully ordained is an instructor of nuns, whether or not he has been appointed by the Sangha as such, and a monk—desiring to disparage him, desiring to give him a bad reputation, desiring to humiliate him—says, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:2.2.7
Pali: “cīvarahetu piṇḍapātahetu senāsanahetu gilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārahetu sakkārahetu garukārahetu mānanahetu vandanahetu pūjanahetu ovadatī”ti bhaṇati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): “He instructs for the sake of robe-cloth,” “… for the sake of almsfood,” “… for the sake of a dwelling,” “… for the sake of medicinal supplies,” “… for the sake of honor,” “… for the sake of respect,” “… for the sake of deference,” “… for the sake of veneration,” “… for the sake of worship,” he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:2.2.8
Pali: Adhammakamme dhammakammasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but he perceives it as legitimate, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:2.2.9
Pali: Adhammakamme vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:2.2.10
Pali: Adhammakamme adhammakammasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and he perceives it as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:2.3.2
Pali: pakatiyā cīvarahetu piṇḍapātahetu senāsanahetu gilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārahetu sakkārahetu garukārahetu mānanahetu vandanahetu pūjanahetu ovadantaṁ bhaṇati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he says it to one who regularly gives the instruction for the sake of robe-cloth, … for the sake of almsfood, … for the sake of a dwelling, … for the sake of medicinal supplies, … for the sake of honor, … for the sake of respect, … for the sake of deference, … for the sake of veneration, … for the sake of worship; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:2.3.3
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:2.3.4
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc24:2.3.5
Pali: Āmisasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ catutthaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on accusing monks of teaching nuns for profit, the fourth, is finished.
