Root: root/pli/ms/vinaya/pli-tv-bu-vb/pli-tv-bu-vb-pc/pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25_root-pli-ms.json

English translation sources:
  - brahmali [published]: translation/en/brahmali/vinaya/pli-tv-bu-vb/pli-tv-bu-vb-pc/pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25_translation-en-brahmali.json

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:0.4
Pali: Ovādavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on the instruction 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:0.5
Pali: 25. Cīvaradānasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 25. Giving robe-cloth to a nun 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu sāvatthiyaṁ aññatarissā visikhāya piṇḍāya carati. Aññatarāpi bhikkhunī tassā visikhāya piṇḍāya carati. 
English (brahmali, published): a certain monk was walking for almsfood along a street in Sāvatthī, as was a certain nun. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:1.3
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu taṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavoca—“gaccha, bhagini, amukasmiṁ okāse bhikkhā diyyatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): That monk said to that nun, “Go to such-and-such a place, sister, and you’ll get alms,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:1.4
Pali: Sāpi kho evamāha—“gacchāyya, amukasmiṁ okāse bhikkhā diyyatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): and she said the same to him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:1.5
Pali: Te abhiṇhadassanena sandiṭṭhā ahesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): And because they met frequently, they became friends. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:1.6
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena saṅghassa cīvaraṁ bhājīyati. 
English (brahmali, published): Just then robe-cloth belonging to the Sangha was being distributed. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:1.7
Pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī ovādaṁ gantvā yena so bhikkhu tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ bhikkhuṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): Then, after going to the instruction, that nun went to that monk and bowed. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:1.8
Pali: Ekamantaṁ ṭhitaṁ kho taṁ bhikkhuniṁ so bhikkhu etadavoca—“ ayaṁ me, bhagini, cīvarapaṭivīso; sādiyissasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): He then said to her, “Sister, will you accept my share of the robe-cloth?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:1.9
Pali: “Āmāyya, dubbalacīvarāmhī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes, venerable, my robes are worn.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:1.10
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu tassā bhikkhuniyā cīvaraṁ adāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): And he gave his robe-cloth to that nun. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:1.11
Pali: Sopi kho bhikkhu dubbalacīvaro hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): As a consequence, his robes, too, became worn. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:1.12
Pali: Bhikkhū taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavocuṁ—“karohi dāni te, āvuso, cīvaran”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Other monks said to him, “Why don’t you make a robe for yourself?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:1.13
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): And he told them what had happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:1.14
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:1.15
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhu bhikkhuniyā cīvaraṁ dassatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could a monk give robe-cloth to a nun?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:1.16
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, bhikkhu, bhikkhuniyā cīvaraṁ adāsī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monk, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:1.17
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:1.18
Pali: “Ñātikā te, bhikkhu, aññātikā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is she a relative of yours?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:1.19
Pali: “Aññātikā, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “No.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:1.20
Pali: “Aññātako, moghapurisa, aññātikāya na jānāti patirūpaṁ vā appatirūpaṁ vā santaṁ vā asantaṁ vā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, a man and a woman who are unrelated don’t know what’s appropriate and inappropriate, what’s good and bad, in dealing with each other. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:1.21
Pali: Kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, aññātikāya bhikkhuniyā cīvaraṁ dassasi. 
English (brahmali, published): And still you did this. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:1.22
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:1.23
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:1.24.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu aññātikāya bhikkhuniyā cīvaraṁ dadeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk gives robe-cloth to an unrelated nun, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:1.25
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū kukkuccāyantā bhikkhunīnaṁ pārivattakaṁ cīvaraṁ na denti. 
English (brahmali, published): Once this had happened, the monks did not even give robe-cloth to the nuns in exchange, being afraid of wrongdoing. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:2.2
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:2.3
Pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā amhākaṁ pārivattakaṁ cīvaraṁ na dassantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can they not give us robe-cloth in exchange?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:2.4
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tāsaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ ujjhāyantīnaṁ khiyyantīnaṁ vipācentīnaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those nuns 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:2.5
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): and they told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:2.6
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:2.7
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, pañcannaṁ pārivattakaṁ dātuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to give things in exchange to five kinds of people: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:2.8
Pali: Bhikkhussa, bhikkhuniyā, sikkhamānāya, sāmaṇerassa, sāmaṇeriyā—
English (brahmali, published): monks, nuns, trainee nuns, novice monks, and novice nuns. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:2.9
Pali: anujānāmi, bhikkhave, imesaṁ pañcannaṁ pārivattakaṁ dātuṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:2.10
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:2.11.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu aññātikāya bhikkhuniyā cīvaraṁ dadeyya, aññatra pārivattakā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk gives robe-cloth to an unrelated nun, except in exchange, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.1.5
Pali: Aññātikā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Unrelated: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.1.6
Pali: mātito vā pitito vā yāva sattamā pitāmahayugā asambaddhā. 
English (brahmali, published): anyone who is not a descendant of one’s male ancestors going back eight generations, either on the mother’s side or on the father’s side. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.1.7
Pali: Bhikkhunī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A nun: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.1.8
Pali: ubhatosaṅghe upasampannā. 
English (brahmali, published): she has been given the full ordination by both Sanghas. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.1.9
Pali: Cīvaraṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Robe-cloth: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.1.10
Pali: channaṁ cīvarānaṁ aññataraṁ cīvaraṁ vikappanupagaṁ pacchimaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one of the six kinds of robe-cloth, but not smaller than what can be assigned to another. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.1.11
Pali: Aññatra pārivattakāti 
English (brahmali, published): Except in exchange: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.1.12
Pali: ṭhapetvā pārivattakaṁ deti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): unless there is an exchange. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.2.1
Pali: Aññātikāya aññātikasaññī cīvaraṁ deti, aññatra pārivattakā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated and he perceives her as such, and he gives her robe-cloth, except in exchange, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.2.2
Pali: Aññātikāya vematiko cīvaraṁ deti, aññatra pārivattakā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated, but he is unsure of it, and he gives her robe-cloth, except in exchange, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.2.3
Pali: Aññātikāya ñātikasaññī cīvaraṁ deti, aññatra pārivattakā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated, but he perceives her as related, and he gives her robe-cloth, except in exchange, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.2.4
Pali: Ekato upasampannāya cīvaraṁ deti, aññatra pārivattakā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he gives robe-cloth to a nun who is fully ordained only on one side, except in exchange, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.2.5
Pali: Ñātikāya aññātikasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is related, but he perceives her as unrelated, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.2.6
Pali: Ñātikāya vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is related, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.2.7
Pali: Ñātikāya ñātikasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is related and he perceives her as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.3.2
Pali: ñātikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is related; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.3.3
Pali: pārivattakaṁ parittena vā vipulaṁ, vipulena vā parittaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if much is exchanged with little or little is exchanged with much; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.3.4
Pali: bhikkhunī vissāsaṁ gaṇhāti, 
English (brahmali, published): if the nun takes it on trust; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.3.5
Pali: tāvakālikaṁ gaṇhāti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she borrows it; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.3.6
Pali: cīvaraṁ ṭhapetvā aññaṁ parikkhāraṁ deti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he gives any requisite apart from robe-cloth; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.3.7
Pali: sikkhamānāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a trainee nun; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.3.8
Pali: sāmaṇeriyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a novice nun; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.3.9
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.3.10
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc25:3.3.11
Pali: Cīvaradānasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ pañcamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on giving robe-cloth to a nun, the fifth, is finished.

Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis

Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:0.4
Pali: Ovādavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on the instruction 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:0.5
Pali: 26. Cīvarasibbanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 26. Sewing a robe for a nun 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā udāyī paṭṭo hoti cīvarakammaṁ kātuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time Venerable Udāyī had become skilled in making robes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:1.3
Pali: Aññatarā bhikkhunī yenāyasmā udāyī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion a certain nun went to Udāyī and said to him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:1.4
Pali: “sādhu me, bhante, ayyo cīvaraṁ sibbatū”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, would you please sew me a robe?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:1.5
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā udāyī tassā bhikkhuniyā cīvaraṁ sibbitvā surattaṁ suparikammakataṁ katvā majjhe paṭibhānacittaṁ vuṭṭhāpetvā saṁharitvā nikkhipi. 
English (brahmali, published): He then sewed a robe for that nun, well-dyed and beautifully made, and he drew a picture in the middle of it. He then folded it and put it aside. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:1.6
Pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī yenāyasmā udāyī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards that nun went to Udāyī and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:1.7
Pali: “kahaṁ taṁ, bhante, cīvaran”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, where’s the robe?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:1.8
Pali: “Handa, bhagini, imaṁ cīvaraṁ yathāsaṁhaṭaṁ haritvā nikkhipitvā yadā bhikkhunisaṅgho ovādaṁ āgacchati tadā imaṁ cīvaraṁ pārupitvā bhikkhunisaṅghassa piṭṭhito piṭṭhito āgacchā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Now, sister, take this robe as it’s folded and put it aside. When the Sangha of nuns goes to the instruction, then put it on and follow right behind the other nuns.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:1.9
Pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī taṁ cīvaraṁ yathāsaṁhaṭaṁ haritvā nikkhipitvā yadā bhikkhunisaṅgho ovādaṁ āgacchati tadā taṁ cīvaraṁ pārupitvā bhikkhunisaṅghassa piṭṭhito piṭṭhito āgacchati. 
English (brahmali, published): And that nun did just that. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:1.10
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:1.11
Pali: “yāva chinnikā imā bhikkhuniyo dhuttikā ahirikāyo, yatra hi nāma cīvare paṭibhānacittaṁ vuṭṭhāpessantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How indecent these nuns are, what shameless scoundrels, in drawing pictures on their robes!” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:1.12
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo evamāhaṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns asked her, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:1.13
Pali: “kassidaṁ kamman”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Who did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:1.14
Pali: “Ayyassa udāyissā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable Udāyī.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:1.15
Pali: “Yepi te chinnakā dhuttakā ahirikā tesampi evarūpaṁ na sobheyya, kiṁ pana ayyassa udāyissā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “This sort of work would not even make an indecent, shameless scoundrel look good, let alone Venerable Udāyī.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:1.16
Pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns told the monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:1.17
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:1.18
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā udāyī bhikkhuniyā cīvaraṁ sibbissatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Udāyī sew a robe for a nun?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:1.19
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, udāyi, bhikkhuniyā cīvaraṁ sibbasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Udāyī, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:1.20
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:1.21
Pali: “Ñātikā te, udāyi, aññātikā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is she a relative of yours?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:1.22
Pali: “Aññātikā, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “No.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:1.23
Pali: “Aññātako, moghapurisa, aññātikāya na jānāti patirūpaṁ vā appatirūpaṁ vā pāsādikaṁ vā apāsādikaṁ vā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, a man and a woman who are unrelated don’t know what’s appropriate and inappropriate, what’s inspiring and uninspiring, in dealing with each other. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:1.24
Pali: Kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, aññātikāya bhikkhuniyā cīvaraṁ sibbissasi. 
English (brahmali, published): And still you did this. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:1.25
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:1.26
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:1.27.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu aññātikāya bhikkhuniyā cīvaraṁ sibbeyya vā sibbāpeyya vā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk sews a robe for an unrelated nun, or has one sewn, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.1.5
Pali: Aññātikā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Unrelated: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.1.6
Pali: mātito vā pitito vā yāva sattamā pitāmahayugā asambaddhā. 
English (brahmali, published): anyone who is not a descendant of one’s male ancestors going back eight generations, either on the mother’s side or on the father’s side. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.1.7
Pali: Bhikkhunī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A nun: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.1.8
Pali: ubhatosaṅghe upasampannā. 
English (brahmali, published): she has been given the full ordination by both Sanghas. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.1.9
Pali: Cīvaraṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A robe: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.1.10
Pali: channaṁ cīvarānaṁ aññataraṁ cīvaraṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one of the six kinds of robes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.1.11
Pali: Sibbeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Sews: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.1.12
Pali: sayaṁ sibbati ārāpathe ārāpathe āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he sews it himself, then for each stitch he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.1.13
Pali: Sibbāpeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Has sewn: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.1.14
Pali: aññaṁ āṇāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he asks another, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.1.15
Pali: Sakiṁ āṇatto bahukampi sibbati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he only asks once, then even if the other sews a lot, he commits one offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.2.1
Pali: Aññātikāya aññātikasaññī cīvaraṁ sibbati vā sibbāpeti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated and he perceives her as such, and he sews her a robe or has one sewn, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.2.2
Pali: Aññātikāya vematiko cīvaraṁ sibbati vā sibbāpeti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated, but he is unsure of it, and he sews her a robe or has one sewn, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.2.3
Pali: Aññātikāya ñātikasaññī cīvaraṁ sibbati vā sibbāpeti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is unrelated, but he perceives her as related, and he sews her a robe or has one sewn, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.2.4
Pali: Ekatoupasampannāya cīvaraṁ sibbati vā sibbāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he sews a robe, or has one sewn, for a nun who is fully ordained only on one side, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.2.5
Pali: Ñātikāya aññātikasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is related, but he perceives her as unrelated, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.2.6
Pali: Ñātikāya vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is related, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.2.7
Pali: Ñātikāya ñātikasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is related and he perceives her as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.3.2
Pali: ñātikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is related; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.3.3
Pali: cīvaraṁ ṭhapetvā aññaṁ parikkhāraṁ sibbati vā sibbāpeti vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he sews any requisite apart from a robe, or has it sewn; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.3.4
Pali: sikkhamānāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a trainee nun; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.3.5
Pali: sāmaṇeriyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a novice nun; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.3.6
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.3.7
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc26:2.3.8
Pali: Cīvarasibbanasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ chaṭṭhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on sewing a robe for a nun, the sixth, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:0.4
Pali: Ovādavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on the instruction 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:0.5
Pali: 27. Saṁvidhānasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 27. Traveling with a nun 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū bhikkhunīhi saddhiṁ saṁvidhāya ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjanti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the monks from the group of six traveled by arrangement with nuns. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:1.3
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:1.4
Pali: “yatheva mayaṁ sapajāpatikā āhiṇḍāma, evamevime samaṇā sakyaputtiyā bhikkhunīhi saddhiṁ saṁvidhāya āhiṇḍantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Just as we walk about with our wives, so these Sakyan monastics walk about by arrangement with nuns.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:1.5
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:1.6
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:1.7
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū bhikkhunīhi saddhiṁ saṁvidhāya ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjissantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the monks from the group of six travel by arrangement with the nuns?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:1.8
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, bhikkhunīhi saddhiṁ saṁvidhāya ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjathā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:1.9
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:1.10
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:1.11
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, bhikkhunīhi saddhiṁ saṁvidhāya ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjissatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:1.12
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:1.13
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:1.14.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhuniyā saddhiṁ saṁvidhāya ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjeyya, antamaso gāmantarampi, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk travels by arrangement with a nun, even just to the next inhabited area, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:1.15
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā bhikkhū ca bhikkhuniyo ca sāketā sāvatthiṁ addhānamaggappaṭipannā honti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards a number of monks and nuns were traveling from Sāketa to Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:2.2
Pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo te bhikkhū etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns said to the monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:2.3
Pali: “mayampi ayyehi saddhiṁ gamissāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Let’s go together.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:2.4
Pali: “Na, bhaginī, kappati bhikkhuniyā saddhiṁ saṁvidhāya ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sisters, it’s not allowable for us to travel by arrangement with nuns. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:2.5
Pali: Tumhe vā paṭhamaṁ gacchatha mayaṁ vā gamissāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Either you go first, or we will.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:2.6
Pali: “Ayyā, bhante, aggapurisā. 
English (brahmali, published): “You have the higher status, venerables. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:2.7
Pali: Ayyāva paṭhamaṁ gacchantū”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Please go first.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:2.8
Pali: Atha kho tāsaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ pacchā gacchantīnaṁ antarāmagge corā acchindiṁsu ca dūsesuñca. 
English (brahmali, published): But because the nuns went behind, they were robbed and raped by bandits. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:2.9
Pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo sāvatthiṁ gantvā bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): When they arrived at Sāvatthī, they told the nuns there what had happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:2.10
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns then told the monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:2.11
Pali: Bhikkhū bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): who in turn told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:2.12
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:2.13
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, satthagamanīye magge sāsaṅkasammate sappaṭibhaye bhikkhuniyā saddhiṁ saṁvidhāya ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to travel by arrangement with a nun if it’s a risky and dangerous road that should be traveled with a group. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:2.14
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:2.15.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhuniyā saddhiṁ saṁvidhāya ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjeyya, antamaso gāmantarampi, aññatra samayā, pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk travels by arrangement with a nun, even just to the next inhabited area, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:2.16
Pali: Tatthāyaṁ samayo. Satthagamanīyo hoti maggo sāsaṅkasammato sappaṭibhayo—ayaṁ tattha samayo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the appropriate occasion: the road is considered risky and dangerous and should be traveled with a group.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.1.5
Pali: Bhikkhunī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A nun: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.1.6
Pali: ubhatosaṅghe upasampannā. 
English (brahmali, published): she has been given the full ordination by both Sanghas. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.1.7
Pali: Saddhinti 
English (brahmali, published): With: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.1.8
Pali: ekato. 
English (brahmali, published): together. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.1.9
Pali: Saṁvidhāyāti—
English (brahmali, published): By arrangement: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.1.10
Pali: “gacchāma, bhagini, gacchāmāyya; gacchāmāyya, gacchāma, bhagini; ajja vā hiyyo vā pare vā gacchāmā”ti saṁvidahati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes an arrangement like this: he says, “Let’s go, sister,” and she replies, “Yes, let’s go, venerable;” or she says, “Let’s go, venerable,” and he replies, “Yes, let’s go, sister;” or he says, “Let’s go today,” “Let’s go tomorrow,” “Let’s go the day after tomorrow,” then he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.1.11
Pali: Antamaso gāmantarampīti 
English (brahmali, published): Even just to the next inhabited area: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.1.12
Pali: kukkuṭasampāte gāme, gāmantare gāmantare āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when the inhabited areas are a chicken’s flight apart, then for every next inhabited area he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.1.13
Pali: Agāmake araññe, addhayojane addhayojane āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When it is an uninhabited area, a wilderness, then for every six kilometers he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.1.14
Pali: Aññatra samayāti 
English (brahmali, published): Except on an appropriate occasion: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.1.15
Pali: ṭhapetvā samayaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): unless it is an appropriate occasion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.1.16
Pali: Satthagamanīyo nāma maggo 
English (brahmali, published): The road should be traveled with a group: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.1.17
Pali: na sakkā hoti vinā satthena gantuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): it is not possible to travel without a group. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.1.18
Pali: Sāsaṅkaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Risky: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.1.19
Pali: tasmiṁ magge corānaṁ niviṭṭhokāso dissati, bhuttokāso dissati, ṭhitokāso dissati, nisinnokāso dissati, nipannokāso dissati. 
English (brahmali, published): a place has been seen along that road where criminals are camping, eating, standing, sitting, or lying down. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.1.20
Pali: Sappaṭibhayaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Dangerous: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.1.21
Pali: tasmiṁ magge corehi manussā hatā dissanti, viluttā dissanti, ākoṭitā dissanti, sappaṭibhayaṁ gantvā appaṭibhayaṁ dassetvā uyyojetabbā— “gacchatha, bhaginiyo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): criminals have been seen along that road, injuring, robbing, or beating people. If they go together thinking it is dangerous, but then see that it is not, the nuns are to be dismissed, “Go, sisters.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.2.1
Pali: Saṁvidahite saṁvidahitasaññī ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjati, antamaso gāmantarampi, aññatra samayā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If there is an arrangement, and he perceives that there is, and he travels with a nun, even just to the next inhabited area, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.2.2
Pali: Saṁvidahite vematiko ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjati, antamaso gāmantarampi, aññatra samayā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If there is an arrangement, but he is unsure of it, and he travels with a nun, even just to the next inhabited area, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.2.3
Pali: Saṁvidahite, asaṁvidahitasaññī ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjati, antamaso gāmantarampi, aññatra samayā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If there is an arrangement, but he does not perceive that there is, and he travels with a nun, even just to the next inhabited area, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.2.4
Pali: Bhikkhu saṁvidahati bhikkhunī na saṁvidahati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the monk makes an arrangement, but the nun does not express her agreement, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.2.5
Pali: Asaṁvidahite saṁvidahitasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If there is no arrangement, but he perceives that there is, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.2.6
Pali: Asaṁvidahite vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If there is no arrangement, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.2.7
Pali: Asaṁvidahite asaṁvidahitasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If there is no arrangement, and he does not perceive that there is, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.3.2
Pali: samaye, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is an appropriate occasion; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.3.3
Pali: asaṁvidahitvā gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he goes without an arrangement; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.3.4
Pali: bhikkhunī saṁvidahati, bhikkhu na saṁvidahati, 
English (brahmali, published): if the nun has made an arrangement, but he has not expressed his agreement; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.3.5
Pali: visaṅketena gacchanti, 
English (brahmali, published): if they go, but not according to the arrangement; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.3.6
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.3.7
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.3.8
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc27:3.3.9
Pali: Saṁvidhānasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ sattamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on traveling with a nun, the seventh, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:1.14.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhuniyā saddhiṁ saṁvidhāya ekaṁ nāvaṁ abhiruheyya, uddhaṅgāminiṁ vā adhogāminiṁ vā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk boards a boat by arrangement with a nun, either to go upstream or downstream, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:1.15
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā bhikkhū ca bhikkhuniyo ca sāketā sāvatthiṁ addhānamaggappaṭipannā honti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards a number of monks and nuns were traveling from Sāketa to Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:2.2
Pali: Antarāmagge nadī taritabbā hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): On the way they needed to cross a river. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:2.3
Pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo te bhikkhū etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns said to the monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:2.4
Pali: “mayampi ayyehi saddhiṁ uttarissāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Let’s cross together.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:2.5
Pali: “Na, bhaginī, kappati bhikkhuniyā saddhiṁ saṁvidhāya ekaṁ nāvaṁ abhiruhituṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): “Sisters, it’s not allowable for us to board a boat by arrangement with a nun. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:2.6
Pali: tumhe vā paṭhamaṁ uttaratha mayaṁ vā uttarissāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Either you cross first, or we will.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:2.7
Pali: “Ayyā, bhante, aggapurisā. 
English (brahmali, published): “You have the higher status, venerables. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:2.8
Pali: Ayyāva paṭhamaṁ uttarantū”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Please go first.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:2.9
Pali: Atha kho tāsaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ pacchā uttarantīnaṁ corā acchindiṁsu ca dūsesuñca. 
English (brahmali, published): But because the nuns crossed afterwards, they were robbed and raped by bandits. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:2.10
Pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo sāvatthiṁ gantvā bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): When they arrived at Sāvatthī they told the nuns there what had happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:2.11
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns then told the monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:2.12
Pali: Bhikkhū bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): who in turn told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:2.13
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:2.14
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, tiriyaṁ taraṇāya bhikkhuniyā saddhiṁ saṁvidhāya ekaṁ nāvaṁ abhiruhituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to board a boat by arrangement with a nun if it’s for the purpose of crossing. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:2.15
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:2.16.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhuniyā saddhiṁ saṁvidhāya ekaṁ nāvaṁ abhiruheyya uddhaṅgāminiṁ vā adhogāminiṁ vā, aññatra tiriyaṁ taraṇāya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk boards a boat by arrangement with a nun, either to go upstream or downstream, except for the purpose of crossing, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.1.5
Pali: Bhikkhunī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A nun: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.1.6
Pali: ubhatosaṅghe upasampannā. 
English (brahmali, published): she has been given the full ordination by both Sanghas. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.1.7
Pali: Saddhinti 
English (brahmali, published): With: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.1.8
Pali: ekato. 
English (brahmali, published): together. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.1.9
Pali: Saṁvidhāyāti 
English (brahmali, published): By arrangement: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.1.10
Pali: “abhiruhāma, bhagini, abhiruhāmāyya; abhiruhāmāyya, abhiruhāma, bhagini; ajja vā hiyyo vā pare vā abhiruhāmā”ti saṁvidahati āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes an arrangement like this: he says, “Let’s board, sister,” and she replies, “Yes, let’s board, venerable;” or she says, “Let’s board, venerable,” and he replies, “Yes, let’s board, sister;” or he says, “Let’s board today,” “Let’s board tomorrow,” “Let’s board the day after tomorrow,” then he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.1.11
Pali: Bhikkhuniyā abhiruḷhe bhikkhu abhiruhati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the monk boards when the nun has already boarded, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.1.12
Pali: Bhikkhumhi abhiruḷhe bhikkhunī abhiruhati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the nun boards when the monk has already boarded, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.1.13
Pali: Ubho vā abhiruhanti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If they both board together, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.1.14
Pali: Uddhaṅgāmininti 
English (brahmali, published): To go upstream: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.1.15
Pali: ujjavanikāya. 
English (brahmali, published): for the purpose of going against the stream. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.1.16
Pali: Adhogāmininti 
English (brahmali, published): To go downstream: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.1.17
Pali: ojavanikāya. 
English (brahmali, published): for the purpose of going with the stream. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.1.18
Pali: Aññatra tiriyaṁ taraṇāyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Except for the purpose of crossing: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.1.19
Pali: ṭhapetvā tiriyaṁ taraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): unless it is to go across. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.1.20
Pali: Kukkuṭasampāte gāme, gāmantare gāmantare āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When the inhabited areas are a chicken’s flight apart, then for every next inhabited area he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.1.21
Pali: Agāmake araññe, aḍḍhayojane aḍḍhayojane āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When it is an uninhabited area, a wilderness, then for every six kilometers he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.2.1
Pali: Saṁvidahite saṁvidahitasaññī ekaṁ nāvaṁ abhiruhati uddhaṅgāminiṁ vā adhogāminiṁ vā, aññatra tiriyaṁ taraṇāya, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If there is an arrangement, and he perceives that there is, and he boards a boat with a nun, either to go upstream or downstream, except for the purpose of crossing, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.2.2
Pali: Saṁvidahite vematiko ekaṁ nāvaṁ abhiruhati uddhaṅgāminiṁ vā adhogāminiṁ vā, aññatra tiriyaṁ taraṇāya, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If there is an arrangement, but he is unsure of it, and he boards a boat with a nun, either to go upstream or downstream, except for the purpose of crossing, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.2.3
Pali: Saṁvidahite asaṁvidahitasaññī ekaṁ nāvaṁ abhiruhati uddhaṅgāminiṁ vā adhogāminiṁ vā, aññatra tiriyaṁ taraṇāya, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If there is an arrangement, but he does not perceive that there is, and he boards a boat with a nun, either to go upstream or downstream, except for the purpose of crossing, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.2.4
Pali: Bhikkhu saṁvidahati, bhikkhunī na saṁvidahati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the monk makes an arrangement, but the nun does not express her agreement, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.2.5
Pali: Asaṁvidahite saṁvidahitasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If there is no arrangement, but he perceives that there is, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.2.6
Pali: Asaṁvidahite vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If there is no arrangement, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.2.7
Pali: Asaṁvidahite, asaṁvidahitasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If there is no arrangement, and he does not perceive that there is, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.3.2
Pali: tiriyaṁ taraṇāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is for the purpose of crossing; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.3.3
Pali: asaṁvidahitvā abhiruhanti, 
English (brahmali, published): if they board without an arrangement; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.3.4
Pali: bhikkhunī saṁvidahati, bhikkhu na saṁvidahati, 
English (brahmali, published): if the nun has made an arrangement, but he has not expressed his agreement; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.3.5
Pali: visaṅketena abhiruhanti, 
English (brahmali, published): if they board, but not according to the arrangement; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.3.6
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.3.7
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.3.8
Pali: ādikammissāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc28:3.3.9
Pali: Nāvābhiruhanasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ aṭṭhamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on traveling on a boat with a nun, the eighth, is finished.

English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis

English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession

Pali: Ovādavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on the instruction 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:0.5
Pali: 29. Paripācitasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 29. Eating food prepared on a nun’s instruction 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Rājagaha in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrel sanctuary. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī aññatarassa kulassa kulūpikā hoti niccabhattikā. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the nun Thullanandā was associating with a family from which she received a regular meal. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:1.3
Pali: Tena ca gahapatinā therā bhikkhū nimantitā honti. 
English (brahmali, published): Now on one occasion the head of that family had invited some senior monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:1.4
Pali: Atha kho thullanandā bhikkhunī pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena taṁ kulaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ gahapatiṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): On the same day, the nun Thullanandā robed up in the morning, took her bowl and robe, and went to that family. And she asked the head of the family, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:1.5
Pali: “kimidaṁ, gahapati, pahūtaṁ khādanīyaṁ bhojanīyaṁ paṭiyattan”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Why have you prepared so much food?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:1.6
Pali: “Therā mayā, ayye, nimantitā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “‘Cause, venerable, I’ve invited the senior monks.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:1.7
Pali: “Ke pana te, gahapati, therā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “But who are those senior monks?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:1.8
Pali: “Ayyo sāriputto ayyo mahāmoggallāno ayyo mahākaccāno ayyo mahākoṭṭhiko ayyo mahākappino ayyo mahācundo ayyo anuruddho ayyo revato ayyo upāli ayyo ānando ayyo rāhulo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable Sāriputta, Venerable Mahāmoggallāna, Venerable Mahākaccāna, Venerable Mahākoṭṭhika, Venerable Mahākappina, Venerable Mahācunda, Venerable Anuruddha, Venerable Revata, Venerable Upāli, Venerable Ānanda, and Venerable Rāhula.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:1.9
Pali: “Kiṁ pana tvaṁ, gahapati, mahānāge tiṭṭhamāne ceṭake nimantesī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “But why do you invite such inferior monks instead of the great ones?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:1.10
Pali: “Ke pana te, ayye, mahānāgā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Who are these great monks?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:1.11
Pali: “Ayyo devadatto ayyo kokāliko ayyo kaṭamodakatissako ayyo khaṇḍadeviyā putto ayyo samuddadatto”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable Devadatta, Venerable Kokālika, Venerable Kaṭamodakatissaka, Venerable Khaṇḍadeviyāputta, and Venerable Samuddadatta.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:1.12
Pali: Ayaṁ carahi thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā antarā kathā vippakatā, atha te therā bhikkhū pavisiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): While this conversation was taking place, the senior monks entered. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:1.13
Pali: “Saccaṁ mahānāgā kho tayā, gahapati, nimantitā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Thullanandā said, “Is it true that you’ve invited these great monks?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:1.14
Pali: “Idāneva kho tvaṁ, ayye, ceṭake akāsi; idāni mahānāge”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Just before you called them inferior and now you call them great.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:1.15
Pali: Gharato ca nikkaḍḍhi, niccabhattañca pacchindi. 
English (brahmali, published): And he threw her out of the house and made an end of her regular meal. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:1.16
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized Devadatta, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:1.17
Pali: “kathañhi nāma devadatto jānaṁ bhikkhuniparipācitaṁ piṇḍapātaṁ bhuñjissatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Devadatta eat almsfood knowing that a nun had it prepared?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:1.18
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, devadatta, jānaṁ bhikkhuniparipācitaṁ piṇḍapātaṁ bhuñjasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Devadatta, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:1.19
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:1.20
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:1.21
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, jānaṁ bhikkhuniparipācitaṁ piṇḍapātaṁ bhuñjissasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:1.22
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:1.23
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:1.24.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu jānaṁ bhikkhuniparipācitaṁ piṇḍapātaṁ bhuñjeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk eats almsfood knowing that a nun had it prepared, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:1.25
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu rājagahā pabbajito ñātikulaṁ agamāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards a monk who had earlier left Rājagaha returned to see his family. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:2.2
Pali: Manussā—“cirassampi bhadanto āgato”ti sakkaccaṁ bhattaṁ akaṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): Because it was a long time since he had last returned, people prepared food for him respectfully. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:2.3
Pali: Tassa kulassa kulūpikā bhikkhunī te manusse etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): And the nun who was associating with that family said to them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:2.4
Pali: “dethayyassa, āvuso, bhattan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Give food to that monk.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:2.5
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu—
English (brahmali, published): The monk thought, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:2.6
Pali: “bhagavatā paṭikkhittaṁ jānaṁ bhikkhuniparipācitaṁ piṇḍapātaṁ bhuñjitun”ti kukkuccāyanto na paṭiggahesi. 
English (brahmali, published): “The Buddha has prohibited us from eating almsfood knowing that a nun had it prepared,” and being afraid of wrongdoing, he did not accept it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:2.7
Pali: Nāsakkhi piṇḍāya carituṁ, chinnabhatto ahosi. 
English (brahmali, published): And because he was unable to walk for alms, he missed his meal. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:2.8
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu ārāmaṁ gantvā bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): After returning to the monastery, he told the monks what had happened, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:2.9
Pali: Bhikkhū bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): and they in turn told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:2.10
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:2.11
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, pubbe gihisamārambhe jānaṁ bhikkhuniparipācitaṁ piṇḍapātaṁ bhuñjituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to eat almsfood knowing that a nun had it prepared if the householder had intended to prepare it anyway. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:2.12
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:2.13.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu jānaṁ bhikkhuniparipācitaṁ piṇḍapātaṁ bhuñjeyya, aññatra pubbe gihisamārambhā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk eats almsfood knowing that a nun had it prepared, except if the householder had intended to prepare it anyway, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.1.5
Pali: Jānāti nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Knowing: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.1.6
Pali: sāmaṁ vā jānāti aññe vā tassa ārocenti sā vā āroceti. 
English (brahmali, published): he knows by himself or others have told him or the nun has told him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.1.7
Pali: Bhikkhunī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A nun: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.1.8
Pali: ubhatosaṅghe upasampannā. 
English (brahmali, published): she has been given the full ordination by both Sanghas. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.1.9
Pali: Paripāceti nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Has it prepared: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.1.10
Pali: pubbe adātukāmānaṁ akattukāmānaṁ— “ayyo bhāṇako, ayyo bahussuto, ayyo suttantiko, ayyo vinayadharo, ayyo dhammakathiko, detha ayyassa, karotha ayyassā”ti esā paripāceti nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): if she says to those who do not already want to give or want to prepare, “This monk is a reciter,” “This monk is learned,” “This monk is an expert on the discourses,” “This monk is an expert on the Monastic Law,” “This monk is an expounder of the Teaching;” “Give to this monk,” “Prepare for this monk”—this is called “has it prepared”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.1.11
Pali: Piṇḍapāto nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Almsfood: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.1.12
Pali: pañcannaṁ bhojanānaṁ aññataraṁ bhojanaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): any of the five cooked foods. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.1.13
Pali: Aññatra pubbe gihisamārambhāti 
English (brahmali, published): Except if the householder had intended to prepare it anyway: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.1.14
Pali: ṭhapetvā gihisamārambhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): unless the householder had intended to prepare it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.1.15
Pali: Gihisamārambho nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The householder had intended to prepare it: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.1.16
Pali: ñātakā vā honti pavāritā vā pakatipaṭiyattaṁ vā. 
English (brahmali, published): they are relatives or they have invited or they give regularly. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.1.17
Pali: Aññatra pubbe gihisamārambhā bhuñjissāmīti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he receives it with the intention of eating it, except if the householder had intended to prepare it anyway, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.1.18
Pali: Ajjhohāre ajjhohāre, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): For every mouthful swallowed, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.2.1
Pali: Paripācite paripācitasaññī bhuñjati, aññatra pubbe gihisamārambhā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a nun had it prepared, and he perceives it as such, and he eats it, except if the householder had intended to prepare it anyway, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.2.2
Pali: Paripācite vematiko bhuñjati, aññatra pubbe gihisamārambhā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a nun had it prepared, but he is unsure of it, and he eats it, except if the householder had intended to prepare it anyway, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.2.3
Pali: Paripācite aparipācitasaññī bhuñjati, aññatra pubbe gihisamārambhā, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If a nun had it prepared, but he does not perceive it as such, and he eats it, except if the householder had intended to prepare it anyway, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.2.4
Pali: Ekatoupasampannāya paripācitaṁ bhuñjati, aññatra pubbe gihisamārambhā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a nun who is fully ordained only on one side had it prepared, and he eats it, except if the householder had intended to prepare it anyway, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.2.5
Pali: Aparipācite paripācitasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a nun did not have it prepared, but he perceives it as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.2.6
Pali: Aparipācite vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a nun did not have it prepared, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.2.7
Pali: Aparipācite aparipācitasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If a nun did not have it prepared, and he does not perceive it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.3.2
Pali: pubbe gihisamārambhe, 
English (brahmali, published): if the householder had intended to prepare it anyway; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.3.3
Pali: sikkhamānā paripāceti, 
English (brahmali, published): if a trainee nun has it prepared; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.3.4
Pali: sāmaṇerī paripāceti, 
English (brahmali, published): if a novice nun has it prepared; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.3.5
Pali: pañca bhojanāni ṭhapetvā sabbattha anāpatti, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is anything apart from the five cooked foods; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.3.6
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.3.7
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc29:3.3.8
Pali: Paripācitasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ navamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on eating food prepared on a nun’s instruction, the ninth, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:0.4
Pali: Ovādavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on the instruction 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:0.5
Pali: 30. Rahonisajjasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 30. Sitting alone with a nun 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmato udāyissa purāṇadutiyikā bhikkhunīsu pabbajitā hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Udāyī’s ex-wife became a nun. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:1.3
Pali: Sā āyasmato udāyissa santike abhikkhaṇaṁ āgacchati, āyasmāpi udāyī tassā bhikkhuniyā santike abhikkhaṇaṁ gacchati. 
English (brahmali, published): She often went to see Udāyī, and he often went to see her. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:1.4
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā udāyī tassā bhikkhuniyā saddhiṁ eko ekāya raho nisajjaṁ kappesi. 
English (brahmali, published): And Udāyī would sit down in private alone with that nun. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:1.5
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:1.6
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā udāyī bhikkhuniyā saddhiṁ eko ekāya raho nisajjaṁ kappessatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can Venerable Udāyī sit down in private alone with a nun?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:1.7
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, udāyi, bhikkhuniyā saddhiṁ eko ekāya raho nisajjaṁ kappesī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Udāyī, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:1.8
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:1.9
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:1.10
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, bhikkhuniyā saddhiṁ eko ekāya raho nisajjaṁ kappessasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, how can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:1.11
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:1.12
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:1.13.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhuniyā saddhiṁ eko ekāya raho nisajjaṁ kappeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk sits down in private alone with a nun, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.1.5
Pali: Bhikkhunī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A nun: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.1.6
Pali: ubhatosaṅghe upasampannā. 
English (brahmali, published): she has been given the full ordination by both Sanghas. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.1.7
Pali: Saddhinti 
English (brahmali, published): With: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.1.8
Pali: ekato. 
English (brahmali, published): together. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.1.9
Pali: Eko ekāyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Alone: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.1.10
Pali: bhikkhu ceva hoti bhikkhunī ca. 
English (brahmali, published): just the monk and the nun. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.1.11
Pali: Raho nāma 
English (brahmali, published): In private: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.1.12
Pali: cakkhussa raho sotassa raho. 
English (brahmali, published): private to the eye and private to the ear. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.1.13
Pali: Cakkhussa raho nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Private to the eye: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.1.14
Pali: na sakkā hoti akkhiṁ vā nikhaṇīyamāne bhamukaṁ vā ukkhipīyamāne sīsaṁ vā ukkhipīyamāne passituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one is unable to see them winking, raising an eyebrow, or nodding. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.1.15
Pali: Sotassa raho nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Private to the ear: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.1.16
Pali: na sakkā hoti pakatikathā sotuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one is unable to hear ordinary speech. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.1.17
Pali: Nisajjaṁ kappeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Sits: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.1.18
Pali: bhikkhuniyā nisinnāya bhikkhu upanisinno vā hoti upanipanno vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if the monk sits down or lies down next to the seated nun, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.1.19
Pali: Bhikkhu nisinne bhikkhunī upanisinnā vā hoti upanipannā vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the nun sits down or lies down next to the seated monk, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.1.20
Pali: Ubho vā nisinnā honti ubho vā nipannā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If both are seated or both are lying down, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.2.1
Pali: Raho rahosaññī eko ekāya nisajjaṁ kappeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is private, and he perceives it as such, and he sits down alone with a nun, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.2.2
Pali: Raho vematiko eko ekāya nisajjaṁ kappeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is private, but he is unsure of it, and he sits down alone with a nun, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.2.3
Pali: Raho arahosaññī eko ekāya nisajjaṁ kappeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is private, but he does not perceive it as such, and he sits down alone with a nun, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.2.4
Pali: Araho rahosaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not private, but he perceives it as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.2.5
Pali: Araho vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not private, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.2.6
Pali: Araho arahosaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not private, and he does not perceive it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.3.2
Pali: yo koci viññū dutiyo hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he has a companion who understands; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.3.3
Pali: tiṭṭhati na nisīdati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he stands and does not sit down; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.3.4
Pali: arahopekkho, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is not seeking privacy; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.3.5
Pali: aññavihito nisīdati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he sits down preoccupied with something else; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.3.6
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.3.7
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.3.8
Pali: Rahonisajjasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ dasamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on sitting alone with a nun, the tenth, is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.3.9
Pali: Ovādavaggo tatiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): The third subchapter on the instruction is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.3.10
Pali: Tassuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.3.11
Pali: Asammataatthaṅgatū, 
English (brahmali, published): “Not appointed, set, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.3.12
Pali: Passayāmisadānena; 
English (brahmali, published): Dwelling place, worldly gain, and with giving; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.3.13
Pali: Sibbati addhānaṁ nāvaṁ bhuñjeyya, 
English (brahmali, published): He sews, a road, a boat, should eat, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc30:2.3.14
Pali: Eko ekāya te dasāti. 
English (brahmali, published): Alone: those are the ten.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31
