English translation sources:
  - brahmali [published]: translation/en/brahmali/vinaya/pli-tv-bu-vb/pli-tv-bu-vb-pc/pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31_translation-en-brahmali.json

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:0.4
Pali: Bhojanavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on eating 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:0.5
Pali: 31. Āvasathapiṇḍasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 31. Eating excessively at a public guesthouse 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sāvatthiyā avidūre aññatarassa pūgassa āvasathapiṇḍo paññatto hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): a certain association was preparing an almsmeal at a public guesthouse not far from Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:1.3
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhū pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya sāvatthiṁ piṇḍāya pavisitvā piṇḍaṁ alabhamānā āvasathaṁ agamaṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): Then, after robing up in the morning, the monks from the group of six took their bowls and robes and entered Sāvatthī for alms. Not getting anything, they went to that public guesthouse. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:1.4
Pali: Manussā—“cirassampi bhadantā āgatā”ti sakkaccaṁ parivisiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): Because it was a long time since they had been there, people served them respectfully. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:1.5
Pali: Atha kho chabbaggiyā bhikkhū dutiyampi divasaṁ …pe… tatiyampi divasaṁ pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya sāvatthiṁ piṇḍāya pavisitvā piṇḍaṁ alabhamānā āvasathaṁ gantvā bhuñjiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): A second and a third day those monks did the same thing. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:1.6
Pali: Atha kho chabbaggiyānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ etadahosi—
English (brahmali, published): Then they thought, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:1.7
Pali: “kiṁ mayaṁ karissāma ārāmaṁ gantvā. 
English (brahmali, published): “What’s the point of returning to the monastery? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:1.8
Pali: Hiyyopi idheva āgantabbaṁ bhavissatī”ti, 
English (brahmali, published): Tomorrow we’ll just have to come back here.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:1.9
Pali: tattheva anuvasitvā anuvasitvā āvasathapiṇḍaṁ bhuñjanti. 
English (brahmali, published): So they stayed on and on right there, eating alms at the guesthouse, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:1.10
Pali: Titthiyā apasakkanti. 
English (brahmali, published): while the monastics of other religions left. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:1.11
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:1.12
Pali: “kathañhi nāma samaṇā sakyaputtiyā anuvasitvā anuvasitvā āvasathapiṇḍaṁ bhuñjissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the Sakyan monastics stay on and on, eating alms at the guesthouse? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:1.13
Pali: Nayimesaññeva āvasathapiṇḍo paññatto; 
English (brahmali, published): We don’t prepare the almsfood just for them; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:1.14
Pali: sabbesaññeva āvasathapiṇḍo paññatto”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): we prepare it for everyone.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:1.15
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:1.16
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:1.17
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū anuvasitvā anuvasitvā āvasathapiṇḍaṁ bhuñjissantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the monks from the group of six stay on and on, eating alms at a public guesthouse?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:1.18
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, anuvasitvā anuvasitvā āvasathapiṇḍaṁ bhuñjathā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:1.19
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:1.20
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:1.21
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, anuvasitvā anuvasitvā āvasathapiṇḍaṁ bhuñjissatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:1.22
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:1.23
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:1.24.1
Pali: “Eko āvasathapiṇḍo bhuñjitabbo. Tato ce uttari bhuñjeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk eats more than one almsmeal at a public guesthouse, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:1.25
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā sāriputto kosalesu janapade sāvatthiṁ gacchanto yena aññataro āvasatho tenupasaṅkami. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards Venerable Sāriputta was traveling through the Kosalan country on his way to Sāvatthī when he came to a public guesthouse. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:2.2
Pali: Manussā—“cirassampi thero āgato”ti sakkaccaṁ parivisiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): Because it was a long time since he had been there, people served him respectfully. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:2.3
Pali: Atha kho āyasmato sāriputtassa bhuttāvissa kharo ābādho uppajji, nāsakkhi tamhā āvasathā pakkamituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): After he had eaten, Sāriputta became severely ill, and he was unable to leave that guesthouse. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:2.4
Pali: Atha kho te manussā dutiyampi divasaṁ āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): On the second day, too, those people said to him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:2.5
Pali: “bhuñjatha, bhante”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please eat, venerable.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:2.6
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā sāriputto—
English (brahmali, published): But since he knew that 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:2.7
Pali: “bhagavatā paṭikkhittaṁ anuvasitvā anuvasitvā āvasathapiṇḍaṁ bhuñjitun”ti kukkuccāyanto na paṭiggahesi; 
English (brahmali, published): the Buddha had prohibited eating alms at a public guesthouse after staying on and on, and because he was afraid of wrongdoing, he did not accept. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:2.8
Pali: chinnabhatto ahosi. 
English (brahmali, published): As a consequence, he missed his meal. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:2.9
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā sāriputto sāvatthiṁ gantvā bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): When he arrived at Sāvatthī, he told the monks what had happened, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:2.10
Pali: Bhikkhū bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): and they in turn told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:2.11
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:2.12
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, gilānena bhikkhunā anuvasitvā anuvasitvā āvasathapiṇḍaṁ bhuñjituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow a sick monk to stay on at a public guesthouse and eat alms there. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:2.13
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:2.14.1
Pali: “Agilānena bhikkhunā eko āvasathapiṇḍo bhuñjitabbo. Tato ce uttari bhuñjeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk who is not sick eats more than one almsmeal at a public guesthouse, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:3.1.1
Pali: Agilāno nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Who is not sick: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:3.1.2
Pali: sakkoti tamhā āvasathā pakkamituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): he is able to leave that public guesthouse. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:3.1.3
Pali: Gilāno nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Who is sick: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:3.1.4
Pali: na sakkoti tamhā āvasathā pakkamituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): he is unable to leave that public guesthouse. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:3.1.5
Pali: Āvasathapiṇḍo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Almsmeal at a public guesthouse: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:3.1.6
Pali: pañcannaṁ bhojanānaṁ aññataraṁ bhojanaṁ— sālāya vā maṇḍape vā rukkhamūle vā ajjhokāse vā anodissa yāvadattho paññatto hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): as much as one needs of any of the five cooked foods, prepared for the general public, in a building, under a roof cover, at the foot of a tree, or out in the open. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:3.1.7
Pali: Agilānena bhikkhunā sakiṁ bhuñjitabbo. Tato ce uttari “bhuñjissāmī”ti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): A monk who is not sick may eat there once. If he receives food beyond that with the intention of eating it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:3.1.8
Pali: Ajjhohāre ajjhohāre āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): For every mouthful swallowed, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:3.2.1
Pali: Agilāno agilānasaññī tatuttari āvasathapiṇḍaṁ bhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is not sick, and he perceives himself as not sick, and he eats more than one almsmeal at a public guesthouse, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:3.2.2
Pali: Agilāno vematiko tatuttari āvasathapiṇḍaṁ bhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is not sick, but he is unsure of it, and he eats more than one almsmeal at a public guesthouse, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:3.2.3
Pali: Agilāno gilānasaññī tatuttari āvasathapiṇḍaṁ bhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is not sick, but he perceives himself as sick, and he eats more than one almsmeal at a public guesthouse, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:3.2.4
Pali: Gilāno agilānasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is sick, but he perceives himself as not sick, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:3.2.5
Pali: Gilāno vematiko āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is sick, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:3.2.6
Pali: Gilāno gilānasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is sick, and he perceives himself as sick, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:3.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:3.3.2
Pali: gilānassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is sick; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:3.3.3
Pali: agilāno sakiṁ bhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is not sick and he eats once; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:3.3.4
Pali: gacchanto, vā āgacchanto vā bhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he eats while coming or going; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:3.3.5
Pali: sāmikā nimantetvā bhojenti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he eats after being invited by the owners; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:3.3.6
Pali: odissa paññatto hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if the food is prepared specifically for him; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:3.3.7
Pali: na yāvadattho paññatto hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is not as much as he needs; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:3.3.8
Pali: pañca bhojanāni ṭhapetvā sabbattha anāpatti, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is anything apart from the five cooked foods; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:3.3.9
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:3.3.10
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc31:3.3.11
Pali: Āvasathapiṇḍasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ paṭhamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on eating excessively at a public guesthouse, the first, is finished.

Pali: 32. Gaṇabhojanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 32. Eating in a group 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Rājagaha in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrel sanctuary. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena devadatto parihīnalābhasakkāro sapariso kulesu viññāpetvā viññāpetvā bhuñjati. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time, because of his loss of material support and honor, Devadatta and his followers had to ask families repeatedly to get invited to meals. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:1.3
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:1.4
Pali: “kathañhi nāma samaṇā sakyaputtiyā kulesu viññāpetvā viññāpetvā bhuñjissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the Sakyan monastics repeatedly ask families to get invited to meals? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:1.5
Pali: Kassa sampannaṁ na manāpaṁ, kassa sāduṁ na ruccatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Who doesn’t like nice food? Who doesn’t prefer tasty food?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:1.6
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those people,

Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:1.8
Pali: “kathañhi nāma devadatto sapariso kulesu viññāpetvā viññāpetvā bhuñjissatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can Devadatta and his followers repeatedly ask families to get invited to meals?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:1.9
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, devadatta, sapariso kulesu viññāpetvā viññāpetvā bhuñjasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Devadatta, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:1.10
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:1.11
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:1.12
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, sapariso kulesu viññāpetvā viññāpetvā bhuñjissasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, how can you do this?

English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” …

English (brahmali, published): This is the appropriate occasion: he is sick.’”

English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:3.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena manussā cīvaradānasamaye sacīvarabhattaṁ paṭiyādetvā bhikkhū nimantenti—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards, during the robe-giving season, people prepared a meal together with robe-cloth and then invited the monks, saying,

Pali: “bhojetvā cīvarena acchādessāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “We wish to offer a meal and then give robe-cloth.”

English (brahmali, published): But knowing that the Buddha had prohibited eating in a group and being afraid of wrongdoing, they did not accept. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:3.4
Pali: Cīvaraṁ parittaṁ uppajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): As a result, they only got a small amount of robe-cloth.

Pali: anujānāmi, bhikkhave, cīvaradānasamaye gaṇabhojanaṁ bhuñjituṁ.

Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū manussehi saddhiṁ addhānaṁ gacchanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards some monks went traveling with a group of people. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:5.2
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū te manusse etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): The monks said to those people, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:5.3
Pali: “muhuttaṁ, āvuso, āgametha; piṇḍāya carissāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please wait a moment while we walk for alms.”

English (brahmali, published): They replied,

Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū manussehi saddhiṁ nāvāya gacchanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards some monks were traveling by boat with a group of people. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:6.2
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū te manusse etadavocuṁ—“muhuttaṁ, āvuso, tīraṁ upanetha; piṇḍāya carissāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks said to those people, “Please go to the shore for a moment while we walk for alms.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:6.3
Pali: Te evamāhaṁsu—“idheva, bhante, bhuñjathā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): They replied, “Venerables, please eat right here.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:6.4
Pali: Bhikkhū kukkuccāyantā na paṭiggaṇhanti—“paṭikkhittaṁ bhagavatā gaṇabhojanan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): But knowing that the Buddha had prohibited eating in a group and being afraid of wrongdoing, they did not accept. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:6.5
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:6.6
Pali: anujānāmi, bhikkhave, nāvābhiruhanasamaye gaṇabhojanaṁ bhuñjituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to eat in a group when on board a boat. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:6.7
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:6.8.1
Pali: “Gaṇabhojane, aññatra samayā, pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk eats in a group, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:6.9
Pali: Tatthāyaṁ samayo. Gilānasamayo, cīvaradānasamayo, cīvarakārasamayo, addhānagamanasamayo, nāvābhiruhanasamayo—ayaṁ tattha samayo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): These are the appropriate occasions: he is sick; it is the robe-giving season; it is a time of making robes; he is traveling; he is on a boat.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:6.10
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:7.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena disāsu vassaṁvuṭṭhā bhikkhū rājagahaṁ āgacchanti bhagavantaṁ dassanāya. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards monks who had completed the rainy-season residence in various regions were coming to Rājagaha to visit the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:7.2
Pali: Manussā nānāverajjake bhikkhū passitvā bhattena nimantenti. 
English (brahmali, published): People saw those monks who had come from various countries and invited them for a meal. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:7.3
Pali: Bhikkhū kukkuccāyantā nādhivāsenti—“paṭikkhittaṁ bhagavatā gaṇabhojanan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): But knowing that the Buddha had prohibited eating in a group and being afraid of wrongdoing, they did not accept. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:7.4
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:7.5
Pali: anujānāmi, bhikkhave, mahāsamaye gaṇabhojanaṁ bhuñjituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to eat in a group on big occasions. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:7.6
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:7.7.1
Pali: “Gaṇabhojane, aññatra samayā, pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk eats in a group, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:7.8
Pali: Tatthāyaṁ samayo. Gilānasamayo, cīvaradānasamayo, cīvarakārasamayo, addhānagamanasamayo, nāvābhiruhanasamayo, mahāsamayo—ayaṁ tattha samayo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): These are the appropriate occasions: he is sick; it is the robe-giving season; it is a time of making robes; he is traveling; he is on a boat; it is a big occasion.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:7.9
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:8.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena rañño māgadhassa seniyassa bimbisārassa ñātisālohito ājīvakesu pabbajito hoti. Atha kho so ājīvako yena rājā māgadho seniyo bimbisāro tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā rājānaṁ māgadhaṁ seniyaṁ bimbisāraṁ etadavoca—“icchāmahaṁ, mahārāja, sabbapāsaṇḍikabhattaṁ kātun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards a relative of King Seniya Bimbisāra of Magadha who had gone forth with the Ājīvaka ascetics went to the king and said, “Great king, I wish to make a meal for the monastics of all religions.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:8.2
Pali: “Sace tvaṁ, bhante, buddhappamukhaṁ bhikkhusaṅghaṁ paṭhamaṁ bhojeyyāsi”. 
English (brahmali, published): “That’s fine, sir, if you first feed the Sangha of monks headed by the Buddha.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:8.3
Pali: “Evaṁ kareyyāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I’ll do that.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:8.4
Pali: Atha kho so ājīvako bhikkhūnaṁ santike dūtaṁ pāhesi—“adhivāsentu me bhikkhū svātanāya bhattan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): And he sent a message to the monks: “Please accept a meal from me tomorrow.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:8.5
Pali: Bhikkhū kukkuccāyantā nādhivāsenti—“paṭikkhittaṁ bhagavatā gaṇabhojanan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): But knowing that the Buddha had prohibited eating in a group and being afraid of wrongdoing, they did not accept. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:8.6
Pali: Atha kho so ājīvako yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi, sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhito kho so ājīvako bhagavantaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): That Ājīvaka ascetic then went to the Buddha, exchanged pleasantries with him, and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:8.7
Pali: “bhavampi gotamo pabbajito, ahampi pabbajito; 
English (brahmali, published): “Good Gotama has gone forth and so have I. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:8.8
Pali: arahati pabbajito pabbajitassa piṇḍaṁ paṭiggahetuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): One who has gone forth should receive alms from another who has gone forth. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:8.9
Pali: Adhivāsetu me bhavaṁ gotamo svātanāya bhattaṁ saddhiṁ bhikkhusaṅghenā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Good Gotama, please accept a meal from me tomorrow together with the Sangha of monks.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:8.10
Pali: Adhivāsesi bhagavā tuṇhībhāvena. 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha consented by remaining silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:8.11
Pali: Atha kho so ājīvako bhagavato adhivāsanaṁ viditvā pakkāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): The Ājīvaka understood that the Buddha had consented, and he left. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:8.12
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:8.13
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, samaṇabhattasamaye gaṇabhojanaṁ bhuñjituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to eat in a group when the meal is given by a monastic. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:8.14
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:8.15.1
Pali: Gaṇabhojane, aññatra samayā, pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk eats in a group, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:8.16
Pali: Tatthāyaṁ samayo. Gilānasamayo, cīvaradānasamayo, cīvarakārasamayo, addhānagamanasamayo, nāvābhiruhanasamayo, mahāsamayo, samaṇabhattasamayo—ayaṁ tattha samayo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): These are the appropriate occasions: he is sick; it is the robe-giving season; it is a time of making robes; he is traveling; he is on a boat; it is a big occasion; it is a meal given by a monastic.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.1.1
Pali: Gaṇabhojanaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Eats in a group: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.1.2
Pali: yattha cattāro bhikkhū pañcannaṁ bhojanānaṁ aññatarena bhojanena nimantitā bhuñjanti. Etaṁ gaṇabhojanaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): wherever four monks, after being invited, eat any of the five cooked foods—this is called “eats in a group”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.1.3
Pali: Aññatra samayāti 
English (brahmali, published): Except on an appropriate occasion: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.1.4
Pali: ṭhapetvā samayaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): unless it is an appropriate occasion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.1.5
Pali: Gilānasamayo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): He is sick: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.1.6
Pali: antamaso pādāpi phalitā honti. “Gilānasamayo”ti bhuñjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): even if he has cracked feet, he may eat in a group. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.1.7
Pali: Cīvaradānasamayo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): It is the robe-giving season: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.1.8
Pali: anatthate kathine vassānassa pacchimo māso, atthate kathine pañcamāsā. “Cīvaradānasamayo”ti bhuñjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if he has not participated in the robe-making ceremony, he may eat in a group during the last month of the rainy season. If he has participated in the robe-making ceremony, he may eat in a group during the five month period. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.1.9
Pali: Cīvarakārasamayo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): It is a time of making robes: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.1.10
Pali: cīvare kayiramāne. “Cīvarakārasamayo”ti bhuñjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): when he is making robes, he may eat in a group. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.1.11
Pali: Addhānagamanasamayo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): He is traveling: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.1.12
Pali: “addhayojanaṁ gacchissāmī”ti bhuñjitabbaṁ, gacchantena bhuñjitabbaṁ, gatena bhuñjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): he may eat in a group if he intends to travel at least six kilometers, while traveling, and after traveling. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.1.13
Pali: Nāvābhiruhanasamayo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): He is on a boat: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.1.14
Pali: “nāvaṁ abhiruhissāmī”ti bhuñjitabbaṁ, āruḷhena bhuñjitabbaṁ, oruḷhena bhuñjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): he may eat in a group if he intends to board a boat, while on board, and after disembarking. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.1.15
Pali: Mahāsamayo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): It is a big occasion: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.1.16
Pali: yattha dve tayo bhikkhū piṇḍāya caritvā yāpenti, catutthe āgate na yāpenti. “Mahāsamayo”ti bhuñjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if two or three monks can get by on walking for alms, but not a group of four, he may eat in a group. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.1.17
Pali: Samaṇabhattasamayo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): It is a meal given by a monastic: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.1.18
Pali: yo koci paribbājakasamāpanno bhattaṁ karoti. “Samaṇabhattasamayo”ti bhuñjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if any kind of wanderer is making the meal, he may eat in a group. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.1.19
Pali: “Aññatra samayā bhuñjissāmī”ti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he receives something intending to eat it, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.1.20
Pali: Ajjhohāre ajjhohāre āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): For every mouthful swallowed, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.2.1
Pali: Gaṇabhojane gaṇabhojanasaññī, aññatra samayā, bhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he eats in a group, and he perceives it as such, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.2.2
Pali: Gaṇabhojane vematiko, aññatra samayā, bhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he eats in a group, but he is unsure of it, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.2.3
Pali: Gaṇabhojane nagaṇabhojanasaññī, aññatra samayā, bhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he eats in a group, but he does not perceive it as such, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.2.4
Pali: Nagaṇabhojane gaṇabhojanasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not eat in a group, but he perceives it as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.2.5
Pali: Nagaṇabhojane vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not eat in a group, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.2.6
Pali: Nagaṇabhojane nagaṇabhojanasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not eat in a group, and he does not perceive it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.3.2
Pali: samaye, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is an appropriate occasion; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.3.3
Pali: dve tayo ekato bhuñjanti, 
English (brahmali, published): if two or three eat together; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.3.4
Pali: piṇḍāya caritvā ekato sannipatitvā bhuñjanti, 
English (brahmali, published): if they eat together after walking for alms; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.3.5
Pali: niccabhattaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a regular meal invitation; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.3.6
Pali: salākabhattaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a meal for which lots are drawn; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.3.7
Pali: pakkhikaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a half-monthly meal; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.3.8
Pali: uposathikaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is on the observance day; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.3.9
Pali: pāṭipadikaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is on the day after the observance day; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.3.10
Pali: pañca bhojanāni ṭhapetvā sabbattha anāpatti, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is anything apart from the five cooked foods; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.3.11
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.3.12
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc32:9.3.13
Pali: Gaṇabhojanasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ dutiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on eating in a group, the second, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:0.4
Pali: Bhojanavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on eating 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:0.5
Pali: 33. Paramparabhojanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 33. Eating meals in succession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying in the house with the peaked roof in the Great Wood near Vesālī, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena vesāliyaṁ paṇītānaṁ bhattānaṁ bhattapaṭipāṭi adhiṭṭhitā hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): a succession of fine meals had been arranged in Vesālī. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.3
Pali: Atha kho aññatarassa daliddassa kammakārassa etadahosi—“na kho idaṁ orakaṁ bhavissati yathayime manussā sakkaccaṁ bhattaṁ karonti; yannūnāhampi bhattaṁ kareyyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): A certain poor worker thought, “Why don’t I prepare a meal? It must be really worthwhile, seeing as these people prepare a meal with such respect.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.4
Pali: Atha kho so daliddo kammakāro yena kirapatiko tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ kirapatikaṁ etadavoca—“icchāmahaṁ, ayyaputta, buddhappamukhassa bhikkhusaṅghassa bhattaṁ kātuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): He then went to his boss Kira and said, “Sir, I wish to prepare a meal for the Sangha of monks headed by the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.5
Pali: Dehi me vetanan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Please give me my salary.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.6
Pali: Sopi kho kirapatiko saddho hoti pasanno. 
English (brahmali, published): Because Kira also had faith and confidence, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.7
Pali: Atha kho so kirapatiko tassa daliddassa kammakārassa abbhātirekaṁ vetanaṁ adāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): he gave the worker his salary and much extra. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.8
Pali: Atha kho so daliddo kammakāro yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so daliddo kammakāro bhagavantaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards that worker went to the Buddha, bowed, sat down, and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.9
Pali: “adhivāsetu me, bhante, bhagavā svātanāya bhattaṁ saddhiṁ bhikkhusaṅghenā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, please accept a meal from me tomorrow together with the Sangha of monks.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.10
Pali: “Mahā kho, āvuso, bhikkhusaṅgho, jānāhī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “The Sangha is large.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.11
Pali: “Hotu, bhante, mahā bhikkhusaṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): “No problem! 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.12
Pali: Bahū me badarā paṭiyattā badaramissena peyyā paripūrissantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I’ve prepared much jujube, supplemented with jujube drinks.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.13
Pali: Adhivāsesi bhagavā tuṇhībhāvena. 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha consented by remaining silent, and the worker understood. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.14
Pali: Atha kho so daliddo kammakāro bhagavato adhivāsanaṁ viditvā uṭṭhāyāsanā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): He got up from his seat, circumambulated the Buddha with his right side toward him, and left. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.15
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū—“daliddena kira kammakārena svātanāya buddhappamukho bhikkhusaṅgho nimantito, badaramissena peyyā paripūrissantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard that a poor worker had invited the Sangha of monks headed by the Buddha on the following day for a meal supplemented with jujube drinks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.16
Pali: Te kālasseva piṇḍāya caritvā bhuñjiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): And so they ate in the morning after walking for alms. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.17
Pali: Assosuṁ kho manussā—“daliddena kira kammakārena buddhappamukho bhikkhusaṅgho nimantito”ti. Te daliddassa kammakārassa pahūtaṁ khādanīyaṁ bhojanīyaṁ abhihariṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): When people heard that a poor worker had invited the Sangha of monks headed by the Buddha for a meal, they brought much food of various kinds to him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.18
Pali: Atha kho so daliddo kammakāro tassā rattiyā accayena paṇītaṁ khādanīyaṁ bhojanīyaṁ paṭiyādāpetvā bhagavato kālaṁ ārocāpesi—“kālo, bhante, niṭṭhitaṁ bhattan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The following morning that worker prepared his food, and then had the Buddha informed that the meal was ready. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.19
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena tassa daliddassa kammakārassa nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi saddhiṁ bhikkhusaṅghena. 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha robed up, took his bowl and robe and, together with the Sangha of monks, went to the house of that poor worker where he sat down on the prepared seat in the dining hall. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.20
Pali: Atha kho so daliddo kammakāro bhattagge bhikkhū parivisati. Bhikkhū evamāhaṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): The worker served the monks, but they kept saying, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.21
Pali: “thokaṁ, āvuso, dehi. Thokaṁ, āvuso, dehī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Give just a little.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.22
Pali: “Mā kho tumhe, bhante, ‘ayaṁ daliddo kammakāro’ti thokaṁ thokaṁ paṭiggaṇhittha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, don’t accept so little because you think I’m just a poor worker. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.23
Pali: Pahūtaṁ me khādanīyaṁ bhojanīyaṁ paṭiyattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): I’ve prepared much food of various kinds. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.24
Pali: Paṭiggaṇhatha, bhante, yāvadatthan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Please accept as much as you like.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.25
Pali: “Na kho mayaṁ, āvuso, etaṅkāraṇā thokaṁ thokaṁ paṭiggaṇhāma. 
English (brahmali, published): “We’re not accepting so little because of that, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.26
Pali: Api ca, mayaṁ kālasseva piṇḍāya caritvā bhuñjimhā; tena mayaṁ thokaṁ thokaṁ paṭiggaṇhāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): but because we ate in the morning after walking for alms.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.27
Pali: Atha kho so daliddo kammakāro ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—
English (brahmali, published): That poor worker complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.28
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhadantā mayā nimantitā aññatra bhuñjissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the venerables eat elsewhere when invited by me? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.29
Pali: Na cāhaṁ paṭibalo yāvadatthaṁ dātun”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): Am I not able to give as much as they need?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.30
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tassa daliddassa kammakārassa ujjhāyantassa khiyyantassa vipācentassa. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of that worker, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.31
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.32
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhū aññatra nimantitā aññatra bhuñjissantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could those monks eat elsewhere when invited for a meal?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.33
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhū aññatra nimantitā aññatra bhuñjantī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that monks did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.34
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.35
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.36
Pali: kathañhi nāma te, bhikkhave, moghapurisā aññatra nimantitā aññatra bhuñjissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could those foolish men do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.37
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.38
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.39.1
Pali: “Paramparabhojane pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk eats a meal before another, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:1.40
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu gilāno hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards a certain monk became sick. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:2.2
Pali: Aññataro bhikkhu piṇḍapātaṁ ādāya yena so bhikkhu tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca—“bhuñjāhi, āvuso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Another monk took some almsfood, went to that monk, and told him to eat it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:2.3
Pali: “Alaṁ, āvuso, atthi me bhattapaccāsā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I can’t. I’m expecting another meal.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:2.4
Pali: Tassa bhikkhuno piṇḍapāto ussūre āharīyittha. So bhikkhu na cittarūpaṁ bhuñji. 
English (brahmali, published): But since that almsfood only arrived at midday, that monk did not get to eat as much as he had intended. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:2.5
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:2.6
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:2.7
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, gilānena bhikkhunā paramparabhojanaṁ bhuñjituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow a sick monk to eat a meal before another. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:2.8
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:2.9.1
Pali: “Paramparabhojane, aññatra samayā, pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk eats a meal before another, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:2.10
Pali: Tatthāyaṁ samayo. Gilānasamayo—ayaṁ tattha samayo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the appropriate occasion: he is sick.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:2.11
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:3.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena manussā cīvaradānasamaye sacīvarabhattaṁ paṭiyādetvā bhikkhū nimantenti—“bhojetvā cīvarena acchādessāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards, during the robe-giving season, people prepared a meal together with robe-cloth and then invited the monks, saying, “We wish to offer a meal and give robe-cloth.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:3.2
Pali: Bhikkhū kukkuccāyantā nādhivāsenti—“paṭikkhittaṁ bhagavatā paramparabhojanan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): But knowing that the Buddha had prohibited eating a meal before another and being afraid of wrongdoing, they did not accept. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:3.3
Pali: Cīvaraṁ parittaṁ uppajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): As a result, they only got a small amount of robe-cloth. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:3.4
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:3.5
Pali: anujānāmi, bhikkhave, cīvaradānasamaye paramparabhojanaṁ bhuñjituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to eat a meal before another during the robe-giving season. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:3.6
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:3.7.1
Pali: “Paramparabhojane, aññatra samayā, pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk eats a meal before another, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:3.8
Pali: Tatthāyaṁ samayo. Gilānasamayo, cīvaradānasamayo—ayaṁ tattha samayo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): These are the appropriate occasions: he is sick; it is the robe-giving season.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:3.9
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:3.10.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena manussā cīvarakārake bhikkhū bhattena nimantenti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards people invited the robe-making monks for a meal. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:3.11
Pali: Bhikkhū kukkuccāyantā nādhivāsenti—“paṭikkhittaṁ bhagavatā paramparabhojanan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): But knowing that the Buddha had prohibited eating a meal before another and being afraid of wrongdoing, they did not accept. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:3.12
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:3.13
Pali: anujānāmi, bhikkhave, cīvarakārasamaye paramparabhojanaṁ bhuñjituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to eat a meal before another at a time when you are making robes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:3.14
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:3.15.1
Pali: “Paramparabhojane, aññatra samayā, pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk eats a meal before another, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:3.16
Pali: Tatthāyaṁ samayo. Gilānasamayo, cīvaradānasamayo, cīvarakārasamayo—ayaṁ tattha samayo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): These are the appropriate occasions: he is sick; it is the robe-giving season; it is a time of making robes.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:3.17
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:4.1
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya āyasmatā ānandena pacchāsamaṇena yena aññataraṁ kulaṁ tenupasaṅkami; 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards, after robing up in the morning, the Buddha took his bowl and robe and went to a certain family with Venerable Ānanda as his attendant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:4.2
Pali: upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi. Atha kho te manussā bhagavato ca āyasmato ca ānandassa bhojanaṁ adaṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): He sat down on the prepared seat, and the people there gave cooked food. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:4.3
Pali: Āyasmā ānando kukkuccāyanto na paṭiggaṇhāti. 
English (brahmali, published): Being afraid of wrongdoing, Ānanda did not accept it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:4.4
Pali: “Gaṇhāhi, ānandā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha said, “Accept it, Ānanda.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:4.5
Pali: “Alaṁ, bhagavā, atthi me bhattapaccāsā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I can’t, sir, I’m expecting another meal.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:4.6
Pali: “Tenahānanda, vikappetvā gaṇhāhī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Well then, Ānanda, assign that meal to someone else and then receive this.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:4.7
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—“ 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:4.8
Pali: anujānāmi, bhikkhave, vikappetvā paramparabhojanaṁ bhuñjituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to eat a meal before another if you assign the other meal to someone else. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:4.9
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, vikappetabbaṁ—‘mayhaṁ bhattapaccāsaṁ itthannāmassa dammī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): And, monks, this is how it should be assigned: ‘I give my expected meal to so-and-so.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.1.1
Pali: Paramparabhojanaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Eats a meal before another: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.1.2
Pali: pañcannaṁ bhojanānaṁ aññatarena bhojanena nimantito, taṁ ṭhapetvā aññaṁ pañcannaṁ bhojanānaṁ aññataraṁ bhojanaṁ bhuñjati, etaṁ paramparabhojanaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): if he has been invited to eat any of the five cooked foods, and he then eats any of the five cooked foods elsewhere—this is called “eats a meal before another”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.1.3
Pali: Aññatra samayāti 
English (brahmali, published): Except on an appropriate occasion: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.1.4
Pali: ṭhapetvā samayaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): unless it is an appropriate occasion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.1.5
Pali: Gilānasamayo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): He is sick: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.1.6
Pali: na sakkoti ekāsane nisinno yāvadatthaṁ bhuñjituṁ. “Gilānasamayo”ti bhuñjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is not able to eat as much as he needs in one sitting, he may eat a meal before another. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.1.7
Pali: Cīvaradānasamayo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): It is the robe-giving season: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.1.8
Pali: anatthate kathine vassānassa pacchimo māso, atthate kathine pañca māsā. “Cīvaradānasamayo”ti bhuñjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if he has not participated in the robe-making ceremony, he may eat a meal before another during the last month of the rainy season. If he has participated in the robe-making ceremony, he may eat a meal before another during the five month period. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.1.9
Pali: Cīvarakārasamayo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): It is a time of making robes: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.1.10
Pali: cīvare kayiramāne. “Cīvarakārasamayo”ti bhuñjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): when he is making robes, he may eat a meal before another. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.1.11
Pali: “Aññatra samayā bhuñjissāmī”ti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he receives food with the intention of eating it, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.1.12
Pali: Ajjhohāre ajjhohāre āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): For every mouthful swallowed, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.2.1
Pali: Paramparabhojane paramparabhojanasaññī, aññatra samayā, bhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a meal before another, and he perceives it as such, and he eats it, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.2.2
Pali: Paramparabhojane vematiko, aññatra samayā, bhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a meal before another, but he is unsure of it, and he eats it, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.2.3
Pali: Paramparabhojane naparamparabhojanasaññī, aññatra samayā, bhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a meal before another, but he does not perceive it as such, and he eats it, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.2.4
Pali: Naparamparabhojane paramparabhojanasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a meal before another, but he perceives it as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.2.5
Pali: Naparamparabhojane vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a meal before another, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.2.6
Pali: Naparamparabhojane naparamparabhojanasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a meal before another, and he does not perceive it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.3.2
Pali: samaye, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is an appropriate occasion; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.3.3
Pali: vikappetvā bhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he assigns his other meal to someone else and then eats; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.3.4
Pali: dve tayo nimantane ekato bhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he eats the food from two or three invitational meals together; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.3.5
Pali: nimantanapaṭipāṭiyā bhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he eats the meals in the same order that the invitations were received; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.3.6
Pali: sakalena gāmena nimantito tasmiṁ gāme yattha katthaci bhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is invited by a whole village and he eats anywhere in that village; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.3.7
Pali: sakalena pūgena nimantito tasmiṁ pūge yattha katthaci bhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is invited by a whole association and he eats anywhere that belongs to that association; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.3.8
Pali: nimantiyamāno bhikkhaṁ gahessāmīti bhaṇati, 
English (brahmali, published): if, when being invited, he says, “I’ll get almsfood;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.3.9
Pali: niccabhatte, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a regular meal invitation; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.3.10
Pali: salākabhatte, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a meal for which lots are drawn; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.3.11
Pali: pakkhike, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a half-monthly meal; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.3.12
Pali: uposathike, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is on the observance day; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.3.13
Pali: pāṭipadike, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is on the day after the observance day; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.3.14
Pali: pañca bhojanāni ṭhapetvā sabbattha anāpatti, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is anything apart from the five cooked foods; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.3.15
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.3.16
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc33:5.3.17
Pali: Paramparabhojanasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ tatiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on eating meals in succession, the third, is finished.

English (brahmali, published): 34. Accepting more than three bowlfuls 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena kāṇamātā upāsikā saddhā hoti pasannā. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time there was a female lay follower Kāṇamātā who had faith and confidence. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.3
Pali: Kāṇā gāmake aññatarassa purisassa dinnā hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): She had a daughter, Kāṇā, who had been given in marriage to a man in a certain village. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.4
Pali: Atha kho kāṇā mātugharaṁ agamāsi kenacideva karaṇīyena. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion Kāṇā went to her mother’s house on some business. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.5
Pali: Atha kho kāṇāya sāmiko kāṇāya santike dūtaṁ pāhesi—“āgacchatu kāṇā, icchāmi kāṇāya āgatan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Kāṇā’s husband sent her a message: “Please come, Kāṇā, I want you back.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.6
Pali: Atha kho kāṇamātā upāsikā “kismiṁ viya rittahatthaṁ gantun”ti pūvaṁ paci. 
English (brahmali, published): Kāṇamātā thought, “It’s shameful to go empty-handed,” and she baked some cookies. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.7
Pali: Pakke pūve aññataro piṇḍacāriko bhikkhu kāṇamātāya upāsikāya nivesanaṁ pāvisi. Atha kho kāṇamātā upāsikā tassa bhikkhuno pūvaṁ dāpesi. 
English (brahmali, published): Just when the cookies were finished, an alms-collecting monk entered Kāṇamātā’s house, and she gave him some. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.8
Pali: So nikkhamitvā aññassa ācikkhi. Tassapi pūvaṁ dāpesi. 
English (brahmali, published): After leaving, he told another monk, and he too was given cookies. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.9
Pali: So nikkhamitvā aññassa ācikkhi. Tassapi pūvaṁ dāpesi. 
English (brahmali, published): And the same happened a third time. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.10
Pali: Yathāpaṭiyattaṁ pūvaṁ parikkhayaṁ agamāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): At that, all the cookies were gone. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.11
Pali: Dutiyampi kho kāṇāya sāmiko kāṇāya santike dūtaṁ pāhesi—“āgacchatu kāṇā, icchāmi kāṇāya āgatan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): A second time Kāṇā’s husband sent her the same message, and everything unfolded as before. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.12
Pali: Dutiyampi kho kāṇamātā upāsikā “kismiṁ viya rittahatthaṁ gantun”ti pūvaṁ paci. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.13
Pali: Pakke pūve aññataro piṇḍacāriko bhikkhu kāṇamātāya upāsikāya nivesanaṁ pāvisi. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.14
Pali: Atha kho kāṇamātā upāsikā tassa bhikkhuno pūvaṁ dāpesi. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.15
Pali: So nikkhamitvā aññassa ācikkhi. Tassapi pūvaṁ dāpesi. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.16
Pali: So nikkhamitvā aññassa ācikkhi. Tassapi pūvaṁ dāpesi. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.17
Pali: Yathāpaṭiyattaṁ pūvaṁ parikkhayaṁ agamāsi. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.18
Pali: Tatiyampi kho kāṇāya sāmiko kāṇāya santike dūtaṁ pāhesi—“āgacchatu kāṇā, icchāmi kāṇāya āgataṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): A third time he sent the same message, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.19
Pali: Sace kāṇā nāgacchissati, ahaṁ aññaṁ pajāpatiṁ ānessāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): adding, “If Kāṇā doesn’t come, I’ll find another wife.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.20
Pali: Tatiyampi kho kāṇamātā upāsikā kismiṁ viya rittahatthaṁ gantunti pūvaṁ paci. 
English (brahmali, published): But once again all the cookies were given to monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.21
Pali: Pakke pūve aññataro piṇḍacāriko bhikkhu kāṇamātāya upāsikāya nivesanaṁ pāvisi. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.22
Pali: Atha kho kāṇamātā upāsikā tassa bhikkhuno pūvaṁ dāpesi. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.23
Pali: So nikkhamitvā aññassa ācikkhi. Tassapi pūvaṁ dāpesi. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.24
Pali: So nikkhamitvā aññassa ācikkhi. Tassapi pūvaṁ dāpesi. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.25
Pali: Yathāpaṭiyattaṁ pūvaṁ parikkhayaṁ agamāsi. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.26
Pali: Atha kho kāṇāya sāmiko aññaṁ pajāpatiṁ ānesi. 
English (brahmali, published): Kāṇā’s husband found another wife, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.27
Pali: Assosi kho kāṇā—“tena kira purisena aññā pajāpati ānītā”ti. Sā rodantī aṭṭhāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): and when Kāṇā heard what had happened, she cried. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.28
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena kāṇamātāya upāsikāya nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards, after robing up in the morning, the Buddha took his bowl and robe and went to Kāṇamātā’s house where he sat down on the prepared seat. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.29
Pali: Atha kho kāṇamātā upāsikā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): Kāṇamātā approached the Buddha, bowed, and sat down. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.30
Pali: Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho kāṇamātaraṁ upāsikaṁ bhagavā etadavoca—“kissāyaṁ kāṇā rodatī”ti? Atha kho kāṇamātā upāsikā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha asked her why Kāṇā was crying, and she told him what had happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.1.31
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā kāṇamātaraṁ upāsikaṁ dhammiyā kathāya sandassetvā samādapetvā samuttejetvā sampahaṁsetvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): After instructing, inspiring, and gladdening her with a teaching, the Buddha got up from his seat and left. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro sattho rājagahā paṭiyālokaṁ gantukāmo hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards a certain caravan was ready to go south from Rājagaha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.2.2
Pali: Aññataro piṇḍacāriko bhikkhu taṁ satthaṁ piṇḍāya pāvisi. 
English (brahmali, published): An alms-collecting monk went up to that caravan to get almsfood, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.2.3
Pali: Aññataro upāsako tassa bhikkhuno sattuṁ dāpesi. 
English (brahmali, published): and a lay follower gave him flour products. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.2.4
Pali: So nikkhamitvā aññassa ācikkhi. Tassapi sattuṁ dāpesi. 
English (brahmali, published): After leaving, that monk told another monk, and he too was given flour products. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.2.5
Pali: So nikkhamitvā aññassa ācikkhi. Tassapi sattuṁ dāpesi. 
English (brahmali, published): And the same happened a third time. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.2.6
Pali: Yathāpaṭiyattaṁ pātheyyaṁ parikkhayaṁ agamāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): At that, all his provisions were gone. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.2.7
Pali: Atha kho so upāsako te manusse etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): That lay follower said to the other people in the caravan, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.2.8
Pali: “ajjaṇho, ayyā, āgametha, yathāpaṭiyattaṁ pātheyyaṁ ayyānaṁ dinnaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sirs, please wait one day. I’ve given my provisions to the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.2.9
Pali: Pātheyyaṁ paṭiyādessāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I need to prepare more.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.2.10
Pali: “Nāyyo sakkā āgametuṁ, payāto sattho”ti agamaṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): “We can’t wait. The caravan is already on its way.” And they left. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.2.11
Pali: Atha kho tassa upāsakassa pātheyyaṁ paṭiyādetvā pacchā gacchantassa corā acchindiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): After preparing provisions, that lay follower followed after the caravan, but he was robbed by thieves. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.2.12
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.2.13
Pali: “kathañhi nāma samaṇā sakyaputtiyā na mattaṁ jānitvā paṭiggahessanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the Sakyan monastics receive without moderation? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.2.14
Pali: Ayaṁ imesaṁ datvā pacchā gacchanto corehi acchinno”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): This man gave to them, and then because he was following after the caravan he was robbed by thieves.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.2.15
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those people 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.2.16
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): and they told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.2.17
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards he gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.2.18
Pali: “tena hi, bhikkhave, bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññapessāmi dasa atthavase paṭicca—
English (brahmali, published): “Well then, monks, I will lay down a training rule for the following ten reasons: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.2.19
Pali: saṅghasuṭṭhutāya, saṅghaphāsutāya …pe… vinayānuggahāya. 
English (brahmali, published): for the well-being of the Sangha, for the comfort of the Sangha, for the restraint of bad people, for the ease of good monks, for the restraint of the corruptions relating to the present life, for the restraint of the corruptions relating to future lives, to give rise to confidence in those without it, to increase the confidence of those who have it, for the longevity of the true Teaching, and for supporting the training. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.2.20
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.2.21.1
Pali: “Bhikkhuṁ paneva kulaṁ upagataṁ pūvehi vā manthehi vā abhihaṭṭhuṁ pavāreyya, ākaṅkhamānena bhikkhunā dvattipattapūrā paṭiggahetabbā tato ce uttari paṭiggaṇheyya pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk goes to a family and is invited to take cookies or crackers, he may accept two or three bowlfuls if he wishes. If he accepts more than that, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:1.2.22
Pali: Dvattipattapūre paṭiggahetvā tato nīharitvā bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ saṁvibhajitabbaṁ. Ayaṁ tattha sāmīcī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he accepts two or three bowlfuls, he should take it away and share it with the monks. This is the proper procedure.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.1.1
Pali: Bhikkhuṁ paneva kulaṁ upagatanti 
English (brahmali, published): A monk goes to a family: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.1.2
Pali: kulaṁ nāma cattāri kulāni—khattiyakulaṁ, brāhmaṇakulaṁ, vessakulaṁ, suddakulaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): a family: there are four kinds of families: the aristocratic family, the brahmin family, the merchant family, the worker family. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.1.3
Pali: Upagatanti 
English (brahmali, published): Goes to: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.1.4
Pali: tattha gataṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): he has gone there. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.1.5
Pali: Pūvaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Cookies: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.1.6
Pali: yaṁ kiñci paheṇakatthāya paṭiyattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): whatever has been prepared for the purpose of sending away. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.1.7
Pali: Manthaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Crackers: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.1.8
Pali: yaṁ kiñci pātheyyatthāya paṭiyattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): whatever has been prepared as provisions for a journey. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.1.9
Pali: Abhihaṭṭhuṁ pavāreyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Is invited to take: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.1.10
Pali: yāvatakaṁ icchasi tāvatakaṁ gaṇhāhīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Take as much as you like.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.1.11
Pali: Ākaṅkhamānenāti 
English (brahmali, published): If he wishes: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.1.12
Pali: icchamānena. 
English (brahmali, published): if he desires. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.1.13
Pali: Dvattipattapūrā paṭiggahetabbāti 
English (brahmali, published): He may accept two or three bowlfuls: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.1.14
Pali: dvetayo pattapūrā paṭiggahetabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): two or three bowlfuls can be accepted. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.1.15
Pali: Tato ce uttari paṭigaṇheyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): If he accepts more than that: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.1.16
Pali: tatuttari paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when he accepts more than that, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.1.17
Pali: Dvattipattapūre paṭiggahetvā 
English (brahmali, published): If he accepts two or three bowlfuls: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.1.18
Pali: tato nikkhamantena bhikkhuṁ passitvā ācikkhitabbaṁ—“amutra mayā dvattipattapūrā paṭiggahitā, mā kho tattha paṭiggaṇhī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): leaving that place and seeing a monk, he should tell him, “I’ve accepted two or three bowlfuls from such-and-such a place; don’t accept anything from there.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.1.19
Pali: Sace passitvā na ācikkhati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he sees a monk, but does not tell him, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.1.20
Pali: Sace ācikkhite paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the one who is told accepts from there regardless, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.1.21
Pali: Tato nīharitvā bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ saṁvibhajitabbanti 
English (brahmali, published): He should take it away and share it with the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.1.22
Pali: paṭikkamanaṁ nīharitvā saṁvibhajitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): he should take it away when returning from almsround and then share it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.1.23
Pali: Ayaṁ tattha sāmīcīti 
English (brahmali, published): This is the proper procedure: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.1.24
Pali: ayaṁ tattha anudhammatā. 
English (brahmali, published): this is the right method. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.2.1
Pali: Atirekadvattipattapūre atirekasaññī paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than two or three bowlfuls, and he perceives it as more, and he accepts it, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.2.2
Pali: Atirekadvattipattapūre vematiko paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than two or three bowlfuls, but he is unsure of it, and he accepts it, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.2.3
Pali: Atirekadvattipattapūre ūnakasaññī paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than two or three bowlfuls, but he perceives it as less, and he accepts it, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.2.4
Pali: Ūnakadvattipattapūre atirekasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than two or three bowlfuls, but he perceives it as more, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.2.5
Pali: Ūnakadvattipattapūre vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than two or three bowlfuls, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.2.6
Pali: Ūnakadvattipattapūre ūnakasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than two or three bowlfuls, and he perceives it as less, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.3.2
Pali: dvattipattapūre paṭiggaṇhāti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he accepts two or three bowlfuls; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.3.3
Pali: ūnakadvattipattapūre paṭiggaṇhāti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he accepts less than two or three bowlfuls; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.3.4
Pali: na paheṇakatthāya na pātheyyatthāya paṭiyattaṁ denti, 
English (brahmali, published): if they give anything that has not been prepared for sending away or as provisions for a journey; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.3.5
Pali: paheṇakatthāya vā pātheyyatthāya vā paṭiyattasesakaṁ denti, 
English (brahmali, published): if they give the leftovers from what was prepared for sending away or as provisions for a journey; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.3.6
Pali: gamane paṭippassaddhe denti, 
English (brahmali, published): if they give after the plans to travel have been canceled; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.3.7
Pali: ñātakānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is from relatives; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.3.8
Pali: pavāritānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is from those who have given an invitation; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.3.9
Pali: aññassatthāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is for the benefit of someone else; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.3.10
Pali: attano dhanena, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is by means of his own property; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.3.11
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.3.12
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc34:2.3.13
Pali: Kāṇamātusikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ catutthaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on accepting more than three bowlfuls, the fourth, is finished.
