Pali: 35. Pavāraṇāsikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 35. Eating more after refusing an invitation 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro brāhmaṇo bhikkhū nimantetvā bhojesi. 
English (brahmali, published): a certain brahmin had invited the monks for a meal. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:1.3
Pali: Bhikkhū bhuttāvī pavāritā ñātikulāni gantvā ekacce bhuñjiṁsu ekacce piṇḍapātaṁ ādāya agamaṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): When the monks had finished and refused an invitation to eat more, they went to their respective families, where some ate and some took away almsfood. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:1.4
Pali: Atha kho so brāhmaṇo paṭivissake etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards that brahmin said to his neighbors, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:1.5
Pali: “bhikkhū mayā, ayyā, santappitā. 
English (brahmali, published): “The monks have been satisfied by me. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:1.6
Pali: Etha, tumhepi santappessāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Come, and I’ll satisfy you, too.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:1.7
Pali: Te evamāhaṁsu—“kiṁ tvaṁ, ayyo, amhe santappessasi? 
English (brahmali, published): “How could you satisfy us? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:1.8
Pali: Yepi tayā nimantitā tepi amhākaṁ gharāni āgantvā ekacce bhuñjiṁsu ekacce piṇḍapātaṁ ādāya agamaṁsū”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Those monks who were invited by you came to our houses. Some of them ate there and some took away almsfood.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:1.9
Pali: Atha kho so brāhmaṇo ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—
English (brahmali, published): That brahmin complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:1.10
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhadantā amhākaṁ ghare bhuñjitvā aññatra bhuñjissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the venerables eat in our house and afterwards eat elsewhere? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:1.11
Pali: Na cāhaṁ paṭibalo yāvadatthaṁ dātun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Am I not able to give them as much as they need?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:1.12
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tassa brāhmaṇassa ujjhāyantassa khiyyantassa vipācentassa. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of that brahmin, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:1.13
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:1.14
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhū bhuttāvī pavāritā aññatra bhuñjissantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could those monks finish their meal and refuse an invitation to eat more, and then eat elsewhere?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:1.15
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhū bhuttāvī pavāritā aññatra bhuñjantī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that monks did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:1.16
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:1.17
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:1.18
Pali: kathañhi nāma te, bhikkhave, moghapurisā bhuttāvī pavāritā aññatra bhuñjissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could those foolish men do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:1.19
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:1.20
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:1.21.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu bhuttāvī pavārito khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā khādeyya vā bhuñjeyya vā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk has finished his meal and refused an invitation to eat more, and then eats fresh or cooked food, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:1.22
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū gilānānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ paṇīte piṇḍapāte nīharanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the monks were bringing back fine almsfood for the sick monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:2.2
Pali: Gilānā na cittarūpaṁ bhuñjanti. 
English (brahmali, published): But because the sick monks were unable to eat as much as they had intended, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:2.3
Pali: Tāni bhikkhū chaṭṭenti. 
English (brahmali, published): the monks threw the leftovers away. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:2.4
Pali: Assosi kho bhagavā uccāsaddaṁ mahāsaddaṁ kākoravasaddaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): When the Buddha heard the loud sound of crows cawing, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:2.5
Pali: Sutvāna āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): he asked Venerable Ānanda, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:2.6
Pali: “kiṁ nu kho so, ānanda, uccāsaddo mahāsaddo kākoravasaddo”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Ānanda, why is there this loud sound of crows cawing?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:2.7
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā ānando bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): Ānanda told him what had happened, and the Buddha said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:2.8
Pali: “Bhuñjeyyuṁ panānanda, bhikkhū gilānātirittan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “But Ānanda, don’t the monks eat the leftovers from those who are sick?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:2.9
Pali: “Na bhuñjeyyuṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “No, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:2.10
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:2.11
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, gilānassa ca agilānassa ca atirittaṁ bhuñjituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to eat the leftovers both from those who are sick and from those who are not sick. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:2.12
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, atirittaṁ kātabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): And, monks, this is how you make food left over: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:2.13
Pali: ‘alametaṁ sabban’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘I’ve had enough.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:2.14
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:2.15.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu bhuttāvī pavārito anatirittaṁ khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā khādeyya vā bhuñjeyya vā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk has finished his meal and refused an invitation to eat more, and then eats fresh or cooked food that is not left over, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.5
Pali: Bhuttāvī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Has finished his meal: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.6
Pali: pañcannaṁ bhojanānaṁ aññataraṁ bhojanaṁ antamaso kusaggenapi bhuttaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): has eaten any of the five cooked foods, even what fits on the tip of a blade of grass. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.7
Pali: Pavārito nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Refused an invitation to eat more: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.8
Pali: asanaṁ paññāyati, bhojanaṁ paññāyati, hatthapāse ṭhito abhiharati, paṭikkhepo paññāyati. 
English (brahmali, published): there is eating; there is cooked food; they stand within arm’s reach; there is an offering; there is a refusal. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.9
Pali: Anatirittaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Not left over: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.10
Pali: akappiyakataṁ hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): the making it left over is done with food that is unallowable; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.11
Pali: appaṭiggahitakataṁ hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): it is done with food that has not been received; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.12
Pali: anuccāritakataṁ hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): it is done with food that is not held in hand; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.13
Pali: ahatthapāse kataṁ hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): it is done by one who is not within arm’s reach; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.14
Pali: abhuttāvinā kataṁ hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): it is done by one who has not finished his meal; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.15
Pali: bhuttāvinā pavāritena āsanā vuṭṭhitena kataṁ hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): it is done by one who has finished his meal, who has refused an invitation to eat more, but who has risen from his seat; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.16
Pali: “alametaṁ sabban”ti avuttaṁ hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): “I’ve had enough,” has not been said; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.17
Pali: na gilānātirittaṁ hoti—
English (brahmali, published): it is not left over from one who is sick—

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.18
Pali: etaṁ anatirittaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): this is called “not left over”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.19
Pali: Atirittaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Left over: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.20
Pali: kappiyakataṁ hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): the making it left over is done with food that is allowable; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.21
Pali: paṭiggahitakataṁ hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): it is done with food that has been received; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.22
Pali: uccāritakataṁ hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): it is done with food that is held in hand; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.23
Pali: hatthapāse kataṁ hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): it is done by one who is within arm’s reach; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.24
Pali: bhuttāvinā kataṁ hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): it is done by one who has finished his meal; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.25
Pali: bhuttāvinā pavāritena āsanā avuṭṭhitena kataṁ hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): it is done by one who has finished his meal, who has refused an invitation to eat more, and who has not risen from his seat; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.26
Pali: “alametaṁ sabban”ti vuttaṁ hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): “I’ve had enough,” has been said; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.27
Pali: gilānātirittaṁ hoti—
English (brahmali, published): it is left over from one who is sick—

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.28
Pali: etaṁ atirittaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): this is called “left over”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.29
Pali: Khādanīyaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Fresh food: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.30
Pali: pañca bhojanāni—yāmakālikaṁ sattāhakālikaṁ yāvajīvikaṁ ṭhapetvā avasesaṁ khādanīyaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): apart from the five cooked foods, the post-midday tonics, the seven-day tonics, and the lifetime tonics, the rest is called “fresh food”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.31
Pali: Bhojanīyaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Cooked food: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.32
Pali: pañca bhojanāni— odano, kummāso, sattu, maccho, maṁsaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): there are five kinds of cooked food: cooked grain, porridge, flour products, fish, and meat. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.1.33
Pali: “Khādissāmi bhuñjissāmī”ti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa ajjhohāre ajjhohāre āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he receives it intending to eat it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. For every mouthful swallowed, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.2.1
Pali: Anatiritte anatirittasaññī khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā khādati vā bhuñjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not left over, and he does not perceive it as such, and he eats fresh or cooked food, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.2.2
Pali: Anatiritte vematiko khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā khādati vā bhuñjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not left over, but he is unsure of it, and he eats fresh or cooked food, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.2.3
Pali: Anatiritte atirittasaññī khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā khādati vā bhuñjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not left over, but he perceives it as such, and he eats fresh or cooked food, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.2.4
Pali: Yāmakālikaṁ sattāhakālikaṁ yāvajīvikaṁ āhāratthāya paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he receives post-midday tonics, seven-day tonics, or lifetime tonics for the purpose of food, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.2.5
Pali: Ajjhohāre ajjhohāre āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): For every mouthful swallowed, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.2.6
Pali: Atiritte anatirittasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is left over, but he does not perceive it as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.2.7
Pali: Atiritte vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is left over, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.2.8
Pali: Atiritte atirittasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is left over, and he perceives it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.3.2
Pali: atirittaṁ kārāpetvā bhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he eats it after having it made left over; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.3.3
Pali: “atirittaṁ kārāpetvā bhuñjissāmī”ti paṭiggaṇhāti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he receives it with the intention of having it made left over and then eating it; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.3.4
Pali: aññassatthāya haranto gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is taking food for the benefit of someone else; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.3.5
Pali: gilānassa sesakaṁ bhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he eats the leftovers from a sick person; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.3.6
Pali: yāmakālikaṁ sattāhakālikaṁ yāvajīvikaṁ sati paccaye paribhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if, when there is a reason, he uses post-midday tonics, seven-day tonics, or lifetime tonics; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.3.7
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.3.8
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc35:3.3.9
Pali: Pavāraṇāsikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ pañcamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on eating more after refusing an invitation, the fifth, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:0.4
Pali: Bhojanavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on eating 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:0.5
Pali: 36. Dutiyapavāraṇāsikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 36. Encouraging a monastic to eat after refusing an invitation 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena dve bhikkhū kosalesu janapade sāvatthiṁ addhānamaggappaṭipannā honti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time two monks were traveling through the Kosalan country on their way to Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:1.3
Pali: Eko bhikkhu anācāraṁ ācarati. 
English (brahmali, published): One monk misbehaved 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:1.4
Pali: Dutiyo bhikkhu taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): and the other said to him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:1.5
Pali: “māvuso, evarūpamakāsi, netaṁ kappatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Don’t do that! It’s not allowable.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:1.6
Pali: So tasmiṁ upanandhi. 
English (brahmali, published): Because of that the first monk became resentful. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:1.7
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū sāvatthiṁ agamaṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): They then carried on to Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:1.8
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sāvatthiyaṁ aññatarassa pūgassa saṅghabhattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards an association in Sāvatthī was offering a meal to the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:1.9
Pali: Dutiyo bhikkhu bhuttāvī pavārito hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): When the second monk had finished his meal and refused an invitation to eat more, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:1.10
Pali: Upanaddho bhikkhu ñātikulaṁ gantvā piṇḍapātaṁ ādāya yena so bhikkhu tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): the resentful monk brought back some almsfood from his own family. He then said to the other, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:1.11
Pali: “bhuñjāhi, āvuso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please eat!” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:1.12
Pali: “Alaṁ, āvuso, paripuṇṇomhī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “There’s no need. I’m full.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:1.13
Pali: “Sundaro, āvuso, piṇḍapāto, bhuñjāhī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “The almsfood is nice, please eat.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:1.14
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu tena bhikkhunā nippīḷiyamāno taṁ piṇḍapātaṁ bhuñji. 
English (brahmali, published): And because he was pressured, he ate the almsfood. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:1.15
Pali: Upanaddho bhikkhu taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): The resentful monk then said to him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:1.16
Pali: “tvampi nāma, āvuso, maṁ vattabbaṁ maññasi yaṁ tvaṁ bhuttāvī pavārito anatirittaṁ bhojanaṁ bhuñjasī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Who are you to correct me when you eat food that’s not left over even though you have finished your meal and refused an invitation to eat more?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:1.17
Pali: “Nanu, āvuso, ācikkhitabban”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Shouldn’t you have told me?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:1.18
Pali: “Nanu, āvuso, pucchitabban”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Shouldn’t you have asked?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:1.19
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): The second monk told the monks what had happened, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:1.20
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized the other, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:1.21
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhu bhikkhuṁ bhuttāviṁ pavāritaṁ anatirittena bhojanena abhihaṭṭhuṁ pavāressatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could a monk invite another monk to eat food that’s not left over, when the other has finished his meal and refused an invitation to eat more?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:1.22
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, bhikkhu, bhikkhuṁ bhuttāviṁ pavāritaṁ anatirittena bhojanena abhihaṭṭhuṁ pavāresī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monk, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:1.23
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:1.24
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:1.25
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, bhikkhuṁ bhuttāviṁ pavāritaṁ anatirittena bhojanena abhihaṭṭhuṁ pavāressasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:1.26
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:1.27
Pali: evañca pana bhikkhave imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:1.28.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhuṁ bhuttāviṁ pavāritaṁ anatirittena khādanīyena vā bhojanīyena vā abhihaṭṭhuṁ pavāreyya—‘handa, bhikkhu, khāda vā bhuñja vā’ti, jānaṁ āsādanāpekkho, bhuttasmiṁ, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk invites a monk, whom he knows has finished his meal and refused an invitation to eat more, to eat fresh or cooked food that is not left over, saying, “Here, monk, eat,” aiming to criticize him, then when the other has eaten, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.5
Pali: Bhikkhunti 
English (brahmali, published): A monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.6
Pali: aññaṁ bhikkhuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): another monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.7
Pali: Bhuttāvī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Whom has finished his meal: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.8
Pali: pañcannaṁ bhojanānaṁ aññataraṁ bhojanaṁ, antamaso kusaggenapi bhuttaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): whom has eaten any of the five cooked foods, even what fits on the tip of a blade of grass. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.9
Pali: Pavārito nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Refused an invitation to eat more: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.10
Pali: asanaṁ paññāyati, bhojanaṁ paññāyati, hatthapāse ṭhito abhiharati, paṭikkhepo paññāyati. 
English (brahmali, published): there is eating; there is cooked food; they stand within arm’s reach; there is an offering; there is a refusal. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.11
Pali: Anatirittaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Not left over: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.12
Pali: akappiyakataṁ hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): the making it left over is done with food that is unallowable; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.13
Pali: appaṭiggahitakataṁ hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): it is done with food that has not been received; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.14
Pali: anuccāritakataṁ hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): it is done with food that is not held in hand; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.15
Pali: ahatthapāse kataṁ hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): it is done by one who is not within arm’s reach; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.16
Pali: abhuttāvinā kataṁ hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): it is done by one who has not finished his meal; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.17
Pali: bhuttāvinā pavāritena āsanā vuṭṭhitena kataṁ hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): it is done by one who has finished his meal, who has refused an invitation to eat more, but who has risen from his seat; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.18
Pali: “alametaṁ sabban”ti avuttaṁ hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): “I’ve had enough,” has not been said; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.19
Pali: na gilānātirittaṁ hoti—
English (brahmali, published): it is not left over from one who is sick—

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.20
Pali: etaṁ anatirittaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): this is called “not left over”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.21
Pali: Khādanīyaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Fresh food: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.22
Pali: pañca bhojanāni—yāmakālikaṁ sattāhakālikaṁ yāvajīvikaṁ ṭhapetvā avasesaṁ khādanīyaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): apart from the five cooked foods, the post-midday tonics, the seven-day tonics, and the lifetime tonics, the rest is called “fresh food”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.23
Pali: Bhojanīyaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Cooked food: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.24
Pali: pañca bhojanāni—odano, kummāso, sattu, maccho, maṁsaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): there are five kinds of cooked food: cooked grain, porridge, flour products, fish, and meat. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.25
Pali: Abhihaṭṭhuṁ pavāreyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Invites to eat: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.26
Pali: yāvatakaṁ icchasi tāvatakaṁ gaṇhāhīti. 
English (brahmali, published): saying, “Take as much as you like.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.27
Pali: Jānāti nāma 
English (brahmali, published): He knows: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.28
Pali: sāmaṁ vā jānāti, aññe vā tassa ārocenti, so vā āroceti. 
English (brahmali, published): he knows by himself or others have told him or the monk has told him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.29
Pali: Āsādanāpekkhoti 
English (brahmali, published): Aiming to criticize him: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.30
Pali: “iminā imaṁ codessāmi sāressāmi paṭicodessāmi paṭisāressāmi maṅkuṁ karissāmī”ti abhiharati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he offers it to him, thinking, “With this I’ll accuse him,” “I’ll remind him,” “I’ll counter accuse him,” “I’ll counter remind him,” “I’ll humiliate him,” he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.31
Pali: Tassa vacanena “khādissāmi bhuñjissāmī”ti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, because of what he says, the other monk receives it with the intention of eating it, then the donor commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.32
Pali: Ajjhohāre ajjhohāre āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): For every mouthful swallowed, the donor commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.1.33
Pali: Bhojanapariyosāne āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When the other monk has finished eating, the donor commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.2.1
Pali: Pavārite pavāritasaññī anatirittena khādanīyena vā bhojanīyena vā abhihaṭṭhuṁ pavāreti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other monk has refused an invitation to eat more, and the donor perceives that he has, and he invites him to eat fresh or cooked food that is not left over, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.2.2
Pali: Pavārite vematiko anatirittena khādanīyena vā bhojanīyena vā abhihaṭṭhuṁ pavāreti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other monk has refused an invitation to eat more, but the donor is unsure of it, and he invites him to eat fresh or cooked food that is not left over, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.2.3
Pali: Pavārite appavāritasaññī anatirittena khādanīyena vā bhojanīyena vā abhihaṭṭhuṁ pavāreti, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other monk has refused an invitation to eat more, but the donor does not perceive that he has, and he invites him to eat fresh or cooked food that is not left over, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.2.4
Pali: Yāmakālikaṁ sattāhakālikaṁ yāvajīvikaṁ āhāratthāya abhiharati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he invites him to eat post-midday tonics, seven-day tonics, or lifetime tonics for the purpose of food, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.2.5
Pali: Tassa vacanena “khādissāmi bhuñjissāmī”ti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, because of what he says, the other monk receives it with the intention of eating it, then the donor commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.2.6
Pali: Ajjhohāre ajjhohāre āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): For every mouthful swallowed, the donor commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.2.7
Pali: Appavārite pavāritasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other monk has not refused an invitation to eat more, but the donor perceives that he has, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.2.8
Pali: Appavārite vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other monk has not refused an invitation to eat more, but the donor is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.2.9
Pali: Appavārite appavāritasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other monk has not refused an invitation to eat more, and the donor does not perceive that he has, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.3.2
Pali: atirittaṁ kārāpetvā deti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he gives it after having it made left over; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.3.3
Pali: “atirittaṁ kārāpetvā bhuñjāhī”ti deti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he gives it, saying, “Have it made left over and then eat it;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.3.4
Pali: aññassatthāya haranto gacchāhīti deti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he gives it, saying, “Take this food for the benefit of someone else;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.3.5
Pali: gilānassa sesakaṁ deti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he gives the leftovers from a sick person; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.3.6
Pali: “yāmakālikaṁ sattāhakālikaṁ yāvajīvikaṁ sati paccaye paribhuñjā”ti deti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he gives, saying, “When there’s a reason, use these post-midday tonics,” “… use these seven-day tonics,” “… use  these lifetime tonics;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.3.7
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.3.8
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc36:2.3.9
Pali: Dutiyapavāraṇāsikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ chaṭṭhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The second training rule on encouraging a monastic to eat after refusing an invitation, the sixth, is finished.

Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis

Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:0.4
Pali: Bhojanavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on eating 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:0.5
Pali: 37. Vikālabhojanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 37. Eating at the wrong time 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Rājagaha in the Bamboo Grove, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena rājagahe giraggasamajjo hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): there was a hilltop fair in Rājagaha, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:1.3
Pali: Sattarasavaggiyā bhikkhū giraggasamajjaṁ dassanāya agamaṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): which the monks from the group of seventeen went to see. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:1.4
Pali: Manussā sattarasavaggiye bhikkhū passitvā nahāpetvā vilimpetvā bhojetvā khādanīyaṁ adaṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): When people saw the monks, they bathed them, anointed them, fed them cooked food, and gave them fresh food. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:1.5
Pali: Sattarasavaggiyā bhikkhū khādanīyaṁ ādāya ārāmaṁ gantvā chabbaggiye bhikkhū etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): They took that food and brought it back to the monastery. And they said to the monks from the group of six, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:1.6
Pali: “gaṇhāthāvuso, khādanīyaṁ khādathā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Help yourselves!” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:1.7
Pali: “Kuto tumhehi, āvuso, khādanīyaṁ laddhan”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “But where did you get this food?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:1.8
Pali: Sattarasavaggiyā bhikkhū chabbaggiyānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): And they told them what had happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:1.9
Pali: “Kiṁ pana tumhe, āvuso, vikāle bhojanaṁ bhuñjathā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “So, do you eat at the wrong time?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:1.10
Pali: “Evamāvuso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:1.11
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhū ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks from the group of six complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:1.12
Pali: “kathañhi nāma sattarasavaggiyā bhikkhū vikāle bhojanaṁ bhuñjissantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can those monks from the group of seventeen eat at the wrong time?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:1.13
Pali: Atha kho chabbaggiyā bhikkhū bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They told the monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:1.14
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:1.15
Pali: “kathañhi nāma sattarasavaggiyā bhikkhū vikāle bhojanaṁ bhuñjissantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the monks from the group of seventeen eat at the wrong time?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:1.16
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, vikāle bhojanaṁ bhuñjathā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:1.17
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:1.18
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:1.19
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, vikāle bhojanaṁ bhuñjissatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:1.20
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:1.21
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:1.22.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu vikāle khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā khādeyya vā bhuñjeyya vā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk eats fresh or cooked food at the wrong time, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:2.1.5
Pali: Vikālo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): At the wrong time: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:2.1.6
Pali: majjhanhike vītivatte yāva aruṇuggamanā. 
English (brahmali, published): when the middle of the day has passed, until dawn. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:2.1.7
Pali: Khādanīyaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Fresh food: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:2.1.8
Pali: pañca bhojanāni—yāmakālikaṁ sattāhakālikaṁ yāvajīvikaṁ ṭhapetvā avasesaṁ khādanīyaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): apart from the five cooked foods, the post-midday tonics, the seven-day tonics, and the lifetime tonics, the rest is called “fresh food”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:2.1.9
Pali: Bhojanīyaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Cooked food: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:2.1.10
Pali: pañca bhojanāni—odano, kummāso, sattu, maccho, maṁsaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): there are five kinds of cooked food: cooked grain, porridge, flour products, fish, and meat. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:2.1.11
Pali: “Khādissāmi bhuñjissāmī”ti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he receives fresh or cooked food with the intention of eating it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:2.1.12
Pali: Ajjhohāre ajjhohāre āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): For every mouthful swallowed, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:2.2.1
Pali: Vikāle vikālasaññī khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā khādati vā bhuñjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is the wrong time, and he perceives it as such, and he eats fresh or cooked food, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:2.2.2
Pali: Vikāle vematiko khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā khādati vā bhuñjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is the wrong time, but he is unsure of it, and he eats fresh or cooked food, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:2.2.3
Pali: Vikāle kālasaññī khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā khādati vā bhuñjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is the wrong time, but he perceives it as the right time, and he eats fresh or cooked food, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:2.2.4
Pali: Yāmakālikaṁ sattāhakālikaṁ yāvajīvikaṁ āhāratthāya paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he receives post-midday tonics, seven-day tonics, or lifetime tonics for the purpose of food, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:2.2.5
Pali: Ajjhohāre ajjhohāre āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): For every mouthful swallowed, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:2.2.6
Pali: Kāle vikālasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is the right time, but he perceives it as the wrong time, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:2.2.7
Pali: Kāle vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is the right time, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:2.2.8
Pali: Kāle kālasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is the right time, and he perceives it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:2.3.2
Pali: yāmakālikaṁ sattāhakālikaṁ yāvajīvikaṁ sati paccaye paribhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if, when there is a reason, he uses post-midday tonics, seven-day tonics, or lifetime tonics; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:2.3.3
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:2.3.4
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc37:2.3.5
Pali: Vikālabhojanasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ sattamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on eating at the wrong time, the seventh, is finished.

Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis

English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:0.4

English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on eating 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:0.5
Pali: 38. Sannidhikārakasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 38. Eating stored food 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmato ānandassa upajjhāyo āyasmā belaṭṭhasīso araññe viharati. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time Venerable Belaṭṭhasīsa, Venerable Ānanda’s preceptor, was staying in the wilderness. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:1.3
Pali: So piṇḍāya caritvā sukkhakuraṁ ārāmaṁ haritvā sukkhāpetvā nikkhipati. 
English (brahmali, published): After walking for alms, he brought plain cooked rice back to the monastery where he dried and stored it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:1.4
Pali: Yadā āhārena attho hoti, tadā udakena temetvā temetvā bhuñjati, cirena gāmaṁ piṇḍāya pavisati. 
English (brahmali, published): Whenever he got hungry, he moistened and ate it. As a result, he only went the village for alms after a long time. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:1.5
Pali: Bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ belaṭṭhasīsaṁ etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): The monks asked him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:1.6
Pali: “kissa tvaṁ, āvuso, cirena gāmaṁ piṇḍāya pavisasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Why do you only go for alms after such a long time?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:1.7
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā belaṭṭhasīso bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): And he told them. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:1.8
Pali: “Kiṁ pana tvaṁ, āvuso, sannidhikārakaṁ bhojanaṁ bhuñjasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “But do you eat food that you’ve stored?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:1.9
Pali: “Evamāvuso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:1.10
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:1.11
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā belaṭṭhasīso sannidhikārakaṁ bhojanaṁ bhuñjissatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can Venerable Belaṭṭhasīsa eat food that he has stored?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:1.12
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, belaṭṭhasīsa, sannidhikārakaṁ bhojanaṁ bhuñjasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Belaṭṭhasīsa, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:1.13
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:1.14
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:1.15
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, belaṭṭhasīsa, sannidhikārakaṁ bhojanaṁ bhuñjissasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Belaṭṭhasīsa, how can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:1.16
Pali: Netaṁ, belaṭṭhasīsa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:1.17
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:1.18.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu sannidhikārakaṁ khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā khādeyya vā bhuñjeyya vā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk eats fresh or cooked food that he has stored, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:2.1.5
Pali: Sannidhikārakaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): That he has stored: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:2.1.6
Pali: ajja paṭiggahitaṁ aparajju khāditaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): received today and eaten on the following day. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:2.1.7
Pali: Khādanīyaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Fresh food: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:2.1.8
Pali: pañca bhojanāni—yāmakālikaṁ sattāhakālikaṁ yāvajīvikaṁ ṭhapetvā avasesaṁ khādanīyaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): apart from the five cooked foods, the post-midday tonics, the seven-day tonics, and the lifetime tonics, the rest is called “fresh food”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:2.1.9
Pali: Bhojanīyaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Cooked food: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:2.1.10
Pali: pañca bhojanāni—odano, kummāso, sattu, maccho, maṁsaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): there are five kinds of cooked food: cooked grain, porridge, flour products, fish, and meat. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:2.1.11
Pali: “Khādissāmi bhuñjissāmī”ti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he receives fresh or cooked food with the intention of eating it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:2.1.12
Pali: Ajjhohāre ajjhohāre āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): For every mouthful swallowed, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:2.2.1
Pali: Sannidhikārake sannidhikārakasaññī khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā khādati vā bhuñjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has been stored, and he perceives that it has, and he eats the fresh or cooked food, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:2.2.2
Pali: Sannidhikārake vematiko khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā khādati vā bhuñjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has been stored, but he is unsure of it, and he eats the fresh or cooked food, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:2.2.3
Pali: Sannidhikārake asannidhikārakasaññī khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā khādati vā bhuñjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has been stored, but he does not perceive that it has, and he eats the fresh or cooked food, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:2.2.4
Pali: Yāmakālikaṁ sattāhakālikaṁ yāvajīvikaṁ āhāratthāya paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he receives post-midday tonics, seven-day tonics, or lifetime tonics for the purpose of food, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:2.2.5
Pali: Ajjhohāre ajjhohāre āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): For every mouthful swallowed, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:2.2.6
Pali: Asannidhikārake sannidhikārakasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been stored, but he perceives that it has, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:2.2.7
Pali: Asannidhikārake vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been stored, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:2.2.8
Pali: Asannidhikārake asannidhikārakasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been stored, and he does not perceive that it has, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:2.3.2
Pali: yāvakālikaṁ yāvakāle nidahitvā bhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he both stores and eats it during the right time; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:2.3.3
Pali: yāmakālikaṁ yāme nidahitvā bhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he both stores and eats post-midday tonics during the remainder of the day; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:2.3.4
Pali: sattāhakālikaṁ sattāhaṁ nidahitvā bhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he both stores and eats seven-day tonics during the seven-day period; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:2.3.5
Pali: yāvajīvikaṁ sati paccaye paribhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he uses lifetime tonics when there is a reason; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:2.3.6
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:2.3.7
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc38:2.3.8
Pali: Sannidhikārakasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ aṭṭhamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on eating stored food, the eighth, is finished.

Pali: Theravāda Vinaya

Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:0.4

English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on eating 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:0.5
Pali: 39. Paṇītabhojanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 39. Asking for fine foods 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū paṇītabhojanāni attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjanti. 
English (brahmali, published): the monks from the group of six were eating fine foods that they had requested for themselves. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:1.3
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:1.4
Pali: “kathañhi nāma samaṇā sakyaputtiyā paṇītabhojanāni attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the Sakyan monastics eat fine foods that they have requested for themselves? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:1.5
Pali: Kassa sampannaṁ na manāpaṁ, kassa sāduṁ na ruccatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Who doesn’t like nice food? Who doesn’t prefer tasty food?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:1.6
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:1.7
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:1.8
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū paṇītabhojanāni attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjissantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the monks from the group of six eat fine foods that they have requested for themselves?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:1.9
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, paṇītabhojanāni attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjathā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:1.10
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:1.11

Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, paṇītabhojanāni attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjissatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:1.13
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:1.14
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:1.15.1
Pali: “Yāni kho pana tāni paṇītabhojanāni, seyyathidaṁ— sappi navanītaṁ telaṁ madhu phāṇitaṁ maccho maṁsaṁ khīraṁ dadhi. Yo pana bhikkhu evarūpāni paṇītabhojanāni attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk asks for any of these kinds of fine foods for himself—that is, ghee, butter, oil, honey, syrup, fish, meat, milk, and curd—and then eats it, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:1.16
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū gilānā honti. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time a number of monks were sick. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:2.2
Pali: Gilānapucchakā bhikkhū gilāne bhikkhū etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): The monks who were looking after them asked, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:2.3
Pali: “kaccāvuso, khamanīyaṁ, kacci yāpanīyan”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “I hope you’re bearing up? I hope you’re getting better?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:2.4
Pali: “Pubbe mayaṁ, āvuso, paṇītabhojanāni attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjāma, tena no phāsu hoti; 
English (brahmali, published): “Previously we ate fine foods that we had requested ourselves, and then we were comfortable. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:2.5
Pali: idāni pana ‘bhagavatā paṭikkhittan’ti kukkuccāyantā na viññāpema, tena no na phāsu hotī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): But now that the Buddha has prohibited this, we don’t request because we’re afraid of wrongdoing. And because of that we’re not comfortable.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:2.6
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:2.7
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards he gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:2.8
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, gilānena bhikkhunā paṇītabhojanāni attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow a sick monk to eat fine foods that he has requested for himself. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:2.9
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:2.10.1
Pali: “Yāni kho pana tāni paṇītabhojanāni, seyyathidaṁ— sappi navanītaṁ telaṁ madhu phāṇitaṁ maccho maṁsaṁ khīraṁ dadhi. Yo pana bhikkhu evarūpāni paṇītabhojanāni agilāno attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk who is not sick asks for any of these kinds of fine foods for himself—that is, ghee, butter, oil, honey, syrup, fish, meat, milk, and curd—and then eats it, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.1
Pali: Yāni kho pana tāni paṇītabhojanānīti 
English (brahmali, published): These kinds of fine foods: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.2
Pali: sappi nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Ghee: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.3
Pali: gosappi vā ajikāsappi vā mahiṁsasappi vā, yesaṁ maṁsaṁ kappati tesaṁ sappi. 
English (brahmali, published): ghee from cows, ghee from goats, ghee from buffaloes, or ghee from whatever animal whose meat is allowable. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.4
Pali: Navanītaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Butter: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.5
Pali: tesaññeva navanītaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): butter from those same animals. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.6
Pali: Telaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Oil: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.7
Pali: tilatelaṁ sāsapatelaṁ madhukatelaṁ eraṇḍatelaṁ vasātelaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): sesame oil, mustard oil, honey-tree oil, castor oil, oil from fat. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.8
Pali: Madhu nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Honey: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.9
Pali: makkhikāmadhu. 
English (brahmali, published): honey from bees. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.10
Pali: Phāṇitaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Syrup: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.11
Pali: ucchumhā nibbattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): from sugarcane. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.12
Pali: Maccho nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Fish: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.13
Pali: udako vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): what lives in water is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.14
Pali: Maṁsaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Meat: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.15
Pali: yesaṁ maṁsaṁ kappati, tesaṁ maṁsaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): the meat of those animals whose meat is allowable. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.16
Pali: Khīraṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Milk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.17
Pali: gokhīraṁ vā ajikākhīraṁ vā mahiṁsakhīraṁ vā, yesaṁ maṁsaṁ kappati, tesaṁ khīraṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): milk from cows, milk from goats, milk from buffaloes, or milk from whatever animal whose meat is allowable. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.18
Pali: Dadhi nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Curd: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.19
Pali: tesaññeva dadhi. 
English (brahmali, published): curd from those same animals. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.20
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.21
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.22
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.23
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.24
Pali: Evarūpāni paṇītabhojanānīti 
English (brahmali, published): Any of these kinds of fine foods: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.25
Pali: tathārūpāni paṇītabhojanāni. 
English (brahmali, published): such kinds of fine foods. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.26
Pali: Agilāno nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Who is not sick: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.27
Pali: yassa vinā paṇītabhojanāni phāsu hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): who is comfortable without fine foods. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.28
Pali: Gilāno nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Who is sick: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.29
Pali: yassa vinā paṇītabhojanāni na phāsu hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): who is not comfortable without fine foods. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.30
Pali: Agilāno attano atthāya viññāpeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is not sick and he requests for himself, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.31
Pali: Paṭilābhena “bhuñjissāmī”ti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When he receives it with the intention of eating it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.1.32
Pali: Ajjhohāre ajjhohāre āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): For every mouthful swallowed, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.2.1
Pali: Agilāno agilānasaññī paṇītabhojanāni attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is not sick, and he does not perceive himself as sick, and he eats fine foods that he has requested for himself, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.2.2
Pali: Agilāno vematiko paṇītabhojanāni attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is not sick, but he is unsure of it, and he eats fine foods that he has requested for himself, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.2.3
Pali: Agilāno gilānasaññī paṇītabhojanāni attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is not sick, but he perceives himself as sick, and he eats fine foods that he has requested for himself, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.2.4
Pali: Gilāno agilānasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is sick, but he does not perceive himself as sick, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.2.5
Pali: Gilāno vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is sick, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.2.6
Pali: Gilāno gilānasaññī anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is sick, and he perceives himself as sick, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.3.2
Pali: gilānassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is sick; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.3.3
Pali: gilāno hutvā viññāpetvā agilāno bhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he asked for it when he was sick, but eats it when he is no longer sick; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.3.4
Pali: gilānassa sesakaṁ bhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he eats the leftovers from one who is sick; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.3.5
Pali: ñātakānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is from relatives; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.3.6
Pali: pavāritānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is from those who have given an invitation; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.3.7
Pali: aññassatthāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is for the benefit of someone else; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.3.8
Pali: attano dhanena, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is by means of his own property; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.3.9
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.3.10
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc39:3.3.11
Pali: Paṇītabhojanasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ navamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on asking for fine foods, the ninth, is finished.

Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:0.4
Pali: Bhojanavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on eating 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:0.5
Pali: 40. Dantaponasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 40. Eating ungiven food 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying in the house with the peaked roof in the Great Wood near Vesālī. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu sabbapaṁsukūliko susāne viharati. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time a monk who only used discarded things was staying in a charnel ground. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:1.3
Pali: So manussehi diyyamānaṁ na icchati paṭiggahetuṁ, susānepi rukkhamūlepi ummārepi ayyavosāṭitakāni sāmaṁ gahetvā paribhuñjati. 
English (brahmali, published): He disliked receiving things from people. Instead he would take whatever was offered to the dead at the charnel ground, at the foot of trees, or at the threshold, and he would use that. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:1.4
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:1.5
Pali: “kathañhi nāma ayaṁ bhikkhu amhākaṁ ayyavosāṭitakāni sāmaṁ gahetvā paribhuñjissati. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can this monk take the offerings to our ancestors and use them? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:1.6
Pali: Theroyaṁ bhikkhu vaṭharo manussamaṁsaṁ maññe khādatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): This monk is big and strong. He probably eats human flesh too!” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:1.7
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:1.8
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:1.9
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhu adinnaṁ mukhadvāraṁ āhāraṁ āharissatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can this monk eat food that hasn’t been given?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:1.10
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, bhikkhu, adinnaṁ mukhadvāraṁ āhāraṁ āharasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monk, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:1.11
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:1.12
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:1.13
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, adinnaṁ mukhadvāraṁ āhāraṁ āharissasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, how can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:1.14
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:1.15
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:1.16.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu adinnaṁ mukhadvāraṁ āhāraṁ āhareyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk eats food that has not been given, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:1.17
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū udakadantapone kukkuccāyanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the monks did not use water or tooth cleaners because they were afraid of wrongdoing. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:2.2
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:2.3
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, udakadantaponaṁ sāmaṁ gahetvā paribhuñjituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to use water and tooth cleaners after taking them yourselves. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:2.4
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:2.5.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu adinnaṁ mukhadvāraṁ āhāraṁ āhareyya, aññatra udakadantaponā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk eats food that has not been given, except for water and tooth cleaners, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.1.5
Pali: Adinnaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): That has not been given: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.1.6
Pali: appaṭiggahitakaṁ vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): what has not been received is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.1.7
Pali: Dinnaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Given: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.1.8
Pali: kāyena vā kāyapaṭibaddhena vā nissaggiyena vā dente hatthapāse ṭhito kāyena vā kāyapaṭibaddhena vā paṭiggaṇhāti, etaṁ dinnaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): standing within arm’s reach of one giving by body or by what is connected to his body or by releasing, he receives it by body or by what is connected to his body—this is called “given”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.1.9
Pali: Āhāro nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Food: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.1.10
Pali: udakadantaponaṁ ṭhapetvā yaṁ kiñci ajjhoharaṇīyaṁ, eso āhāro nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): whatever is edible, apart from water and tooth cleaners—this is called “food”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.1.11
Pali: Aññatra udakadantaponāti 
English (brahmali, published): Except for water and tooth cleaners: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.1.12
Pali: ṭhapetvā udakadantaponaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): apart from water and tooth cleaners. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.1.13
Pali: “Khādissāmi bhuñjissāmī”ti gaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he takes it with the intention of eating it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.1.14
Pali: Ajjhohāre ajjhohāre āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): For every mouthful swallowed, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.2.1
Pali: Appaṭiggahitake appaṭiggahitakasaññī adinnaṁ mukhadvāraṁ āhāraṁ āhāreti, aññatra udakadantaponā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been received, and he does not perceive it as such, and he eats it, except for water and tooth cleaners, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.2.2
Pali: Appaṭiggahitake vematiko adinnaṁ mukhadvāraṁ āhāraṁ āhāreti, aññatra udakadantaponā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been received, but he is unsure of it, and he eats it, except for water and tooth cleaners, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.2.3
Pali: Appaṭiggahitake paṭiggahitakasaññī adinnaṁ mukhadvāraṁ āhāraṁ āhāreti, aññatra udakadantaponā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been not received, but he perceives it as such, and he eats it, except for water and tooth cleaners, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.2.4
Pali: Paṭiggahitake appaṭiggahitakasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has been received, but he does not perceive it as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.2.5
Pali: Paṭiggahitake vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has been received, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.2.6
Pali: Paṭiggahitake paṭiggahitakasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has been received, and he perceives it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.3.2
Pali: udakadantapone, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is water or tooth cleaners; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.3.3
Pali: cattāri mahāvikaṭāni sati paccaye asati kappiyakārake sāmaṁ gahetvā paribhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if, when there is a reason, but there is no attendant, he himself takes the four foul edibles and eats them; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.3.4
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.3.5
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.3.6
Pali: Dantaponasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ dasamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on eating ungiven food, the tenth, is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.3.7
Pali: Bhojanavaggo catuttho. 
English (brahmali, published): The fourth subchapter on eating is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.3.8
Pali: Tassuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.3.9
Pali: Piṇḍo gaṇaṁ paraṁ pūvaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): “Alms, group, another, cookie, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.3.10
Pali: dve ca vuttā pavāraṇā; 
English (brahmali, published): And two are spoken on invitations; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.3.11
Pali: Vikāle sannidhī khīraṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): At the wrong time, store, milk, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc40:3.3.12
Pali: dantaponena te dasāti. 
English (brahmali, published): With tooth cleaner—those are the ten.”
