English translation sources:
  - brahmali [published]: translation/en/brahmali/vinaya/pli-tv-bu-vb/pli-tv-bu-vb-pc/pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41_translation-en-brahmali.json

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:0.4
Pali: Acelakavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on naked ascetics 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:0.5
Pali: 41. Acelakasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 41. Giving food to non-Buddhist monastics 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:1.1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying in the house with the peaked roof in the Great Wood near Vesālī, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:1.1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena saṅghassa khādanīyaṁ ussannaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): the Sangha had an abundance of fresh food. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:1.1.3
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā ānando bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesi. “Tenahānanda, vighāsādānaṁ pūvaṁ dehī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Ānanda told the Buddha, who said, “Well then, Ānanda, give the cookies to those who take leftovers.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:1.1.4
Pali: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho āyasmā ānando bhagavato paṭissuṇitvā vighāsāde paṭipāṭiyā nisīdāpetvā ekekaṁ pūvaṁ dento aññatarissā paribbājikāya ekaṁ maññamāno dve pūve adāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes, sir.” Ānanda had them sit in a row and gave them one cookie each, until he accidentally gave two to a female wanderer. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:1.1.5
Pali: Sāmantā paribbājikāyo taṁ paribbājikaṁ etadavocuṁ—“jāro te eso samaṇo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The female wanderers sitting next to her said to her, “This monastic is your lover.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:1.1.6
Pali: “Na me so samaṇo jāro, ekaṁ maññamāno dve pūve adāsī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “He’s not. He gave me two, thinking they were one.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:1.1.7
Pali: Dutiyampi kho …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): And a second time … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:1.1.8
Pali: tatiyampi kho āyasmā ānando ekekaṁ pūvaṁ dento tassāyeva paribbājikāya ekaṁ maññamāno dve pūve adāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): And a third time Ānanda gave them one cookie each, until he accidentally gave two to that same female wanderer. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:1.1.9
Pali: Sāmantā paribbājikāyo taṁ paribbājikaṁ etadavocuṁ—“jāro te eso samaṇo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Once again the female wanderers sitting next to her said to her, “This monastic is your lover.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:1.1.10
Pali: “Na me so samaṇo jāro, ekaṁ maññamāno dve pūve adāsī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “He’s not. He gave me two, thinking they were one.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:1.1.11
Pali: “Jāro na jāro”ti bhaṇḍiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): And they started to argue about whether or not they were lovers. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:1.2.1
Pali: Aññataropi ājīvako parivesanaṁ agamāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): A certain Ājīvaka ascetic, too, went to that distribution of food. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:1.2.2
Pali: Aññataro bhikkhu pahūtena sappinā odanaṁ madditvā tassa ājīvakassa mahantaṁ piṇḍaṁ adāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): A monk mixed rice with a large amount of ghee and gave him a large lump. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:1.2.3
Pali: Atha kho so ājīvako taṁ piṇḍaṁ ādāya agamāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): He took it and left. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:1.2.4
Pali: Aññataro ājīvako taṁ ājīvakaṁ etadavoca—“kuto tayā, āvuso, piṇḍo laddho”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): Another Ājīvaka asked him, “Where did you get that lump?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:1.2.5
Pali: “Tassāvuso, samaṇassa gotamassa muṇḍagahapatikassa parivesanāya laddho”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “From the food distribution of the ascetic Gotama, that shaven-headed householder.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:1.2.6
Pali: Assosuṁ kho upāsakā tesaṁ ājīvakānaṁ imaṁ kathāsallāpaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Some lay followers overheard that conversation between those Ājīvaka ascetics. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:1.2.7
Pali: Atha kho te upāsakā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te upāsakā bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): They then went to the Buddha, bowed, sat down, and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:1.2.8
Pali: “ime, bhante, titthiyā avaṇṇakāmā buddhassa avaṇṇakāmā dhammassa avaṇṇakāmā saṅghassa. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, these monastics of other religions want to disparage the Buddha, the Teaching, and the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:1.2.9
Pali: Sādhu, bhante, ayyā titthiyānaṁ sahatthā na dadeyyun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): It would be good if the monks didn’t personally give anything to the monastics of other religions.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:1.2.10
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā te upāsake dhammiyā kathāya sandassesi samādapesi samuttejesi sampahaṁsesi. Atha kho te upāsakā bhagavatā dhammiyā kathāya sandassitā samādapitā samuttejitā sampahaṁsitā uṭṭhāyāsanā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkamiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): After the Buddha had instructed, inspired, and gladdened those lay followers with a teaching, they got up from their seats, bowed down, circumambulated him with their right sides toward him, and left. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:1.2.11
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:1.2.12
Pali: “tena hi, bhikkhave, bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññapessāmi dasa atthavase paṭicca—
English (brahmali, published): “Well then, monks, I will lay down a training rule for the following ten reasons: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:1.2.13
Pali: saṅghasuṭṭhutāya, saṅghaphāsutāya …pe… saddhammaṭṭhitiyā, vinayānuggahāya. 
English (brahmali, published): for the well-being of the Sangha, for the comfort of the Sangha, for the restraint of bad people, for the ease of good monks, for the restraint of the corruptions relating to the present life, for the restraint of the corruptions relating to future lives, to give rise to confidence in those without it, to increase the confidence of those who have it, for the longevity of the true Teaching, and for supporting the training. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:1.2.14
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:1.2.15.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu acelakassa vā paribbājakassa vā paribbājikāya vā sahatthā khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā dadeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk personally gives fresh or cooked food to a naked ascetic, to a male wanderer, or to a female wanderer, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.1.5
Pali: Acelako nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A naked ascetic: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.1.6
Pali: yo koci paribbājakasamāpanno naggo. 
English (brahmali, published): any wanderer who is naked. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.1.7
Pali: Paribbājako nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A male wanderer: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.1.8
Pali: bhikkhuñca sāmaṇerañca ṭhapetvā yo koci paribbājakasamāpanno. 
English (brahmali, published): any male wanderer apart from Buddhist monks and novice monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.1.9
Pali: Paribbājikā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A female wanderer: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.1.10
Pali: bhikkhuniñca sikkhamānañca sāmaṇeriñca ṭhapetvā yā kāci paribbājikasamāpannā. 
English (brahmali, published): any female wanderer apart from Buddhist nuns, trainee nuns, and novice nuns. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.1.11
Pali: Khādanīyaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Fresh food: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.1.12
Pali: pañca bhojanāni—udakadantaponaṁ ṭhapetvā avasesaṁ khādanīyaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): apart from the five cooked foods, water, and tooth cleaners, the rest is called “fresh food”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.1.13
Pali: Bhojanīyaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Cooked food: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.1.14
Pali: pañca bhojanāni—odano, kummāso, sattu, maccho, maṁsaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): there are five kinds of cooked food: cooked grain, porridge, flour products, fish, and meat. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.1.15
Pali: Dadeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Gives: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.1.16
Pali: kāyena vā kāyapaṭibaddhena vā nissaggiyena vā deti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he gives by body or by what is connected to the body or by releasing, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.2.1
Pali: Titthiye titthiyasaññī sahatthā khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā deti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a monastic of another religion, and he perceives them as such, and he personally gives them fresh or cooked food, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.2.2
Pali: Titthiye vematiko sahatthā khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā deti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a monastic of another religion, but he is unsure of it, and he personally gives them fresh or cooked food, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.2.3
Pali: Titthiye atitthiyasaññī sahatthā khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā deti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a monastic of another religion, but he does not perceive them as such, and he personally gives them fresh or cooked food, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.2.4
Pali: Udakadantaponaṁ deti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he gives water or a tooth cleaner, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.2.5
Pali: Atitthiye titthiyasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a monastic of another religion, but he perceives them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.2.6
Pali: Atitthiye vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a monastic of another religion, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.2.7
Pali: Atitthiye atitthiyasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a monastic of another religion, and he does not perceive them as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.3.2
Pali: dāpeti na deti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not give, but has it given; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.3.3
Pali: upanikkhipitvā deti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he gives by placing it near the person; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.3.4
Pali: bāhirālepaṁ deti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he gives ointments for external use; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.3.5
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.3.6
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc41:2.3.7
Pali: Acelakasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ paṭhamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on giving food to non-Buddhist monastics, the first, is finished.

Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:0.4
Pali: Acelakavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on naked ascetics 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:0.5
Pali: 42. Uyyojanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 42. Dismissing a monk during almsround 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā upanando sakyaputto bhātuno saddhivihārikaṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Upananda the Sakyan said to his brother’s student, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:1.3
Pali: “ehāvuso, gāmaṁ piṇḍāya pavisissāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Come, let’s go to the village for alms.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:1.4
Pali: Tassa adāpetvā uyyojesi—
English (brahmali, published): Then, without getting him any food, he sent him away, saying, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:1.5
Pali: “gacchāvuso, na me tayā saddhiṁ kathā vā nisajjā vā phāsu hoti, ekakassa me kathā vā nisajjā vā phāsu hotī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Go away! I’m not comfortable talking or sitting with you, but only if I talk and sit by myself.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:1.6
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu upakaṭṭhe kāle nāsakkhi piṇḍāya carituṁ, paṭikkamanepi bhattavissaggaṁ na sambhāvesi, chinnabhatto ahosi. 
English (brahmali, published): But since the right time for eating was coming to an end, the student was unable to walk for alms. As he returned to the monastery, there was nobody offering food, and so he missed his meal. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:1.7
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu ārāmaṁ gantvā bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): He then went to the monastery and told the monks what had happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:1.8
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized Upananda, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:1.9
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā upanando sakyaputto bhikkhuṁ—‘ehāvuso, gāmaṁ piṇḍāya pavisissāmā’ti tassa adāpetvā uyyojessatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Upananda say to a monk, ‘Come, let’s go to the village for alms,’ and then send him away without getting him any food?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:1.10
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, upananda, bhikkhuṁ—‘ehāvuso, gāmaṁ piṇḍāya pavisissāmā’ti tassa adāpetvā uyyojesī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Upananda, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:1.11
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:1.12
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:1.13
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, bhikkhuṁ—“ehāvuso, gāmaṁ piṇḍāya pavisissāmā”ti tassa adāpetvā uyyojessasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:1.14
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:1.15
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:1.16.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhuṁ—‘ehāvuso, gāmaṁ vā nigamaṁ vā piṇḍāya pavisissāmā’ti tassa dāpetvā vā adāpetvā vā uyyojeyya— ‘gacchāvuso, na me tayā saddhiṁ kathā vā nisajjā vā phāsu hoti, ekakassa me kathā vā nisajjā vā phāsu hotī’ti, etadeva paccayaṁ karitvā anaññaṁ, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk says to a monk, “Come, let’s go to the village or town for alms,” and then, whether he has had food given to him or not, sends him away, saying, “Go away! I’m not comfortable talking or sitting with you, but only if I talk and sit by myself,” and he does so only for this reason and no other, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.1.2
Pali: yo, yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.1.5
Pali: Bhikkhunti 
English (brahmali, published): To a monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.1.6
Pali: aññaṁ bhikkhuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): to another monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.1.7
Pali: Ehāvuso, gāmaṁ vā nigamaṁ vāti 
English (brahmali, published): Come … to the village or town: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.1.8
Pali: gāmopi nigamopi nagarampi, gāmo ceva nigamo ca. 
English (brahmali, published): a village, also a town, also a city; both a village and a town. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.1.9
Pali: Tassa dāpetvāti 
English (brahmali, published): He has had food given to him: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.1.10
Pali: yāguṁ vā bhattaṁ vā khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā dāpetvā. 
English (brahmali, published): he has had congee, a meal, fresh food, or cooked food given to him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.1.11
Pali: Adāpetvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Not: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.1.12
Pali: na kiñci dāpetvā. 
English (brahmali, published): he has not had anything given to him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.1.13
Pali: Uyyojeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Sends away: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.1.14
Pali: mātugāmena saddhiṁ hasitukāmo kīḷitukāmo raho nisīditukāmo anācāraṁ ācaritukāmo evaṁ vadeti— “gacchāvuso, na me tayā saddhiṁ kathā vā nisajjā vā phāsu hoti, ekakassa me kathā vā nisajjā vā phāsu hotī”ti uyyojeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if, wanting to laugh with a woman, wanting to enjoy himself with her, wanting to sit down in private with her, wanting to misbehave with her, he says, “Go away! I’m not comfortable talking or sitting with you, but only if I talk and sit by myself,” and he sends him away, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.1.15
Pali: Dassanūpacāraṁ vā savanūpacāraṁ vā vijahantassa āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the second monk is in the process of going beyond sight or beyond hearing, the first monk commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.1.16
Pali: Vijahite, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When the second monk has gone beyond, the first monk commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.1.17
Pali: Etadeva paccayaṁ karitvā anaññanti 
English (brahmali, published): He does so only for this reason and no other: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.1.18
Pali: na añño koci paccayo hoti uyyojetuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): there is no other reason for sending him away. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.2.1
Pali: Upasampanne upasampannasaññī uyyojeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the second monk is fully ordained, and the first monk perceives him as such, and he sends him away, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.2.2
Pali: Upasampanne vematiko uyyojeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the second monk is fully ordained, but the first monk is unsure of it, and he sends him away, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.2.3
Pali: Upasampanne anupasampannasaññī uyyojeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the second monk is fully ordained, but the first monk does not perceive him as such, and he sends him away, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.2.4
Pali: Kalisāsanaṁ āropeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he puts him down, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.2.5
Pali: Anupasampannaṁ uyyojeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he sends away one who is not fully ordained, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.2.6
Pali: Kalisāsanaṁ āropeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he puts him down, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.2.7
Pali: Anupasampanne upasampannasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other is not fully ordained, but he perceives them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.2.8
Pali: Anupasampanne vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other is not fully ordained, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.2.9
Pali: Anupasampanne anupasampannasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other is not fully ordained, and he does not perceive them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.3.2
Pali: “ubho ekato na yāpessāmā”ti uyyojeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he sends him away, thinking, “Together we won’t get enough;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.3.3
Pali: “mahagghaṁ bhaṇḍaṁ passitvā lobhadhammaṁ uppādessatī”ti uyyojeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he sends him away, thinking, “If he sees these valuable goods, he’ll become greedy;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.3.4
Pali: “mātugāmaṁ passitvā anabhiratiṁ uppādessatī”ti uyyojeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he sends him away, thinking, “If he sees this woman, he’ll become lustful;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.3.5
Pali: “gilānassa vā ohiyyakassa vā vihārapālassa vā yāguṁ vā bhattaṁ vā khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā nīharā”ti uyyojeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he sends him away, saying, “Take congee or a meal or fresh food or cooked food to the one who is sick or to the one who is left behind or to the one who is guarding the dwellings;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.3.6
Pali: na anācāraṁ ācaritukāmo, 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not want to misbehave; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.3.7
Pali: sati karaṇīye uyyojeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he sends him away when there is something to be done; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.3.8
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.3.9
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc42:2.3.10
Pali: Uyyojanasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ dutiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on dismissing a monk during almsround, the second, is finished.

English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis

English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:0.4
Pali: Acelakavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on naked ascetics 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:0.5
Pali: 43. Sabhojanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 43. Intruding on a lustful couple 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā upanando sakyaputto sahāyakassa gharaṁ gantvā tassa pajāpatiyā saddhiṁ sayanighare nisajjaṁ kappesi. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Upananda the Sakyan went to the house of a friend and sat down with his wife in their bedroom. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:1.3
Pali: Atha kho so puriso yenāyasmā upanando sakyaputto tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ upanandaṁ sakyaputtaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): The husband approached Upananda, bowed, and sat down. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:1.4
Pali: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so puriso pajāpatiṁ etadavoca—“dadehāyyassa bhikkhan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): He then said to his wife, “Please give him alms.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:1.5
Pali: Atha kho sā itthī āyasmato upanandassa sakyaputtassa bhikkhaṁ adāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): And she did so. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:1.6
Pali: Atha kho so puriso āyasmantaṁ upanandaṁ sakyaputtaṁ etadavoca—“gacchatha, bhante, yato ayyassa bhikkhā dinnā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards he said, “Please leave, sir, the alms have been given.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:1.7
Pali: Atha kho sā itthī sallakkhetvā—“pariyuṭṭhito ayaṁ puriso”ti, āyasmantaṁ upanandaṁ sakyaputtaṁ etadavoca—“nisīdatha, bhante, mā agamitthā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): But the woman, knowing that her husband was lustful, said, “Please sit, sir, don’t go.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:1.8
Pali: Dutiyampi kho so puriso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): A second time and 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:1.9
Pali: tatiyampi kho so puriso āyasmantaṁ upanandaṁ sakyaputtaṁ etadavoca—“gacchatha, bhante, yato ayyassa bhikkhā dinnā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): a third time he repeated his request, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:1.10
Pali: Tatiyampi kho sā itthī āyasmantaṁ upanandaṁ sakyaputtaṁ etadavoca—“nisīdatha, bhante, mā agamitthā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): and both times his wife repeated hers. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:1.11
Pali: Atha kho so puriso nikkhamitvā bhikkhū ujjhāpesi—
English (brahmali, published): He then left the house and complained to the monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:1.12
Pali: “ayaṁ, bhante, ayyo upanando mayhaṁ pajāpatiyā saddhiṁ sayanighare nisinno. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, Venerable Upananda is seated with my wife in our bedroom. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:1.13
Pali: So mayā uyyojīyamāno na icchati gantuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): When I ask him to leave because we’re busy, he doesn’t want to go.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:1.14
Pali: Bahukiccā mayaṁ bahukaraṇīyā”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:1.15
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized Upananda, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:1.16
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā upanando sakyaputto sabhojane kule anupakhajja nisajjaṁ kappessatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Upananda sit down intruding on a lustful couple?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:1.17
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, upananda, sabhojane kule anupakhajja nisajjaṁ kappesī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Upananda, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:1.18
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:1.19
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:1.20
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, sabhojane kule anupakhajja nisajjaṁ kappessasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:1.21
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:1.22
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:1.23.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu sabhojane kule anupakhajja nisajjaṁ kappeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk sits down intruding on a lustful couple, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:2.1.5
Pali: Sabhojanaṁ nāma kulaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): A lustful couple: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:2.1.6
Pali: itthī ceva hoti puriso ca, itthī ca puriso ca ubho anikkhantā honti, ubho avītarāgā. 
English (brahmali, published): both a woman and a man are present. The woman and the man have not both left, and both are not without lust. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:2.1.7
Pali: Anupakhajjāti 
English (brahmali, published): Intruding on: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:2.1.8
Pali: anupavisitvā. 
English (brahmali, published): enters after. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:2.1.9
Pali: Nisajjaṁ kappeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Sits down: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:2.1.10
Pali: mahallake ghare piṭṭhasaṅghāṭassa hatthapāsaṁ vijahitvā nisīdati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): in a large house, if he sits down more than one arm’s reach inside the door frame, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:2.1.11
Pali: Khuddake ghare piṭṭhivaṁsaṁ atikkamitvā nisīdati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): In a small house, if he sits down beyond the ridge beam, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:2.2.1
Pali: Sayanighare sayanigharasaññī sabhojane kule anupakhajja nisajjaṁ kappeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a bedroom, and he perceives it as such, and he sits down intruding on a lustful couple, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:2.2.2
Pali: Sayanighare vematiko sabhojane kule anupakhajja nisajjaṁ kappeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a bedroom, but he is unsure of it, and he sits down intruding on a lustful couple, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:2.2.3
Pali: Sayanighare nasayanigharasaññī sabhojane kule anupakhajja nisajjaṁ kappeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a bedroom, but he does not perceive it as such, and he sits down intruding on a lustful couple, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:2.2.4
Pali: Nasayanighare sayanigharasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a bedroom, but he perceives it as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:2.2.5
Pali: Nasayanighare vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a bedroom, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:2.2.6
Pali: Nasayanighare nasayanigharasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a bedroom, and he does not perceive it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:2.3.2
Pali: mahallake ghare piṭṭhasaṅghāṭassa hatthapāsaṁ avijahitvā nisīdati, 
English (brahmali, published): if, in a large house, he sits down, but not more than one arm’s reach inside the door frame; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:2.3.3
Pali: khuddake ghare piṭṭhivaṁsaṁ anatikkamitvā nisīdati, 
English (brahmali, published): if, in a small house, he sits down, but not beyond the ridge beam; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:2.3.4
Pali: bhikkhu dutiyo hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he has a companion monk; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:2.3.5
Pali: ubho nikkhantā honti, 
English (brahmali, published): if both have left; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:2.3.6
Pali: ubho vītarāgā, 
English (brahmali, published): if both are without lust; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:2.3.7
Pali: nasayanighare, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is not a bedroom; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:2.3.8
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:2.3.9
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc43:2.3.10
Pali: Sabhojanasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ tatiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on intruding on a lustful couple, the third, is finished.

Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tassa purisassa ujjhāyantassa khiyyantassa vipācentassa. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of that man,

Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu mātugāmena saddhiṁ raho paṭicchanne āsane nisajjaṁ kappeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk sits down in private on a concealed seat with a woman, he commits an offense entailing confession.’”

Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.1.5
Pali: Mātugāmo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A woman: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.1.6
Pali: manussitthī, na yakkhī na petī na tiracchānagatā, antamaso tadahujātāpi dārikā, pageva mahattarī. 
English (brahmali, published): a human female, not a female spirit, not a female ghost, not a female animal; even a girl born on that very day, let alone an older one. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.1.7
Pali: Saddhinti 
English (brahmali, published): With: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.1.8
Pali: ekato. 
English (brahmali, published): together. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.1.9
Pali: Raho nāma 
English (brahmali, published): In private: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.1.10
Pali: cakkhussa raho sotassa raho. 
English (brahmali, published): private to the eye and private to the ear. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.1.11
Pali: Cakkhussa raho nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Private to the eye: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.1.12
Pali: na sakkā hoti akkhiṁ vā nikhaṇīyamāne bhamukaṁ vā ukkhipīyamāne sīsaṁ vā ukkhipīyamāne passituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one is unable to see them winking, raising an eyebrow, or nodding. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.1.13
Pali: Sotassa raho nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Private to the ear: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.1.14
Pali: na sakkā hoti pakatikathā sotuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one is unable to hear ordinary speech. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.1.15
Pali: Paṭicchannaṁ nāma āsanaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): A concealed seat: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.1.16
Pali: kuṭṭena vā kavāṭena vā kilañjena vā sāṇipākārena vā rukkhena vā thambhena vā kotthaḷiyā vā, yena kenaci paṭicchannaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): it is concealed by a wall, a screen, a door, a cloth screen, a tree, a pillar, a grain container, or anything else. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.1.17
Pali: Nisajjaṁ kappeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Sits down: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.1.18
Pali: mātugāme nisinne bhikkhu upanisinno vā hoti upanipanno vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if the monk sits down or lies down next to the seated woman, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.1.19
Pali: Bhikkhu nisinne mātugāmo upanisinno vā hoti upanipanno vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the woman sits down or lies down next to the seated monk, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.1.20
Pali: Ubho vā nisinnā honti ubho vā nipannā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If both are seated or both are lying down, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.2.1
Pali: Mātugāme mātugāmasaññī raho paṭicchanne āsane nisajjaṁ kappeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a woman, and he perceives her as such, and he sits down in private on a concealed seat with her, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.2.2
Pali: Mātugāme vematiko raho paṭicchanne āsane nisajjaṁ kappeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a woman, but he is unsure of it, and he sits down in private on a concealed seat with her, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.2.3
Pali: Mātugāme amātugāmasaññī raho paṭicchanne āsane nisajjaṁ kappeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a woman, but he does not perceive her as such, and he sits down in private on a concealed seat with her, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.2.4
Pali: Yakkhiyā vā petiyā vā paṇḍakena vā tiracchānagatāya vā manussaviggahitthiyā vā saddhiṁ raho paṭicchanne āsane nisajjaṁ kappeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he sits down in private on a concealed seat with a female spirit, a female ghost, a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>paṇḍaka</i>, or a female animal in the form of a woman, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.2.5
Pali: Amātugāme mātugāmasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a woman, but he perceives them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.2.6
Pali: Amātugāme vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a woman, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.2.7
Pali: Amātugāme amātugāmasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a woman, and he does not perceive them as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.3.2
Pali: yo koci viññū puriso dutiyo hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he has a male companion who understands; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.3.3
Pali: tiṭṭhati na nisīdati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he stands and does not sit down; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.3.4
Pali: arahopekkho, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is not seeking privacy; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.3.5
Pali: aññavihito nisīdati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he sits down preoccupied with something else; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.3.6
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.3.7
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc44:2.3.8
Pali: Rahopaṭicchannasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ catutthaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on sitting with a woman in a concealed place, the fourth, is finished.

Pali: 45. Rahonisajjasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 45. Sitting alone with a woman 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā upanando sakyaputto sahāyakassa gharaṁ gantvā tassa pajāpatiyā saddhiṁ eko ekāya raho nisajjaṁ kappesi. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Upananda the Sakyan went to the house of a friend and sat down in private alone with his wife. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:1.3

English (brahmali, published): The husband complained and criticized him,

English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Upananda sit down in private alone with my wife?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:1.5
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tassa purisassa ujjhāyantassa khiyyantassa vipācentassa. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of that man, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:1.6
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized Upananda, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:1.7
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā upanando sakyaputto mātugāmena saddhiṁ eko ekāya raho nisajjaṁ kappessatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Upananda sit down in private alone with a woman?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:1.8
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, upananda, mātugāmena saddhiṁ eko ekāya raho nisajjaṁ kappesī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Upananda, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:1.9
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:1.10

Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pāsādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” …

English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:1.14.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu mātugāmena saddhiṁ eko ekāya raho nisajjaṁ kappeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk sits down in private alone with a woman, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:2.1.5
Pali: Mātugāmo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A woman: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:2.1.6
Pali: manussitthī, na yakkhī na petī na tiracchānagatā, viññū paṭibalā subhāsitadubbhāsitaṁ duṭṭhullāduṭṭhullaṁ ājānituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): a human female, not a female spirit, not a female ghost, not a female animal. She understands and is capable of discerning good and bad speech, what is decent and what is indecent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:2.1.7
Pali: Saddhinti 
English (brahmali, published): With: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:2.1.8
Pali: ekato. 
English (brahmali, published): together. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:2.1.9
Pali: Eko ekāyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Alone: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:2.1.10
Pali: bhikkhu ceva hoti mātugāmo ca. 
English (brahmali, published): just the monk and the woman. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:2.1.11
Pali: Raho nāma 
English (brahmali, published): In private: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:2.1.12
Pali: cakkhussa raho, sotassa raho. 
English (brahmali, published): private to the eye and private to the ear. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:2.1.13
Pali: Cakkhussa raho nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Private to the eye: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:2.1.14
Pali: na sakkā hoti akkhiṁ vā nikhaṇīyamāne bhamukaṁ vā ukkhipīyamāne sīsaṁ vā ukkhipīyamāne passituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one is unable to see them winking, raising an eyebrow, or nodding. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:2.1.15
Pali: Sotassa raho nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Private to the ear: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:2.1.16
Pali: na sakkā hoti pakatikathā sotuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one is unable to hear ordinary speech. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:2.1.17
Pali: Nisajjaṁ kappeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Sits down: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:2.1.18
Pali: mātugāme nisinne bhikkhu upanisinno vā hoti upanipanno vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if the monk sits down or lies down next to the seated woman, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:2.1.19
Pali: Bhikkhu nisinne mātugāmo upanisinno vā hoti upanipanno vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the woman sits down or lies down next to the seated monk, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:2.1.20
Pali: Ubho vā nisinnā honti ubho vā nipannā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If both are seated or both are lying down, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:2.2.1

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:2.2.3
Pali: Mātugāme amātugāmasaññī eko ekāya raho nisajjaṁ kappeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a woman, but he does not perceive her as such, and he sits down in private alone with her, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:2.2.4
Pali: Yakkhiyā vā petiyā vā paṇḍakena vā tiracchānagatamanussaviggahitthiyā vā saddhiṁ eko ekāya raho nisajjaṁ kappeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he sits down in private alone with a female spirit, a female ghost, a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>paṇḍaka</i>, or a female animal in the form of a woman, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:2.2.5
Pali: Amātugāme mātugāmasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a woman, but he perceives them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:2.2.6
Pali: Amātugāme vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a woman, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:2.2.7
Pali: Amātugāme amātugāmasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a woman, and he does not perceive them as such, there is no offense.

Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense:

English (brahmali, published): if he stands and does not sit down; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:2.3.4
Pali: arahopekkho 
English (brahmali, published): if he is not seeking privacy; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc45:2.3.5
Pali: aññavihito nisīdati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he sits down preoccupied with something else;

English (brahmali, published): if he is insane;

Pali: ādikammikassāti.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:0.5
Pali: 46. Cārittasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 46. Visiting families before a meal 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Rājagaha in the Bamboo Grove, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmato upanandassa sakyaputtassa upaṭṭhākakulaṁ āyasmantaṁ upanandaṁ sakyaputtaṁ bhattena nimantesi. 
English (brahmali, published): a family that was supporting Venerable Upananda the Sakyan had invited him to a meal, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:1.3
Pali: Aññepi bhikkhū bhattena nimantesi. 
English (brahmali, published): and they had invited other monks too. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:1.4

English (brahmali, published): But since Upananda was visiting other families before that meal, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:1.5
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū te manusse etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): the other monks said to that family,

Pali: “dethāvuso, bhattan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please give the meal.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:1.7
Pali: “Āgametha, bhante, yāvāyyo upanando āgacchatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please wait, sirs, until Venerable Upananda arrives.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:1.8
Pali: Dutiyampi kho te bhikkhū …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): A second time … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:1.9
Pali: tatiyampi kho te bhikkhū te manusse etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): A third time those monks said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:1.10
Pali: “dethāvuso, bhattaṁ; pure kālo atikkamatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please give the meal before it is too late.”

Pali: “Yampi mayaṁ, bhante, bhattaṁ karimhā ayyassa upanandassa kāraṇā.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:1.12
Pali: Āgametha, bhante, yāvāyyo upanando āgacchatī”ti.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:1.20
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, nimantito sabhatto samāno purebhattaṁ kulesu cārittaṁ āpajjissasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, how can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:1.21
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:1.22
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:1.23.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu nimantito sabhatto samāno purebhattaṁ kulesu cārittaṁ āpajjeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk who has been invited to a meal visits families beforehand, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:1.24
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmato upanandassa sakyaputtassa upaṭṭhākakulaṁ saṅghassatthāya khādanīyaṁ pāhesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards a family that was supporting Upananda sent fresh food to the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:2.2
Pali: “ayyassa upanandassa dassetvā saṅghassa dātabban”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): They instructed that the food should be shown to Upananda and then given to the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:2.3
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā upanando sakyaputto gāmaṁ piṇḍāya paviṭṭho hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): But on that occasion Upananda had gone to the village for alms. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:2.4
Pali: Atha kho te manussā ārāmaṁ gantvā bhikkhū pucchiṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): When those people arrived at the monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:2.5
Pali: “kahaṁ, bhante, ayyo upanando”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): they asked for Upananda, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:2.6
Pali: “Esāvuso, āyasmā upanando sakyaputto gāmaṁ piṇḍāya paviṭṭho”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): and they were told where he was. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:2.7
Pali: “Idaṁ, bhante, khādanīyaṁ ayyassa upanandassa dassetvā saṅghassa dātabban”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): They said, “Venerables, after showing it to Venerable Upananda, this fresh food is to be given to the Sangha.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:2.8
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks told the Buddha, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:2.9
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): who then gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:2.10
Pali: “tena hi, bhikkhave, paṭiggahetvā nikkhipatha yāva upanando āgacchatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Well then, monks, receive it and put it aside until Upananda returns.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:2.11
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā upanando sakyaputto—“bhagavatā paṭikkhittaṁ purebhattaṁ kulesu cārittaṁ āpajjitun”ti pacchābhattaṁ kulāni payirupāsitvā divā paṭikkami, khādanīyaṁ ussāriyittha. 
English (brahmali, published): When he heard that the Buddha had prohibited visiting families before the meal, Upananda visited them after the meal instead. As a consequence, he returned late to the monastery, and the food had to be returned to the donors. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:2.12
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized Upananda, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:2.13
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā upanando sakyaputto pacchābhattaṁ kulesu cārittaṁ āpajjissatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can Venerable Upananda visit families after the meal?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:2.14
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, upananda, pacchābhattaṁ kulesu cārittaṁ āpajjasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Upananda, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:2.15
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:2.16
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:2.17
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, pacchābhattaṁ kulesu cārittaṁ āpajjissasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, how can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:2.18
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:2.19
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:2.20.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu nimantito sabhatto samāno purebhattaṁ vā pacchābhattaṁ vā kulesu cārittaṁ āpajjeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk who has been invited to a meal visits families beforehand or afterwards, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:2.21
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:3.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū cīvaradānasamaye kukkuccāyantā kulāni na payirupāsanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards it was the robe-giving season. But being afraid of wrongdoing, the monks did not visit families. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:3.2
Pali: Cīvaraṁ parittaṁ uppajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): As a result, they only got a small amount of robe-cloth. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:3.3
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:3.4
Pali: anujānāmi, bhikkhave, cīvaradānasamaye kulāni payirupāsituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to visit families during the robe-giving season. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:3.5
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:3.6.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu nimantito sabhatto samāno purebhattaṁ vā pacchābhattaṁ vā kulesu cārittaṁ āpajjeyya, aññatra samayā, pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk who has been invited to a meal visits families beforehand or afterwards, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:3.7
Pali: Tatthāyaṁ samayo. Cīvaradānasamayo—ayaṁ tattha samayo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the appropriate occasion: it is the robe-giving season.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:3.8
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:4.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū cīvarakammaṁ karonti, attho ca hoti sūciyāpi suttenapi satthakenapi. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the monks were making robes, and they needed a needle, thread, and scissors. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:4.2
Pali: Bhikkhū kukkuccāyantā kulāni na payirupāsanti. 
English (brahmali, published): But being afraid of wrongdoing, they did not visit families. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:4.3
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:4.4
Pali: anujānāmi, bhikkhave, cīvarakārasamaye kulāni payirupāsituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to visit families at a time of making robes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:4.5
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:4.6.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu nimantito sabhatto samāno purebhattaṁ vā pacchābhattaṁ vā kulesu cārittaṁ āpajjeyya, aññatra samayā, pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk who has been invited to a meal visits families beforehand or afterwards, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:4.7
Pali: Tatthāyaṁ samayo. Cīvaradānasamayo, cīvarakārasamayo—ayaṁ tattha samayo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): These are the appropriate occasions: it is the robe-giving season; it is a time of making robes.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:4.8
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:5.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū gilānā honti, attho ca hoti bhesajjehi. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards there were sick monks who needed medicines. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:5.2
Pali: Bhikkhū kukkuccāyantā kulāni na payirupāsanti. 
English (brahmali, published): But being afraid of wrongdoing, the monks did not visit families. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:5.3
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:5.4
Pali: anujānāmi, bhikkhave, santaṁ bhikkhuṁ āpucchā kulāni payirupāsituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to visit families after informing an available monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:5.5
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:5.6.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu nimantito sabhatto samāno santaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpucchā purebhattaṁ vā pacchābhattaṁ vā kulesu cārittaṁ āpajjeyya, aññatra samayā, pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk who has been invited to a meal visits families beforehand or afterwards without informing an available monk, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:5.7
Pali: Tatthāyaṁ samayo. Cīvaradānasamayo, cīvarakārasamayo—ayaṁ tattha samayo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): These are the appropriate occasions: it is the robe-giving season; it is a time of making robes.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.1.5
Pali: Nimantito nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Invited: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.1.6
Pali: pañcannaṁ bhojanānaṁ aññatarena bhojanena nimantito. 
English (brahmali, published): invited to eat any of the five cooked foods. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.1.7
Pali: Sabhatto nāma 
English (brahmali, published): To a meal: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.1.8
Pali: yena nimantito tena sabhatto. 
English (brahmali, published): the invitation includes a meal. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.1.9
Pali: Santaṁ nāma bhikkhuṁ 
English (brahmali, published): An available monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.1.10
Pali: sakkā hoti āpucchā pavisituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): he is able to inform and then enter. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.1.11
Pali: Asantaṁ nāma bhikkhuṁ 
English (brahmali, published): No available monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.1.12
Pali: na sakkā hoti āpucchā pavisituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): he is not able to inform and then enter. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.1.13
Pali: Purebhattaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Beforehand: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.1.14
Pali: yena nimantito taṁ abhuttāvī. 
English (brahmali, published): he has not yet eaten what he has been invited to eat. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.1.15
Pali: Pacchābhattaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Afterwards: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.1.16
Pali: yena nimantito taṁ antamaso kusaggenapi bhuttaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): even if he has just eaten what fits on the tip of a blade of grass from what he has been invited to eat. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.1.17
Pali: Kulaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A family: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.1.18
Pali: cattāri kulāni— khattiyakulaṁ, brāhmaṇakulaṁ, vessakulaṁ, suddakulaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): there are four kinds of families: the aristocratic family, the brahmin family, the merchant family, the worker family. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.1.19
Pali: Kulesu cārittaṁ āpajjeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Visits families: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.1.20
Pali: aññassa gharūpacāraṁ okkamantassa āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he enters the vicinity of someone else’s house, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.1.21
Pali: Paṭhamaṁ pādaṁ ummāraṁ atikkāmeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he crosses the threshold with the first foot, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.1.22
Pali: Dutiyaṁ pādaṁ atikkāmeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he crosses the threshold with the second foot, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.1.23
Pali: Aññatra samayāti 
English (brahmali, published): Except on an appropriate occasion: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.1.24
Pali: ṭhapetvā samayaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): unless it is an appropriate occasion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.1.25
Pali: Cīvaradānasamayo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): It is the robe-giving season: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.1.26
Pali: anatthate kathine vassānassa pacchimo māso, atthate kathine pañca māsā. 
English (brahmali, published): for one who has not participated in the robe-making ceremony, it is the last month of the rainy season. For one who has participated in the robe-making ceremony, it is the five-month period. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.1.27
Pali: Cīvarakārasamayo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): It is a time of making robes: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.1.28
Pali: cīvare kayiramāne. 
English (brahmali, published): when he is making robes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.2.1
Pali: Nimantite nimantitasaññī santaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpucchā purebhattaṁ vā pacchābhattaṁ vā kulesu cārittaṁ āpajjati, aññatra samayā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has been invited, and he perceives that he has, and he visits families beforehand or afterwards without informing an available monk, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.2.2
Pali: Nimantite vematiko santaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpucchā purebhattaṁ vā pacchābhattaṁ vā kulesu cārittaṁ āpajjati, aññatra samayā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has been invited, but he is unsure of it, and he visits families beforehand or afterwards without informing an available monk, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.2.3
Pali: Nimantite animantitasaññī santaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpucchā purebhattaṁ vā pacchābhattaṁ vā kulesu cārittaṁ āpajjati, aññatra samayā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has been invited, but he does not perceive that he has, and he visits families beforehand or afterwards without informing an available monk, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.2.4
Pali: Animantite nimantitasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has not been invited, but he perceives that he has, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.2.5
Pali: Animantite vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has not been invited, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.2.6
Pali: Animantite animantitasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has not been invited, and he does not perceive that he has, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.3.2
Pali: samaye, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is an appropriate occasion; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.3.3
Pali: santaṁ bhikkhuṁ āpucchā pavisati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he enters after informing an available monk; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.3.4
Pali: asantaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpucchā pavisati, 
English (brahmali, published): if, when there is no available monk, he enters without informing anyone; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.3.5
Pali: aññassa gharena maggo hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if the road passes someone else’s house; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.3.6
Pali: gharūpacārena maggo hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if the road passes the vicinity of someone else’s house; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.3.7
Pali: antarārāmaṁ gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is going between monasteries; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.3.8
Pali: bhikkhunupassayaṁ gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is going to a nunnery; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.3.9
Pali: titthiyaseyyaṁ gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is going to the dwelling place of the monastics of another religion; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.3.10
Pali: paṭikkamanaṁ gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is returning to the monastery; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.3.11
Pali: bhattiyagharaṁ gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is going to the house where he has been invited; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.3.12
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.3.13
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.3.14
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc46:6.3.15
Pali: Cārittasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ chaṭṭhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on visiting families before a meal, the sixth, is finished.
