pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:0.5
Pali: 47. Mahānāmasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 47. Accepting an invitation of more than four months 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sakkesu viharati kapilavatthusmiṁ nigrodhārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying in the Sakyan country in the Banyan Tree Monastery at Kapilavatthu, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena mahānāmassa sakkassa bhesajjaṁ ussannaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): Mahānāma the Sakyan had an abundance of tonics. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.1.3
Pali: Atha kho mahānāmo sakko yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho mahānāmo sakko bhagavantaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): He went to the Buddha, bowed, sat down, and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.1.4
Pali: “icchāmahaṁ, bhante, saṅghaṁ catumāsaṁ bhesajjena pavāretun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, I wish to invite the Sangha to ask for tonics for four months.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.1.5
Pali: “Sādhu sādhu, mahānāma. 
English (brahmali, published): “Good, good, Mahānāma. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.1.6
Pali: Tena hi tvaṁ, mahānāma, saṅghaṁ catumāsaṁ bhesajjena pavārehī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Please do so.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.1.7
Pali: Bhikkhū kukkuccāyantā nādhivāsenti. 
English (brahmali, published): But the monks were afraid of wrongdoing and did not accept. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.1.8
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): They then told the Buddha what had happened. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.1.9
Pali: anujānāmi, bhikkhave, catumāsaṁ bhesajjappaccayapavāraṇaṁ sāditun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to accept an invitation to ask for tonics for four months.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū mahānāmaṁ sakkaṁ parittaṁ bhesajjaṁ viññāpenti. Tatheva mahānāmassa sakkassa bhesajjaṁ ussannaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): Yet the monks only asked Mahānāma for a small amount of tonics, and so he still had an abundance. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.2.2
Pali: Dutiyampi kho mahānāmo sakko yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho mahānāmo sakko bhagavantaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): A second time he went to the Buddha, bowed, sat down, and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.2.3
Pali: “icchāmahaṁ, bhante, saṅghaṁ aparampi catumāsaṁ bhesajjena pavāretun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, I wish to invite the Sangha to ask for tonics for a further four months.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.2.4
Pali: “Sādhu sādhu, mahānāma. 
English (brahmali, published): “Good, good, Mahānāma. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.2.5
Pali: Tena hi tvaṁ, mahānāma, saṅghaṁ aparampi catumāsaṁ bhesajjena pavārehī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Please do so.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.2.6
Pali: Bhikkhū kukkuccāyantā nādhivāsenti. 
English (brahmali, published): Again the monks were afraid of wrongdoing and did not accept. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.2.7
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.2.8
Pali: anujānāmi, bhikkhave, puna pavāraṇampi sāditun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to accept a further invitation.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.3.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū mahānāmaṁ sakkaṁ parittaṁyeva bhesajjaṁ viññāpenti. Tatheva mahānāmassa sakkassa bhesajjaṁ ussannaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): Once again the monks only asked Mahānāma for a small amount of tonics, and so he still had an abundance. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.3.2
Pali: Tatiyampi kho mahānāmo sakko yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho mahānāmo sakko bhagavantaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): A third time he went to the Buddha, bowed, sat down, and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.3.3
Pali: “icchāmahaṁ, bhante, saṅghaṁ yāvajīvaṁ bhesajjena pavāretun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, I wish to invite the Sangha to ask for tonics for life.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.3.4
Pali: “Sādhu sādhu, mahānāma. 
English (brahmali, published): “Good, good, Mahānāma. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.3.5
Pali: Tena hi tvaṁ, mahānāma, saṅghaṁ yāvajīvaṁ bhesajjena pavārehī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Please do so.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.3.6
Pali: Bhikkhū kukkuccāyantā nādhivāsenti. 
English (brahmali, published): Yet again the monks were afraid of wrongdoing and did not accept. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.3.7
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.3.8
Pali: anujānāmi, bhikkhave, niccapavāraṇampi sāditun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to accept a permanent invitation.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.4.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū dunnivatthā honti duppārutā anākappasampannā. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the monks from the group of six were shabbily dressed and improper in appearance. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.4.2
Pali: Mahānāmo sakko vattā hoti—“kissa tumhe, bhante, dunnivatthā duppārutā anākappasampannā? 
English (brahmali, published): Mahānāma criticized them, “Venerables, why are you shabbily dressed and improper in appearance? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.4.3
Pali: Nanu nāma pabbajitena sunivatthena bhavitabbaṁ supārutena ākappasampannenā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): Shouldn’t one who has gone forth be suitably dressed and proper in appearance?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.4.4
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhū mahānāme sakke upanandhiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks from the group of six developed a grudge against Mahānāma. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.4.5
Pali: Atha kho chabbaggiyānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ etadahosi—“kena nu kho mayaṁ upāyena mahānāmaṁ sakkaṁ maṅkuṁ kareyyāmā”ti? Atha kho chabbaggiyānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ etadahosi—
English (brahmali, published): Thinking of ways to humiliate him, it occurred to them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.4.6
Pali: “mahānāmena kho, āvuso, sakkena saṅgho bhesajjena pavārito. Handa mayaṁ, āvuso, mahānāmaṁ sakkaṁ sappiṁ viññāpemā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Mahānāma has invited the Sangha to ask for tonics. Let’s ask him for ghee.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.4.7
Pali: Atha kho chabbaggiyā bhikkhū yena mahānāmo sakko tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā mahānāmaṁ sakkaṁ etadavocuṁ—“doṇena, āvuso, sappinā attho”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): They then went to Mahānāma and said, “We need a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>doṇa</i> measure of ghee.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.4.8
Pali: “Ajjaṇho, bhante, āgametha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please wait until tomorrow. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.4.9
Pali: Manussā vajaṁ gatā sappiṁ āharituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): People have gone to the cow-pen to get ghee. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.4.10
Pali: Kālaṁ āharissathā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): You may come and get it in the morning.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.4.11
Pali: Dutiyampi kho …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): A second time 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.4.12
Pali: tatiyampi kho chabbaggiyā bhikkhū mahānāmaṁ sakkaṁ etadavocuṁ—“doṇena, āvuso, sappinā attho”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): and a third time the monks from the group of six said the same thing, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.4.13
Pali: “Ajjaṇho, bhante, āgametha. 
English (brahmali, published): and Mahānāma replied as before. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.4.14
Pali: Manussā vajaṁ gatā sappiṁ āharituṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.4.15
Pali: Kālaṁ āharissathā”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.4.16
Pali: “Kiṁ pana tayā, āvuso, adātukāmena pavāritena, yaṁ tvaṁ pavāretvā na desī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): They then said, “Why do you give an invitation if you don’t wish to give?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.4.17
Pali: Atha kho mahānāmo sakko ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—“kathañhi nāma bhadantā—‘ajjaṇho, bhante, āgamethā’ti vuccamānā nāgamessantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Mahānāma complained and criticized them, “How can they not wait for one day when asked?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.4.18
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū mahānāmassa sakkassa ujjhāyantassa khiyyantassa vipācentassa. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of Mahānāma, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.4.19
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.4.20
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū mahānāmena sakkena—‘ajjaṇho, bhante, āgamethā’ti vuccamānā nāgamessantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the monks from the group of six not wait for one day when asked by Mahānāma?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.4.21
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, mahānāmena sakkena—‘ajjaṇho, bhante, āgamethā’ti vuccamānā nāgamethā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you acted like this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.4.22
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.4.23
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.4.24
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, mahānāmena sakkena—“ajjaṇho, bhante, āgamethā”ti vuccamānā nāgamessatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how could you act like this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.4.25
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.4.26
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:1.4.27.1
Pali: “Agilānena bhikkhunā catumāsappaccayapavāraṇā sāditabbā, aññatra punapavāraṇāya, aññatra niccapavāraṇāya; tato ce uttari sādiyeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘A monk who is not sick may accept an invitation to ask for requisites for four months. If he accepts one beyond that limit, except if it is a further invitation or a permanent invitation, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.1.1
Pali: Agilānena bhikkhunā catumāsappaccayapavāraṇā sāditabbāti 
English (brahmali, published): A monk who is not sick may accept an invitation to ask for requisites for four months: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.1.2
Pali: gilānappaccayapavāraṇā sāditabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): he may accept an invitation to ask for requisites for the sick. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.1.3
Pali: Punapavāraṇāpi sāditabbāti 
English (brahmali, published): He may also accept a further invitation: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.1.4
Pali: yadā gilāno bhavissāmi tadā viññāpessāmīti. 
English (brahmali, published): he should think, “I’ll ask when I’m sick.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.1.5
Pali: Niccapavāraṇāpi sāditabbāti 
English (brahmali, published): He may also accept a permanent invitation: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.1.6
Pali: yadā gilāno bhavissāmi tadā viññāpessāmīti. 
English (brahmali, published): he should think, “I’ll ask when I’m sick.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.1.7
Pali: Tato ce uttari sādiyeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): If he accepts one beyond that limit: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.1.8
Pali: atthi pavāraṇā bhesajjapariyantā na rattipariyantā, 
English (brahmali, published): there are invitations that have a limit on the tonics, but no limit on the time period; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.1.9
Pali: atthi pavāraṇā rattipariyantā na bhesajjapariyantā, 
English (brahmali, published): there are invitations that have a limit on the time period, but no limit on the tonics; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.1.10
Pali: atthi pavāraṇā bhesajjapariyantā ca rattipariyantā ca, 
English (brahmali, published): there are invitations that have a limit on both the tonics and the time period; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.1.11
Pali: atthi pavāraṇā neva bhesajjapariyantā na rattipariyantā. 
English (brahmali, published): there are invitations that have neither a limit on the tonics nor on the time period. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.1.12
Pali: Bhesajjapariyantā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Limit on the tonics: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.1.13
Pali: bhesajjāni pariggahitāni honti—“ettakehi bhesajjehi pavāremī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): the tonics are restricted: “I invite you to ask for these particular tonics.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.1.14
Pali: Rattipariyantā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Limit on the time period: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.1.15
Pali: rattiyo pariggahitāyo honti—“ettakāsu rattīsu pavāremī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): the time period is restricted: “I invite you to ask during this particular period of time.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.1.16
Pali: Bhesajjapariyantā ca rattipariyantā ca nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Limit on both the tonics and the time period: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.1.17
Pali: bhesajjāni ca pariggahitāni honti rattiyo ca pariggahitāyo honti—“ettakehi bhesajjehi ettakāsu rattīsu pavāremī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): both the tonics and the time period are restricted: “I invite you to ask for these particular tonics during this particular period of time.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.1.18
Pali: Neva bhesajjapariyantā na rattipariyantā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Neither a limit on the tonics nor on the time period: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.1.19
Pali: bhesajjāni ca apariggahitāni honti rattiyo ca apariggahitāyo honti. 
English (brahmali, published): neither the tonics nor the time period is restricted. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.1.20
Pali: Bhesajjapariyante—yehi bhesajjehi pavārito hoti tāni bhesajjāni ṭhapetvā aññāni bhesajjāni viññāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When there is a limit on the tonics, if he asks for tonics other than those he has been invited to ask for, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.1.21
Pali: Rattipariyante—yāsu rattīsu pavārito hoti, tā rattiyo ṭhapetvā aññāsu rattīsu viññāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When there is a limit on the time period, if he asks outside of the period during which he has been invited to ask, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.1.22
Pali: Bhesajjapariyante ca rattipariyante ca—yehi bhesajjehi pavārito hoti, tāni bhesajjāni ṭhapetvā yāsu rattīsu pavārito hoti, tā rattiyo ṭhapetvā aññāni bhesajjāni aññāsu rattīsu viññāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When there is a limit on both the tonics and on the time period, if he asks for tonics other than those he has been invited to ask for or he asks outside of the period during which he has been invited to ask, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.1.23
Pali: Neva bhesajjapariyante na rattipariyante, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): When there is neither a limit on the tonics nor on the time period, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.2.1
Pali: Na bhesajjena karaṇīyena bhesajjaṁ viññāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he asks for tonics when he has no need for tonics, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.2.2
Pali: Aññena bhesajjena karaṇīyena aññaṁ bhesajjaṁ viññāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he asks for a tonic other than the tonic he needs, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.2.3.1
Pali: Tatuttari tatuttarisaññī bhesajjaṁ viññāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is beyond the limit, and he perceives it as such, and he asks for tonics, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.2.4
Pali: Tatuttari vematiko bhesajjaṁ viññāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is beyond the limit, but he is unsure of it, and he asks for tonics, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.2.5
Pali: Tatuttari natatuttarisaññī bhesajjaṁ viññāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is beyond the limit, but he does not perceive it as such, and he asks for tonics, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.2.6
Pali: Natatuttari tatuttarisaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not beyond the limit, but he perceives it as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.2.7
Pali: Natatuttari vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not beyond the limit, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.2.8
Pali: Natatuttari natatuttarisaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not beyond the limit, and he does not perceive it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.3.2
Pali: yehi bhesajjehi pavārito hoti tāni bhesajjāni viññāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he asks for those tonics for which he was invited to ask; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.3.3
Pali: yāsu rattīsu pavārito hoti tāsu rattīsu viññāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he asks during the time period for which he was invited to ask; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.3.4
Pali: “imehi tayā bhesajjehi pavāritāmha, amhākañca iminā ca iminā ca bhesajjena attho”ti ācikkhitvā viññāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he asks by informing, “You have invited me to ask for these tonics, but I need such-and-such a tonic;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.3.5
Pali: “yāsu tayā rattīsu pavāritāmha tāyo ca rattiyo vītivattā amhākañca bhesajjena attho”ti ācikkhitvā viññāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he asks by informing, “The time period during which you invited me to ask has passed, but I need tonics;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.3.6
Pali: ñātakānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): if it is from relatives; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.3.7
Pali: pavāritānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is from those who have given an invitation; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.3.8
Pali: aññassatthāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is for the benefit of someone else; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.3.9
Pali: attano dhanena, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is by means of his own property; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.3.10
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.3.11
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc47:2.3.12
Pali: Mahānāmasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ sattamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on accepting an invitation of more than four months, the seventh, is finished.

Pali: 48. Uyyuttasenāsikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 48. Going to see a mobilized army 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena rājā pasenadi kosalo senāya abbhuyyāto hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): King Pasenadi of Kosala was marching out with the army, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:1.3
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhū uyyuttaṁ senaṁ dassanāya agamaṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): and the monks from the group of six went to see it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:1.4
Pali: Addasā kho rājā pasenadi kosalo chabbaggiye bhikkhū dūratova āgacchante. 
English (brahmali, published): When King Pasenadi saw the monks coming, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:1.5
Pali: Disvāna pakkosāpetvā etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): he summoned them and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:1.6
Pali: “kissa tumhe, bhante, āgatatthā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, why have you come here?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:1.7
Pali: “Mahārājānaṁ mayaṁ daṭṭhukāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “We wish to see the great king.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:1.8
Pali: “Kiṁ, bhante, maṁ diṭṭhena yuddhābhinandinaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): “What’s the use of seeing me finding pleasure in battle? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:1.9
Pali: nanu bhagavā passitabbo”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): Shouldn’t you see the Buddha?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:1.10
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): And people complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:1.11
Pali: “kathañhi nāma samaṇā sakyaputtiyā uyyuttaṁ senaṁ dassanāya āgacchissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the Sakyan monastics go to see the army? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:1.12
Pali: Amhākampi alābhā, amhākampi dulladdhaṁ, ye mayaṁ ājīvassa hetu puttadārassa kāraṇā senāya āgacchāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): It’s our misfortune that we must go out with the army for the sake of our livelihoods and because of our wives and children.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:1.13
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:1.14
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:1.15
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū uyyuttaṁ senaṁ dassanāya gacchissantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the monks from the group of six go to see the army?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:1.16
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, uyyuttaṁ senaṁ dassanāya gacchathā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:1.17
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:1.18
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:1.19
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, uyyuttaṁ senaṁ dassanāya gacchissatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:1.20
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:1.21
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:1.22.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu uyyuttaṁ senaṁ dassanāya gaccheyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk goes to see an army, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:1.23
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarassa bhikkhuno mātulo senāya gilāno hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards a certain monk had a sick uncle in the army. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:2.2
Pali: So tassa bhikkhuno santike dūtaṁ pāhesi—
English (brahmali, published): The uncle sent a message to that monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:2.3
Pali: “ahañhi senāya gilāno. 
English (brahmali, published): “I’m with the army and I’m sick. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:2.4
Pali: Āgacchatu bhadanto. 
English (brahmali, published): Please come, venerable. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:2.5
Pali: Icchāmi bhadantassa āgatan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I want you to come.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:2.6
Pali: Atha kho tassa bhikkhuno etadahosi—“bhagavatā sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ—‘na uyyuttaṁ senaṁ dassanāya gantabban’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Knowing that the Buddha had laid down a rule against going to see an army, that monk thought, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:2.7
Pali: Ayañca me mātulo senāya gilāno. 
English (brahmali, published): “I have a sick uncle in the army. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:2.8
Pali: Kathaṁ nu kho mayā paṭipajjitabban”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): What should I do now?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:2.9
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): And he told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:2.10
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:2.11
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, tathārūpappaccayā senāya gantuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to go to the army when there’s a suitable reason. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:2.12
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:2.13.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu uyyuttaṁ senaṁ dassanāya gaccheyya, aññatra tathārūpappaccayā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk goes to see an army, except if there is a suitable reason, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.1.5
Pali: Uyyuttā nāma senā 
English (brahmali, published): An army: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.1.6
Pali: gāmato nikkhamitvā niviṭṭhā vā hoti payātā vā. 
English (brahmali, published): it has left the inhabited area and is either encamped or marching. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.1.7
Pali: Senā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): An army: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.1.8
Pali: hatthī assā rathā pattī. 
English (brahmali, published): elephants, horses, chariots, infantry. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.1.9
Pali: Dvādasapuriso hatthī, tipuriso asso, catupuriso ratho, cattāro purisā sarahatthā patti. 
English (brahmali, published): An elephant has twelve men; a horse has three men; a chariot has four men; an infantry unit has four men with arrows in hand. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.1.10
Pali: Dassanāya gacchati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is on his way to see it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.1.11
Pali: Yattha ṭhito passati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): Wherever he stands to see it, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.1.12
Pali: Dassanūpacāraṁ vijahitvā punappunaṁ passati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): Every time he goes beyond the range of sight and then sees it again, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.1.13
Pali: Aññatra tathārūpappaccayāti 
English (brahmali, published): Except if there is a suitable reason: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.1.14
Pali: ṭhapetvā tathārūpappaccayaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): unless there is a suitable reason. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.2.1
Pali: Uyyutte uyyuttasaññī dassanāya gacchati, aññatra tathārūpappaccayā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an army, and he perceives it as such, and he goes to see it, except if there is a suitable reason, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.2.2
Pali: Uyyutte vematiko dassanāya gacchati, aññatra tathārūpappaccayā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an army, but he is unsure of it, and he goes to see it, except if there is a suitable reason, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.2.3
Pali: Uyyutte anuyyuttasaññī dassanāya gacchati, aññatra tathārūpappaccayā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an army, but he does not perceive it as such, and he goes to see it, except if there is a suitable reason, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.2.4
Pali: Ekamekaṁ dassanāya gacchati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is on his way to see one division of a fourfold army, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.2.5
Pali: Yattha ṭhito passati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): Wherever he stands to see it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.2.6
Pali: Dassanūpacāraṁ vijahitvā punappunaṁ passati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): Every time he goes beyond the range of sight and then sees it again, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.2.7
Pali: Anuyyutte uyyuttasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not an army, but he perceives it as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.2.8
Pali: Anuyyutte vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not an army, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.2.9
Pali: Anuyyutte anuyyuttasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not an army, and he does not perceive it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.3.2
Pali: ārāme ṭhito passati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he sees it while standing in a monastery; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.3.3
Pali: bhikkhussa ṭhitokāsaṁ vā nisinnokāsaṁ vā nipannokāsaṁ vā āgacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if the army comes to where the monk is standing, sitting, or lying down; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.3.4
Pali: paṭipathaṁ gacchanto passati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he sees it while walking in the opposite direction; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.3.5
Pali: tathārūpappaccayā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he has a suitable reason; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.3.6
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.3.7
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.3.8
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc48:3.3.9
Pali: Uyyuttasenāsikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ aṭṭhamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on going to see a mobilized army, the eighth, is finished.

Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis

English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:0.4
Pali: Acelakavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on naked ascetics 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:0.5
Pali: 49. Senāvāsasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 49. Staying more than three days with an army 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū sati karaṇīye senaṁ gantvā atirekatirattaṁ senāya vasanti. 
English (brahmali, published): the monks from the group of six went to the army on some business, and they stayed there for more than three nights. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:1.3
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:1.4
Pali: “kathañhi nāma samaṇā sakyaputtiyā senāya vasissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the Sakyan monastics stay with the army? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:1.5
Pali: Amhākampi alābhā, amhākampi dulladdhaṁ, ye mayaṁ ājīvassa hetu puttadārassa kāraṇā senāya paṭivasāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): It’s our misfortune that we must stay with the army for the sake of our livelihoods and because of our wives and children.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:1.6
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:1.7
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:1.8
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū atirekatirattaṁ senāya vasissantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the monks from the group of six stay with the army for more than three nights?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:1.9
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, atirekatirattaṁ senāya vasathā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:1.10
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:1.11
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:1.12
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, atirekatirattaṁ senāya vasissatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:1.13
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:1.14
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:1.15.1
Pali: “Siyā ca tassa bhikkhuno kocideva paccayo senaṁ gamanāya, dirattatirattaṁ tena bhikkhunā senāya vasitabbaṁ. Tato ce uttari vaseyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If that monk has a reason for going to the army, he may stay with the army for two or three nights. If he stays longer than that, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:2.1.1
Pali: Siyā ca tassa bhikkhuno kocideva paccayo senaṁ gamanāyāti 
English (brahmali, published): If that monk has a reason for going to the army: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:2.1.2
Pali: siyā paccayo siyā karaṇīyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if he has a reason, if he has something to  do. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:2.1.3
Pali: Dirattatirattaṁ tena bhikkhunā senāya vasitabbanti 
English (brahmali, published): He may stay with the army for two or three nights: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:2.1.4
Pali: dvetisso rattiyo vasitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): he may stay for two or for three nights. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:2.1.5
Pali: Tato ce uttari vaseyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): If he stays longer than that: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:2.1.6
Pali: catutthe divase atthaṅgate sūriye senāya vasati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is staying with the army at sunset on the fourth day, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:2.2.1
Pali: Atirekatiratte atirekasaññī senāya vasati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than three nights, and he perceives it as more, and he is staying with the army, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:2.2.2
Pali: Atirekatiratte vematiko senāya vasati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than three nights, but he is unsure of it, and he is staying with the army, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:2.2.3
Pali: Atirekatiratte ūnakasaññī senāya vasati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than three nights, but he perceives it as less, and he is staying with the army, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:2.2.4
Pali: Ūnakatiratte atirekasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than three nights, but he perceives it as more, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:2.2.5
Pali: Ūnakatiratte vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than three nights, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:2.2.6
Pali: Ūnakatiratte ūnakasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than three nights, and he perceives it as less, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:2.3.2
Pali: dvetisso rattiyo vasati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he stays for two or three nights; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:2.3.3
Pali: ūnakadvetisso rattiyo vasati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he stays for less than two or three nights; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:2.3.4
Pali: dve rattiyo vasitvā tatiyāya rattiyā purāruṇā nikkhamitvā puna vasati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he stays for two nights, then leaves before dawn on the third night, and then stays again; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:2.3.5
Pali: gilāno vasati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he stays because he is sick; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:2.3.6
Pali: gilānassa karaṇīyena vasati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he stays because he has to take care of someone who is sick; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:2.3.7
Pali: senā vā paṭisenāya ruddhā hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if the army is obstructed by an enemy army; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:2.3.8
Pali: kenaci palibuddho hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is obstructed from leaving; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:2.3.9
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:2.3.10
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:2.3.11
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc49:2.3.12
Pali: Senāvāsasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ navamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on staying more than three days with an army, the ninth, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:0.4
Pali: Acelakavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on naked ascetics 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:0.5
Pali: 50. Uyyodhikasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 50. Seeing an army in action 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū dirattatirattaṁ senāya vasamānā uyyodhikampi balaggampi senābyūhampi anīkadassanampi gacchanti. 
English (brahmali, published): the monks from the group of six were staying with the army for two or three nights. They went to see battles, troop reviews, the massing of the army, and troop inspections. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:1.3
Pali: Aññataropi chabbaggiyo bhikkhu uyyodhikaṁ gantvā kaṇḍena paṭividdho hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): One of the monks who went to a battle was struck by an arrow. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:1.4
Pali: Manussā taṁ bhikkhuṁ uppaṇḍesuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): People teased him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:1.5
Pali: “kacci, bhante, suyuddhaṁ ahosi, kati te lakkhāni laddhānī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “We hope you had a good battle, venerable. How many targets did you hit?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:1.6
Pali: So bhikkhu tehi manussehi uppaṇḍiyamāno maṅku ahosi. 
English (brahmali, published): And because they teased him, he felt humiliated. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:1.7
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:1.8
Pali: “kathañhi nāma samaṇā sakyaputtiyā uyyodhikaṁ dassanāya āgacchissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the Sakyan monastics go to see a battle? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:1.9
Pali: Amhākampi alābhā, amhākampi dulladdhaṁ, ye mayaṁ ājīvassa hetu puttadārassa kāraṇā uyyodhikaṁ āgacchāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): It’s our misfortune that we must go to battles for the sake of our livelihoods and because of our wives and children.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:1.10
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:1.11
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:1.12
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū uyyodhikaṁ dassanāya gacchissantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the monks from the group of six go to see a battle?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:1.13
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, uyyodhikaṁ dassanāya gacchathā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:1.14
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:1.15
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:1.16
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, uyyodhikaṁ dassanāya gacchissatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:1.17
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:1.18
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:1.19.1
Pali: “Dirattatirattañce bhikkhu senāya vasamāno uyyodhikaṁ vā balaggaṁ vā senābyūhaṁ vā anīkadassanaṁ vā gaccheyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk who is staying with an army for two or three nights goes to a battle, a troop review, a massing of the army, or a troop inspection, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.1.1
Pali: Dirattatirattañce bhikkhu senāya vasamānoti 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk who is staying with an army for two or three nights: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.1.2
Pali: dvetisso rattiyo vasamāno. 
English (brahmali, published): he is staying for two or for three nights. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.1.3
Pali: Uyyodhikaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A battle: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.1.4
Pali: yattha sampahāro dissati. 
English (brahmali, published): wherever fighting is seen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.1.5
Pali: Balaggaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A troop review: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.1.6
Pali: ettakā hatthī, ettakā assā, ettakā rathā, ettakā pattī. 
English (brahmali, published): so many elephants, so many horses, so many chariots, so much infantry. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.1.7
Pali: Senābyūhaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A massing of the army: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.1.8
Pali: ito hatthī hontu, ito assā hontu, ito rathā hontu, ito pattikā hontu. 
English (brahmali, published): the elephants should set out from here; the horses should set out from here; the chariots should set out from here; the infantry should set out from here. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.1.9
Pali: Anīkaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A troop: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.1.10
Pali: hatthānīkaṁ, assānīkaṁ, rathānīkaṁ, pattānīkaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): an elephant troop, a horse troop, a chariot troop, an infantry troop. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.1.11
Pali: Tayo hatthī pacchimaṁ hatthānīkaṁ, tayo assā pacchimaṁ assānīkaṁ, tayo rathā pacchimaṁ rathānīkaṁ, cattāro purisā sarahatthā pattī pacchimaṁ pattānīkaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The smallest troop of elephants is three elephants; the smallest troop of horses is three horses; the smallest troop of chariots is three chariots; the smallest troop of infantry is four men with arrows in hand. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.1.12
Pali: Dassanāya gacchati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is on his way to see it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.1.13
Pali: Yattha ṭhito passati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): Wherever he stands to see it, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.1.14
Pali: Dassanūpacāraṁ vijahitvā punappunaṁ passati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): Every time he goes beyond the range of sight and then sees it again, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.2.1
Pali: Ekamekaṁ dassanāya gacchati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is on his way to see one division of a fourfold army, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.2.2
Pali: Yattha ṭhito passati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): Wherever he stands to see it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.2.3
Pali: Dassanūpacāraṁ vijahitvā punappunaṁ passati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): Every time he goes beyond the range of sight and then sees it again, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.3.2
Pali: ārāme ṭhito passati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he sees it while standing in a monastery; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.3.3
Pali: bhikkhussa ṭhitokāsaṁ vā nisinnokāsaṁ vā nipannokāsaṁ vā āgantvā sampahāro dissati, 
English (brahmali, published): if the army comes to where the monk is standing, sitting, or lying down, and he then sees fighting; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.3.4
Pali: paṭipathaṁ gacchanto passati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he sees it while walking in the opposite direction; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.3.5
Pali: sati karaṇīye gantvā passati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he goes because there is something to be done, and he then sees it; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.3.6
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.3.7
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.3.8
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.3.9
Pali: Uyyodhikasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ dasamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on seeing an army in action, the tenth, is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.3.10
Pali: Acelakavaggo pañcamo. 
English (brahmali, published): The fifth subchapter on naked ascetics is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.3.11
Pali: Tassuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.3.12
Pali: Pūvaṁ kathopanandassa, 
English (brahmali, published): “Cookie, talking, three on Upananda, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.3.13
Pali: tayaṁpaṭṭhākameva ca; 
English (brahmali, published): And indeed supporting; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.3.14
Pali: Mahānāmo pasenadi, 
English (brahmali, published): Mahānāma, Pasenadi, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc50:2.3.15
Pali: senāviddho ime dasāti. 
English (brahmali, published): Army, and struck: those are the ten.”

Pali: 51. Surāpānasikkhāpada

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā cetiyesu cārikaṁ caramāno yena bhaddavatikā tena pāyāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion when the Buddha was wandering in the country of Ceti on his way to Bhaddavatikā, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.2
Pali: Addasaṁsu kho gopālakā pasupālakā kassakā pathāvino bhagavantaṁ dūratova āgacchantaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): he was seen by a number of cowherds, shepherds, farmers, and travelers. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.3
Pali: Disvāna bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ—“mā kho, bhante, bhagavā ambatitthaṁ agamāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): They said to him, “Sir, don’t go to Ambatittha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.4
Pali: Ambatitthe, bhante, jaṭilassa assame nāgo paṭivasati iddhimā āsiviso ghoraviso. 
English (brahmali, published): There’s a highly venomous dragon with supernormal powers there, in the hermitage of a dreadlocked ascetic. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.5
Pali: So bhagavantaṁ mā viheṭhesī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Don’t let it harm you.”

English (brahmali, published): The Buddha was silent.

English (brahmali, published): They repeated their request a second

Pali: tatiyampi kho gopālakā pasupālakā kassakā pathāvino bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): and a third time,

Pali: “mā kho, bhante, bhagavā ambatitthaṁ agamāsi. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.10

Pali: Tatiyampi kho bhagavā tuṇhī ahosi. 
English (brahmali, published): and the Buddha remained silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.13
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā anupubbena cārikaṁ caramāno yena bhaddavatikā tadavasari. Tatra sudaṁ bhagavā bhaddavatikāyaṁ viharati. 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha then continued on to Bhaddavatikā, and he stayed there. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.14
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā sāgato yena ambatitthassa jaṭilassa assamo tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā agyāgāraṁ pavisitvā tiṇasanthārakaṁ paññapetvā nisīdi pallaṅkaṁ ābhujitvā ujuṁ kāyaṁ paṇidhāya parimukhaṁ satiṁ upaṭṭhapetvā. 
English (brahmali, published): Just then Venerable Sāgata went to the hermitage of that dreadlocked ascetic and entered his fire hut. After preparing a spread of grass, he sat down, crossed his legs, straightened his body, and established mindfulness in front of him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.15
Pali: Addasā kho so nāgo āyasmantaṁ sāgataṁ paviṭṭhaṁ. Disvāna dummano padhūpāyi. 
English (brahmali, published): Seeing that Sāgata had entered the fire hut, the dragon was upset and emitted smoke. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.16
Pali: Āyasmāpi sāgato padhūpāyi. 
English (brahmali, published): Sāgata, too, emitted smoke. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.17
Pali: Atha kho so nāgo makkhaṁ asahamāno pajjali. 
English (brahmali, published): The dragon was not able to contain his rage and emitted flames. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.18
Pali: Āyasmāpi sāgato tejodhātuṁ samāpajjitvā pajjali. 
English (brahmali, published): Sāgata entered the fire element and he, too, emitted flames. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.19
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā sāgato tassa nāgassa tejasā tejaṁ pariyādiyitvā yena bhaddavatikā tenupasaṅkami. 
English (brahmali, published): Then, after conquering fire with fire, Sāgata went to Bhaddavatikā. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.20
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā bhaddavatikāyaṁ yathābhirantaṁ viharitvā yena kosambī tena cārikaṁ pakkāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): After staying at Bhaddavatikā for as long as he liked, the Buddha set out wandering toward Kosambī. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.21
Pali: Assosuṁ kho kosambikā upāsakā—“ayyo kira sāgato ambatitthikena nāgena saddhiṁ saṅgāmesī”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.22
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā anupubbena cārikaṁ caramāno yena kosambī tadavasari. Atha kho kosambikā upāsakā bhagavato paccuggamanaṁ karitvā yenāyasmā sāgato tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; 
English (brahmali, published): When he arrived, the lay followers there received him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.23
Pali: upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ sāgataṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhaṁsu. Ekamantaṁ ṭhitā kho kosambikā upāsakā āyasmantaṁ sāgataṁ etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): But the lay followers at Kosambī had heard about Sāgata fighting the Ambatittha dragon. And so after receiving the Buddha, they went to see Sāgata. They bowed, stood to one side, and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.24
Pali: “kiṁ, bhante, ayyānaṁ dullabhañca manāpañca, kiṁ paṭiyādemā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, what can we prepare for you that’s delicious but hard to get?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.25
Pali: Evaṁ vutte, chabbaggiyā bhikkhū kosambike upāsake etadavocuṁ—“atthāvuso, kāpotikā nāma pasannā bhikkhūnaṁ dullabhā ca manāpā ca, taṁ paṭiyādethā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks from the group of six replied, “There’s a delicious liquor called Kāpotikā, which is hard for the monks to get. Prepare that.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.26
Pali: Atha kho kosambikā upāsakā ghare ghare kāpotikaṁ pasannaṁ paṭiyādetvā āyasmantaṁ sāgataṁ piṇḍāya paviṭṭhaṁ disvāna āyasmantaṁ sāgataṁ etadavocuṁ—“pivatu, bhante, ayyo sāgato kāpotikaṁ pasannaṁ, pivatu, bhante, ayyo sāgato kāpotikaṁ pasannan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): And the lay followers prepared Kāpotikā in house after house. Then, when they saw that Sāgata had entered the town for alms, they said to him, “Drink, venerable, drink the Kāpotikā liquor.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.27
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā sāgato ghare ghare kāpotikaṁ pasannaṁ pivitvā nagaramhā nikkhamanto nagaradvāre paripati. 
English (brahmali, published): Sāgata drank that liquor in house after house, and as he was leaving town, he collapsed at the town gate. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.28
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā sambahulehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ nagaramhā nikkhamanto addasa āyasmantaṁ sāgataṁ nagaradvāre paripatantaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Just then the Buddha, together with a number of monks, was also leaving town, and he saw Sāgata at the town gate. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.29
Pali: Disvāna bhikkhū āmantesi—“gaṇhatha, bhikkhave, sāgatan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): He said, “Monks, pick up Sāgata.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.30
Pali: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho te bhikkhū bhagavato paṭissuṇitvā āyasmantaṁ sāgataṁ ārāmaṁ netvā yena bhagavā tena sīsaṁ katvā nipātesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Saying, “Yes, sir,” they led him to the monastery, where they put him down with his head toward the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.31
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā sāgato parivattitvā yena bhagavā tena pāde karitvā seyyaṁ kappesi. 
English (brahmali, published): But Sāgata turned around, pointing his feet toward the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.32
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā bhikkhū āmantesi—“nanu, bhikkhave, pubbe sāgato tathāgate sagāravo ahosi sappatisso”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha said, “Previously, monks, wasn’t Sāgata respectful and deferential toward me?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.33
Pali: “Evaṁ, bhante”. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.34
Pali: “Api nu kho, bhikkhave, sāgato etarahi tathāgate sagāravo sappatisso”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “But is he now?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.35
Pali: “No hetaṁ, bhante”. 
English (brahmali, published): “Certainly not.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.36
Pali: “Nanu, bhikkhave, sāgato ambatitthikena nāgena saddhiṁ saṅgāmesī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Just recently, didn’t Sāgata fight the Ambatittha dragon?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.37
Pali: “Evaṁ, bhante”. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.38
Pali: “Api nu kho, bhikkhave, sāgato etarahi pahoti nāgena saddhiṁ saṅgāmetun”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Would he now be able to fight a dragon?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.39
Pali: “No hetaṁ, bhante”. 
English (brahmali, published): “Certainly not.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.40
Pali: “Api nu kho, bhikkhave, taṁ pātabbaṁ yaṁ pivitvā visaññī assā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “So, monks, should one drink that which makes one senseless?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.41
Pali: “No hetaṁ, bhante”. 
English (brahmali, published): “Certainly not, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.42
Pali: “Ananucchavikaṁ, bhikkhave, sāgatassa ananulomikaṁ appatirūpaṁ assāmaṇakaṁ akappiyaṁ akaraṇīyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s not suitable, monks, it’s not proper for Sāgata, it’s not worthy of a monastic, it’s not allowable, it’s not to be done. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.43
Pali: Kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, sāgato majjaṁ pivissati. 
English (brahmali, published): How could Sāgata drink alcoholic drinks? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.44
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.45
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:1.46.1
Pali: “Surāmerayapāne pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk drinks this or that kind of alcoholic drink, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:2.1.1
Pali: Surā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): This kind of alcoholic drink: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:2.1.2
Pali: piṭṭhasurā pūvasurā odanasurā kiṇṇapakkhittā sambhārasaṁyuttā. 
English (brahmali, published): alcoholic drinks made from flour, alcoholic drinks made from cookies, alcoholics drink made from rice, those with yeast added, those made from a combination of ingredients. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:2.1.3
Pali: Merayo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): That kind of alcoholic drink: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:2.1.4
Pali: pupphāsavo phalāsavo madhvāsavo guḷāsavo sambhārasaṁyutto. 
English (brahmali, published): alcoholic drinks made from flowers, alcoholic drinks made from fruit, alcoholic drinks made from honey, alcoholic drinks made from sugar, those made from a combination of ingredients. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:2.1.5
Pali: Piveyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Drinks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:2.1.6
Pali: antamaso kusaggenapi pivati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he drinks even what fits on the tip of a blade of grass, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:2.2.1
Pali: Majje majjasaññī pivati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an alcoholic drink, and he perceives it as such, and he drinks it, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:2.2.2
Pali: Majje vematiko pivati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an alcoholic drink, but he is unsure of it, and he drinks it, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:2.2.3
Pali: Majje amajjasaññī pivati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an alcoholic drink, but he perceives it as non-alcoholic, and he drinks it, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:2.2.4
Pali: Amajje majjasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a non-alcoholic drink, but he perceives it as alcoholic, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:2.2.5
Pali: Amajje vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a non-alcoholic drink, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:2.2.6
Pali: Amajje amajjasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a non-alcoholic drink, and he perceives it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:2.3.2
Pali: amajjañca hoti majjavaṇṇaṁ majjagandhaṁ majjarasaṁ taṁ pivati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he drinks a non-alcoholic drink that has the color, smell, or taste of an alcoholic drink; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:2.3.3
Pali: sūpasampāke, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is cooked in a bean curry; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:2.3.4
Pali: maṁsasampāke, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is cooked with meat; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:2.3.5
Pali: telasampāke, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is cooked with oil; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:2.3.6
Pali: āmalakaphāṇite, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is in syrup from emblic myrobalan; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:2.3.7
Pali: amajjaṁ ariṭṭhaṁ pivati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he drinks a drink that is normally alcoholic, but which is actually without alcohol; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:2.3.8
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:2.3.9
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc51:2.3.10
Pali: Surāpānasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ paṭhamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on drinking alcohol, the first, is finished.

English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession

English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on drinking alcohol 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:0.5
Pali: 52. Aṅgulipatodakasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 52. Tickling 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū sattarasavaggiyaṁ bhikkhuṁ aṅgulipatodakena hāsesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): the monks from the group of six tickled one of the monks from the group of seventeen to make him laugh. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:1.3
Pali: So bhikkhu uttanto anassāsako kālamakāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): Not being able to catch his breath, he died. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:1.4
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:1.5
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū bhikkhuṁ aṅgulipatodakena hāsessantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the monks from the group of six tickle a monk to make him laugh?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:1.6
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, bhikkhuṁ aṅgulipatodakena hāsethā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:1.7
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:1.8
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:1.9
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, bhikkhuṁ aṅgulipatodakena hāsessatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:1.10
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:1.11
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:1.12.1
Pali: “Aṅgulipatodake pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk tickles someone, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:2.1.1
Pali: Aṅgulipatodako nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Tickles someone: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:2.1.2
Pali: upasampanno upasampannaṁ hasādhippāyo kāyena kāyaṁ āmasati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if one who is fully ordained makes physical contact with another who is fully ordained, body to body, with the aim of making him laugh, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:2.2.1
Pali: Upasampanne upasampannasaññī aṅgulipatodakena hāseti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, and he perceives him as such, and he tickles him to make him laugh, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:2.2.2
Pali: Upasampanne vematiko aṅgulipatodakena hāseti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, but he is unsure of it, and he tickles him to make him laugh, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:2.2.3
Pali: Upasampanne anupasampannasaññī aṅgulipatodakena hāseti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, but he does not perceive him as such, and he tickles him to make him laugh, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:2.2.4
Pali: Kāyena kāyapaṭibaddhaṁ āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, with his own body, he makes physical contact with something connected to the other monk’s body, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:2.2.5
Pali: Kāyapaṭibaddhena kāyaṁ āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, with something connected to his own body, he makes physical contact with the other monk’s body, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:2.2.6
Pali: Kāyapaṭibaddhena kāyapaṭibaddhaṁ āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, with something connected to his own body, he makes physical contact with something connected to the other monk’s body, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:2.2.7
Pali: Nissaggiyena kāyaṁ āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, by releasing something, he makes physical contact with the other monk’s body, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:2.2.8
Pali: Nissaggiyena kāyapaṭibaddhaṁ āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, by releasing something, he makes physical contact with something connected to the other monk’s body, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:2.2.9
Pali: Nissaggiyena nissaggiyaṁ āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, by releasing something, he makes physical contact with something released by the other monk, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:2.2.10
Pali: Anupasampannaṁ kāyena kāyaṁ āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, with his own body, he makes physical contact with the body of someone who is not fully ordained, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:2.2.11
Pali: Kāyena kāyapaṭibaddhaṁ āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, with his own body, he makes physical contact with something connected to the body of someone who is not fully ordained, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:2.2.12
Pali: Kāyapaṭibaddhena kāyaṁ āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, with something connected to his own body, he makes physical contact with the body of someone who is not fully ordained, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:2.2.13
Pali: Kāyapaṭibaddhena kāyapaṭibaddhaṁ āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, with something connected to his own body, he makes physical contact with something connected to the body of someone who is not fully ordained, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:2.2.14
Pali: Nissaggiyena kāyaṁ āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, by releasing something, he makes physical contact with the body of someone who is not fully ordained, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:2.2.15
Pali: Nissaggiyena kāyapaṭibaddhaṁ āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, by releasing something, he makes physical contact with something connected to the body of someone who is not fully ordained, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:2.2.16
Pali: Nissaggiyena nissaggiyaṁ āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, by releasing something, he makes physical contact with something released by someone who is not fully ordained, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:2.2.17
Pali: Anupasampanne upasampannasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, but he perceives them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:2.2.18
Pali: Anupasampanne vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:2.2.19
Pali: Anupasampanne anupasampannasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, and he does not perceive them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:2.3.2
Pali: na hasādhippāyo, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is not aiming to make him laugh; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:2.3.3
Pali: sati karaṇīye āmasati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes physical contact with him when there is a need; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:2.3.4
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:2.3.5
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc52:2.3.6
Pali: Aṅgulipatodakasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ dutiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on tickling, the second, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53
Root: root/pli/ms/vinaya/pli-tv-bu-vb/pli-tv-bu-vb-pc/pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53_root-pli-ms.json

English translation sources:
  - brahmali [published]: translation/en/brahmali/vinaya/pli-tv-bu-vb/pli-tv-bu-vb-pc/pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53_translation-en-brahmali.json

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:0.4
Pali: Surāpānavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on drinking alcohol 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:0.5
Pali: 53. Hasadhammasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 53. Playing in water 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sattarasavaggiyā bhikkhū aciravatiyā nadiyā udake kīḷanti. 
English (brahmali, published): the monks from the group of seventeen were playing in the water of the river Aciravatī. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:1.3
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena rājā pasenadi kosalo mallikāya deviyā saddhiṁ uparipāsādavaragato hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): Just then, while King Pasenadi of Kosala was up in his finest stilt house with Queen Mallikā, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:1.4
Pali: Addasā kho rājā pasenadi kosalo sattarasavaggiye bhikkhū aciravatiyā nadiyā udake kīḷante. 
English (brahmali, published): he saw the monks from the group of seventeen playing in the river. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:1.5
Pali: Disvāna mallikaṁ deviṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): He said to Queen Mallikā, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:1.6
Pali: “ete te, mallike, arahanto udake kīḷantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Mallikā, these perfected ones are playing in the water.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:1.7
Pali: “Nissaṁsayaṁ kho, mahārāja, bhagavatā sikkhāpadaṁ apaññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Great king, no doubt the Buddha hasn’t laid down a rule. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:1.8
Pali: Te vā bhikkhū appakataññuno”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Either that, or these monks are ignorant.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:1.9
Pali: Atha kho rañño pasenadissa kosalassa etadahosi—
English (brahmali, published): King Pasenadi thought, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:1.10
Pali: “kena nu kho ahaṁ upāyena bhagavato ca na āroceyyaṁ, bhagavā ca jāneyya ime bhikkhū udake kīḷitā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the Buddha find out about these monks playing in the water without me telling him?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:1.11
Pali: Atha kho rājā pasenadi kosalo sattarasavaggiye bhikkhū pakkosāpetvā mahantaṁ guḷapiṇḍaṁ adāsi—
English (brahmali, published): Having sent for those monks, King Pasenadi gave them a large lump of sugar, saying, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:1.12
Pali: “imaṁ, bhante, guḷapiṇḍaṁ bhagavato dethā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sirs, please give this lump of sugar to the Buddha.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:1.13
Pali: Sattarasavaggiyā bhikkhū taṁ guḷapiṇḍaṁ ādāya yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; 
English (brahmali, published): The monks took the lump of sugar, went to the Buddha, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:1.14
Pali: upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:1.15
Pali: “imaṁ, bhante, guḷapiṇḍaṁ rājā pasenadi kosalo bhagavato detī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, this lump of sugar is a gift from King Pasenadi.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:1.16
Pali: “Kahaṁ pana tumhe, bhikkhave, rājā addasā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “But, monks, where did you see the king?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:1.17
Pali: “Aciravatiyā nadiyā, bhagavā, udake kīḷante”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “From the river Aciravatī, while playing in the water.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:1.18
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:1.19
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, udake kīḷissatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how can you play in water? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:1.20
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:1.21
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:1.22.1
Pali: “Udake hasadhamme pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk plays in water, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:2.1.1
Pali: Udake hasadhammo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Plays in water: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:2.1.2
Pali: uparigopphake udake hasādhippāyo nimujjati vā ummujjati vā palavati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if, aiming to have fun, he immerses himself or emerges on the surface or swims in water that is more than ankle deep, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:2.2.1
Pali: Udake hasadhamme hasadhammasaññī, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is playing in water, and he perceives that he is, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:2.2.2
Pali: Udake hasadhamme vematiko, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is playing in water, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:2.2.3
Pali: Udake hasadhamme ahasadhammasaññī, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is playing in water, but he does not perceive that he is, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:2.2.4
Pali: Heṭṭhāgopphake udake kīḷati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is playing in water less than ankle deep, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:2.2.5
Pali: Udake nāvāya kīḷati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is playing in a boat in water, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:2.2.6
Pali: Hatthena vā pādena vā kaṭṭhena vā kaṭhalāya vā udakaṁ paharati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he strikes the water with his hand, with his foot, with a stick, or with a stone, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:2.2.7
Pali: Bhājanagataṁ udakaṁ vā kañjikaṁ vā khīraṁ vā takkaṁ vā rajanaṁ vā passāvaṁ vā cikkhallaṁ vā kīḷati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he plays with water in a vessel, or with congee, milk, buttermilk, dye, urine, or mud in a vessel, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:2.2.8
Pali: Udake ahasadhamme hasadhammasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is not playing in water, but he perceives that he is, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:2.2.9
Pali: Udake ahasadhamme vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is not playing in water, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:2.2.10
Pali: Udake ahasadhamme ahasadhammasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is not playing in water, and he does not perceive that he is, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:2.3.2
Pali: na hasādhippāyo, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is not aiming to have fun; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:2.3.3
Pali: sati karaṇīye udakaṁ otaritvā nimujjati vā ummujjati vā palavati vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if, when there is something to be done, he enters the water and then immerses himself or emerges on the surface or swims; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:2.3.4
Pali: pāraṁ gacchanto nimujjati vā ummujjati vā palavati vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if, while crossing a body of water, he immerses himself or emerges on the surface or swims; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:2.3.5
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:2.3.6
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:2.3.7
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc53:2.3.8
Pali: Hasadhammasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ tatiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on playing in water, the third, is finished.
