English translation sources:
  - brahmali [published]: translation/en/brahmali/vinaya/pli-tv-bu-vb/pli-tv-bu-vb-pc/pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54_translation-en-brahmali.json

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:0.4
Pali: Surāpānavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on drinking alcohol 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:0.5
Pali: 54. Anādariyasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 54. Disrespect 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā kosambiyaṁ viharati ghositārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Kosambī in Ghosita’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā channo anācāraṁ ācarati. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Channa was misbehaving. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:1.3
Pali: Bhikkhū evamāhaṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): The monks would tell him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:1.4
Pali: “māvuso channa, evarūpaṁ akāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Channa, don’t do that; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:1.5
Pali: Netaṁ kappatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): it’s not allowable,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:1.6
Pali: So anādariyaṁ paṭicca karotiyeva. 
English (brahmali, published): and he just did it again out of disrespect. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:1.7
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:1.8
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā channo anādariyaṁ karissatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can Venerable Channa act disrespectfully?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:1.9
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, channa, anādariyaṁ karosī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Channa, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:1.10
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:1.11
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:1.12
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, anādariyaṁ karissasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, how can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:1.13
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:1.14
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:1.15.1
Pali: “Anādariye pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk is disrespectful, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:2.1.1
Pali: Anādariyaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Disrespectful: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:2.1.2
Pali: dve anādariyāni— puggalānādariyañca dhammānādariyañca. 
English (brahmali, published): there are two kinds of disrespect: disrespect for the person and disrespect for the rule. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:2.1.3
Pali: Puggalānādariyaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Disrespect for the person: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:2.1.4
Pali: upasampannena paññattena vuccamāno— “ayaṁ ukkhittako vā vambhito vā garahito vā, imassa vacanaṁ akataṁ bhavissatī”ti anādariyaṁ karoti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if, when corrected by one who is fully ordained about a rule that has been laid down, he thinks, “They’ve been ejected,” “They’ve been reproved,” or “They’ve been censured,” and then, “I won’t do what they say,” and he acts disrespectfully, then he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:2.1.5
Pali: Dhammānādariyaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Disrespect for the rule: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:2.1.6
Pali: upasampannena paññattena vuccamāno— “kathāyaṁ nasseyya vā vinasseyya vā antaradhāyeyya vā”, taṁ vā na sikkhitukāmo anādariyaṁ karoti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if, when corrected by one who is fully ordained about a rule that has been laid down, he thinks, “What can be done so that this rule is lost?” “What can be done so that it perishes?”  or “What can be done so that it disappears?” or he does not want to train in that rule, and he acts disrespectfully, then he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:2.2.1
Pali: Upasampanne upasampannasaññī anādariyaṁ karoti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, and he perceives them as such, and he acts disrespectfully, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:2.2.2
Pali: Upasampanne vematiko anādariyaṁ karoti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, but he is unsure of it, and he acts disrespectfully, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:2.2.3
Pali: Upasampanne anupasampannasaññī anādariyaṁ karoti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, but he does not perceive them as such, and he acts disrespectfully, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:2.2.4
Pali: Apaññattena vuccamāno—
English (brahmali, published): If, when corrected about something that has not been laid down, he thinks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:2.2.5
Pali: “idaṁ na sallekhāya na dhutatthāya na pāsādikatāya na apacayāya na vīriyārambhāya saṁvattatī”ti anādariyaṁ karoti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): “This isn’t conducive to self-effacement,” “This isn’t conducive to ascetic practices,” “This isn’t conducive to being inspiring,” “This isn’t conducive to a reduction in things,” or “This isn’t conducive to being energetic,” and he acts disrespectfully, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:2.2.6
Pali: Anupasampannena paññattena vā apaññattena vā vuccamāno—
English (brahmali, published): If, when corrected by one who is not fully ordained, whether or not it has been laid down, he thinks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:2.2.7
Pali: “idaṁ na sallekhāya na dhutatthāya na pāsādikatāya na apacayāya na vīriyārambhāya saṁvattatī”ti anādariyaṁ karoti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): “This isn’t conducive to self-effacement,” “This isn’t conducive to ascetic practices,” “This isn’t conducive to being inspiring,” “This isn’t conducive to a reduction in things,” or “This isn’t conducive to being energetic,” and he acts disrespectfully, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:2.2.8
Pali: Anupasampanne upasampannasaññī āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, but he perceives them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:2.2.9
Pali: Anupasampanne vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:2.2.10
Pali: Anupasampanne anupasampannasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, and he does not perceive them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:2.3.2
Pali: “evaṁ amhākaṁ ācariyānaṁ uggaho paripucchā”ti bhaṇati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he says, “This is how we were taught and tested by our teachers;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:2.3.3
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:2.3.4
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc54:2.3.5
Pali: Anādariyasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ catutthaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on disrespect, the fourth, is finished.

Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis

English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession

English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on drinking alcohol 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:0.5
Pali: 55. Bhiṁsāpanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 55. Scaring a monk 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū sattarasavaggiye bhikkhū bhiṁsāpenti. 
English (brahmali, published): the monks from the group of six were scaring the monks from the group of seventeen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:1.3
Pali: Te bhiṁsāpīyamānā rodanti. 
English (brahmali, published): They cried. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:1.4
Pali: Bhikkhū evamāhaṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): Other monks asked them why, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:1.5
Pali: “kissa tumhe, āvuso, rodathā”ti? 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:1.6
Pali: “Ime, āvuso, chabbaggiyā bhikkhū amhe bhiṁsāpentī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): and they told them. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:1.7
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:1.8
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū bhikkhuṁ bhiṁsāpessantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the monks from the group of six scare other monks?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:1.9
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, bhikkhuṁ bhiṁsāpethā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:1.10
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:1.11
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:1.12
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, bhikkhuṁ bhiṁsāpessatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:1.13
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:1.14
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:1.15.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhuṁ bhiṁsāpeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk scares a monk, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): …The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:2.1.5
Pali: Bhikkhunti 
English (brahmali, published): A monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:2.1.6
Pali: aññaṁ bhikkhuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): another monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:2.1.7
Pali: Bhiṁsāpeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Scares: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:2.1.8
Pali: upasampanno upasampannaṁ bhiṁsāpetukāmo rūpaṁ vā saddaṁ vā gandhaṁ vā rasaṁ vā phoṭṭhabbaṁ vā upasaṁharati. Bhāyeyya vā so na vā bhāyeyya, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if one who is fully ordained, wishing to scare another who is fully ordained, arranges a sight, a sound, a smell, a taste, or a physical contact, then whether the other monk is scared or not, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:2.1.9
Pali: Corakantāraṁ vā vāḷakantāraṁ vā pisācakantāraṁ vā ācikkhati. Bhāyeyya vā so na vā bhāyeyya, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he tells him about a wilderness inhabited by criminals, predatory animals, or demons, then whether the other monk is scared or not, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:2.2.1
Pali: Upasampanne upasampannasaññī bhiṁsāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, and he perceives him as such, and he scares him, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:2.2.2
Pali: Upasampanne vematiko bhiṁsāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, but he is unsure of it, and he scares him, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:2.2.3
Pali: Upasampanne anupasampannasaññī bhiṁsāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, but he does not perceive him as such, and he scares him, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:2.2.4
Pali: Anupasampannaṁ bhiṁsāpetukāmo rūpaṁ vā saddaṁ vā gandhaṁ vā rasaṁ vā phoṭṭhabbaṁ vā upasaṁharati. 
English (brahmali, published): If, wishing to scare someone who is not fully ordained, he arranges a sight, a sound, a smell, a taste, or a physical contact, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:2.2.5
Pali: Bhāyeyya vā so na vā bhāyeyya, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): then whether the other person is scared or not, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:2.2.6
Pali: Corakantāraṁ vā vāḷakantāraṁ vā pisācakantāraṁ vā ācikkhati. 
English (brahmali, published): If he tells him about a wilderness inhabited by criminals, predatory animals, or demons, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:2.2.7
Pali: Bhāyeyya vā so na vā bhāyeyya, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): then whether the other person is scared or not, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:2.2.8
Pali: Anupasampanne upasampannasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, but he perceives them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:2.2.9
Pali: Anupasampanne vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:2.2.10
Pali: Anupasampanne anupasampannasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, and he does not perceive them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:2.3.2
Pali: na bhiṁsāpetukāmo rūpaṁ vā saddaṁ vā gandhaṁ vā rasaṁ vā phoṭṭhabbaṁ vā upasaṁharati, corakantāraṁ vā vāḷakantāraṁ vā pisācakantāraṁ vā ācikkhati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he arranges a sight, a sound, a smell, a taste, or a physical contact, or he tells about a wilderness inhabited by criminals, predatory animals, or demons, but not because he wishes to scare anyone; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:2.3.3
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:2.3.4
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc55:2.3.5
Pali: Bhiṁsāpanasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ pañcamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on scaring a monk, the fifth, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:0.4
Pali: Surāpānavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on drinking alcohol 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:0.5
Pali: 56. Jotikasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 56. Lighting a fire 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:1.1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā bhaggesu viharati susumāragire bhesakaḷāvane migadāye. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying in the Bhagga country at Susumāragira in the Bhesakaḷā Grove, the deer park. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:1.1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū hemantike kāle aññataraṁ mahantaṁ susirakaṭṭhaṁ jotiṁ samādahitvā visibbesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time, during winter, the monks were warming themselves after setting fire to a hollow log. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:1.1.3
Pali: Tasmiñca susire kaṇhasappo agginā santatto nikkhamitvā bhikkhū paripātesi. 
English (brahmali, published): Heated by the fire, a black snake came out of the log and attacked the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:1.1.4
Pali: Bhikkhū tahaṁ tahaṁ upadhāviṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks ran here and there. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:1.1.5
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:1.1.6
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhū jotiṁ samādahitvā visibbessantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could those monks light a fire to warm themselves?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:1.1.7
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhū jotiṁ samādahitvā visibbentī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that monks did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:1.1.8
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:1.1.9
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:1.1.10
Pali: kathañhi nāma te, bhikkhave, moghapurisā jotiṁ samādahitvā visibbessanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could those foolish men do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:1.1.11
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:1.1.12
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:1.1.13.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu visibbanāpekkho jotiṁ samādaheyya vā samādahāpeyya vā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk lights a fire to warm himself, or has one lit, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:1.1.14
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:1.2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū gilānā honti. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time a number of monks were sick. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:1.2.2
Pali: Gilānapucchakā bhikkhū gilāne bhikkhū etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): The monks who were looking after them asked, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:1.2.3
Pali: “kaccāvuso, khamanīyaṁ, kacci yāpanīyan”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “I hope you’re bearing up? I hope you’re getting better?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:1.2.4
Pali: “Pubbe mayaṁ, āvuso, jotiṁ samādahitvā visibbema; 
English (brahmali, published): “Previously we lit a fire to warm ourselves, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:1.2.5
Pali: tena no phāsu hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): and then we were comfortable. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:1.2.6
Pali: Idāni pana ‘bhagavatā paṭikkhittan’ti kukkuccāyantā na visibbema, tena no na phāsu hotī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): But now that the Buddha has prohibited this, we don’t warm ourselves because we’re afraid of wrongdoing. Because of that we’re not comfortable.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:1.2.7
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:1.2.8
Pali: anujānāmi, bhikkhave, gilānena bhikkhunā jotiṁ samādahitvā vā samādahāpetvā vā visibbetuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow a sick monk to light a fire to warm himself, or to have one lit. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:1.2.9
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:1.2.10.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu agilāno visibbanāpekkho jotiṁ samādaheyya vā samādahāpeyya vā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk who is not sick lights a fire to warm himself, or has one lit, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:1.2.11
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū padīpepi jotikepi jantāgharepi kukkuccāyanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the monks did not light lamps, small fires, or saunas because they were afraid of wrongdoing. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:2.2
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:2.3
Pali: anujānāmi, bhikkhave, tathārūpappaccayā jotiṁ samādahituṁ samādahāpetuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to light a fire, or to have one lit, if there’s a suitable reason. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:2.4
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:2.5.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu agilāno visibbanāpekkho jotiṁ samādaheyya vā samādahāpeyya vā, aññatra tathārūpappaccayā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk who is not sick lights a fire to warm himself, or has one lit, except if there is a suitable reason, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): …The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.1.5
Pali: Agilāno nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Who is not sick: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.1.6
Pali: yassa vinā agginā phāsu hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): who is comfortable without a fire. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.1.7
Pali: Gilāno nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Who is sick: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.1.8
Pali: yassa vinā agginā na phāsu hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): who is not comfortable without a fire. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.1.9
Pali: Visibbanāpekkhoti 
English (brahmali, published): To warm himself: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.1.10
Pali: tappitukāmo. 
English (brahmali, published): wanting to heat himself. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.1.11
Pali: Joti nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A fire: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.1.12
Pali: aggi vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): flames are what is meant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.1.13
Pali: Samādaheyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Lights: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.1.14
Pali: sayaṁ samādahati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he lights it himself, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.1.15
Pali: Samādahāpeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Has one lit: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.1.16
Pali: aññaṁ āṇāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he asks another, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.1.17
Pali: Sakiṁ āṇatto bahukampi samādahati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he only asks once, then even if the other lights many fires, he commits one offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.1.18
Pali: Aññatra tathā rūpappaccayāti 
English (brahmali, published): Except if there is a suitable reason: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.1.19
Pali: ṭhapetvā tathārūpappaccayaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): unless there is a suitable reason. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.2.1
Pali: Agilāno agilānasaññī visibbanāpekkho jotiṁ samādahati vā samādahāpeti vā, aññatra tathārūpappaccayā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is not sick, and he does not perceive himself as sick, and he lights a fire to warm himself, or has one lit, except if there is a suitable reason, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.2.2
Pali: Agilāno vematiko visibbanāpekkho jotiṁ samādahati vā samādahāpeti vā, aññatra tathārūpappaccayā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is not sick, but he is unsure of it, and he lights a fire to warm himself, or has one lit, except if there is a suitable reason, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.2.3
Pali: Agilāno gilānasaññī visibbanāpekkho jotiṁ samādahati vā samādahāpeti vā, aññatra tathārūpappaccayā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is not sick, but he perceives himself as sick, and he lights a fire to warm himself, or has one lit, except if there is a suitable reason, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.2.4
Pali: Paṭilātaṁ ukkhipati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he puts back a burning piece of wood that has fallen off, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.2.5
Pali: Gilāno agilānasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is sick, but he does not perceive himself as sick, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.2.6
Pali: Gilāno vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is sick, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.2.7
Pali: Gilāno gilānasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is sick, and he perceives himself as sick, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.3.2
Pali: gilānassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is sick; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.3.3
Pali: aññena kataṁ visibbeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he warms himself over a fire lit by another; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.3.4
Pali: vītaccitaṅgāraṁ visibbeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he warms himself over flameless coals; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.3.5
Pali: padīpe jotike jantāghare tathārūpappaccayā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he lights a lamp, a small fire, or a sauna, when there is a suitable reason; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.3.6
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.3.7
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.3.8
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc56:3.3.9
Pali: Jotikasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ chaṭṭhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on lighting a fire, the sixth, is finished.

Pali: 57. Nahānasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 57. Bathing too often 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Rājagaha in the Bamboo Grove, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū tapode nahāyanti. 
English (brahmali, published): the monks were bathing in the hot springs. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:1.3
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena rājā māgadho seniyo bimbisāro “sīsaṁ nahāyissāmī”ti tapodaṁ gantvā—
English (brahmali, published): Just then King Seniya Bimbisāra of Magadha went to the hot springs, intending to wash his hair. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:1.4
Pali: “yāvāyyā nahāyantī”ti ekamantaṁ paṭimānesi. Bhikkhū yāva samandhakārā nahāyiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): He waited respectfully for the monks to finish, but they kept on bathing until dark. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:1.5
Pali: Atha kho rājā māgadho seniyo bimbisāro vikāle sīsaṁ nahāyitvā, nagaradvāre thakite bahinagare vasitvā, kālasseva asambhinnena vilepanena yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; 
English (brahmali, published): Only then was King Bimbisāra able to wash his hair. And because the town gates had been shut, he had to spend the night outside the city. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:1.6
Pali: upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): In the morning, with his make-up still on, he went to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:1.7
Pali: Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho rājānaṁ māgadhaṁ seniyaṁ bimbisāraṁ bhagavā etadavoca—“kissa tvaṁ, mahārāja, kālasseva āgato asambhinnena vilepanenā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha said to him, “Great king, why have you come so early in the morning, with your make-up still on?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:1.8
Pali: Atha kho rājā māgadho seniyo bimbisāro bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): The king told him what had happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:1.9
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā rājānaṁ māgadhaṁ seniyaṁ bimbisāraṁ dhammiyā kathāya sandassesi samādapesi samuttejesi sampahaṁsesi. Atha kho rājā māgadho seniyo bimbisāro bhagavatā dhammiyā kathāya sandassito samādapito samuttejito sampahaṁsito uṭṭhāyāsanā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha then instructed, inspired, and gladdened him with a teaching, after which the king got up from his seat, bowed down, circumambulated the Buddha with his right side toward him, and left. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:1.10
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe bhikkhusaṅghaṁ sannipātāpetvā bhikkhū paṭipucchi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:1.11

English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that monks bathed without moderation, even after seeing the king?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:1.12
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:1.15

Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu orenaddhamāsaṁ nahāyeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk bathes at intervals of less than a half-month, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:1.18
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū uṇhasamaye pariḷāhasamaye kukkuccāyantā na nahāyanti, sedagatena gattena sayanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards, because they were afraid of wrongdoing, the monks did not bathe when it was hot or when they had a fever, and they went to sleep covered in sweat.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:2.5

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:2.7
Pali: Tatthāyaṁ samayo. Diyaḍḍho māso seso gimhānanti vassānassa paṭhamo māso iccete aḍḍhateyyamāsā uṇhasamayo pariḷāhasamayo—ayaṁ tattha samayo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the appropriate occasion: it is the two-and-a-half-month period of summer and the fever season, comprising the last one-and-a-half months of summer and the first month of the rainy season.’”

English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk bathes at intervals of less than a half-month, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:4.7
Pali: Tatthāyaṁ samayo. Diyaḍḍho māso seso gimhānanti vassānassa paṭhamo māso iccete aḍḍhateyyamāsā uṇhasamayo, pariḷāhasamayo, gilānasamayo, kammasamayo—ayaṁ tattha samayo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): These are the appropriate occasions: it is the two-and-a-half-month period of summer and the fever season, comprising the last one-and-a-half months of summer and the first month of the rainy season; he is sick; he is working.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:4.8
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:5.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū addhānaṁ gantvā kukkuccāyantā na nahāyanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the monks were traveling, but because they were afraid of wrongdoing they did not bathe. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:5.2
Pali: Te sedagatena gattena sayanti. Cīvarampi senāsanampi dussati. 
English (brahmali, published): As a consequence, they went to sleep covered in sweat, and their robes and beds got dirty. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:5.3
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:5.4
Pali: anujānāmi, bhikkhave, addhānagamanasamaye orenaddhamāsaṁ nahāyituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to bathe at intervals of less than a half-month when you’re traveling. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:5.5
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:5.6.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu orenaddhamāsaṁ nahāyeyya, aññatra samayā, pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk bathes at intervals of less than a half-month, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:5.7
Pali: Tatthāyaṁ samayo. Diyaḍḍho māso seso gimhānanti vassānassa paṭhamo māso iccete aḍḍhateyyamāsā uṇhasamayo, pariḷāhasamayo, gilānasamayo, kammasamayo, addhānagamanasamayo—ayaṁ tattha samayo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): These are the appropriate occasions: it is the two-and-a-half-month period of summer and the fever season, comprising the last one-and-a-half months of summer and the first month of the rainy season; he is sick; he is working; he is traveling.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:5.8
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:6.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā bhikkhū ajjhokāse cīvarakammaṁ karontā sarajena vātena okiṇṇā honti. Devo ca thokaṁ thokaṁ phusāyati. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards a number of monks were making robes out in the open, when they were hit by dusty winds and fine rain. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:6.2
Pali: Bhikkhū kukkuccāyantā na nahāyanti, kilinnena gattena sayanti. 
English (brahmali, published): But because they were afraid of wrongdoing, they did not bathe afterwards, and they went to sleep while still wet. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:6.3
Pali: Cīvarampi senāsanampi dussati. 
English (brahmali, published): As a consequence, their robes and beds got dirty. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:6.4
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:6.5
Pali: anujānāmi, bhikkhave, vātavuṭṭhisamaye orenaddhamāsaṁ nahāyituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, if there is wind and rain, I allow you to bathe at intervals of less than a half-month. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:6.6
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:6.7.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu orenaddhamāsaṁ nahāyeyya, aññatra samayā, pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk bathes at intervals of less than a half-month, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:6.8
Pali: Tatthāyaṁ samayo. Diyaḍḍho māso seso gimhānanti vassānassa paṭhamo māso iccete aḍḍhateyyamāsā uṇhasamayo, pariḷāhasamayo, gilānasamayo, kammasamayo, addhānagamanasamayo, vātavuṭṭhisamayo—ayaṁ tattha samayo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): These are the appropriate occasions: it is the two-and-a-half-month period of summer and the fever season, comprising the last one-and-a-half months of summer and the first month of the rainy season; he is sick; he is working; he is traveling; there is wind and rain.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.1.5
Pali: Orenaddhamāsanti 
English (brahmali, published): At intervals of less than a half-month: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.1.6
Pali: ūnakaddhamāsaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): after less than a half-month. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.1.7
Pali: Nahāyeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Bathes: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.1.8
Pali: cuṇṇena vā mattikāya vā nahāyati, payoge payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if he bathes with bath powder or soap, then for every effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.1.9
Pali: Nahānapariyosāne, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When the bath is finished, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.1.10
Pali: Aññatra samayāti 
English (brahmali, published): Except on an appropriate occasion: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.1.11
Pali: ṭhapetvā samayaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): unless it is an appropriate occasion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.1.12
Pali: Uṇhasamayo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Summer: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.1.13
Pali: diyaḍḍho māso seso gimhānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): the last month-and-a-half of summer. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.1.14
Pali: Pariḷāhasamayo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The fever season: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.1.15
Pali: vassānassa paṭhamo māso “iccete aḍḍhateyyamāsā uṇhasamayo pariḷāhasamayo”ti nahāyitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): the first month of the rainy season. During the two-and-a-half-month period of summer and the fever season, he may bathe. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.1.16
Pali: Gilānasamayo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): He is sick: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.1.17
Pali: yassa vinā nahānena na phāsu hoti. Gilānasamayoti nahāyitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): he is not comfortable without bathing. If he is sick, he may bathe. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.1.18
Pali: Kammasamayo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): He is working: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.1.19
Pali: antamaso pariveṇampi sammaṭṭhaṁ hoti. “Kammasamayo”ti nahāyitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): even if he just sweeps the yard of a building. If he is working, he may bathe. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.1.20
Pali: Addhānagamanasamayo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): He is traveling: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.1.21
Pali: “addhayojanaṁ gacchissāmī”ti nahāyitabbaṁ, gacchantena nahāyitabbaṁ, gatena nahāyitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if he intends to travel six kilometers, he may bathe; while traveling, he may bathe; after he has traveled, he may bathe. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.1.22
Pali: Vātavuṭṭhisamayo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): There is wind and rain: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.1.23
Pali: bhikkhū sarajena vātena okiṇṇā honti, dve vā tīṇi vā udakaphusitāni kāye patitāni honti. “Vātavuṭṭhisamayo”ti nahāyitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): monks are hit by dusty winds, and two or three drops of rain fall on their bodies. If there is wind and rain, they may bathe. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.2.1
Pali: Ūnakaddhamāse ūnakasaññī, aññatra samayā, nahāyati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an interval of less than a half-month, and he perceives it as less, and he bathes, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.2.2
Pali: Ūnakaddhamāse vematiko, aññatra samayā, nahāyati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an interval of less than a half-month, but he is unsure of it, and he bathes, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.2.3
Pali: Ūnakaddhamāse atirekasaññī, aññatra samayā, nahāyati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an interval of less than a half-month, but he perceives it as more, and he bathes, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.2.4
Pali: Atirekaddhamāse ūnakasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an interval of more than a half-month, but he perceives it as less, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.2.5
Pali: Atirekaddhamāse vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an interval of more than a half-month, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.2.6
Pali: Atirekaddhamāse atirekasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an interval of more than a half-month, and he perceives it as more, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.3.2
Pali: samaye, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is an appropriate occasion; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.3.3
Pali: addhamāsaṁ nahāyati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he bathes at intervals of a half-month; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.3.4
Pali: atirekaddhamāsaṁ nahāyati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he bathes at intervals of more than a half-month; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.3.5
Pali: pāraṁ gacchanto nahāyati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he bathes while crossing a body of water; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.3.6
Pali: sabbapaccantimesu janapadesu, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is outside the central Ganges plain; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.3.7
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.3.8
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.3.9
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc57:7.3.10
Pali: Nahānasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ sattamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on bathing too often, the seventh is finished.

Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession

Pali: Surāpānavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on drinking alcohol 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:0.5
Pali: 58. Dubbaṇṇakaraṇasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 58. Not making stains on a new robe 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā bhikkhū ca paribbājakā ca sāketā sāvatthiṁ addhānamaggappaṭipannā honti. Antarāmagge corā nikkhamitvā te acchindiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): a number of monks and wanderers were robbed while traveling from Sāketa to Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:1.3
Pali: Sāvatthiyā rājabhaṭā nikkhamitvā te core sabhaṇḍe gahetvā bhikkhūnaṁ santike dūtaṁ pāhesuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): The king’s men set out from Sāvatthī and caught the thieves and their loot. They then sent a message to the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:1.4
Pali: “āgacchantu, bhadantā, sakaṁ sakaṁ cīvaraṁ sañjānitvā gaṇhantū”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, please come and pick out your own robes.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:1.5
Pali: Bhikkhū na sañjānanti. 
English (brahmali, published): But the monks did not recognize them. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:1.6
Pali: Te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The people complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:1.7
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhadantā attano attano cīvaraṁ na sañjānissantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can they not recognize their own robes?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:1.8
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:1.9
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): and they told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:1.10
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe bhikkhusaṅghaṁ sannipātāpetvā bhikkhūnaṁ tadanucchavikaṁ tadanulomikaṁ dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): He had the Sangha gathered, gave a teaching on what is right and proper, and then addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:1.11
Pali: “tena hi, bhikkhave, bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññapessāmi dasa atthavase paṭicca—
English (brahmali, published): “Well then, monks, I will lay down a training rule for the following ten reasons: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:1.12
Pali: saṅghasuṭṭhutāya, saṅghaphāsutāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): for the well-being of the Sangha, for the comfort of the Sangha, for the restraint of bad people, for the ease of good monks, for the restraint of the corruptions relating to the present life, for the restraint of the corruptions relating to future lives, to give rise to confidence in those without it, to increase the confidence of those who have it, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:1.13
Pali: saddhammaṭṭhitiyā, vinayānuggahāya. 
English (brahmali, published): for the longevity of the true Teaching, and for supporting the training. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:1.14
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:1.15.1
Pali: “Navaṁ pana bhikkhunā cīvaralābhena tiṇṇaṁ dubbaṇṇakaraṇānaṁ aññataraṁ dubbaṇṇakaraṇaṁ ādātabbaṁ—nīlaṁ vā kaddamaṁ vā kāḷasāmaṁ vā. Anādā ce bhikkhu tiṇṇaṁ dubbaṇṇakaraṇānaṁ aññataraṁ dubbaṇṇakaraṇaṁ navaṁ cīvaraṁ paribhuñjeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘When a monk gets a new robe, he should apply one of three kinds of stains: blue-green, mud-color, or dark brown. If a monk uses a new robe without applying any of the three kinds of stains, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.1.1
Pali: Navaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): New: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.1.2
Pali: akatakappaṁ vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): a mark has not been made is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.1.3
Pali: Cīvaraṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A robe: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.1.4
Pali: channaṁ cīvarānaṁ aññataraṁ cīvaraṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one of the six kinds of robes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.1.5
Pali: Tiṇṇaṁ dubbaṇṇakaraṇānaṁ aññataraṁ dubbaṇṇakaraṇaṁ ādātabbanti 
English (brahmali, published): He should apply one of three kinds of stains: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.1.6
Pali: antamaso kusaggenapi ādātabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): even if he just applies what fits on the tip of a blade of grass. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.1.7
Pali: Nīlaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Blue-green: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.1.8
Pali: dve nīlāni— kaṁsanīlaṁ, palāsanīlaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): there are two kinds of blue-green: the color of copper sulfate and the color of leaves. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.1.9
Pali: Kaddamo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Mud-color: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.1.10
Pali: odako vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): watery is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.1.11
Pali: Kāḷasāmaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Dark brown: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.1.12
Pali: yaṁ kiñci kāḷasāmakaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): whatever is dark brownish. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.1.13
Pali: Anādā ce bhikkhu tiṇṇaṁ dubbaṇṇakaraṇānaṁ aññataraṁ dubbaṇṇakaraṇanti 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk … without applying any of the three kinds of stains: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.1.14
Pali: antamaso kusaggenapi anādiyitvā tiṇṇaṁ dubbaṇṇakaraṇānaṁ aññataraṁ dubbaṇṇakaraṇaṁ navaṁ cīvaraṁ paribhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he uses a new robe without first applying one of the three kinds of stains, even just the amount on the tip of a blade of grass, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.2.1
Pali: Anādinne anādinnasaññī paribhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been applied, and he perceives that it has not, and he uses the robe, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.2.2
Pali: Anādinne vematiko paribhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been applied, but he is unsure of it, and he uses the robe, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.2.3
Pali: Anādinne ādinnasaññī paribhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been applied, but he perceives that it has, and he uses the robe, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.2.4
Pali: Ādinne anādinnasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has been applied, but he perceives that it has not, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.2.5
Pali: Ādinne vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has been applied, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.2.6
Pali: Ādinne ādinnasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has been applied, and he perceives that it has, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.3.2
Pali: ādiyitvā paribhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he applies the stain and then uses it; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.3.3
Pali: kappo naṭṭho hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if the mark has disappeared; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.3.4
Pali: kappakatokāso jiṇṇo hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if the area where the mark was applied is worn; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.3.5
Pali: kappakatena akappakataṁ saṁsibbitaṁ hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if what had been marked is sewn together with what has not been marked; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.3.6
Pali: aggaḷe, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a patch; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.3.7
Pali: anuvāte, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a lengthwise border; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.3.8
Pali: paribhaṇḍe, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a crosswise border; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.3.9
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.3.10
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc58:2.3.11
Pali: Dubbaṇṇakaraṇasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ aṭṭhamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on not making stains on a new robe, the eighth, is finished.

Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

Pali: Surāpānavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on drinking alcohol 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:0.5
Pali: 59. Vikappanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 59. Using assigned property that has not been relinquished 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā upanando sakyaputto bhātuno saddhivihārikassa bhikkhuno sāmaṁ cīvaraṁ vikappetvā appaccuddhāraṇaṁ paribhuñjati. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Upananda the Sakyan assigned the ownership of a robe to a monk who was his brother’s student. He then used that robe without that monk having relinquished it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:1.3
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi—
English (brahmali, published): That monk told the monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:1.4
Pali: “ayaṁ, āvuso, āyasmā upanando sakyaputto mayhaṁ cīvaraṁ sāmaṁ vikappetvā appaccuddhāraṇaṁ paribhuñjatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable Upananda is using a robe that he had assigned to me, even though I haven’t relinquished it.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:1.5
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized Upananda, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:1.6
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā upanando sakyaputto bhikkhussa sāmaṁ cīvaraṁ vikappetvā appaccuddhāraṇaṁ paribhuñjissatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Upananda use a robe he had assigned to a monk, without that monk first relinquishing it?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:1.7
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, upananda, bhikkhussa sāmaṁ cīvaraṁ vikappetvā appaccuddhāraṇaṁ paribhuñjasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Upananda, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:1.8
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:1.9
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:1.10

English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:1.12

English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:1.13.1

English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk himself assigns the ownership of a robe to a monk, a nun, a trainee nun, a novice monk, or a novice nun, and he then uses it without the other first relinquishing it, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.1.2

Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.1.5
Pali: Bhikkhussāti 
English (brahmali, published): To a monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.1.6
Pali: aññassa bhikkhussa. 
English (brahmali, published): to another monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.1.7
Pali: Bhikkhunī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A nun: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.1.8
Pali: ubhatosaṅghe upasampannā. 
English (brahmali, published): she has been given the full ordination by both Sanghas. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.1.9
Pali: Sikkhamānā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A trainee nun: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.1.10
Pali: dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhā. 
English (brahmali, published): one training for two years in the six rules. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.1.11
Pali: Sāmaṇero nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A novice monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.1.12
Pali: dasasikkhāpadiko. 
English (brahmali, published): a male training in the ten training rules. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.1.13
Pali: Sāmaṇerī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A novice nun: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.1.14
Pali: dasasikkhāpadikā. 
English (brahmali, published): a female training in the ten training rules. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.1.15
Pali: Sāmanti 
English (brahmali, published): Himself: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.1.16
Pali: sayaṁ vikappetvā. 
English (brahmali, published): having himself done the assignment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.1.17
Pali: Cīvaraṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A robe: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.1.18
Pali: channaṁ cīvarānaṁ aññataraṁ cīvaraṁ vikappanupagaṁ pacchimaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one of the six kinds of robe-cloth, but not smaller than what can be assigned to another. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.1.19
Pali: Vikappanā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Assigns the ownership of: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.1.20
Pali: dve vikappanā— sammukhāvikappanā ca parammukhāvikappanā ca. 
English (brahmali, published): there are two kinds of assignment: assignment in the presence of and assignment in the absence of. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.1.21
Pali: Sammukhāvikappanā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Assignment in the presence of: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.1.22
Pali: “imaṁ cīvaraṁ tuyhaṁ vikappemi itthannāmassa vā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): one should say, “I assign this robe-cloth to you,” or “I assign this robe-cloth to so-and-so.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.1.23
Pali: Parammukhāvikappanā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Assignment in the absence of: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.1.24
Pali: “imaṁ cīvaraṁ vikappanatthāya tuyhaṁ dammī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): one should say, “I give this robe-cloth to you for the purpose of assigning it.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.1.25
Pali: Tena vattabbo— “ko te mitto vā sandiṭṭho vā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): The other should ask, “Who is your friend or companion?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.1.26
Pali: “Itthannāmo ca itthannāmo cā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): One should reply, “So-and-so and so-and-so.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.1.27
Pali: Tena vattabbo— “ahaṁ tesaṁ dammi, tesaṁ santakaṁ paribhuñja vā vissajjehi vā yathāpaccayaṁ vā karohī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The other should say, “I give it to them. Please use their property, give it away, or do as you like with it.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.1.28
Pali: Appaccuddhāraṇaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Without it first being relinquished: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.1.29
Pali: tassa vā adinnaṁ, tassa vā avissasanto paribhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if it is not given to him or he uses it without taking it on trust, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.2.1
Pali: Appaccuddhāraṇe appaccuddhāraṇasaññī paribhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been relinquished, and he perceives that it has not, and he uses it, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.2.2
Pali: Appaccuddhāraṇe vematiko paribhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been relinquished, but he is unsure of it, and he uses it, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.2.3
Pali: Appaccuddhāraṇe appaccuddhāraṇasaññī paribhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been relinquished, but he perceives that it has, and he uses it, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.2.4
Pali: Adhiṭṭheti vā vissajjeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he determines it or gives it away, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.2.5
Pali: Paccuddhāraṇe appaccuddhāraṇasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has been relinquished, but he perceives that it has not, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.2.6
Pali: Paccuddhāraṇe vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has been relinquished, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.2.7
Pali: Paccuddhāraṇe paccuddhāraṇasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has been relinquished, and he perceives that it has, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.3.2
Pali: so vā deti, 
English (brahmali, published): if the other person gives it or he uses it after taking the other person’s property on trust; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.3.3
Pali: tassa vā vissasanto paribhuñjati, 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.3.4
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.3.5
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc59:2.3.6
Pali: Vikappanasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ navamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on using assigned property that has not been relinquished, the ninth, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sattarasavaggiyā bhikkhū asannihitaparikkhārā honti.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:1.3
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhū sattarasavaggiyānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ pattampi cīvarampi apanidhenti.

Pali: Sattarasavaggiyā bhikkhū chabbaggiye bhikkhū etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): The monks from the group of seventeen said to them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:1.5

English (brahmali, published): “Give us our bowls and robes.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:1.6
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhū hasanti, te rodanti. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks from the group of six laughed, but the monks from the group of seventeen cried. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:1.7
Pali: Bhikkhū evamāhaṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): The monks asked them,

English (brahmali, published): “’Cause the monks from the group of six have hidden our bowls and robes.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:1.10
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them,

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:1.18.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhussa pattaṁ vā cīvaraṁ vā nisīdanaṁ vā sūcigharaṁ vā kāyabandhanaṁ vā apanidheyya vā apanidhāpeyya vā, antamaso hasāpekkhopi, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk hides a monk’s bowl, robe, sitting mat, needle case, or belt, or he has it hidden, even just for a laugh, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.1.5
Pali: Bhikkhussāti 
English (brahmali, published): A monk’s: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.1.6
Pali: aññassa bhikkhussa. 
English (brahmali, published): another monk’s. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.1.7
Pali: Patto nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Bowl: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.1.8
Pali: dve pattā—ayopatto, mattikāpatto. 
English (brahmali, published): there are two kinds of bowls: iron bowls and ceramic bowls. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.1.9
Pali: Cīvaraṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Robe: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.1.10
Pali: channaṁ cīvarānaṁ aññataraṁ cīvaraṁ, vikappanupagaṁ pacchimaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one of the six kinds of robe-cloth, but not smaller than what can be assigned to another. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.1.11
Pali: Nisīdanaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Sitting mat: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.1.12
Pali: sadasaṁ vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): one with a border is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.1.13
Pali: Sūcigharaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Needle case: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.1.14
Pali: sasūcikaṁ vā asūcikaṁ vā. 
English (brahmali, published): with or without needles. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.1.15
Pali: Kāyabandhanaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Belt: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.1.16
Pali: dve kāyabandhanāni— paṭṭikā, sūkarantakaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): there are two kinds of belts: those made from strips of cloth and those made from pigs’ intestines. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.1.17
Pali: Apanidheyya vāti 
English (brahmali, published): Hides: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.1.18
Pali: sayaṁ apanidheti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he hides it himself, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.1.19
Pali: Apanidhāpeyya vāti 
English (brahmali, published): Has hidden: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.1.20
Pali: aññaṁ āṇāpesi, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he asks another, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.1.21
Pali: Sakiṁ āṇatto bahukampi apanidheti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he only asks once, then even if the other hides many things, he commits one offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.1.22
Pali: Antamaso hasāpekkhopīti 
English (brahmali, published): Even just for a laugh: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.1.23
Pali: kīḷādhippāyo. 
English (brahmali, published): aiming to have fun. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.2.1
Pali: Upasampanne upasampannasaññī pattaṁ vā cīvaraṁ vā nisīdanaṁ vā sūcigharaṁ vā kāyabandhanaṁ vā apanidheti vā apanidhāpeti vā, antamaso hasāpekkhopi, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other monk is fully ordained, and he perceives him as such, and he hides his bowl or robe or sitting mat or needle case or belt, or he has it hidden, even just for a laugh, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.2.2
Pali: Upasampanne vematiko …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If the other monk is fully ordained, but he is unsure of it, and he hides his bowl or robe or sitting mat or needle case or belt, or he has it hidden, even just for a laugh, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.2.3
Pali: upasampanne anupasampannasaññī pattaṁ vā cīvaraṁ vā nisīdanaṁ vā sūcigharaṁ vā kāyabandhanaṁ vā apanidheti vā apanidhāpeti vā, antamaso hasāpekkhopi, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other monk is fully ordained, but he does not perceive him as such, and he hides his bowl or robe or sitting mat or needle case or belt, or he has it hidden, even just for a laugh, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.2.4
Pali: Aññaṁ parikkhāraṁ apanidheti vā apanidhāpeti vā, antamaso hasāpekkhopi, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he hides another requisite, or he has it hidden, even just for a laugh, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.2.5
Pali: Anupasampannassa pattaṁ vā cīvaraṁ vā aññaṁ vā parikkhāraṁ apanidheti vā apanidhāpeti vā, antamaso hasāpekkhopi, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he hides the bowl or robe or other requisite of someone who is not fully ordained, or he has it hidden, even just for a laugh, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.2.6
Pali: Anupasampanne upasampannasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, but he perceives them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.2.7
Pali: Anupasampanne vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.2.8
Pali: Anupasampanne anupasampannasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, and he does not perceive them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.3.2
Pali: nahasādhippāyo, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is not aiming to have fun; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.3.3
Pali: dunnikkhittaṁ paṭisāmeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he puts away what has been improperly put away; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.3.4
Pali: “dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā dassāmī”ti paṭisāmeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he puts something away with the thought, “After giving a teaching, I’ll give it back;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.3.5
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.3.6
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.3.7
Pali: Cīvaraapanidhānasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ dasamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on hiding a robe, the tenth, is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.3.8
Pali: Surāpānavaggo chaṭṭho. 
English (brahmali, published): The sixth subchapter on drinking alcohol is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.3.9
Pali: Tassuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.3.10
Pali: Surā aṅguli hāso ca, 
English (brahmali, published): “Alcohol, finger, and laughter, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.3.11
Pali: anādariyañca bhiṁsanaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): And disrespect, scaring; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.3.12
Pali: Jotinahānadubbaṇṇaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): Fire, bathing, stain, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc60:2.3.13
Pali: sāmaṁ apanidhena cāti. 
English (brahmali, published): Himself, and with hiding.”

English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on containing living beings 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:0.5
Pali: 61. Sañciccasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 61. Intentionally killing a living being 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā udāyī issāso hoti, kākā cassa amanāpā honti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time Venerable Udāyī was skilled in archery. And because he disliked crows, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:1.3
Pali: So kāke vijjhitvā vijjhitvā sīsaṁ chinditvā sūle paṭipāṭiyā ṭhapesi. 
English (brahmali, published): he shot them. He cut off their heads and then set them out in a row impaled on stakes. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:1.4
Pali: Bhikkhū evamāhaṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): The monks asked him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:1.5
Pali: “kenime, āvuso, kākā jīvitā voropitā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Who killed these crows?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:1.6
Pali: “Mayā, āvuso. 
English (brahmali, published): “I did. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:1.7
Pali: Amanāpā me kākā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I don’t like crows.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:1.8
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:1.9
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā udāyī sañcicca pāṇaṁ jīvitā voropessatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can Venerable Udāyī intentionally kill living beings?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:1.10
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, udāyi, sañcicca pāṇaṁ jīvitā voropesī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Udāyī, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:1.11
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:1.12
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:1.13
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, sañcicca pāṇaṁ jīvitā voropessasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, how can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:1.14
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:1.15
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:1.16.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu sañcicca pāṇaṁ jīvitā voropeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk intentionally kills a living being, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:2.1.5
Pali: Sañciccāti 
English (brahmali, published): Intentionally: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:2.1.6
Pali: jānanto sañjānanto cecca abhivitaritvā vītikkamo. 
English (brahmali, published): knowing, perceiving, having intended, having decided, he transgresses. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:2.1.7
Pali: Pāṇo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A living being: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:2.1.8
Pali: tiracchānagatapāṇo vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): an animal is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:2.1.9
Pali: Jīvitā voropeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Kills: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:2.1.10
Pali: jīvitindriyaṁ upacchindati uparodheti santatiṁ vikopeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he cuts off and makes an end of the life faculty, if he destroys its continuity, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:2.2.1
Pali: Pāṇe pāṇasaññī jīvitā voropeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a living being, and he perceives it as such, and he kills it, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:2.2.2
Pali: Pāṇe vematiko jīvitā voropeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a living being, but he is unsure of it, and he kills it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:2.2.3
Pali: Pāṇe appāṇasaññī jīvitā voropeti, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a living being, but he does not perceive it as such, and he kills it, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:2.2.4
Pali: Appāṇe pāṇasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a living being, but he perceives it as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:2.2.5
Pali: Appāṇe vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a living being, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:2.2.6
Pali: Appāṇe appāṇasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a living being, and he does not perceive it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:2.3.2
Pali: asañcicca, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is unintentional; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:2.3.3
Pali: assatiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is not mindful; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:2.3.4
Pali: ajānantassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not know; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:2.3.5
Pali: namaraṇādhippāyassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is not aiming at death; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:2.3.6
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:2.3.7
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc61:2.3.8
Pali: Sañciccasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ paṭhamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on intentionally killing a living being, the first, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc62:1.1

English (brahmali, published): the monks from the group of six were using water that they knew contained living beings.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc62:2.1.7
Pali: “Sappāṇakan”ti jānanto, “paribhogena marissantī”ti jānanto paribhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he uses it, knowing that it contains living beings and knowing that they will die if the water is used, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc62:2.2.1
Pali: Sappāṇake sappāṇakasaññī paribhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc62:2.2.2

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc62:2.2.3
Pali: Sappāṇake appāṇakasaññī paribhuñjati, anāpatti.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc62:2.2.5
Pali: Appāṇake vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it does not contain living beings, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc62:2.2.6
Pali: Appāṇake appāṇakasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it does not contain living beings, and he does not perceive it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc62:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc62:2.3.2
Pali: “sappāṇakan”ti ajānanto, 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not know that it contains living beings; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc62:2.3.3
Pali: “appāṇakan”ti jānanto, 
English (brahmali, published): if he knows that it does not contain living beings; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc62:2.3.4
Pali: “paribhogena na marissantī”ti jānanto paribhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he uses it knowing that they will not die; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc62:2.3.5
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc62:2.3.6
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc62:2.3.7

English (brahmali, published): The training rule on using water with living beings in it, the second, is finished.

Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis
