Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:0.4
Pali: Sappāṇakavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on containing living beings 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:0.5
Pali: 63. Ukkoṭanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 63. Reopening a settled legal issue 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū jānaṁ yathādhammaṁ nihatādhikaraṇaṁ punakammāya ukkoṭenti—
English (brahmali, published): At that time the monks from the group of six were reopening a legal issue that they knew had been legitimately settled, saying, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:1.3
Pali: “akataṁ kammaṁ dukkaṭaṁ kammaṁ puna kātabbaṁ kammaṁ anihataṁ dunnihataṁ puna nihanitabban”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “The legal procedure hasn’t been done;” “It’s been done badly;” “It should be done again;” “It hasn’t been settled;” “It’s been badly settled;” “It should be settled again.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:1.4
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:1.5
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū jānaṁ yathādhammaṁ nihatādhikaraṇaṁ punakammāya ukkoṭessantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the monks from the group of six reopen a legal issue that they know has been legitimately settled?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:1.6
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, jānaṁ yathādhammaṁ nihatādhikaraṇaṁ punakammāya ukkoṭethā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you’re doing this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:1.7
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:1.8
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:1.9
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, jānaṁ yathādhammaṁ nihatādhikaraṇaṁ punakammāya ukkoṭessatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:1.10
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:1.11
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:1.12.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu jānaṁ yathādhammaṁ nihatādhikaraṇaṁ punakammāya ukkoṭeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk reopens a legal issue that he knows has been legitimately settled, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:2.1.5
Pali: Jānāti nāma 
English (brahmali, published): He knows: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:2.1.6
Pali: sāmaṁ vā jānāti, aññe vā tassa ārocenti, so vā āroceti. 
English (brahmali, published): he knows by himself or others have told him or the Sangha has told him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:2.1.7
Pali: Yathādhammaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Legitimately: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:2.1.8
Pali: dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena kataṁ, etaṁ yathādhammaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): done according to the Teaching, according to the Monastic Law, according to the Teacher’s instruction—this is called “legitimately”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:2.1.9
Pali: Adhikaraṇaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A legal issue: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:2.1.10
Pali: cattāri adhikaraṇāni— vivādādhikaraṇaṁ, anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ, āpattādhikaraṇaṁ, kiccādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): there are four kinds of legal issues: legal issues arising from disputes, legal issues arising from accusations, legal issues arising from offenses, legal issues arising from business. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:2.1.11
Pali: Punakammāya ukkoṭeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Reopens: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:2.1.12
Pali: “akataṁ kammaṁ dukkaṭaṁ kammaṁ punakātabbaṁ kammaṁ anihataṁ dunnihataṁ puna nihanitabban”ti ukkoṭeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he reopens it, saying, “The legal procedure hasn’t been done;” “It’s been done badly;” “It should be done again;” “It hasn’t been settled;” “It’s been badly settled;” “It should be settled again,” he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:2.2.1
Pali: Dhammakamme dhammakammasaññī ukkoṭeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and he perceives it as such, and he reopens it, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:2.2.2
Pali: Dhammakamme vematiko ukkoṭeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but he is unsure of it, and he reopens it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:2.2.3
Pali: Dhammakamme adhammakammasaññī ukkoṭeti, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but he perceives it as illegitimate, and he reopens it, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:2.2.4
Pali: Adhammakamme dhammakammasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but he perceives it as legitimate, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:2.2.5
Pali: Adhammakamme vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:2.2.6
Pali: Adhammakamme adhammakammasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and he perceives it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:2.3.2
Pali: “adhammena vā vaggena vā na kammārahassa vā kammaṁ katan”ti jānanto ukkoṭeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he reopens it because he knows that the legal procedure was illegitimate, done by an incomplete assembly, or done against one who did not deserve it; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:2.3.3
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:2.3.4
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc63:2.3.5
Pali: Ukkoṭanasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ tatiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on reopening a settled legal issue, the third, is finished.

English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession

English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on containing living beings 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:0.5
Pali: 64. Duṭṭhullasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 64. Concealing a grave offense 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā upanando sakyaputto sañcetanikaṁ sukkavissaṭṭhiṁ āpattiṁ āpajjitvā bhātuno saddhivihārikassa bhikkhuno ārocesi—
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Upananda the Sakyan had committed an offense of intentional emission of semen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:1.3
Pali: “ahaṁ, āvuso, sañcetanikaṁ sukkavissaṭṭhiṁ āpattiṁ āpanno. 
English (brahmali, published): He told his brother’s student about this, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:1.4
Pali: Mā kassaci ārocehī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): adding, “Don’t tell anyone.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:1.5
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu sañcetanikaṁ sukkavissaṭṭhiṁ āpattiṁ āpajjitvā saṅghaṁ tassā āpattiyā parivāsaṁ yāci. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards another monk also committed an offense of intentional emission of semen. He asked the Sangha for probation, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:1.6
Pali: Tassa saṅgho tassā āpattiyā parivāsaṁ adāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): which he got. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:1.7
Pali: So parivasanto taṁ bhikkhuṁ passitvā etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): While he was on probation, he saw Upananda’s brother’s student and said to him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:1.8
Pali: “ahaṁ, āvuso, sañcetanikaṁ sukkavissaṭṭhiṁ āpattiṁ āpajjitvā saṅghaṁ tassā āpattiyā parivāsaṁ yāciṁ, tassa me saṅgho tassā āpattiyā parivāsaṁ adāsi, sohaṁ parivasāmi, vediyāmahaṁ, āvuso, vediyatīti maṁ āyasmā dhāretū”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I’ve committed an offense of intentional emission of semen. I asked the Sangha for probation, which I got. I’m now undergoing probation. Please remember me as such.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:1.9
Pali: “Kiṁ nu kho, āvuso, yo aññopi imaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjati sopi evaṁ karotī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Do others who have committed this offense need to do the same?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:1.10
Pali: “Evamāvuso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:1.11
Pali: “Ayaṁ, āvuso, āyasmā upanando sakyaputto sañcetanikaṁ sukkavissaṭṭhiṁ āpattiṁ āpajjitvā so me āroceti mā kassaci ārocehī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable Upananda committed this offense and told me not to tell anyone.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:1.12
Pali: “Kiṁ pana tvaṁ, āvuso, paṭicchādesī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “So did you conceal it?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:1.13
Pali: “Evamāvuso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:1.14
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): That monk then told other monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:1.15
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:1.16
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhu bhikkhussa jānaṁ duṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ paṭicchādessatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could a monk knowingly conceal a monk’s grave offense?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:1.17
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, bhikkhu, bhikkhussa jānaṁ duṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ paṭicchādesī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monk, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:1.18
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:1.19
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:1.20
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, bhikkhussa jānaṁ duṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ paṭicchādessasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:1.21
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:1.22
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:1.23.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhussa jānaṁ duṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ paṭicchādeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk knowingly conceals a monk’s grave offense, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.1.5
Pali: Bhikkhussāti 
English (brahmali, published): A monk’s: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.1.6
Pali: aññassa bhikkhussa. 
English (brahmali, published): another monk’s. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.1.7
Pali: Jānāti nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Knowingly: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.1.8
Pali: sāmaṁ vā jānāti aññe vā tassa ārocenti, so vā āroceti. 
English (brahmali, published): he knows by himself or others have told him or the offender has told him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.1.9
Pali: Duṭṭhullā nāma āpatti 
English (brahmali, published): Grave offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.1.10
Pali: cattāri ca pārājikāni terasa ca saṅghādisesā. 
English (brahmali, published): the four offenses entailing expulsion and the thirteen entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.1.11
Pali: Paṭicchādeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Conceals: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.1.12
Pali: “imaṁ jānitvā codessanti, sāressanti khuṁsessanti, vambhessanti, maṅkuṁ karissanti nārocessāmī”ti dhuraṁ nikkhittamatte, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): thinking, “If they find out about this, they’ll accuse him, remind him, scold him, censure him, humiliate him; I won’t tell,” then by the mere fact of abandoning his duty, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.2.1
Pali: Duṭṭhullāya āpattiyā duṭṭhullāpattisaññī paṭicchādeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a grave offense, and he perceives it as such, and he conceals it, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.2.2
Pali: Duṭṭhullāya āpattiyā vematiko paṭicchādeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a grave offense, but he is unsure of it, and he conceals it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.2.3
Pali: Duṭṭhullāya āpattiyā aduṭṭhullāpattisaññī paṭicchādeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a grave offense, but he perceives it as minor, and he conceals it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.2.4
Pali: Aduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ paṭicchādeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he conceals a minor offense, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.2.5
Pali: Anupasampannassa duṭṭhullaṁ vā aduṭṭhullaṁ vā ajjhācāraṁ paṭicchādeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he conceals the grave or minor misconduct of one who is not fully ordained, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.2.6
Pali: Aduṭṭhullāya āpattiyā duṭṭhullāpattisaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a minor offense, but he perceives it as grave, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.2.7
Pali: Aduṭṭhullāya āpattiyā vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a minor offense, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.2.8
Pali: Aduṭṭhullāya āpattiyā aduṭṭhullāpattisaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a minor offense, and he perceives it as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.3.2
Pali: “saṅghassa bhaṇḍanaṁ vā kalaho vā viggaho vā vivādo vā bhavissatī”ti nāroceti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not tell because he thinks there will be quarrels or disputes in the Sangha; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.3.3
Pali: “saṅghabhedo vā saṅgharāji vā bhavissatī”ti nāroceti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not tell because he thinks there will be a fracture or schism in the Sangha; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.3.4
Pali: “ayaṁ kakkhaḷo pharuso jīvitantarāyaṁ vā brahmacariyantarāyaṁ vā karissatī”ti nāroceti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not tell because he thinks the person he is telling about is cruel and harsh and that he might become a threat to life or to the monastic life; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.3.5
Pali: aññe patirūpe bhikkhū apassanto nāroceti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not tell because he does not see any suitable monks; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.3.6
Pali: nachādetukāmo nāroceti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not tell, but not because he wants to conceal; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.3.7
Pali: “paññāyissati sakena kammenā”ti nāroceti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not tell because he thinks the other person will be known through his own actions; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.3.8
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.3.9
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc64:2.3.10
Pali: Duṭṭhullasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ catutthaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on concealing a grave offense, the fourth, is finished.

Pali: 65. Ūnavīsativassasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 65. Ordaining a person under twenty 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Rājagaha in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrel sanctuary. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena rājagahe sattarasavaggiyā dārakā sahāyakā honti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time in Rājagaha there was a group of seventeen boys who were friends, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.3
Pali: Upālidārako tesaṁ pāmokkho hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): with Upāli as their leader. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.4
Pali: Atha kho upālissa mātāpitūnaṁ etadahosi—
English (brahmali, published): Upāli’s parents considered, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.5
Pali: “kena nu kho upāyena upāli amhākaṁ accayena sukhañca jīveyya na ca kilameyyā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How can we make sure that Upāli is able to live happily without exhausting himself after we’ve passed away? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.6
Pali: Atha kho upālissa mātāpitūnaṁ etadahosi—
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.7
Pali: “sace kho upāli lekhaṁ sikkheyya, evaṁ kho upāli amhākaṁ accayena sukhañca jīveyya, na ca kilameyyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): He could become a clerk, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.8
Pali: Atha kho upālissa mātāpitūnaṁ etadahosi—
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.9
Pali: “sace kho upāli lekhaṁ sikkhissati, aṅguliyo dukkhā bhavissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): but then his fingers will hurt. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.10
Pali: Sace kho upāli gaṇanaṁ sikkheyya, evaṁ kho upāli amhākaṁ accayena sukhañca jīveyya na ca kilameyyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Or he could become an accountant, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.11
Pali: Atha kho upālissa mātāpitūnaṁ etadahosi—
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.12
Pali: “sace kho upāli gaṇanaṁ sikkhissati, urassa dukkho bhavissati. 
English (brahmali, published): but then his chest will hurt. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.13
Pali: Sace kho upāli rūpaṁ sikkheyya, evaṁ kho upāli amhākaṁ accayena sukhañca jīveyya na ca kilameyyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Or he could become a banker, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.14
Pali: Atha kho upālissa mātāpitūnaṁ etadahosi—
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.15
Pali: “sace kho upāli rūpaṁ sikkhissati, akkhīni dukkhā bhavissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): but then his eyes will hurt. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.16
Pali: Ime kho samaṇā sakyaputtiyā sukhasīlā sukhasamācārā subhojanāni bhuñjitvā nivātesu sayanesu sayanti. 
English (brahmali, published): But these Sakyan monastics have pleasant habits and a happy life. They eat nice food and sleep in beds sheltered from the wind. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.17
Pali: Sace kho upāli samaṇesu sakyaputtiyesu pabbajeyya, evaṁ kho upāli amhākaṁ accayena sukhañca jīveyya, na ca kilameyyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If Upāli goes forth with them, he’ll be able to live happily without exhausting himself after we’ve passed away.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.18
Pali: Assosi kho upālidārako mātāpitūnaṁ imaṁ kathāsallāpaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Upāli overheard this conversation between his parents. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.19
Pali: Atha kho upālidārako yena te dārakā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā te dārake etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): He then went to the other boys and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.20
Pali: “etha mayaṁ, ayyā, samaṇesu sakyaputtiyesu pabbajissāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Come, let’s go forth with the Sakyan monastics.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.21
Pali: “Sace kho tvaṁ, ayya, pabbajissasi, evaṁ mayampi pabbajissāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “If you go forth, so will we.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.22
Pali: Atha kho te dārakā ekamekassa mātāpitaro upasaṅkamitvā etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): The boys went each to his own parents and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.23
Pali: “anujānātha maṁ agārasmā anagāriyaṁ pabbajjāyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please allow me to go forth into homelessness.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.24
Pali: Atha kho tesaṁ dārakānaṁ mātāpitaro—
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.25
Pali: “sabbepime dārakā samānacchandā kalyāṇādhippāyā”ti anujāniṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): Since the parents knew that the boys all had the same desire and good intention, they gave their approval. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.26
Pali: Te bhikkhū upasaṅkamitvā pabbajjaṁ yāciṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): The boys then went to the monks and asked for the going forth. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.27
Pali: Te bhikkhū pabbājesuṁ upasampādesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): And the monks gave them the going forth and the full ordination. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.28
Pali: Te rattiyā paccūsasamayaṁ paccuṭṭhāya rodanti—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards they got up early in the morning and cried, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.29
Pali: “yāguṁ detha, bhattaṁ detha, khādanīyaṁ dethā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Give us congee, give us a meal, give us fresh food.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.30
Pali: Bhikkhū evamāhaṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): The monks said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.31
Pali: “āgametha, āvuso, yāva ratti vibhāyati. 
English (brahmali, published): “Wait until it gets light. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.32
Pali: Sace yāgu bhavissati, pivissatha. 
English (brahmali, published): If any of that becomes available then, you can have it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.33
Pali: Sace bhattaṁ bhavissati, bhuñjissatha. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.34
Pali: Sace khādanīyaṁ bhavissati, khādissatha. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.35
Pali: No ce bhavissati yāgu vā bhattaṁ vā khādanīyaṁ vā, piṇḍāya caritvā bhuñjissathā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If not, you’ll eat after walking for alms.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.36
Pali: Evampi kho te bhikkhū bhikkhūhi vuccamānā rodantiyeva—
English (brahmali, published): But they carried on as before. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.37
Pali: “yāguṁ detha, bhattaṁ detha, khādanīyaṁ dethā”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.38
Pali: Senāsanaṁ ūhadantipi ummihantipi. 
English (brahmali, published): And they defecated and urinated on the furniture. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.39
Pali: Assosi kho bhagavā rattiyā paccūsasamayaṁ paccuṭṭhāya dārakasaddaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): After rising early in the morning, the Buddha heard the sound of those boys. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.40
Pali: Sutvāna āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): He asked Venerable Ānanda, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.41
Pali: “kiṁ nu kho so, ānanda, dārakasaddo”ti? 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.42
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā ānando bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): who told him what was happening. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.43
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe bhikkhusaṅghaṁ sannipātāpetvā bhikkhū paṭipucchi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.44
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhū jānaṁ ūnavīsativassaṁ puggalaṁ upasampādentī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that the monks give the full ordination to people they know are less than twenty years old?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.45
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.46
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.47
Pali: kathañhi nāma te, bhikkhave, moghapurisā jānaṁ ūnavīsativassaṁ puggalaṁ upasampādessanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can those foolish men do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.48
Pali: Ūnakavīsativasso, bhikkhave, puggalo akkhamo hoti sītassa uṇhassa jighacchāya pipāsāya ḍaṁsamakasavātātapasarīsapasamphassānaṁ duruttānaṁ durāgatānaṁ vacanapathānaṁ uppannānaṁ sārīrikānaṁ vedanānaṁ dukkhānaṁ tibbānaṁ kharānaṁ kaṭukānaṁ asātānaṁ amanāpānaṁ pāṇaharānaṁ anadhivāsakajātiko hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): A person who is less than twenty years old is unable to endure cold and heat; hunger and thirst; horseflies, mosquitoes, wind, and the burning sun; creeping animals and insects; and rude and unwelcome speech. And they are unable to bear up with bodily feelings that are painful, severe, sharp, and destructive of life. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.49
Pali: Vīsativassova kho, bhikkhave, puggalo khamo hoti sītassa uṇhassa …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): But a person who’s twenty is able to endure these things. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.50
Pali: pāṇaharānaṁ adhivāsakajātiko hoti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.51
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.52
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:1.53.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu jānaṁ ūnavīsativassaṁ puggalaṁ upasampādeyya, so ca puggalo anupasampanno, te ca bhikkhū gārayhā, idaṁ tasmiṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk gives the full ordination to a person he knows is less than twenty years old, he commits an offense entailing confession. Moreover, that person has not received the full ordination and those monks are blameworthy.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:2.1.5
Pali: Jānāti nāma 
English (brahmali, published): He knows: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:2.1.6
Pali: sāmaṁ vā jānāti, aññe vā tassa ārocenti, so vā āroceti. 
English (brahmali, published): he knows by himself or others have told him or the candidate has told him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:2.1.7
Pali: Ūnavīsativasso nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Less than twenty years old: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:2.1.8
Pali: appattavīsativasso. 
English (brahmali, published): who has not reached twenty years. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:2.1.9
Pali: “Upasampādessāmī”ti gaṇaṁ vā ācariyaṁ vā pattaṁ vā cīvaraṁ vā pariyesati, sīmaṁ vā sammannati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, intending to give the full ordination, he searches for a group, a teacher, a bowl, or a robe, or he establishes a monastery zone, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:2.1.10
Pali: Ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): After the motion, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:2.1.11
Pali: Dvīhi kammavācāhi dukkaṭā. 
English (brahmali, published): After each of the first two announcements, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:2.1.12
Pali: Kammavācāpariyosāne upajjhāyassa āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When the last announcement is finished, the preceptor commits an offense entailing confession, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:2.1.13
Pali: Gaṇassa ca ācariyassa ca āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): while the group and the teacher commit an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:2.2.1
Pali: Ūnavīsativasse ūnavīsativassasaññī upasampādeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is less than twenty years old, and he perceives them as less, and he gives them the full ordination, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:2.2.2
Pali: Ūnavīsativasse vematiko upasampādeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is less than twenty years old, but he is unsure of it, and he gives them the full ordination, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:2.2.3
Pali: Ūnavīsativasse paripuṇṇavīsativassasaññī upasampādeti, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is less than twenty years old, but he perceives them as more, and he gives them the full ordination, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:2.2.4
Pali: Paripuṇṇavīsativasse ūnavīsativassasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is more than twenty years old, but he perceives them as less, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:2.2.5
Pali: Paripuṇṇavīsativasse vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is more than twenty years old, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:2.2.6
Pali: Paripuṇṇavīsativasse paripuṇṇavīsativassasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is more than twenty years old, and he perceives them as more, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:2.3.2
Pali: ūnavīsativassaṁ paripuṇṇavīsativassasaññī upasampādeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he gives the full ordination to someone less than twenty years old, but he perceives them as more than twenty; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:2.3.3
Pali: paripuṇṇavīsativassaṁ paripuṇṇavīsativassasaññī upasampādeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he gives the full ordination to someone more than twenty years old, and he perceives them as more than twenty; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:2.3.4
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:2.3.5
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc65:2.3.6
Pali: Ūnavīsativassasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ pañcamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on ordaining a person under twenty, the fifth, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:0.5
Pali: 66. Theyyasatthasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 66. Traveling with thieves 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro sattho rājagahā paṭiyālokaṁ gantukāmo hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): a certain group of travelers was about to go south from Rājagaha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:1.3
Pali: Aññataro bhikkhu te manusse etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): A monk said to those people, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:1.4
Pali: “ahampāyasmantehi saddhiṁ gamissāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Let me travel with you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:1.5
Pali: “Mayaṁ kho, bhante, suṅkaṁ pariharissāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “But we’re smuggling goods.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:1.6
Pali: “Pajānāthāvuso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “That’s your business.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:1.7
Pali: Assosuṁ kho kammiyā—
English (brahmali, published): The customs officers heard 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:1.8
Pali: “sattho kira suṅkaṁ pariharissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): about that group of travelers. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:1.9
Pali: Te magge pariyuṭṭhiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): They then blocked the road, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:1.10
Pali: Atha kho te kammiyā taṁ satthaṁ gahetvā acchinditvā taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): caught the group, confiscated the goods, and asked that monk, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:1.11
Pali: “kissa tvaṁ, bhante, jānaṁ theyyasatthena saddhiṁ gacchasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, why are you knowingly traveling with a group of thieves?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:1.12
Pali: Palibundhetvā muñciṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): And they detained him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:1.13
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu sāvatthiṁ gantvā bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): After being released, that monk went to Sāvatthī, where he told the monks what had happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:1.14
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:1.15
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhu jānaṁ theyyasatthena saddhiṁ saṁvidhāya ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjissatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could a monk knowingly travel by arrangement with a group of thieves?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:1.16
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, bhikkhu, jānaṁ theyyasatthena saddhiṁ saṁvidhāya ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monk, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:1.17
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:1.18
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:1.19
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, jānaṁ theyyasatthena saddhiṁ saṁvidhāya ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjissasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:1.20
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:1.21
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:1.22.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu jānaṁ theyyasatthena saddhiṁ saṁvidhāya ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjeyya, antamaso gāmantarampi, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk knowingly travels by arrangement with a group of thieves, even just to the next inhabited area, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.1.5
Pali: Jānāti nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Knowingly: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.1.6
Pali: sāmaṁ vā jānāti, aññe vā tassa ārocenti, so vā āroceti. 
English (brahmali, published): he knows by himself or others have told him or the group of travelers has told him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.1.7
Pali: Theyyasattho nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A group of thieves: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.1.8
Pali: corā katakammā vā honti akatakammā vā, rājānaṁ vā theyyaṁ gacchanti suṅkaṁ vā pariharanti. 
English (brahmali, published): thieves who have done their deed or thieves who have not. They are stealing from the king or smuggling. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.1.9
Pali: Saddhinti 
English (brahmali, published): With: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.1.10
Pali: ekato. 
English (brahmali, published): together. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.1.11
Pali: Saṁvidhāyāti—
English (brahmali, published): By arrangement: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.1.12
Pali: “gacchāmāvuso, gacchāma, bhante; gacchāma, bhante, gacchāmāvuso, ajja vā hiyyo vā pare vā gacchāmā”ti saṁvidahati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes an arrangement like this: the monk says, “Let’s go,” and they reply, “Yes, let’s go, venerable;” or they say, “Let’s go, venerable,” and the monk replies, “Yes, let’s go;” or the monk says, “Let’s go today,” “Let’s go tomorrow,” or “Let’s go the day after tomorrow,” then he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.1.13
Pali: Antamaso gāmantarampīti 
English (brahmali, published): Even just to the next inhabited area: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.1.14
Pali: kukkuṭasampāte gāme gāmantare gāmantare āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when the inhabited areas are a chicken’s flight apart, then for every next inhabited area he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.1.15
Pali: Agāmake araññe addhayojane addhayojane āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When it is an uninhabited area, a wilderness, then for every six kilometers he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.2.1
Pali: Theyyasatthe theyyasatthasaññī saṁvidhāya ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjati, antamaso gāmantarampi, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a group of traveling thieves, and he perceives it as such, and he travels by arrangement with them, even just to the next inhabited area, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.2.2
Pali: Theyyasatthe vematiko saṁvidhāya ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjati, antamaso gāmantarampi, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a group of traveling thieves, but he is unsure of it, and he travels by arrangement with them, even just to the next inhabited area, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.2.3
Pali: Theyyasatthe atheyyasatthasaññī saṁvidhāya ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjati, antamaso gāmantarampi, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a group of traveling thieves, but he does not perceive it as such, and he travels by arrangement with them, even just to the next inhabited area, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.2.4
Pali: Bhikkhu saṁvidahati, manussā na saṁvidahanti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the monk makes an arrangement, but the group does not express its agreement, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.2.5
Pali: Atheyyasatthe theyyasatthasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a group of traveling thieves, but he perceives it as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.2.6
Pali: Atheyyasatthe vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a group of traveling thieves, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.2.7
Pali: Atheyyasatthe atheyyasatthasaññī anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a group of traveling thieves, and he does not perceive it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.3.2
Pali: asaṁvidahitvā gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he goes, but not by arrangement; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.3.3
Pali: manussā saṁvidahanti bhikkhu na saṁvidahati, 
English (brahmali, published): if the group has made an arrangement, but he has not expressed his agreement; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.3.4
Pali: visaṅketena gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he goes, but not according to the arrangement; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.3.5
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.3.6
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.3.7
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc66:2.3.8
Pali: Theyyasatthasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ chaṭṭhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on traveling with thieves, the sixth, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:0.5
Pali: 67. Saṁvidhānasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 67. Traveling with a woman 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu kosalesu janapade sāvatthiṁ gacchanto aññatarena gāmadvārena atikkamati. 
English (brahmali, published): a monk who was traveling through the Kosalan country on his way to Sāvatthī walked through the gateway of a certain village. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:1.3
Pali: Aññatarā itthī sāmikena saha bhaṇḍitvā gāmato nikkhamitvā taṁ bhikkhuṁ passitvā etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): A woman who had had an argument with her husband walked through the same gateway. When she saw that monk, she asked him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:1.4
Pali: “kahaṁ, bhante, ayyo gamissatī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, where are you going?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:1.5
Pali: “Sāvatthiṁ kho ahaṁ, bhagini, gamissāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I’m going to Sāvatthī.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:1.6
Pali: “Ahaṁ ayyena saddhiṁ gamissāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “May I go with you?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:1.7
Pali: “Eyyāsi, bhaginī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sure.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:1.8
Pali: Atha kho tassā itthiyā sāmiko gāmato nikkhamitvā manusse pucchi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards that woman’s husband also left that village. He asked around, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:1.9
Pali: “apāyyo, evarūpiṁ itthiṁ passeyyāthā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Have you seen such-and-such a woman?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:1.10
Pali: “Esāyyo, pabbajitena saha gacchatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “She’s walking along with a monastic.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:1.11
Pali: Atha kho so puriso anubandhitvā taṁ bhikkhuṁ gahetvā ākoṭetvā muñci. 
English (brahmali, published): He then followed after them, caught that monk, and gave him a beating. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:1.12
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu aññatarasmiṁ rukkhamūle padhūpento nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): The monk sat down fuming at the foot of a tree. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:1.13
Pali: Atha kho sā itthī taṁ purisaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): And the woman said to her husband, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:1.14
Pali: “nāyyo, so bhikkhu maṁ nippātesi; 
English (brahmali, published): “This monk didn’t make me go; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:1.15
Pali: api ca ahameva tena bhikkhunā saddhiṁ gacchāmi; 
English (brahmali, published): I was the one who went with him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:1.16
Pali: akārako so bhikkhu; 
English (brahmali, published): He’s innocent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:1.17
Pali: gaccha, naṁ khamāpehī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Go and ask his forgiveness.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:1.18
Pali: Atha kho so puriso taṁ bhikkhuṁ khamāpesi. 
English (brahmali, published): And he did so. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:1.19
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu sāvatthiṁ gantvā bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): That monk then went to Sāvatthī where he told the monks what had happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:1.20
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:1.21
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhu mātugāmena saddhiṁ saṁvidhāya ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjissatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could a monk travel by arrangement with a woman?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:1.22
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, bhikkhu, mātugāmena saddhiṁ saṁvidhāya ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monk, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:1.23
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:1.24

English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:1.25
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, mātugāmena saddhiṁ saṁvidhāya ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjissasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, how could do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:1.26
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:1.27
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ, uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:1.28.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu mātugāmena saddhiṁ saṁvidhāya ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjeyya, antamaso gāmantarampi, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk travels by arrangement with a woman, even just to the next inhabited area, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:2.1.5
Pali: Mātugāmo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A woman: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:2.1.6
Pali: manussitthī, na yakkhī na petī na tiracchānagatā viññū paṭibalā subhāsitadubbhāsitaṁ duṭṭhullāduṭṭhullaṁ ājānituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): a human female, not a female spirit, not a female ghost, not a female animal. She understands and is capable of discerning good and bad speech, what is decent and what is indecent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:2.1.7
Pali: Saddhinti 
English (brahmali, published): With: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:2.1.8
Pali: ekato. 
English (brahmali, published): together. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:2.1.9
Pali: Saṁvidhāyāti—
English (brahmali, published): By arrangement: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:2.1.10
Pali: “gacchāma bhagini, gacchāmāyya, gacchāmāyya, gacchāma bhagini, ajja vā hiyyo vā pare vā gacchāmā”ti saṁvidahati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes an arrangement like this: he says, “Let’s go,” and she replies, “Yes, let’s go, venerable;” or she says, “Let’s go, venerable,” and he replies, “Yes, let’s go;” or he says, “Let’s go today,” “Let’s go tomorrow,” or “Let’s go the day after tomorrow,” then he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:2.1.11
Pali: Antamaso gāmantarampīti 
English (brahmali, published): Even just to the next inhabited area: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:2.1.12
Pali: kukkuṭasampāte gāme gāmantare gāmantare āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when the inhabited areas are a chicken’s flight apart, then for every next inhabited area he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:2.1.13
Pali: Agāmake araññe addhayojane addhayojane āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When it is an uninhabited area, a wilderness, then for every six kilometers he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:2.2.1
Pali: Mātugāme mātugāmasaññī saṁvidhāya ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjati, antamaso gāmantarampi, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a woman, and he perceives her as such, and he travels by arrangement with her, even just to the next inhabited area, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:2.2.2
Pali: Mātugāme vematiko saṁvidhāya ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjati, antamaso gāmantarampi, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a woman, but he is unsure of it, and he travels by arrangement with her, even just to the next inhabited area, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:2.2.3
Pali: Mātugāme amātugāmasaññī saṁvidhāya ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjati, antamaso gāmantarampi, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a woman, but he does not perceive her as such, and he travels by arrangement with her, even just to the next inhabited area, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:2.2.4
Pali: Bhikkhu saṁvidahati mātugāmo na saṁvidahati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the monk makes an arrangement, but the woman does not express her agreement, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:2.2.5
Pali: Yakkhiyā vā petiyā paṇḍakena vā tiracchānagatamanussaviggahitthiyā vā saddhiṁ saṁvidhāya ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjati, antamaso gāmantarampi, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he travels by arrangement with a female spirit, with a female ghost, with a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>paṇḍaka</i>, with a female animal in the form of a woman, even just to the next inhabited area, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:2.2.6
Pali: Amātugāme mātugāmasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a woman, but he perceives them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:2.2.7
Pali: Amātugāme vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a woman, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:2.2.8
Pali: Amātugāme amātugāmasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a woman, and he does not perceive them as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:2.3.2
Pali: asaṁvidahitvā gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he goes, but not by arrangement; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:2.3.3
Pali: mātugāmo saṁvidahati bhikkhu na saṁvidahati, 
English (brahmali, published): if the woman has made an arrangement, but he has not expressed his agreement; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:2.3.4
Pali: visaṅketena gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he goes, but not according to the arrangement; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:2.3.5
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:2.3.6
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:2.3.7
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc67:2.3.8
Pali: Saṁvidhānasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ sattamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on traveling with a woman, the seventh, is finished.

Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis

English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:0.4
Pali: Sappāṇakavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on containing living beings 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:0.5
Pali: 68. Ariṭṭhasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 68. Not giving up a bad view 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena ariṭṭhassa nāma bhikkhuno gaddhabādhipubbassa evarūpaṁ pāpakaṁ diṭṭhigataṁ uppannaṁ hoti—
English (brahmali, published): At that time the monk Ariṭṭha, the ex-vulture-hunter, had the following bad and erroneous view: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.3
Pali: “tathāhaṁ bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi yathā yeme antarāyikā dhammā vuttā bhagavatā te paṭisevato nālaṁ antarāyāyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “As I understand the Teaching of the Buddha, the things he calls obstacles are unable to obstruct one who indulges in them.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.4
Pali: Assosuṁ kho sambahulā bhikkhū—
English (brahmali, published): A number of monks heard 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.5
Pali: “ariṭṭhassa kira nāma bhikkhuno gaddhabādhipubbassa evarūpaṁ pāpakaṁ diṭṭhigataṁ uppannaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): that Ariṭṭha had that view. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.6
Pali: ‘tathāhaṁ bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi, yathā yeme antarāyikā dhammā vuttā bhagavatā, te paṭisevato nālaṁ antarāyāyā’”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.7
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū yena ariṭṭho bhikkhu gaddhabādhipubbo tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.8
Pali: upasaṅkamitvā ariṭṭhaṁ bhikkhuṁ gaddhabādhipubbaṁ etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): They went to him and asked, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.9
Pali: “saccaṁ kira te, āvuso ariṭṭha, evarūpaṁ pāpakaṁ diṭṭhigataṁ uppannaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Ariṭṭha, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.10
Pali: ‘tathāhaṁ bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi, yathā yeme antarāyikā dhammā vuttā bhagavatā, te paṭisevato nālaṁ antarāyāyā’”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): that you have such a view?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.11
Pali: “Evaṁ byā kho ahaṁ, āvuso, bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi— ‘yathā yeme antarāyikā dhammā vuttā bhagavatā te paṭisevato nālaṁ antarāyāyā’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes, indeed. As I understand the Buddha’s Teaching, the things he calls obstacles are unable to obstruct one who indulges in them.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.12
Pali: “Mā, āvuso ariṭṭha, evaṁ avaca. Mā bhagavantaṁ abbhācikkhi. Na hi sādhu bhagavato abbhakkhānaṁ. Na hi bhagavā evaṁ vadeyya. 
English (brahmali, published): “No, Ariṭṭha, don’t misrepresent the Buddha, for it’s not good to misrepresent him. The Buddha would never say such a thing. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.13
Pali: Anekapariyāyenāvuso ariṭṭha, antarāyikā dhammā antarāyikā vuttā bhagavatā. Alañca pana te paṭisevato antarāyāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha has given many discourses about the obstacles being obstructive and how they obstruct one who indulges in them. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.14
Pali: Appassādā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā bahudukkhā bahupāyāsā, ādīnavo ettha bhiyyo. 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha has said that the enjoyment provided by worldly pleasures is small, whereas the suffering and trouble with them are huge, and so their drawbacks are greater. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.15
Pali: Aṭṭhikaṅkalūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā bahudukkhā bahupāyāsā, ādīnavo ettha bhiyyo. 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha has said that worldly pleasures are similar to a skeleton … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.16
Pali: Maṁsapesūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): similar to a piece of meat … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.17
Pali: tiṇukkūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā … 
English (brahmali, published): similar to a grass torch … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.18
Pali: aṅgārakāsūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā … 
English (brahmali, published): similar to a pit of coals … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.19
Pali: supinakūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā … 
English (brahmali, published): similar to a dream … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.20
Pali: yācitakūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā … 
English (brahmali, published): similar to borrowed goods … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.21
Pali: rukkhaphalūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā … 
English (brahmali, published): similar to fruits on a tree … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.22
Pali: asisūnūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā … 
English (brahmali, published): similar to a knife and chopping block … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.23
Pali: sattisūlūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā … 
English (brahmali, published): similar to swords and stakes … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.24
Pali: sappasirūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā bahudukkhā bahupāyāsā, ādīnavo ettha bhiyyo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): similar to a snake’s head; the suffering and trouble with them are huge, and so their drawbacks are greater.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.25
Pali: Evampi kho ariṭṭho bhikkhu gaddhabādhipubbo tehi bhikkhūhi vuccamāno tatheva taṁ pāpakaṁ diṭṭhigataṁ thāmasā parāmāsā abhinivissa voharati—
English (brahmali, published): But even though the monks corrected Ariṭṭha like this, he stubbornly held on to that bad and erroneous view, and continued to insist on it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.26
Pali: “evaṁ byā kho ahaṁ, āvuso, bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi— ‘yathā yeme antarāyikā dhammā vuttā bhagavatā, te paṭisevato nālaṁ antarāyāyā’”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.27
Pali: Yato ca kho te bhikkhū nāsakkhiṁsu ariṭṭhaṁ bhikkhuṁ gaddhabādhipubbaṁ etasmā pāpakā diṭṭhigatā vivecetuṁ, atha kho te bhikkhū yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Since they were unable to make him give up that view, they went to the Buddha and told him what had happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.28
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe bhikkhusaṅghaṁ sannipātāpetvā ariṭṭhaṁ bhikkhuṁ gaddhabādhipubbaṁ paṭipucchi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha had the Sangha gathered and questioned Ariṭṭha: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.29
Pali: “saccaṁ kira te, ariṭṭha, evarūpaṁ pāpakaṁ diṭṭhigataṁ uppannaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Ariṭṭha, that you have such a view?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.30
Pali: ‘tathāhaṁ bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi yathā yeme antarāyikā dhammā vuttā bhagavatā te paṭisevato nālaṁ antarāyāyā’”ti? 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.31
Pali: “Evaṁ byā kho ahaṁ, bhante, bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi— ‘yathā yeme antarāyikā dhammā vuttā bhagavatā te paṭisevato nālaṁ antarāyāyā’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes indeed, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.32
Pali: Kassa nu kho nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, mayā evaṁ dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāsi? 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, who do you think I have taught like this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.33

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.45
Pali: Atha ca pana tvaṁ, moghapurisa, attanā duggahitena amhe ceva abbhācikkhasi, attānañca khaṇasi, bahuñca apuññaṁ pasavasi. 
English (brahmali, published): And yet, foolish man, by misunderstanding you have misrepresented me, hurt yourself, and made much demerit. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.46

English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.48
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.49.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu evaṁ vadeyya—
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk says, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.50

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.51

English (brahmali, published): then the monks should correct him like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.52

English (brahmali, published): “No, venerable, don’t misrepresent the Buddha, for it’s not good to misrepresent the Buddha. The Buddha would never say such a thing. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.53

English (brahmali, published): In many discourses the Buddha has declared the obstacles to be obstructive and how they obstruct one who indulges in them.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.54
Pali: Evañca so bhikkhu bhikkhūhi vuccamāno tatheva paggaṇheyya, so bhikkhu bhikkhūhi yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsitabbo tassa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): If that monk continues as before, the monks should press him up to three times to make him give up that view. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.55
Pali: Yāvatatiyañce samanubhāsiyamāno taṁ paṭinissajjeyya, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): If he then gives it up, all is well. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:1.56
Pali: no ce paṭinissajjeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.5
Pali: Evaṁ vadeyyāti—
English (brahmali, published): Says: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.6
Pali: “tathāhaṁ bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi, yathā yeme antarāyikā dhammā vuttā bhagavatā te paṭisevato nālaṁ antarāyāyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “As I understand the Buddha’s Teaching, the things he calls obstacles are unable to obstruct one who indulges in them.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.7
Pali: So bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Him: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.8
Pali: yo so evaṁvādī bhikkhu. 
English (brahmali, published): the monk who speaks in that way. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.9
Pali: Bhikkhūhīti 
English (brahmali, published): The monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.10
Pali: aññehi bhikkhūhi. Ye passanti ye suṇanti tehi vattabbo—
English (brahmali, published): other monks, those who see it or hear about it. They should correct him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.11
Pali: “māyasmā evaṁ avaca, mā bhagavantaṁ abbhācikkhi, na hi sādhu bhagavato abbhakkhānaṁ, na hi bhagavā evaṁ vadeyya, 
English (brahmali, published): “No, venerable, don’t misrepresent the Buddha, for it’s not good to misrepresent the Buddha. The Buddha would never say such a thing. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.12
Pali: anekapariyāyenāvuso antarāyikā dhammā antarāyikā vuttā bhagavatā. Alañca pana te paṭisevato antarāyāyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): In many discourses the Buddha has declared the obstacles to be obstructive and how they obstruct one who indulges in them.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.13
Pali: Dutiyampi vattabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): And they should correct him a second 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.14
Pali: Tatiyampi vattabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): and a third time. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.15
Pali: Sace paṭinissajjati, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): If he gives up that view, all is well. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.16
Pali: no ce paṭinissajjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.17
Pali: Sutvā na vadanti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If those who hear about it do not say anything, they commit an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.18
Pali: So bhikkhu saṅghamajjhampi ākaḍḍhitvā vattabbo—
English (brahmali, published): That monk, even if he has to be pulled into the Sangha, should be corrected like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.19
Pali: “māyasmā evaṁ avaca, mā bhagavantaṁ abbhācikkhi, na hi sādhu bhagavato abbhakkhānaṁ, na hi bhagavā evaṁ vadeyya,

English (brahmali, published): And they should correct him a second 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.22

English (brahmali, published): and a third time. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.23
Pali: Sace paṭinissajjati, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he gives up that view, all is well. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.24
Pali: No ce paṭinissajjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.25
Pali: So bhikkhu samanubhāsitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): He should then be pressed. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.26
Pali: “Evañca pana, bhikkhave, samanubhāsitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, he should be pressed like this. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.27
Pali: Byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena saṅgho ñāpetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): A competent and capable monk should inform the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.28
Pali: ‘Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.29
Pali: Itthannāmassa bhikkhuno evarūpaṁ pāpakaṁ diṭṭhigataṁ uppannaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): Monk so-and-so has the following bad and erroneous view: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.30
Pali: “tathāhaṁ bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi, yathā yeme antarāyikā dhammā vuttā bhagavatā, te paṭisevato nālaṁ antarāyāyā”ti.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.31
Pali: So taṁ diṭṭhiṁ na paṭinissajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): He is not giving up that view. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.32
Pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuṁ samanubhāseyya—
English (brahmali, published): If the Sangha is ready, it should press monk so-and-so 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.33
Pali: tassā diṭṭhiyā paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): to make him give it up. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.34
Pali: Esā ñatti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the motion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.35
Pali: Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

English (brahmali, published): Monk so-and-so has the following bad and erroneous view:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.38

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.39
Pali: Saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuṁ samanubhāsati tassā diṭṭhiyā paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha presses monk so-and-so to make him give up that view. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.40
Pali: Yassāyasmato khamati itthannāmassa bhikkhuno samanubhāsanā, tassā diṭṭhiyā paṭinissaggāya, so tuṇhassa; 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who approves of pressing monk so-and-so to make him give up that view should remain silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.41
Pali: yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up.

English (brahmali, published): Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.46

English (brahmali, published): The Sangha presses monk so-and-so to make him give up that view. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.49
Pali: Yassāyasmato khamati itthannāmassa bhikkhuno samanubhāsanā, tassā diṭṭhiyā paṭinissaggāya, so tuṇhassa;

Pali: yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.51
Pali: Samanubhaṭṭho saṅghena itthannāmo bhikkhu, tassā diṭṭhiyā paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha has pressed monk so-and-so to make him give up that view. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.52
Pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.53
Pali: Ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): After the motion, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.54
Pali: Dvīhi kammavācāhi dukkaṭā. 
English (brahmali, published): After each of the first two announcements, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.1.55
Pali: Kammavācāpariyosāne āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When the last announcement is finished, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.2.1
Pali: Dhammakamme dhammakammasaññī na paṭinissajjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and he perceives it as such, and he does not give up his view, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.2.2
Pali: Dhammakamme vematiko na paṭinissajjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but he is unsure of it, and he does not give up his view, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.2.3
Pali: Dhammakamme adhammakammasaññī na paṭinissajjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but he perceives it as illegitimate, and he does not give up his view, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.2.4
Pali: Adhammakamme dhammakammasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but he perceives it as legitimate, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.2.5
Pali: Adhammakamme vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.2.6
Pali: Adhammakamme adhammakammasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and he perceives it as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.3.2
Pali: asamanubhāsantassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he has not been pressed; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.3.3
Pali: paṭinissajjantassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he gives it up; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.3.4
Pali: ummattakassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.3.5
Pali: 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc68:2.3.6
Pali: Ariṭṭhasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ aṭṭhamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on not giving up a bad view, the eighth, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69
Root: root/pli/ms/vinaya/pli-tv-bu-vb/pli-tv-bu-vb-pc/pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69_root-pli-ms.json
