pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:0.4
Pali: Sappāṇakavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on containing living beings 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:0.5
Pali: 69. Ukkhittasambhogasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 69. Living with an ejected monk 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū jānaṁ tathāvādinā ariṭṭhena bhikkhunā akaṭānudhammena taṁ diṭṭhiṁ appaṭinissaṭṭhena saddhiṁ sambhuñjantipi saṁvasantipi sahāpi seyyaṁ kappenti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the monks from the group of six lived with the monk Ariṭṭha, and they did formal meetings and shared a sleeping place with him. Yet they knew that he was saying such things, that he had not made amends according to the rule, and that he had not given up that view. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:1.3
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:1.4
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū jānaṁ tathāvādinā ariṭṭhena bhikkhunā akaṭānudhammena taṁ diṭṭhiṁ appaṭinissaṭṭhena saddhiṁ sambhuñjissantipi saṁvasissantipi sahāpi seyyaṁ kappessantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the monks from the group of six live, do formal meetings, and share a sleeping place with the monk Ariṭṭha, even though they know that he’s saying such things, that he hasn’t made amends according to the rule, and that he hasn’t given up that view?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:1.5
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, jānaṁ tathāvādinā ariṭṭhena bhikkhunā akaṭānudhammena taṁ diṭṭhiṁ appaṭinissaṭṭhena saddhiṁ sambhuñjathāpi saṁvasathāpi sahāpi seyyaṁ kappethā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:1.6
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:1.7
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:1.8
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, jānaṁ tathāvādinā ariṭṭhena bhikkhunā akaṭānudhammena taṁ diṭṭhiṁ appaṭinissaṭṭhena saddhiṁ sambhuñjissathāpi saṁvasissathāpi sahāpi seyyaṁ kappessatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:1.9
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:1.10
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:1.11.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu jānaṁ tathāvādinā bhikkhunā akaṭānudhammena taṁ diṭṭhiṁ appaṭinissaṭṭhena saddhiṁ sambhuñjeyya vā saṁvaseyya vā saha vā seyyaṁ kappeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk lives, does formal meetings, or shares a sleeping place with a monk who he knows is saying such things, who has not made amends according to the rule, and who has not given up that view, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.1.5
Pali: Jānāti nāma 
English (brahmali, published): He knows: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.1.6
Pali: sāmaṁ vā jānāti, aññe vā tassa ārocenti, so vā āroceti. 
English (brahmali, published): he knows by himself or others have told him or the offender has told him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.1.7
Pali: Tathāvādināti—
English (brahmali, published): Who is saying such things: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.1.8
Pali: “tathāhaṁ bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi, yathā yeme antarāyikā dhammā vuttā bhagavatā, te paṭisevato nālaṁ antarāyāyā”ti evaṁ vādinā. 
English (brahmali, published): one who says this: “As I understand the Buddha’s Teaching, the things he calls obstacles are unable to obstruct one who indulges in them.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.1.9
Pali: Akaṭānudhammo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Who has not made amends according to the rule: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.1.10
Pali: ukkhitto anosārito. 
English (brahmali, published): who has been ejected and not readmitted. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.1.11
Pali: Taṁ diṭṭhiṁ appaṭinissaṭṭhena saddhinti 
English (brahmali, published): With a monk who has not given up that view: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.1.12
Pali: etaṁ diṭṭhiṁ appaṭinissaṭṭhena saddhiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): with a monk who has not given up this view. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.1.13
Pali: Sambhuñjeyya vāti 
English (brahmali, published): Lives with: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.1.14
Pali: sambhogo nāma dve sambhogā— āmisasambhogo ca dhammasambhogo ca. 
English (brahmali, published): there are two types of living together: material living together and spiritual living together. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.1.15
Pali: Āmisasambhogo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Material living together: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.1.16
Pali: āmisaṁ deti vā paṭiggaṇhāti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he gives or receives material things, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.1.17
Pali: Dhammasambhogo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Spiritual living together: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.1.18
Pali: uddisati vā uddisāpeti vā, padena uddisati vā uddisāpeti vā, pade pade āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): he recites or has the other recite. If he recites or has the other recite by the line, then for every line he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.1.19
Pali: Akkharāya uddisati vā uddisāpeti vā, akkharakkharāya āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he recites or has the other recite by the syllable, then for every syllable he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.1.20
Pali: Saṁvaseyya vāti 
English (brahmali, published): Does formal meetings with: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.1.21
Pali: ukkhittakena saddhiṁ uposathaṁ vā pavāraṇaṁ vā saṅghakammaṁ vā karoti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he does the observance-day ceremony, the invitation ceremony, or a legal procedure with one who has been ejected, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.1.22
Pali: Saha vā seyyaṁ kappeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Shares a sleeping place with: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.1.23
Pali: ekacchanne ukkhittake nipanne bhikkhu nipajjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): under the same ceiling: if the monk lies down when the one who has been ejected is already lying down, he commits an offense entailing confession; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.1.24
Pali: Bhikkhu nipanne ukkhittako nipajjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if the monk is already lying down when the one who has been ejected lies down, he commits an offense entailing confession; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.1.25
Pali: Ubho vā nipajjanti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if they both lie down together, he commits an offense entailing confession; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.1.26
Pali: Uṭṭhahitvā punappunaṁ nipajjanti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): every time they get up and then lie down again, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.2.1
Pali: Ukkhittake ukkhittakasaññī sambhuñjati vā saṁvasati vā saha vā seyyaṁ kappeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other monk has been ejected, and he perceives him as such, and he lives or does formal meetings or shares a sleeping place with him, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.2.2
Pali: Ukkhittake vematiko sambhuñjati vā saṁvasati vā saha vā seyyaṁ kappeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other monk has been ejected, but he is unsure of it, and he lives or does formal meetings or shares a sleeping place with him, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.2.3
Pali: Ukkhittake anukkhittakasaññī sambhuñjati vā saṁvasati vā saha vā seyyaṁ kappeti, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other monk has been ejected, but he does not perceive him as such, and he lives or does formal meetings or shares a sleeping place with him, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.2.4
Pali: Anukkhittake ukkhittakasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other monk has not been ejected, but he perceives him as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.2.5
Pali: Anukkhittake vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other monk has not been ejected, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.2.6
Pali: Anukkhittake anukkhittakasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other monk has not been ejected, and he does not perceive him as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.3.2
Pali: anukkhittoti jānāti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he knows that he has not been ejected; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.3.3
Pali: ukkhitto osāritoti jānāti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he knows that he has been readmitted after being ejected; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.3.4
Pali: taṁ diṭṭhiṁ paṭinissaṭṭhoti jānāti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he knows that he has given up that view; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.3.5
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.3.6
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc69:2.3.7
Pali: Ukkhittasambhogasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ navamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on living with an ejected monk, the ninth, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70
Root: root/pli/ms/vinaya/pli-tv-bu-vb/pli-tv-bu-vb-pc/pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70_root-pli-ms.json

English translation sources:
  - brahmali [published]: translation/en/brahmali/vinaya/pli-tv-bu-vb/pli-tv-bu-vb-pc/pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70_translation-en-brahmali.json

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:0.4
Pali: Sappāṇakavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on containing living beings 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:0.5
Pali: 70. Kaṇṭakasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 70. Supporting a novice monk with a bad view 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena kaṇṭakassa nāma samaṇuddesassa evarūpaṁ pāpakaṁ diṭṭhigataṁ uppannaṁ hoti—
English (brahmali, published): At that time the novice monastic Kaṇṭaka had the following bad and erroneous view: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.3
Pali: “tathāhaṁ bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi, yathā yeme antarāyikā dhammā vuttā bhagavatā te paṭisevato nālaṁ antarāyāyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “As I understand the Teaching of the Buddha, the things he calls obstacles are unable to obstruct one who indulges in them.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.4
Pali: Assosuṁ kho sambahulā bhikkhū kaṇṭakassa nāma kira samaṇuddesassa evarūpaṁ pāpakaṁ diṭṭhigataṁ uppannaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): A number of monks heard that the novice monastic Kaṇṭaka had that view. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.5
Pali: “tathāhaṁ bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi, yathā yeme antarāyikā dhammā vuttā bhagavatā, te paṭisevato nālaṁ antarāyāyā”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.6
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū yena kaṇṭako samaṇuddeso tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā kaṇṭakaṁ samaṇuddesaṁ etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): They went to see him and asked, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.7
Pali: “saccaṁ kira te, āvuso kaṇṭaka, evarūpaṁ pāpakaṁ diṭṭhigataṁ uppannaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Kaṇṭaka, that you have such a view?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.8
Pali: ‘tathāhaṁ bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi, yathā yeme antarāyikā dhammā vuttā bhagavatā te paṭisevato nālaṁ antarāyāyā’”ti? 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.9
Pali: “Evaṁ byā kho ahaṁ, bhante, bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi— ‘yathā yeme antarāyikā dhammā vuttā bhagavatā te paṭisevato nālaṁ antarāyāyā’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes, indeed. As I understand the Buddha’s Teaching, the things he calls obstacles are unable to obstruct one who indulges in them.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.10
Pali: “Mā, āvuso kaṇṭaka, evaṁ avaca. Mā bhagavantaṁ abbhācikkhi. Na hi sādhu bhagavato abbhakkhānaṁ. Na hi bhagavā evaṁ vadeyya. 
English (brahmali, published): “No, Kaṇṭaka, don’t misrepresent the Buddha, for it’s not good to misrepresent him. The Buddha would never say such a thing. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.11
Pali: Anekapariyāyenāvuso kaṇṭaka, antarāyikā dhammā antarāyikā vuttā bhagavatā. Alañca pana te paṭisevato antarāyāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha has given many discourses about the obstacles being obstructive and how they obstruct one who indulges in them. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.12
Pali: Appassādā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā bahudukkhā bahupāyāsā, ādīnavo ettha bhiyyo …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha has said that the enjoyment provided by worldly pleasures is small, whereas the suffering and trouble with them are huge, and so their drawbacks are greater. …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.13
Pali: evampi kho kaṇṭako samaṇuddeso tehi bhikkhūhi vuccamāno tatheva taṁ pāpakaṁ diṭṭhigataṁ thāmasā parāmāsā abhinivissa voharati—
English (brahmali, published): But even though the monks corrected Kaṇṭaka like this, he stubbornly held on to that bad and erroneous view, and he continued to insist on it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.14
Pali: “evaṁ byā kho ahaṁ, bhante, bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi— ‘yathā yeme antarāyikā dhammā vuttā bhagavatā te paṭisevato nālaṁ antarāyāyā’”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.15
Pali: Yato ca kho te bhikkhū nāsakkhiṁsu kaṇṭakaṁ samaṇuddesaṁ etasmā pāpakā diṭṭhigatā vivecetuṁ, atha kho te bhikkhū yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Since those monks were unable to make him give up that view, they went to the Buddha and told him what had happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.16
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe bhikkhusaṅghaṁ sannipātāpetvā kaṇṭakaṁ samaṇuddesaṁ paṭipucchi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha had the Sangha gathered and questioned the Kaṇṭaka: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.17
Pali: “saccaṁ kira te, kaṇṭaka, evarūpaṁ pāpakaṁ diṭṭhigataṁ uppannaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Kaṇṭaka, that you have such a view?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.18
Pali: ‘tathāhaṁ bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi, yathā yeme antarāyikā dhammā vuttā bhagavatā te paṭisevato nālaṁ antarāyāyā’”ti? 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.19
Pali: “Evaṁ byā kho ahaṁ, bhante, bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi— ‘yathā yeme antarāyikā dhammā vuttā bhagavatā te paṭisevato nālaṁ antarāyāyā’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes indeed, venerable sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.20
Pali: “Kassa nu kho nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, mayā evaṁ dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāsi? 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, who do you think I have taught like this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.21
Pali: Nanu mayā, moghapurisa, anekapariyāyena antarāyikā dhammā antarāyikā vuttā, alañca pana te paṭisevato antarāyāya? 
English (brahmali, published): Haven’t I given many discourses about the obstacles being obstructive and how they obstruct one who indulges in them? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.22
Pali: Appassādā kāmā vuttā mayā bahudukkhā bahupāyāsā, ādīnavo ettha bhiyyo. 
English (brahmali, published): I have said that the enjoyment provided by worldly pleasures is small, whereas the suffering and trouble with them are huge, and so their drawbacks are greater. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.23
Pali: Aṭṭhikaṅkalūpamā kāmā vuttā mayā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): I have said that worldly pleasures are similar to a skeleton … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.24
Pali: sappasirūpamā kāmā vuttā mayā bahudukkhā bahupāyāsā, ādīnavo ettha bhiyyo. 
English (brahmali, published): similar to a snake’s head; the suffering and trouble with them are huge, and so their drawbacks are greater. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.25
Pali: Atha ca pana tvaṁ, moghapurisa, attanā duggahitena amhe ceva abbhācikkhasi attānañca khaṇasi bahuñca apuññaṁ pasavasi. 
English (brahmali, published): And yet, foolish man, by misunderstanding you have misrepresented me, hurt yourself, and made much demerit. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.26
Pali: Tañhi te, moghapurisa, bhavissati dīgharattaṁ ahitāya dukkhāya. 
English (brahmali, published): This will be for your long-lasting harm and suffering. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.27
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.28
Pali: pasannānañca ekaccānaṁ aññathattāyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): and cause some to lose it.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.29
Pali: Vigarahitvā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): Having rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.30
Pali: dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.31
Pali: “tena hi, bhikkhave, saṅgho kaṇṭakaṁ samaṇuddesaṁ nāsetu. 
English (brahmali, published): “Well then, monks, the Sangha should expel the novice monastic Kaṇṭaka. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.32
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nāsetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): And this is how he should be expelled: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.33
Pali: ‘ajjatagge te, āvuso kaṇṭaka, na ceva so bhagavā satthā apadisitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘From today, Kaṇṭaka, you may not refer to the Buddha as your teacher. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.34
Pali: Yampi caññe samaṇuddesā labhanti bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ dirattatirattaṁ sahaseyyaṁ sāpi te natthi. 
English (brahmali, published): And, unlike other novices, you can no longer share a sleeping place with the monks for two or three nights. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.35
Pali: Cara pire vinassā’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Go! Away with you!’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.36
Pali: Atha kho saṅgho kaṇṭakaṁ samaṇuddesaṁ nāsesi. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha then expelled Kaṇṭaka. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.37
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū jānaṁ tathānāsitaṁ kaṇṭakaṁ samaṇuddesaṁ upalāpentipi upaṭṭhāpentipi sambhuñjantipi sahāpi seyyaṁ kappenti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the monks from the group of six befriended Kaṇṭaka, and they were attended on by him, lived with him, and shared a sleeping place with him. Yet they knew that he had been expelled. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.38
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.39
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū jānaṁ tathānāsitaṁ kaṇṭakaṁ samaṇuddesaṁ upalāpessantipi upaṭṭhāpessantipi sambhuñjissantipi sahāpi seyyaṁ kappessantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the monks from the group of six befriend Kaṇṭaka, and be attended on by him, live with him, and share a sleeping place with him, even though they know that he has been expelled?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.40
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, jānaṁ tathānāsitaṁ kaṇṭakaṁ samaṇuddesaṁ upalāpethāpi upaṭṭhāpethāpi sambhuñjathāpi sahāpi seyyaṁ kappethā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.41
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.42
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.43
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, jānaṁ tathānāsitaṁ kaṇṭakaṁ samaṇuddesaṁ upalāpessathāpi upaṭṭhāpessathāpi sambhuñjissathāpi sahāpi seyyaṁ kappessatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.44
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.45
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.46.1
Pali: “Samaṇuddesopi ce evaṁ vadeyya—
English (brahmali, published): ‘Also if a novice monastic says, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.47
Pali: ‘tathāhaṁ bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi, yathā yeme antarāyikā dhammā vuttā bhagavatā te paṭisevato nālaṁ antarāyāyā’ti, 
English (brahmali, published): “As I understand the Buddha’s Teaching, the things he calls obstacles are unable to obstruct one who indulges in them,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.48
Pali: so samaṇuddeso bhikkhūhi evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): then the monks should correct him like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.49
Pali: ‘māvuso samaṇuddesa, evaṁ avaca, mā bhagavantaṁ abbhācikkhi, na hi sādhu bhagavato abbhakkhānaṁ, na hi bhagavā evaṁ vadeyya. 
English (brahmali, published): “No, don’t misrepresent the Buddha, for it’s not good to misrepresent the Buddha. The Buddha would never say such a thing. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.50
Pali: Anekapariyāyenāvuso samaṇuddesa, antarāyikā dhammā antarāyikā vuttā bhagavatā. Alañca pana te paṭisevato antarāyāyā’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): In many discourses the Buddha has declared the obstacles to be obstructive and how they obstruct one who indulges in them.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.51
Pali: Evañca so samaṇuddeso bhikkhūhi vuccamāno tatheva paggaṇheyya, so samaṇuddeso bhikkhūhi evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): If that novice monastic continues as before, he should be told: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.52
Pali: ‘ajjatagge te, āvuso samaṇuddesa, na ceva so bhagavā satthā apadisitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): “From today on you may not refer to the Buddha as your teacher. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.53
Pali: Yampi caññe samaṇuddesā labhanti bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ dirattatirattaṁ sahaseyyaṁ sāpi te natthi. 
English (brahmali, published): And, unlike other novices, you can no longer share a sleeping place with the monks for two or three nights. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.54
Pali: Cara pire vinassā’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Go! Away with you!” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:1.55
Pali: Yo pana bhikkhu jānaṁ tathānāsitaṁ samaṇuddesaṁ upalāpeyya vā upaṭṭhāpeyya vā sambhuñjeyya vā saha vā seyyaṁ kappeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk befriends that novice monastic, or he is attended on by him, lives with him, or shares a sleeping place with him, even though he knows that he has been expelled in this way, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.1
Pali: Samaṇuddeso nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A novice monastic: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.2
Pali: sāmaṇero vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): a novice monk is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.3
Pali: Evaṁ vadeyyāti—
English (brahmali, published): Says: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.4
Pali: “tathāhaṁ bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi, yathā yeme antarāyikā dhammā vuttā bhagavatā, te paṭisevato nālaṁ antarāyāyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “As I understand the Buddha’s Teaching, the things he calls obstacles are unable to obstruct one who indulges in them.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.5
Pali: So samaṇuddesoti 
English (brahmali, published): Him: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.6
Pali: yo so evaṁvādī samaṇuddeso. 
English (brahmali, published): the novice monastic who speaks in that way. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.7
Pali: Bhikkhūhīti 
English (brahmali, published): The monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.8
Pali: aññehi bhikkhūhi, ye passanti ye suṇanti tehi vattabbo— “mā, āvuso samaṇuddesa, evaṁ avaca. Mā bhagavantaṁ abbhācikkhi. Na hi sādhu bhagavato abbhakkhānaṁ. Na hi bhagavā evaṁ vadeyya. 
English (brahmali, published): other monks, those who see it or hear about it. They should correct him, “No, don’t misrepresent the Buddha, for it’s not good to misrepresent the Buddha. The Buddha would never say such a thing. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.9
Pali: Anekapariyāyenāvuso samaṇuddesa, antarāyikā dhammā antarāyikā vuttā bhagavatā. Alañca pana te paṭisevato antarāyāyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): In many discourses the Buddha has declared the obstacles to be obstructive and how they obstruct one who indulges in them.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.10
Pali: Dutiyampi vattabbo … 
English (brahmali, published): And they should correct him a second time … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.11
Pali: tatiyampi vattabbo … 
English (brahmali, published): And they should correct him a third time … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.12
Pali: sace paṭinissajjati iccetaṁ kusalaṁ, no ce paṭinissajjati so samaṇuddeso bhikkhūhi evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): If he gives up that view, all is well. If he does not, he should be told: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.13
Pali: “ajjatagge te, āvuso samaṇuddesa, na ceva so bhagavā satthā apadisitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): “From today on you may not refer to the Buddha as your teacher. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.14
Pali: Yampi caññe samaṇuddesā labhanti bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ dirattatirattaṁ sahaseyyaṁ sāpi te natthi. 
English (brahmali, published): And, unlike other novices, you can no longer share a sleeping place with the monks for two or three nights. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.15
Pali: Cara pire vinassā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Go! Away with you!” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.16
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.17
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.18
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.19
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.20
Pali: Jānāti nāma 
English (brahmali, published): He knows: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.21
Pali: sāmaṁ vā jānāti, aññe vā tassa ārocenti, so vā āroceti. 
English (brahmali, published): he knows by himself or others have told him or the offending novice monastic has told him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.22
Pali: Tathānāsitanti 
English (brahmali, published): Expelled in this way: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.23
Pali: evaṁ nāsitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): expelled like this. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.24
Pali: Samaṇuddeso nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Novice monastic: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.25
Pali: sāmaṇero vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): novice monk is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.26
Pali: Upalāpeyya vāti 
English (brahmali, published): Befriends: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.27
Pali: tassa pattaṁ vā cīvaraṁ vā uddesaṁ vā paripucchaṁ vā dassāmīti upalāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he befriends him, saying, “I’ll give him a bowl,” “I’ll give him a robe,” “I’ll recite to him,” or “I’ll test him,” he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.28
Pali: Upaṭṭhāpeyya vāti 
English (brahmali, published): Is attended on by: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.29
Pali: tassa cuṇṇaṁ vā mattikaṁ vā dantakaṭṭhaṁ vā mukhodakaṁ vā sādiyati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he accepts bath powder, soap, a tooth cleaner, or water for rinsing the mouth from him, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.30
Pali: Sambhuñjeyya vāti 
English (brahmali, published): Lives with: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.31
Pali: sambhogo nāma dve sambhogā—
English (brahmali, published): there are two types of living together: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.32
Pali: āmisasambhogo ca dhammasambhogo ca. 
English (brahmali, published): material living together and spiritual living together. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.33
Pali: Āmisasambhogo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Material living together: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.34
Pali: āmisaṁ deti vā paṭiggaṇhāti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he gives or receives material things, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.35
Pali: Dhammasambhogo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Spiritual living together: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.36
Pali: uddisati vā uddisāpeti vā, padena uddisati vā uddisāpeti vā, pade pade āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): he recites or has the other recite. If he recites or has the other recite by the line, then for every line he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.37
Pali: Akkharāya uddisati vā uddisāpeti vā, akkharakkharāya āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he recites or has the other recite by the syllable, then for every syllable he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.38
Pali: Saha vā seyyaṁ kappeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Shares a sleeping place with: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.39
Pali: ekacchanne nāsitake samaṇuddese nipanne bhikkhu nipajjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): under the same ceiling: if the monk lies down when the expelled novice monastic is already lying down, he commits an offense entailing confession; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.40
Pali: Bhikkhu nipanne nāsitako samaṇuddeso nipajjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if the monk is already lying down when the expelled novice monastic lies down, he commits an offense entailing confession; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.41
Pali: Ubho vā nipajjanti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if they both lie down together, he commits an offense entailing confession; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.1.42
Pali: Uṭṭhahitvā punappunaṁ nipajjanti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): every time they get up and then lie down again, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.2.1
Pali: Nāsitake nāsitakasaññī upalāpeti vā upaṭṭhāpeti vā sambhuñjati vā saha vā seyyaṁ kappeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the novice monastic has been expelled, and he perceives him as such, and he befriends him or is attended on by him or lives with him or shares a sleeping place with him, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.2.2
Pali: Nāsitake vematiko upalāpeti vā upaṭṭhāpeti vā sambhuñjati vā saha vā seyyaṁ kappeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the novice monastic has been expelled, but he is unsure of it, and he befriends him or is attended on by him or lives with him or shares a sleeping place with him, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.2.3
Pali: Nāsitake anāsitakasaññī upalāpeti vā upaṭṭhāpeti vā sambhuñjati vā saha vā seyyaṁ kappeti, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If the novice monastic has been expelled, but he does not perceive him as such, and he befriends him or is attended on by him or lives with him or shares a sleeping place with him, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.2.4
Pali: Anāsitake nāsitakasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the novice monastic has not been expelled, but he perceives him such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.2.5
Pali: Anāsitake vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the novice monastic has not been expelled, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.2.6
Pali: Anāsitake anāsitakasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If the novice monastic has not been expelled, and he does not perceive him as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.3.2
Pali: anāsitakoti jānāti; 
English (brahmali, published): if he knows that he has not been expelled; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.3.3
Pali: taṁ diṭṭhiṁ paṭinissaṭṭhoti jānāti; 
English (brahmali, published): if he knows that he has given up that view; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.3.4
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.3.5
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.3.6
Pali: Kaṇṭakasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ dasamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on supporting a novice monk with a bad view, the tenth, is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.3.7
Pali: Sappāṇakavaggo sattamo. 
English (brahmali, published): The seventh subchapter on containing living beings is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.3.8
Pali: Tassuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.3.9
Pali: Sañciccavadhasappāṇaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): “Intentionally killing, containing living beings, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.3.10
Pali: ukkoṭaṁ duṭṭhullachādanaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): Reopening, concealing what is grave; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.3.11
Pali: Ūnavīsati satthañca, 
English (brahmali, published): Less than twenty, and group of travelers, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.3.12
Pali: saṁvidhānaṁ ariṭṭhakaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): Arrangement, on Ariṭṭha; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.3.13
Pali: Ukkhittaṁ kaṇṭakañceva, 
English (brahmali, published): Ejected, and Kaṇṭaka: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc70:2.3.14
Pali: dasa sikkhāpadā imeti. 
English (brahmali, published): These ten training rules.”

English (brahmali, published): 71. Not accepting legitimate correction 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā kosambiyaṁ viharati ghositārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Kosambī in Ghosita’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā channo anācāraṁ ācarati. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Channa was misbehaving. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:1.3
Pali: Bhikkhū evamāhaṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): The monks told him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:1.4
Pali: “māvuso channa, evarūpaṁ akāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Don’t do that, Channa, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:1.5
Pali: Netaṁ kappatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): it’s not allowable,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:1.6
Pali: So evaṁ vadeti—
English (brahmali, published): and he would reply, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:1.7
Pali: “na tāvāhaṁ, āvuso, etasmiṁ sikkhāpade sikkhissāmi yāva na aññaṁ bhikkhuṁ byattaṁ vinayadharaṁ paripucchāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I won’t practice this training rule until I’ve questioned a monk who is an expert on the Monastic Law.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:1.8
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:1.9
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā channo bhikkhūhi sahadhammikaṁ vuccamāno evaṁ vakkhati—
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Channa say this when legitimately corrected by the monks?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:1.10
Pali: ‘na tāvāhaṁ, āvuso, etasmiṁ sikkhāpade sikkhissāmi yāva na aññaṁ bhikkhuṁ byattaṁ vinayadharaṁ paripucchāmī’”ti …pe… 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:1.11
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, channa, bhikkhūhi sahadhammikaṁ vuccamāno evaṁ vadesi—
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Channa, that you said this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:1.12
Pali: ‘na tāvāhaṁ, āvuso, etasmiṁ sikkhāpade sikkhissāmi yāva na aññaṁ bhikkhuṁ byattaṁ vinayadharaṁ paripucchāmī’”ti? 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:1.13
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:1.14
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:1.15
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, bhikkhūhi sahadhammikaṁ vuccamāno evaṁ vakkhasi—
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, how could you say this when legitimately corrected by the monks? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:1.16
Pali: ‘na tāvāhaṁ, āvuso, etasmiṁ sikkhāpade sikkhissāmi yāva na aññaṁ bhikkhuṁ byattaṁ vinayadharaṁ paripucchāmī’ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:1.17
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:1.18
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:1.19.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhūhi sahadhammikaṁ vuccamāno evaṁ vadeyya—‘na tāvāhaṁ, āvuso, etasmiṁ sikkhāpade sikkhissāmi yāva na aññaṁ bhikkhuṁ byattaṁ vinayadharaṁ paripucchāmī’ti, pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk, when legitimately corrected by the monks, says, “I won’t practice this training rule until I’ve questioned a monk who is an expert on the Monastic Law,” he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:1.20
Pali: Sikkhamānena, bhikkhave, bhikkhunā aññātabbaṁ paripucchitabbaṁ paripañhitabbaṁ. Ayaṁ tattha sāmīcī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): A monk who is training should understand, should question, should enquire. This is the proper procedure.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.1.5
Pali: Bhikkhūhīti 
English (brahmali, published): By the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.1.6
Pali: aññehi bhikkhūhi. 
English (brahmali, published): by other monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.1.7
Pali: Sahadhammikaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Legitimately: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.1.8
Pali: yaṁ bhagavatā paññattaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ etaṁ sahadhammikaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): the training rules laid down by the Buddha—this is called “legitimately”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.1.9
Pali: Tena vuccamāno evaṁ vadeti— “na tāvāhaṁ, āvuso, etasmiṁ sikkhāpade sikkhissāmi yāva na aññaṁ bhikkhuṁ byattaṁ vinayadharaṁ paripucchāmī”ti. Paṇḍitaṁ byattaṁ medhāviṁ bahussutaṁ dhammakathikaṁ paripucchāmīti bhaṇati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When corrected in regard to this, he says, “I won’t practice this training rule until I’ve questioned a monk who’s an expert on the Monastic Law.” If he says, “I’ll question one who’s wise,” “I’ll question one who’s competent,” “I’ll question one who’s intelligent,” “I’ll question one who’s learned,” “I’ll question an expounder of the Teaching,” he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.2.1
Pali: Upasampanne upasampannasaññī evaṁ vadeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the one who corrects him is fully ordained, and he perceives him as such, and he says such a thing, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.2.2
Pali: Upasampanne vematiko evaṁ vadeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the one who corrects him is fully ordained, but he is unsure of it, and he says such a thing, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.2.3
Pali: Upasampanne anupasampannasaññī evaṁ vadeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the one who corrects him is fully ordained, but he does not perceive him as such, and he says such a thing, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.2.4
Pali: Apaññattena vuccamāno—
English (brahmali, published): If he is corrected about something that has not been laid down: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.2.5
Pali: “idaṁ na sallekhāya na dhutatthāya na pāsādikatāya na apacayāya na vīriyārambhāya saṁvattatī”ti evaṁ vadeti, “na tāvāhaṁ, āvuso, etasmiṁ sikkhāpade sikkhissāmi yāva na aññaṁ bhikkhuṁ byattaṁ vinayadharaṁ paṇḍitaṁ medhāviṁ bahussutaṁ dhammakathikaṁ paripucchāmī”ti bhaṇati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): “This isn’t conducive to self-effacement,” “This isn’t conducive to ascetic practices,” “This isn’t conducive to being inspiring,” “This isn’t conducive to a reduction in things,” “This isn’t conducive to being energetic,” and he says, “I won’t practice this training rule until I’ve questioned a monk who’s competent,” “… until I’ve questioned a monk who’s an expert on the Monastic Law,” “… until I’ve questioned a monk who’s wise,” “… until I’ve questioned a monk who’s intelligent,” “… until I’ve questioned a monk who’s learned,” “… until I’ve questioned a monk who’s an expounder of the Teaching,” he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.2.6
Pali: Anupasampannena paññattena vā apaññattena vā vuccamāno—
English (brahmali, published): If he is corrected by one who is not fully ordained about something that has or has not been laid down: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.2.7
Pali: “idaṁ na sallekhāya na dhutatthāya na pāsādikatāya na apacayāya na vīriyārambhāya saṁvattatī”ti evaṁ vadeti, “na tāvāhaṁ, āvuso, etasmiṁ sikkhāpade sikkhissāmi yāva na aññaṁ bhikkhuṁ byattaṁ vinayadharaṁ paṇḍitaṁ medhāviṁ bahussutaṁ dhammakathikaṁ paripucchāmī”ti bhaṇati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): “This isn’t conducive to self-effacement,” “This isn’t conducive to ascetic practices,” “This isn’t conducive to being inspiring,” “This isn’t conducive to a reduction in things,” “This isn’t conducive to being energetic,” and he says, “I won’t practice this training rule until I’ve questioned a monk who’s competent,” “… until I’ve questioned a monk who’s an expert on the Monastic Law,” “… until I’ve questioned a monk who’s wise,” “… until I’ve questioned a monk who’s intelligent,” “… until I’ve questioned a monk who’s learned,” “… until I’ve questioned a monk who’s an expounder of the Teaching,” he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.2.8
Pali: Anupasampanne upasampannasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the one who corrects him is not fully ordained, but he perceives them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.2.9
Pali: Anupasampanne vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the one who corrects him is not fully ordained, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.2.10
Pali: Anupasampanne anupasampannasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the one who corrects him is not fully ordained, and he does not perceive them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.2.11.1
Pali: Sikkhamānenāti 
English (brahmali, published): Who is training: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.2.12
Pali: sikkhitukāmena. 
English (brahmali, published): who wants to train. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.2.13
Pali: Aññātabbanti 
English (brahmali, published): Should understand: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.2.14
Pali: jānitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): should find out. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.2.15
Pali: Paripucchitabbanti 
English (brahmali, published): Should question: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.2.16
Pali: “idaṁ, bhante, kathaṁ; imassa vā kvattho”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): should ask, “Venerable, how is this? What’s the meaning of this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.2.17
Pali: Paripañhitabbanti 
English (brahmali, published): Should enquire: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.2.18
Pali: cintetabbaṁ tulayitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): should reflect, should weigh up. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.2.19
Pali: Ayaṁ tattha sāmīcīti 
English (brahmali, published): This is the proper procedure: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.2.20
Pali: ayaṁ tattha anudhammatā. 
English (brahmali, published): this is the right method. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.3.2
Pali: “jānissāmi sikkhissāmī”ti bhaṇati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he says, “I’ll find out and I’ll train;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.3.3
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.3.4
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc71:2.3.5
Pali: Sahadhammikasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ paṭhamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on not accepting legitimate correction, the first, is finished.

English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on legitimate correction 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:0.5
Pali: 72. Vilekhanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 72. Disparaging the training rules 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā bhikkhūnaṁ anekapariyāyena vinayakathaṁ katheti, vinayassa vaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, vinayapariyattiyā vaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, ādissa ādissa āyasmato upālissa vaṇṇaṁ bhāsati. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the Buddha gave many talks about the Monastic Law, spoke in praise of the Monastic Law and of learning the Monastic Law, and repeatedly praised Venerable Upāli. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:1.3
Pali: Bhikkhūnaṁ etadahosi—
English (brahmali, published): When the monks heard this, they thought, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:1.4
Pali: “bhagavā kho anekapariyāyena vinayakathaṁ katheti, vinayassa vaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, vinayapariyattiyā vaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, ādissa ādissa āyasmato upālissa vaṇṇaṁ bhāsati. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:1.5
Pali: Handa mayaṁ, āvuso, āyasmato upālissa santike vinayaṁ pariyāpuṇāmā”ti, te ca bahū bhikkhū therā ca navā ca majjhimā ca āyasmato upālissa santike vinayaṁ pariyāpuṇanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Well then, let’s learn the Monastic Law from Venerable Upāli.” And many monks, both senior and junior, as well as those of middle standing, learned the Monastic Law from Upāli. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:1.6
Pali: Atha kho chabbaggiyānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ etadahosi—
English (brahmali, published): The monks from the group of six 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:1.7
Pali: “etarahi kho, āvuso, bahū bhikkhū therā ca navā ca majjhimā ca āyasmato upālissa santike vinayaṁ pariyāpuṇanti. 
English (brahmali, published): considered this and thought, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:1.8
Pali: Sace ime vinaye pakataññuno bhavissanti amhe yenicchakaṁ yadicchakaṁ yāvadicchakaṁ ākaḍḍhissanti parikaḍḍhissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “If these monks become well-versed in the Monastic Law, they’ll boss us around as they like. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:1.9
Pali: Handa mayaṁ, āvuso, vinayaṁ vivaṇṇemā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): So let’s disparage the Monastic Law.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:1.10
Pali: Atha kho chabbaggiyā bhikkhū bhikkhū upasaṅkamitvā evaṁ vadanti—
English (brahmali, published): They went to the other monks and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:1.11
Pali: “kiṁ panimehi khuddānukhuddakehi sikkhāpadehi uddiṭṭhehi, yāvadeva kukkuccāya vihesāya vilekhāya saṁvattantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “What’s the point of reciting these minor training rules, when they just lead to anxiety, oppression, and annoyance?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:1.12
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:1.13
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū vinayaṁ vivaṇṇessantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the monks from the group of six disparage the Monastic Law?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:1.14
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, vinayaṁ vivaṇṇethā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:1.15
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:1.16
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:1.17
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, vinayaṁ vivaṇṇessatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:1.18
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:1.19
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:1.20.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu pātimokkhe uddissamāne evaṁ vadeyya—‘kiṁ panimehi khuddānukhuddakehi sikkhāpadehi uddiṭṭhehi, yāvadeva kukkuccāya vihesāya vilekhāya saṁvattantī’ti, sikkhāpadavivaṇṇake pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘When the Monastic Code is being recited, if a monk says, “What’s the point of reciting these minor training rules, when they just lead to anxiety, oppression, and annoyance?” then in disparaging the training rules, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:2.1.5
Pali: Pātimokkhe uddissamāneti 
English (brahmali, published): When the Monastic Code is being recited: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:2.1.6
Pali: uddisante vā uddisāpente vā sajjhāyaṁ vā karonte. 
English (brahmali, published): when reciting it, when having it recited, or when practicing it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:2.1.7
Pali: Evaṁ vadeyyāti—
English (brahmali, published): Says: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:2.1.8
Pali: “kiṁ panimehi khuddānukhuddakehi sikkhāpadehi uddiṭṭhehi, yāvadeva kukkuccāya vihesāya vilekhāya saṁvattantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “What’s the point of reciting these minor training rules, when they just lead to anxiety, oppression, and annoyance?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:2.1.9
Pali: “Ye imaṁ pariyāpuṇanti tesaṁ kukkuccaṁ hoti, vihesā hoti, vilekhā hoti, ye imaṁ na pariyāpuṇanti tesaṁ kukkuccaṁ na hoti vihesā na hoti vilekhā na hoti. Anuddiṭṭhaṁ idaṁ varaṁ, anuggahitaṁ idaṁ varaṁ, apariyāpuṭaṁ idaṁ varaṁ, adhāritaṁ idaṁ varaṁ, vinayo vā antaradhāyatu, ime vā bhikkhū appakataññuno hontū”ti upasampannassa vinayaṁ vivaṇṇeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he disparages the Monastic Law to one who is fully ordained, saying, “Those who learn this will be anxious,” “They will feel oppressed,” “They will be annoyed;” “Those who don’t learn this won’t be anxious,” “They won’t feel oppressed,” “They won’t be annoyed;” “It’s better left unrecited,” “It’s better left unlearned,” “It’s better left unstudied,” “It’s better left unmastered;” “May the Monastic Law disappear, or may these monks remain ignorant,” then he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:2.2.1
Pali: Upasampanne upasampannasaññī vinayaṁ vivaṇṇeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he disparages the Monastic Law to one who is fully ordained, and he perceives them as fully ordained, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:2.2.2
Pali: Upasampanne vematiko vinayaṁ vivaṇṇeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he disparages the Monastic Law to one who is fully ordained, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:2.2.3
Pali: Upasampanne anupasampannasaññī vinayaṁ vivaṇṇeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he disparages the Monastic Law to one who is fully ordained, but he does not perceive them as fully ordained, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:2.2.4
Pali: Aññaṁ dhammaṁ vivaṇṇeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he disparages some other rule, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:2.2.5
Pali: Anupasampannassa vinayaṁ vā aññaṁ vā dhammaṁ vivaṇṇeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he disparages the Monastic Law or some other rule to one who is not fully ordained, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:2.2.6
Pali: Anupasampanne upasampannasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is to one who is not fully ordained, but he perceives them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:2.2.7
Pali: Anupasampanne vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is to one who is not fully ordained, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:2.2.8
Pali: Anupasampanne anupasampannasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is to one who is not fully ordained, and he does not perceive them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:2.3.2
Pali: na vivaṇṇetukāmo, “iṅgha tvaṁ suttante vā gāthāyo vā abhidhammaṁ vā pariyāpuṇassu, pacchā vinayaṁ pariyāpuṇissasī”ti bhaṇati, 
English (brahmali, published): if, not desiring to disparage, he says, “Listen, learn discourses or verses or philosophy, and later you can learn the Monastic Law;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:2.3.3
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:2.3.4
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc72:2.3.5
Pali: Vilekhanasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ dutiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on disparaging the training rules, the second, is finished.

Pali: 73. Mohanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 73. Deception 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū anācāraṁ ācaritvā “aññāṇakena āpannāti jānantū”ti pātimokkhe uddissamāne evaṁ vadanti—
English (brahmali, published): the monks from the group of six were misbehaving. They said to each other, “Let’s make the other monks think that we committed these offenses because we didn’t know the rules.” Then, during the recitation of the Monastic Code, they said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:1.3
Pali: “idāneva kho mayaṁ jānāma, ayampi kira dhammo suttāgato suttapariyāpanno anvaddhamāsaṁ uddesaṁ āgacchatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Just now did we find out that this rule too is included in the Monastic Code and comes up for recitation every half-month.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:1.4
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:1.5
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū pātimokkhe uddissamāne evaṁ vakkhanti—
English (brahmali, published): “How could the monks from the group of six say this during the recitation of the Monastic Code?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:1.6
Pali: ‘idāneva kho mayaṁ jānāma, ayampi kira dhammo suttāgato suttapariyāpanno anvaddhamāsaṁ uddesaṁ āgacchatī’”ti …pe… 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:1.7
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, pātimokkhe uddissamāne evaṁ vadetha—
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you said this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:1.8
Pali: ‘idāneva kho mayaṁ jānāma, ayampi kira dhammo suttāgato suttapariyāpanno anvaddhamāsaṁ uddesaṁ āgacchatī’”ti? 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:1.9
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:1.10
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:1.11
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, pātimokkhe uddissamāne evaṁ vakkhatha—
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how could you say this during the recitation of the Monastic Code? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:1.12
Pali: ‘idāneva kho mayaṁ jānāma, ayampi kira dhammo suttāgato suttapariyāpanno anvaddhamāsaṁ uddesaṁ āgacchatī’ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:1.13
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:1.14
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:1.15.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu anvaddhamāsaṁ pātimokkhe uddissamāne evaṁ vadeyya—
English (brahmali, published): ‘During the half-monthly recitation of the Monastic Code, a monk might say, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:1.16
Pali: ‘idāneva kho ahaṁ jānāmi, ayampi kira dhammo suttāgato suttapariyāpanno anvaddhamāsaṁ uddesaṁ āgacchatī’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Just now did I find out that this rule too has come down in the Monastic Code, is included in the Monastic Code, and comes up for recitation every half-month.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:1.17
Pali: Tañce bhikkhuṁ aññe bhikkhū jāneyyuṁ nisinnapubbaṁ iminā bhikkhunā dvattikkhattuṁ pātimokkhe uddissamāne, ko pana vādo bhiyyo, na ca tassa bhikkhuno aññāṇakena mutti atthi, yañca tattha āpattiṁ āpanno tañca yathādhammo kāretabbo, uttari cassa moho āropetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): If other monks know that that monk has previously sat through at least two or three recitations of the Monastic Code, then that monk is not let off because of ignorance, and he is to be dealt with according to the rule. Further, he should be charged with deception: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:1.18
Pali: ‘tassa te, āvuso, alābhā, tassa te dulladdhaṁ, yaṁ tvaṁ pātimokkhe uddissamāne na sādhukaṁ aṭṭhiṁ katvā manasi karosī’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s a loss for you that you don’t pay proper attention during the recitation of the Monastic Code.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:1.19
Pali: Idaṁ tasmiṁ mohanake pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): And for the act of deception, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.1.5
Pali: Anvaddhamāsanti 
English (brahmali, published): Half-monthly: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.1.6
Pali: anuposathikaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): on every observance day. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.1.7
Pali: Pātimokkhe uddissamāneti 
English (brahmali, published): During the recitation of the Monastic Code: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.1.8
Pali: uddisante. 
English (brahmali, published): during the reciting. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.1.9
Pali: Evaṁ vadeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Might say: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.1.10
Pali: anācāraṁ ācaritvā—
English (brahmali, published): after misbehaving, he thinks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.1.11
Pali: “aññāṇakena āpannoti jānantū”ti pātimokkhe uddissamāne evaṁ vadeti—
English (brahmali, published): “Let them think that I committed these offenses because I didn’t know the rules.” If, during the recitation of the Monastic Code, he then says, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.1.12
Pali: “idāneva kho ahaṁ jānāmi, ayampi kira dhammo suttāgato suttapariyāpanno anvaddhamāsaṁ uddesaṁ āgacchatī”ti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): “Just now did I find out that this rule too has come down in the Monastic Code, is included in the Monastic Code, and comes up for recitation every half-month,” then he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.1.13
Pali: Tañce mohetukāmaṁ bhikkhuṁ aññe bhikkhū jāneyyuṁ nisinnapubbaṁ iminā bhikkhunā dvattikkhattuṁ pātimokkhe uddissamāne, ko pana vādo bhiyyo, na ca tassa bhikkhuno aññāṇakena mutti atthi, yañca tattha āpattiṁ āpanno, tañca yathādhammo kāretabbo, uttari cassa moho āropetabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): If other monks know that the monk who wants to deceive has previously sat through at least two or three recitations of the Monastic Code, then that monk is not let off because of ignorance, and he is to be dealt with according to the rule. Further, he should be charged with deception. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.1.14
Pali: “Evañca pana, bhikkhave, āropetabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, he is to be charged like this. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.1.15
Pali: Byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena saṅgho ñāpetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): A competent and capable monk should inform the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.1.16
Pali: ‘Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.1.17
Pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmo bhikkhu pātimokkhe uddissamāne na sādhukaṁ aṭṭhiṁ katvā manasi karoti. 
English (brahmali, published): Monk so-and-so did not pay proper attention during the recitation of the Monastic Code. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.1.18
Pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmassa bhikkhuno mohaṁ āropeyya. 
English (brahmali, published): If the Sangha is ready, it should charge monk so-and-so with deception. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.1.19
Pali: Esā ñatti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the motion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.1.20
Pali: Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.1.21
Pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmo bhikkhu pātimokkhe uddissamāne na sādhukaṁ aṭṭhiṁ katvā manasi karoti. 
English (brahmali, published): Monk so-and-so did not pay proper attention during the recitation of the Monastic Code. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.1.22
Pali: Saṅgho itthannāmassa bhikkhuno mohaṁ āropeti. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha charges monk so-and-so with deception. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.1.23
Pali: Yassāyasmato khamati itthannāmassa bhikkhuno mohassa āropanā, so tuṇhassa; 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who approves of charging monk so-and-so with deception should remain silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.1.24
Pali: yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.1.25
Pali: Āropito saṅghena itthannāmassa bhikkhuno moho. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha has charged monk so-and-so with deception. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.1.26
Pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.1.27
Pali: Anāropite mohe moheti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he deceives, but he has not been charged with deception, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.1.28
Pali: Āropite mohe moheti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he deceives, and he has been charged with deception, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.2.1
Pali: Dhammakamme dhammakammasaññī moheti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and he perceives it as legitimate, and he deceives, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.2.2
Pali: Dhammakamme vematiko moheti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but he is unsure of it, and he deceives, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.2.3
Pali: Dhammakamme adhammakammasaññī moheti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but he perceives it as illegitimate, and he deceives, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.2.4
Pali: Adhammakamme dhammakammasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but he perceives it as legitimate, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.2.5
Pali: Adhammakamme vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.2.6
Pali: Adhammakamme adhammakammasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and he perceives it as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.3.2
Pali: na vitthārena sutaṁ hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he has not heard it in full; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.3.3
Pali: ūnakadvattikkhattuṁ vitthārena sutaṁ hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he has heard it fewer than two or three times in full; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.3.4
Pali: na mohetukāmassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not want to deceive; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.3.5
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.3.6
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc73:2.3.7
Pali: Mohanasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ tatiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on deception, the third, is finished.

English (brahmali, published): They cried. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc74:1.4
Pali: Bhikkhū evamāhaṁsu—

English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them,

English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc74:1.11

English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc74:1.15.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhussa kupito anattamano pahāraṁ dadeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk hits a monk in anger, he commits an offense entailing confession.’”

Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc74:2.1.2

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc74:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk:

Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc74:2.1.5
Pali: Bhikkhussāti 
English (brahmali, published): A monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc74:2.1.6
Pali: aññassa bhikkhussa. 
English (brahmali, published): another monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc74:2.1.7
Pali: Kupito anattamanoti 
English (brahmali, published): In anger: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc74:2.1.8
Pali: anabhiraddho āhatacitto khilajāto. 
English (brahmali, published): discontent, having hatred, hostile. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc74:2.1.9
Pali: Pahāraṁ dadeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Hits: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc74:2.1.10
Pali: kāyena vā kāyapaṭibaddhena vā nissaggiyena vā antamaso uppalapattenapi pahāraṁ deti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he hits with his body, with anything connected to his body, or with anything released, even if just with a water-lily leaf, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc74:2.2.1
Pali: Upasampanne upasampannasaññī kupito anattamano pahāraṁ deti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is one who is fully ordained, and he perceives him as such, and he hits him in anger, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc74:2.2.2
Pali: Upasampanne vematiko kupito anattamano pahāraṁ deti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is one who is fully ordained, but he is unsure of it, and he hits him in anger, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc74:2.2.3
Pali: Upasampanne anupasampannasaññī kupito anattamano pahāraṁ deti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is one who is fully ordained, but he does not perceive him as such, and he hits him in anger, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc74:2.2.4
Pali: Anupasampannassa kupito anattamano pahāraṁ deti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he hits one who is not fully ordained in anger, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc74:2.2.5
Pali: Anupasampanne upasampannasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is one who is not fully ordained, but he perceives them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc74:2.2.6
Pali: Anupasampanne vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is one who is not fully ordained, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc74:2.2.7
Pali: Anupasampanne anupasampannasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is one who is not fully ordained, and he does not perceive them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc74:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc74:2.3.2
Pali: kenaci viheṭhīyamāno mokkhādhippāyo pahāraṁ deti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he hits in self-defense; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc74:2.3.3
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc74:2.3.4
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc74:2.3.5
Pali: Pahārasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ catutthaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on hitting in anger, the fourth, is finished.

English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on legitimate correction 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc75:0.5
Pali: 75. Talasattikasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 75. Raising a hand in anger 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc75:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc75:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū kupitā anattamanā sattarasavaggiyānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ talasattikaṁ uggiranti. 
English (brahmali, published): the monks from the group of six raised their hands in anger against the monks from the group of seventeen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc75:1.3
Pali: Te pahārasamuccitā rodanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Expecting to be hit, they cried. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc75:1.4
Pali: Bhikkhū evamāhaṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): Other monks asked them why, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc75:1.5
Pali: “kissa tumhe, āvuso, rodathā”ti? 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc75:1.6
Pali: “Ime, āvuso, chabbaggiyā bhikkhū kupitā anattamanā amhākaṁ talasattikaṁ uggirantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): and they told them what had happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc75:1.7
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc75:1.8
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū kupitā anattamanā sattarasavaggiyānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ talasattikaṁ uggirissantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the monks from the group of six do this?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc75:1.9
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, kupitā anattamanā sattarasavaggiyānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ talasattikaṁ uggirathā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc75:1.10
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc75:1.11
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc75:1.12
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, kupitā anattamanā sattarasavaggiyānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ talasattikaṁ uggirissatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc75:1.13
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc75:1.14
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc75:1.15.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhussa kupito anattamano talasattikaṁ uggireyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk raises a hand in anger against a monk, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc75:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc75:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever …

Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc75:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc75:2.1.5
Pali: Bhikkhussāti 
English (brahmali, published): Against a monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc75:2.1.6
Pali: aññassa bhikkhussa. 
English (brahmali, published): against another monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc75:2.1.7
Pali: Kupito anattamanoti 
English (brahmali, published): In anger: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc75:2.1.8
Pali: anabhiraddho āhatacitto khilajāto. 
English (brahmali, published): discontent, having hatred, hostile. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc75:2.1.9
Pali: Talasattikaṁ uggireyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Raises a hand: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc75:2.1.10

English (brahmali, published): if he raises any part of his body or anything connected to his body, even if just a water-lily leaf, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc75:2.2.1

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc75:2.2.4

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc75:2.2.5

English (brahmali, published): if he accuses him or has him accused, he commits an offense entailing confession.

English (brahmali, published): If it is one who is fully ordained, and he perceives him as such, and he groundlessly charges him with an offense entailing suspension, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc76:2.2.2
Pali: Upasampanne vematiko amūlakena saṅghādisesena anuddhaṁseti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is one who is fully ordained, but he is unsure of it, and he groundlessly charges him with an offense entailing suspension, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc76:2.2.3
Pali: Upasampanne anupasampannasaññī amūlakena saṅghādisesena anuddhaṁseti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is one who is fully ordained, but he does not perceive him as such, and he groundlessly charges him with an offense entailing suspension, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc76:2.2.4
Pali: Ācāravipattiyā vā diṭṭhivipattiyā vā anuddhaṁseti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he charges someone with failure in conduct or failure in view, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc76:2.2.5
Pali: Anupasampannaṁ anuddhaṁseti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he charges one who is not fully ordained, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc76:2.2.6
Pali: Anupasampanne upasampannasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is one who is not fully ordained, but he perceives them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc76:2.2.7
Pali: Anupasampanne vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is one who is not fully ordained, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc76:2.2.8
Pali: Anupasampanne anupasampannasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is one who is not fully ordained, and he does not perceive them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc76:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc76:2.3.2
Pali: tathāsaññī codeti vā codāpeti vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he accuses someone, or has someone accused, according to what he has perceived; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc76:2.3.3
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc76:2.3.4
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc76:2.3.5

English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession
