English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on legitimate correction 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:0.5
Pali: 77. Sañciccasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 77. Intentionally making a monk anxious 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū sattarasavaggiyānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ sañcicca kukkuccaṁ upadahanti—
English (brahmali, published): the monks from the group of six intentionally made the monks from the group of seventeen anxious. They said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:1.3
Pali: “bhagavatā, āvuso, sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): “The Buddha has laid down a rule 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:1.4
Pali: ‘na ūnavīsativasso puggalo upasampādetabbo’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): that a person who is less than twenty years old shouldn’t be given the full ordination. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:1.5
Pali: Tumhe ca ūnavīsativassā upasampannā. 
English (brahmali, published): And you were less than twenty when you got the full ordination. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:1.6
Pali: Kacci no tumhe anupasampannā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): Could it be that you’re not fully ordained?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:1.7
Pali: Te rodanti. 
English (brahmali, published): They cried. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:1.8
Pali: Bhikkhū evamāhaṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): Other monks asked them why, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:1.9
Pali: “kissa tumhe, āvuso, rodathā”ti? 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:1.10
Pali: “Ime, āvuso, chabbaggiyā bhikkhū amhākaṁ sañcicca kukkuccaṁ upadahantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): and they said, “The monks from the group of six intentionally make us anxious.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:1.11
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:1.12
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū bhikkhūnaṁ sañcicca kukkuccaṁ upadahissantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the monks from the group of six do this?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:1.13
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, bhikkhūnaṁ sañcicca kukkuccaṁ upadahathā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:1.14
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:1.15
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:1.16
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, bhikkhūnaṁ sañcicca kukkuccaṁ upadahissatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:1.17
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:1.18
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:1.19.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhussa sañcicca kukkuccaṁ upadaheyya—‘itissa muhuttampi aphāsu bhavissatī’ti etadeva paccayaṁ karitvā anaññaṁ, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk intentionally makes a monk anxious, thinking, “In this way he will be ill at ease at least for a moment,” and he does so only for this reason and no other, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:2.1.5
Pali: Bhikkhussāti 
English (brahmali, published): A monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:2.1.6
Pali: aññassa bhikkhussa. 
English (brahmali, published): another monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:2.1.7
Pali: Sañciccāti 
English (brahmali, published): Intentionally: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:2.1.8
Pali: jānanto sañjānanto cecca abhivitaritvā vītikkamo. 
English (brahmali, published): knowing, perceiving, having intended, having decided, he transgresses. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:2.1.9
Pali: Kukkuccaṁ upadaheyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Makes anxious: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:2.1.10
Pali: “ūnavīsativasso maññe tvaṁ upasampanno, vikāle maññe tayā bhuttaṁ, majjaṁ maññe tayā pītaṁ, mātugāmena saddhiṁ raho maññe tayā nisinnan”ti kukkuccaṁ upadahati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes him anxious, saying, “It would seem that you were less than twenty years old when you were given the full ordination,” “It would seem that you have eaten at the wrong time,” “It would seem that you have drunk alcohol,” “It would seem that you have been sitting in private with a woman,” he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:2.1.11
Pali: Etadeva paccayaṁ karitvā, anaññanti 
English (brahmali, published): He does so only for this reason and no other: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:2.1.12
Pali: na añño koci paccayo hoti kukkuccaṁ upadahituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): there is no other reason for making him anxious. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:2.2.1
Pali: Upasampanne upasampannasaññī sañcicca kukkuccaṁ upadahati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is one who is fully ordained, and he perceives him as such, and he intentionally makes him anxious, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:2.2.2
Pali: Upasampanne vematiko sañcicca kukkuccaṁ upadahati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is one who is fully ordained, but he is unsure of it, and he intentionally makes him anxious, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:2.2.3
Pali: Upasampanne anupasampannasaññī sañcicca kukkuccaṁ upadahati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is one who is fully ordained, but he does not perceive him as such, and he intentionally makes him anxious, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:2.2.4
Pali: Anupasampannassa sañcicca kukkuccaṁ upadahati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he intentionally makes one who is not fully ordained anxious, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:2.2.5
Pali: Anupasampanne upasampannasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is one who is not fully ordained, but he perceives them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:2.2.6
Pali: Anupasampanne vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is one who is not fully ordained, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:2.2.7
Pali: Anupasampanne anupasampannasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is one who is not fully ordained, and he does not perceive them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:2.3.2
Pali: na kukkuccaṁ upadahitukāmo “ūnavīsativasso maññe tvaṁ upasampanno, vikāle maññe tayā bhuttaṁ, majjaṁ maññe tayā pītaṁ, mātugāmena saddhiṁ raho maññe tayā nisinnaṁ, iṅgha jānāhi, mā te pacchā kukkuccaṁ ahosī”ti bhaṇati, 
English (brahmali, published): if, not wanting to make him anxious, he says, “It would seem that you were less than twenty years old when you were given the full ordination,” “It would seem that you have eaten at the wrong time,” “It would seem that you have drunk alcohol,” “It would seem that you have been sitting in private with a woman,” and then, “Find out about it, so that you don’t get anxious later;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:2.3.3
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:2.3.4
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc77:2.3.5
Pali: Sañciccasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ sattamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on intentionally making a monk anxious, the seventh, is finished.

Pali: 78. Upassutisikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 78. Eavesdropping 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū pesalehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ bhaṇḍanti. 
English (brahmali, published): the monks from the group of six were arguing with the good monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:1.3

English (brahmali, published): The good monks said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:1.4

English (brahmali, published): “These monks from the group of six are shameless; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:1.5
Pali: Na sakkā imehi saha bhaṇḍitun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): it’s not possible to argue with them.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:1.6
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhū evaṁ vadanti—
English (brahmali, published): And the monks from the group of six said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:1.7
Pali: “kissa tumhe, āvuso, amhe alajjivādena pāpethā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Why are you slandering us by calling us shameless?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:1.8
Pali: “Kahaṁ pana tumhe, āvuso, assutthā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How did you know?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:1.9
Pali: “Mayaṁ āyasmantānaṁ upassutiṁ tiṭṭhamhā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “We were eavesdropping on you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:1.10
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them,

English (brahmali, published): “How could the monks from the group of six eavesdrop on monks they are arguing and disputing with?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:1.12

English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how could you do this?

English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:1.17
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:1.18.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhūnaṁ bhaṇḍanajātānaṁ kalahajātānaṁ vivādāpannānaṁ upassutiṁ tiṭṭheyya—‘yaṁ ime bhaṇissanti taṁ sossāmī’ti etadeva paccayaṁ karitvā anaññaṁ, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk eavesdrops on monks who are arguing and disputing, thinking, “I’ll hear what they say,” and he does so only for this reason and no other, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:2.1.2

English (brahmali, published): whoever …

Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:2.1.5
Pali: Bhikkhūnanti 
English (brahmali, published): On monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:2.1.6
Pali: aññesaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): on other monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:2.1.7
Pali: Bhaṇḍanajātānaṁ kalahajātānaṁ vivādāpannānanti 
English (brahmali, published): Who are arguing and disputing: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:2.1.8
Pali: adhikaraṇajātānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): who are engaged in a legal issue. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:2.1.9
Pali: Upassutiṁ tiṭṭheyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Eavesdrops: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:2.1.10
Pali: “imesaṁ sutvā codessāmi sāressāmi paṭicodessāmi paṭisāressāmi maṅkū karissāmī”ti gacchati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is on his way to eavesdrop, thinking, “After hearing what they say, I’ll accuse them,” “… I’ll remind them,” “… I’ll counter accuse them,” “… I’ll counter remind them,” “… I’ll humiliate them,” he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:2.1.11
Pali: Yattha ṭhito suṇāti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): Wherever he stands to listen, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:2.1.12
Pali: Pacchato gacchanto turito gacchati sossāmīti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is walking behind someone, and he speeds up with the intention to eavesdrop, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:2.1.13
Pali: Yattha ṭhito suṇāti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): Wherever he stands to listen, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:2.1.14
Pali: Purato gacchanto ohiyyati sossāmīti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is walking in front of someone, and he slows down with the intention to eavesdrop, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:2.1.15
Pali: Yattha ṭhito suṇāti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): Wherever he stands to listen, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:2.1.16
Pali: Bhikkhussa ṭhitokāsaṁ vā nisinnokāsaṁ vā nipannokāsaṁ vā āgantvā mantentaṁ ukkāsitabbaṁ, vijānāpetabbaṁ, no ce ukkāseyya vā vijānāpeyya vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he goes to where a monk who is speaking privately is standing, sitting, or lying down, he should clear his throat or make his presence known. If he does not clear his throat or make his presence known, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:2.1.17
Pali: Etadeva paccayaṁ karitvā anaññanti 
English (brahmali, published): He does so only for this reason and no other: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:2.1.18
Pali: na añño koci paccayo hoti upassutiṁ tiṭṭhituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): there is no other reason for eavesdropping. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:2.2.1
Pali: Upasampanne upasampannasaññī upassutiṁ tiṭṭhati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is one who is fully ordained, and he perceives him as such, and he eavesdrops on him, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:2.2.2
Pali: Upasampanne vematiko upassutiṁ tiṭṭhati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is one who is fully ordained, but he is unsure of it, and he eavesdrops on him, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:2.2.3
Pali: Upasampanne anupasampannasaññī upassutiṁ tiṭṭhati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is one who is fully ordained, but he does not perceive him as such, and he eavesdrops on him, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:2.2.4
Pali: Anupasampannassa upassutiṁ tiṭṭhati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he eavesdrops on one who is not fully ordained, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:2.2.5
Pali: Anupasampanne upasampannasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is one who is not fully ordained, but he perceives them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:2.2.6
Pali: Anupasampanne vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is one who is not fully ordained, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:2.2.7
Pali: Anupasampanne anupasampannasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is one who is not fully ordained, and he does not perceive them as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:2.3.2
Pali: “imesaṁ sutvā oramissāmi viramissāmi vūpasamissāmi attānaṁ parimocessāmī”ti gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he goes, thinking, “After hearing what they say, I’ll hold back,” “… I’ll abstain,” “… I’ll resolve it,” “… I’ll free myself;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:2.3.3
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:2.3.4
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc78:2.3.5
Pali: Upassutisikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ aṭṭhamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on eavesdropping, the eighth, is finished.

  - brahmali [published]: translation/en/brahmali/vinaya/pli-tv-bu-vb/pli-tv-bu-vb-pc/pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79_translation-en-brahmali.json

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:0.4
Pali: Sahadhammikavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on legitimate correction 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:0.5
Pali: 79. Kammapaṭibāhanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 79. Criticizing a legitimate legal procedure 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū anācāraṁ ācaritvā ekamekassa kamme kayiramāne paṭikkosanti. 
English (brahmali, published): the monks from the group of six were misbehaving, but when a legal procedure was being done against any one of them, they would object. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:1.3
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena saṅgho sannipatito hoti kenacideva karaṇīyena. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion the Sangha had gathered on some business. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:1.4
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhū cīvarakammaṁ karontā ekassa chandaṁ adaṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks from the group of six were busy making robes and so they gave their consent to one among them. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:1.5
Pali: Atha kho saṅgho—
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:1.6
Pali: “ayaṁ, āvuso, chabbaggiyo bhikkhu ekako āgato, handassa mayaṁ kammaṁ karomā”ti tassa kammaṁ akāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): When the monks saw that only one monk from the group of six had come, they did a legal procedure against him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:1.7
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu yena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū tenupasaṅkami. 
English (brahmali, published): When he returned to the monks from the group of six, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:1.8
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhū taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): they asked him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:1.9
Pali: “kiṁ, āvuso, saṅgho akāsī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What did the Sangha do?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:1.10
Pali: “Saṅgho me, āvuso, kammaṁ akāsī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It did a legal procedure against me.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:1.11
Pali: “Na mayaṁ, āvuso, etadatthāya chandaṁ adamhā—
English (brahmali, published): “We didn’t give our consent for that. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:1.12
Pali: ‘tuyhaṁ kammaṁ karissatī’ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:1.13
Pali: Sace ca mayaṁ jāneyyāma ‘tuyhaṁ kammaṁ karissatī’ti, na mayaṁ chandaṁ dadeyyāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If we had known that a procedure would be done against you, we wouldn’t have given our consent.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:1.14
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:1.15
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū dhammikānaṁ kammānaṁ chandaṁ datvā pacchā khīyanadhammaṁ āpajjissantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the monks from the group of six give their consent to legitimate legal procedures and then criticize them afterwards?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:1.16
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, dhammikānaṁ kammānaṁ chandaṁ datvā pacchā khīyanadhammaṁ āpajjathā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:1.17
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:1.18
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:1.19
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, dhammikānaṁ kammānaṁ chandaṁ datvā pacchā khīyanadhammaṁ āpajjissatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:1.20
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:1.21
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:1.22.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu dhammikānaṁ kammānaṁ chandaṁ datvā pacchā khīyanadhammaṁ āpajjeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk gives his consent to legitimate legal procedures, and then criticizes them afterwards, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:2.1.5
Pali: Dhammikaṁ nāma kammaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): A legitimate legal procedure: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:2.1.6
Pali: apalokanakammaṁ ñattikammaṁ ñattidutiyakammaṁ ñatticatutthakammaṁ dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena kataṁ, etaṁ dhammikaṁ nāma kammaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): a legal procedure consisting of getting permission, a legal procedure consisting of one motion, a legal procedure consisting of one motion and one announcement, a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements; done according to the Teaching, according to the Monastic Law, according to the Teacher’s instruction. This is called a “legitimate legal procedure”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:2.1.7
Pali: Chandaṁ datvā khiyyati āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he gives his consent, and then criticizes the procedure, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:2.2.1
Pali: Dhammakamme dhammakammasaññī chandaṁ datvā khiyyati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and he perceives it as such, and he criticizes it after giving his consent, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:2.2.2
Pali: Dhammakamme vematiko chandaṁ datvā khiyyati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but he is unsure of it, and he criticizes it after giving his consent, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:2.2.3
Pali: Dhammakamme adhammakammasaññī chandaṁ datvā khiyyati, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but he perceives it as illegitimate, and he criticizes it after giving his consent, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:2.2.4
Pali: Adhammakamme dhammakammasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but he perceives it as legitimate, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:2.2.5
Pali: Adhammakamme vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:2.2.6
Pali: Adhammakamme adhammakammasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and he perceives it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:2.3.2
Pali: “adhammena vā vaggena vā na kammārahassa vā kammaṁ katan”ti jānanto khiyyati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he criticizes it because he knows that the legal procedure was illegitimate, done by an incomplete assembly, or done against one who did not deserve it; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:2.3.3
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:2.3.4
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc79:2.3.5
Pali: Kammapaṭibāhanasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ navamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on criticizing a legitimate legal procedure, the ninth, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:0.4
Pali: Sahadhammikavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on legitimate correction 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:0.5
Pali: 80. Chandaṁadatvāgamanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 80. Leaving without giving consent 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena saṅgho sannipatito hoti kenacideva karaṇīyena. 
English (brahmali, published): the Sangha had gathered on some business. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:1.3
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhū cīvarakammaṁ karontā ekassa chandaṁ adaṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks from the group of six were busy making robes and so they gave their consent to one among them. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:1.4
Pali: Atha kho saṅgho “yassatthāya sannipatito taṁ kammaṁ karissāmī”ti ñattiṁ ṭhapesi. 
English (brahmali, published): When the Sangha was ready to do the legal procedure for which it had gathered, it put forward a motion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:1.5
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu—
English (brahmali, published): That monk from the group of six thought, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:1.6
Pali: “evamevime ekamekassa kammaṁ karonti, kassa tumhe kammaṁ karissathā”ti chandaṁ adatvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): “This is just how they do legal procedures against us one by one, but against who will you do this one?” and without giving his consent, he got up from his seat and left. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:1.7
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:1.8
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhu saṅghe vinicchayakathāya vattamānāya chandaṁ adatvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkamissatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “When the Sangha is in the middle of a discussion, how could that monk get up from his seat and leave without giving his consent?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:1.9
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, bhikkhu, saṅghe vinicchayakathāya vattamānāya chandaṁ adatvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkamasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monk, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:1.10
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:1.11
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:1.12
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, saṅghe vinicchayakathāya vattamānāya chandaṁ adatvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkamissasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:1.13
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:1.14
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:1.15.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu saṅghe vinicchayakathāya vattamānāya chandaṁ adatvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkameyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘When the Sangha is in the middle of a discussion, if a monk gets up from his seat and leaves without first giving his consent, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:2.1.5
Pali: Saṅghe vinicchayakathā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): When the Sangha is in the middle of a discussion: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:2.1.6
Pali: vatthu vā ārocitaṁ hoti avinicchitaṁ, ñatti vā ṭhapitā hoti, kammavācā vā vippakatā hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): when the topic has been announced but the discussion has not yet been concluded, or when the motion has been put forward, or when the announcement is still under way. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:2.1.7
Pali: Chandaṁ adatvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkameyyāti—
English (brahmali, published): Gets up from his seat and leaves without first giving his consent: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:2.1.8
Pali: “kathaṁ idaṁ kammaṁ kuppaṁ assa vaggaṁ assa na kareyyā”ti gacchati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he leaves, thinking, “How may this legal procedure be disturbed?” or “How may this legal procedure be done by an incomplete assembly?” or “How may this legal procedure not be done?” then he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:2.1.9
Pali: Parisāya hatthapāsaṁ vijahantassa āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is in the process of going beyond arm’s reach of the gathering, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:2.1.10
Pali: Vijahite āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When he has gone beyond arm’s reach, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:2.2.1
Pali: Dhammakamme dhammakammasaññī chandaṁ adatvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkamati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and he perceives it as such, and he gets up from his seat and leaves without first giving his consent, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:2.2.2
Pali: Dhammakamme vematiko chandaṁ adatvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkamati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but he is unsure of it, and he gets up from his seat and leaves without first giving his consent, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:2.2.3
Pali: Dhammakamme adhammakammasaññī chandaṁ adatvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkamati, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but he perceives it as illegitimate, and he gets up from his seat and leaves without first giving his consent, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:2.2.4
Pali: Adhammakamme dhammakammasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but he perceives it as legitimate, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:2.2.5
Pali: Adhammakamme vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:2.2.6
Pali: Adhammakamme adhammakammasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and he perceives it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:2.3.2
Pali: “saṅghassa bhaṇḍanaṁ vā kalaho vā viggaho vā vivādo vā bhavissatī”ti gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he leaves because he thinks there will be quarrels or disputes in the Sangha; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:2.3.3
Pali: “saṅghabhedo vā saṅgharāji vā bhavissatī”ti gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he leaves because he thinks there will be a fracture or schism in the Sangha; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:2.3.4
Pali: “adhammena vā vaggena vā na kammārahassa vā kammaṁ karissatī”ti gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he leaves because he thinks the legal procedure will be illegitimate, done by an incomplete assembly, or done against one who does not deserve it; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:2.3.5
Pali: gilāno gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he leaves because he is sick; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:2.3.6
Pali: gilānassa karaṇīyena gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he leaves because he has to take care of someone who is sick; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:2.3.7
Pali: uccārena vā passāvena vā pīḷito gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he leaves because he needs to relieve himself; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:2.3.8
Pali: “na kammaṁ kopetukāmo puna paccāgamissāmī”ti gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he leaves with the intention to return, and not because he wants to invalidate the legal procedure; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:2.3.9
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:2.3.10
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc80:2.3.11
Pali: Chandaṁ adatvā gamanasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ dasamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on leaving without giving consent, the tenth, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:1.1

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:1.3
Pali: So cāyasmā dubbalacīvaro hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): had a robe that was worn out. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:1.4
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena saṅghassa ekaṁ cīvaraṁ uppannaṁ hoti.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:1.6
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhū ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks from the group of six complained and criticized it,

English (brahmali, published): “The monks are diverting the Sangha’s material support according to friendship.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:1.8
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them,

English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:1.14
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ, vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:1.15
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:1.16.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu samaggena saṅghena cīvaraṁ datvā pacchā khīyanadhammaṁ āpajjeyya ‘yathāsanthutaṁ bhikkhū saṅghikaṁ lābhaṁ pariṇāmentī’ti, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk gives out a robe as part of a unanimous Sangha and then criticizes it afterwards, saying, “The monks are diverting the Sangha’s material support according to friendship,” he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.1.5
Pali: Samaggo nāma saṅgho 
English (brahmali, published): A unanimous Sangha: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.1.6
Pali: samānasaṁvāsako samānasīmāyaṁ ṭhito. 
English (brahmali, published): belonging to the same Buddhist sect and staying within the same monastery zone. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.1.7
Pali: Cīvaraṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A robe: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.1.8
Pali: channaṁ cīvarānaṁ aññataraṁ cīvaraṁ vikappanupagaṁ pacchimaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one of the six kinds of robe-cloth, but not smaller than what can be assigned to another. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.1.9
Pali: Datvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Gives out: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.1.10
Pali: sayaṁ datvā. 
English (brahmali, published): gives out himself. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.1.11
Pali: Yathāsanthutaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): According to friendship: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.1.12
Pali: yathāmittatā yathāsandiṭṭhatā yathāsambhattatā yathāsamānupajjhāyakatā yathāsamānācariyakatā. 
English (brahmali, published): according to friendship, according to companionship, according to who one is devoted to, according to being a co-student, according to being a co-pupil. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.1.13
Pali: Saṅghikaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha’s: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.1.14
Pali: saṅghassa dinnaṁ hoti pariccattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): given to the Sangha, given up to the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.1.15
Pali: Lābho nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Material support: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.1.16
Pali: cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārā, antamaso cuṇṇapiṇḍopi, dantakaṭṭhampi, dasikasuttampi. 
English (brahmali, published): robes, almsfood, a dwelling, and medicinal supplies; even a bit of bath powder, a tooth cleaner, or a piece of string. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.1.17
Pali: Pacchā khīyanadhammaṁ āpajjeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Criticizes it afterwards: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.1.18
Pali: upasampannassa saṅghena sammatassa senāsanapaññāpakassa vā bhattuddesakassa vā yāgubhājakassa vā phalabhājakassa vā khajjabhājakassa vā appamattakavissajjakassa vā cīvaraṁ dinne khiyyati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when robe-cloth has been given to someone who is fully ordained and who is the assigner of dwellings or the designator of meals or the distributor of congee or the distributor of fruit or the distributor of fresh foods or the distributor of minor requisites, and he has been appointed by the Sangha as such, then if a monk criticizes the giving, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.2.1
Pali: Dhammakamme dhammakammasaññī cīvaraṁ dinne khiyyati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and he perceives it as such, and he criticizes the giving of robe-cloth, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.2.2
Pali: Dhammakamme vematiko cīvaraṁ dinne khiyyati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but he is unsure of it, and he criticizes the giving of robe-cloth, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.2.3
Pali: Dhammakamme adhammakammasaññī cīvaraṁ dinne khiyyati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but he perceives it as illegitimate, and he criticizes the giving of robe-cloth, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.2.4
Pali: Aññaṁ parikkhāraṁ dinne khiyyati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he criticizes the giving of another requisite, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.2.5
Pali: Upasampannassa saṅghena asammatassa senāsanapaññāpakassa vā bhattuddesakassa vā yāgubhājakassa vā phalabhājakassa vā khajjabhājakassa vā appamattakavissajjakassa vā cīvaraṁ vā aññaṁ vā parikkhāraṁ dinne khiyyati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When robe-cloth or another requisite has been given to someone who is fully ordained and who is the assigner of dwellings or the designator of meals or the distributor of congee or the distributor of fruit or the distributor of fresh foods or the distributor of minor requisites, but he has not been appointed by the Sangha as such, then if a monk criticizes the giving, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.2.6
Pali: Anupasampannassa saṅghena sammatassa vā asammatassa vā senāsanapaññāpakassa vā bhattuddesakassa vā yāgubhājakassa vā phalabhājakassa vā khajjabhājakassa vā appamattakavissajjakassa vā cīvaraṁ vā aññaṁ vā parikkhāraṁ dinne khiyyati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When robe-cloth or another requisite has been given to someone who is not fully ordained and who is the assigner of dwellings or the designator of meals or the distributor of congee or the distributor of fruit or the distributor of fresh foods or the distributor of minor requisites, whether he has been appointed by the Sangha as such or not, then if a monk criticizes the giving, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.2.7
Pali: Adhammakamme dhammakammasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but he perceives it as legitimate, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.2.8
Pali: Adhammakamme vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.2.9
Pali: Adhammakamme adhammakammasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and he perceives it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.3.2
Pali: pakatiyā chandā dosā mohā bhayā karontaṁ “kvattho tassa dinnena laddhāpi vinipātessati na sammā upanessatī”ti khiyyati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he criticizes one who regularly acts out of favoritism, ill will, confusion, or fear, saying, “What’s the point of giving it to him—he’ll ruin it or use it inappropriately;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.3.3
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.3.4
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc81:2.3.5
Pali: Dubbalasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ ekādasamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on criticizing a robe distribution, the eleventh, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:0.4
Pali: Sahadhammikavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on legitimate correction 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:0.5
Pali: 82. Pariṇāmanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 82. Diverting gains intended for the Sangha 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sāvatthiyaṁ aññatarassa pūgassa saṅghassa sacīvarabhattaṁ paṭiyattaṁ hoti—
English (brahmali, published): an association had prepared a meal together with robe-cloth for the Sangha. They said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:1.3
Pali: “bhojetvā cīvarena acchādessāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “After giving the food, we’ll offer the robe-cloth.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:1.4
Pali: Atha kho chabbaggiyā bhikkhū yena so pūgo tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; 
English (brahmali, published): But the monks from the group of six went to that association 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:1.5
Pali: upasaṅkamitvā taṁ pūgaṁ etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:1.6
Pali: “dethāvuso, imāni cīvarāni imesaṁ bhikkhūnan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please give the robe-cloth to these monks.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:1.7
Pali: “Na mayaṁ, bhante, dassāma. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, we can’t do that. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:1.8
Pali: Amhākaṁ saṅghassa anuvassaṁ sacīvarabhikkhā paññattā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): We’ve prepared our annual alms-offering together with robe-cloth for the Sangha.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:1.9
Pali: “Bahū, āvuso, saṅghassa dāyakā, bahū saṅghassa bhattā. 
English (brahmali, published): “The Sangha has many donors and supporters. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:1.10
Pali: Ime tumhe nissāya tumhe sampassantā idha viharanti. 
English (brahmali, published): But since these monks are staying here, they are looking to you for support. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:1.11
Pali: Tumhe ce imesaṁ na dassatha, atha ko carahi imesaṁ dassati? 
English (brahmali, published): If you don’t give to them, who will? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:1.12
Pali: Dethāvuso, imāni cīvarāni imesaṁ bhikkhūnan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): So give them the robe-cloth.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:1.13
Pali: Atha kho so pūgo chabbaggiyehi bhikkhūhi nippīḷiyamāno yathāpaṭiyattaṁ cīvaraṁ chabbaggiyānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ datvā saṅghaṁ bhattena parivisi. 
English (brahmali, published): Being pressured by the monks from the group of six, that association gave the prepared robe-cloth to those monks and served the food to the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:1.14
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū jānanti saṅghassa sacīvarabhattaṁ paṭiyattaṁ “na ca jānanti chabbaggiyānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ dinnan”ti te evamāhaṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): The monks who knew that a meal together with robe-cloth had been prepared for the Sangha, but not that the robe-cloth had been given to those monks from the group of six, said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:1.15
Pali: “oṇojethāvuso, saṅghassa cīvaran”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “You may dedicate the robe-cloth.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:1.16
Pali: “Natthi, bhante. 
English (brahmali, published): “There isn’t any. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:1.17
Pali: Yathāpaṭiyattaṁ cīvaraṁ ayyā chabbaggiyā ayyānaṁ chabbaggiyānaṁ pariṇāmesun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The robe-cloth we had prepared was diverted by the monks from the group of six.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:1.18
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:1.19
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū jānaṁ saṅghikaṁ lābhaṁ pariṇataṁ puggalassa pariṇāmessantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the monks from the group of six divert to an individual things they knew were intended for the Sangha?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:1.20
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, jānaṁ saṅghikaṁ lābhaṁ pariṇataṁ puggalassa pariṇāmethā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:1.21
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:1.22
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:1.23
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, jānaṁ saṅghikaṁ lābhaṁ pariṇataṁ puggalassa pariṇāmessatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:1.24
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:1.25
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:1.26.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu jānaṁ saṅghikaṁ lābhaṁ pariṇataṁ puggalassa pariṇāmeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk diverts to an individual material support that he knows was intended for the Sangha, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.1.5
Pali: Jānāti nāma 
English (brahmali, published): He knows: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.1.6
Pali: sāmaṁ vā jānāti, aññe vā tassa ārocenti, so vā āroceti. 
English (brahmali, published): he knows by himself or others have told him or the donor has told him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.1.7
Pali: Saṅghikaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): For the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.1.8
Pali: saṅghassa dinnaṁ hoti pariccattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): given to the Sangha, given up to the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.1.9
Pali: Lābho nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Material support: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.1.10
Pali: cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārā, antamaso cuṇṇapiṇḍopi, dantakaṭṭhampi, dasikasuttampi. 
English (brahmali, published): robe-cloth, almsfood, a dwelling, and medicinal supplies; even a bit of bath powder, a tooth cleaner, or a piece of string. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.1.11
Pali: Pariṇataṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Intended: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.1.12
Pali: “dassāma karissāmā”ti vācā bhinnā hoti, taṁ puggalassa pariṇāmeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if they have said, “We’ll give,” “We’ll prepare,” and he diverts it to an individual, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.2.1
Pali: Pariṇate pariṇatasaññī puggalassa pariṇāmeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it was intended for the Sangha and he perceives it as such, and he diverts it to an individual, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.2.2
Pali: Pariṇate vematiko puggalassa pariṇāmeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it was intended for the Sangha, but he is unsure of it, and he diverts it to an individual, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.2.3
Pali: Pariṇate apariṇatasaññī puggalassa pariṇāmeti, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it was intended for the Sangha, but he does not perceive it as such, and he diverts it to an individual, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.2.4
Pali: Saṅghassa pariṇataṁ aññasaṅghassa vā cetiyassa vā pariṇāmeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it was intended for one Sangha and he diverts it to another Sangha or to a shrine, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.2.5
Pali: Cetiyassa pariṇataṁ aññacetiyassa vā saṅghassa vā puggalassa vā pariṇāmeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it was intended for one shrine and he diverts it to another shrine or to a sangha or to an individual, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.2.6
Pali: Puggalassa pariṇataṁ aññapuggalassa vā saṅghassa vā cetiyassa vā pariṇāmeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it was intended for an individual and he diverts it to another individual or to a sangha or to a shrine, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.2.7
Pali: Apariṇate pariṇatasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it was not intended for the Sangha, but he perceives it as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.2.8
Pali: Apariṇate vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it was not intended for the Sangha, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.2.9
Pali: Apariṇate apariṇatasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it was not intended for the Sangha and he does not perceive it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.3.2
Pali: “kattha demā”ti pucchīyamāno—
English (brahmali, published): if being asked, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.3.3
Pali: “yattha tumhākaṁ deyyadhammo paribhogaṁ vā labheyya paṭisaṅkhāraṁ vā labheyya ciraṭṭhitiko vā assa yattha vā pana tumhākaṁ cittaṁ pasīdati tattha dethā”ti bhaṇati, 
English (brahmali, published): “Where may we give?” he says, “Give where your gift will be useful,” “… where it goes toward repairs,” “… where it will last for a long time,” “… where you feel inspired;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.3.4
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.3.5
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.3.6
Pali: Pariṇāmanasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ dvādasamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on diverting gains intended for the Sangha, the twelfth, is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.3.7
Pali: Sahadhammikavaggo aṭṭhamo. 
English (brahmali, published): The eighth subchapter on legitimate correction is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.3.8
Pali: Tassuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.3.9
Pali: Sahadhamma vivaṇṇañca, 
English (brahmali, published): “Legitimately, and disparaging, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.3.10
Pali: mohāpanaṁ pahārakaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): Deception, hitting; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.3.11
Pali: Talasatti amūlañca, 
English (brahmali, published): Raising a hand, and groundless, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.3.12
Pali: sañcicca ca upassuti; 
English (brahmali, published): And intentionally, eavesdropping; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.3.13
Pali: Paṭibāhana chandañca, 
English (brahmali, published): Obstructing, and consent, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc82:2.3.14
Pali: dabbañca pariṇāmananti. 
English (brahmali, published): And Dabba, diverting.”

Pali: 83. Antepurasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 83. Entering a royal compound unannounced 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena rājā pasenadi kosalo uyyānapālaṁ āṇāpesi—“gaccha, bhaṇe, uyyānaṁ sodhehi. Uyyānaṁ gamissāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): King Pasenadi of Kosala told his park-keeper, “Go and clean up the park; I’ll be going there.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.1.3
Pali: “Evaṁ, devā”ti kho so uyyānapālo rañño pasenadissa kosalassa paṭissutvā uyyānaṁ sodhento addasa bhagavantaṁ aññatarasmiṁ rukkhamūle nisinnaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes, sir.” While cleaning the park, he saw the Buddha seated at the foot of a tree. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.1.4
Pali: Disvāna yena rājā pasenadi kosalo tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā rājānaṁ pasenadiṁ kosalaṁ etadavoca—“suddhaṁ, deva, uyyānaṁ. Api ca bhagavā tattha nisinno”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): He then went to King Pasenadi and said, “The park is clean, sir, but the Buddha is sitting there.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.1.5
Pali: “Hotu, bhaṇe. Mayaṁ bhagavantaṁ payirupāsissāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Marvelous! I’ll visit him.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.1.6
Pali: Atha kho rājā pasenadi kosalo uyyānaṁ gantvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami. Tena kho pana samayena aññataro upāsako bhagavantaṁ payirupāsanto nisinno hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): The king went to the park and approached the Buddha, but just then a lay follower was seated there. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.1.7
Pali: Addasā kho rājā pasenadi kosalo taṁ upāsakaṁ bhagavantaṁ payirupāsantaṁ nisinnaṁ. Disvāna bhīto aṭṭhāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): When the king saw him, he became fearful and stopped. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.1.8
Pali: Atha kho rañño pasenadissa kosalassa etadahosi—“nārahatāyaṁ puriso pāpo hotuṁ, yathā bhagavantaṁ payirupāsatī”ti. Yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): But he considered, “This man isn’t likely to be bad, since he’s visiting the Buddha,” and so he approached the Buddha, bowed, and sat down. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.1.9
Pali: Atha kho so upāsako bhagavato gāravena rājānaṁ pasenadiṁ kosalaṁ neva abhivādesi na paccuṭṭhāsi. Atha kho rājā pasenadi kosalo anattamano ahosi—“kathañhi nāmāyaṁ puriso mayi āgate neva abhivādessati na paccuṭṭhessatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): But when that lay follower, because of his respect for the Buddha, neither bowed down to the king nor stood up for him, the king became annoyed. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.1.10
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā rājānaṁ pasenadiṁ kosalaṁ anattamanaṁ viditvā rājānaṁ pasenadiṁ kosalaṁ etadavoca—“eso kho, mahārāja, upāsako bahussuto āgatāgamo kāmesu vītarāgo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha realized what was happening and said to the king, “Great king, this lay follower is learned, a master of the tradition, and he’s free from sensual desire.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.1.11
Pali: Atha kho rañño pasenadissa kosalassa etadahosi—“nārahatāyaṁ upāsako orako hotuṁ, bhagavāpi imassa vaṇṇaṁ bhāsatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The king thought, “This lay follower doesn’t deserve to be in an inferior position, since even the Buddha praises him.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.1.12
Pali: Taṁ upāsakaṁ etadavoca—“vadeyyāsi, upāsaka, yena attho”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): And he said to that lay follower, “Please say what you want.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.1.13
Pali: “Suṭṭhu, devā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Thank you, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.1.14
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā rājānaṁ pasenadiṁ kosalaṁ dhammiyā kathāya sandassesi samādapesi samuttejesi sampahaṁsesi. Atha kho rājā pasenadi kosalo bhagavatā dhammiyā kathāya sandassito samādapito samuttejito sampahaṁsito uṭṭhāyāsanā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha then instructed, inspired, and gladdened King Pasenadi with a teaching, after which the king got up from his seat, bowed down, circumambulated the Buddha with his right side toward him, and left. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.1.15
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena rājā pasenadi kosalo uparipāsādavaragato hoti. Addasā kho rājā pasenadi kosalo taṁ upāsakaṁ rathikāya chattapāṇiṁ gacchantaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards King Pasenadi was up in his finest stilt house, when he saw that lay follower walking along the street, holding a sunshade. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.1.16
Pali: Disvāna pakkosāpetvā etadavoca—“tvaṁ kira, upāsaka, bahussuto āgatāgamo. 
English (brahmali, published): He summoned him and said, “You are a learned Buddhist, a master of the tradition. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.1.17
Pali: Sādhu, upāsaka, amhākaṁ itthāgāraṁ dhammaṁ vācehī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Please teach my harem.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.1.18
Pali: “Yamahaṁ, deva, jānāmi ayyānaṁ vāhasā, ayyāva devassa itthāgāraṁ dhammaṁ vācessantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Whatever I know, sir, I know because of the monks. They should teach the harem.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.2.1
Pali: Atha kho rājā pasenadi kosalo—“saccaṁ kho upāsako āhā”ti yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho rājā pasenadi kosalo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Knowing that the lay follower was right, the king went to the Buddha, bowed, sat down, and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.2.2
Pali: “sādhu, bhante, bhagavā ekaṁ bhikkhuṁ āṇāpetu yo amhākaṁ itthāgāraṁ dhammaṁ vācessatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, please ask a monk to teach my harem.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.2.3
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā rājānaṁ pasenadiṁ kosalaṁ dhammiyā kathāya sandassesi …pe… padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha then instructed, inspired, and gladdened King Pasenadi with a teaching, after which the king got up from his seat, paid his respects as before, and left. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.2.4
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ āmantesi—“tenahānanda, rañño itthāgāraṁ dhammaṁ vācehī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards, the Buddha said to Venerable Ānanda, “Well then, Ānanda, teach the king’s harem.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.2.5
Pali: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho āyasmā ānando bhagavato paṭissutvā kālena kālaṁ pavisitvā rañño itthāgāraṁ dhammaṁ vāceti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes, sir.” And from time to time he would enter the harem and teach. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.2.6
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā ānando pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena rañño pasenadissa kosalassa nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkami. 
English (brahmali, published): Then, after robing up in the morning, Ānanda took his bowl and robe and went to King Pasenadi’s house. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.2.7
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena rājā pasenadi kosalo mallikāya deviyā saddhiṁ sayanagato hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): On that occasion the king was in bed with Queen Mallikā. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.2.8
Pali: Addasā kho mallikā devī āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ dūratova āgacchantaṁ. Disvāna sahasā vuṭṭhāsi; pītakamaṭṭhaṁ dussaṁ pabhassittha. 
English (brahmali, published): The queen saw Ānanda coming and she quickly got up, but her burnished golden dressing gown fell off. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.2.9
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā ānando tatova paṭinivattitvā ārāmaṁ gantvā bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): Ānanda turned around right there and returned to the monastery. And he told the monks what had happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.2.10
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.2.11
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā ānando pubbe appaṭisaṁvidito rañño antepuraṁ pavisissatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Ānanda enter the royal compound without first being announced?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.2.12
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, ānanda, pubbe appaṭisaṁvidito rañño antepuraṁ pavisasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Ānanda, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.2.13
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.2.14
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.2.15
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, ānanda, pubbe appaṭisaṁvidito rañño antepuraṁ pavisissasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Ānanda, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.2.16
Pali: Netaṁ, ānanda, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.2.17
Pali: vigarahitvā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): Having rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.2.18
Pali: dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.1
Pali: “Dasayime, bhikkhave, ādīnavā rājantepurappavesane. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, there are these ten dangers of entering a royal compound. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.2
Pali: Katame dasa? 
English (brahmali, published): What ten? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.3
Pali: Idha, bhikkhave, rājā mahesiyā saddhiṁ nisinno hoti, tattha bhikkhu pavisati. 
English (brahmali, published): It may be that a monk enters where the king is sitting with his queen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.4
Pali: Mahesī vā bhikkhuṁ disvā sitaṁ pātukaroti. 
English (brahmali, published): The queen smiles when she sees the monk 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.5
Pali: Bhikkhu vā mahesiṁ disvā sitaṁ pātukaroti. 
English (brahmali, published): or the monk smiles when he sees the queen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.6
Pali: Tattha rañño evaṁ hoti—
English (brahmali, published): The king thinks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.7
Pali: ‘addhā imesaṁ kataṁ vā karissanti vā’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Surely they’ve done it, or they’re going to.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.8
Pali: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, paṭhamo ādīnavo rājantepurappavesane. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.9
Pali: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, rājā bahukicco bahukaraṇīyo. 
English (brahmali, published): Again, because the king is very busy, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.10
Pali: Aññataraṁ itthiṁ gantvā nassarati. 
English (brahmali, published): he does not remember having slept with a certain woman, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.11
Pali: Sā tena gabbhaṁ gaṇhi. 
English (brahmali, published): yet she becomes pregnant because of that. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.12
Pali: Tattha rañño evaṁ hoti—
English (brahmali, published): The king thinks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.13
Pali: ‘na kho idha añño koci pavisati aññatra pabbajitena. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Only the monk enters here. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.14
Pali: Siyā nu kho pabbajitassa kamman’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Is he responsible for this?’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.15
Pali: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, dutiyo ādīnavo rājantepurappavesane. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.16
Pali: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, rañño antepure aññataraṁ ratanaṁ nassati. 
English (brahmali, published): Again, a gem disappears from the royal compound. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.17
Pali: Tattha rañño evaṁ hoti—
English (brahmali, published): The king thinks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.18
Pali: ‘na kho idha añño koci pavisati aññatra pabbajitena. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Only the monk enters here. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.19
Pali: Siyā nu kho pabbajitassa kamman’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Is he responsible for this?’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.20
Pali: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, tatiyo ādīnavo rājantepurappavesane. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.21
Pali: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, rañño antepure abbhantarā guyhamantā bahiddhā sambhedaṁ gacchanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Again, secret discussions in the royal compound are spread outside. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.22
Pali: Tattha rañño evaṁ hoti—
English (brahmali, published): The king thinks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.23
Pali: ‘na kho idha añño koci pavisati aññatra pabbajitena. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Only the monk enters here. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.24
Pali: Siyā nu kho pabbajitassa kamman’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Is he responsible for this?’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.25
Pali: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, catuttho ādīnavo rājantepurappavesane. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.26
Pali: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, rañño antepure putto vā pitaraṁ pattheti pitā vā puttaṁ pattheti. 
English (brahmali, published): Again, in the royal compound a father longs for his son, or a son longs for his father. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.27
Pali: Tesaṁ evaṁ hoti—
English (brahmali, published): They think, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.28
Pali: ‘na kho idha añño koci pavisati aññatra pabbajitena. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Only the monk enters here. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.29
Pali: Siyā nu kho pabbajitassa kamman’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Is he responsible for this?’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.30
Pali: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, pañcamo ādīnavo rājantepurappavesane. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.31
Pali: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, rājā nīcaṭṭhāniyaṁ ucce ṭhāne ṭhapeti. 
English (brahmali, published): Again, the king promotes someone. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.32
Pali: Yesaṁ taṁ amanāpaṁ tesaṁ evaṁ hoti—
English (brahmali, published): Those who dislike this think, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.33
Pali: ‘rājā kho pabbajitena saṁsaṭṭho. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘The king is close to the monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.34
Pali: Siyā nu kho pabbajitassa kamman’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Is he responsible for this?’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.35
Pali: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, chaṭṭho ādīnavo, rājantepurappavesane. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.36
Pali: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, rājā uccaṭṭhāniyaṁ nīce ṭhāne ṭhapeti. 
English (brahmali, published): Again, the king demotes someone. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.37
Pali: Yesaṁ taṁ amanāpaṁ tesaṁ evaṁ hoti—
English (brahmali, published): Those who dislike this think, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.38
Pali: ‘rājā kho pabbajitena saṁsaṭṭho. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘The king is close to the monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.39
Pali: Siyā nu kho pabbajitassa kamman’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Is he responsible for this?’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.40
Pali: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, sattamo ādīnavo rājantepurappavesane. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.41
Pali: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, rājā akāle senaṁ uyyojeti. 
English (brahmali, published): Again, the king sends out the army at an inappropriate time. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.42
Pali: Yesaṁ taṁ amanāpaṁ tesaṁ evaṁ hoti—
English (brahmali, published): Those who dislike this think, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.43
Pali: ‘rājā kho pabbajitena saṁsaṭṭho. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘The king is close to the monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.44
Pali: Siyā nu kho pabbajitassa kamman’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Is he responsible for this?’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.45
Pali: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, aṭṭhamo ādīnavo rājantepurappavesane. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.46
Pali: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, rājā kāle senaṁ uyyojetvā antarāmaggato nivattāpeti. 
English (brahmali, published): Again, after sending out the army at an appropriate time, the king orders it to turn back while still en route. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.47
Pali: Yesaṁ taṁ amanāpaṁ tesaṁ evaṁ hoti—
English (brahmali, published): Those who dislike this think, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.48
Pali: ‘rājā kho pabbajitena saṁsaṭṭho. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘The king is close to the monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.49
Pali: Siyā nu kho pabbajitassa kamman’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Is he responsible for this?’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.50
Pali: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, navamo ādīnavo rājantepurappavesane. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.51
Pali: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, rañño rājantepuraṁ hatthisammaddaṁ assasammaddaṁ rathasammaddaṁ rajanīyāni rūpasaddagandharasaphoṭṭhabbāni, yāni na pabbajitassa sāruppāni. 
English (brahmali, published): Again, a royal compound is crowded with elephants, horses, and chariots, as well as enticing sights, sounds, smells, tastes, and tangibles that are not suitable for a monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.52
Pali: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, dasamo ādīnavo rājantepurappavesane. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.53
Pali: Ime kho, bhikkhave, dasa ādīnavā rājantepurappavesane”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Monks, these are the ten dangers of entering a royal compound.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.54
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā dubbharatāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): Then, after rebuking Ānanda in many ways, the Buddha spoke in dispraise of being difficult to support … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.55
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:1.3.56.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu rañño khattiyassa muddhāvasittassa anikkhantarājake aniggataratanake pubbe appaṭisaṁvidito indakhīlaṁ atikkāmeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk, without first being announced, crosses the threshold to the bedroom of a consecrated aristocrat king, when both the king and the queen are present, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.1.5
Pali: Khattiyo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Aristocrat: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.1.6
Pali: ubhato sujāto hoti, mātito ca pitito ca saṁsuddhagahaṇiko, yāva sattamā pitāmahayugā akkhitto anupakuṭṭho jātivādena. 
English (brahmali, published): well-born on both his mother’s side and his father’s side, pure in descent, irreproachable and impeccable with respect to birth going back eight generations of male ancestors. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.1.7
Pali: Muddhāvasitto nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Consecrated: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.1.8
Pali: khattiyābhisekena abhisitto hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): consecrated with the aristocratic consecration. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.1.9
Pali: Anikkhantarājaketi 
English (brahmali, published): The king is present: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.1.10
Pali: rājā sayanigharā anikkhanto hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): the king has not left the bedroom. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.1.11
Pali: Aniggataratanaketi 
English (brahmali, published): The queen is present: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.1.12
Pali: mahesī sayanigharā anikkhantā hoti, ubho vā anikkhantā honti. 
English (brahmali, published): the queen has not left the bedroom. Or both have not left. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.1.13
Pali: Pubbe appaṭisaṁviditoti 
English (brahmali, published): Without first being announced: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.1.14
Pali: pubbe anāmantetvā. 
English (brahmali, published): without first having informed. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.1.15
Pali: Indakhīlo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The threshold: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.1.16
Pali: sayanigharassa ummāro vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): the threshold to the bedroom is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.1.17
Pali: Sayanigharaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The bedroom: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.1.18
Pali: yattha katthaci rañño sayanaṁ paññattaṁ hoti, antamaso sāṇipākāraparikkhittampi. 
English (brahmali, published): wherever a king’s bed is prepared, even if just enclosed by a cloth screen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.1.19
Pali: Indakhīlaṁ atikkāmeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Crosses the threshold to the bedroom: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.1.20
Pali: paṭhamaṁ pādaṁ ummāraṁ atikkāmeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he crosses the threshold with the first foot, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.1.21
Pali: Dutiyaṁ pādaṁ atikkāmeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he crosses with the second foot, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.2.1
Pali: Appaṭisaṁvidite appaṭisaṁviditasaññī indakhīlaṁ atikkāmeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has not been announced, and he perceives that he has not, and he crosses the threshold to the bedroom, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.2.2
Pali: Appaṭisaṁvidite vematiko indakhīlaṁ atikkāmeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has not been announced, but he is unsure of it, and he crosses the threshold to the bedroom, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.2.3
Pali: Appaṭisaṁvidite paṭisaṁviditasaññī indakhīlaṁ atikkāmeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has not been announced, but he perceives that he has, and he crosses the threshold to the bedroom, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.2.4
Pali: Paṭisaṁvidite appaṭisaṁviditasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has been announced, but he perceives that he has not, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.2.5
Pali: Paṭisaṁvidite vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has been announced, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.2.6
Pali: Paṭisaṁvidite paṭisaṁviditasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has been announced, and he perceives that he has, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.3.2
Pali: paṭisaṁvidite, 
English (brahmali, published): if he has been announced; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.3.3
Pali: na khattiyo hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is not an aristocrat king; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.3.4
Pali: na khattiyābhisekena abhisitto hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if the king has not been consecrated with the aristocratic consecration; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.3.5
Pali: rājā sayanigharā nikkhanto hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if the king has left the bedroom; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.3.6
Pali: mahesī sayanigharā nikkhantā hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if the queen has left the bedroom; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.3.7
Pali: ubho vā nikkhantā honti, 
English (brahmali, published): if they both have left; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.3.8
Pali: na sayanighare, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is not a bedroom; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.3.9
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.3.10
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc83:2.3.11
Pali: Antepurasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ paṭhamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on entering a royal compound unannounced, the first, is finished.

English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on precious things
