Pali: 84. Ratanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 84. Picking up precious things 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu aciravatiyā nadiyā nahāyati. Aññataropi brāhmaṇo pañcasatānaṁ thavikaṁ thale nikkhipitvā aciravatiyā nadiyā nahāyanto vissaritvā agamāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): a monk was bathing in the river Aciravatī, when a brahmin, too, came there to bathe. He deposited a bag with five hundred coins on the ground, bathed, forgot about the bag, and left. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:1.3
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu—“tassāyaṁ brāhmaṇassa thavikā, mā idha nassī”ti aggahesi. 
English (brahmali, published): The monk thought, “This is the bag belonging to that brahmin; it wouldn’t be nice if it got lost,” and he picked it up. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:1.4
Pali: Atha kho so brāhmaṇo saritvā turito ādhāvitvā taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca—“api me, bho, thavikaṁ passeyyāsī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): Soon the brahmin remembered. He hurried back and asked that monk, “Sir, did you see my bag?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:1.5
Pali: “Handa, brāhmaṇā”ti adāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): Saying, “I did indeed,” he gave it to him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:1.6
Pali: Atha kho tassa brāhmaṇassa etadahosi—“kena nu kho ahaṁ upāyena imassa bhikkhuno puṇṇapattaṁ na dadeyyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The brahmin thought, “How can I avoid giving a reward to this monk?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:1.7
Pali: “Na me, bho, pañcasatāni, sahassaṁ me”ti palibundhetvā muñci. 
English (brahmali, published): And he said, “I didn’t have five hundred coins, I had a thousand!” And he seized hold of that monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:1.8
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu ārāmaṁ gantvā bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): After being released, that monk went to the monastery and told the monks what had happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:1.9
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:1.10
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhu ratanaṁ uggahessatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could a monk pick up precious things?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:1.11
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, bhikkhu, ratanaṁ uggahesī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monk, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:1.12
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:1.13
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:1.14
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, ratanaṁ uggahessasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:1.15
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:1.16
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:1.17.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu ratanaṁ vā ratanasammataṁ vā uggaṇheyya vā uggaṇhāpeyya vā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk picks up something precious or something regarded as precious, or he has it picked up, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:1.18
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sāvatthiyā ussavo hoti. Manussā alaṅkatappaṭiyattā uyyānaṁ gacchanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards there was a celebration in Sāvatthī. People were going to the park all dressed up, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:2.2
Pali: Visākhāpi migāramātā alaṅkatappaṭiyattā “uyyānaṁ gamissāmī”ti 
English (brahmali, published): as did Visākhā Migāramātā. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:2.3
Pali: gāmato nikkhamitvā—“kyāhaṁ karissāmi uyyānaṁ gantvā, yannūnāhaṁ bhagavantaṁ payirupāseyyan”ti 
English (brahmali, published): As she was leaving her village, she thought, “What will I do when I get to the park? Why don’t I pay a visit to the Buddha!” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:2.4
Pali: ābharaṇaṁ omuñcitvā uttarāsaṅgena bhaṇḍikaṁ bandhitvā dāsiyā adāsi—“handa, je, imaṁ bhaṇḍikaṁ gaṇhāhī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): She then removed all her ornaments, bound them in a bundle with her upper robe, and gave it to her slave girl, saying, “Listen, look after this bundle.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:2.5
Pali: Atha kho visākhā migāramātā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): Visākhā then went to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:2.6
Pali: Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho visākhaṁ migāramātaraṁ bhagavā dhammiyā kathāya sandassesi samādapesi samuttejesi sampahaṁsesi. Atha kho visākhā migāramātā bhagavatā dhammiyā kathāya sandassitā samādapitā samuttejitā sampahaṁsitā uṭṭhāyāsanā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): And the Buddha instructed, inspired, and gladdened her with a teaching, after which she got up from her seat, bowed down, circumambulated him with her right side toward him, and left. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:2.7
Pali: Atha kho sā dāsī taṁ bhaṇḍikaṁ vissaritvā agamāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): And the slave girl left too, forgetting the bundle. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:2.8
Pali: Bhikkhū passitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. “Tena hi, bhikkhave, uggahetvā nikkhipathā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks saw it and told the Buddha. “Well then, monks, pick it up and put it aside.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:2.9
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:2.10
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, ratanaṁ vā ratanasammataṁ vā ajjhārāme uggahetvā vā uggahāpetvā vā nikkhipituṁ—‘yassa bhavissati so harissatī’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Within a monastery, monks, you should pick up precious things or what’s regarded as precious, or have it picked up, and then put it aside with the thought, ‘Whoever owns it will come and get it.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:2.11
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:2.12.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu ratanaṁ vā ratanasammataṁ vā, aññatra ajjhārāmā, uggaṇheyya vā uggaṇhāpeyya vā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk picks up something precious or something regarded as precious, or he has it picked up, except within a monastery, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:2.13
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:3.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena kāsīsu janapade anāthapiṇḍikassa gahapatissa kammantagāmo hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the householder Anāthapiṇḍika had a whole village working for him in the country of Kāsi, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:3.2
Pali: Tena ca gahapatinā antevāsī āṇatto hoti—“sace bhadantā āgacchanti, bhattaṁ kareyyāsī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): and he had told an apprentice there that if the monks arrive he should make them a meal. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:3.3
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā bhikkhū kāsīsu janapade cārikaṁ caramānā yena anāthapiṇḍikassa gahapatissa kammantagāmo tenupasaṅkamiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards a number of monks were wandering in the country of Kāsi, when they came to that village. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:3.4
Pali: Addasā kho so puriso te bhikkhū dūratova āgacchante. Disvāna yena te bhikkhū tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā te bhikkhū abhivādetvā etadavoca—“adhivāsentu, bhante, ayyā svātanāya gahapatino bhattan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): When that man saw them coming, he approached them, bowed, and said, “Venerables, please accept a meal from Anāthapiṇḍika tomorrow.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:3.5
Pali: Adhivāsesuṁ kho te bhikkhū tuṇhībhāvena. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks accepted by remaining silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:3.6
Pali: Atha kho so puriso tassā rattiyā accayena paṇītaṁ khādanīyaṁ bhojanīyaṁ paṭiyādāpetvā kālaṁ ārocāpetvā aṅgulimuddikaṁ omuñcitvā te bhikkhū bhattena parivisitvā—
English (brahmali, published): The following morning, after having various kinds of fine foods prepared, he had the monks informed that it was time for the meal. He removed a ring from his finger and then offered the food to the monks, saying, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:3.7
Pali: “ayyā bhuñjitvā gacchantu, ahampi kammantaṁ gamissāmī”ti aṅgulimuddikaṁ vissaritvā agamāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, please leave after you’ve eaten. I have to go to work.” And he left, forgetting his ring. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:3.8
Pali: Bhikkhū passitvā—“sace mayaṁ gamissāma nassissatāyaṁ aṅgulimuddikā”ti tattheva acchiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks saw it and said, “If we just go, this ring will be lost,” and so they stayed right there. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:3.9
Pali: Atha kho so puriso kammantā āgacchanto te bhikkhū passitvā etadavoca—“kissa, bhante, ayyā idheva acchantī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): When that man returned from work, he saw the monks and said to them, “Why are you still here?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:3.10
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū tassa purisassa etamatthaṁ ārocetvā 
English (brahmali, published): And the monks told him what had happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:3.11
Pali: sāvatthiṁ gantvā bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Those monks then went to Sāvatthī where they told the monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:3.12
Pali: Bhikkhū bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): who in turn told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:3.13
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): After giving a teaching, the Buddha addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:3.14
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, ratanaṁ vā ratanasammataṁ vā ajjhārāme vā ajjhāvasathe vā uggahetvā vā uggahāpetvā vā nikkhipituṁ—
English (brahmali, published): “Within a monastery, monks, or inside a lodging, you should pick up precious things or what’s regarded as precious, or have it picked up, and then put it aside with the thought, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:3.15
Pali: ‘yassa bhavissati so harissatī’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Whoever owns it will come and get it.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:3.16
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:3.17.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu ratanaṁ vā ratanasammataṁ vā, aññatra ajjhārāmā vā ajjhāvasathā vā, uggaṇheyya vā uggaṇhāpeyya vā, pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk picks up something precious or something regarded as precious, or he has it picked up, except within a monastery or inside a lodging, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:3.18
Pali: Ratanaṁ vā pana bhikkhunā ratanasammataṁ vā ajjhārāme vā ajjhāvasathe vā uggahetvā vā uggahāpetvā vā nikkhipitabbaṁ—‘yassa bhavissati so harissatī’ti. Ayaṁ tattha sāmīcī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he picks up something precious or something regarded as precious, or he has it picked up, within a monastery or inside a lodging, he should put it aside with the thought, “Whoever owns it will come and get it.” This is the proper procedure.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.1.5
Pali: Ratanaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Something precious: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.1.6
Pali: muttā maṇi veḷuriyo saṅkho silā pavālaṁ rajataṁ jātarūpaṁ lohitaṅko masāragallaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): a pearl, a gem, a beryl, mother-of-pearl, quartz, a coral, silver, gold, a ruby, a cat’s eye. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.1.7
Pali: Ratanasammataṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Something regarded as precious: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.1.8
Pali: yaṁ manussānaṁ upabhogaparibhogaṁ, etaṁ ratanasammataṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): whatever people regard as valuable or useful—this is called “regarded as precious”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.1.9
Pali: Aññatra ajjhārāmā vā ajjhāvasathā vāti 
English (brahmali, published): Except within a monastery or inside a lodging: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.1.10
Pali: ṭhapetvā ajjhārāmaṁ ajjhāvasathaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): apart from within a monastery or inside a lodging. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.1.11
Pali: Ajjhārāmo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Within a monastery: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.1.12
Pali: parikkhittassa ārāmassa anto ārāmo, aparikkhittassa upacāro. 
English (brahmali, published): if the monastery is enclosed, then within the enclosure. If the monastery is unenclosed, then in the vicinity. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.1.13
Pali: Ajjhāvasatho nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Inside a lodging: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.1.14
Pali: parikkhittassa āvasathassa anto āvasatho, aparikkhittassa upacāro. 
English (brahmali, published): if the lodging is enclosed, then within the enclosure. If the lodging is unenclosed, then in the vicinity. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.1.15
Pali: Uggaṇheyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Picks up: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.1.16
Pali: sayaṁ gaṇhāti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he takes hold of it himself, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.1.17
Pali: Uggaṇhāpeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Has picked up: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.1.18
Pali: aññaṁ gāhāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he gets someone else to take hold of it, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.1.19
Pali: Ratanaṁ vā pana bhikkhunā ratanasammataṁ vā ajjhārāme vā ajjhāvasathe vā uggahetvā vā uggahāpetvā vā nikkhipitabbanti 
English (brahmali, published): If he picks up something precious or something regarded as precious, or he has it picked up, within a monastery or inside a lodging, he should put it aside: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.1.20
Pali: rūpena vā nimittena vā saññāṇaṁ katvā nikkhipitvā ācikkhitabbaṁ—“yassa bhaṇḍaṁ naṭṭhaṁ so āgacchatū”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): after taking note of its appearance or distinguishing marks, he should put it aside, and then make an announcement: “Whoever has lost anything should come.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.1.21
Pali: Sace tattha āgacchati so vattabbo—“āvuso, kīdisaṁ te bhaṇḍan”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): If anyone comes, they should be told, “Please describe it.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.1.22
Pali: Sace rūpena vā nimittena vā sampādeti dātabbaṁ, no ce sampādeti “vicināhi, āvuso”ti vattabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): If they rightly describe its appearance or distinguishing marks, it should be given to them. If they do not, they should be told, “Keep looking.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.1.23
Pali: Tamhā āvāsā pakkamantena ye tattha honti bhikkhū patirūpā, tesaṁ hatthe nikkhipitvā pakkamitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If that monk is leaving that monastery, he should first place that item into the hands of suitable monks there. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.1.24
Pali: No ce honti bhikkhū patirūpā, ye tattha honti gahapatikā patirūpā, tesaṁ hatthe nikkhipitvā pakkamitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If there are no suitable monks, he should place it into the hands of suitable householders there. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.1.25
Pali: Ayaṁ tattha sāmīcīti 
English (brahmali, published): This is the proper procedure: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.1.26
Pali: ayaṁ tattha anudhammatā. 
English (brahmali, published): this is the right method. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.2.2
Pali: ratanaṁ vā ratanasammataṁ vā ajjhārāme vā ajjhāvasathe vā uggahetvā vā uggahāpetvā vā nikkhipati—“yassa bhavissati so harissatī”ti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he picks up something precious or something regarded as precious, or he has it picked up, within a monastery or inside a lodging, and then puts it aside with the thought, “Whoever owns it will come and get it;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.2.3
Pali: ratanasammataṁ vissāsaṁ gaṇhāti, tāvakālikaṁ gaṇhāti, paṁsukūlasaññissa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he takes something regarded as precious on trust or he borrows it or he perceives it as discarded; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.2.4
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.2.5
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc84:4.2.6
Pali: Ratanasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ dutiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on picking up precious things, the second, is finished.

Pali: 85. Vikālagāmappavisanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 85. Entering an inhabited area at the wrong time 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū vikāle gāmaṁ pavisitvā sabhāyaṁ nisīditvā anekavihitaṁ tiracchānakathaṁ kathenti, seyyathidaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): At that time the monks from the group of six entered the village at the wrong time, sat down in the public meeting hall, and talked about all sorts of worldly things: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:1.3
Pali: rājakathaṁ corakathaṁ mahāmattakathaṁ senākathaṁ bhayakathaṁ yuddhakathaṁ annakathaṁ pānakathaṁ vatthakathaṁ sayanakathaṁ mālākathaṁ gandhakathaṁ ñātikathaṁ yānakathaṁ gāmakathaṁ nigamakathaṁ nagarakathaṁ janapadakathaṁ itthikathaṁ sūrakathaṁ visikhākathaṁ kumbhaṭṭhānakathaṁ pubbapetakathaṁ nānattakathaṁ lokakkhāyikaṁ samuddakkhāyikaṁ itibhavābhavakathaṁ iti vā. 
English (brahmali, published): about kings, gangsters, and officials; about armies, dangers, and battles; about food, drink, clothes, and beds; about garlands and perfumes; about relatives, vehicles, villages, towns, and countries; about women and heroes; gossip; about the departed; about various trivialities; about the world and the sea; about the various kinds of existence.

English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:1.23.1

English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk enters an inhabited area at the wrong time, he commits an offense entailing confession.’”

Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā bhikkhū kosalesu janapade sāvatthiṁ gacchantā sāyaṁ aññataraṁ gāmaṁ upagacchiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards a number of monks were walking through the Kosalan country on their way to Sāvatthī, when one evening they came to a certain village. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:2.2
Pali: Manussā te bhikkhū passitvā etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): People saw them and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:2.3
Pali: “pavisatha, bhante”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, please enter the village.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:2.4
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū—
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:2.5
Pali: “bhagavatā paṭikkhittaṁ vikāle gāmaṁ pavisitun”ti kukkuccāyantā na pavisiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): But knowing that entering a village at the wrong time had been prohibited by the Buddha and being afraid of wrongdoing, they declined. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:2.6
Pali: Corā te bhikkhū acchindiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): And so thieves robbed them. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:2.7
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū sāvatthiṁ gantvā bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They then went to Sāvatthī and told the monks what had happened, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:2.8
Pali: Bhikkhū bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): who in turn told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:2.9
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:2.10
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, āpucchā vikāle gāmaṁ pavisituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to enter an inhabited area at the wrong time after informing someone. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:2.11
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:2.12.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu anāpucchā vikāle gāmaṁ paviseyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk, without informing anyone, enters an inhabited area at the wrong time, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:2.13
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:3.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu kosalesu janapade sāvatthiṁ gacchanto sāyaṁ aññataraṁ gāmaṁ upagacchi. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards a monk was walking through the Kosalan country on his way to Sāvatthī, when one evening he came to a certain village.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:3.4

English:

English (brahmali, published): But knowing that entering a village at the wrong time without informing anyone had been prohibited by the Buddha and being afraid of wrongdoing, he declined. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:3.6

English (brahmali, published): And so thieves robbed him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:3.7
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu sāvatthiṁ gantvā bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): He then went to Sāvatthī and told the monks what had happened, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:3.8

English (brahmali, published): who in turn told the Buddha.

English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:3.10
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, santaṁ bhikkhuṁ āpucchā vikāle gāmaṁ pavisituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to enter an inhabited area at the wrong time after informing an available monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:3.11

English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:3.12.1

English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk, when a monk is available, enters an inhabited area at the wrong time without informing him, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:3.13
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:4.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu ahinā daṭṭho hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards a monk was bitten by a snake. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:4.2

English (brahmali, published): Another monk went to the village to get fire. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:4.3
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu—
English (brahmali, published): He then remembered 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:4.4

English (brahmali, published): that the Buddha has prohibited entering a village at the wrong time without informing an available monk. And being afraid of wrongdoing, he did not enter. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:4.5
Pali: bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:4.6
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:4.7
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, tathārūpe accāyike karaṇīye santaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpucchā vikāle gāmaṁ pavisituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to enter an inhabited area at the wrong time without informing an available monk if there is some appropriate urgent business. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:4.8
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:4.9.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu santaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpucchā vikāle gāmaṁ paviseyya, aññatra tathārūpā accāyikā karaṇīyā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk, when another monk is available, enters an inhabited area at the wrong time without informing him, except if there is some appropriate urgent business, he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.1.5
Pali: Santo nāma bhikkhu 
English (brahmali, published): An available monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.1.6
Pali: sakkā hoti āpucchā pavisituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): he is able to inform him and then enter. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.1.7
Pali: Asanto nāma bhikkhu 
English (brahmali, published): No available monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.1.8
Pali: na sakkā hoti āpucchā pavisituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): he is not able to inform anyone and then enter. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.1.9
Pali: Vikālo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): At the wrong time: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.1.10
Pali: majjhanhike vītivatte yāva aruṇuggamanā. 
English (brahmali, published): when the middle of the day has passed, until dawn. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.1.11
Pali: Gāmaṁ paviseyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Enters an inhabited area: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.1.12
Pali: parikkhittassa gāmassa parikkhepaṁ atikkamantassa āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he crosses the boundary of an enclosed inhabited area, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.1.13
Pali: Aparikkhittassa gāmassa upacāraṁ okkamantassa āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he enters the vicinity of an unenclosed inhabited area, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.1.14
Pali: Aññatra tathārūpā accāyikā karaṇīyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Except if there is some appropriate urgent business: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.1.15
Pali: ṭhapetvā tathārūpaṁ accāyikaṁ karaṇīyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): unless there is some appropriate urgent business. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.2.1
Pali: Vikāle vikālasaññī santaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpucchā gāmaṁ pavisati, aññatra tathārūpā accāyikā karaṇīyā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is the wrong time, and he perceives it as such, and he enters an inhabited area without informing an available monk, except if there is some appropriate urgent business, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.2.2
Pali: Vikāle vematiko santaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpucchā gāmaṁ pavisati, aññatra tathārūpā accāyikā karaṇīyā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is the wrong time, but he is unsure of it, and he enters an inhabited area without informing an available monk, except if there is some appropriate urgent business, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.2.3
Pali: Vikāle kālasaññī santaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpucchā gāmaṁ pavisati, aññatra tathārūpā accāyikā karaṇīyā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is the wrong time, but he perceives it as the right time, and he enters an inhabited area without informing an available monk, except if there is some appropriate urgent business, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.2.4
Pali: Kāle vikālasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is the right time, but he perceives it as the wrong time, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.2.5
Pali: Kāle vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is the right time, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.2.6
Pali: Kāle kālasaññī, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is the right time, and he perceives it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.3.2
Pali: tathārūpe accāyike karaṇīye, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is some appropriate urgent business; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.3.3
Pali: santaṁ bhikkhuṁ āpucchā pavisati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he enters after informing an available monk; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.3.4
Pali: asantaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpucchā pavisati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he enters without informing anyone when there is no available monk; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.3.5
Pali: antarārāmaṁ gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is going between monasteries; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.3.6
Pali: bhikkhunupassayaṁ gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is going to the nunnery; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.3.7
Pali: titthiyaseyyaṁ gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is going to the dwelling place of the monastics of another religion; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.3.8
Pali: paṭikkamanaṁ gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is returning to the monastery; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.3.9
Pali: gāmena maggo hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if the road goes via an inhabited area; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.3.10
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.3.11
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.3.12
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc85:5.3.13
Pali: Vikālagāmappavisanasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ tatiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on entering an inhabited area at the wrong time, the third, is finished.

English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on precious things 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:0.5
Pali: 86. Sūcigharasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 86. Making an inappropriate needle case 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sakkesu viharati kapilavatthusmiṁ nigrodhārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying in the Sakyan country in the Banyan Tree Monastery at Kapilavatthu. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarena dantakārena bhikkhū pavāritā honti—
English (brahmali, published): At that time an ivory-worker had invited the monks 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:1.3
Pali: “yesaṁ ayyānaṁ sūcigharena attho ahaṁ sūcigharenā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): who needed needle cases to ask for one. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:1.4
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū bahū sūcighare viññāpenti. 
English (brahmali, published): And the monks asked for many needle cases. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:1.5
Pali: Yesaṁ khuddakā sūcigharā te mahante sūcighare viññāpenti. 
English (brahmali, published): Those who had small needle cases asked for large ones, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:1.6
Pali: Yesaṁ mahantā sūcigharā te khuddake sūcighare viññāpenti. 
English (brahmali, published): and those who had large needle cases asked for small ones. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:1.7
Pali: Atha kho so dantakāro bhikkhūnaṁ bahū sūcighare karonto na sakkoti aññaṁ vikkāyikaṁ bhaṇḍaṁ kātuṁ, attanāpi na yāpeti, puttadāropissa kilamati. 
English (brahmali, published): The ivory-worker was so busy making needle cases for the monks that he was unable to make goods for sale. He could not make a living for himself, and his wives and children suffered. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:1.8
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:1.9
Pali: “kathañhi nāma samaṇā sakyaputtiyā na mattaṁ jānitvā bahū sūcighare viññāpessanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the Sakyan monastics not have any sense of moderation and ask for many needle cases? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:1.10
Pali: Ayaṁ imesaṁ bahū sūcighare karonto na sakkoti aññaṁ vikkāyikaṁ bhaṇḍaṁ kātuṁ, attanāpi na yāpeti, puttadāropissa kilamatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): This ivory-worker is so busy making needle cases for them that he’s unable to make goods for sale. He can’t make a living for himself, and his wives and children are suffering.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:1.11
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:1.12
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:1.13
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhū na mattaṁ jānitvā bahū sūcighare viññāpessantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can those monks not have a sense of moderation and ask for many needle cases?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:1.14
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhū na mattaṁ jānitvā bahū sūcighare viññāpentī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that there are monks who do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:1.15
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:1.16
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:1.17
Pali: kathañhi nāma te, bhikkhave, moghapurisā na mattaṁ jānitvā bahū sūcighare viññāpessanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can those foolish men do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:1.18
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:1.19
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:1.20.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu aṭṭhimayaṁ vā dantamayaṁ vā visāṇamayaṁ vā sūcigharaṁ kārāpeyya bhedanakaṁ, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk has a needle case made from bone, ivory, or horn, it is to be destroyed, and he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.1.5
Pali: Aṭṭhi nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Bone: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.1.6
Pali: yaṁ kiñci aṭṭhi. 
English (brahmali, published): any kind of bone. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.1.7
Pali: Danto nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Ivory: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.1.8
Pali: hatthidanto vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): elephant tooth is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.1.9
Pali: Visāṇaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Horn: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.1.10
Pali: yaṁ kiñci visāṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): any kind of horn. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.1.11
Pali: Kārāpeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Has made: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.1.12
Pali: karoti vā kārāpeti vā, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes one or has one made, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.1.13
Pali: Paṭilābhena bhinditvā pācittiyaṁ desetabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): When he gets it, it is to be destroyed, and he is then to confess an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.2.1
Pali: Attanā vippakataṁ attanā pariyosāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he finishes what he began himself, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.2.2
Pali: Attanā vippakataṁ parehi pariyosāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has others finish what he began himself, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.2.3
Pali: Parehi vippakataṁ attanā pariyosāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he finishes himself what was begun by others, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.2.4
Pali: Parehi vippakataṁ parehi pariyosāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has others finish what was begun by others, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.2.5
Pali: Aññassatthāya karoti vā kārāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes one or has one made for the benefit of someone else, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.2.6
Pali: Aññena kataṁ paṭilabhitvā paribhuñjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he gets one that was made by someone else and then uses it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.3.2
Pali: gaṇṭhikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a toggle; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.3.3
Pali: araṇike, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a fire kindler; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.3.4
Pali: vidhe, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a buckle; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.3.5
Pali: añjaniyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is an ointment box; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.3.6
Pali: añjanisalākāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is an ointment stick; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.3.7
Pali: vāsijaṭe, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a machete handle; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.3.8
Pali: udakapuñchaniyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a water wiper; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.3.9
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.3.10
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc86:2.3.11
Pali: Sūcigharasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ catutthaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on making an inappropriate needle case, the fourth, is finished.

English (brahmali, published): 87. Making an inappropriate bed or bench 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc87:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc87:1.2

English (brahmali, published): Venerable Upananda the Sakyan was sleeping on a high bed. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc87:1.3

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc87:1.5

English (brahmali, published): and said to him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc87:1.6

English (brahmali, published): “Sir, please come and see my bed.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc87:1.7
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā tatova paṭinivattitvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha turned around on the spot and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc87:1.8
Pali: “āsayato, bhikkhave, moghapuriso veditabbo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “A fool, monks, can be recognized by his sleeping place.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc87:1.11.1
Pali: “Navaṁ pana bhikkhunā mañcaṁ vā pīṭhaṁ vā kārayamānena aṭṭhaṅgulapādakaṁ kāretabbaṁ sugataṅgulena, aññatra heṭṭhimāya aṭaniyā; taṁ atikkāmayato chedanakaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk is having a new bed or bench made, it is to have legs eight standard fingerbreadths long below the lowest frame. If the legs exceed that, they are to be cut down, and he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc87:2.1.1

English (brahmali, published): there are four kinds of beds: one with legs and frame, called <i lang='pi' translate='no'>masāraka</i>; one with legs and frame, called <i lang='pi' translate='no'>bundikābaddha</i>; one with crooked legs; one with detachable legs. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc87:2.1.5
Pali: Pīṭhaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A bench: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc87:2.1.6
Pali: cattāri pīṭhāni— masārakaṁ, bundikābaddhaṁ, kuḷīrapādakaṁ, āhaccapādakaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): there are four kinds of benches: one with legs and frame, called <i lang='pi' translate='no'>masāraka</i>; one with legs and frame, called <i lang='pi' translate='no'>bundikābaddha</i>; one with crooked legs; one with detachable legs. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc87:2.1.7
Pali: Kārayamānenāti 
English (brahmali, published): Is having made: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc87:2.1.8
Pali: karonto vā kārāpento vā. 
English (brahmali, published): making it himself or having it made. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc87:2.1.9
Pali: Aṭṭhaṅgulapādakaṁ kāretabbaṁ sugataṅgulena, aññatra heṭṭhimāya aṭaniyāti 
English (brahmali, published): It is to have legs eight standard fingerbreadths long below the lowest frame: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc87:2.1.10
Pali: ṭhapetvā heṭṭhimaṁ aṭaniṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): apart from the lowest frame. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc87:2.1.11
Pali: taṁ atikkāmetvā karoti vā kārāpeti vā, payoge dukkaṭaṁ, paṭilābhena chinditvā pācittiyaṁ desetabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes one, or has one made, that exceeds that, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. When he gets it, the legs are to be cut down, and he is then to confess an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc87:2.1.12.1
Pali: Attanā vippakataṁ attanā pariyosāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he finishes what he began himself, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc87:2.1.13
Pali: Attanā vippakataṁ parehi pariyosāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has others finish what he began himself, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc87:2.1.14
Pali: Parehi vippakataṁ attanā pariyosāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he finishes himself what was begun by others, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc87:2.1.15
Pali: Parehi vippakataṁ parehi pariyosāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has others finish what was begun by others, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc87:2.1.16
Pali: Aññassatthāya karoti vā kārāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes one or has one made for the benefit of someone else, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc87:2.1.17
Pali: Aññena kataṁ paṭilabhitvā paribhuñjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he gets one that was made by someone else and then uses it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc87:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc87:2.2.2
Pali: pamāṇikaṁ karoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes it the right height; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc87:2.2.3
Pali: ūnakaṁ karoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes it lower than the right height; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc87:2.2.4
Pali: aññena kataṁ pamāṇātikkantaṁ paṭilabhitvā chinditvā paribhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he gets one made by another that exceeds the right height and then cuts the legs down before using it; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc87:2.2.5
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc87:2.2.6
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc87:2.2.7
Pali: Mañcapīṭhasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ pañcamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on making an inappropriate bed or bench, the fifth, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:1.2

English (brahmali, published): the monks from the group of six had beds and benches made upholstered with cotton down. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:1.3

English (brahmali, published): When people walking about the dwellings saw this, they complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:1.4

English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those people,

English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized those monks,

English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:1.14.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu mañcaṁ vā pīṭhaṁ vā tūlonaddhaṁ kārāpeyya, uddālanakaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk has a bed or a bench made upholstered with cotton down, it is to be stripped, and he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.1.5
Pali: Mañco nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A bed: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.1.6
Pali: cattāro mañcā— masārako, bundikābaddho, kuḷīrapādako, āhaccapādako. 
English (brahmali, published): there are four kinds of beds: one with legs and frame, called <i lang='pi' translate='no'>masāraka</i>; one with legs and frame, called <i lang='pi' translate='no'>bundikābaddha</i>; one with crooked legs; one with detachable legs. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.1.7
Pali: Pīṭhaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A bench: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.1.8
Pali: cattāri pīṭhāni— masārakaṁ, bundikābaddhaṁ, kuḷīrapādakaṁ, āhaccapādakaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): there are four kinds of benches: one with legs and frame, called <i lang='pi' translate='no'>masāraka</i>; one with legs and frame, called <i lang='pi' translate='no'>bundikābaddha</i>; one with crooked legs; one with detachable legs. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.1.9
Pali: Tūlaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Cotton down: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.1.10
Pali: tīṇi tūlāni— rukkhatūlaṁ, latātūlaṁ, poṭakitūlaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): there are three kinds of cotton down: cotton down from trees, cotton down from creepers, cotton down from grass. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.1.11
Pali: Kārāpeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Has made: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.1.12
Pali: karoti vā kārāpeti vā, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes one or has one made, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.1.13
Pali: Paṭilābhena uddāletvā pācittiyaṁ desetabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): When he gets it, it is to be stripped, and he is then to confess an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.1.14.1
Pali: Attanā vippakataṁ attanā pariyosāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he finishes what he began himself, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.1.15
Pali: Attanā vippakataṁ parehi pariyosāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has others finish what he began himself, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.1.16
Pali: Parehi vippakataṁ attanā pariyosāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he finishes himself what was begun by others, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.1.17
Pali: Parehi vippakataṁ parehi pariyosāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has others finish what was begun by others, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.1.18
Pali: Aññassatthāya karoti vā kārāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes one or has one made for the benefit of someone else, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.1.19

English (brahmali, published): If he gets one that was made by someone else and then uses it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.2.2
Pali: āyoge, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is for a back-and-knee strap; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.2.3
Pali: kāyabandhane, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is for a belt; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.2.4
Pali: aṁsabaddhake, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is for a shoulder strap; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.2.5
Pali: pattatthavikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is for a bowl bag; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.2.6
Pali: parissāvane, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is for a water filter; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.2.7
Pali: bibbohanaṁ karoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is making a pillow; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.2.8
Pali: aññena kataṁ paṭilabhitvā uddāletvā paribhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he gets one made by another and then strips it before using it; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.2.9
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.2.10
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc88:2.2.11
Pali: Tūlonaddhasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ chaṭṭhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on making an upholstered bed or bench, the sixth, is finished.

English (brahmali, published): If it exceeds that, it is to be cut down, and he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc89:1.17
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc89:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā udāyī mahākāyo hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time there was a big monk called Venerable Udāyī. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc89:2.2
Pali: So bhagavato purato nisīdanaṁ paññapetvā samantato samañchamāno nisīdati. 
English (brahmali, published): After putting out his sitting mat in front of the Buddha, he stretched it on all sides before sitting down. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc89:2.3
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha asked him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc89:2.4
Pali: “kissa tvaṁ, udāyi, nisīdanaṁ samantato samañchasi; 
English (brahmali, published): “Udāyī, why are you stretching the sitting mat on all sides, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc89:2.5
Pali: seyyathāpi purāṇāsikoṭṭho”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): as if an old hide?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc89:2.6
Pali: “Tathā hi pana, bhante, bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ atikhuddakaṁ nisīdanaṁ anuññātan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Because, sir, the sitting mat you’ve allowed for the monks is too small.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc89:2.7

English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc89:2.8
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, nisīdanassa dasaṁ vidatthiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow a border of one handspan for the sitting mat. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc89:2.9

English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc89:2.10.1

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc89:2.11

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc89:2.12
Pali: Taṁ atikkāmayato chedanakaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it exceeds that, it is to be cut down, and he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc89:3.1.1
Pali: Nisīdanaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A sitting mat: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc89:3.1.2
Pali: sadasaṁ vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): one with a border is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc89:3.1.3

English (brahmali, published): Is having made: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc89:3.1.4
Pali: karonto vā kārāpento vā pamāṇikaṁ kāretabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): making it himself or having it made, it should be made the right size. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc89:3.1.5
Pali: Tatridaṁ pamāṇaṁ— dīghaso dve vidatthiyo, sugatavidatthiyā; tiriyaṁ diyaḍḍhaṁ. Dasā vidatthi. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the right size: two standard handspans long and one-and-a-half wide, and a border of one handspan. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc89:3.1.6
Pali: Taṁ atikkāmetvā karoti vā kārāpeti vā, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes one, or has one made, that exceeds that, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc89:3.1.7
Pali: Paṭilābhena chinditvā pācittiyaṁ desetabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): When he gets it, it is to be cut down, and he is then to confess an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc89:3.1.8.1

Pali: Parehi vippakataṁ attanā pariyosāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc89:3.1.12

Pali: Anāpatti—

English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc89:3.2.7
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc89:3.2.8
Pali: Nisīdanasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ sattamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on making an oversize sitting mat, the seventh, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:0.5
Pali: 90. Kaṇḍuppaṭicchādisikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 90. Making an oversize itch cloth 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ kaṇḍuppaṭicchādi anuññātā hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): he allowed itch-covering cloths for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:1.3
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhū—
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:1.4
Pali: “bhagavatā kaṇḍuppaṭicchādi anuññātā”ti appamāṇikāyo kaṇḍuppaṭicchādiyo dhārenti; 
English (brahmali, published): Knowing this, the monks from the group of six wore itch covers that were inappropriate in size. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:1.5
Pali: puratopi pacchatopi ākaḍḍhantā āhiṇḍanti. 
English (brahmali, published): As they were walking about, they were dragging them along, both in front and behind. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:1.6
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:1.7
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū appamāṇikāyo kaṇḍuppaṭicchādiyo dhāressantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the monks from the group of six wear such itch covers?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:1.8
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, appamāṇikāyo kaṇḍuppaṭicchādiyo dhārethā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:1.9
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:1.10
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:1.11
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, appamāṇikāyo kaṇḍuppaṭicchādiyo dhāressatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:1.12
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:1.13
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:1.14.1
Pali: “Kaṇḍuppaṭicchādiṁ pana bhikkhunā kārayamānena pamāṇikā kāretabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk is having an itch-covering cloth made, it should be made the right size. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:1.15
Pali: Tatridaṁ pamāṇaṁ—dīghaso catasso vidatthiyo, sugatavidatthiyā; tiriyaṁ dve vidatthiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the right size: four standard handspans long and two wide. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:1.16
Pali: Taṁ atikkāmayato chedanakaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it exceeds that, it is to be cut down, and he commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:2.1
Pali: Kaṇḍuppaṭicchādi nāma 
English (brahmali, published): An itch-covering cloth: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:2.2
Pali: yassa adhonābhi ubbhajāṇumaṇḍalaṁ kaṇḍu vā pīḷakā vā assāvo vā thullakacchu vā ābādho, tassa paṭicchādanatthāya. 
English (brahmali, published): it is for the purpose of covering an itch or a boil or a running sore or a carbuncle, below the navel and above the knees. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:2.3
Pali: Kārayamānenāti 
English (brahmali, published): Is having made: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:2.4
Pali: karonto vā kārāpento vā. Pamāṇikā kāretabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): making it himself or having it made, it should be made the right size. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:2.5
Pali: Tatridaṁ pamāṇaṁ— dīghaso catasso vidatthiyo, sugatavidatthiyā; tiriyaṁ dve vidatthiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the right size: four standard handspans long and two wide. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:2.6
Pali: Taṁ atikkāmetvā karoti vā kārāpeti vā, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes one or has one made that exceeds that, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:2.7
Pali: Paṭilābhena chinditvā pācittiyaṁ desetabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): When he gets it, it is to be cut down, and he is then to confess an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:2.8.1
Pali: Attanā vippakataṁ attanā pariyosāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he finishes what he began himself, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:2.9
Pali: Attanā vippakataṁ parehi pariyosāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has others finish what he began himself, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:2.10
Pali: Parehi vippakataṁ attanā pariyosāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he finishes himself what was begun by others, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:2.11
Pali: Parehi vippakataṁ parehi pariyosāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has others finish what was begun by others, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:2.12
Pali: Aññassatthāya karoti vā kārāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes one or has one made for the benefit of someone else, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:2.13
Pali: Aññena kataṁ paṭilabhitvā paribhuñjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he gets one that was made by someone else and then uses it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:2.14.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:2.15
Pali: pamāṇikaṁ karoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes it the right size; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:2.16
Pali: ūnakaṁ karoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes it smaller than the right size; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:2.17
Pali: aññena kataṁ pamāṇātikkantaṁ paṭilabhitvā chinditvā paribhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he gets one made by another that exceeds the right size and then cuts it down before using it; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:2.18
Pali: vitānaṁ vā bhūmattharaṇaṁ vā sāṇipākāraṁ vā bhisiṁ vā bibbohanaṁ vā karoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes a canopy, a floor cover, a cloth screen, a mattress, or a pillow; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:2.19
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:2.20
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc90:2.21
Pali: Kaṇḍuppaṭicchādisikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ aṭṭhamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on making an oversize itch cloth, the eighth, is finished.

  - brahmali [published]: translation/en/brahmali/vinaya/pli-tv-bu-vb/pli-tv-bu-vb-pc/pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92_translation-en-brahmali.json

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis

Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:0.4
Pali: Ratanavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on precious things 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:0.5
Pali: 92. Nandasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 92. Making an oversize robe 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā nando bhagavato mātucchāputto abhirūpo hoti dassanīyo pāsādiko caturaṅgulomako bhagavatā. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time there was a handsome and graceful monk called Venerable Nanda, the Buddha’s cousin. He was seven centimeters shorter than the Buddha, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:1.3
Pali: So sugatacīvarappamāṇaṁ cīvaraṁ dhāreti. 
English (brahmali, published): but he wore a robe that was the same size as the Buddha’s. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:1.4
Pali: Addasaṁsu kho therā bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ nandaṁ dūratova āgacchantaṁ.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:1.5
Pali: Disvāna—
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:1.6
Pali: “bhagavā āgacchatī”ti āsanā vuṭṭhahanti. 
English (brahmali, published): they thought it was the Buddha and got up from their seats. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:1.7
Pali: Te upagate jānitvā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): But when he came close, they realized who it was, and they complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:1.8
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā nando sugatacīvarappamāṇaṁ cīvaraṁ dhāressatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can Venerable Nanda wear a robe the same size as the Buddha’s?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:1.9
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, nanda, sugatacīvarappamāṇaṁ cīvaraṁ dhāresī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Nanda, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:1.10
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:1.11
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:1.12
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, nanda, sugatacīvarappamāṇaṁ cīvaraṁ dhāressasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Nanda, how can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:1.13
Pali: Netaṁ, nanda, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:1.14
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:1.15.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu sugatacīvarappamāṇaṁ cīvaraṁ kārāpeyya atirekaṁ vā, chedanakaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk has a robe made that is the standard robe size or larger, it is to be cut down, and he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:1.16
Pali: Tatridaṁ sugatassa sugatacīvarappamāṇaṁ—dīghaso nava vidatthiyo, sugatavidatthiyā; tiriyaṁ cha vidatthiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the standard robe size: nine standard handspans long and six wide.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:1.17
Pali: Idaṁ sugatassa sugatacīvarappamāṇan”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.1.5
Pali: Sugatacīvarappamāṇaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The standard robe size: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.1.6
Pali: dīghaso nava vidatthiyo, sugatavidatthiyā; tiriyaṁ cha vidatthiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): nine standard handspans long and six wide. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.1.7
Pali: Kārāpeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Has made: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.1.8
Pali: karoti vā kārāpeti vā, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes one or has one made, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.1.9
Pali: Paṭilābhena chinditvā pācittiyaṁ desetabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): When he gets it, it is to be cut down, and he is then to confess an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.1.10.1
Pali: Attanā vippakataṁ attanā pariyosāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he finishes what he began himself, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.1.11
Pali: Attanā vippakataṁ parehi pariyosāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has others finish what he began himself, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.1.12
Pali: Parehi vippakataṁ attanā pariyosāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he finishes himself what was begun by others, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.1.13
Pali: Parehi vippakataṁ parehi pariyosāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has others finish what was begun by others, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.1.14
Pali: Aññassatthāya karoti vā kārāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes one or has one made for the benefit of someone else, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.1.15
Pali: Aññena kataṁ paṭilabhitvā paribhuñjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he gets one that was made by someone else and then uses it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.2.2
Pali: ūnakaṁ karoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes it smaller than the standard robe; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.2.3
Pali: aññena kataṁ paṭilabhitvā chinditvā paribhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he gets one made by another that is too large and then cuts it down before using it; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.2.4
Pali: vitānaṁ vā bhūmattharaṇaṁ vā sāṇipākāraṁ vā bhisiṁ vā bibbohanaṁ vā karoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes a canopy, a floor cover, a cloth screen, a mattress, or a pillow; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.2.5
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.2.6
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.2.7
Pali: Nandasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ dasamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on making an oversize robe, the tenth, is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.2.8
Pali: Ratanavaggo navamo. 
English (brahmali, published): The ninth subchapter on precious things is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.2.9
Pali: Tassuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.2.10
Pali: Rañño ca ratanaṁ santaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): “And a king’s, precious things, available, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.2.11
Pali: sūci mañcañca tūlikaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): Needle, and bed, cotton down; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.2.12
Pali: Nisīdanañca kaṇḍuñca, 
English (brahmali, published): And sitting mat, and itch, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.2.13
Pali: vassikā sugatena cāti. 
English (brahmali, published): Rainy-season, and by the standard.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.2.14
Pali: Uddiṭṭhā kho, āyasmanto, dvenavuti pācittiyā dhammā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, the ninety-two rules on confession have been recited. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.2.15
Pali: Tatthāyasmante pucchāmi—
English (brahmali, published): In regard to this I ask you, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.2.16
Pali: “kaccittha parisuddhā”? 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.2.17
Pali: Dutiyampi pucchāmi—
English (brahmali, published): A second time I ask, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.2.18
Pali: “kaccittha parisuddhā”? 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.2.19
Pali: Tatiyampi pucchāmi—
English (brahmali, published): A third time I ask, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.2.20
Pali: “kaccittha parisuddhā”? 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.2.21
Pali: Parisuddhetthāyasmanto, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmīti. 
English (brahmali, published): You are pure in this and therefore silent. I will remember it thus.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.2.22
Pali: Khuddakaṁ samattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The section on minor rules has been completed. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pc92:2.2.23
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession is finished.

English (brahmali, published): Venerables, these four rules on acknowledgment come up for recitation. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pd1:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

English (brahmali, published): a certain nun was returning from almsround in Sāvatthī. She saw a monk and said to him,

English (brahmali, published): “Here, venerable, please take some almsfood.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pd1:1.4
Pali: “Suṭṭhu, bhaginī”ti sabbeva aggahesi. 
English (brahmali, published): Saying, “Alright, sister,” he took everything. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pd1:1.5
Pali: Sā upakaṭṭhe kāle nāsakkhi piṇḍāya carituṁ, chinnabhattā ahosi. 
English (brahmali, published): But because the time for eating was coming to an end, she was not able to go for alms, and she missed her meal.

English (brahmali, published): The next day

English (brahmali, published): and the following day the same thing happened again.

English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pd1:1.9

pli-tv-bu-vb-pd1:1.10

English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pd1:1.11
Pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī catutthe divase rathikāya pavedhentī gacchati. 
English (brahmali, published): On the fourth day that nun was walking along a street, trembling. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pd1:1.12
Pali: Seṭṭhi gahapati rathena paṭipathaṁ āgacchanto taṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): A wealthy merchant coming by carriage in the opposite direction saw her and shouted out,

English (brahmali, published): “Watch out, venerable!”

English (brahmali, published): As she stepped aside, she collapsed right there. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pd1:1.15

English (brahmali, published): “How can the venerables receive food directly from a nun? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pd1:1.23
Pali: Kicchalābho mātugāmo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): It’s difficult for women to get material support.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pd1:1.24
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tassa seṭṭhissa gahapatissa ujjhāyantassa khiyyantassa vipācentassa. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of that merchant, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pd1:1.25
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized that monk,

Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhu bhikkhuniyā hatthato āmisaṁ paṭiggahessatī”ti …pe…
