English (brahmali, published): Haven’t I given many teachings for the sake of dispassion, not for the sake of lust; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:5.11.9
Pali: visaṁyogāya dhammo desito, no saṁyogāya; 
English (brahmali, published): for the sake of freedom from bondage, not for the sake of bondage; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:5.11.10
Pali: anupādānāya dhammo desito, no saupādānāya. 
English (brahmali, published): for the sake of non-grasping, not for the sake of grasping? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:5.11.11
Pali: Tattha nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, mayā virāgāya dhamme desite sarāgāya cetessasi, visaṁyogāya dhamme desite saṁyogāya cetessasi, anupādānāya dhamme desite saupādānāya cetessasi. 
English (brahmali, published): When I have taught in this way, how could you choose lust, bondage, and grasping? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:5.11.12
Pali: Nanu mayā, moghapurisa, anekapariyāyena rāgavirāgāya dhammo desito. 
English (brahmali, published): Haven’t I given many teachings for the fading away of lust, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:5.11.13
Pali: Madanimmadanāya pipāsavinayāya ālayasamugghātāya vaṭṭupacchedāya taṇhākkhayāya virāgāya nirodhāya nibbānāya dhammo desito. 
English (brahmali, published): for the clearing away of intoxication, for the removal of thirst, for the uprooting of attachment, for the cutting off of the round of rebirth, for the stopping of craving, for fading away, for ending, for extinguishment? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:5.11.14
Pali: Nanu mayā, moghapurisa, anekapariyāyena kāmānaṁ pahānaṁ akkhātaṁ,

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:5.11.15
Pali: kāmasaññānaṁ pariññā akkhātā, 
English (brahmali, published): the full understanding of the perceptions of worldly pleasures, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:5.11.16
Pali: kāmapipāsānaṁ paṭivinayo akkhāto, 
English (brahmali, published): the removal of thirst for worldly pleasures, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:5.11.17
Pali: kāmavitakkānaṁ samugghāto akkhāto, 
English (brahmali, published): the elimination of thoughts of worldly pleasures, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:5.11.18
Pali: kāmapariḷāhānaṁ vūpasamo akkhāto. 
English (brahmali, published): the stilling of the fevers of worldly pleasures? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:5.11.19
Pali: Varaṁ te, moghapurisa, āsivisassa ghoravisassa mukhe aṅgajātaṁ pakkhittaṁ, na tveva mātugāmassa aṅgajāte aṅgajātaṁ pakkhittaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): It would be better, foolish man, for your penis to enter the mouth of a highly venomous snake than to enter a woman. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:5.11.20
Pali: Varaṁ te, moghapurisa, kaṇhasappassa mukhe aṅgajātaṁ pakkhittaṁ, na tveva mātugāmassa aṅgajāte aṅgajātaṁ pakkhittaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): It would be better for your penis to enter the mouth of a black snake than to enter a woman. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:5.11.21
Pali: Varaṁ te, moghapurisa, aṅgārakāsuyā ādittāya sampajjalitāya sajotibhūtāya aṅgajātaṁ pakkhittaṁ, na tveva mātugāmassa aṅgajāte aṅgajātaṁ pakkhittaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): It would be better for your penis to enter a blazing charcoal pit than to enter a woman. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:5.11.22
Pali: Taṁ kissa hetu? 
English (brahmali, published): Why is that? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:5.11.23
Pali: Tatonidānañhi, moghapurisa, maraṇaṁ vā nigaccheyya maraṇamattaṁ vā dukkhaṁ, na tveva tappaccayā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjeyya. 
English (brahmali, published): For although it might cause death or death-like suffering, it would not cause you to be reborn in a bad destination. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:5.11.24
Pali: Itonidānañca kho, moghapurisa, kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjeyya. 
English (brahmali, published): But <em>this</em> might. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:5.11.25
Pali: Tattha nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, yaṁ tvaṁ asaddhammaṁ gāmadhammaṁ vasaladhammaṁ duṭṭhullaṁ odakantikaṁ rahassaṁ dvayaṁdvayasamāpattiṁ samāpajjissasi, 
English (brahmali, published): Foolish man, you’ve practiced what is contrary to the true Teaching, the common practice, the low practice, the coarse practice, that which ends with a wash, that which is done in private, that which is done wherever there are couples. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:5.11.26
Pali: bahūnaṁ kho tvaṁ, moghapurisa, akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ ādikattā pubbaṅgamo. 
English (brahmali, published): You are the forerunner, the first performer of many unwholesome things. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:5.11.27
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya, pasannānaṁ vā bhiyyobhāvāya; 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:5.11.28
Pali: atha khvetaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānañceva appasādāya, pasannānañca ekaccānaṁ aññathattāyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): and cause some to lose it.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:5.11.29
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ sudinnaṁ anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā dubbharatāya dupposatāya mahicchatāya asantuṭṭhitāya saṅgaṇikāya kosajjassa avaṇṇaṁ bhāsitvā anekapariyāyena subharatāya suposatāya appicchassa santuṭṭhassa sallekhassa dhutassa pāsādikassa apacayassa vīriyārambhassa vaṇṇaṁ bhāsitvā 
English (brahmali, published): Then the Buddha spoke in many ways in dispraise of being difficult to support and maintain, in dispraise of great desires, discontent, socializing, and laziness; but he spoke in many ways in praise of being easy to support and maintain, of fewness of wishes, contentment, self-effacement, ascetic practices, serenity, reduction in things, and being energetic. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:5.11.30
Pali: bhikkhūnaṁ tadanucchavikaṁ tadanulomikaṁ dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): After giving a teaching on what is right and proper, he addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:5.11.31
Pali: “tena hi, bhikkhave, bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññapessāmi dasa atthavase paṭicca—
English (brahmali, published): “Well then, monks, I will lay down a training rule for the following ten reasons: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:5.11.32
Pali: saṅghasuṭṭhutāya, saṅghaphāsutāya, dummaṅkūnaṁ puggalānaṁ niggahāya, pesalānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ phāsuvihārāya, diṭṭhadhammikānaṁ āsavānaṁ saṁvarāya, samparāyikānaṁ āsavānaṁ paṭighātāya, appasannānaṁ pasādāya, pasannānaṁ bhiyyobhāvāya, saddhammaṭṭhitiyā, vinayānuggahāya. 
English (brahmali, published): for the well-being of the Sangha, for the comfort of the Sangha, for the restraint of bad people, for the ease of good monks, for the restraint of the corruptions relating to the present life, for the restraint of the corruptions relating to future lives, to give rise to confidence in those without it, to increase the confidence of those who have it, for the longevity of the true Teaching, and for supporting the training. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:5.11.33
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:5.11.34.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭiseveyya, pārājiko hoti asaṁvāso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk has sexual intercourse, he is expelled and excluded from the community.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:5.11.35
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:5.11.36
Pali: Sudinnabhāṇavāro niṭṭhito. 
English (brahmali, published): The section for recitation on Sudinna is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.0
Pali: 2. Makkaṭīvatthu 
English (brahmali, published): Second substory: the account of the female monkey 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu vesāliyaṁ mahāvane makkaṭiṁ āmisena upalāpetvā tassā methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevati. 
English (brahmali, published): Sometime later, in the Great Wood near Vesālī, a certain monk befriended a female monkey by giving her food. He then had sexual intercourse with her. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.2
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaraṁ ādāya vesāliṁ piṇḍāya pāvisi. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards, after robing up in the morning, he took his bowl and robe and entered Vesālī for alms. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.3
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā bhikkhū senāsanacārikaṁ āhiṇḍantā yena tassa bhikkhuno vihāro tenupasaṅkamiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): Just then a number of monks who were walking about the dwellings came to the one belonging to this monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.4
Pali: Addasa kho sā makkaṭī te bhikkhū dūratova āgacchante. 
English (brahmali, published): The female monkey saw those monks coming. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.5
Pali: Disvāna yena te bhikkhū tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā tesaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ purato kaṭimpi cālesi cheppampi cālesi, kaṭimpi oḍḍi, nimittampi akāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): She went up to them, shook her buttocks in front of them, wagged her tail, presented her backside, and made a gesture. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.6
Pali: Atha kho tesaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ etadahosi—“nissaṁsayaṁ kho so bhikkhu imissā makkaṭiyā methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevatī”ti. Ekamantaṁ nilīyiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks thought, “This monk must be having sexual intercourse with this monkey,” and they hid to one side. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.7
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu vesāliyaṁ piṇḍāya caritvā piṇḍapātaṁ ādāya paṭikkami. 
English (brahmali, published): Then, when that monk had finished his almsround in Vesālī and had returned with his almsfood, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.8
Pali: Atha kho sā makkaṭī yena so bhikkhu tenupasaṅkami. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.9
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu taṁ piṇḍapātaṁ ekadesaṁ bhuñjitvā ekadesaṁ tassā makkaṭiyā adāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): he ate one part himself and gave the rest to that female monkey. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.10
Pali: Atha kho sā makkaṭī taṁ piṇḍapātaṁ bhuñjitvā tassa bhikkhuno kaṭiṁ oḍḍi. Atha kho so bhikkhu tassā makkaṭiyā methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevati. 
English (brahmali, published): After eating the food, the monkey presented her buttocks to the monk, and he had sexual intercourse with her. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.11
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavocuṁ—“nanu, āvuso, bhagavatā sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): The other monks said to him, “Hasn’t a training rule been laid down by the Buddha? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.12
Pali: kissa tvaṁ, āvuso, makkaṭiyā methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): Why then do you have sexual intercourse with a monkey?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.13
Pali: “Saccaṁ, āvuso, bhagavatā sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ; tañca kho manussitthiyā, no tiracchānagatāyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true that a training rule has been laid down by the Buddha, but it concerns women, not animals.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.14
Pali: “Nanu, āvuso, tatheva taṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): “But that’s just the same. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.15
Pali: Ananucchavikaṁ, āvuso, ananulomikaṁ appaṭirūpaṁ assāmaṇakaṁ akappiyaṁ akaraṇīyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): It’s not suitable, it’s not proper, it’s not worthy of a monastic, it’s not allowable, it’s not to be done. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.16
Pali: Kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, āvuso, evaṁ svākkhāte dhammavinaye pabbajitvā na sakkhissasi yāvajīvaṁ paripuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ carituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): How could you go forth on such a well-proclaimed spiritual path and not be able to practice the perfectly complete and pure spiritual life to the end? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.17
Pali: Nanu, āvuso, bhagavatā anekapariyāyena virāgāya dhammo desito, no sarāgāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): Hasn’t the Buddha given many teachings for the sake of dispassion … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.18
Pali: kāmapariḷāhānaṁ vūpasamo akkhāto. 
English (brahmali, published): the stilling of the fevers of worldly pleasures? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.19
Pali: Netaṁ, āvuso, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya pasannānaṁ vā bhiyyobhāvāya. 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.20
Pali: Atha khvetaṁ, āvuso, appasannānañceva appasādāya, pasannānañca ekaccānaṁ aññathattāyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): and cause some to lose it.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.21
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū taṁ bhikkhuṁ anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking that monk in many ways, they told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.22
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe bhikkhusaṅghaṁ sannipātāpetvā taṁ bhikkhuṁ paṭipucchi—
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha then had the Sangha of monks gathered and questioned that monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.23
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, bhikkhu, makkaṭiyā methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monk, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.24
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.25
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā—
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.26
Pali: “ananucchavikaṁ, moghapurisa, ananulomikaṁ appatirūpaṁ assāmaṇakaṁ akappiyaṁ akaraṇīyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, it’s not suitable, it’s not proper, it’s not worthy of a monastic, it’s not allowable, it’s not to be done. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.27
Pali: Kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, evaṁ svākkhāte dhammavinaye pabbajitvā na sakkhissasi yāvajīvaṁ paripuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ carituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): How could you go forth on such a well-proclaimed spiritual path and not be able to practice the perfectly complete and pure spiritual life to the end? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.28
Pali: Nanu mayā, moghapurisa, anekapariyāyena virāgāya dhammo desito, no sarāgāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): Haven’t I given many teachings for the sake of dispassion … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.29
Pali: kāmapariḷāhānaṁ vūpasamo akkhāto. 
English (brahmali, published): for the stilling of the fevers of worldly desire? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.30
Pali: Varaṁ te, moghapurisa, āsivisassa ghoravisassa mukhe aṅgajātaṁ pakkhittaṁ, na tveva makkaṭiyā aṅgajāte aṅgajātaṁ pakkhittaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): It would be better, foolish man, for your penis to enter the mouth of a highly venomous snake than to enter a female monkey. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.31
Pali: Varaṁ te, moghapurisa, kaṇhasappassa mukhe aṅgajātaṁ pakkhittaṁ, na tveva makkaṭiyā aṅgajāte aṅgajātaṁ pakkhittaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): It would be better for your penis to enter the mouth of a black snake than to enter a female monkey. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.32
Pali: Varaṁ te, moghapurisa, aṅgārakāsuyā ādittāya sampajjalitāya sajotibhūtāya aṅgajātaṁ pakkhittaṁ, na tveva makkaṭiyā aṅgajāte aṅgajātaṁ pakkhittaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): It would be better for your penis to enter a blazing charcoal pit than to enter a female monkey. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.33
Pali: Taṁ kissa hetu? 
English (brahmali, published): Why is that? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.34
Pali: Tatonidānañhi, moghapurisa, maraṇaṁ vā nigaccheyya maraṇamattaṁ vā dukkhaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): For although it might cause death or death-like suffering, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.35
Pali: na tveva tappaccayā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjeyya. 
English (brahmali, published): it would not cause you to be reborn in a bad destination. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.36
Pali: Itonidānañca kho, moghapurisa, kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjeyya. 
English (brahmali, published): But <em>this</em> might. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.37
Pali: Tattha nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, yaṁ tvaṁ asaddhammaṁ gāmadhammaṁ vasaladhammaṁ duṭṭhullaṁ odakantikaṁ rahassaṁ dvayaṁdvayasamāpattiṁ samāpajjissasi. 
English (brahmali, published): Foolish man, you’ve practiced what is contrary to the true Teaching, the common practice, the low practice, the coarse practice, that which ends with a wash, that which is done in private, that which is done wherever there are couples. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.38
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.39
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.40.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭiseveyya antamaso tiracchānagatāyapi, pārājiko hoti asaṁvāso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk has sexual intercourse, even with a female animal, he is expelled and excluded from the community.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.41
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:6.42
Pali: Makkaṭīvatthu niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The account of the female monkey is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:7.1.0
Pali: 3. Santhatabhāṇavāra 
English (brahmali, published): Third substory: the section for recitation on covering 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:7.1.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā vesālikā vajjiputtakā bhikkhū yāvadatthaṁ bhuñjiṁsu, yāvadatthaṁ supiṁsu, yāvadatthaṁ nhāyiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards there were a number of Vajjian monks from Vesālī who ate, slept, and bathed as much as they liked. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:7.1.2
Pali: Yāvadatthaṁ bhuñjitvā yāvadatthaṁ supitvā yāvadatthaṁ nhāyitvā ayoniso manasi karitvā sikkhaṁ apaccakkhāya dubbalyaṁ anāvikatvā methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭiseviṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): Then, not reflecting properly and without first renouncing the training and revealing their weakness, they had sexual intercourse. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:7.1.3
Pali: Te aparena samayena ñātibyasanenapi phuṭṭhā bhogabyasanenapi phuṭṭhā rogabyasanenapi phuṭṭhā āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ upasaṅkamitvā evaṁ vadanti—
English (brahmali, published): After some time they were affected by loss of relatives, loss of property, and loss of health. They then went to Venerable Ānanda and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:7.1.4
Pali: “na mayaṁ, bhante ānanda, buddhagarahino na dhammagarahino na saṅghagarahino; 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable Ānanda, we don’t blame the Buddha, the Teaching, or the Sangha; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:7.1.5
Pali: attagarahino mayaṁ, bhante ānanda, anaññagarahino. 
English (brahmali, published): we only have ourselves to blame. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:7.1.6
Pali: Mayamevamhā alakkhikā mayaṁ appapuññā, 
English (brahmali, published): We were unfortunate and had little merit—

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:7.1.7
Pali: ye mayaṁ evaṁ svākkhāte dhammavinaye pabbajitvā nāsakkhimhā yāvajīvaṁ paripuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ carituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): after going forth on such a well-proclaimed spiritual path, we were unable to practice the perfectly complete and pure spiritual life to the end. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:7.1.8
Pali: Idāni cepi mayaṁ, bhante ānanda, labheyyāma bhagavato santike pabbajjaṁ labheyyāma upasampadaṁ, idānipi mayaṁ vipassakā kusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ pubbarattāpararattaṁ bodhipakkhikānaṁ dhammānaṁ bhāvanānuyogamanuyuttā vihareyyāma. 
English (brahmali, published): If we were now to get the going forth and the full ordination in the presence of the Buddha, we would have clarity about wholesome qualities and be devoted day in and day out to developing the aids to awakening. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:7.1.9
Pali: Sādhu, bhante ānanda, bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocehī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Ānanda, please inform the Buddha.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:7.1.10
Pali: “Evamāvuso”ti kho āyasmā ānando vesālikānaṁ vajjiputtakānaṁ paṭissuṇitvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): Saying, “Yes,” he went to the Buddha and told him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:7.1.11
Pali: “Aṭṭhānametaṁ, ānanda, anavakāso yaṁ tathāgato vajjīnaṁ vā vajjiputtakānaṁ vā kāraṇā sāvakānaṁ pārājikaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ samūhaneyyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s impossible, Ānanda, that the Buddha should abolish a training rule that entails expulsion because of the Vajjians.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:7.1.12
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha then gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:7.1.13
Pali: “yo, bhikkhave, sikkhaṁ apaccakkhāya dubbalyaṁ anāvikatvā methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevati so āgato na upasampādetabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, if someone, without first renouncing the training and revealing their weakness, has sexual intercourse, they may not receive the full ordination once again. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:7.1.14
Pali: Yo ca kho, bhikkhave, sikkhaṁ paccakkhāya dubbalyaṁ āvikatvā methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevati so āgato upasampādetabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): But, monks, if someone has sexual intercourse after first renouncing the training and revealing their weakness, they may receive the full ordination once again. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:7.1.15
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:7.1.16.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāsājīvasamāpanno sikkhaṁ apaccakkhāya dubbalyaṁ anāvikatvā methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭiseveyya, antamaso tiracchānagatāyapi, pārājiko hoti asaṁvāso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk, after taking on the monks’ training and way of life, without first renouncing the training and revealing his weakness, has sexual intercourse, even with a female animal, he is expelled and excluded from the community.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso yathāyutto yathājacco yathānāmo yathāgotto yathāsīlo yathāvihārī yathāgocaro thero vā navo vā majjhimo vā. Eso vuccati “yo panā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): whoever, of such a kind, of such activity, of such caste, of such name, of such family, of such conduct, of such behavior, of such association, who is senior, who is junior, or who is of middle standing—this is called “a”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.1.3
Pali: Bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.1.4
Pali: bhikkhakoti bhikkhu, 
English (brahmali, published): he is a monk because he lives on alms; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.1.5
Pali: bhikkhācariyaṁ ajjhupagatoti bhikkhu, 
English (brahmali, published): a monk because he has gone over to living on alms; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.1.6
Pali: bhinnapaṭadharoti bhikkhu, 
English (brahmali, published): a monk because he wears a patchwork cloth; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.1.7
Pali: samaññāya bhikkhu, 
English (brahmali, published): a monk by convention; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.1.8
Pali: paṭiññāya bhikkhu, 
English (brahmali, published): a monk on account of his claim; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.1.9
Pali: ehi bhikkhūti bhikkhu, 
English (brahmali, published): a “come, monk” monk; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.1.10
Pali: tīhi saraṇagamanehi upasampannoti bhikkhu, 
English (brahmali, published): a monk given the full ordination by taking the three refuges; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.1.11
Pali: bhadro bhikkhu, 
English (brahmali, published): a good monk; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.1.12
Pali: sāro bhikkhu, 
English (brahmali, published): a monk of substance; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.1.13
Pali: sekho bhikkhu, 
English (brahmali, published): a trainee monk; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.1.14
Pali: asekho bhikkhu, 
English (brahmali, published): a fully trained monk; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.1.15
Pali: samaggena saṅghena ñatticatutthena kammena akuppena ṭhānārahena upasampannoti bhikkhu. 
English (brahmali, published): a monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.1.16
Pali: Tatra yvāyaṁ bhikkhu samaggena saṅghena ñatticatutthena kammena akuppena ṭhānārahena upasampanno, ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.1.17
Pali: Sikkhāti 
English (brahmali, published): Training: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.1.18
Pali: tisso sikkhā—adhisīlasikkhā, adhicittasikkhā, adhipaññāsikkhā. 
English (brahmali, published): the three trainings: the training in the higher morality, the training in the higher mind, the training in the higher wisdom. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.1.19
Pali: Tatra yāyaṁ adhisīlasikkhā, ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā sikkhāti. 
English (brahmali, published): The training in the higher morality is the training meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.1.20
Pali: Sājīvaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Way of life: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.1.21
Pali: yaṁ bhagavatā paññattaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ, etaṁ sājīvaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): whatever training rule has been laid down by the Buddha—this is called “way of life”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.1.22
Pali: Tasmiṁ sikkhati, tena vuccati sājīvasamāpannoti. 
English (brahmali, published): One trains in this; therefore it is called “after taking on the way of life”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.2.1
Pali: Sikkhaṁ apaccakkhāya dubbalyaṁ anāvikatvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Without first renouncing the training and revealing his weakness: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.2.2
Pali: atthi, bhikkhave, dubbalyāvikammañceva hoti sikkhā ca apaccakkhātā; 
English (brahmali, published): “There is, monks, a revealing of weakness without the training being renounced; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.2.3
Pali: atthi, bhikkhave, dubbalyāvikammañceva hoti sikkhā ca paccakkhātā. 
English (brahmali, published): and there is a revealing of weakness together with the training being renounced. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.2.4
Pali: Kathañca, bhikkhave, dubbalyāvikammañceva hoti sikkhā ca apaccakkhātā. 
English (brahmali, published): And how is there a revealing of weakness without the training being renounced? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.2.5
Pali: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu ukkaṇṭhito anabhirato sāmaññā cavitukāmo 
English (brahmali, published): It may be that a monk is dissatisfied, discontent, desiring to give up the monastic life; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.2.6
Pali: bhikkhubhāvaṁ aṭṭīyamāno harāyamāno jigucchamāno 
English (brahmali, published): troubled, ashamed, and disgusted with the monkhood; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.2.7
Pali: gihibhāvaṁ patthayamāno 
English (brahmali, published): longing to be a householder, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.2.8
Pali: upāsakabhāvaṁ patthayamāno 
English (brahmali, published): longing to be a lay follower, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.2.9
Pali: ārāmikabhāvaṁ patthayamāno 
English (brahmali, published): longing to be a monastery worker, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.2.10
Pali: sāmaṇerabhāvaṁ patthayamāno 
English (brahmali, published): longing to be a novice,

English (brahmali, published): longing to be a monastic of another religion, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.2.12
Pali: titthiyasāvakabhāvaṁ patthayamāno 
English (brahmali, published): longing to be a lay follower of another religion, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.2.13
Pali: assamaṇabhāvaṁ patthayamāno 
English (brahmali, published): longing to be a non-ascetic, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.2.14
Pali: asakyaputtiyabhāvaṁ patthayamāno—
English (brahmali, published): longing to be a non-monastic, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.2.15
Pali: “yannūnāhaṁ buddhaṁ paccakkheyyan”ti vadati viññāpeti. 
English (brahmali, published): and he says and declares: ‘Why don’t I renounce the Buddha?’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.2.16
Pali: Evampi, bhikkhave, dubbalyāvikammañceva hoti sikkhā ca apaccakkhātā. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way, monks, there’s a revealing of weakness without the training being renounced. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.2.17
Pali: Atha vā pana ukkaṇṭhito anabhirato sāmaññā cavitukāmo 
English (brahmali, published): Or again, dissatisfied, discontent, desiring to give up the monastic life;

Pali: bhikkhubhāvaṁ aṭṭīyamāno harāyamāno jigucchamāno 
English (brahmali, published): troubled, ashamed, and disgusted with the monkhood;

Pali: gihibhāvaṁ patthayamāno …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): longing to be a householder …

Pali: “nābhiramāmī”ti vadati viññāpeti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘I take no delight.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.2.125
Pali: Evampi kho, bhikkhave, dubbalyāvikammañceva hoti sikkhā ca apaccakkhātā. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way too, monks, there’s a revealing of weakness without the training being renounced. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.3.1

English (brahmali, published): And how is there a revealing of weakness together with the training being renounced?

English (brahmali, published): Or whatever other synonyms there are for the Buddha, for the Teaching, for the Sangha, for the practice, for the training, for the Monastic Code, for the recitation, for a preceptor, for a teacher, for a student, for a pupil, for a co-student, for a co-pupil, for a fellow monastic, for a householder, for a lay follower, for a monastery worker, for a novice monk, for a monastic of another religion, for a lay follower of another religion, for a non-ascetic, or for a non-monastic—

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.3.50
Pali: tehi ākārehi tehi liṅgehi tehi nimittehi vadati viññāpeti. 
English (brahmali, published): he speaks and declares by way of these indications, by way of these marks, by way of these signs. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.3.51
Pali: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, dubbalyāvikammañceva hoti sikkhā ca paccakkhātā. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way, monks, there’s a revealing of weakness together with the training being renounced. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.4.1
Pali: Kathañca, bhikkhave, apaccakkhātā hoti sikkhā? 
English (brahmali, published): And how is the training not renounced? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.4.2
Pali: Idha, bhikkhave, yehi ākārehi yehi liṅgehi yehi nimittehi sikkhā paccakkhātā hoti tehi ākārehi tehi liṅgehi tehi nimittehi ummattako sikkhaṁ paccakkhāti, apaccakkhātā hoti sikkhā. 
English (brahmali, published): If you renounce the training by way of these indications, by way of these marks, by way of these signs, but you’re insane, then the training isn’t renounced. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.4.3
Pali: Ummattakassa santike sikkhaṁ paccakkhāti, apaccakkhātā hoti sikkhā. 
English (brahmali, published): If you renounce the training to one who’s insane, the training isn’t renounced. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.4.4
Pali: Khittacitto sikkhaṁ paccakkhāti, apaccakkhātā hoti sikkhā. 
English (brahmali, published): If you renounce the training when you’re deranged, the training isn’t renounced. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.4.5
Pali: Khittacittassa santike sikkhaṁ paccakkhāti, apaccakkhātā hoti sikkhā. 
English (brahmali, published): If you renounce the training to one who’s deranged, the training isn’t renounced. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.4.6
Pali: Vedanāṭṭo sikkhaṁ paccakkhāti, apaccakkhātā hoti sikkhā. 
English (brahmali, published): If you renounce the training when you’re overwhelmed by pain, the training isn’t renounced. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.4.7
Pali: Vedanāṭṭassa santike sikkhaṁ paccakkhāti, apaccakkhātā hoti sikkhā. 
English (brahmali, published): If you renounce the training to one who’s overwhelmed by pain, the training isn’t renounced. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.4.8
Pali: Devatāya santike sikkhaṁ paccakkhāti, apaccakkhātā hoti sikkhā. 
English (brahmali, published): If you renounce the training to a god, the training isn’t renounced. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.4.9
Pali: Tiracchānagatassa santike sikkhaṁ paccakkhāti, apaccakkhātā hoti sikkhā. 
English (brahmali, published): If you renounce the training to an animal, the training isn’t renounced. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.4.10
Pali: Ariyakena milakkhassa santike sikkhaṁ paccakkhāti, so ca na paṭivijānāti, apaccakkhātā hoti sikkhā. 
English (brahmali, published): If an Indo-Aryan renounces the training to a non-Indo-Aryan who doesn’t understand, the training isn’t renounced. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.4.11
Pali: Milakkhakena ariyassa santike sikkhaṁ paccakkhāti, so ca na paṭivijānāti, apaccakkhātā hoti sikkhā. 
English (brahmali, published): If a non-Indo-Aryan renounces the training to an Indo-Aryan who doesn’t understand, the training isn’t renounced. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.4.12
Pali: Ariyakena ariyassa santike sikkhaṁ paccakkhāti, so ca na paṭivijānāti, apaccakkhātā hoti sikkhā. 
English (brahmali, published): If an Indo-Aryan renounces the training to an Indo-Aryan who doesn’t understand, the training isn’t renounced. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.4.13
Pali: Milakkhakena milakkhassa santike sikkhaṁ paccakkhāti, so ca na paṭivijānāti, apaccakkhātā hoti sikkhā. 
English (brahmali, published): If a non-Indo-Aryan renounces the training to a non-Indo-Aryan who doesn’t understand, the training isn’t renounced. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.4.14
Pali: Davāya sikkhaṁ paccakkhāti, apaccakkhātā hoti sikkhā. 
English (brahmali, published): If you renounce the training as a joke, the training isn’t renounced. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.4.15
Pali: Ravāya sikkhaṁ paccakkhāti, apaccakkhātā hoti sikkhā. 
English (brahmali, published): If you renounce the training because of speaking too fast, the training isn’t renounced. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.4.16
Pali: Asāvetukāmo sāveti, apaccakkhātā hoti sikkhā. 
English (brahmali, published): If you announce what you don’t wish to announce, the training isn’t renounced. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.4.17
Pali: Sāvetukāmo na sāveti, apaccakkhātā hoti sikkhā. 
English (brahmali, published): If you don’t announce what you wish to announce, the training isn’t renounced. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.4.18
Pali: Aviññussa sāveti, apaccakkhātā hoti sikkhā. 
English (brahmali, published): If you announce to one who doesn’t understand, the training isn’t renounced. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.4.19
Pali: Viññussa na sāveti, apaccakkhātā hoti sikkhā. 
English (brahmali, published): If you don’t announce to one who understands, the training isn’t renounced. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.4.20
Pali: Sabbaso vā pana na sāveti, apaccakkhātā hoti sikkhā. 
English (brahmali, published): Or if you don’t make a full announcement, the training isn’t renounced. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.4.21
Pali: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, apaccakkhātā hoti sikkhā. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way, monks, the training isn’t renounced.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.5.1
Pali: Methunadhammo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Sexual intercourse: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.5.2
Pali: yo so asaddhammo gāmadhammo vasaladhammo duṭṭhullaṁ odakantikaṁ rahassaṁ dvayaṁdvayasamāpatti, eso methunadhammo nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): what is contrary to the true Teaching, the common practice, the low practice, the coarse practice, that which ends with a wash, that which is done in private, that which is done wherever there are couples—this is called “sexual intercourse”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.5.3
Pali: Paṭisevati nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Has: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.5.4
Pali: yo nimittena nimittaṁ aṅgajātena aṅgajātaṁ antamaso tilaphalamattampi paveseti, eso paṭisevati nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): whoever makes an organ enter an organ, a genital enter a genital, even to the depth of a sesame seed—this is called “has”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.5.5
Pali: Antamaso tiracchānagatāyapīti 
English (brahmali, published): Even with a female animal: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.5.6
Pali: tiracchānagatitthiyāpi methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevitvā assamaṇo hoti asakyaputtiyo, pageva manussitthiyā. Tena vuccati— “antamaso tiracchānagatāyapī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): even having had sexual intercourse with a female animal, he is not an ascetic, not a Sakyan monastic, let alone with a woman—therefore it is called “even with a female animal”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.5.7
Pali: Pārājiko hotīti 
English (brahmali, published): He is expelled: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.5.8
Pali: seyyathāpi nāma puriso sīsacchinno abhabbo tena sarīrabandhanena jīvituṁ; evameva bhikkhu methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevitvā assamaṇo hoti asakyaputtiyo. Tena vuccati— “pārājiko hotī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): just as a man with his head cut off is unable to continue living by reconnecting it to the body, so too is a monk who has had sexual intercourse not an ascetic, not a Sakyan monastic. Therefore it is said, “he is expelled.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.5.9
Pali: Asaṁvāsoti 
English (brahmali, published): Excluded from the community: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.5.10
Pali: saṁvāso nāma ekakammaṁ ekuddeso samasikkhatā— eso saṁvāso nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): Community: joint legal procedures, a joint recitation, the same training—this is called “community”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:8.5.11
Pali: So tena saddhiṁ natthi. Tena vuccati—“asaṁvāso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): He does not take part in this—therefore it is called “excluded from the community”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.1.1
Pali: Tisso itthiyo—
English (brahmali, published): There are three kinds of females: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.1.2
Pali: manussitthī, amanussitthī, tiracchānagatitthī. 
English (brahmali, published): a human female, a female spirit, a female animal. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.1.3
Pali: Tayo ubhatobyañjanakā—
English (brahmali, published): There are three kinds of gynandromorphs:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.1.7
Pali: Tayo purisā—
English (brahmali, published): There are three kinds of males: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.1.8
Pali: manussapuriso, amanussapuriso, tiracchānagatapuriso. 
English (brahmali, published): a human male, a male spirit, a male animal. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.1.9.1
Pali: Manussitthiyā tayo magge methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevantassa āpatti pārājikassa—
English (brahmali, published): He commits an offense entailing expulsion if he has sexual intercourse with a human female through three orifices: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.1.10

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.1.12

English (brahmali, published): He commits an offense entailing expulsion if he has sexual intercourse with a female animal through three orifices: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.1.13
Pali: vaccamagge, passāvamagge, mukhe. 
English (brahmali, published): the anus, the vagina, or the mouth. …

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.1.15
Pali: amanussubhatobyañjanakassa … 
English (brahmali, published): with a gynandromorph spirit … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.1.16
Pali: tiracchānagatubhatobyañjanakassa tayo magge methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevantassa āpatti pārājikassa—
English (brahmali, published): He commits an offense entailing expulsion if he has sexual intercourse with a gynandromorph animal through three orifices: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.1.17
Pali: vaccamagge, passāvamagge, mukhe. 
English (brahmali, published): the anus, the vagina, or the mouth. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.1.18
Pali: Manussapaṇḍakassa dve magge methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevantassa āpatti pārājikassa—
English (brahmali, published): He commits an offense entailing expulsion if he has sexual intercourse with a human <i lang='pi' translate='no'>paṇḍaka</i> through two orifices: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.1.19
Pali: vaccamagge, mukhe. 
English (brahmali, published): the anus or the mouth. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.1.20
Pali: Amanussapaṇḍakassa … 
English (brahmali, published): with a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>paṇḍaka</i> spirit … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.1.21
Pali: tiracchānagatapaṇḍakassa … 
English (brahmali, published): with a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>paṇḍaka</i> animal … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.1.22
Pali: manussapurisassa … 
English (brahmali, published): with a human male … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.1.23
Pali: amanussapurisassa … 
English (brahmali, published): with a male spirit … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.1.24
Pali: tiracchānagatapurisassa dve magge methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevantassa āpatti pārājikassa—
English (brahmali, published): He commits an offense entailing expulsion if he has sexual intercourse with a male animal through two orifices: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.1.25
Pali: vaccamagge, mukhe. 
English (brahmali, published): the anus or the mouth. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.2.1
Pali: Bhikkhussa sevanacittaṁ upaṭṭhite manussitthiyā vaccamaggaṁ aṅgajātaṁ pavesentassa āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk has the intention of sexual relations and he makes his penis enter the anus of a human female … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.2.2

English (brahmali, published): the anus of a male animal … the mouth of a male animal, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.3.1
Pali: Bhikkhupaccatthikā manussitthiṁ bhikkhussa santike ānetvā vaccamaggena aṅgajātaṁ abhinisīdenti. 
English (brahmali, published): Enemy monks bring a human female to a monk and make her sit down so that his penis enters her anus. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.3.2
Pali: So ce pavesanaṁ sādiyati, paviṭṭhaṁ sādiyati, ṭhitaṁ sādiyati, uddharaṇaṁ sādiyati, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he consents to the entry, and he consents to having entered, and he consents to the remaining, and he consents to the taking out, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.3.3
Pali: Bhikkhupaccatthikā manussitthiṁ bhikkhussa santike ānetvā vaccamaggena aṅgajātaṁ abhinisīdenti. 
English (brahmali, published): Enemy monks bring a human female to a monk and make her sit down so that his penis enters her anus. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.3.4
Pali: So ce pavesanaṁ na sādiyati, paviṭṭhaṁ sādiyati, ṭhitaṁ sādiyati, uddharaṇaṁ sādiyati, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not consent to the entry, but he consents to having entered, and he consents to the remaining, and he consents to the taking out, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.3.5
Pali: Bhikkhupaccatthikā manussitthiṁ bhikkhussa santike ānetvā vaccamaggena aṅgajātaṁ abhinisīdenti. 
English (brahmali, published): Enemy monks bring a human female to a monk and make her sit down so that his penis enters her anus. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.3.6
Pali: So ce pavesanaṁ na sādiyati, paviṭṭhaṁ na sādiyati, ṭhitaṁ sādiyati, uddharaṇaṁ sādiyati, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not consent to the entry, nor to having entered, but he consents to the remaining, and he consents to the taking out, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.3.7
Pali: Bhikkhupaccatthikā manussitthiṁ bhikkhussa santike ānetvā vaccamaggena aṅgajātaṁ abhinisīdenti. 
English (brahmali, published): Enemy monks bring a human female to a monk and make her sit down so that his penis enters her anus. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.3.8
Pali: So ce pavesanaṁ na sādiyati, paviṭṭhaṁ na sādiyati, ṭhitaṁ na sādiyati, uddharaṇaṁ sādiyati, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not consent to the entry, nor to having entered, nor to the remaining, but he consents to the taking out, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.3.9
Pali: Bhikkhupaccatthikā manussitthiṁ bhikkhussa santike ānetvā vaccamaggena aṅgajātaṁ abhinisīdenti. 
English (brahmali, published): Enemy monks bring a human female to a monk and make her sit down so that his penis enters her anus. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:9.3.10
Pali: So ce pavesanaṁ na sādiyati, paviṭṭhaṁ na sādiyati, ṭhitaṁ na sādiyati, uddharaṇaṁ na sādiyati, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not consent to the entry, nor to having entered, nor to the remaining, nor to the taking out, there is no offense.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:10.1.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu makkaṭiyā methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevi.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:10.1.3

English (brahmali, published): Could it be that I’ve committed an offense entailing expulsion?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:10.1.5
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): He told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:10.1.6
Pali: “Āpattiṁ tvaṁ, bhikkhu, āpanno pārājikan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “You’ve committed an offense entailing expulsion.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:10.2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā vesālikā vajjiputtakā bhikkhū sikkhaṁ apaccakkhāya dubbalyaṁ anāvikatvā methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭiseviṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time a number of Vajjian monks from Vesālī had sexual intercourse without first renouncing the training and revealing their weakness. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:10.2.2
Pali: Tesaṁ kukkuccaṁ ahosi—
English (brahmali, published): They became anxious, thinking, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:10.2.3
Pali: “bhagavatā sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): “The Buddha has laid down a training rule.

Pali: kacci nu kho mayaṁ pārājikaṁ āpattiṁ āpannā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): Could it be that we’ve committed an offense entailing expulsion?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:10.2.5
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj1:10.2.6
Pali: “Āpattiṁ tumhe, bhikkhave, āpannā pārājikan”ti.

English (brahmali, published): “There’s no offense when it occurs while dreaming.”

English (brahmali, published): The first offense entailing expulsion is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā rājagahe viharati gijjhakūṭe pabbate. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying on the Vulture Peak at Rājagaha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā sandiṭṭhā sambhattā bhikkhū isigilipasse tiṇakuṭiyo karitvā vassaṁ upagacchiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time a number of monks who were friends had made grass huts on the slope of Mount Isigili and had entered the rainy-season residence there. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.1.3
Pali: Āyasmāpi dhaniyo kumbhakāraputto tiṇakuṭikaṁ karitvā vassaṁ upagacchi. 
English (brahmali, published): Among them was Venerable Dhaniya the potter. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.1.4
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū vassaṁvuṭṭhā temāsaccayena tiṇakuṭiyo bhinditvā tiṇañca kaṭṭhañca paṭisāmetvā janapadacārikaṁ pakkamiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): When the three months were over and they had completed the rainy-season residence, the monks demolished their grass huts, put away the grass and sticks, and left to wander the country. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.1.5
Pali: Āyasmā pana dhaniyo kumbhakāraputto tattheva vassaṁ vasi, tattha hemantaṁ, tattha gimhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): But Venerable Dhaniya spent the winter and the summer right there. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.1.6
Pali: Atha kho āyasmato dhaniyassa kumbhakāraputtassa gāmaṁ piṇḍāya paviṭṭhassa tiṇahāriyo kaṭṭhahāriyo tiṇakuṭikaṁ bhinditvā tiṇañca kaṭṭhañca ādāya agamaṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): Then, on one occasion, while Dhaniya was in the village to collect almsfood, some women gathering grass and firewood demolished his grass hut and took away the grass and sticks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.1.7
Pali: Dutiyampi kho āyasmā dhaniyo kumbhakāraputto tiṇañca kaṭṭhañca saṅkaḍḍhitvā tiṇakuṭikaṁ akāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): A second time Dhaniya collected grass and sticks and made a grass hut,

English (brahmali, published): and again the hut was demolished in the same way. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.1.9
Pali: Tatiyampi kho āyasmā dhaniyo kumbhakāraputto tiṇañca kaṭṭhañca saṅkaḍḍhitvā tiṇakuṭikaṁ akāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): The same thing happened a third time. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.1.10

English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.1.11
Pali: Atha kho āyasmato dhaniyassa kumbhakāraputtassa etadahosi—
English (brahmali, published): Dhaniya thought,

Pali: “yāvatatiyakaṁ kho me gāmaṁ piṇḍāya paviṭṭhassa tiṇahāriyo kaṭṭhahāriyo tiṇakuṭikaṁ bhinditvā tiṇañca kaṭṭhañca ādāya agamaṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): “Three times this has happened. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.1.13
Pali: Ahaṁ kho pana susikkhito anavayo sake ācariyake kumbhakārakamme pariyodātasippo. 
English (brahmali, published): But I’m well-trained and experienced in my own craft of pottery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.1.14
Pali: Yannūnāhaṁ sāmaṁ cikkhallaṁ madditvā sabbamattikāmayaṁ kuṭikaṁ kareyyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Why don’t I knead mud myself and make a hut entirely of clay?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.1.15
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā dhaniyo kumbhakāraputto sāmaṁ cikkhallaṁ madditvā sabbamattikāmayaṁ kuṭikaṁ karitvā tiṇañca kaṭṭhañca gomayañca saṅkaḍḍhitvā taṁ kuṭikaṁ paci. 
English (brahmali, published): And he did just that. He then collected grass, sticks, and cow-dung, and he baked his hut. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.1.16
Pali: Sā ahosi kuṭikā abhirūpā dassanīyā pāsādikā lohitikā, seyyathāpi indagopako. 
English (brahmali, published): It was a pretty and attractive little hut, red in color like a scarlet rain-mite. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.1.17
Pali: Seyyathāpi nāma kiṅkaṇikasaddo; evamevaṁ tassā kuṭikāya saddo ahosi. 
English (brahmali, published): And when struck, it sounded just like a bell. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.2.1
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā sambahulehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ gijjhakūṭā pabbatā orohanto addasa taṁ kuṭikaṁ abhirūpaṁ dassanīyaṁ pāsādikaṁ lohitikaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha was descending from the Vulture Peak with a number of monks when he saw that hut. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.2.2
Pali: Disvāna bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): He said to the monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.2.3

English (brahmali, published): “What’s this pretty and attractive thing that’s red in color like a scarlet rain-mite?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.2.4
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks told the Buddha, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.2.5
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā—
English (brahmali, published): who then rebuked Dhaniya: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.2.6
Pali: “ananucchavikaṁ, bhikkhave, tassa moghapurisassa ananulomikaṁ appatirūpaṁ assāmaṇakaṁ akappiyaṁ akaraṇīyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s not suitable for that foolish man, it’s not proper, it’s not worthy of a monastic, it’s not allowable, it’s not to be done. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.2.7
Pali: Kathañhi nāma so, bhikkhave, moghapuriso sabbamattikāmayaṁ kuṭikaṁ karissati. 
English (brahmali, published): How could he make a hut entirely of clay? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.2.8
Pali: Na hi nāma, bhikkhave, tassa moghapurisassa pāṇesu anuddayā anukampā avihesā bhavissati. 
English (brahmali, published): Doesn’t he have any consideration, compassion, and mercy for living beings? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.2.9
Pali: Gacchathetaṁ, bhikkhave, kuṭikaṁ bhindatha. 
English (brahmali, published): Go, monks, and demolish this hut, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.2.10
Pali: Mā pacchimā janatā pāṇesu pātabyataṁ āpajji. 
English (brahmali, published): so that future generations don’t follow his example. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.2.11
Pali: Na ca, bhikkhave, sabbamattikāmayā kuṭikā kātabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): And, monks, you shouldn’t make a hut entirely of clay. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.2.12
Pali: Yo kareyya, āpatti dukkaṭassā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If you do, you commit an offense of wrong conduct.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.2.13
Pali: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti, kho te bhikkhū bhagavato paṭissuṇitvā yena sā kuṭikā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ kuṭikaṁ bhindiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): Saying, “Yes, sir,” they went to demolish it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.2.14
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā dhaniyo kumbhakāraputto te bhikkhū etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): And Dhaniya said to them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.2.15
Pali: “kissa me tumhe, āvuso, kuṭikaṁ bhindathā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Why are you demolishing my hut?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.2.16
Pali: “Bhagavā, āvuso, bhedāpetī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “The Buddha has asked us to.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.2.17
Pali: “Bhindathāvuso, sace dhammassāmī bhedāpetī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Demolish it then, if the Lord of the Truth has said so.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.3.1
Pali: Atha kho āyasmato dhaniyassa kumbhakāraputtassa etadahosi—
English (brahmali, published): Dhaniya thought, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.3.2
Pali: “yāvatatiyakaṁ kho me gāmaṁ piṇḍāya paviṭṭhassa tiṇahāriyo kaṭṭhahāriyo tiṇakuṭikaṁ bhinditvā tiṇañca kaṭṭhañca ādāya agamaṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): “Three times, while I was in the village to collect almsfood, women gathering grass and firewood demolished my hut and took away the grass and sticks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.3.3
Pali: Yāpi mayā sabbamattikāmayā kuṭikā katā sāpi bhagavatā bhedāpitā. 
English (brahmali, published): And now my hut made entirely of clay has been demolished at the Buddha’s request. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.3.4
Pali: Atthi ca me dārugahe gaṇako sandiṭṭho. 
English (brahmali, published): Now, the caretaker of the woodyard is a friend of mine. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.3.5
Pali: Yannūnāhaṁ dārugahe gaṇakaṁ dārūni yācitvā dārukuṭikaṁ kareyyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Why don’t I ask him for timber and make a hut out of that?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.3.6
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā dhaniyo kumbhakāraputto yena dārugahe gaṇako tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā dārugahe gaṇakaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Dhaniya then went to the caretaker of the woodyard and told him what had happened, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.3.7
Pali: “yāvatatiyakaṁ kho me, āvuso, gāmaṁ piṇḍāya paviṭṭhassa tiṇahāriyo kaṭṭhahāriyo tiṇakuṭikaṁ bhinditvā tiṇañca kaṭṭhañca ādāya agamaṁsu. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.3.8
Pali: Yāpi mayā sabbamattikāmayā kuṭikā katā sāpi bhagavatā bhedāpitā. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.3.9
Pali: Dehi me, āvuso, dārūni. 
English (brahmali, published): adding, “Please give me some timber, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.3.10
Pali: Icchāmi dārukuṭikaṁ kātun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I want to make a wooden hut.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.3.11
Pali: “Natthi, bhante, tādisāni dārūni yānāhaṁ ayyassa dadeyyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “There’s no timber, sir, that I could give you. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.3.12
Pali: Atthi, bhante, devagahadārūni nagarapaṭisaṅkhārikāni āpadatthāya nikkhittāni. 
English (brahmali, published): This timber is held by the king. It’s meant for repairs of the town and put aside in case of an emergency. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.3.13
Pali: Sace tāni dārūni rājā dāpeti harāpetha, bhante”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): You can only have it if the king gives it away.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.3.14
Pali: “Dinnāni, āvuso, raññā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Actually, it’s been given by the king.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.3.15
Pali: Atha kho dārugahe gaṇakassa etadahosi—
English (brahmali, published): The caretaker of the woodyard thought, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.3.16
Pali: “ime kho samaṇā sakyaputtiyā dhammacārino samacārino brahmacārino saccavādino sīlavanto kalyāṇadhammā. 
English (brahmali, published): “These Sakyan monastics have integrity. They are celibate and their conduct is good, and they are truthful, moral, and have a good character. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.3.17
Pali: Rājāpimesaṁ abhippasanno. 
English (brahmali, published): Even the king has faith in them. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.3.18
Pali: Nārahati adinnaṁ dinnanti vattun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): These venerables wouldn’t say something is given if it wasn’t.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.3.19
Pali: Atha kho dārugahe gaṇako āyasmantaṁ dhaniyaṁ kumbhakāraputtaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): And he said to Dhaniya, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.3.20
Pali: “harāpetha, bhante”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “You may take it, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.3.21
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā dhaniyo kumbhakāraputto tāni dārūni khaṇḍākhaṇḍikaṁ chedāpetvā sakaṭehi nibbāhāpetvā dārukuṭikaṁ akāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): Dhaniya then had that timber cut into pieces, took it away on carts, and made a wooden hut. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.4.1
Pali: Atha kho vassakāro brāhmaṇo magadhamahāmatto rājagahe kammante anusaññāyamāno yena dārugahe gaṇako tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā dārugahe gaṇakaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the brahmin Vassakāra, the chief minister of Magadha, was inspecting the public works in Rājagaha when he went to the caretaker of the woodyard and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.4.2
Pali: “yāni tāni, bhaṇe, 
English (brahmali, published): “What’s going on?

English (brahmali, published): “How could the king give away this timber to Dhaniya the potter?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.4.7

English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, sir, that you have given away to Dhaniya the potter the timber that was held for repairs of the town and put aside in case of an emergency?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.4.9

English (brahmali, published): “Who said that?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.4.10

English (brahmali, published): “The caretaker of the woodyard.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.4.12

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.4.13

English (brahmali, published): Dhaniya saw this 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.4.14
Pali: Disvāna dārugahe gaṇakaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): and said to him,

English (brahmali, published): “Please come before I’m done for.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.5.1

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.5.5
Pali: “Evaṁ, mahārājā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes, great king.”

English (brahmali, published): we may give and not remember.

Pali: iṅgha, bhante, sarāpehī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Please remind me.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.5.9
Pali: “Sarasi tvaṁ, mahārāja, paṭhamābhisitto evarūpiṁ vācaṁ bhāsitā—
English (brahmali, published): “Do you remember, great king, when you were first anointed, speaking these words:

English (brahmali, published): “I remember. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.5.12
Pali: Santi, bhante, samaṇabrāhmaṇā lajjino kukkuccakā sikkhākāmā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are monastics and brahmins who have a sense of conscience, who are afraid of wrongdoing and fond of the training. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.5.13
Pali: Tesaṁ appamattakepi kukkuccaṁ uppajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): They are afraid of wrongdoing even in regard to small matters. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.5.14
Pali: Tesaṁ mayā sandhāya bhāsitaṁ, tañca kho araññe apariggahitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): When I spoke, I was referring to them, and it concerned what’s ownerless in the wilderness. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.5.15
Pali: So tvaṁ, bhante, tena lesena dārūni adinnaṁ harituṁ maññasi. 
English (brahmali, published): Yet you imagine that you can take timber not given to you by means of this pretext? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.5.16
Pali: Kathañhi nāma mādiso samaṇaṁ vā brāhmaṇaṁ vā vijite vasantaṁ haneyya vā bandheyya vā pabbājeyya vā. 
English (brahmali, published): Even so, I cannot beat, imprison, or banish a monastic or brahmin living in my own kingdom. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.5.17

English (brahmali, published): Go, you’re free because of your status, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.5.18
Pali: Māssu punapi evarūpaṁ akāsī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): but don’t do such a thing again.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.6.1
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): But people complained and criticized him:

English (brahmali, published): They even deceive the king, never mind other people.”

English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires, who had a sense of conscience, and who were contented, afraid of wrongdoing, and fond of the training, complained and criticized Venerable Dhaniya, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.6.11

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.6.16

English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.6.17

Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya pasannānaṁ vā bhiyyobhāvāya; 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.6.20

English (brahmali, published): and cause some to lose it.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.6.21

English (brahmali, published): The Buddha said to him,

English (brahmali, published): “For stealing a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>pāda</i> coin, sir, or the value of a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>pāda</i>.”

English (brahmali, published): At that time in Rājagaha a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>pāda</i> coin was worth five <i lang='pi' translate='no'>māsaka</i> coins.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.6.28.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu adinnaṁ theyyasaṅkhātaṁ ādiyeyya, 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk, intending to steal, takes what has not been given to him—

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.6.29

English (brahmali, published): the sort of stealing for which kings, having caught a thief, would beat, imprison, or banish him, saying, “You’re a bandit, you’re a fool, you’ve gone astray, you’re a thief”—

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:1.6.30

English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū rajakattharaṇaṁ gantvā rajakabhaṇḍikaṁ avaharitvā ārāmaṁ haritvā bhājesuṁ.
