pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:2.4
Pali: Bahuṁ tumhākaṁ cīvaraṁ uppannan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): seeing that you’ve gotten so much robe-cloth.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:2.5
Pali: “Kuto, āvuso, amhākaṁ puññaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): “How is it that we have merit? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:2.6
Pali: idāni mayaṁ rajakattharaṇaṁ gantvā rajakabhaṇḍikaṁ avaharimhā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Just now we went to the dyers and stole their cloth.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:2.7
Pali: “Nanu, āvuso, bhagavatā sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “But hasn’t the Buddha laid down a training rule? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:2.8
Pali: Kissa tumhe, āvuso, rajakabhaṇḍikaṁ avaharitthā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): Why then do you steal the dyers’ cloth?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:2.9
Pali: “Saccaṁ, āvuso, bhagavatā sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true that the Buddha has laid down a training rule, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:2.10
Pali: Tañca kho gāme, no araññe”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): but it concerns inhabited areas, not the wilderness.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:2.11
Pali: “Nanu, āvuso, tathevetaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): “But that’s just the same. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:2.12
Pali: Ananucchavikaṁ, āvuso, ananulomikaṁ appatirūpaṁ assāmaṇakaṁ akappiyaṁ akaraṇīyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): It’s not suitable, it’s not proper, it’s not worthy of a monastic, it’s not allowable, it’s not to be done. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:2.13
Pali: Kathañhi nāma tumhe, āvuso, rajakabhaṇḍikaṁ avaharissatha. 
English (brahmali, published): How could you steal the dyers’ cloth? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:2.14
Pali: Netaṁ, āvuso, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya pasannānaṁ vā bhiyyobhāvāya; 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:2.15
Pali: atha khvetaṁ, āvuso, appasannānañceva appasādāya pasannānañca ekaccānaṁ aññathattāyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): and cause some to lose it.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:2.16
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū chabbaggiye bhikkhū anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking those monks in many ways, they told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:2.17
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe bhikkhusaṅghaṁ sannipātāpetvā chabbaggiye bhikkhū paṭipucchi—
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha had the Sangha of monks gathered and questioned those monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:2.18
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, rajakattharaṇaṁ gantvā rajakabhaṇḍikaṁ avaharitthā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:2.19
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:2.20
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā—
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:2.21
Pali: “ananucchavikaṁ, moghapurisā, ananulomikaṁ appaṭirūpaṁ assāmaṇakaṁ akappiyaṁ akaraṇīyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s not suitable, foolish men, it’s not proper, it’s not worthy of a monastic, it’s not allowable, it’s not to be done. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:2.22
Pali: Kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, rajakabhaṇḍikaṁ avaharissatha. 
English (brahmali, published): How could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:2.23
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya pasannānaṁ vā bhiyyobhāvāya; 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:2.24
Pali: atha khvetaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānañceva appasādāya pasannānañca ekaccānaṁ aññathattāyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): and cause some to lose it.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:2.25
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā chabbaggiye bhikkhū anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā dubbharatāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): Then, after rebuking the monks from the group of six in many ways, the Buddha spoke in dispraise of being difficult to support … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:2.26
Pali: vīriyārambhassa vaṇṇaṁ bhāsitvā bhikkhūnaṁ tadanucchavikaṁ tadanulomikaṁ dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi … pe 
English (brahmali, published): but he spoke in praise of … being energetic. Having given a teaching on what is right and proper, he addressed the monks … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:2.27
Pali: … evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:2.28.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu gāmā vā araññā vā adinnaṁ theyyasaṅkhātaṁ ādiyeyya, 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk, intending to steal, takes from an inhabited area or from the wilderness what has not been given to him—

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:2.29
Pali: yathārūpe adinnādāne rājāno coraṁ gahetvā haneyyuṁ vā bandheyyuṁ vā pabbājeyyuṁ vā—‘corosi bālosi mūḷhosi thenosī’ti, tathārūpaṁ bhikkhu adinnaṁ ādiyamāno 
English (brahmali, published): the sort of stealing for which kings, having caught a thief, would beat, imprison, or banish him, saying, “You’re a bandit, you’re a fool, you’ve gone astray, you’re a thief”—

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:2.30
Pali: ayampi pārājiko hoti asaṁvāso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): he too is expelled and excluded from the community.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.5
Pali: Gāmo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): An inhabited area: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.6
Pali: ekakuṭikopi gāmo, dvikuṭikopi gāmo, tikuṭikopi gāmo, catukuṭikopi gāmo, samanussopi gāmo, amanussopi gāmo, parikkhittopi gāmo, aparikkhittopi gāmo, gonisādiniviṭṭhopi gāmo, yopi sattho atirekacatumāsaniviṭṭho sopi vuccati gāmo. 
English (brahmali, published): an inhabited area of one hut, an inhabited area of two huts, an inhabited area of three huts, an inhabited area of four huts, an inhabited area with people, an inhabited area without people, an enclosed inhabited area, an unenclosed inhabited area, a disorganized inhabited area, and even a caravan settled for more than four months is called “an inhabited area”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.7
Pali: Gāmūpacāro nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The access to an inhabited area: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.8
Pali: parikkhittassa gāmassa indakhīle ṭhitassa majjhimassa purisassa leḍḍupāto, 
English (brahmali, published): of an enclosed inhabited area: a stone’s throw of a man of average height standing at the threshold of the gateway to the inhabited area; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.9
Pali: aparikkhittassa gāmassa gharūpacāre ṭhitassa majjhimassa purisassa leḍḍupāto. 
English (brahmali, published): of an unenclosed inhabited area: a stone’s throw of a man of average height standing at the access to a house. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.10
Pali: Araññaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The wilderness: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.11
Pali: ṭhapetvā gāmañca gāmūpacārañca avasesaṁ araññaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): apart from inhabited areas and the access to inhabited areas, the remainder is called “the wilderness”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.12
Pali: Adinnaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): What has not been given: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.13
Pali: yaṁ adinnaṁ anissaṭṭhaṁ apariccattaṁ rakkhitaṁ gopitaṁ mamāyitaṁ parapariggahitaṁ. Etaṁ adinnaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): what has not been given, what has not been let go of, what has not been relinquished; what is guarded, what is protected, what is regarded as “mine”, what belongs to someone else. This is called “what has not been given”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.14
Pali: Theyyasaṅkhātanti 
English (brahmali, published): Intending to steal: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.15
Pali: theyyacitto avaharaṇacitto. 
English (brahmali, published): the thought of theft, the thought of stealing. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.16
Pali: Ādiyeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Takes: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.17
Pali: ādiyeyya hareyya avahareyya iriyāpathaṁ vikopeyya ṭhānā cāveyya saṅketaṁ vītināmeyya. 
English (brahmali, published): takes, carries off, steals, interrupts the movement of, moves from its base, does not stick to an arrangement. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.18
Pali: Yathārūpaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The sort: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.19
Pali: pādaṁ vā pādārahaṁ vā atirekapādaṁ vā. 
English (brahmali, published): a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>pāda</i> coin, the value of a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>pāda</i>, or more than a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>pāda</i>. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.20
Pali: Rājāno nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Kings: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.21
Pali: pathabyārājā padesarājā maṇḍalikā antarabhogikā akkhadassā mahāmattā, ye vā pana chejjabhejjaṁ karontā anusāsanti. Ete rājāno nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): kings of the earth, kings of a region, rulers of islands, rulers of border areas, judges, government officials, or whoever metes out physical punishment—these are called “kings”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.22
Pali: Coro nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A thief: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.23
Pali: yo pañcamāsakaṁ vā atirekapañcamāsakaṁ vā agghanakaṁ adinnaṁ theyyasaṅkhātaṁ ādiyati. Eso coro nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): whoever, intending to steal, takes anything that has not been given, having a value of five <i lang='pi' translate='no'>māsaka</i> coins or more—he is called “a thief”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.24
Pali: Haneyyuṁ vāti 
English (brahmali, published): Would beat: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.25
Pali: hatthena vā pādena vā kasāya vā vettena vā aḍḍhadaṇḍakena vā chejjāya vā haneyyuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): would beat with the hand, the foot, a whip, a cane, a cudgel, or by mutilation. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.26
Pali: Bandheyyuṁ vāti 
English (brahmali, published): Would imprison: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.27
Pali: rajjubandhanena vā andubandhanena vā saṅkhalikabandhanena vā gharabandhanena vā nagarabandhanena vā gāmabandhanena vā nigamabandhanena vā bandheyyuṁ, purisaguttiṁ vā kareyyuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): would imprison by constricting with a rope, by constricting with shackles, by constricting with chains, by constricting to a house, by constricting to a city, by constricting to a village, by constricting to a town, or by guarding. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.28
Pali: Pabbājeyyuṁ vāti 
English (brahmali, published): Would banish: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.29
Pali: gāmā vā nigamā vā nagarā vā janapadā vā janapadapadesā vā pabbājeyyuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): would banish from a village, from a town, from a city, from a country, or from a district. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.30
Pali: Corosi bālosi mūḷhosi thenosīti 
English (brahmali, published): You’re a bandit, you’re a fool, you’ve gone astray, you’re a thief: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.31
Pali: paribhāso eso. 
English (brahmali, published): this is a rebuke. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.32
Pali: Tathārūpaṁ nāma 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.33
Pali: pādaṁ vā pādārahaṁ vā atirekapādaṁ vā. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.34
Pali: Ādiyamānoti 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.35
Pali: ādiyamāno haramāno avaharamāno iriyāpathaṁ vikopayamāno ṭhānā cāvayamāno saṅketaṁ vītināmayamāno. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.36
Pali: Ayampīti 
English (brahmali, published): He too: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.37
Pali: purimaṁ upādāya vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): this is said with reference to the preceding offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.38
Pali: Pārājiko hotīti 
English (brahmali, published): Is expelled: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.39
Pali: seyyathāpi nāma paṇḍupalāso bandhanā pavutto abhabbo haritatthāya; 
English (brahmali, published): just as a fallen, withered leaf is incapable of becoming green again, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.40
Pali: evameva bhikkhu pādaṁ vā pādārahaṁ vā atirekapādaṁ vā adinnaṁ theyyasaṅkhātaṁ ādiyitvā assamaṇo hoti asakyaputtiyo. Tena vuccati— “pārājiko hotī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): so too is a monk who, intending to steal, takes an ungiven <i lang='pi' translate='no'>pāda</i> coin, the value of a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>pāda</i>, or more than a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>pāda</i>, not an ascetic, not a Sakyan monastic. Therefore it is said, “he is expelled.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.41
Pali: Asaṁvāsoti 
English (brahmali, published): Excluded from the community: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.42
Pali: saṁvāso nāma ekakammaṁ ekuddeso samasikkhatā. Eso saṁvāso nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): Community: joint legal procedures, a joint recitation, the same training—this is called “community”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:3.43
Pali: So tena saddhiṁ natthi. Tena vuccati—“asaṁvāso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): He does not take part in this—therefore it is called “excluded from the community”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.1.1
Pali: Bhūmaṭṭhaṁ thalaṭṭhaṁ ākāsaṭṭhaṁ vehāsaṭṭhaṁ udakaṭṭhaṁ nāvaṭṭhaṁ yānaṭṭhaṁ bhāraṭṭhaṁ ārāmaṭṭhaṁ vihāraṭṭhaṁ khettaṭṭhaṁ vatthuṭṭhaṁ gāmaṭṭhaṁ araññaṭṭhaṁ udakaṁ dantapoṇaṁ vanappati haraṇakaṁ upanidhi suṅkaghātaṁ pāṇo apadaṁ dvipadaṁ catuppadaṁ bahuppadaṁ ocarako oṇirakkho saṁvidāvahāro saṅketakammaṁ nimittakammanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Being underground, being on the ground, being in the air, being above ground, being in water, being in a boat, being in a vehicle, carried as a load, being in a park, being in a monastic dwelling, being in a field, being on a site, being in an inhabited area, being in the wilderness, water, tooth cleaner, forest tree, that which is carried, that which is deposited, customs station, a living being, footless, two-footed, four-footed, many-footed, a spy, a keeper of entrusted property, mutually agreed stealing, acting by arrangement, making a sign. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.2.1
Pali: Bhūmaṭṭhaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Being underground: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.2.2
Pali: bhaṇḍaṁ bhūmiyaṁ nikkhittaṁ hoti nikhātaṁ paṭicchannaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): the goods have been placed underground, buried, concealed. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.2.3
Pali: Bhūmaṭṭhaṁ bhaṇḍaṁ avaharissāmīti theyyacitto dutiyaṁ vā pariyesati kudālaṁ vā piṭakaṁ vā pariyesati gacchati vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, intending to steal, he thinks, “I’ll steal the underground goods,” and he seeks for a companion, seeks for a spade or a basket, or goes there, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.2.4
Pali: Tattha jātakaṁ kaṭṭhaṁ vā lataṁ vā chindati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he breaks a twig or a creeper growing there, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.2.5
Pali: Tattha paṁsuṁ khaṇati vā byūhati vā uddharati vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he digs the soil or heaps it up or removes it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.2.6
Pali: Kumbhiṁ āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he touches the container, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.2.7
Pali: Phandāpeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes it stir, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.2.8
Pali: Ṭhānā cāveti, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he moves it from its base, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.2.9
Pali: Attano bhājanaṁ pavesetvā pañcamāsakaṁ vā atirekapañcamāsakaṁ vā agghanakaṁ theyyacitto āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, intending to steal, he puts his own vessel into the container and touches something worth five <i lang='pi' translate='no'>māsaka</i> coins or more, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.2.10
Pali: Phandāpeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes it stir, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.2.11
Pali: Attano bhājanagataṁ vā karoti muṭṭhiṁ vā chindati, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes it enter his own vessel or takes it with his fist, there is an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.2.12
Pali: Suttāruḷhaṁ bhaṇḍaṁ pāmaṅgaṁ vā kaṇṭhasuttakaṁ vā kaṭisuttakaṁ vā sāṭakaṁ vā veṭhanaṁ vā theyyacitto āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, intending to steal, he touches goods made of string—an ornamental hanging string, a necklace, an ornamental girdle, a wrap garment, or a turban—he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.2.13
Pali: Phandāpeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes it stir, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.2.14
Pali: Koṭiyaṁ gahetvā uccāreti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he grasps it at the top and pulls it, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.2.15
Pali: Ghaṁsanto nīharati, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he rubs it while lifting it, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.2.16
Pali: Antamaso kesaggamattampi kumbhimukhā moceti, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he removes the goods even as much as a hair’s breadth over the rim of the container, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.2.17
Pali: Sappiṁ vā telaṁ vā madhuṁ vā phāṇitaṁ vā pañcamāsakaṁ vā atirekapañcamāsakaṁ vā agghanakaṁ theyyacitto ekena payogena pivati, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, intending to steal, he drinks—in a single action—ghee, oil, honey, or syrup having a value of five <i lang='pi' translate='no'>māsaka</i> coins or more, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.2.18
Pali: Tattheva bhindati vā chaḍḍeti vā jhāpeti vā aparibhogaṁ vā karoti āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he destroys it, throws it away, burns it, or renders it useless, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.3.1
Pali: Thalaṭṭhaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Being on the ground: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.3.2
Pali: bhaṇḍaṁ thale nikkhittaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): the goods have been placed on the ground. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.3.3
Pali: Thalaṭṭhaṁ bhaṇḍaṁ avaharissāmīti theyyacitto dutiyaṁ vā pariyesati gacchati vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, intending to steal, he thinks, “I’ll steal the goods on the ground,” and he either searches for a companion or goes there, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.3.4
Pali: Āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he touches them, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.3.5
Pali: Phandāpeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes them stir, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.3.6
Pali: Ṭhānā cāveti, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he moves them from their base, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.4.1
Pali: Ākāsaṭṭhaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Being in the air: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.4.2
Pali: bhaṇḍaṁ ākāsagataṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): the goods are in the air—

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.4.3
Pali: Moro vā kapiñjaro vā tittiro vā vaṭṭako vā, sāṭakaṁ vā veṭhanaṁ vā hiraññaṁ vā suvaṇṇaṁ vā chijjamānaṁ patati. 
English (brahmali, published): a peacock, a partridge, or a quail; or a wrap garment or a turban; or money or gold that falls after being cut loose. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.4.4
Pali: Ākāsaṭṭhaṁ bhaṇḍaṁ avaharissāmīti theyyacitto dutiyaṁ vā pariyesati gacchati vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, intending to steal, he thinks, “I’ll steal the goods in the air,” and he either searches for a companion or goes there, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.4.5
Pali: Gamanaṁ upacchindati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he cuts off their course of movement, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.4.6
Pali: Āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he touches them, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.4.7
Pali: Phandāpeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes them stir, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.4.8
Pali: Ṭhānā cāveti, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he moves them from their base, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.5.1
Pali: Vehāsaṭṭhaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Being above ground: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.5.2
Pali: bhaṇḍaṁ vehāsagataṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): the goods are above ground—

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.5.3
Pali: Mañce vā pīṭhe vā cīvaravaṁse vā cīvararajjuyā vā bhittikhile vā nāgadante vā rukkhe vā laggitaṁ hoti, antamaso pattādhārakepi. 
English (brahmali, published): on a bed, on a bench, on a bamboo robe rack, on a clothesline, on a wall peg, in a tree, or even just on a bowl rest. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.5.4
Pali: Vehāsaṭṭhaṁ bhaṇḍaṁ avaharissāmīti theyyacitto dutiyaṁ vā pariyesati gacchati vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, intending to steal, he thinks, “I’ll steal the goods that are above ground,” and he either searches for a companion or goes there, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.5.5
Pali: Āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he touches them, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.5.6
Pali: Phandāpeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes them stir, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.5.7
Pali: Ṭhānā cāveti, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he moves them from their base, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.6.1
Pali: Udakaṭṭhaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Being in the water: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.6.2
Pali: bhaṇḍaṁ udake nikkhittaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): the goods have been placed in water. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.6.3
Pali: Udakaṭṭhaṁ bhaṇḍaṁ avaharissāmīti theyyacitto dutiyaṁ vā pariyesati gacchati vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, intending to steal, he thinks, “I’ll steal the goods in the water,” and he either searches for a companion or goes there, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.6.4
Pali: Nimujjati vā ummujjati vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he either dives into the water or floats on the surface, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.6.5
Pali: Āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he touches the goods, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.6.6
Pali: Phandāpeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes them stir, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.6.7
Pali: Ṭhānā cāveti, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he moves them from their base, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.6.8
Pali: Tattha jātakaṁ uppalaṁ vā padumaṁ vā puṇḍarīkaṁ vā bhisaṁ vā macchaṁ vā kacchapaṁ vā pañcamāsakaṁ vā atirekapañcamāsakaṁ vā agghanakaṁ theyyacitto āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, intending to steal, he touches either a red or white lotus or a blue water lily growing there, or a lotus root, or a fish, or a turtle having a value of five <i lang='pi' translate='no'>māsaka</i> coins or more, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.6.9
Pali: Phandāpeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes them stir, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.6.10
Pali: Ṭhānā cāveti, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he moves them from their base, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.7.1
Pali: Nāvā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A boat: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.7.2
Pali: yāya tarati. 
English (brahmali, published): that by means of which one crosses. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.7.3
Pali: Nāvaṭṭhaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Being in a boat: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.7.4
Pali: bhaṇḍaṁ nāvāya nikkhittaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): the goods have been placed in a boat. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.7.5
Pali: “Nāvaṭṭhaṁ bhaṇḍaṁ avaharissāmī”ti theyyacitto dutiyaṁ vā pariyesati gacchati vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, intending to steal, he thinks, “I’ll steal the goods in the boat,” and he either searches for a companion or goes there, he commits an offense of wrong conduct.

Pali: Phandāpeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes them stir, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.7.8
Pali: Ṭhānā cāveti, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he moves them from their base, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.7.9
Pali: Nāvaṁ avaharissāmīti theyyacitto dutiyaṁ vā pariyesati gacchati vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, intending to steal, he thinks, “I’ll steal the boat,” and he either searches for a companion or goes there, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.7.10
Pali: Āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he touches it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.7.11
Pali: Phandāpeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes it stir, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.7.12
Pali: Bandhanaṁ moceti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he loosens the moorings, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.7.13
Pali: Bandhanaṁ mocetvā āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, after loosening the moorings, he touches it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.7.14
Pali: Phandāpeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes it stir, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.7.15
Pali: Uddhaṁ vā adho vā tiriyaṁ vā antamaso kesaggamattampi saṅkāmeti, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes it move upstream or downstream or across the water, even as much as a hair’s breadth, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.8.1
Pali: Yānaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A vehicle:

Pali: vayhaṁ ratho sakaṭaṁ sandamānikā.

English (brahmali, published): If he moves it from its base, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.9.1
Pali: Bhāro nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A load: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.9.2
Pali: sīsabhāro khandhabhāro kaṭibhāro olambako. 
English (brahmali, published): a load carried on the head, a load carried on the shoulder, a load carried on the hip, one hanging down. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.9.3
Pali: Sīse bhāraṁ theyyacitto āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, intending to steal, he touches the load on the head, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.9.4
Pali: Phandāpeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes it stir, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.9.5
Pali: Khandhaṁ oropeti, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he lowers it to the shoulder, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.9.6
Pali: Khandhe bhāraṁ theyyacitto āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, intending to steal, he touches the load on the shoulder, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.9.7
Pali: Phandāpeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes it stir, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.9.8
Pali: Kaṭiṁ oropeti, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he lowers it to the hip, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.9.9
Pali: Kaṭiyā bhāraṁ theyyacitto āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, intending to steal, he touches the load on the hip, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.9.10
Pali: Phandāpeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he causes it to stir, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.9.11
Pali: Hatthena gaṇhāti, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he takes it with the hand, there is an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.9.12
Pali: Hatthe bhāraṁ theyyacitto bhūmiyaṁ nikkhipati, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, intending to steal a load in the hand, he places it on the ground, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.9.13
Pali: Theyyacitto bhūmito gaṇhāti, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, intending to steal, he picks it up from the ground, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.10.1
Pali: Ārāmo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A park: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.10.2
Pali: pupphārāmo phalārāmo. 
English (brahmali, published): a garden, an orchard. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.10.3
Pali: Ārāmaṭṭhaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Being in a park: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.10.4
Pali: bhaṇḍaṁ ārāme catūhi ṭhānehi nikkhittaṁ hoti— bhūmaṭṭhaṁ thalaṭṭhaṁ, ākāsaṭṭhaṁ, vehāsaṭṭhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): the goods have been placed in a park in four locations: underground, on the ground, in the air, above the ground. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.10.5
Pali: Ārāmaṭṭhaṁ bhaṇḍaṁ avaharissāmīti theyyacitto dutiyaṁ vā pariyesati gacchati vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, intending to steal, he thinks, “I’ll steal the goods in the park,” and he either searches for a companion or goes there, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.10.6
Pali: Āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he touches them, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.10.7
Pali: Phandāpeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes them stir, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.10.8
Pali: Ṭhānā cāveti, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he moves them from their base, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.10.9
Pali: Tattha jātakaṁ mūlaṁ vā tacaṁ vā pattaṁ vā pupphaṁ vā phalaṁ vā pañcamāsakaṁ vā atirekapañcamāsakaṁ vā agghanakaṁ theyyacitto āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, intending to steal, he touches something growing there—a root, a piece of bark, a leaf, a flower, or a fruit—having a value of five <i lang='pi' translate='no'>māsaka</i> coins or more, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.10.10
Pali: Phandāpeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes it stir, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.10.11
Pali: Ṭhānā cāveti, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he moves it from its base, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.10.12
Pali: Ārāmaṁ abhiyuñjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he claims the park, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.10.13
Pali: Sāmikassa vimatiṁ uppādeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he evokes doubt in the owner as to his ownership, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.10.14
Pali: Sāmiko na mayhaṁ bhavissatīti dhuraṁ nikkhipati, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the owner thinks, “I won’t get it back,” and he gives up the effort of reclaiming it, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.10.15
Pali: Dhammaṁ caranto sāmikaṁ parājeti, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he resorts to the law and defeats the owner, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.10.16
Pali: Dhammaṁ caranto parajjati, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he resorts to the law but is defeated, he commits a serious offense.

English (brahmali, published): Being in a monastic dwelling:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.12.1
Pali: Khettaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A field: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.12.2
Pali: yattha pubbaṇṇaṁ vā aparaṇṇaṁ vā jāyati. 
English (brahmali, published): where grain or vegetables grow. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.12.3
Pali: Khettaṭṭhaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Being in a field: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.12.4

English (brahmali, published): If, intending to steal, he thinks, “I’ll steal the goods in the field,” and he either searches for a companion or goes there, he commits an offense of wrong conduct.

Pali: Āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he touches them, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.12.7
Pali: Phandāpeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes them stir, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.12.8
Pali: Ṭhānā cāveti, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he moves them from their base, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.12.9
Pali: Tattha jātakaṁ pubbaṇṇaṁ vā aparaṇṇaṁ vā pañcamāsakaṁ vā atirekapañcamāsakaṁ vā agghanakaṁ theyyacitto āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, intending to steal, he touches grain or vegetables that grow there, having a value of five <i lang='pi' translate='no'>māsaka</i> coins or more, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.12.10
Pali: Phandāpeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes them stir, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.12.11
Pali: Ṭhānā cāveti, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he moves them from their base, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.12.12
Pali: Khettaṁ abhiyuñjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he claims the field, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.12.13
Pali: Sāmikassa vimatiṁ uppādeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he evokes doubt in the owner as to his ownership, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.12.14
Pali: Sāmiko na mayhaṁ bhavissatīti dhuraṁ nikkhipati, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the owner thinks, “I won’t get it back,” and he gives up the effort of reclaiming it, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.12.15
Pali: Dhammaṁ caranto sāmikaṁ parājeti, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he resorts to the law and defeats the owner, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.12.16
Pali: Dhammaṁ caranto parajjati, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he resorts to the law but is defeated, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.12.17
Pali: Khīlaṁ vā rajjuṁ vā vatiṁ vā mariyādaṁ vā saṅkāmeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he moves a post, a rope, a fence, or a boundary, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.12.18
Pali: Ekaṁ payogaṁ anāgate, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When one action of the moving remains, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.12.19
Pali: Tasmiṁ payoge āgate, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When the last action of the moving is completed, he commits an offense entailing expulsion.

English (brahmali, published): Being on a site:

English (brahmali, published): the goods have been placed on a site in four locations: underground, on the ground, in the air, above the ground.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.16.1
Pali: Udakaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Water: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.16.2
Pali: bhājanagataṁ vā hoti pokkharaṇiyā vā taḷāke vā. 
English (brahmali, published): in a vessel, in a pond, or in a reservoir. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.16.3
Pali: Theyyacitto āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, intending to steal, he touches it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.16.4
Pali: Phandāpeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes it stir, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.16.5
Pali: Ṭhānā cāveti, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he moves it from its base, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.16.6
Pali: Attano bhājanaṁ pavesetvā pañcamāsakaṁ vā atirekapañcamāsakaṁ vā agghanakaṁ udakaṁ theyyacitto āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, intending to steal, he puts his own vessel into the container holding the water, and he touches water having a value of five <i lang='pi' translate='no'>māsaka</i> coins or more, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.16.7
Pali: Phandāpeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes it stir, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.16.8
Pali: Attano bhājanagataṁ karoti, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he puts it into his own vessel, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.16.9
Pali: Mariyādaṁ bhindati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he breaks the embankment, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.16.10
Pali: Mariyādaṁ bhinditvā pañcamāsakaṁ vā atirekapañcamāsakaṁ vā agghanakaṁ udakaṁ nikkhāmeti, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, after breaking the embankment, he allows water to escape that has a value of five <i lang='pi' translate='no'>māsaka</i> coins or more, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.16.11
Pali: Atirekamāsakaṁ vā ūnapañcamāsakaṁ vā agghanakaṁ udakaṁ nikkhāmeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he allows water to escape that has a value of more than one <i lang='pi' translate='no'>māsaka</i> but less than five <i lang='pi' translate='no'>māsakas</i>, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.16.12
Pali: Māsakaṁ vā ūnamāsakaṁ vā agghanakaṁ udakaṁ nikkhāmeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he allows water to escape that has a value of one <i lang='pi' translate='no'>māsaka</i> or less, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.17.1

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.17.2

Pali: Vanappati nāma

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.18.2

English (brahmali, published): whatever useful tree is owned by people.

English (brahmali, published): If, intending to steal, he fells it, then for each blow he commits an offense of wrong conduct.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.19.1

English (brahmali, published): Goods being carried: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.19.2
Pali: aññassa haraṇakaṁ bhaṇḍaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): the goods of another are being carried. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.19.3

English (brahmali, published): If, intending to steal, he touches them, he commits an offense of wrong conduct.

English (brahmali, published): If he makes them stir, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.19.5

English (brahmali, published): If he moves them from their base, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.19.6
Pali: Sahabhaṇḍahārakaṁ padasā nessāmīti paṭhamaṁ pādaṁ saṅkāmeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he thinks, “Together with the carrier I’ll carry off the goods,” and he makes the carrier move one foot, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.19.7

English (brahmali, published): If he thinks, “I’ll take the fallen goods,” and he makes them fall, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.19.9

English (brahmali, published): If, intending to steal, he touches fallen goods having a value of five <i lang='pi' translate='no'>māsaka</i> coins or more, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.19.10
Pali: Phandāpeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes them stir, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.19.11
Pali: Ṭhānā cāveti, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he moves them from their base, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.20.1
Pali: Upanidhi nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Deposit: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.20.2
Pali: upanikkhittaṁ bhaṇḍaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): goods deposited with a monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.20.3
Pali: Dehi me bhaṇḍanti vuccamāno nāhaṁ gaṇhāmīti bhaṇati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the monk is told, “Give me my goods,” and he says, “I won’t get them for you,” he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.20.4
Pali: Sāmikassa vimatiṁ uppādeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he evokes doubt in the mind of the owner as to whether he will get them back, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.20.5
Pali: Sāmiko na mayhaṁ dassatīti dhuraṁ nikkhipati, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the owner thinks, “He won’t give them to me,” and he gives up the effort of getting them back, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.20.6
Pali: Dhammaṁ caranto sāmikaṁ parājeti, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he resorts to the law and defeats the owner, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.20.7
Pali: Dhammaṁ caranto parajjati, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he resorts to the law but is defeated, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.21.1
Pali: Suṅkaghātaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Customs station: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.21.2
Pali: raññā ṭhapitaṁ hoti pabbatakhaṇḍe vā nadītitthe vā gāmadvāre vā—
English (brahmali, published): it is established by a king in a mountain-pass, at a ford in a river, or at the gateway of a village 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.21.3
Pali: “atra paviṭṭhassa suṅkaṁ gaṇhantū”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): so that tax can be collected from any person passing through. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.21.4
Pali: Tatra pavisitvā rājaggaṁ bhaṇḍaṁ pañcamāsakaṁ vā atirekapañcamāsakaṁ vā agghanakaṁ theyyacitto āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, intending to steal and having entered the customs station, he touches goods that have a tax value to the king of five <i lang='pi' translate='no'>māsaka</i> coins or more, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.21.5
Pali: Phandāpeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes them stir, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.21.6
Pali: Paṭhamaṁ pādaṁ suṅkaghātaṁ atikkāmeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he goes beyond the customs station with one foot, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.21.7
Pali: Dutiyaṁ pādaṁ atikkāmeti, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he goes beyond the customs station with the second foot, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.21.8
Pali: Antosuṅkaghāte ṭhito bahisuṅkaghātaṁ pāteti, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, standing within the customs station, he makes the goods fall outside the customs station, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.21.9
Pali: Suṅkaṁ pariharati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he avoids the customs station altogether, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.22.1
Pali: Pāṇo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Creature: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.22.2
Pali: manussapāṇo vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): a human being is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.22.3
Pali: Theyyacitto āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, intending to steal, he touches the person, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.22.4
Pali: Phandāpeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes the person stir, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.22.5
Pali: Ṭhānā cāveti, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he moves the person from their base, he commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.22.6
Pali: Padasā nessāmīti paṭhamaṁ pādaṁ saṅkāmeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he thinks, “I’ll take the person away on foot,” and he makes them move the first foot, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.22.7
Pali: Dutiyaṁ pādaṁ saṅkāmeti, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he makes them move the second foot, he commits an offense entailing expulsion.

Pali: Apadaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Footless: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.23.2
Pali: ahi macchā. 
English (brahmali, published): snakes and fish. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.23.3

Pali: Saṁvidāvahāro nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Mutually agreed stealing:

English (brahmali, published): a number have agreed together.

English (brahmali, published): If only one steals the goods, there is an offense entailing expulsion for all of them. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.30.1
Pali: Saṅketakammaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Acting by arrangement:

English (brahmali, published): one makes an arrangement for before the meal or for after the meal, for the night or for the day. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.30.3
Pali: tena saṅketena taṁ bhaṇḍaṁ avaharā”ti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he says, “Steal those goods according to this arrangement,” he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.30.4
Pali: Tena saṅketena taṁ bhaṇḍaṁ avaharati, āpatti ubhinnaṁ pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other steals those goods according to that arrangement, there is an offense entailing expulsion for both. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.30.5
Pali: Taṁ saṅketaṁ pure vā pacchā vā taṁ bhaṇḍaṁ avaharati, mūlaṭṭhassa anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he steals those goods before or after the time of the arrangement, there is no offense for the instigator, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.30.6
Pali: Avahārakassa āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): but an offense entailing expulsion for the thief. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.31.1
Pali: Nimittakammaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Making a sign: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.31.2
Pali: nimittaṁ karoti. 
English (brahmali, published): he makes a sign. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.31.3
Pali: Akkhiṁ vā nikhaṇissāmi bhamukaṁ vā ukkhipissāmi sīsaṁ vā ukkhipissāmi, tena nimittena taṁ bhaṇḍaṁ avaharāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he says, “When I wink, at that sign steal the goods,” or, “When I raise an eyebrow, at that sign steal the goods,” or, “When I nod, at that sign steal the goods,” he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.31.4
Pali: Tena nimittena taṁ bhaṇḍaṁ avaharati, āpatti ubhinnaṁ pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, at that sign, the other steals the goods, there is an offense entailing expulsion for both.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:4.31.6
Pali: Avahārakassa āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): but an offense entailing expulsion for the thief. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:5.1.1

English (brahmali, published): If a monk tells a monk,

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:5.2.1
Pali: Bhikkhu bhikkhuṁ āṇāpeti—
English (brahmali, published): If a monk tells a monk, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:5.2.2
Pali: “itthannāmassa pāvada—‘itthannāmo itthannāmassa pāvadatu—itthannāmo itthannāmaṁ bhaṇḍaṁ avaharatū’”ti, 
English (brahmali, published): “Tell so-and-so to tell so-and-so to steal such-and-such goods,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:5.2.3
Pali: āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:5.2.4
Pali: So itarassa āroceti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): In telling the next person, there is an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:5.2.5
Pali: Avahārako paṭiggaṇhāti, mūlaṭṭhassa āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the potential thief agrees, there is a serious offense for the instigator. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:5.2.6
Pali: So taṁ bhaṇḍaṁ avaharati, āpatti sabbesaṁ pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he steals those goods, there is an offense entailing expulsion for all of them.

Pali: Bhikkhu bhikkhuṁ āṇāpeti—
English (brahmali, published): If a monk tells a monk, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:5.2.8

English (brahmali, published): “Tell so-and-so to tell so-and-so to steal such-and-such goods,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:5.2.9
Pali: āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:5.2.10
Pali: So aññaṁ āṇāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other monk tells someone else than the one he was told to tell, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:5.2.11
Pali: Avahārako paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the potential thief agrees, there is an offense of wrong conduct.

Pali: Āṇāpakassa ca avahārakassa ca āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): but there is an offense entailing expulsion for the messenger and for the thief. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:5.3.1

English (brahmali, published): If a monk tells another monk,

English (brahmali, published): If the other monk goes, but returns, saying,

Pali: āpatti ubhinnaṁ pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): there is an offense entailing expulsion for both.

Pali: “itthannāmaṁ bhaṇḍaṁ avaharā”ti, 
English (brahmali, published): “Steal such-and-such goods,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:5.4.18
Pali: āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:5.4.19
Pali: So āṇāpetvā vippaṭisārī sāveti—
English (brahmali, published): He then regrets it and says, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:5.4.20
Pali: “mā avaharī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Don’t steal them.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:5.4.21
Pali: So “sādhū”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): If the other monk replies, “Fine,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:5.4.22
Pali: Oramati, ubhinnaṁ anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): and desists, there is no offense for either. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.1.1
Pali: Pañcahi ākārehi adinnaṁ ādiyantassa āpatti pārājikassa—
English (brahmali, published): For one who steals there is an offense entailing expulsion when five factors are fulfilled: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.1.2
Pali: parapariggahitañca hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): it is the possession of another; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.1.3
Pali: parapariggahitasaññī ca, 
English (brahmali, published): he perceives it as such; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.1.4
Pali: garuko ca hoti parikkhāro, pañcamāsako vā atirekapañcamāsako vā,. 
English (brahmali, published): it is a valuable possession worth five <i lang='pi' translate='no'>māsaka</i> coins or more; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.1.5
Pali: theyyacittañca paccupaṭṭhitaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): he has the intention to steal it; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.1.6
Pali: Āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he touches it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.1.7
Pali: Phandāpeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he makes it stir, he commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.1.8
Pali: Ṭhānā cāveti, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he moves it from its base, he commits an offense entailing expulsion.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.2
Pali: sasaññissa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he perceives it as his own; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.3
Pali: vissāsaggāhe, 
English (brahmali, published): if he takes it on trust; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.4
Pali: tāvakālike, 
English (brahmali, published): if he borrows it; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.5
Pali: petapariggahe, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is the possession of a ghost; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.6
Pali: tiracchānagatapariggahe, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is the possession of an animal; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.7
Pali: paṁsukūlasaññissa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he perceives it as discarded; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.8
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.9
Pali: khittacittassa 
English (brahmali, published): if he is deranged; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.10
Pali: vedanāṭṭassa 
English (brahmali, published): if he is overwhelmed by pain; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.11
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.12
Pali: Adinnādānamhi paṭhamabhāṇavāro niṭṭhito. 
English (brahmali, published): The first section for recitation on stealing is finished.

English (brahmali, published): “Five are told with dyers,

English (brahmali, published): Five are told with ways of speaking,

Pali: vātehi apare duve; 
English (brahmali, published): Another two with wind; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.20

English (brahmali, published): Fresh, drawing lots, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.21
Pali: jantaggena sahā dasa. 
English (brahmali, published): With the sauna it is ten. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.22
Pali: Vighāsehi pañca akkhātā, 
English (brahmali, published): Five are told with animal kills, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.23
Pali: pañca ceva amūlakā; 
English (brahmali, published): And five on without proper reason; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.24
Pali: Dubbhikkhe kuramaṁsañca, 
English (brahmali, published): Cooked rice during a shortage of food, and meat,

Pali: pūvasakkhalimodakā.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.26

English (brahmali, published): Six on requisites, and bag,

Pali: bhisivaṁsā na nikkhame; 
English (brahmali, published): Mattress, bamboo, on not coming out; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.28

English (brahmali, published): And taking fresh food on trust, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.29
Pali: sasaññāyapare duve. 
English (brahmali, published): Another two on perceiving as one’s own.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.36

English (brahmali, published): And pigs, deer, fish, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.37
Pali: yānañcāpi pavattayi. 
English (brahmali, published): And he set a vehicle in motion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.38
Pali: Duve pesī duve dārū, 
English (brahmali, published): Two on a piece, two on wood, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.39
Pali: paṁsukūlaṁ duve dakā; 
English (brahmali, published): Discarded, two on water; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.40
Pali: Anupubbavidhānena, 
English (brahmali, published): Step by step, by arrangement, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.41
Pali: tadañño na paripūrayi. 
English (brahmali, published): Another did not amount to it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.42

English (brahmali, published): Four handfuls at Sāvatthī,

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.46

English (brahmali, published): Wood, water, clay, two on grass, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.47
Pali: Saṅghassa satta avahāsi seyyaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): Seven on stealing the Sangha’s bedding; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.48
Pali: Sassāmikaṁ na cāpi nīhareyya, 
English (brahmali, published): And one should not take away what has an owner, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:6.4.49
Pali: Hareyya sassāmikaṁ tāvakālikaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): One may borrow what has an owner.

English (brahmali, published): Campā, and in Rājagaha,

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.1.0
Pali: 2.2. Vinītavatthu 
English (brahmali, published): Case studies 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.1.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū rajakattharaṇaṁ gantvā rajakabhaṇḍikaṁ avahariṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion the monks from the group of six went to the dyers and stole their collection of cloth. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.1.2
Pali: Tesaṁ kukkuccaṁ ahosi—
English (brahmali, published): They became anxious, thinking, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.1.3
Pali: “bhagavatā sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “The Buddha has laid down a training rule. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.1.4
Pali: Kacci nu kho mayaṁ pārājikaṁ āpattiṁ āpannā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Could it be that we’ve committed an offense entailing expulsion?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.1.5
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.1.6
Pali: “Āpattiṁ tumhe, bhikkhave, āpannā pārājikan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, you have committed an offense entailing expulsion.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu rajakattharaṇaṁ gantvā mahagghaṁ dussaṁ passitvā theyyacittaṁ uppādesi. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion a monk went to the dyers, saw a valuable cloth, and had the intention to steal it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.2.2
Pali: Tassa kukkuccaṁ ahosi—
English (brahmali, published): He became anxious … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.2.3
Pali: “bhagavatā sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): “The Buddha has laid down a training rule. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.2.4
Pali: kacci nu kho ahaṁ pārājikaṁ āpattiṁ āpanno”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): Could it be that I’ve committed an offense entailing expulsion?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.2.5
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): He told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.2.6
Pali: “Anāpatti, bhikkhu, cittuppāde”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “There’s no offense for the arising of a thought.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.2.7
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu rajakattharaṇaṁ gantvā mahagghaṁ dussaṁ passitvā theyyacitto āmasi. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion a monk went to the dyers, saw a valuable cloth, and touched it, intending to steal it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.2.8
Pali: Tassa kukkuccaṁ ahosi …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): He became anxious … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.2.9
Pali: “anāpatti, bhikkhu, pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): “There’s no offense entailing expulsion, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.2.10
Pali: Āpatti dukkaṭassā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): but there’s an offense of wrong conduct.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.2.11
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu rajakattharaṇaṁ gantvā mahagghaṁ dussaṁ passitvā theyyacitto phandāpesi. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion a monk went to the dyers, saw a valuable cloth, and made it stir, intending to steal it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.2.12
Pali: Tassa kukkuccaṁ ahosi …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): He became anxious … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.2.13
Pali: “anāpatti, bhikkhu, pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): “There’s no offense entailing expulsion, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.2.14
Pali: Āpatti thullaccayassā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): but there’s a serious offense.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.2.15
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu rajakattharaṇaṁ gantvā mahagghaṁ dussaṁ passitvā theyyacitto ṭhānā cāvesi.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.2.16
Pali: Tassa kukkuccaṁ ahosi …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): He became anxious … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.2.17

English (brahmali, published): “You have committed an offense entailing expulsion.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.8.1

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.8.2

English (brahmali, published): The ghost was still dwelling in that body, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.8.3

English (brahmali, published): and it said to the monk,

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.8.6

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.8.9

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.21.1

Pali: tālapakkacorakā … 
English (brahmali, published): palm-fruit thieves … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.21.16
Pali: ucchucorakā … 
English (brahmali, published): sugarcane thieves … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.21.17
Pali: timbarūsakacorakā timbarūsake uccinitvā bhaṇḍikaṁ ādāya agamaṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): gaub-fruit thieves picked some fruit, collected them in a bundle, and left. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.21.18
Pali: Sāmikā te corake anubandhiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): The owners pursued them. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.21.19
Pali: Corakā sāmike passitvā bhaṇḍikaṁ pātetvā palāyiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): When they saw the owners, the thieves dropped the bundle and ran away. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.21.20
Pali: Bhikkhū paṁsukūlasaññino paṭiggahāpetvā paribhuñjiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): Some monks perceived those gaub fruits as discarded, had them offered, and ate them. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.21.21
Pali: Sāmikā te bhikkhū codesuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): But the owners accused them, saying, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.21.22

English (brahmali, published): “You’re not monastics anymore!” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.21.23
Pali: Tesaṁ kukkuccaṁ ahosi …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): They became anxious … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.21.24
Pali: “anāpatti, bhikkhave, paṁsukūlasaññissā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “There’s no offense for one who perceives something as discarded.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.22.1

English (brahmali, published): “You have committed an offense entailing expulsion.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.25.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu gāmakaṁ gacchanto aññataraṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion a certain monk who was going to the village said to another monk, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.25.2
Pali: “āvuso, tuyhaṁ upaṭṭhākakulaṁ vutto vajjemī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I can take a message to the family that supports you.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.25.3
Pali: So gantvā ekaṁ sāṭakaṁ āharāpetvā attanā paribhuñji. 
English (brahmali, published): He went there and brought back a wrap garment that he used himself.

English (brahmali, published): “There’s no offense entailing expulsion.

Pali: Na ca, bhikkhave, vutto vajjemīti vattabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): But you shouldn’t say, ‘I can take a message.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.25.9
Pali: Yo vadeyya, āpatti dukkaṭassā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If you do, you commit an offense of wrong conduct.”

Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu gāmakaṁ gacchati. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion a certain monk was going to the village.

English (brahmali, published): Another monk said to him,

English (brahmali, published): He replied, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.25.23

English (brahmali, published): “There’s no offense entailing expulsion.

Pali: Yo vadeyya, āpatti dukkaṭassā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If you do, you commit an offense of wrong conduct.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.26.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro puriso mahagghaṁ maṇiṁ ādāya aññatarena bhikkhunā saddhiṁ addhānamaggappaṭipanno hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time a man who was traveling with a monk was carrying a valuable gem. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.26.2
Pali: Atha kho so puriso suṅkaṭṭhānaṁ passitvā tassa bhikkhuno ajānantassa thavikāya maṇiṁ pakkhipitvā suṅkaṭṭhānaṁ atikkamitvā aggahesi. 
English (brahmali, published): When the man saw a customs station, he put the gem into the monk’s bag without his knowing. When they had gone past the customs station, he retrieved it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.26.3
Pali: Tassa kukkuccaṁ ahosi …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The monk was anxious … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.26.4
Pali: “kiṁcitto tvaṁ, bhikkhū”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What were you thinking, monk?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.26.5
Pali: “Nāhaṁ, bhagavā, jānāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I didn’t know, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.26.6
Pali: “Anāpatti, bhikkhu, ajānantassā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “There’s no offense for one who doesn’t know.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.26.7
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro puriso mahagghaṁ maṇiṁ ādāya aññatarena bhikkhunā saddhiṁ addhānamaggappaṭipanno hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time a man who was traveling with a monk was carrying a valuable gem. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.26.8
Pali: Atha kho so puriso suṅkaṭṭhānaṁ passitvā gilānālayaṁ karitvā attano bhaṇḍikaṁ tassa bhikkhuno adāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): When the man saw a customs station, he pretended to be sick, and gave his own bag to the monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.26.9
Pali: Atha kho so puriso suṅkaṭṭhānaṁ atikkamitvā taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): When they had passed the customs station, he said to the monk, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.26.10
Pali: “āhara me, bhante, bhaṇḍikaṁ; nāhaṁ akallako”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please give me my bag, sir, I’m not sick.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.26.11
Pali: “Kissa pana tvaṁ, āvuso, evarūpaṁ akāsī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Then why did you say so?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.26.12
Pali: Atha kho so puriso tassa bhikkhuno etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): The man told the monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.26.13
Pali: Tassa kukkuccaṁ ahosi …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): He became anxious … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.26.14
Pali: “kiṁcitto tvaṁ, bhikkhū”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What were you thinking, monk?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.26.15
Pali: “Nāhaṁ, bhagavā, jānāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I didn’t know, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.26.16
Pali: “Anāpatti, bhikkhu, ajānantassā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “There’s no offense for one who doesn’t know.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.26.17
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu satthena saddhiṁ addhānamaggappaṭipanno hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time a monk was traveling with a group. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.26.18
Pali: Aññataro puriso taṁ bhikkhuṁ āmisena upalāpetvā suṅkaṭṭhānaṁ passitvā mahagghaṁ maṇiṁ tassa bhikkhuno adāsi—
English (brahmali, published): A man bribed that monk by giving him food. Seeing a customs station, he gave the monk a valuable gem, saying,

English (brahmali, published): “You have committed an offense entailing expulsion.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.27.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu pāse bandhaṁ sūkaraṁ kāruññena muñci.

English (brahmali, published): “There’s no offense for one who is motivated by compassion.”

Pali: Tassa kukkuccaṁ ahosi …pe…

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.46.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena vesāliyaṁ āyasmato ajjukassa upaṭṭhākassa gahapatino dve dārakā honti— putto ca bhāgineyyo ca.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.46.2
Pali: Atha kho so gahapati āyasmantaṁ ajjukaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): He said to Ajjuka, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.46.3
Pali: “imaṁ, bhante, okāsaṁ yo imesaṁ dvinnaṁ dārakānaṁ saddho hoti pasanno tassa ācikkheyyāsī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, please assign my property to the one of these two boys who has faith and confidence.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.46.4
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena tassa gahapatino bhāgineyyo saddho hoti pasanno. Atha kho āyasmā ajjuko taṁ okāsaṁ tassa dārakassa ācikkhi. 
English (brahmali, published): It turned out that the householder’s nephew had faith and confidence, and so Ajjuka assigned the property to him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.46.5
Pali: So tena sāpateyyena kuṭumbañca saṇṭhapesi dānañca paṭṭhapesi.

English (brahmali, published): “Who is the father’s heir, Venerable Ānanda, the son or the nephew?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.46.8
Pali: “Putto kho, āvuso, pituno dāyajjo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “The son is the father’s heir.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.47.1

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.48.1

Pali: “Anāpatti, bhikkhave, adinnādāne pārājikassa; 
English (brahmali, published): “There’s no offense entailing expulsion for stealing, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.48.13
Pali: āpatti methunadhammasamāyoge pārājikassā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): but there’s an offense entailing expulsion for having sexual intercourse.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.49.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sāgalāyaṁ āyasmato daḷhikassa saddhivihāriko bhikkhu anabhiratiyā pīḷito āpaṇikassa veṭhanaṁ avaharitvā āyasmantaṁ daḷhikaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): At that time a monk at Sāgalā who was a student of Venerable Daḷhika was plagued by lust. He stole a turban from a shopkeeper and said to Daḷhika, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.49.2
Pali: “assamaṇo ahaṁ, bhante, vibbhamissāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, I’m not a monastic anymore. I’ll disrobe.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.49.3
Pali: “Kiṁ tayā, āvuso, katan”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “But what have you done?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.49.4
Pali: So tamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): He told him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.49.5
Pali: Āharāpetvā agghāpesi. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Daḷhika had the turban brought and valued. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.49.6
Pali: Taṁ agghāpentaṁ na pañcamāsake agghati. 
English (brahmali, published): It was worth less than five <i lang='pi' translate='no'>māsaka</i> coins. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.49.7
Pali: “Anāpatti, āvuso, pārājikassā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Saying, “There’s no offense entailing expulsion,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.49.8
Pali: Dhammakathaṁ akāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): he gave a teaching. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.49.9
Pali: So bhikkhu abhiramatīti. 
English (brahmali, published): And that monk was delighted. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj2:7.49.10
Pali: Dutiyapārājikaṁ samattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The second offense entailing expulsion is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj3:1.1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying in the house with the peaked roof in the Great Wood near Vesālī. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj3:1.1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā bhikkhūnaṁ anekapariyāyena asubhakathaṁ katheti, asubhāya vaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, asubhabhāvanāya vaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, ādissa ādissa asubhasamāpattiyā vaṇṇaṁ bhāsati. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the Buddha spoke to the monks in many ways about unattractiveness—he spoke in praise of unattractiveness, of developing the mind in unattractiveness, and of the attainment of unattractiveness. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj3:1.1.3
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha then addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj3:1.1.4
Pali: “icchāmahaṁ, bhikkhave, addhamāsaṁ paṭisallīyituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I wish to go into solitary retreat for half a month. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj3:1.1.5
Pali: Namhi kenaci upasaṅkamitabbo, aññatra ekena piṇḍapātanīhārakenā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): No-one should visit me except the one who brings me almsfood.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj3:1.1.6
Pali: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti, kho te bhikkhū bhagavato paṭissuṇitvā nāssudha koci bhagavantaṁ upasaṅkamati, aññatra ekena piṇḍapātanīhārakena. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes, venerable sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj3:1.1.7
Pali: Bhikkhū—“bhagavā kho anekapariyāyena asubhakathaṁ katheti, asubhāya vaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, asubhabhāvanāya vaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, ādissa ādissa asubhasamāpattiyā vaṇṇaṁ bhāsatī”ti, 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the monks reflected that the Buddha had praised unattractiveness in many ways, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj3:1.1.8
Pali: te anekākāravokāraṁ asubhabhāvanānuyogamanuyuttā viharanti. 
English (brahmali, published): and they devoted themselves to developing the mind in unattractiveness in its many different facets. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj3:1.1.9
Pali: Te sakena kāyena aṭṭīyanti harāyanti jigucchanti. 
English (brahmali, published): As a consequence, they became troubled by their own bodies, ashamed of and disgusted with them. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj3:1.1.10
Pali: Seyyathāpi nāma itthī vā puriso vā daharo yuvā maṇḍanakajātiko sīsaṁnhāto ahikuṇapena vā kukkurakuṇapena vā manussakuṇapena vā kaṇṭhe āsattena aṭṭīyeyya harāyeyya jiguccheyya; 
English (brahmali, published): Just as a young woman or man—someone fond of adornments, with freshly washed hair—would be ashamed, humiliated, and disgusted if the carcass of a snake, dog, or man was hung around her neck, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj3:1.1.11
Pali: evameva te bhikkhū sakena kāyena aṭṭīyantā harāyantā jigucchantā 
English (brahmali, published): just so those monks were troubled by their own bodies. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj3:1.1.12
Pali: attanāpi attānaṁ jīvitā voropenti, aññamaññampi jīvitā voropenti, 
English (brahmali, published): They took their own lives, took the lives of one another, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj3:1.1.13
Pali: migalaṇḍikampi samaṇakuttakaṁ upasaṅkamitvā evaṁ vadanti—
English (brahmali, published): and they went to Migalaṇḍika, the monastic lookalike, and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj3:1.1.14
Pali: “sādhu no, āvuso, jīvitā voropehi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please kill us. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj3:1.1.15
Pali: Idaṁ te pattacīvaraṁ bhavissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): You will get our bowl and robes.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj3:1.1.16
Pali: Atha kho migalaṇḍiko samaṇakuttako pattacīvarehi bhaṭo sambahule bhikkhū jīvitā voropetvā lohitakaṁ asiṁ ādāya yena vaggumudā nadī tenupasaṅkami. 
English (brahmali, published): And hired for a bowl and robes, Migalaṇḍika killed a number of monks. He then took his blood-stained knife to the river Vaggumudā. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj3:1.1.17
Pali: Atha kho migalaṇḍikassa samaṇakuttakassa lohitakaṁ taṁ asiṁ dhovantassa ahudeva kukkuccaṁ ahu vippaṭisāro—
English (brahmali, published): While washing it, he became anxious and remorseful, thinking, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj3:1.1.18

English (brahmali, published): “What the heck have I done? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj3:1.1.19

English (brahmali, published): I’ve made so much demerit by killing good monks.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj3:1.1.20

English (brahmali, published): “Well done, superior man, you’re truly fortunate. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj3:1.1.22

English (brahmali, published): You’ve made much merit by helping across those who hadn’t yet crossed.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj3:1.1.23

English (brahmali, published): Migalaṇḍika thought,

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj3:1.1.25

English (brahmali, published): “Who hasn’t crossed yet? Who can I help across?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj3:1.1.27

English (brahmali, published): The Buddha then went to the assembly hall, sat down on the prepared seat, and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj3:1.3.1
Pali: “Ayampi kho, bhikkhave, ānāpānassatisamādhi bhāvito bahulīkato santo ceva paṇīto ca asecanako ca sukho ca vihāro uppannuppanne ca pāpake akusale dhamme ṭhānaso antaradhāpeti vūpasameti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, when stillness by mindfulness of breathing is developed and cultivated, it is peaceful and sublime, and a satisfying state of bliss. And it removes bad and unwholesome qualities on the spot, whenever they arise. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj3:1.3.2
Pali: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, gimhānaṁ pacchime māse uhataṁ rajojallaṁ tamenaṁ mahāakālamegho ṭhānaso antaradhāpeti vūpasameti; 
English (brahmali, published): Just as a great, unseasonal storm in the last month of summer removes the dust and dirt from the air, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj3:1.3.3

English (brahmali, published): just so, when stillness by mindfulness of breathing is developed and cultivated, it is peaceful and sublime, and it removes bad and unwholesome qualities on the spot, whenever they arise. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj3:1.3.4
