pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.7
Pali: Evameva kho, bhikkhave, idhekaccassa pāpabhikkhuno evaṁ hoti—
English (brahmali, published): Just so, monks, a bad monk thinks like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.8
Pali: ‘kudāssu nāmāhaṁ satena vā sahassena vā parivuto gāmanigamarājadhānīsu cārikaṁ carissāmi 
English (brahmali, published): ‘When the heck will I walk about in villages, towns, and royal capitals, with a following of a hundred or a thousand people, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.9
Pali: sakkato garukato mānito pūjito apacito gahaṭṭhānañceva pabbajitānañca, lābhī cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārānan’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): being honored, respected, and revered by both lay people and those gone forth, getting robes, almsfood, dwellings, and medicinal supplies?’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.10
Pali: So aparena samayena satena vā sahassena vā parivuto gāmanigamarājadhānīsu cārikaṁ carati sakkato garukato mānito pūjito apacito gahaṭṭhānañceva pabbajitānañca, lābhī cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Then after some time, he does just that. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.11
Pali: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, paṭhamo mahācoro santo saṁvijjamāno lokasmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the first notorious gangster to be found in the world. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.12
Pali: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, idhekacco pāpabhikkhu tathāgatappaveditaṁ dhammavinayaṁ pariyāpuṇitvā attano dahati. 
English (brahmali, published): Or a bad monk learns the spiritual path proclaimed by the Buddha and takes it as his own. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.13
Pali: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, dutiyo mahācoro santo saṁvijjamāno lokasmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the second notorious gangster to be found in the world. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.14
Pali: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, idhekacco pāpabhikkhu suddhaṁ brahmacāriṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ carantaṁ amūlakena abrahmacariyena anuddhaṁseti. 
English (brahmali, published): Or a bad monk groundlessly charges someone living a pure spiritual life with a sexual offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.15
Pali: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, tatiyo mahācoro santo saṁvijjamāno lokasmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the third notorious gangster to be found in the world. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.16
Pali: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, idhekacco pāpabhikkhu yāni tāni saṅghassa garubhaṇḍāni garuparikkhārāni, seyyathidaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): Or a bad monk takes valuable goods and requisites from the Sangha—

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.17
Pali: ārāmo ārāmavatthu vihāro vihāravatthu mañco pīṭhaṁ bhisi bibbohanaṁ lohakumbhī lohabhāṇakaṁ lohavārako lohakaṭāhaṁ vāsi parasu kuṭhārī kudālo nikhādanaṁ valli veḷu muñjaṁ pabbajaṁ tiṇaṁ mattikā dārubhaṇḍaṁ mattikābhaṇḍaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): a monastery, the site of a monastery, a dwelling, the site of a dwelling, a bed, a bench, a mattress, a pillow, a metal pot, a metal jar, a metal bucket, a metal bowl, a machete, a hatchet, an ax, a spade, a chisel, a creeper, bamboo, a reed, grass, clay, wooden goods, ceramic goods—

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.18
Pali: tehi gihiṁ saṅgaṇhāti upalāpeti. 
English (brahmali, published): and uses them to bribe and create a following among householders. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.19
Pali: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, catuttho mahācoro santo saṁvijjamāno lokasmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the fourth notorious gangster to be found in the world. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.20
Pali: Sadevake, bhikkhave, loke samārake sabrahmake sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya ayaṁ aggo mahācoro yo asantaṁ abhūtaṁ uttarimanussadhammaṁ ullapati. 
English (brahmali, published): But in this world with its gods, lords of death, and supreme beings, in this society with its monastics and brahmins, its gods and humans, this is the most notorious gangster of all: one who claims to have a non-existent superhuman quality. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.21
Pali: Taṁ kissa hetu? 
English (brahmali, published): Why is that? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.22
Pali: Theyyāya vo, bhikkhave, raṭṭhapiṇḍo bhutto”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Monks, you’ve eaten the country’s almsfood by theft.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.23
Pali: Aññathā santamattānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): “Whoever should declare himself 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.24
Pali: aññathā yo pavedaye; 
English (brahmali, published): To be other than he truly is, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.25
Pali: Nikacca kitavasseva, 
English (brahmali, published): Has eaten this by theft, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.26
Pali: bhuttaṁ theyyena tassa taṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Like a cheater who has deceived. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.27
Pali: Kāsāvakaṇṭhā bahavo, 
English (brahmali, published): Many ocher-necks of bad qualities, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.28
Pali: pāpadhammā asaññatā; 
English (brahmali, published): Uncontrolled and wicked—

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.29
Pali: Pāpā pāpehi kammehi, 
English (brahmali, published): By their wicked deeds, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.30
Pali: nirayaṁ te upapajjare. 
English (brahmali, published): They are reborn in hell. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.31
Pali: Seyyo ayoguḷo bhutto, 
English (brahmali, published): It’s better to eat an iron ball, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.32
Pali: tatto aggisikhūpamo; 
English (brahmali, published): As hot as a licking flame, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.33
Pali: Yañce bhuñjeyya dussīlo, 
English (brahmali, published): Than for the immoral and uncontrolled 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.34
Pali: raṭṭhapiṇḍaṁ asaññatoti. 
English (brahmali, published): To eat the country’s alms.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.35
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā vaggumudātīriye bhikkhū anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā dubbharatāya dupposatāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking the monks from the banks of the Vaggumudā in many ways for being difficult to maintain, difficult to support … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.36
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.37.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu anabhijānaṁ uttarimanussadhammaṁ attupanāyikaṁ alamariyañāṇadassanaṁ samudācareyya—‘iti jānāmi iti passāmī’ti, 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk falsely claims for himself a superhuman quality, a knowledge and vision worthy of the noble ones, saying, “This I know, this I see,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.38
Pali: tato aparena samayena samanuggāhīyamāno vā asamanuggāhīyamāno vā āpanno visuddhāpekkho evaṁ vadeyya—‘ajānamevaṁ, āvuso, avacaṁ jānāmi, apassaṁ passāmi. Tucchaṁ musā vilapin’ti, 
English (brahmali, published): but after some time—whether he is questioned or not, but having committed the offense and seeking purification—should say: “Not knowing I said that I know, not seeing that I see; what I said was empty and false,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.39
Pali: ayampi pārājiko hoti asaṁvāso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): he too is expelled and excluded from the community.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.40
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā bhikkhū adiṭṭhe diṭṭhasaññino apatte pattasaññino anadhigate adhigatasaññino asacchikate sacchikatasaññino adhimānena aññaṁ byākariṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards a number of monks, thinking they had seen and realized what in fact they had not, declared final knowledge because of overestimation. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.2
Pali: Tesaṁ aparena samayena rāgāyapi cittaṁ namati dosāyapi cittaṁ namati mohāyapi cittaṁ namati. 
English (brahmali, published): After some time, their minds inclined to sensual desire, ill will, and confusion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.3
Pali: Tesaṁ kukkuccaṁ ahosi—
English (brahmali, published): They became anxious, thinking, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.4
Pali: “bhagavatā sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “The Buddha has laid down a training rule, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.5
Pali: Mayañcamha adiṭṭhe diṭṭhasaññino apatte pattasaññino anadhigate adhigatasaññino asacchikate sacchikatasaññino, adhimānena aññaṁ byākarimhā. 
English (brahmali, published): yet we declared final knowledge because of overestimation. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.6
Pali: Kacci nu kho mayaṁ pārājikaṁ āpattiṁ āpannā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): Could it be that we’ve committed an offense entailing expulsion?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.7
Pali: Te āyasmato ānandassa etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They told Venerable Ānanda, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.8
Pali: Āyasmā ānando bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): who told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.9
Pali: “Honti ye te, ānanda, bhikkhū adiṭṭhe diṭṭhasaññino apatte pattasaññino anadhigate adhigatasaññino asacchikate sacchikatasaññino adhimānena aññaṁ byākaronti. 
English (brahmali, published): He said, “This is negligible, Ānanda. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.10
Pali: Tañca kho etaṁ abbohārikan”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.11
Pali: “Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.12.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu anabhijānaṁ uttarimanussadhammaṁ attupanāyikaṁ alamariyañāṇadassanaṁ samudācareyya—‘iti jānāmi iti passāmī’ti, 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk falsely claims for himself a superhuman quality, a knowledge and vision worthy of the noble ones, saying, “This I know, this I see,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.13
Pali: tato aparena samayena samanuggāhīyamāno vā asamanuggāhīyamāno vā āpanno visuddhāpekkho evaṁ vadeyya—‘ajānamevaṁ, āvuso, avacaṁ jānāmi, apassaṁ passāmi. Tucchaṁ musā vilapin’ti, 
English (brahmali, published): but after some time—whether he is questioned or not, but having committed the offense and seeking purification—should say: “Not knowing I said that I know, not seeing that I see; what I said was empty and false,” then, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.14
Pali: aññatra adhimānā, 
English (brahmali, published): except if it is due to overestimation, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.15
Pali: ayampi pārājiko hoti asaṁvāso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): he too is expelled and excluded from the community.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.5
Pali: Anabhijānanti 
English (brahmali, published): Falsely: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.6
Pali: asantaṁ abhūtaṁ asaṁvijjamānaṁ ajānanto apassanto attani kusalaṁ dhammaṁ— atthi me kusalo dhammoti. 
English (brahmali, published): although a certain wholesome quality is non-existent in himself, not real, not to be found, and he does not see it or know it, he says, “I have this wholesome quality.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.7
Pali: Uttarimanussadhammo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A superhuman quality: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.8
Pali: jhānaṁ vimokkho samādhi samāpatti ñāṇadassanaṁ maggabhāvanā phalasacchikiriyā kilesappahānaṁ vinīvaraṇatā cittassa suññāgāre abhirati. 
English (brahmali, published): absorption, release, stillness, attainment, knowledge and vision, development of the path, realization of the fruits, abandoning the defilements, a mind without hindrances, delighting in solitude. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.9
Pali: Attupanāyikanti 
English (brahmali, published): For himself: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.10
Pali: te vā kusale dhamme attani upaneti attānaṁ vā tesu kusalesu dhammesu upaneti. 
English (brahmali, published): either he presents those good qualities as in himself, or he presents himself as among those good qualities. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.11
Pali: Ñāṇanti 
English (brahmali, published): Knowledge: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.12
Pali: tisso vijjā. 
English (brahmali, published): the three true insights. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.13
Pali: Dassananti 
English (brahmali, published): Vision: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.14
Pali: yaṁ ñāṇaṁ taṁ dassanaṁ, yaṁ dassanaṁ taṁ ñāṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): knowledge and vision are equivalent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.15
Pali: Samudācareyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Claims: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.16
Pali: āroceyya itthiyā vā purisassa vā gahaṭṭhassa vā pabbajitassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): announces to a woman or a man, to a householder or a monastic. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.17
Pali: Iti jānāmi iti passāmīti 
English (brahmali, published): This I know, this I see: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.18
Pali: jānāmahaṁ ete dhamme, passāmahaṁ ete dhamme atthi ca ete dhammā mayi, ahañca etesu dhammesu sandissāmīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I know these qualities,” “I see these qualities,” “These qualities are found in me and I conform to them.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.19
Pali: Tato aparena samayenāti 
English (brahmali, published): After some time: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.20
Pali: yasmiṁ khaṇe samudāciṇṇaṁ hoti taṁ khaṇaṁ taṁ layaṁ taṁ muhuttaṁ vītivatte. 
English (brahmali, published): the moment, the second, the instant after he has made the claim. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.21
Pali: Samanuggāhīyamānoti 
English (brahmali, published): He is questioned: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.22
Pali: yaṁ vatthu paṭiññātaṁ hoti tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ samanuggāhīyamāno—
English (brahmali, published): he is questioned in regard to what he has claimed: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.23
Pali: “kinte adhigataṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): “What did you attain?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.24
Pali: kinti te adhigataṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): “How did you attain it?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.25
Pali: kadā te adhigataṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): “When did you attain it?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.26
Pali: kattha te adhigataṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): “Where did you attain it?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.27
Pali: katame te kilesā pahīnā, 
English (brahmali, published): “Which defilements did you abandon?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.28
Pali: katamesaṁ tvaṁ dhammānaṁ lābhī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Which qualities did you gain?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.29
Pali: Asamanuggāhīyamānoti 
English (brahmali, published): Not: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.30
Pali: na kenaci vuccamāno. 
English (brahmali, published): he is not spoken to by anyone. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.31
Pali: Āpannoti 
English (brahmali, published): Having committed the offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.32
Pali: pāpiccho icchāpakato asantaṁ abhūtaṁ uttarimanussadhammaṁ ullapitvā pārājikaṁ āpattiṁ āpanno hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): having bad desires, overcome by desire, claiming to have a non-existent superhuman quality, he has committed an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.33
Pali: Visuddhāpekkhoti 
English (brahmali, published): Seeking purification: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.34
Pali: gihī vā hotukāmo upāsako vā hotukāmo ārāmiko vā hotukāmo sāmaṇero vā hotukāmo. 
English (brahmali, published): he desires to be a householder, a lay follower, a monastery worker, or a novice monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.35
Pali: Ajānamevaṁ, āvuso, avacaṁ—jānāmi, apassaṁ passāmīti 
English (brahmali, published): Not knowing I said that I know, not seeing that I see: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.36
Pali: nāhaṁ ete dhamme jānāmi, nāhaṁ ete dhamme passāmi, natthi ca ete dhammā mayi, na cāhaṁ etesu dhammesu sandissāmīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I don’t know these qualities,” “I don’t see these qualities,” “These qualities aren’t found in me and I don’t conform to them.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.37
Pali: Tucchaṁ musā vilapinti 
English (brahmali, published): What I said was empty and false: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.38
Pali: tucchakaṁ mayā bhaṇitaṁ, musā mayā bhaṇitaṁ, abhūtaṁ mayā bhaṇitaṁ, ajānantena mayā bhaṇitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “What I said was empty,” “What I said was false,” “What I said was unreal,” “I said it without knowing.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.39
Pali: Aññatra adhimānāti 
English (brahmali, published): Except if it is due to overestimation: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.40
Pali: ṭhapetvā adhimānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): unless it is due to overestimation. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.41
Pali: Ayampīti 
English (brahmali, published): He too: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.42
Pali: purime upādāya vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): this is said with reference to the preceding offenses entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.43
Pali: Pārājiko hotīti 
English (brahmali, published): Is expelled: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.44
Pali: seyyathāpi nāma tālo matthakacchinno abhabbo puna virūḷhiyā; 
English (brahmali, published): just as a palm tree with its crown cut off is incapable of further growth, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.45
Pali: evameva bhikkhu pāpiccho icchāpakato asantaṁ abhūtaṁ uttarimanussadhammaṁ ullapitvā assamaṇo hoti asakyaputtiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): so too is a monk with bad desires, overcome by desire, who claims to have a non-existent superhuman quality not an ascetic, not a Sakyan monastic. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.46
Pali: Tena vuccati— “pārājiko hotī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Therefore it is said, “he is expelled.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.47
Pali: Asaṁvāsoti 
English (brahmali, published): Excluded from the community: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.48
Pali: saṁvāso nāma ekakammaṁ ekuddeso samasikkhatā— eso saṁvāso nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): Community: joint legal procedures, a joint recitation, the same training—this is called “community”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.49
Pali: So tena saddhiṁ natthi. Tena vuccati— “asaṁvāso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): He does not take part in this—therefore it is called “excluded from the community”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.1
Pali: Uttarimanussadhammo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A superhuman quality: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.2
Pali: jhānaṁ vimokkho samādhi samāpatti ñāṇadassanaṁ maggabhāvanā phalasacchikiriyā kilesappahānaṁ vinīvaraṇatā cittassa suññāgāre abhirati. 
English (brahmali, published): absorption, release, stillness, attainment, knowledge and vision, development of the path, realization of the fruits, abandoning the defilements, a mind without hindrances, delighting in solitude. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.3.1
Pali: Jhānanti 
English (brahmali, published): Absorption: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.4
Pali: paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ tatiyaṁ jhānaṁ catutthaṁ jhānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): the first absorption, the second absorption, the third absorption, the fourth absorption. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.5
Pali: Vimokkhoti 
English (brahmali, published): Release: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.6
Pali: suññato vimokkho animitto vimokkho appaṇihito vimokkho. 
English (brahmali, published): emptiness release, signless release, desireless release. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.7
Pali: Samādhīti 
English (brahmali, published): Stillness: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.8
Pali: suññato samādhi animitto samādhi appaṇihito samādhi. 
English (brahmali, published): emptiness stillness, signless stillness, desireless stillness. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.9
Pali: Samāpattīti 
English (brahmali, published): Attainment: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.10
Pali: suññatā samāpatti animittā samāpatti appaṇihitā samāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): emptiness attainment, signless attainment, desireless attainment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.11
Pali: Ñāṇadassananti 
English (brahmali, published): Knowledge and vision: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.12
Pali: tisso vijjā. 
English (brahmali, published): the three true insights. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.13
Pali: Maggabhāvanāti 
English (brahmali, published): Development of the path: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.14
Pali: cattāro satipaṭṭhānā, cattāro sammappadhānā, cattāro iddhipādā, pañcindriyāni, pañca balāni, satta bojjhaṅgā, ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo. 
English (brahmali, published): the four applications of mindfulness, the four right efforts, the four foundations for supernormal power, the five spiritual faculties, the five spiritual powers, the seven factors of awakening, the noble eightfold path. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.15
Pali: Phalasacchikiriyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Realization of the fruits: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.16
Pali: sotāpattiphalassa sacchikiriyā, sakadāgāmiphalassa sacchikiriyā, anāgāmiphalassa sacchikiriyā, arahattassa sacchikiriyā. 
English (brahmali, published): realization of the fruit of streamentry, realization of the fruit of once-returning, realization of the fruit of non-returning, realization of perfection. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.17
Pali: Kilesappahānanti 
English (brahmali, published): Abandoning the defilements: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.18
Pali: rāgassa pahānaṁ dosassa pahānaṁ mohassa pahānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): the abandoning of sensual desire, the abandoning of ill will, the abandoning of confusion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.19
Pali: Vinīvaraṇatā cittassāti 
English (brahmali, published): A mind without hindrances: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.20
Pali: rāgā cittaṁ vinīvaraṇatā, dosā cittaṁ vinīvaraṇatā, mohā cittaṁ vinīvaraṇatā. 
English (brahmali, published): a mind without sensual desire, a mind without ill will, a mind without confusion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.21
Pali: Suññāgāre abhiratīti 
English (brahmali, published): Delighting in solitude: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.22
Pali: paṭhamena jhānena suññāgāre abhirati, 
English (brahmali, published): because of the first absorption, there is delight in solitude; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.23
Pali: dutiyena jhānena suññāgāre abhirati, 
English (brahmali, published): because of the second absorption, there is delight in solitude; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.24
Pali: tatiyena jhānena suññāgāre abhirati, 
English (brahmali, published): because of the third absorption, there is delight in solitude; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.25
Pali: catutthena jhānena suññāgāre abhirati. 
English (brahmali, published): because of the fourth absorption, there is delight in solitude. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.1
Pali: Tīhākārehi paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjinti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pārājikassa—
English (brahmali, published): If he lies in full awareness, saying, “I attained the first absorption,” he commits an offense entailing expulsion when three conditions are fulfilled: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.2
Pali: pubbevassa hoti musā bhaṇissanti, 
English (brahmali, published): before he has lied, he knows he is going to lie; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.3
Pali: bhaṇantassa hoti musā bhaṇāmīti, 
English (brahmali, published): while lying, he knows he is lying; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.4
Pali: bhaṇitassa hoti musā mayā bhaṇitanti. 
English (brahmali, published): after he has lied, he knows he has lied. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.5

English (brahmali, published): before he has lied, he knows he is going to lie; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.67

English (brahmali, published): while lying, he knows he is lying;

English (brahmali, published): after he has lied, he knows he has lied; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.69
Pali: vinidhāya diṭṭhiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): he misrepresents his view of what is true.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.65

English (brahmali, published): If he lies in full awareness, saying, “I’ve given up sensual desire,  renounced it, let it go, abandoned it, relinquished it, forsaken it, thrown it aside,” he commits an offense entailing expulsion when three conditions are fulfilled. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.66
Pali: Tīhākārehi doso me catto vanto mutto pahīno paṭinissaṭṭho ukkheṭito samukkheṭitoti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he lies in full awareness, saying, “I’ve given up ill will, renounced it, let it go, abandoned it, relinquished it, forsaken it, thrown it aside,” he commits an offense entailing expulsion when three conditions are fulfilled. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.67
Pali: Tīhākārehi moho me catto vanto mutto pahīno paṭinissaṭṭho ukkheṭito samukkheṭitoti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he lies in full awareness, saying, “I’ve given up confusion, renounced it, let it go, abandoned it, relinquished it, forsaken it, thrown it aside,” he commits an offense entailing expulsion when three conditions are fulfilled. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.68

English (brahmali, published): If he lies in full awareness, saying, “My mind is free from the hindrance of sensual desire,” he commits an offense entailing expulsion when three conditions are fulfilled. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.69

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.70

English (brahmali, published): If he lies in full awareness, saying, “My mind is free from the hindrance of confusion,” he commits an offense entailing expulsion when three conditions are fulfilled … when seven conditions are fulfilled: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.71
Pali: pubbevassa hoti musā bhaṇissanti, 
English (brahmali, published): before he has lied, he knows he is going to lie; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.72
Pali: bhaṇantassa hoti musā bhaṇāmīti, 
English (brahmali, published): while lying, he knows he is lying; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.73
Pali: bhaṇitassa hoti musā mayā bhaṇitanti, 
English (brahmali, published): after he has lied, he knows he has lied; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.74
Pali: vinidhāya diṭṭhiṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): he misrepresents his view of what is true; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.75
Pali: vinidhāya khantiṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): he misrepresents his belief of what is true; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.76
Pali: vinidhāya ruciṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): he misrepresents his acceptance of what is true; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.77
Pali: vinidhāya bhāvaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): he misrepresents his sentiment of what is true.

Pali: Suddhikaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The basic series is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.8.108
Pali: pubbevassa hoti musā bhaṇissanti, 
English (brahmali, published): before he has lied, he knows he is going to lie;

English (brahmali, published): he misrepresents his acceptance of what is true; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.8.114
Pali: vinidhāya bhāvaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): he misrepresents his sentiment of what is true. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.8.115
Pali: Ekamūlakaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The section based on one item is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.9.1
Pali: Yathā ekamūlakaṁ vitthāritaṁ evameva dumūlakādipi vitthāretabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The sections based on two items, etc., are to be given in detail in the same way as the section based on one item. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.9.2.1
Pali: Idaṁ sabbamūlakaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the section based on all items: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.9.3
Pali: Tīhākārehi …pe… sattahākārehi paṭhamañca jhānaṁ dutiyañca jhānaṁ tatiyañca jhānaṁ catutthañca jhānaṁ suññatañca vimokkhaṁ animittañca vimokkhaṁ appaṇihitañca vimokkhaṁ suññatañca samādhiṁ animittañca samādhiṁ appaṇihitañca samādhiṁ suññatañca samāpattiṁ animittañca samāpattiṁ appaṇihitañca samāpattiṁ tisso ca vijjā cattāro ca satipaṭṭhāne cattāro ca sammappadhāne cattāro ca iddhipāde pañca ca indriyāni pañca ca balāni satta ca bojjhaṅge ariyañca aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ sotāpattiphalañca sakadāgāmiphalañca anāgāmiphalañca arahattañca samāpajjiṁ … 
English (brahmali, published): If he lies in full awareness, saying, “I attained the first absorption and the second absorption and the third absorption and the fourth absorption and the emptiness release and the signless release and the desireless release and the emptiness stillness and the signless stillness and the desireless stillness and the emptiness attainment and the signless attainment and the desireless attainment and the three true insights and the four applications of mindfulness and the four right efforts and the four foundations for supernormal power and the five spiritual faculties and the five spiritual powers and the seven factors of awakening and the noble eightfold path and the fruit of streamentry and the fruit of once-returning and the fruit of non-returning and perfection … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.9.4
Pali: samāpajjāmi … 
English (brahmali, published): and I’m attaining … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.9.5
Pali: samāpanno …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): and I’ve attained … etc. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.9.6
Pali: rāgo ca me catto, doso ca me catto, moho ca me catto vanto mutto pahīno paṭinissaṭṭho ukkheṭito samukkheṭito. 
English (brahmali, published): and I’ve given up sensual desire, renounced it, let it go, abandoned it, relinquished it, forsaken it, thrown it aside; and I’ve given up ill will, renounced it, let it go, abandoned it, relinquished it, forsaken it, thrown it aside; and I’ve given up confusion, renounced it, let it go, abandoned it, relinquished it, forsaken it, thrown it aside; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.9.7
Pali: Rāgā ca me cittaṁ vinīvaraṇaṁ, dosā ca me cittaṁ vinīvaraṇaṁ, mohā ca me cittaṁ vinīvaraṇanti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pārājikassa—
English (brahmali, published): and my mind is free from the hindrance of sensual desire and my mind is free from the hindrance of ill will and my mind is free from the hindrance of confusion,” he commits an offense entailing expulsion when three conditions are fulfilled … when seven conditions are fulfilled: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.9.8
Pali: pubbevassa hoti musā bhaṇissanti, 
English (brahmali, published): before he has lied, he knows he is going to lie; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.9.9
Pali: bhaṇantassa hoti musā bhaṇāmīti, 
English (brahmali, published): while lying, he knows he is lying; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.9.10
Pali: bhaṇitassa hoti musā mayā bhaṇitanti, 
English (brahmali, published): after he has lied, he knows he has lied; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.9.11
Pali: vinidhāya diṭṭhiṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): he misrepresents his view of what is true; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.9.12
Pali: vinidhāya khantiṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): he misrepresents his belief of what is true; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.9.13
Pali: vinidhāya ruciṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): he misrepresents his acceptance of what is true; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.9.14
Pali: vinidhāya bhāvaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): he misrepresents his sentiment of what is true. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.9.15
Pali: Sabbamūlakaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The section based on all items is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.9.16
Pali: Suddhikavārakathā niṭṭhitā. 
English (brahmali, published): The exposition of the section on the basic series is finished. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.1.1

English (brahmali, published): If he lies in full awareness, meaning to say, “I attained the first absorption,” while actually saying, “I attained the second absorption,” then, if the listener understands, he commits an offense entailing expulsion when three conditions are fulfilled;

English (brahmali, published): he has realized the fourth absorption in solitude,” then, if the listener understands, he commits a serious offense when three conditions are fulfilled; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:6.2.35
Pali: na paṭivijānantassa āpatti dukkaṭassa—
English (brahmali, published): if the listener does not understand, he commits an offense of wrong conduct when three conditions are fulfilled … when seven conditions are fulfilled: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:6.2.36
Pali: pubbevassa hoti musā bhaṇissanti, 
English (brahmali, published): before he has lied, he knows he is going to lie; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:6.2.37
Pali: bhaṇantassa hoti musā bhaṇāmīti, 
English (brahmali, published): while lying, he knows he is lying; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:6.2.38
Pali: bhaṇitassa hoti musā mayā bhaṇitanti, 
English (brahmali, published): after he has lied, he knows he has lied; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:6.2.39
Pali: vinidhāya diṭṭhiṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): he misrepresents his view of what is true; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:6.2.40

English (brahmali, published): he misrepresents his belief of what is true; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:6.2.41

English (brahmali, published): he misrepresents his acceptance of what is true; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:6.2.42
Pali: vinidhāya bhāvaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): he misrepresents his sentiment of what is true. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:6.2.43

English (brahmali, published): The successive fifteen are finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.8
Pali: 4.1. Vinītavatthuuddānagāthā 
English (brahmali, published): Summary verses of case studies 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.9
Pali: Adhimāne araññamhi, 
English (brahmali, published): “About overestimation, in the wilderness, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.10
Pali: piṇḍopajjhāriyāpatho; 
English (brahmali, published): Almsfood, preceptor, behavior; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.11

English (brahmali, published): Fetters, qualities while in solitude, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.12
Pali: vihāro paccupaṭṭhito. 
English (brahmali, published): Dwelling, supported. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.13
Pali: Na dukkaraṁ viriyamathopi maccuno, 
English (brahmali, published): Not difficult, and then energy, fear of death, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.14
Pali: Bhāyāvuso vippaṭisāri sammā; 
English (brahmali, published): Remorseful friend, rightly; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.15
Pali: Viriyena yogena ārādhanāya, 
English (brahmali, published): To be reached by energy, to be reached by exertion, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.16
Pali: Atha vedanāya adhivāsanā duve. 
English (brahmali, published): Then two on the enduring of feeling. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.17
Pali: Brāhmaṇe pañca vatthūni, 
English (brahmali, published): Five cases of a brahmin, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.18
Pali: aññaṁ byākaraṇā tayo; 
English (brahmali, published): Three on declaring final knowledge; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.19
Pali: Agārāvaraṇā kāmā, 
English (brahmali, published): Home, rejected worldly pleasures, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.20
Pali: rati cāpi apakkami. 
English (brahmali, published): And delight, set out. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.21
Pali: Aṭṭhi pesi ubho gāvaghātakā, 
English (brahmali, published): Bone, and lump—both are cattle butchers; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.22
Pali: Piṇḍo sākuṇiko nicchavi orabbhi; 
English (brahmali, published): A morsel is a poultry butcher, a sheep butcher is flayed; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.23
Pali: Asi ca sūkariko satti māgavi, 
English (brahmali, published): And a pig butcher and sword, a deer hunter and knife, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.24
Pali: Usu ca kāraṇiko sūci sārathi. 
English (brahmali, published): And a torturer and arrow, a horse trainer and needle. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.25
Pali: Yo ca sibbīyati sūcako hi so, 
English (brahmali, published): And a slanderer is sewn, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.26
Pali: Aṇḍabhāri ahu gāmakūṭako; 
English (brahmali, published): A corrupt magistrate had testicles as burden; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.27
Pali: Kūpe nimuggo hi so pāradāriko, 
English (brahmali, published): An adulterer submerged in a pit, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.28
Pali: Gūthakhādī ahu duṭṭhabrāhmaṇo. 
English (brahmali, published): An eater of feces was a wicked brahmin. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.29
Pali: Nicchavitthī aticārinī ahu, 
English (brahmali, published): A flayed woman was an adulteress, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.30
Pali: Maṅgulitthī ahu ikkhaṇitthikā; 
English (brahmali, published): An ugly woman was a fortune-teller; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.31
Pali: Okilinī hi sapattaṅgārokiri, 
English (brahmali, published): A sweating woman poured coals on a co-wife, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.32
Pali: Sīsacchinno ahu coraghātako. 
English (brahmali, published): A beheaded man was an executioner. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.33
Pali: Bhikkhu bhikkhunī sikkhamānā, 
English (brahmali, published): A monk, a nun, a trainee nun, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.34

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.35

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.36

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:7.37

English (brahmali, published): The perfected monk Sobhita

English (brahmali, published): Could it be that I’ve committed an offense entailing expulsion?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.1.5

English (brahmali, published): He told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.1.6

English (brahmali, published): At one time a monk was ill. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.8.2
Pali: Taṁ bhikkhū etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): The monks said to him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.8.3

English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, do you have any superhuman qualities?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.8.4
Pali: “Nāvuso, dukkaraṁ aññaṁ byākātun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s not difficult to declare final knowledge.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.8.5

English (brahmali, published): He became anxious and thought, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.8.6

English (brahmali, published): At one time a number of monks entered the rainy-season residence in a certain monastery, making this agreement: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.14.2
Pali: “yo imamhā āvāsā paṭhamaṁ pakkamissati taṁ mayaṁ arahāti jānissāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Whoever sets out from this monastery first, we’ll know him as a perfected one.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.14.3
Pali: Aññataro bhikkhu—
English (brahmali, published): One of them thought, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.14.4
Pali: “maṁ arahāti jānantū”ti, 
English (brahmali, published): “Let them think I’m a perfected one,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.14.5
Pali: tamhā āvāsā paṭhamaṁ pakkāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): and he set out first from that monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.14.6
Pali: Tassa kukkuccaṁ ahosi …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): He became anxious … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.14.7
Pali: “āpattiṁ tvaṁ, bhikkhu, āpanno pārājikan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “You’ve committed an offense entailing expulsion.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:9.1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:9.1.2

English (brahmali, published): Venerable Lakkhaṇa and Venerable Mahāmoggallāna were staying on the Vulture Peak.

English (brahmali, published): “Yes.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:9.1.6

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:9.1.7

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:9.2.1

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:9.2.24

English (brahmali, published): As a result of her deeds, she was tormented in hell for many hundreds of thousands of years. And now, because of the remaining result of her actions, she’s experiencing such an existence.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:9.7.14

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:9.7.25
Pali: Tassuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:9.7.26
Pali: Methunādinnādānañca, 
English (brahmali, published): “Sexual intercourse, and stealing, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:9.7.27
Pali: manussaviggahuttari; 
English (brahmali, published): Person, super—

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:9.7.28
Pali: Pārājikāni cattāri, 
English (brahmali, published): The four offenses entailing expulsion, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:9.7.29
Pali: chejjavatthū asaṁsayāti. 
English (brahmali, published): Definitive grounds for cutting off.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:9.7.30
Pali: Pārājikakaṇḍaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing expulsion is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:0.3
Pali: Sekhiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on training 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:0.4
Pali: Parimaṇḍalavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on evenly all around 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:0.5
Pali: 1. Parimaṇḍalasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 1. Not wearing the sarong evenly all around 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:0.6
Pali: Ime kho panāyasmanto sekhiyā dhammā uddesaṁ āgacchanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerables, these rules of training come up for recitation. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū puratopi pacchatopi olambentā nivāsenti. 
English (brahmali, published): the monks from the group of six were wearing their sarongs hanging down in front and behind. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:1.3
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:1.4
Pali: “kathañhi nāma samaṇā sakyaputtiyā puratopi pacchatopi olambentā nivāsessanti, seyyathāpi gihī kāmabhogino”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the Sakyan monastics wear their sarongs hanging down in front and behind? They’re just like householders who indulge in worldly pleasures!” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:1.5
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:1.6
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:1.7
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū puratopi pacchatopi olambentā nivāsessantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the monks from the group of six wear their sarongs hanging down in front and behind?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:1.8
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū chabbaggiye bhikkhū anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking those monks in many ways, they told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:1.9
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe bhikkhusaṅghaṁ sannipātāpetvā chabbaggiye bhikkhū paṭipucchi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks from the group of six: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:1.10
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, puratopi pacchatopi olambentā nivāsethā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:1.11
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:1.12
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:1.13
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, puratopi pacchatopi olambentā nivāsessatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:1.14
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:1.15
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:1.16.1
Pali: “Parimaṇḍalaṁ nivāsessāmīti sikkhā karaṇīyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘“I will wear my sarong evenly all around,” this is how you should train.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:1.17
Pali: Parimaṇḍalaṁ nivāsetabbaṁ nābhimaṇḍalaṁ jāṇumaṇḍalaṁ paṭicchādentena. 
English (brahmali, published): One should wear one’s sarong evenly all around, covering the navel and the knees. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:1.18
Pali: Yo anādariyaṁ paṭicca purato vā pacchato vā olambento nivāseti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk, out of disrespect, wears his sarong hanging down in front or behind, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:1.19.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:1.20
Pali: asañcicca, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is unintentional; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:1.21
Pali: assatiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is not mindful; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:1.22
Pali: ajānantassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not know; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:1.23
Pali: gilānassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is sick; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:1.24
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:1.25
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:1.26
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk1:1.27
Pali: Paṭhamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The first training rule is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk6:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk6:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū hatthampi pādampi kīḷāpentā antaraghare nisīdanti …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the monks from the group of six were playing with their hands and feet while sitting in inhabited areas. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk6:1.3.1
Pali: “Susaṁvuto antaraghare nisīdissāmīti sikkhā karaṇīyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “‘I will be well-restrained while sitting in inhabited areas,’ this is how you should train.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk6:1.4
Pali: Susaṁvutena antaraghare nisīditabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): One should be well-restrained while sitting in an inhabited area. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk6:1.5
Pali: Yo anādariyaṁ paṭicca hatthaṁ vā pādaṁ vā kīḷāpento antaraghare nisīdati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk, out of disrespect, plays with his hands or feet while sitting in an inhabited area, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk6:1.6.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk6:1.7
Pali: asañcicca, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is unintentional; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk6:1.8
Pali: assatiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is not mindful; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk6:1.9
Pali: ajānantassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not know; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk6:1.10
Pali: gilānassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is sick; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk6:1.11
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk6:1.12
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk6:1.13
Pali: Chaṭṭhasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The sixth training rule is finished.

Pali: 7. Okkhittacakkhusikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 7. Not lowering the eyes 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk7:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk7:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū tahaṁ tahaṁ olokentā antaraghare gacchanti …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the monks from the group of six were looking here and there while walking in inhabited areas. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk7:1.3.1
Pali: “Okkhittacakkhu antaraghare gamissāmīti sikkhā karaṇīyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “‘I will lower my eyes while walking in inhabited areas,’ this is how you should train.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk7:1.4
Pali: Okkhittacakkhunā antaraghare gantabbaṁ yugamattaṁ pekkhantena. 
English (brahmali, published): One should lower one’s eyes while walking in an inhabited area, looking a plow’s length ahead. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk7:1.5
Pali: Yo anādariyaṁ paṭicca tahaṁ tahaṁ olokento antaraghare gacchati, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk7:1.7
Pali: asañcicca, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is unintentional; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk7:1.8
Pali: assatiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is not mindful; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk7:1.9
Pali: ajānantassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not know; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk7:1.10
Pali: gilānassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is sick; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk7:1.11
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk7:1.12
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk7:1.13
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk7:1.14
Pali: Sattamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The seventh training rule is finished.

Pali: ajānantassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not know; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk11:1.10
Pali: gilānassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is sick; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk11:1.11
Pali: hasanīyasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ mihitamattaṁ karoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he merely smiles when something is funny; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk11:1.12

English (brahmali, published): if it is unintentional; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk12:1.8
Pali: assatiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is not mindful; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk12:1.9
Pali: ajānantassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not know; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk12:1.10
Pali: gilānassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is sick; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk12:1.11
Pali: hasanīyasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ mihitamattaṁ karoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he merely smiles when something is funny; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk12:1.12
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk12:1.13
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk12:1.14
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk12:1.15
Pali: Dutiyasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The second training rule is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk19:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk19:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū sīsappacālakaṁ antaraghare gacchanti sīsaṁ olambentā …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the monks from the group of six were swaying and tilting their heads while walking in inhabited areas. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk19:1.3.1
Pali: “Na sīsappacālakaṁ antaraghare gamissāmīti sikkhā karaṇīyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “‘I will not sway my head while walking in inhabited areas,’ this is how you should train.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk19:1.4
Pali: Na sīsappacālakaṁ antaraghare gantabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): One should not sway one’s head while walking in an inhabited area; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk19:1.5
Pali: Sīsaṁ paggahetvā gantabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one should walk keeping one’s head straight. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk19:1.6
Pali: Yo anādariyaṁ paṭicca sīsappacālakaṁ antaraghare gacchati sīsaṁ olambento, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk, out of disrespect, sways and tilts his head while walking in an inhabited area, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk19:1.7.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk19:1.8.1
Pali: asañcicca …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): if it is unintentional; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk19:1.9
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk19:1.10
Pali: Navamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The ninth training rule is finished.

English (brahmali, published): The chapter on training 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk26:0.4
Pali: Khambhakatavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on hands on hips 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk26:0.5
Pali: 26. Pallatthikasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 26. Clasping the knees 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk26:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk26:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū pallatthikāya antaraghare nisīdanti …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the monks from the group of six were clasping their knees while sitting in inhabited areas. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk26:1.3.1
Pali: “Na pallatthikāya antaraghare nisīdissāmīti sikkhā karaṇīyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “‘I will not clasp my knees while sitting in inhabited areas,’ this is how you should train.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk26:1.4
Pali: Na pallatthikāya antaraghare nisīditabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): One should not clasp one’s knees while sitting in an inhabited area. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk26:1.5
Pali: Yo anādariyaṁ paṭicca hatthapallatthikāya vā dussapallatthikāya vā antaraghare nisīdati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk, out of disrespect, clasps his knees with his hands or with a cloth while sitting in an inhabited area, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk26:1.6.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk26:1.7
Pali: asañcicca, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is unintentional; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk26:1.8
Pali: assatiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is not mindful; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk26:1.9
Pali: ajānantassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not know; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk26:1.10
Pali: gilānassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is sick; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk26:1.11
Pali: vāsūpagatassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he has entered his dwelling; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk26:1.12
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk26:1.13
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk26:1.14
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk26:1.15
Pali: Chaṭṭhasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The sixth training rule is finished.

English (brahmali, published): Bean curry: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk29:1.5
Pali: dve sūpā— muggasūpo, māsasūpo hatthahāriyo. 
English (brahmali, published): there are two kinds of bean curry, to be taken with the hand: mung-bean curry and black-gram curry. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk29:1.6
Pali: Samasūpako piṇḍapāto paṭiggahetabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): Almsfood is to be received with the right proportion of bean curry. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk29:1.7
Pali: Yo anādariyaṁ paṭicca sūpaññeva bahuṁ paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk29:1.11
Pali: ajānantassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not know; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk29:1.12
Pali: gilānassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is sick; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk29:1.13
Pali: rasarase, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is any food apart from bean curry; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk29:1.14

English (brahmali, published): if it is from those who have given an invitation; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk29:1.16
Pali: aññassatthāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is for the benefit of someone else; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk29:1.17
Pali: attano dhanena, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is by means of his own property; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk29:1.18

English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency;

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk32:1.4
Pali: Pattasaññinā piṇḍapāto bhuñjitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): Almsfood is to be eaten with attention on the bowl. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk32:1.5
Pali: Yo anādariyaṁ paṭicca tahaṁ tahaṁ olokento piṇḍapātaṁ bhuñjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk, out of disrespect, eats almsfood while looking here and there, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk32:1.6.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk32:1.7.1
Pali: asañcicca …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): if it is unintentional; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk32:1.8
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk32:1.9
Pali: Dutiyasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The second training rule is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:1.4

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:1.5
Pali: Kassa sampannaṁ na manāpaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Who doesn’t like nice food? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:1.6
Pali: Kassa sāduṁ na ruccatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Who doesn’t prefer tasty food?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:1.7
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:1.8
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:1.9
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū sūpampi odanampi attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjissantī”ti …pe…

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:1.10
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, sūpampi odanampi attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjathā”ti?

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:1.11
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:1.15

English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:1.16.1
Pali: “Na sūpaṁ vā odanaṁ vā attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjissāmīti sikkhā karaṇīyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘“I will not request bean curry or rice for myself and then eat it,” this is how you should train.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:1.17
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū gilānā honti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards a number of monks were sick. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:2.2
Pali: Gilānapucchakā bhikkhū gilāne bhikkhū etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): The monks who were looking after them asked, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:2.3
Pali: “kaccāvuso, khamanīyaṁ, kacci yāpanīyan”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “I hope you’re bearing up? I hope you’re getting better?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:2.4
Pali: “Pubbe mayaṁ, āvuso, sūpampi odanampi attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjāma, tena no phāsu hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Previously we ate rice and bean curry that we had requested ourselves, and then we were comfortable. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:2.5
Pali: Idāni pana—
English (brahmali, published): But now 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:2.6
Pali: ‘bhagavatā paṭikkhittan’ti kukkuccāyantā na viññāpema, tena no na phāsu hotī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): that the Buddha has prohibited this, we don’t ask because we’re afraid of wrongdoing. And because of that we’re not comfortable.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:2.7
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:2.8
Pali: anujānāmi, bhikkhave, gilānena bhikkhunā sūpampi odanampi attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow a sick monk to eat rice and bean curry that he has requested for himself. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:2.9
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:2.10.1
Pali: “Na sūpaṁ vā odanaṁ vā agilāno attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjissāmīti sikkhā karaṇīyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘“When not sick, I will not request bean curry or rice for myself and then eat it,” this is how you should train.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:2.11
Pali: Na sūpaṁ vā odanaṁ vā agilānena attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): When one is not sick, one should not request bean curry or rice for oneself and then eat it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:2.12
Pali: Yo anādariyaṁ paṭicca sūpaṁ vā odanaṁ vā agilāno attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk who is not sick, out of disrespect, eats bean curry or rice that he has requested for himself, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:2.13.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:2.14
Pali: asañcicca, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is unintentional; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:2.15
Pali: assatiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is not mindful; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:2.16
Pali: ajānantassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not know; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:2.17
Pali: gilānassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is sick; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:2.18
Pali: ñātakānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is from relatives; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:2.19
Pali: pavāritānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is from those who have given an invitation; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:2.20
Pali: aññassatthāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is for the benefit of someone else; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:2.21
Pali: attano dhanena, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is by means of his own property; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:2.22
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:2.23
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:2.24
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk37:2.25
Pali: Sattamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The seventh training rule is finished.

English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on respectfully 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk38:0.5
Pali: 38. Ujjhānasaññīsikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 38. Finding fault 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk38:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk38:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū ujjhānasaññī paresaṁ pattaṁ olokenti …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the monks from the group of six were looking at the bowls of others finding fault. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk38:1.3.1
Pali: “Na ujjhānasaññī paresaṁ pattaṁ olokessāmīti sikkhā karaṇīyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “‘I will not look at another’s almsbowl finding fault,’ this is how you should train.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk38:1.4
Pali: Na ujjhānasaññinā paresaṁ patto oloketabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): One should not look at the bowl of another finding fault. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk38:1.5
Pali: Yo anādariyaṁ paṭicca ujjhānasaññī paresaṁ pattaṁ oloketi, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk, out of disrespect, looks at the bowl of another finding fault, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk38:1.6.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk38:1.7.1
Pali: asañcicca, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is unintentional; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk38:1.7.2
Pali: assatiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is not mindful; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk38:1.7.3
Pali: ajānantassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not know; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk38:1.7.4
Pali: “dassāmī”ti vā “dāpessāmī”ti vā oloketi, 
English (brahmali, published): if he looks with the intention of giving or having someone give; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk38:1.7.5
Pali: na ujjhānasaññissa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is not finding fault; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk38:1.7.6
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk38:1.8
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk38:1.9
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk38:1.10
Pali: Aṭṭhamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The eighth training rule is finished.

Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk43:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū sakabaḷena mukhena byāharanti …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the monks from the group of six spoke with food in their mouths. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk43:1.3.1
Pali: “Na sakabaḷena mukhena byāharissāmīti sikkhā karaṇīyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “‘I will not speak with food in my mouth,’ this is how you should train.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk43:1.4
Pali: Na sakabaḷena mukhena byāharitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): One should not speak with food in one’s mouth. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk43:1.5
Pali: Yo anādariyaṁ paṭicca sakabaḷena mukhena byāharati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk, out of disrespect, speaks with food in his mouth, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk43:1.6.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk43:1.7.1
Pali: asañcicca …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): if it is unintentional; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk43:1.8
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk43:1.9
Pali: Tatiyasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The third training rule is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk44:1.8

English (brahmali, published): if he is not mindful; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk44:1.9
Pali: ajānantassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not know; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk44:1.10

English (brahmali, published): if he is sick; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk44:1.11

English (brahmali, published): if it is a fresh food; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk44:1.12

English (brahmali, published): if it is any kind of fruit; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk44:1.13
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk44:1.14
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk44:1.15
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk44:1.16

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk51:1.3

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk51:1.9

English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …”

English (brahmali, published): One should not receive the drinking-water vessel with a hand soiled with food. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk55:1.16
Pali: Yo anādariyaṁ paṭicca sāmisena hatthena pānīyathālakaṁ paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk, out of disrespect, receives the drinking-water vessel with a hand soiled with food, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk55:1.17.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk55:1.18
Pali: asañcicca, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is unintentional; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk55:1.19
Pali: assatiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is not mindful; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk55:1.20
Pali: ajānantassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not know; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk55:1.21
Pali: gilānassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is sick; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk55:1.22
Pali: “dhovissāmī”ti vā “dhovāpessāmī”ti vā paṭiggaṇhāti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he receives it with the intention of washing it or having it washed; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk55:1.23
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk55:1.24
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk55:1.25
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk55:1.26
Pali: Pañcamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The fifth training rule is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk56:0.5
Pali: 56. Sasitthakasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 56. Discarding waste water with rice 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk56:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā bhaggesu viharati susumāragire bhesakaḷāvane migadāye. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying in the Bhagga country at Susumāragira in the Bhesakaḷā Grove, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk56:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū kokanade pāsāde sasitthakaṁ pattadhovanaṁ antaraghare chaḍḍenti. 
English (brahmali, published): the monks in the Kokanada stilt house were discarding their bowl-washing water containing rice in inhabited areas. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk56:1.3
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk56:1.4
Pali: “kathañhi nāma samaṇā sakyaputtiyā sasitthakaṁ pattadhovanaṁ antaraghare chaḍḍessanti, seyyathāpi gihī kāmabhogino”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the Sakyan monastics discard their bowl-washing water containing rice in inhabited areas? They’re just like householders who indulge in worldly pleasures!” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk56:1.5
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk56:1.6
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk56:1.7
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhū sasitthakaṁ pattadhovanaṁ antaraghare chaḍḍessantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can those monks do this?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk56:1.8
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhū sasitthakaṁ pattadhovanaṁ antaraghare chaḍḍentī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that they do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk56:1.9
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk56:1.10
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk56:1.11

English (brahmali, published): “How can those foolish men do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk56:1.12
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk56:1.13
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk56:1.14.1

English (brahmali, published): ‘“I will not discard bowl-washing water containing rice in inhabited areas,” this is how you should train.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk56:1.15
Pali: Na sasitthakaṁ pattadhovanaṁ antaraghare chaḍḍetabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): One should not discard bowl-washing water containing rice in an inhabited area. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk56:1.16
Pali: Yo anādariyaṁ paṭicca sasitthakaṁ pattadhovanaṁ antaraghare chaḍḍeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk, out of disrespect, discards bowl-washing water containing rice in an inhabited area, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk56:1.17.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk56:1.18
Pali: asañcicca, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is unintentional; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk56:1.19
Pali: assatiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is not mindful; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk56:1.20
Pali: ajānantassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not know; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk56:1.21
Pali: gilānassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is sick; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk56:1.22
Pali: uddharitvā vā bhinditvā vā paṭiggahe vā nīharitvā vā chaḍḍeti,

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk56:1.23
Pali: āpadāsu,

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk56:1.24

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk57:2.1

English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk57:2.5
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): and they told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk57:2.6
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards he gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk57:2.7

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk57:2.8
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk57:2.9.1

English (brahmali, published): ‘“I will not give a teaching to anyone holding a sunshade who is not sick,” this is how you should train.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk57:2.10.1
Pali: Chattaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A sunshade: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk57:2.11
Pali: tīṇi chattāni— setacchattaṁ, kilañjacchattaṁ, paṇṇacchattaṁ maṇḍalabaddhaṁ salākabaddhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): there are three kinds of sunshades: the white sunshade, the reed sunshade, the leaf sunshade. They are bound at the rim and bound at the ribs. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk57:2.12
Pali: Dhammo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Teaching: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk57:2.13
Pali: buddhabhāsito sāvakabhāsito isibhāsito devatābhāsito atthūpasañhito dhammūpasañhito. 
English (brahmali, published): what has been spoken by the Buddha, what has been spoken by disciples, what has been spoken by sages, what has been spoken by gods, what is connected with what is beneficial, what is connected with the Teaching. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk57:2.14
Pali: Deseyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Give: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk57:2.15
Pali: padena deseti, pade pade āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if he teaches by the line, then for every line he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk57:2.16

English (brahmali, published): If he teaches by the syllable, then for every syllable he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk57:2.17
Pali: Na chattapāṇissa agilānassa dhammo desetabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): One should not give a teaching to anyone holding a sunshade who is not sick. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk57:2.18

Pali: Navamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ.

  - brahmali [published]: translation/en/brahmali/vinaya/pli-tv-bu-vb/pli-tv-bu-vb-sk/pli-tv-bu-vb-sk67_translation-en-brahmali.json

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk67:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk67:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk67:0.3
Pali: Sekhiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on training 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk67:0.4
Pali: Pādukavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on shoes 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk67:0.5
Pali: 67. Oguṇṭhitasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 67. Covering the head 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk67:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk67:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū oguṇṭhitasīsassa dhammaṁ desenti …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the monks from the group of six gave teachings to people with covered heads. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk67:1.3.1
Pali: “Na oguṇṭhitasīsassa agilānassa dhammaṁ desessāmīti sikkhā karaṇīyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “‘I will not give a teaching to anyone with a covered head who is not sick,’ this is how you should train.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk67:1.4.1
Pali: Oguṇṭhitasīso nāma 
English (brahmali, published): With a covered head: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk67:1.5
Pali: sasīsaṁ pāruto vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): the upper robe covering the head is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk67:1.6
Pali: Na oguṇṭhitasīsassa agilānassa dhammo desetabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): One should not give a teaching to anyone with a covered head who is not sick. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk67:1.7
Pali: Yo anādariyaṁ paṭicca oguṇṭhitasīsassa agilānassa dhammaṁ deseti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk, out of disrespect, gives a teaching to someone with a covered head who is not sick, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk67:1.8.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk67:1.9
Pali: asañcicca, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is unintentional; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk67:1.10
Pali: assatiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is not mindful; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk67:1.11
Pali: ajānantassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not know; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk67:1.12
Pali: gilānassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is sick; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk67:1.13
Pali: sīsaṁ vivarāpetvā deseti, 
English (brahmali, published): if he teaches the person after having them uncover their head; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk67:1.14
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk67:1.15
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk67:1.16
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk67:1.17
Pali: Sattamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The seventh training rule is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk68:1.6.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense:

English (brahmali, published): The eighth training rule is finished.
