pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69
Root: root/pli/ms/vinaya/pli-tv-bu-vb/pli-tv-bu-vb-sk/pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69_root-pli-ms.json

English translation sources:
  - brahmali [published]: translation/en/brahmali/vinaya/pli-tv-bu-vb/pli-tv-bu-vb-sk/pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69_translation-en-brahmali.json

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:0.3
Pali: Sekhiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on training 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:0.4
Pali: Pādukavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on shoes 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:0.5
Pali: 69. Nīcāsanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 69. Sitting on a low seat 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū nīce āsane nisīditvā ucce āsane nisinnassa dhammaṁ desenti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the monks from the group of six were sitting on low seats while giving teachings to people sitting on high seats. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.3
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.4
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū nīce āsane nisīditvā ucce āsane nisinnassa dhammaṁ desessantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the monks from the group of six sit on low seats while giving teachings to people sitting on high seats?” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.5
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, nīce āsane nisīditvā ucce āsane nisinnassa dhammaṁ desethā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.6
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.7
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.8
Pali: kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, nīce āsane nisīditvā ucce āsane nisinnassa dhammaṁ desessatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.9
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.10
Pali: vigarahitvā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.11
Pali: dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): he gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.12.1
Pali: “Bhūtapubbaṁ, bhikkhave, bārāṇasiyaṁ aññatarassa chapakassa pajāpati gabbhinī ahosi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Once upon a time in Bārāṇasī, monks, there was a low-caste man whose wife became pregnant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.13
Pali: Atha kho, bhikkhave, sā chapakī taṁ chapakaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): She said to him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.14
Pali: ‘gabbhinīmhi, ayyaputta. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘I’m pregnant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.15
Pali: Icchāmi ambaṁ khāditun’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I crave mangoes.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.16
Pali: ‘Natthi ambaṁ, akālo ambassā’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘But there are no mangoes. It’s the wrong season.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.17
Pali: ‘Sace na labhissāmi marissāmī’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If I don’t get any, I’ll die.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.18
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena rañño ambo dhuvaphalo hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the king had a mango tree that was always bearing fruit. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.19
Pali: Atha kho, bhikkhave, so chapako yena so ambo tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ ambaṁ abhiruhitvā nilīno acchi. 
English (brahmali, published): Then that low-caste man went to that mango tree, climbed it, and hid himself. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.20
Pali: Atha kho, bhikkhave, rājā purohitena brāhmaṇena saddhiṁ yena so ambo tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā ucce āsane nisīditvā mantaṁ pariyāpuṇāti. 
English (brahmali, published): Just then the king and his brahmin counselor went to that same mango tree. There the king sat on a high seat while learning the Vedas. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.21
Pali: Atha kho, bhikkhave, tassa chapakassa etadahosi—
English (brahmali, published): The low-caste man thought, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.22
Pali: ‘yāva adhammiko ayaṁ rājā, yatra hi nāma ucce āsane nisīditvā mantaṁ pariyāpuṇissati. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘How wrong-headed this king is, sitting on a high seat while learning the Vedas. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.23
Pali: Ayañca brāhmaṇo adhammiko, yatra hi nāma nīce āsane nisīditvā ucce āsane nisinnassa mantaṁ vācessati. 
English (brahmali, published): And the brahmin is wrong-headed too, sitting on a low seat while teaching the Vedas to one sitting on a high seat. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.24
Pali: Ahañcamhi adhammiko, yohaṁ itthiyā kāraṇā rañño ambaṁ avaharāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): And I’m wrong-headed too, stealing mangoes from the king because of a woman. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.25
Pali: Sabbamidaṁ carimaṁ katan’ti 
English (brahmali, published): This is all so low!’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.26
Pali: tattheva paripati. 
English (brahmali, published): And he fell out of the tree right there. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.27.1
Pali: ‘Ubho atthaṁ na jānanti, 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Neither understands what is good, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.28
Pali: ubho dhammaṁ na passare; 
English (brahmali, published): Neither sees the Truth: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.29
Pali: Yo cāyaṁ mantaṁ vāceti, 
English (brahmali, published): Not he who teaches the Vedas, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.30
Pali: yo cādhammenadhīyati’. 
English (brahmali, published): Nor he who learns improperly.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.31.1
Pali: ‘Sālīnaṁ odano bhutto, 
English (brahmali, published): ‘I’ve eaten the finest rice, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.32
Pali: sucimaṁsūpasecano; 
English (brahmali, published): With a curry of pure meat: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.33
Pali: Tasmā dhamme na vattāmi, 
English (brahmali, published): Therefore I’m not practicing the Teaching, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.34
Pali: dhammo ariyebhi vaṇṇito’. 
English (brahmali, published): The Teaching praised by the Noble Ones.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.35.1
Pali: ‘Dhiratthu taṁ dhanalābhaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): ‘A curse it is the obtaining of wealth, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.36
Pali: yasalābhañca brāhmaṇa; 
English (brahmali, published): And so is becoming popular, brahmin; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.37
Pali: Yā vutti vinipātena, 
English (brahmali, published): These things come with a low rebirth, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.38
Pali: adhammacaraṇena vā. 
English (brahmali, published): Or with wrong-headed behavior. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.39
Pali: Paribbaja mahābrahme, 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Go forth, great brahmin, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.40
Pali: pacantaññepi pāṇino; 
English (brahmali, published): Other beings will do the cooking; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.41
Pali: Mā tvaṁ adhammo ācarito, 
English (brahmali, published): Don’t go against the Teaching, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.42
Pali: asmā kumbhamivābhidā’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Because you’ll break like a jar.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.43
Pali: Tadāpi me, bhikkhave, amanāpā nīce āsane nisīditvā ucce āsane nisinnassa mantaṁ vācetuṁ, kimaṅgaṁ pana etarahi na amanāpā bhavissati nīce āsane nisīditvā ucce āsane nisinnassa dhammaṁ desetuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Even at that time, monks, I was displeased by someone teaching the Vedas while sitting on a low seat to someone sitting on a high seat. How, then, could it not be displeasing now? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.44
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.45
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.46.1
Pali: “Na nīce āsane nisīditvā ucce āsane nisinnassa agilānassa dhammaṁ desessāmīti sikkhā karaṇīyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘“I will not give a teaching while sitting on a low seat to anyone sitting on a high seat who is not sick,” this is how you should train.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.47
Pali: Na nīce āsane nisīditvā ucce āsane nisinnassa agilānassa dhammo desetabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): One should not give a teaching while sitting on a low seat to anyone sitting on a high seat who is not sick. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.48
Pali: Yo anādariyaṁ paṭicca nīce āsane nisīditvā ucce āsane nisinnassa agilānassa dhammaṁ deseti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk, out of disrespect, gives a teaching while sitting on a low seat to someone sitting on a high seat who is not sick, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.49.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.50.1
Pali: asañcicca …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): if it is unintentional; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.51
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk69:1.52
Pali: Navamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The ninth training rule is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk71:0.5
Pali: 71. Pacchatogamanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 71. Walking behind 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk71:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk71:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū pacchato gacchantā purato gacchantassa dhammaṁ desenti …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the monks from the group of six were giving teachings to people walking in front of them. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk71:1.3.1
Pali: “Na pacchato gacchanto purato gacchantassa agilānassa dhammaṁ desessāmīti sikkhā karaṇīyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “‘I will not give a teaching to anyone walking in front of me who is not sick,’ this is how you should train.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk71:1.4
Pali: Na pacchato gacchantena purato gacchantassa agilānassa dhammo desetabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): One should not give a teaching to anyone walking in front of oneself who is not sick. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk71:1.5
Pali: Yo anādariyaṁ paṭicca pacchato gacchanto purato gacchantassa agilānassa dhammaṁ deseti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk, out of disrespect, gives a teaching to someone walking in front of him who is not sick, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk71:1.6.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk71:1.7.1
Pali: asañcicca …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): if it is unintentional; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk71:1.8
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-sk71:1.9
Pali: Ekādasamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The eleventh training rule is finished.

Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:1.1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā seyyasako anabhirato brahmacariyaṁ carati. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time Venerable Seyyasaka was dissatisfied with the spiritual life. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:1.1.3
Pali: So tena kiso hoti lūkho dubbaṇṇo uppaṇḍuppaṇḍukajāto dhamanisanthatagatto. 
English (brahmali, published): He became thin, haggard, and pale, with veins protruding all over his body. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:1.1.4
Pali: Addasa kho āyasmā udāyī āyasmantaṁ seyyasakaṁ kisaṁ lūkhaṁ dubbaṇṇaṁ uppaṇḍuppaṇḍukajātaṁ dhamanisanthatagattaṁ. Disvāna āyasmantaṁ seyyasakaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Udāyī saw him in this condition and said to him,

English (brahmali, published): “Seyyasaka, you’re not looking well. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:1.1.6
Pali: Kacci no tvaṁ, āvuso seyyasaka, anabhirato brahmacariyaṁ carasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): You’re not dissatisfied with the spiritual life, are you?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:1.1.7
Pali: “Evamāvuso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I am.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:1.1.8

English (brahmali, published): “Well then, eat, sleep, and bathe as much as you like. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:1.1.9

English (brahmali, published): And whenever you become discontent and lust overwhelms you, just masturbate with your hand.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:1.1.10

English (brahmali, published): “But is that allowable?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:1.1.11
Pali: “Āma, āvuso. Ahampi evaṁ karomī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes, I do it too.”

English (brahmali, published): “No.

English (brahmali, published): I just eat, sleep, and bathe as much as I like,

English (brahmali, published): and whenever I become discontent and lust overwhelms me, I masturbate with my hand.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:1.2.1

English (brahmali, published): “Do you eat the food given in faith with the same hand you use to masturbate?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:1.2.2

English (brahmali, published): “Yes.”

English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized him,

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:1.2.5

English (brahmali, published): They rebuked Seyyasaka in many ways and then told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:1.2.6

English (brahmali, published): The Buddha then had the Sangha gathered and questioned Seyyasaka:

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:1.2.8
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:1.2.9

English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:1.2.10

English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, it’s not suitable, it’s not proper, it’s not worthy of a monastic, it’s not allowable, it’s not to be done. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:1.2.11
Pali: Kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, hatthena upakkamitvā asuciṁ mocessasi. 
English (brahmali, published): How can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:1.2.12

English (brahmali, published): Haven’t I given many teachings for the sake of dispassion, not for the sake of passion; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:1.2.13

English (brahmali, published): for freedom from bondage, not for the sake of bondage;

English (brahmali, published): for the sake of non-grasping, not for the sake of grasping? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:1.2.15

English (brahmali, published): When I’ve taught like this, how can you choose passion, bondage, and grasping? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:1.2.16

English (brahmali, published): Haven’t I given many teachings for the fading away of lust, for the clearing away of intoxication, for the removal of thirst, for the uprooting of attachment, for the cutting off of the round of rebirth, for the stopping of craving, for fading away, for ending, for extinguishment? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:1.2.17

English (brahmali, published): Haven’t I given many teachings for the abandoning of worldly pleasures, for the full understanding of the perceptions of worldly pleasures, for the removal of thirst for worldly pleasures, for the elimination of thoughts of worldly pleasures, for the stilling of the fevers of worldly pleasures? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:1.2.18

English (brahmali, published): only the Sangha gives probation for that offense, sends back to the beginning, gives the trial period, and rehabilitates—not several monks, not an individual. Therefore it is called “an offense entailing suspension”.

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.8
Pali: ākāse vāyamantassa aṅgajātaṁ kammaniyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): for one making an effort in the air, the penis becomes erect. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.9
Pali: Rāgūpatthambheti 
English (brahmali, published): Because of stiffness due to sensual desire: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.10
Pali: rāgena pīḷitassa aṅgajātaṁ kammaniyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): for one oppressed by sensual desire, the penis becomes erect. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.11
Pali: Vaccūpatthambheti 
English (brahmali, published): Because of stiffness due to feces: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.12
Pali: vaccena pīḷitassa aṅgajātaṁ kammaniyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): for one oppressed by feces, the penis becomes erect. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.13
Pali: Passāvūpatthambheti 
English (brahmali, published): Because of stiffness due to urine: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.14
Pali: passāvena pīḷitassa aṅgajātaṁ kammaniyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): for one oppressed by urine, the penis becomes erect. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.15
Pali: Vātūpatthambheti 
English (brahmali, published): Because of stiffness due to intestinal gas: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.16
Pali: vātena pīḷitassa aṅgajātaṁ kammaniyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): for one oppressed by intestinal gas, the penis becomes erect. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.17
Pali: Uccāliṅgapāṇakadaṭṭhūpatthambheti 
English (brahmali, published): Because of stiffness due to being bitten by caterpillars: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.18
Pali: uccāliṅgapāṇakadaṭṭhena aṅgajātaṁ kammaniyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): for one bitten by caterpillars, the penis becomes erect. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.19
Pali: Ārogyatthāyāti 
English (brahmali, published): For the sake of health: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.20
Pali: arogo bhavissāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): thinking, “I’ll be healthy.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.21
Pali: Sukhatthāyāti 
English (brahmali, published): For the sake of pleasure: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.22
Pali: sukhaṁ vedanaṁ uppādessāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): thinking, “I’ll produce a pleasant feeling.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.23
Pali: Bhesajjatthāyāti 
English (brahmali, published): For the sake of medicine: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.24
Pali: bhesajjaṁ bhavissati. 
English (brahmali, published): thinking, “There will be medicine.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.25

English (brahmali, published): For the sake of a gift: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.26
Pali: dānaṁ dassāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): thinking, “I’ll give a gift.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.27

English (brahmali, published): For the sake of merit: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.28

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.29

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.30
Pali: yaññaṁ yajissāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): thinking, “I’ll offer a sacrifice.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.31
Pali: Saggatthāyāti

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.32
Pali: saggaṁ gamissāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): thinking, “I’ll go to heaven.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.33
Pali: Bījatthāyāti 
English (brahmali, published): For the sake of seed: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.34
Pali: bījaṁ bhavissati. 
English (brahmali, published): thinking, “There will be seed.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.35
Pali: Vīmaṁsatthāyāti 
English (brahmali, published): For the sake of investigating: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.36
Pali: nīlaṁ bhavissati, pītakaṁ bhavissati, lohitakaṁ bhavissati, odātaṁ bhavissati, takkavaṇṇaṁ bhavissati, dakavaṇṇaṁ bhavissati, telavaṇṇaṁ bhavissati, khīravaṇṇaṁ bhavissati, dadhivaṇṇaṁ bhavissati, sappivaṇṇaṁ bhavissatīti. 
English (brahmali, published): thinking, “Will it be blue?”, “Will it be yellow?”, “Will it be red?”, “Will it be white?”, “Will it be the color of buttermilk?”, “Will it be the color of water?”, “Will it be the color of oil?”, “Will it be the color of milk?”, “Will it be the color of curd?”, “Will it be the color of ghee?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.37
Pali: Davatthāyāti 
English (brahmali, published): For the sake of fun: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.2.38
Pali: khiḍḍādhippāyo. 
English (brahmali, published): desiring to play. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.3.1
Pali: Ajjhattarūpe ceteti upakkamati muccati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, by means of his own body, he intends and makes an effort, and semen is emitted, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.3.2
Pali: Bahiddhārūpe ceteti upakkamati muccati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, by means of something external, he intends and makes an effort, and semen is emitted, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.3.3
Pali: Ajjhattabahiddhārūpe ceteti upakkamati muccati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, both by means of his own body and by means of something external, he intends and makes an effort, and semen is emitted, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:3.3.4
Pali: Ākāse kaṭiṁ kampento ceteti upakkamati muccati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, shaking the pelvis in the air, he intends and makes an effort, and semen is emitted, he commits an offense entailing suspension.

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:4.1
Pali: Ceteti upakkamati muccati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he intends, makes an effort, and semen is emitted, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:4.2
Pali: Ceteti upakkamati na muccati, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he intends, makes an effort, but semen is not emitted, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:4.3
Pali: Ceteti na upakkamati muccati, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he intends, but does not make an effort, yet semen is emitted, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:4.4
Pali: Ceteti na upakkamati na muccati, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he intends, but does not make an effort, nor is semen emitted, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:4.5
Pali: Na ceteti upakkamati muccati, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not intend, but makes an effort, and semen is emitted, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:4.6
Pali: Na ceteti upakkamati na muccati, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not intend, but makes an effort, yet semen is not emitted, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:4.7
Pali: Na ceteti na upakkamati muccati, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not intend, nor make an effort, yet semen is emitted, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:4.8
Pali: Na ceteti na upakkamati na muccati, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not intend, nor make an effort, nor is semen emitted, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:4.9.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:4.10
Pali: supinantena, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is while dreaming; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:4.11
Pali: namocanādhippāyassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is not aiming at emission; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:4.12
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:4.13
Pali: khittacittassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is deranged; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:4.14
Pali: vedanāṭṭassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is overwhelmed by pain; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:4.15
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:4.16
Pali: 1.1. Vinītavatthuuddānagāthā 
English (brahmali, published): Summary verses of case studies 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:4.17
Pali: Supinoccārapassāvo, 
English (brahmali, published): “Dream, feces, urine, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:4.18
Pali: vitakkuṇhodakena ca; 
English (brahmali, published): Thought, and with warm water; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:4.19
Pali: Bhesajjaṁ kaṇḍuvaṁ maggo, 
English (brahmali, published): Medicine, scratching, path, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:4.20
Pali: vatthi jantāgharupakkamo. 
English (brahmali, published): Foreskin, sauna, massage. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:4.21
Pali: Sāmaṇero ca sutto ca, 
English (brahmali, published): Novice, and asleep, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:4.22
Pali: ūru muṭṭhinā pīḷayi; 
English (brahmali, published): Thigh, pressed with the fist; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:4.23
Pali: Ākāse thambhaṁ nijjhāyi, 
English (brahmali, published): In the air, rigid, staring, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:4.24
Pali: chiddaṁ kaṭṭhena ghaṭṭayi. 
English (brahmali, published): Keyhole, rubbed with wood. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:4.25
Pali: Soto udañjalaṁ dhāvaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): Current, mud, running, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:4.26
Pali: pupphāvaliyaṁ pokkharaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): Mud play, lotus; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:4.27
Pali: Vālikā kaddamusseko, 
English (brahmali, published): Sand, mud, pouring, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:4.28
Pali: sayanaṅguṭṭhakena cāti. 
English (brahmali, published): Bed, and with the thumb.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:5.1.0
Pali: 1.2. Vinītavatthu 
English (brahmali, published): Case studies 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:5.1.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarassa bhikkhuno supinantena asuci mucci. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion a monk had an emission of semen while dreaming. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:5.1.2
Pali: Tassa kukkuccaṁ ahosi—
English (brahmali, published): He became anxious, thinking, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:5.1.3
Pali: “bhagavatā sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ, kacci nu kho ahaṁ saṅghādisesaṁ āpattiṁ āpanno”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “The Buddha has laid down a training rule. Could it be that I’ve committed an offense entailing suspension?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:5.1.4
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): He told the Buddha, who said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:5.1.5
Pali: “Anāpatti, bhikkhu, supinantenā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “There’s no offense when it occurs while dreaming.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:5.2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarassa bhikkhuno uccāraṁ karontassa asuci mucci. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion a monk was defecating, and semen was emitted. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:5.2.2
Pali: Tassa kukkuccaṁ ahosi …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): He became anxious … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:5.2.3
Pali: “kiṁcitto tvaṁ, bhikkhū”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What were you thinking, monk?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:5.2.4
Pali: “Nāhaṁ, bhagavā, mocanādhippāyo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I wasn’t aiming at emission, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:5.2.5
Pali: “Anāpatti, bhikkhu, namocanādhippāyassā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “There’s no offense if one isn’t aiming at emission.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:5.2.6
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarassa bhikkhuno passāvaṁ karontassa asuci mucci. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion a monk was urinating, and semen was emitted. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:5.2.7
Pali: Tassa kukkuccaṁ ahosi …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): He became anxious … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:5.2.8
Pali: “anāpatti, bhikkhu, namocanādhippāyassā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “There’s no offense if one isn’t aiming at emission.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:5.3.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarassa bhikkhuno kāmavitakkaṁ vitakkentassa asuci mucci. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion a monk was thinking a worldly thought, and semen was emitted. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:5.3.2
Pali: Tassa kukkuccaṁ ahosi …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): He became anxious … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:5.3.3
Pali: “anāpatti, bhikkhu, vitakkentassā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “There’s no offense for one thinking a worldly thought.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:5.4.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarassa bhikkhuno uṇhodakena nhāyantassa asuci mucci. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion a monk was bathing in warm water, and semen was emitted. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:5.4.2
Pali: Tassa kukkuccaṁ ahosi …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): He became anxious … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:5.4.3
Pali: “kiṁcitto tvaṁ, bhikkhū”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What were you thinking, monk?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:5.4.4
Pali: “Nāhaṁ, bhagavā, mocanādhippāyo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I wasn’t aiming at emission, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:5.4.5
Pali: “Anāpatti, bhikkhu, namocanādhippāyassā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “There’s no offense if one isn’t aiming at emission.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:5.4.6
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarassa bhikkhuno mocanādhippāyassa uṇhodakena nhāyantassa asuci mucci. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion a monk bathed in warm water aiming at emission, and semen was emitted. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss1:5.4.7
Pali: Tassa kukkuccaṁ ahosi …pe…

English (brahmali, published): At that time Venerable Udāyī was staying in the wilderness.

English (brahmali, published): The bed and bench were nicely made up,

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:1.1.27
Pali: Kathañhi nāma samaṇo udāyī mama bhariyāya aṅgamaṅgāni parāmasissati. 
English (brahmali, published): How could the ascetic Udāyī touch my wife all over? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:1.1.28
Pali: Na hi sakkā kulitthīhi kuladhītāhi kulakumārīhi kulasuṇhāhi kuladāsīhi ārāmaṁ vā vihāraṁ vā gantuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): It’s not possible to go to a monastery or a monk’s dwelling with a wife from a respectable family, or with a daughter, a girl, a daughter-in-law, or a female slave from a respectable family. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:1.1.29
Pali: Sace kulitthiyo kuladhītaro kulakumāriyo kulasuṇhāyo kuladāsiyo ārāmaṁ vā vihāraṁ vā gaccheyyuṁ, tāpi samaṇā sakyaputtiyā dūseyyun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If you do, the Sakyan monastics might molest them.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:1.2.1

English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized Udāyī,

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:1.2.4

English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:1.2.5
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe bhikkhusaṅghaṁ sannipātāpetvā āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ paṭipucchi—
English (brahmali, published): He then had the Sangha gathered and questioned Udāyī: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:1.2.6
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, udāyi, mātugāmena saddhiṁ kāyasaṁsaggaṁ samāpajjasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Udāyī, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:1.2.7
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:1.2.8
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā—
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:1.2.9
Pali: “ananucchavikaṁ, moghapurisa, ananulomikaṁ appatirūpaṁ assāmaṇakaṁ akappiyaṁ akaraṇīyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, it’s not suitable, it’s not proper, it’s not worthy of a monastic, it’s not allowable, it’s not to be done. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:1.2.10
Pali: Kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, mātugāmena saddhiṁ kāyasaṁsaggaṁ samāpajjissasi. 
English (brahmali, published): How could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:1.2.11
Pali: Nanu mayā, moghapurisa, anekapariyāyena virāgāya dhammo desito no sarāgāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): Haven’t I given many teachings for the sake of dispassion, not for the sake of passion … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:1.2.12
Pali: kāmapariḷāhānaṁ vūpasamo akkhāto. 
English (brahmali, published): the stilling of the fevers of worldly pleasures? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:1.2.13
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:1.2.14
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:1.2.15.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu otiṇṇo vipariṇatena cittena mātugāmena saddhiṁ kāyasaṁsaggaṁ samāpajjeyya hatthaggāhaṁ vā veṇiggāhaṁ vā aññatarassa vā aññatarassa vā aṅgassa parāmasanaṁ, saṅghādiseso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk, overcome by lust and with a distorted mind, makes physical contact with a woman—holding her hand or hair, or touching any part of her body—he commits an offense entailing suspension.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.1.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.1.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.1.5
Pali: Otiṇṇo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Overcome by lust: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.1.6
Pali: sāratto apekkhavā paṭibaddhacitto. 
English (brahmali, published): having lust, longing for, in love with. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.1.7
Pali: Vipariṇatanti 
English (brahmali, published): Distorted: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.1.8
Pali: rattampi cittaṁ vipariṇataṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): a lustful mind is distorted; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.1.9
Pali: Duṭṭhampi cittaṁ vipariṇataṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): an angry mind is distorted; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.1.10
Pali: Mūḷhampi cittaṁ vipariṇataṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): a confused mind is distorted. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.1.11
Pali: Api ca rattaṁ cittaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetaṁ vipariṇatanti. 
English (brahmali, published): But in this case “distorted” refers to the lustful mind. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.1.12
Pali: Mātugāmo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A woman: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.1.13
Pali: manussitthī, na yakkhī na petī, na tiracchānagatā. Antamaso tadahujātāpi dārikā, pageva mahattarī. 
English (brahmali, published): a human female, not a female spirit, not a female ghost, not a female animal; even a girl born on that very day, let alone an older one. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.1.14
Pali: Saddhinti 
English (brahmali, published): With: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.1.15
Pali: ekato. 
English (brahmali, published): together. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.1.16
Pali: Kāyasaṁsaggaṁ samāpajjeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Makes physical contact: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.1.17
Pali: ajjhācāro vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): misconduct is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.1.18
Pali: Hattho nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Hand: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.1.19
Pali: kapparaṁ upādāya yāva agganakhā. 
English (brahmali, published): from the elbow to the tip of the nails. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.1.20
Pali: Veṇī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Hair: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.1.21
Pali: suddhakesā vā, suttamissā vā, mālāmissā vā, hiraññamissā vā, suvaṇṇamissā vā, muttāmissā vā, maṇimissā vā. 
English (brahmali, published): just the hair; or the hair with strings in it, with a garland, with gold coins, with gold, with pearls, or with gems. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.1.22
Pali: Aṅgaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Any part of her body: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.1.23
Pali: hatthañca veṇiñca ṭhapetvā avasesaṁ aṅgaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): anything apart from the hand and the hair is called “any part of her body”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.2.1
Pali: Āmasanā, parāmasanā, omasanā, ummasanā, olaṅghanā, ullaṅghanā, ākaḍḍhanā, patikaḍḍhanā, abhiniggaṇhanā, abhinippīḷanā, gahaṇaṁ, chupanaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Physical contact, touching, stroking downwards, stroking upwards, pulling down, lifting up, pulling, pushing, squeezing, pressing, taking hold of, contacting. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.2.2
Pali: Āmasanā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Physical contact:

English (brahmali, published): mere physical contact. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.2.4

English (brahmali, published): Touching: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.2.5
Pali: itocito ca sañcopanā. 
English (brahmali, published): touching here and there. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.2.6
Pali: Omasanā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Stroking downwards: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.2.7
Pali: heṭṭhā oropanā. 
English (brahmali, published): lowering down. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.2.8

English (brahmali, published): Stroking upwards: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.2.9

Pali: patippaṇāmanā.

English (brahmali, published): Squeezing:

Pali: aṅgaṁ gahetvā nippīḷanā. 
English (brahmali, published): taking hold of a bodily part and then pressing.

English (brahmali, published): Pressing: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.2.21

English (brahmali, published): pressing with something.

Pali: Gahaṇaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Take hold of:

English (brahmali, published): mere taking hold of.

Pali: Chupanaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Contacting:

Pali: phuṭṭhamattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): mere contact. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.2.26
Pali: Saṅghādisesoti 
English (brahmali, published): He commits an offense entailing suspension: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:2.2.27
Pali: …pe… tenapi vuccati “saṅghādiseso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): … Therefore, too, it is called “an offense entailing suspension”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:3.1.1
Pali: Itthī ca hoti itthisaññī sāratto ca bhikkhu ca. 
English (brahmali, published): It is a woman, he perceives her as a woman, and he has lust. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:3.1.2
Pali: Naṁ itthiyā kāyena kāyaṁ āmasati parāmasati omasati ummasati olaṅgheti ullaṅgheti ākaḍḍhati patikaḍḍhati abhiniggaṇhāti abhinippīḷeti gaṇhāti chupati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the monk makes physical contact with the woman, body to body, if he touches her, strokes her downwards, strokes her upwards, pulls her down, lifts her up, pulls her, pushes her, squeezes her, presses her, takes hold of her, contacts her, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:3.1.3
Pali: Itthī ca hoti vematiko sāratto ca. 
English (brahmali, published): It is a woman, but he is unsure of it, and he has lust. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:3.1.4
Pali: Bhikkhu ca naṁ itthiyā kāyena kāyaṁ āmasati parāmasati …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If the monk makes physical contact with the woman, body to body, if he touches her … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:3.1.5
Pali: gaṇhāti chupati, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): takes hold of her, contacts her, he commits a serious offense.

English (brahmali, published): It is two <i lang='pi' translate='no'>paṇḍakas</i>, he perceives both as <i lang='pi' translate='no'>paṇḍakas</i>, and he has lust. If the monk makes physical contact with the two <i lang='pi' translate='no'>paṇḍakas</i>, body to body, if he touches them …

English (brahmali, published): takes hold of them, contacts them, he commits two serious offenses.

Pali: Itthī ca hoti itthisaññī sāratto ca.

English (brahmali, published): It is a woman and a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>paṇḍaka</i>, but he perceives both as women, and he has lust. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:3.4.17
Pali: Bhikkhu ca naṁ ubhinnaṁ kāyapaṭibaddhena kāyaṁ āmasati parāmasati …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If the monk, with something connected to his own body, makes physical contact with the body of both, if he touches them …

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:3.5.1
Pali: Itthī ca hoti itthisaññī sāratto ca. 
English (brahmali, published): It is a woman, he perceives her as a woman, and he has lust. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:3.5.2
Pali: Itthī ca naṁ bhikkhussa kāyena kāyaṁ āmasati parāmasati omasati ummasati olaṅgheti ullaṅgheti ākaḍḍhati patikaḍḍhati abhiniggaṇhāti abhinippīḷeti gaṇhāti chupati, sevanādhippāyo kāyena vāyamati phassaṁ paṭivijānāti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): If the woman makes physical contact with the monk, body to body, if she touches him, strokes him downwards, strokes him upwards, pulls him down, lifts him up, pulls him, pushes him, squeezes him, presses him, takes hold of him, contacts him, and he, aiming at connection, makes an effort with the body and experiences contact, he commits an offense entailing suspension. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:3.5.3
Pali: Dve itthiyo dvinnaṁ itthīnaṁ itthisaññī sāratto ca. 
English (brahmali, published): It is two women, he perceives both as women, and he has lust. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2:3.5.4
Pali: Itthiyo ca naṁ bhikkhussa kāyena kāyaṁ āmasanti parāmasanti omasanti ummasanti olaṅghenti ullaṅghenti ākaḍḍhanti patikaḍḍhanti abhiniggaṇhanti abhinippīḷenti gaṇhanti chupanti, sevanādhippāyo kāyena vāyamati phassaṁ paṭivijānāti, āpatti dvinnaṁ saṅghādisesānaṁ …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): If the women make physical contact with the monk, body to body, if they touch him, stroke him downwards, stroke him upwards, pull him down, lift him up, pull him, push him, squeeze him, press him, take hold of him, contact him, and he, aiming at connection, makes an effort with the body and experiences contact, he commits two offenses entailing suspension. …

English (brahmali, published): and he, aiming at connection, makes an effort with the body and experiences contact, he commits two offenses of wrong conduct. …

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:0.3
Pali: Saṅghādisesakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing suspension 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:0.4
Pali: 3. Duṭṭhullavācāsikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 3. Indecent speech 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:1.1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:1.1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā udāyī araññe viharati. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time Venerable Udāyī was staying in a beautiful dwelling in the wilderness. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:1.1.3
Pali: Tassāyasmato vihāro abhirūpo hoti dassanīyo pāsādiko. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:1.1.4
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā itthiyo ārāmaṁ āgamaṁsu vihārapekkhikāyo. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion a number of women came to the monastery to see the dwellings. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:1.1.5
Pali: Atha kho tā itthiyo yenāyasmā udāyī tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; 
English (brahmali, published): They approached Udāyī 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:1.1.6
Pali: upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:1.1.7
Pali: “icchāma mayaṁ, bhante, ayyassa vihāraṁ pekkhitun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, we would like to see your dwelling.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:1.1.8
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā udāyī tā itthiyo vihāraṁ pekkhāpetvā tāsaṁ itthīnaṁ vaccamaggaṁ passāvamaggaṁ ādissa vaṇṇampi bhaṇati avaṇṇampi bhaṇati yācatipi āyācatipi pucchatipi paṭipucchatipi ācikkhatipi anusāsatipi akkosatipi. 
English (brahmali, published): Then, after showing them his dwelling, he praised and disparaged their private parts; he requested and implored, asked and enquired, described, instructed, and abused their private parts. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:1.1.9
Pali: Yā tā itthiyo chinnikā dhuttikā ahirikāyo tā āyasmatā udāyinā saddhiṁ uhasantipi ullapantipi ujjagghantipi uppaṇḍentipi. 
English (brahmali, published): The shameless and indecent women flirted with Udāyī; they called out to him, laughed with him, and teased him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:1.1.10
Pali: Yā pana tā itthiyo hirimanā tā nikkhamitvā bhikkhū ujjhāpenti—
English (brahmali, published): But after leaving, those of them who had a sense of shame complained to the monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:1.1.11
Pali: “idaṁ, bhante, nacchannaṁ nappatirūpaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this isn’t suitable or appropriate. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:1.1.12
Pali: Sāmikenapi mayaṁ evaṁ vuttā na iccheyyāma, kiṁ panāyyena udāyinā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): We wouldn’t want to hear such speech from our own husbands, let alone from Venerable Udāyī.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:1.2.1
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:1.2.2
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā udāyī mātugāmaṁ duṭṭhullāhi vācāhi obhāsissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Udāyī speak indecently to women?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:1.2.3
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They rebuked Udāyī in many ways and told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:1.2.4
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe bhikkhusaṅghaṁ sannipātāpetvā āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ paṭipucchi—
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha had the Sangha gathered and questioned Udāyī: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:1.2.5
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, udāyi, mātugāmaṁ duṭṭhullāhi vācāhi obhāsasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Udāyī, that you spoke like this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:1.2.6
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:1.2.7
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā—
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:1.2.8
Pali: “ananucchavikaṁ, moghapurisa, ananulomikaṁ appatirūpaṁ assāmaṇakaṁ akappiyaṁ akaraṇīyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s not suitable, foolish man, it’s not proper, it’s not worthy of a monastic, it’s not allowable, it’s not to be done. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:1.2.9
Pali: Kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, mātugāmaṁ duṭṭhullāhi vācāhi obhāsissasi. 
English (brahmali, published): How could you speak like this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:1.2.10
Pali: Nanu mayā, moghapurisa, anekapariyāyena virāgāya dhammo desito no sarāgāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): Haven’t I given many teachings for the sake of dispassion, not for the sake of passion … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:1.2.11
Pali: kāmapariḷāhānaṁ vūpasamo akkhāto. 
English (brahmali, published): the stilling of the fevers of worldly pleasures? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:1.2.12
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:1.2.13
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:1.2.14.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu otiṇṇo vipariṇatena cittena mātugāmaṁ duṭṭhullāhi vācāhi obhāseyya yathā taṁ yuvā yuvatiṁ methunupasaṁhitāhi, saṅghādiseso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk, overcome by lust and with a distorted mind, speaks indecent words to a woman, as a young man might to a young woman and referring to sexual intercourse, he commits an offense entailing suspension.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:2.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:2.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:2.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:2.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:2.5
Pali: Otiṇṇo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Overcome by lust: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:2.6
Pali: sāratto apekkhavā paṭibaddhacitto. 
English (brahmali, published): having lust, longing for, in love with. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:2.7
Pali: Vipariṇatanti 
English (brahmali, published): Distorted: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:2.8
Pali: rattampi cittaṁ vipariṇataṁ, duṭṭhampi cittaṁ vipariṇataṁ mūḷhampi cittaṁ vipariṇataṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): a lustful mind is distorted; an angry mind is distorted; a confused mind is distorted. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:2.9
Pali: Api ca rattaṁ cittaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetaṁ vipariṇatanti. 
English (brahmali, published): But in this case “distorted” refers to the lustful mind. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:2.10
Pali: Mātugāmo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A woman: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:2.11
Pali: manussitthī, na yakkhī, na petī, na tiracchānagatā. Viññū paṭibalā subhāsitadubbhāsitaṁ duṭṭhullāduṭṭhullaṁ ājānituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): a human female, not a female spirit, not a female ghost, not a female animal. She understands and is capable of discerning good and bad speech, what is decent and what is indecent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:2.12
Pali: Duṭṭhullā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Indecent words: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:2.13
Pali: vācā vaccamaggapassāvamaggamethunadhammappaṭisaṁyuttā vācā. 
English (brahmali, published): speech connected with the anus, the vagina, or sexual intercourse. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:2.14
Pali: Obhāseyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Speaks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:2.15
Pali: ajjhācāro vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): misconduct is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:2.16
Pali: Yathā taṁ yuvā yuvatinti 
English (brahmali, published): As a young man might to a young woman: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:2.17
Pali: daharo dahariṁ, taruṇo taruṇiṁ, kāmabhogī kāmabhoginiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): a male youth to a female youth, a boy to a girl, a male who indulges in worldly pleasures to a female who indulges in worldly pleasures. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:2.18
Pali: Methunupasaṁhitāhīti 
English (brahmali, published): Referring to sexual intercourse: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:2.19
Pali: methunadhammappaṭisaṁyuttāhi. 
English (brahmali, published): connected with the sexual act. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:2.20
Pali: Saṅghādisesoti 
English (brahmali, published): He commits an offense entailing suspension: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:2.21
Pali: …pe… tenapi vuccati saṅghādisesoti. 
English (brahmali, published): … Therefore, too, it is called “an offense entailing suspension”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.1
Pali: Dve magge ādissa vaṇṇampi bhaṇati, avaṇṇampi bhaṇati, yācatipi, āyācatipi, pucchatipi, paṭipucchatipi, ācikkhatipi, anusāsatipi, akkosatipi. 
English (brahmali, published): Referring to the two private orifices, he praises, disparages, requests, implores, asks, enquires, describes, instructs, abuses. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.2.1
Pali: Vaṇṇaṁ bhaṇati nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Praises: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.3
Pali: dve magge thometi vaṇṇeti pasaṁsati. 
English (brahmali, published): extols, praises, commends the two private orifices. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.4
Pali: Avaṇṇaṁ bhaṇati nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Disparages: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.5
Pali: dve magge khuṁseti vambheti garahati. 
English (brahmali, published): despises, reviles, censures the two private orifices. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.6
Pali: Yācati nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Requests: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.7
Pali: dehi me, arahasi me dātunti. 
English (brahmali, published): saying, “Give to me; you should give to me.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.8
Pali: Āyācati nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Implores: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.9
Pali: kadā te mātā pasīdissati, 
English (brahmali, published): saying, “When will you please your mother?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.10
Pali: kadā te pitā pasīdissati, 
English (brahmali, published): “When will you please your father?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.11
Pali: kadā te devatāyo pasīdissanti, 
English (brahmali, published): “When will you please the gods?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.12
Pali: kadā sukhaṇo sulayo sumuhutto bhavissati, 
English (brahmali, published): “When will there be a good opportunity, a good time, a good moment?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.13
Pali: kadā te methunaṁ dhammaṁ labhissāmīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “When will I have sexual intercourse with you?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.14
Pali: Pucchati nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Asks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.15
Pali: kathaṁ tvaṁ sāmikassa desi, 
English (brahmali, published): saying, “How do you give to your husband?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.16
Pali: kathaṁ jārassa desīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How do you give to your lover?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.17
Pali: Paṭipucchati nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Enquires: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.18
Pali: evaṁ kira tvaṁ sāmikassa desi, 
English (brahmali, published): saying, “So you give to your husband like this, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.19
Pali: evaṁ jārassa desīti. 
English (brahmali, published): and to your lover like this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.20
Pali: Ācikkhati nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Describes: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.21
Pali: puṭṭho bhaṇati— “evaṁ dehi. Evaṁ dentā sāmikassa piyā bhavissati manāpā cā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): when asked, he says, “Give like this. If you do, you’ll be dear and pleasing to your husband.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.22
Pali: Anusāsati nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Instructs: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.23
Pali: apuṭṭho bhaṇati— “evaṁ dehi. Evaṁ dentā sāmikassa piyā bhavissati manāpā cā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): without being asked, he says, “Give like this. If you do, you’ll be dear and pleasing to your husband.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.24
Pali: Akkosati nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Abuses: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.25
Pali: animittāsi, 
English (brahmali, published): saying, “You lack genitals;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.26
Pali: nimittamattāsi, 
English (brahmali, published): “You have incomplete genitals;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.27
Pali: alohitāsi, 
English (brahmali, published): “You don’t menstruate;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.28
Pali: dhuvalohitāsi, 
English (brahmali, published): “You menstruate continuously;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.29
Pali: dhuvacoḷāsi, 
English (brahmali, published): “You always wear a menstruation pad;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.30
Pali: paggharantīsi, 
English (brahmali, published): “You’re incontinent;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.31
Pali: sikharaṇīsi, 
English (brahmali, published): “You have genital prolapse;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.32
Pali: itthipaṇḍakāsi, 
English (brahmali, published): “You lack sexual organs;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.33
Pali: vepurisikāsi, 
English (brahmali, published): “You’re manlike;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.34
Pali: sambhinnāsi, 
English (brahmali, published): “You have fistula;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.1.35
Pali: ubhatobyañjanāsīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “You’re a gynandromorph.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.2.1
Pali: Itthī ca hoti itthisaññī sāratto ca. 
English (brahmali, published): It is a woman, he perceives her as a woman, and he has lust. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.2.2
Pali: Bhikkhu ca naṁ itthiyā vaccamaggaṁ passāvamaggaṁ ādissa vaṇṇampi bhaṇati avaṇṇampi bhaṇati yācatipi āyācatipi pucchatipi paṭipucchatipi ācikkhatipi anusāsatipi akkosatipi, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the monk, referring to the anus or the vagina of the woman, praises, disparages, requests, implores, asks, enquires, describes, instructs, or abuses, he commits an offense entailing suspension. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.2.3
Pali: …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): … (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2#3.1.3'>Bu Ss 2:3.1.3</a>–3.1.49, with appropriate substitutions.) … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.2.4.1
Pali: Dve itthiyo dvinnaṁ itthīnaṁ itthisaññī sāratto ca. 
English (brahmali, published): It is two women, he perceives them both as women, and he has lust. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.2.5
Pali: Bhikkhu ca naṁ dvinnaṁ itthīnaṁ vaccamaggaṁ passāvamaggaṁ ādissa vaṇṇampi bhaṇati avaṇṇampi bhaṇati …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If the monk, referring to the anus or the vagina of both women, praises, disparages … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.2.6
Pali: akkosatipi, āpatti dvinnaṁ saṅghādisesānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): or abuses, he commits two offenses entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.2.7
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): … (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2#3.2.4'>Bu Ss 2:3.2.4</a>–3.2.26, with appropriate substitutions.) … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.2.8.1
Pali: Itthī ca paṇḍako ca ubhinnaṁ itthisaññī sāratto ca. 
English (brahmali, published): It is a woman and a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>paṇḍaka</i>, but he perceives both as women, and he has lust. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.2.9
Pali: Bhikkhu ca naṁ ubhinnaṁ vaccamaggaṁ passāvamaggaṁ ādissa vaṇṇampi bhaṇati avaṇṇampi bhaṇati …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If the monk, referring to the anus or the vagina of both of them, praises, disparages … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.2.10
Pali: akkosatipi, āpatti saṅghādisesena dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): or abuses, he commits one offense entailing suspension and one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.2.11
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): … (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2#3.3.4'>Bu Ss 2:3.3.4</a>–3.3.61, with appropriate substitutions.) … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.3.1
Pali: Itthī ca hoti itthisaññī sāratto ca. 
English (brahmali, published): It is a woman, he perceives her as a woman, and he has lust. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.3.2
Pali: Bhikkhu ca naṁ itthiyā vaccamaggaṁ passāvamaggaṁ ṭhapetvā adhakkhakaṁ ubbhajāṇumaṇḍalaṁ ādissa vaṇṇampi bhaṇati avaṇṇampi bhaṇati …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If the monk, referring to any part of the woman below the collar bone but above the knees, apart from the anus or the vagina, praises, disparages … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.3.3
Pali: akkosatipi, āpatti thullaccayassa …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): or abuses, he commits a serious offense. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.3.4
Pali: Dve itthiyo dvinnaṁ itthīnaṁ itthisaññī sāratto ca. 
English (brahmali, published): It is two women, he perceives them both as women, and he has lust. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.3.5
Pali: Bhikkhu ca naṁ dvinnaṁ itthīnaṁ vaccamaggaṁ passāvamaggaṁ ṭhapetvā adhakkhakaṁ ubbhajāṇumaṇḍalaṁ ādissa vaṇṇampi bhaṇati avaṇṇampi bhaṇati …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If the monk, referring to any part of both women below the collar bone but above the knees, apart from the anus or the vagina, praises, disparages … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.3.6
Pali: akkosatipi, āpatti dvinnaṁ thullaccayānaṁ …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): or abuses, he commits two serious offenses. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.3.7
Pali: Itthī ca paṇḍako ca ubhinnaṁ itthisaññī sāratto ca. 
English (brahmali, published): It is a woman and a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>paṇḍaka</i>, but he perceives both as women, and he has lust. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.3.8
Pali: Bhikkhu ca naṁ ubhinnaṁ vaccamaggaṁ passāvamaggaṁ ṭhapetvā adhakkhakaṁ ubbhajāṇumaṇḍalaṁ ādissa vaṇṇampi bhaṇati avaṇṇampi bhaṇati …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If the monk, referring to any part of both of them below the collar bone but above the knees, apart from the anus or the vagina, praises, disparages … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.3.9
Pali: akkosatipi, āpatti thullaccayena dukkaṭassa …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): or abuses, he commits one serious offense and one offense of wrong conduct. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.4.1
Pali: Itthī ca hoti itthisaññī sāratto ca. 
English (brahmali, published): It is a woman, he perceives her as a woman, and he has lust. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss3:3.4.2

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.1.3

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.1.4

English (brahmali, published): One morning Udāyī robed up, took his bowl and robe, and went to the house of a beautiful widow where he sat down on the prepared seat. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.1.5
Pali: Atha kho sā itthī yenāyasmā udāyī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): She then approached Udāyī, bowed, and sat down. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.1.6
Pali: Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho taṁ itthiṁ āyasmā udāyī dhammiyā kathāya sandassesi samādapesi samuttejesi sampahaṁsesi. 
English (brahmali, published): And Udāyī instructed, inspired, and gladdened her with a teaching. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.1.7
Pali: Atha kho sā itthī āyasmatā udāyinā dhammiyā kathāya sandassitā samādapitā samuttejitā sampahaṁsitā āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): She then said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.1.8
Pali: “vadeyyātha, bhante, yena attho. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, please tell me what you need. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.1.9
Pali: Paṭibalā mayaṁ ayyassa dātuṁ yadidaṁ cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhāran”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I can give robe-cloth, almsfood, a dwelling, and medicinal supplies.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.1.10
Pali: “Na kho te, bhagini, amhākaṁ dullabhā yadidaṁ cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārā. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s not hard for us to get those requisites. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.1.11
Pali: Api ca yo amhākaṁ dullabho taṁ dehī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Give instead what’s hard for us to get.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.1.12
Pali: “Kiṁ, bhante”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What’s that, venerable?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.1.13
Pali: “Methunadhamman”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sexual intercourse.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.1.14
Pali: “Attho, bhante”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it wanted now?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.1.15
Pali: “Attho, bhaginī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.1.16
Pali: “Ehi, bhante”ti, 
English (brahmali, published): Saying, “Come,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.1.17
Pali: ovarakaṁ pavisitvā sāṭakaṁ nikkhipitvā mañcake uttānā nipajji. 
English (brahmali, published): she entered her bedroom, took off her wrap, and lay back on the bed. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.1.18
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā udāyī yena sā itthī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā—
English (brahmali, published): Udāyī went up to her and spat out, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.1.19
Pali: “ko imaṁ vasalaṁ duggandhaṁ āmasissatī”ti, 
English (brahmali, published): “Who would touch this stinking wretch!” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.1.20
Pali: niṭṭhubhitvā pakkāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): and he left. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.1.21
Pali: Atha kho sā itthī ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—
English (brahmali, published): That woman then complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.1.22
Pali: “alajjino ime samaṇā sakyaputtiyā dussīlā musāvādino. 
English (brahmali, published): “These Sakyan monastics are shameless and immoral liars. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.1.23
Pali: Ime hi nāma dhammacārino samacārino brahmacārino saccavādino sīlavanto kalyāṇadhammā paṭijānissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): They claim to have integrity, to be celibate and of good conduct, to be truthful, moral, and good. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.1.24
Pali: Natthi imesaṁ sāmaññaṁ natthi imesaṁ brahmaññaṁ, naṭṭhaṁ imesaṁ sāmaññaṁ naṭṭhaṁ imesaṁ brahmaññaṁ, kuto imesaṁ sāmaññaṁ kuto imesaṁ brahmaññaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): But they don’t have the good character of a monastic or  brahmin. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.1.25
Pali: apagatā ime sāmaññā apagatā ime brahmaññā. 
English (brahmali, published): They’ve lost the plot! 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.1.26
Pali: Kathañhi nāma samaṇo udāyī maṁ sāmaṁ methunadhammaṁ yācitvā, ‘ko imaṁ vasalaṁ duggandhaṁ āmasissatī’ti niṭṭhubhitvā pakkamissati. 
English (brahmali, published): How could the ascetic Udāyī ask for sexual intercourse from me, but then spit out, ‘Who would touch this stinking wretch!’ and leave? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.1.27
Pali: Kiṁ me pāpakaṁ kiṁ me duggandhaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): What’s bad about me? How am I stinking? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.1.28
Pali: kassāhaṁ kena hāyāmī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): How am I inferior to anyone?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.2.1
Pali: Aññāpi itthiyo ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): Other women, too, criticized him 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.2.2
Pali: “alajjino ime samaṇā sakyaputtiyā dussīlā musāvādino …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): in the same way. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.2.3
Pali: kathañhi nāma samaṇo udāyī imissā sāmaṁ methunadhammaṁ yācitvā, ‘ko imaṁ vasalaṁ duggandhaṁ āmasissatī’ti niṭṭhubhitvā pakkamissati. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.2.4
Pali: Kiṁ imissā pāpakaṁ kiṁ imissā duggandhaṁ, kassāyaṁ kena hāyatī”ti? 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.2.5
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tāsaṁ itthīnaṁ ujjhāyantīnaṁ khiyyantīnaṁ vipācentīnaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the criticism of those women, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.2.6
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.2.7
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā udāyī mātugāmassa santike attakāmapāricariyāya vaṇṇaṁ bhāsissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Udāyī encourage a woman to satisfy his own desires?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.2.8
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Those monks rebuked Udāyī in many ways and then told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.2.9
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe bhikkhusaṅghaṁ sannipātāpetvā āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ paṭipucchi—
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha had the Sangha gathered and questioned Udāyī: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.2.10
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, udāyi, mātugāmassa santike attakāmapāricariyāya vaṇṇaṁ bhāsasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Udāyī, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.2.11
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.2.12
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā—
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.2.13
Pali: “ananucchavikaṁ, moghapurisa, ananulomikaṁ appatirūpaṁ assāmaṇakaṁ akappiyaṁ akaraṇīyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s not suitable, foolish man, it’s not proper, it’s not worthy of a monastic, it’s not allowable, it’s not to be done. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.2.14
Pali: Kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, mātugāmassa santike attakāmapāricariyāya vaṇṇaṁ bhāsissasi. 
English (brahmali, published): How could you do such a thing? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.2.15
Pali: Nanu mayā, moghapurisa, anekapariyāyena virāgāya dhammo desito no sarāgāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): Haven’t I given many teachings for the sake of dispassion, not for the sake of passion … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.2.16
Pali: kāmapariḷāhānaṁ vūpasamo akkhāto? 
English (brahmali, published): the stilling of the fevers of worldly pleasures? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.2.17
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.2.18
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:1.2.19.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu otiṇṇo vipariṇatena cittena mātugāmassa santike attakāmapāricariyāya vaṇṇaṁ bhāseyya— ‘etadaggaṁ, bhagini, pāricariyānaṁ yā mādisaṁ sīlavantaṁ kalyāṇadhammaṁ brahmacāriṁ etena dhammena paricareyyāti methunupasaṁhitena’, saṅghādiseso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk, overcome by lust and with a distorted mind, encourages a woman to satisfy his own desires, saying, “Sister, she provides the highest service who in this way satisfies one like me, who is virtuous, celibate, and of good character,” and if it is a reference to sexual intercourse, he commits an offense entailing suspension.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.5
Pali: Otiṇṇo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Overcome by lust: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.6
Pali: sāratto apekkhavā paṭibaddhacitto. 
English (brahmali, published): having lust, longing for, in love with. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.7
Pali: Vipariṇatanti 
English (brahmali, published): Distorted: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.8
Pali: rattampi cittaṁ vipariṇataṁ, duṭṭhampi cittaṁ vipariṇataṁ, mūḷhampi cittaṁ vipariṇataṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): a lustful mind is distorted; an angry mind is distorted; a confused mind is distorted. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.9
Pali: Api ca rattaṁ cittaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetaṁ vipariṇatanti. 
English (brahmali, published): But in this case “distorted” refers to the lustful mind. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.10
Pali: Mātugāmo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A woman: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.11
Pali: manussitthī, na yakkhī, na petī, na tiracchānagatā. Viññū paṭibalā subhāsitadubbhāsitaṁ duṭṭhullāduṭṭhullaṁ ājānituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): a human female, not a female spirit, not a female ghost, not a female animal. She understands and is capable of discerning good and bad speech, what is decent and what is indecent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.12
Pali: Mātugāmassa santiketi 
English (brahmali, published): A woman: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.13
Pali: mātugāmassa sāmantā, mātugāmassa avidūre. 
English (brahmali, published): near a woman, close to a woman. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.14
Pali: Attakāmanti 
English (brahmali, published): His own desires: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.15
Pali: attano kāmaṁ attano hetuṁ attano adhippāyaṁ attano pāricariyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): his own desires, for the sake of himself, aiming at himself, satisfying himself. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.16
Pali: Etadagganti 
English (brahmali, published): The highest: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.17
Pali: etaṁ aggaṁ etaṁ seṭṭhaṁ etaṁ mokkhaṁ etaṁ uttamaṁ etaṁ pavaraṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): this is the highest, this is the best, this is the foremost, this is the utmost, this is the most excellent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.18
Pali: Yāti 
English (brahmali, published): She: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.19
Pali: khattiyī vā brāhmaṇī vā vessī vā suddī vā. 
English (brahmali, published): an aristocrat woman, a brahmin woman, a merchant woman, or a woman worker. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.20
Pali: Mādisanti 
English (brahmali, published): One like me: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.21
Pali: khattiyaṁ vā brāhmaṇaṁ vā vessaṁ vā suddaṁ vā. 
English (brahmali, published): a male aristocrat, a male brahmin, a male merchant, or a male worker. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.22
Pali: Sīlavantanti 
English (brahmali, published): Who is virtuous: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.23
Pali: pāṇātipātā paṭivirataṁ, adinnādānā paṭivirataṁ, musāvādā paṭivirataṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one who refrains from killing living beings, who refrains from stealing, who refrains from lying. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.24
Pali: Brahmacārinti 
English (brahmali, published): Celibate: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.25
Pali: methunadhammā paṭivirataṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one who refrains from sexual intercourse. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.26
Pali: Kalyāṇadhammo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Of good character: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.27
Pali: tena ca sīlena tena ca brahmacariyena kalyāṇadhammo hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): he is one of good character because of that virtue and because of being celibate. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.28
Pali: Etena dhammenāti 
English (brahmali, published): In this way: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.29
Pali: methunadhammena. 
English (brahmali, published): with sexual intercourse. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.30
Pali: Paricareyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Satisfies: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.31
Pali: abhirameyya. 
English (brahmali, published): gives pleasure to. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.32
Pali: Methunupasaṁhitenāti 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a reference to sexual intercourse: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.33
Pali: methunadhammappaṭisaṁyuttena. 
English (brahmali, published): if it is connected with the sexual act. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.34
Pali: Saṅghādisesoti 
English (brahmali, published): He commits an offense entailing suspension: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:2.35
Pali: …pe… tenapi vuccati “saṅghādiseso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): … Therefore, too, it is called “an offense entailing suspension”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:3.1.1
Pali: Itthī ca hoti itthisaññī sāratto ca. 
English (brahmali, published): It is a woman, he perceives her as a woman, and he has lust. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:3.1.2
Pali: Bhikkhu ca naṁ itthiyā santike attakāmapāricariyāya vaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the monk encourages the woman to satisfy his own desires, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:3.1.3
Pali: Itthī ca hoti vematiko …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): It is a woman, but he is unsure of it …

English (brahmali, published): It is a woman, but he perceives her as a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>paṇḍaka</i> …

English (brahmali, published): It is a woman, but he perceives her as a man …

Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bu-vb-ss2#3.3.4'>Bu Ss 2:3.3.4</a>–3.3.61, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:3.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:3.2.2
Pali: “cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārena upaṭṭhahā”ti bhaṇati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he says, “Support us with robe-cloth, almsfood, dwellings, and medicinal supplies;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:3.2.3
Pali: ummattakassa 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:3.2.4
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:3.2.5
Pali: 4.1. Vinītavatthuuddānagāthā 
English (brahmali, published): Summary verses of case studies 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:3.2.6
Pali: Kathaṁ vañjhā labhe puttaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): “How a barren woman, may she have a child, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:3.2.7
Pali: Piyā ca subhagā siyaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): And dear to, may I be fortunate; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:3.2.8
Pali: Kiṁ dajjaṁ kenupaṭṭheyyaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): What may I give, how may I support, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:3.2.9
Pali: Kathaṁ gaccheyyaṁ suggatinti. 
English (brahmali, published): How may I get a good rebirth.”

English (brahmali, published): Case studies

English (brahmali, published): “Sir, how can I get pregnant?”

Pali: “Tena hi, bhagini, aggadānaṁ dehī”ti.

English (brahmali, published): “What’s that?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss4:4.1.5
Pali: “Methunadhamman”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sexual intercourse.”

English (brahmali, published): He became anxious …

English (brahmali, published): “You’ve committed an offense entailing suspension.”

English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing suspension 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:0.4
Pali: 5. Sañcarittasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 5. Matchmaking 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā udāyī sāvatthiyaṁ kulūpako hoti. Bahukāni kulāni upasaṅkamati. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Udāyī was associating with and visiting a number of families in Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.1.3
Pali: Yattha passati kumārakaṁ vā apajāpatikaṁ, kumārikaṁ vā apatikaṁ, kumārakassa mātāpitūnaṁ santike kumārikāya vaṇṇaṁ bhaṇati—
English (brahmali, published): When Udāyī saw a young man without a wife and a young woman without a husband, he would praise the young woman to the parents of the young man, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.1.4
Pali: “amukassa kulassa kumārikā abhirūpā dassanīyā pāsādikā paṇḍitā byattā medhāvinī dakkhā analasā. 
English (brahmali, published): “The young woman of such-and-such a family is beautiful, intelligent, skilled, and diligent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.1.5
Pali: Channā sā kumārikā imassa kumārakassā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): She’s suitable for your son.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.1.6
Pali: Te evaṁ vadanti—
English (brahmali, published): They would reply, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.1.7
Pali: “ete kho, bhante, amhe na jānanti—‘ke vā ime kassa vā’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “They don’t know who we are, venerable. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.1.8
Pali: Sace, bhante, ayyo dāpeyya āneyyāma mayaṁ taṁ kumārikaṁ imassa kumārakassā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If you could persuade them to give the girl, we would take her for our son.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.1.9
Pali: Kumārikāya mātāpitūnaṁ santike kumārakassa vaṇṇaṁ bhaṇati—
English (brahmali, published): And he praised the young man to the parents of the young woman, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.1.10
Pali: “amukassa kulassa kumārako abhirūpo dassanīyo pāsādiko paṇḍito byatto medhāvī dakkho analaso. 
English (brahmali, published): “The young man of such-and-such a family is handsome, intelligent, skilled, and diligent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.1.11
Pali: Channāyaṁ kumārikā tassa kumārakassā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): He’s suitable for your daughter.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.1.12
Pali: Te evaṁ vadanti—
English (brahmali, published): They would reply, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.1.13
Pali: “ete kho, bhante, amhe na jānanti—‘ke vā ime kassa vā’ti, 
English (brahmali, published): “They don’t know who we are, venerable, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.1.14
Pali: kismiṁ viya kumārikāya vattuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): and we would be ashamed to speak to them for the sake of the girl. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.1.15
Pali: Sace, bhante, ayyo yācāpeyya dajjeyyāma mayaṁ imaṁ kumārikaṁ tassa kumārakassā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): But if you could persuade them to ask us, we would give our girl to the young man.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.1.16
Pali: Eteneva upāyena āvāhānipi kārāpeti, vivāhānipi kārāpeti, vāreyyānipi kārāpeti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way he arranged for the taking of a bride, for the giving of a bride, and for marriage. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarissā purāṇagaṇakiyā dhītā abhirūpā hoti dassanīyā pāsādikā. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time there was a former courtesan who had a beautiful daughter. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.2.2
Pali: Tirogāmakā ājīvakasāvakā āgantvā taṁ gaṇakiṁ etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion some lay followers of the Ājīvaka religion came from another village and said to that courtesan, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.2.3
Pali: “dehāyye, imaṁ kumārikaṁ amhākaṁ kumārakassā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Ma’am, please give your girl to our boy.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.2.4
Pali: Sā evamāha—“ahaṁ khvayyo, tumhe na jānāmi—‘ke vā ime kassa vā’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I don’t know who you are, sirs, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.2.5
Pali: Ayañca me ekadhītikā, tirogāmo ca gantabbo, nāhaṁ dassāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): and I won’t give my only daughter to be taken to another village.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.2.6
Pali: Manussā te ājīvakasāvake etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): People asked those Ājīvaka lay followers

Pali: “kissa tumhe, ayyo, āgatatthā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): why they had come. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.2.8

Pali: Sā evamāha—‘ahaṁ khvayyo, tumhe na jānāmi—ke vā ime kassa vā’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): but she refused.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.2.10
Pali: Ayañca me ekadhītikā, tirogāmo ca gantabbo, nāhaṁ dassāmī”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.2.11

English (brahmali, published): “But why did you ask the courtesan? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.2.12
Pali: Nanu ayyo udāyī vattabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): You should speak to Venerable Udāyī. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.2.13
Pali: Ayyo udāyī dāpessatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): He’ll persuade her.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.2.14

English (brahmali, published): They then went to Udāyī and said,
