pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.2.16
Pali: Sā evamāha—‘ahaṁ khvayyo, tumhe na jānāmi—ke vā ime kassa vāti. 
English (brahmali, published): but she refused. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.2.17
Pali: Ayañca me ekadhītikā, tirogāmo ca gantabbo, nāhaṁ dassāmī’ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.2.18
Pali: Sādhu, bhante, ayyo taṁ gaṇakiṁ dhītaraṁ dāpetu amhākaṁ kumārakassā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Would you please persuade her to give her daughter?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.2.19
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā udāyī yena sā gaṇakī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ gaṇakiṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Udāyī agreed. Soon afterwards he went to that courtesan and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.2.20
Pali: “kissimesaṁ dhītaraṁ na desī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Why didn’t you give them your daughter?”

Pali: “Ahaṁ khvayya, ime na jānāmi—‘ke vā ime kassa vā’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I don’t know who they are, sir, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.2.22
Pali: Ayañca me ekadhītikā, tirogāmo ca gantabbo, nāhaṁ dassāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): and I won’t give my only daughter to be taken to another village.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.2.23
Pali: “Dehimesaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please give her to them. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.2.24
Pali: Ahaṁ ime jānāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I know them.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.2.25
Pali: “Sace, bhante, ayyo jānāti, dassāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “If you know them, I’ll give her away.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.3.1
Pali: Atha kho sā gaṇakī tesaṁ ājīvakasāvakānaṁ dhītaraṁ adāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): She then gave her daughter to those Ājīvaka followers, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.3.2
Pali: Atha kho te ājīvakasāvakā taṁ kumārikaṁ netvā māsaṁyeva suṇisabhogena bhuñjiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): and they took her away. For a month they treated her like a daughter-in-law, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.3.3
Pali: Tato aparena dāsibhogena bhuñjanti. 
English (brahmali, published): but then like a slave. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.3.4
Pali: Atha kho sā kumārikā mātuyā santike dūtaṁ pāhesi—
English (brahmali, published): The girl sent a message to her mother, saying, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.3.5
Pali: “ahañhi duggatā dukkhitā, na sukhaṁ labhāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Mom, I’m unhappy and miserable. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.3.6
Pali: Māsaṁyeva maṁ suṇisabhogena bhuñjiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): For a month they treated me like a daughter-in-law, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.3.7
Pali: Tato aparena dāsibhogena bhuñjanti. 
English (brahmali, published): but then like a slave. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.3.8
Pali: Āgacchatu me, mātā, maṁ nessatū”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Come, mom, and take me home.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.3.9

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.4.1

English (brahmali, published): The girl, too, complained and criticized him in the same way,

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.4.6

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:1.4.7

English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk acts as a matchmaker, conveying a man’s intention to a woman or a woman’s intention to a man, for marriage or for an affair, he commits an offense entailing suspension.’”

Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:2.2.13.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu sañcarittaṁ samāpajjeyya, itthiyā vā purisamatiṁ purisassa vā itthimatiṁ, jāyattane vā jārattane vā, antamaso taṅkhaṇikāyapi, saṅghādiseso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk acts as a matchmaker, conveying a man’s intention to a woman or a woman’s intention to a man, for marriage or for an affair, even if just a brief one, he commits an offense entailing suspension.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:3.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:3.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:3.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:3.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:3.5
Pali: Sañcarittaṁ samāpajjeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Acts as a matchmaker, conveying: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:3.6
Pali: itthiyā vā pahito purisassa santike gacchati, purisena vā pahito itthiyā santike gacchati. 
English (brahmali, published): sent by a woman he goes to a man, or sent by a man he goes to a woman. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:3.7
Pali: Itthiyā vā purisamatinti 
English (brahmali, published): A man’s intention to a woman: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:3.8
Pali: purisassa matiṁ itthiyā āroceti. 
English (brahmali, published): he informs a woman of a man’s intention. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:3.9
Pali: Purisassa vā itthimatinti 
English (brahmali, published): A woman’s intention to a man: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:3.10
Pali: itthiyā matiṁ purisassa āroceti. 
English (brahmali, published): he informs a man of a woman’s intention. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:3.11
Pali: Jāyattane vāti 
English (brahmali, published): For marriage: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:3.12
Pali: jāyā bhavissasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “You should be his wife.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:3.13
Pali: Jārattane vāti 
English (brahmali, published): For an affair: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:3.14
Pali: jārī bhavissasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “You should be his mistress.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:3.15
Pali: Antamaso taṅkhaṇikāyapīti 
English (brahmali, published): Even if just a brief one: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:3.16
Pali: muhuttikā bhavissasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “You will have a short relationship.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:3.17
Pali: Saṅghādisesoti 
English (brahmali, published): He commits an offense entailing suspension: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:3.18
Pali: …pe… tenapi vuccati “saṅghādiseso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): … Therefore, too, it is called “an offense entailing suspension”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.1.1
Pali: Dasa itthiyo— māturakkhitā piturakkhitā mātāpiturakkhitā bhāturakkhitā bhaginirakkhitā ñātirakkhitā gottarakkhitā dhammarakkhitā sārakkhā saparidaṇḍā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are ten kinds of women: the one protected by her mother, the one protected by her father, the one protected by her parents, the one protected by her brother, the one protected by her sister, the one protected by her relatives, the one protected by her family, the one protected by her religion, the one otherwise protected, the one protected by the threat of punishment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.1.2
Pali: Dasa bhariyāyo— dhanakkītā chandavāsinī bhogavāsinī paṭavāsinī odapattakinī obhaṭacumbaṭā dāsī ca bhariyā ca kammakārī ca bhariyā ca dhajāhaṭā muhuttikā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are ten kinds of wives: the bought wife, the wife by choice, the wife through property, the wife through clothes, the wife through the bowl-of-water ritual, the wife through removing the head pad, the slave wife, the servant wife, the captured wife, the momentary wife. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.2.1
Pali: Māturakkhitā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The one protected by her mother: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.2.2
Pali: mātā rakkhati gopeti issariyaṁ kāreti vasaṁ vatteti. 
English (brahmali, published): her mother protects, guards, wields authority, controls. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.2.3
Pali: Piturakkhitā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The one protected by her father: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.2.4
Pali: pitā rakkhati gopeti issariyaṁ kāreti vasaṁ vatteti. 
English (brahmali, published): her father protects, guards, wields authority, controls. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.2.5
Pali: Mātāpiturakkhitā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The one protected by her parents: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.2.6
Pali: mātāpitaro rakkhanti gopenti issariyaṁ kārenti vasaṁ vattenti. 
English (brahmali, published): her parents protect, guard, wield authority, control. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.2.7
Pali: Bhāturakkhitā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The one protected by her brother: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.2.8
Pali: bhātā rakkhati gopeti issariyaṁ kāreti vasaṁ vatteti. 
English (brahmali, published): her brother protects, guards, wields authority, controls. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.2.9
Pali: Bhaginirakkhitā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The one protected by her sister: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.2.10
Pali: bhaginī rakkhati gopeti issariyaṁ kāreti vasaṁ vatteti. 
English (brahmali, published): her sister protects, guards, wields authority, controls. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.2.11
Pali: Ñātirakkhitā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The one protected by her relatives: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.2.12
Pali: ñātakā rakkhanti gopenti issariyaṁ kārenti vasaṁ vattenti. 
English (brahmali, published): her relatives protect, guard, wield authority, control. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.2.13
Pali: Gottarakkhitā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The one protected by her family: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.2.14
Pali: sagottā rakkhanti gopenti issariyaṁ kārenti vasaṁ vattenti. 
English (brahmali, published): her family protects, guards, wields authority, controls. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.2.15
Pali: Dhammarakkhitā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The one protected by her religion: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.2.16
Pali: sahadhammikā rakkhanti gopenti issariyaṁ kārenti vasaṁ vattenti. 
English (brahmali, published): her fellow believers protect, guard, wield authority, control. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.2.17
Pali: Sārakkhā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The one otherwise protected: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.2.18
Pali: gabbhepi pariggahitā hoti— mayhaṁ esāti. Antamaso mālāguḷaparikkhittāpi. 
English (brahmali, published): even in the womb someone takes possession of her, thinking, “She is mine,” and so too for one engaged to be married. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.2.19
Pali: Saparidaṇḍā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The one protected by the threat of punishment: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.2.20
Pali: kehici daṇḍo ṭhapito hoti—yo itthannāmaṁ itthiṁ gacchati ettako daṇḍoti. 
English (brahmali, published): those who punish will punish anyone going to her with a fixed punishment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.3.1
Pali: Dhanakkītā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The bought wife: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.3.2
Pali: dhanena kiṇitvā vāseti. 
English (brahmali, published): after buying her with money, they live together. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.3.3
Pali: Chandavāsinī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The wife by choice: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.3.4
Pali: piyo piyaṁ vāseti. 
English (brahmali, published): being dear to each other, they live together. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.3.5
Pali: Bhogavāsinī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The wife through property: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.3.6
Pali: bhogaṁ datvā vāseti. 
English (brahmali, published): after giving property, they live together. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.3.7
Pali: Paṭavāsinī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The wife through clothes: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.3.8
Pali: paṭaṁ datvā vāseti. 
English (brahmali, published): after giving clothes, they live together. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.3.9
Pali: Odapattakinī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The wife through the bowl-of-water ritual: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.3.10
Pali: udakapattaṁ āmasitvā vāseti. 
English (brahmali, published): after touching a bowl of water, they live together. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.3.11
Pali: Obhaṭacumbaṭā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The wife through removing the head pad: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.3.12
Pali: cumbaṭaṁ oropetvā vāseti. 
English (brahmali, published): after removing the head pad, they live together. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.3.13
Pali: Dāsī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The slave wife: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.3.14
Pali: dāsī ceva hoti bhariyā ca. 
English (brahmali, published): she is a slave and a wife. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.3.15
Pali: Kammakārī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The servant wife: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.3.16
Pali: kammakārī ceva hoti bhariyā ca. 
English (brahmali, published): she is a servant and a wife. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.3.17
Pali: Dhajāhaṭā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The captured wife: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.3.18
Pali: karamarānītā vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): one brought back as a captive is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.3.19
Pali: Muhuttikā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The momentary wife: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.3.20
Pali: taṅkhaṇikā vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): a wife for one occasion is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.4.1
Pali: Puriso bhikkhuṁ pahiṇati—
English (brahmali, published): A man sends a monk, saying,

English (brahmali, published): “Sir, go to so-and-so protected by her mother and say,

Pali: ‘hohi kira itthannāmassa bhariyā dhanakkītā’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Please be the bought wife of so-and-so.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.4.4

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.12.1
Pali: Paṭiggaṇhāti vīmaṁsati paccāharati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he accepts the mission, finds out the response, and reports back, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.12.2
Pali: Paṭiggaṇhāti vīmaṁsati na paccāharati, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he accepts the mission, and finds out the response, but does not report back, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.12.3
Pali: Paṭiggaṇhāti na vīmaṁsati paccāharati, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he accepts the mission, but does not find out the response, yet reports back, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.12.4
Pali: Paṭiggaṇhāti na vīmaṁsati na paccāharati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he accepts the mission, but neither finds out the response, nor reports back, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.12.5
Pali: Na paṭiggaṇhāti vīmaṁsati paccāharati, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not accept the mission, yet finds out the response and reports back, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.12.6
Pali: Na paṭiggaṇhāti vīmaṁsati na paccāharati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not accept the mission, yet finds out the response, but does not report back, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.12.7
Pali: Na paṭiggaṇhāti na vīmaṁsati paccāharati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he neither accepts the mission, nor finds out the response, yet reports back, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.12.8
Pali: Na paṭiggaṇhāti na vīmaṁsati na paccāharati, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not accept the mission, nor finds out the response, nor reports back, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.13.1
Pali: Puriso sambahule bhikkhū āṇāpeti—
English (brahmali, published): A man tells a number of monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.13.2
Pali: “gacchatha, bhante, itthannāmaṁ itthiṁ vīmaṁsathā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, find out about such-and-such a woman.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.13.3
Pali: Sabbe paṭiggaṇhanti sabbe vīmaṁsanti sabbe paccāharanti, āpatti sabbesaṁ saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If they all accept the mission, all find out the response, and all report back, they all commit an offense entailing suspension.

English (brahmali, published): A man tells a number of monks,

English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, find out about such-and-such a woman.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.13.12
Pali: Sabbe paṭiggaṇhanti ekaṁ vīmaṁsāpetvā ekaṁ paccāharāpenti, āpatti sabbesaṁ saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If they all accept the mission, but only one finds out the response, and only one reports back, they all commit an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.14.1
Pali: Puriso bhikkhuṁ āṇāpeti—
English (brahmali, published): A man tells a monk, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.14.2

English (brahmali, published): “Sir, find out about such-and-such a woman.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.14.3
Pali: Paṭiggaṇhāti vīmaṁsati paccāharati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he accepts the mission, finds out the response, and reports back, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.14.4
Pali: Puriso bhikkhuṁ āṇāpeti—
English (brahmali, published): A man tells a monk, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.14.5

English (brahmali, published): “Sir, find out about such-and-such a woman.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.14.6
Pali: Paṭiggaṇhāti vīmaṁsati antevāsiṁ paccāharāpeti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he accepts the mission, finds out the response, but gets a pupil to report back, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.14.7
Pali: Puriso bhikkhuṁ āṇāpeti—
English (brahmali, published): A man tells a monk, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.14.8
Pali: “gaccha, bhante, itthannāmaṁ itthiṁ vīmaṁsā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, find out about such-and-such a woman.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.14.9
Pali: Paṭiggaṇhāti antevāsiṁ vīmaṁsāpetvā attanā paccāharati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he accepts the mission, but gets a pupil to find out the response, and then reports back himself, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.14.10
Pali: Puriso bhikkhuṁ āṇāpeti—
English (brahmali, published): A man tells a monk, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.14.11
Pali: “gaccha, bhante, itthannāmaṁ itthiṁ vimaṁsā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, find out about such-and-such a woman.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.14.12
Pali: Paṭiggaṇhāti antevāsiṁ vīmaṁsāpeti antevāsī vīmaṁsitvā bahiddhā paccāharati, āpatti ubhinnaṁ thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he accepts the mission, but gets a pupil to find out the response, and the pupil then reports back on his own initiative, they both commit a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.15.1
Pali: Gacchanto sampādeti, āgacchanto visaṁvādeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he fulfills the agreement when he goes, but not when he returns, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.15.2
Pali: Gacchanto visaṁvādeti, āgacchanto sampādeti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not fulfill the agreement when he goes, but he does when he returns, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.15.3
Pali: Gacchanto sampādeti, āgacchanto sampādeti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he fulfills the agreement both when he goes and when he returns, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.15.4
Pali: Gacchanto visaṁvādeti, āgacchanto visaṁvādeti, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he neither fulfills the agreement when he goes nor when he returns, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.16.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.16.2
Pali: saṅghassa vā cetiyassa vā gilānassa vā karaṇīyena gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if he goes because of business for the Sangha, for a shrine, or for one who is sick; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.16.3
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.16.4
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss5:4.16.5
Pali: 5.1. Vinītavatthuuddānagāthā 
English (brahmali, published): Summary verses of case studies

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Rājagaha in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrel sanctuary. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āḷavakā bhikkhū saññācikāyo kuṭiyo kārāpenti assāmikāyo attuddesikāyo appamāṇikāyo. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the monks of Āḷavī were building huts by means of begging. The huts were intended for themselves, did not have a sponsoring owner, and were inappropriately large. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.1.3
Pali: Tāyo na niṭṭhānaṁ gacchanti. 
English (brahmali, published): And since they were never finished, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.1.4
Pali: Te yācanabahulā viññattibahulā viharanti—
English (brahmali, published): the monks kept on begging and asking, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.1.5

English (brahmali, published): “Please give a man, a servant, an ox, a cart, a machete, a hatchet, an ax, a spade, a chisel; give creepers, bamboo, reeds, grass, clay.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.1.6
Pali: Manussā upaddutā yācanāya upaddutā viññattiyā bhikkhū disvā ubbijjantipi uttasantipi palāyantipi aññenapi gacchanti aññenapi mukhaṁ karonti dvārampi thakenti, 
English (brahmali, published): People felt oppressed by all the begging and asking, so much so that when they saw a monk they became alarmed and fearful. They turned away, took a different path, ran off, and closed their doors. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.1.7
Pali: gāvimpi disvā palāyanti bhikkhūti maññamānā. 
English (brahmali, published): They even ran away when they saw cows, thinking they were monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.1.8
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā mahākassapo rājagahe vassaṁvuṭṭho yena āḷavī tena pakkāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): Just then Venerable Mahākassapa, after completing the rainy-season residence at Rājagaha, set out for Āḷavī.

English (brahmali, published): When he eventually arrived,

English (brahmali, published): One morning he robed up, took his bowl and robe, and entered Āḷavī for alms. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.1.12

English (brahmali, published): Then, when Venerable Mahākassapa had eaten his meal and returned from almsround, he said to the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.1.14

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.1
Pali: “Bhūtapubbaṁ, bhikkhave, dve bhātaro isayo gaṅgaṁ nadiṁ upanissāya vihariṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): “Once upon a time, monks, two sages who were brothers lived near the river Ganges. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.2
Pali: Atha kho, bhikkhave, maṇikaṇṭho nāgarājā gaṅgaṁ nadiṁ uttaritvā yena kaniṭṭho isi tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā kaniṭṭhaṁ isiṁ sattakkhattuṁ bhogehi parikkhipitvā uparimuddhani mahantaṁ phaṇaṁ karitvā aṭṭhāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion the dragon king Maṇikaṇṭha emerged from the Ganges and went up to the younger sage. He encircled him with seven coils and spread his large hood over his head. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.3
Pali: Atha kho, bhikkhave, kaniṭṭho isi tassa nāgassa bhayā kiso ahosi lūkho dubbaṇṇo uppaṇḍuppaṇḍukajāto dhamanisanthatagatto. 
English (brahmali, published): Then, because of his fear of that dragon, the younger sage became thin, haggard, and pale, with veins protruding all over his body. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.4
Pali: Addasa kho, bhikkhave, jeṭṭho isi kaniṭṭhaṁ isiṁ kisaṁ lūkhaṁ dubbaṇṇaṁ uppaṇḍuppaṇḍukajātaṁ dhamanisanthatagattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The older sage saw him like this 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.5
Pali: Disvāna kaniṭṭhaṁ isiṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): and asked him what was the matter. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.6
Pali: ‘kissa tvaṁ, bho, kiso lūkho dubbaṇṇo uppaṇḍuppaṇḍukajāto dhamanisanthatagatto’ti? 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.7
Pali: ‘Idha, bho, maṇikaṇṭho nāgarājā gaṅgaṁ nadiṁ uttaritvā yenāhaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā maṁ sattakkhattuṁ bhogehi parikkhipitvā uparimuddhani mahantaṁ phaṇaṁ karitvā aṭṭhāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): The younger sage told him. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.8
Pali: Tassāhaṁ, bho, nāgassa bhayā kiso lūkho dubbaṇṇo uppaṇḍuppaṇḍukajāto dhamanisanthatagatto’ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.9
Pali: ‘Icchasi pana tvaṁ, bho, tassa nāgassa anāgamanan’ti? 
English (brahmali, published): The elder sage said, ‘So, do you want that dragon to stay away?’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.10
Pali: ‘Icchāmahaṁ, bho, tassa nāgassa anāgamanan’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Yes.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.11
Pali: ‘Api pana tvaṁ, bho, tassa nāgassa kiñci passasī’ti? 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Well then, did you see anything belonging to that dragon?’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.12
Pali: ‘Passāmahaṁ, bho, maṇimassa kaṇṭhe pilandhanan’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘I saw an ornamental gem on his neck.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.13
Pali: ‘Tena hi tvaṁ, bho, taṁ nāgaṁ maṇiṁ yāca—
English (brahmali, published): ‘In that case, ask the dragon for that gem.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.14
Pali: “maṇiṁ me, bho, dehi; 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.15
Pali: maṇinā me attho”’ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.16
Pali: Atha kho, bhikkhave, maṇikaṇṭho nāgarājā gaṅgaṁ nadiṁ uttaritvā yena kaniṭṭho isi tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon the dragon king again emerged from the Ganges and went up to the younger sage. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.17
Pali: Ekamantaṁ ṭhitaṁ kho, bhikkhave, maṇikaṇṭhaṁ nāgarājānaṁ kaniṭṭho isi etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): And the sage said to him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.18
Pali: ‘maṇiṁ me, bho, dehi; 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Sir, give me the gem. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.19
Pali: maṇinā me attho’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I want the gem.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.20
Pali: Atha kho, bhikkhave, maṇikaṇṭho nāgarājā—
English (brahmali, published): The dragon thought, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.21

English (brahmali, published): ‘The monk is asking for the gem; he wants the gem,’ and he left in a hurry. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.22

English (brahmali, published): Once more the dragon king emerged from the Ganges and approached the younger sage. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.23

English (brahmali, published): The sage saw him coming

English (brahmali, published): and said to him,

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.26

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.27

English (brahmali, published): When the dragon heard him,

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.34
Pali: Atha kho, bhikkhave, maṇikaṇṭho nāgarājā kaniṭṭhaṁ isiṁ gāthāhi ajjhabhāsi—
English (brahmali, published): The dragon king then spoke these verses to the sage: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.35
Pali: ‘Mamannapānaṁ vipulaṁ uḷāraṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): ‘My food and drink are abundant and sublime, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.36
Pali: Uppajjatīmassa maṇissa hetu; 
English (brahmali, published): And they appear because of this gem. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.37
Pali: Taṁ te na dassaṁ atiyācakosi, 
English (brahmali, published): I won’t give it to you—you ask too much—

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.38
Pali: Na cāpi te assamamāgamissaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Nor will I return to your hermitage. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.39
Pali: Susū yathā sakkharadhotapāṇī, 
English (brahmali, published): Like a youth holding a sword polished on a rock, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.40
Pali: Tāsesi maṁ selamāyācamāno; 
English (brahmali, published): You frighten me, asking for this gem. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.41
Pali: Taṁ te na dassaṁ atiyācakosi, 
English (brahmali, published): I won’t give it to you—you ask too much—

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.42
Pali: Na cāpi te assamamāgamissan’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Nor will I return to your hermitage.’

English (brahmali, published): And the dragon king Maṇikaṇṭha thought,

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.46
Pali: Atha kho, bhikkhave, kaniṭṭho isi tassa nāgassa dassanīyassa adassanena bhiyyoso mattāya kiso ahosi lūkho dubbaṇṇo, uppaṇḍuppaṇḍukajāto dhamanisanthatagatto. 
English (brahmali, published): Because he did not get to see that beautiful dragon, the young sage became even thinner, more haggard and pale, his veins protruding even more.

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.50

English (brahmali, published): He replied, ‘It’s because I no longer get to see that beautiful dragon.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.51
Pali: Atha kho, bhikkhave, jeṭṭho isi kaniṭṭhaṁ isiṁ gāthāya ajjhabhāsi—
English (brahmali, published): The older sage then spoke to him in verse: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.52
Pali: ‘Na taṁ yāce yassapiyaṁ jigīse, 
English (brahmali, published): ‘One shouldn’t beg from those one wishes to be dear to; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.53
Pali: Videsso hoti atiyācanāya; 
English (brahmali, published): One is detested for asking for too much. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.54
Pali: Nāgo maṇiṁ yācito brāhmaṇena, 
English (brahmali, published): When the brahmin asked the dragon for his gem, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.55
Pali: Adassanaññeva tadajjhagamā’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): It left and was never to be seen again.’

English (brahmali, published): One will be disliked even by animals, monks, for begging and asking, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.3.57
Pali: Kimaṅgaṁ pana manussabhūtānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): let alone by human beings.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.4.1
Pali: Bhūtapubbaṁ, bhikkhave, aññataro bhikkhu himavantapasse viharati aññatarasmiṁ vanasaṇḍe. 
English (brahmali, published): “At one time, monks, a certain monk lived in a forest grove on the slopes of the Himalayas. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.4.2
Pali: Tassa kho, bhikkhave, vanasaṇḍassa avidūre mahantaṁ ninnaṁ pallalaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Not far from that grove was a large, low-lying marsh. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.4.3
Pali: Atha kho, bhikkhave, mahāsakuṇasaṅgho tasmiṁ pallale divasaṁ gocaraṁ caritvā sāyaṁ taṁ vanasaṇḍaṁ vāsāya upagacchati. 
English (brahmali, published): A great flock of birds fed in the marsh during the day and entered the grove to roost at night. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.4.4
Pali: Atha kho, bhikkhave, so bhikkhu tassa sakuṇasaṅghassa saddena ubbāḷho yenāhaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā maṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): The monk was disturbed by the noise of the flocking birds, and so he came to see me. He bowed, sat down,

English (brahmali, published): and I said to him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.4.6
Pali: ‘kacci, bhikkhu, khamanīyaṁ kacci yāpanīyaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): ‘I hope you’re keeping well, monk, I hope you’re getting by?

Pali: kaccisi appakilamathena addhānaṁ āgato? 
English (brahmali, published): I hope you’re not tired from traveling?

English (brahmali, published): ‘I’m keeping well, sir, I’m getting by.

Pali: Appakilamathena cāhaṁ, bhante, addhānaṁ āgato. 
English (brahmali, published): I’m not tired from traveling.’

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.4.13

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.4.14

English (brahmali, published): ‘That’s where I’ve come from, sir.

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.5.1
Pali: Bhūtapubbaṁ, bhikkhave, raṭṭhapālassa kulaputtassa pitā raṭṭhapālaṁ kulaputtaṁ gāthāya ajjhabhāsi—
English (brahmali, published): “Raṭṭhapāla’s father, monks, once spoke to his son with this verse: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.5.2
Pali: ‘Apāhaṁ te na jānāmi, 
English (brahmali, published): ‘All these people, Raṭṭhāpāla, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.5.3
Pali: raṭṭhapāla bahū janā; 
English (brahmali, published): Who come to me and beg—

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.5.4
Pali: Te maṁ saṅgamma yācanti, 
English (brahmali, published): I don’t even know them. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.5.5
Pali: kasmā maṁ tvaṁ na yācasī’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): So why don’t <em>you</em> beg from me?’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.5.6
Pali: ‘Yācako appiyo hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): ‘The beggar is disliked, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.5.7
Pali: yācaṁ adadamappiyo; 
English (brahmali, published): And so is one who doesn’t give when asked. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.5.8
Pali: Tasmāhaṁ taṁ na yācāmi, 
English (brahmali, published): That’s why I do not beg from you; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.5.9
Pali: mā me videssanā ahū’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Please don’t hate me for this.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.5.10
Pali: So hi nāma, bhikkhave, raṭṭhapālo kulaputto sakaṁ pitaraṁ evaṁ vakkhati. 
English (brahmali, published): If the gentleman Raṭṭhapāla could say this to his own father, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.5.11
Pali: Kimaṅgaṁ pana jano janaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): how much more can one person to another. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.6.1
Pali: Gihīnaṁ, bhikkhave, dussaṁharāni bhogāni sambhatānipi durakkhiyāni. 
English (brahmali, published): It’s hard, monks, for householders to acquire and protect their possessions. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.6.2
Pali: Tattha nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, evaṁ dussaṁharesu bhogesu sambhatesupi durakkhiyesu yācanabahulā viññattibahulā viharissatha—
English (brahmali, published): And still, foolish men, you kept on begging and asking 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.6.3
Pali: ‘purisaṁ detha, purisatthakaraṁ detha, goṇaṁ detha, sakaṭaṁ detha, vāsiṁ detha, parasuṁ detha, kuṭhāriṁ detha, kudālaṁ detha, nikhādanaṁ detha, valliṁ detha, veḷuṁ detha, muñjaṁ detha, pabbajaṁ detha, tiṇaṁ detha, mattikaṁ dethā’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): for all sorts of things. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.6.4
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.6.5
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.6.6.1
Pali: “Saññācikāya pana bhikkhunā kuṭiṁ kārayamānena assāmikaṁ attuddesaṁ pamāṇikā kāretabbā. Tatridaṁ pamāṇaṁ—dīghaso dvādasa vidatthiyo, sugatavidatthiyā; tiriyaṁ sattantarā. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘When a monk, by means of begging, builds a hut without a sponsoring owner and intended for himself, it is to be no more than twelve standard handspans long and seven wide inside. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.6.7
Pali: Bhikkhū abhinetabbā vatthudesanāya. Tehi bhikkhūhi vatthu desetabbaṁ— anārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): He must have monks approve a site where no harm will be done and which has space on all sides. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:1.6.8
Pali: Sārambhe ce bhikkhu vatthusmiṁ aparikkamane saññācikāya kuṭiṁ kāreyya, bhikkhū vā anabhineyya vatthudesanāya, pamāṇaṁ vā atikkāmeyya, saṅghādiseso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk, by means of begging, builds a hut on a site where harm will be done and which lacks space on all sides, or he does not have monks approve the site, or he exceeds the right size, he commits an offense entailing suspension.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.1.1
Pali: Saññācikā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): By means of begging: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.1.2
Pali: sayaṁ yācitvā purisampi purisatthakarampi goṇampi sakaṭampi vāsimpi parasumpi kuṭhārimpi kudālampi nikhādanampi vallimpi veḷumpi muñjampi pabbajampi tiṇampi mattikampi. 
English (brahmali, published): having himself begged for a man, a servant, an ox, a cart, a machete, a hatchet, an ax, a spade, a chisel; creepers, bamboo, reed, grass, clay. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.1.3
Pali: Kuṭi nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A hut: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.1.4
Pali: ullittā vā hoti avalittā vā ullittāvalittā vā. 
English (brahmali, published): plastered inside or plastered outside or plastered both inside and outside. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.1.5
Pali: Kārayamānenāti 
English (brahmali, published): Builds: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.1.6
Pali: karonto vā kārāpento vā. 
English (brahmali, published): building it himself or having it built. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.1.7
Pali: Assāmikanti 
English (brahmali, published): Without a sponsoring owner: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.1.8
Pali: na añño koci sāmiko hoti, itthī vā puriso vā gahaṭṭho vā pabbajito vā. 
English (brahmali, published): there is no other owner, either a woman or a man, either a householder or a monastic. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.1.9
Pali: Attuddesanti 
English (brahmali, published): Intended for himself: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.1.10
Pali: attano atthāya. 
English (brahmali, published): for his own use. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.1.11
Pali: Pamāṇikā kāretabbā. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.1.12
Pali: Tatridaṁ pamāṇaṁ—dīghaso dvādasa vidatthiyo, sugatavidatthiyāti 
English (brahmali, published): It is to be no more than twelve standard handspans long: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.1.13
Pali: bāhirimena mānena. 
English (brahmali, published): measured outside. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.1.14
Pali: Tiriyaṁ sattantarāti 
English (brahmali, published): And seven wide inside: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.1.15
Pali: abbhantarimena mānena. 
English (brahmali, published): measured inside. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.1
Pali: Bhikkhū abhinetabbā vatthudesanāyāti 
English (brahmali, published): He must have monks approve a site: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.2
Pali: tena kuṭikārakena bhikkhunā kuṭivatthuṁ sodhetvā saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā vuḍḍhānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ pāde vanditvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): the monk who wants to build a hut should clear a site. He should then approach the Sangha, arrange his upper robe over one shoulder, pay respect at the feet of the senior monks, squat on his heels, raise his joined palms, and say: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.3
Pali: “ahaṁ, bhante, saññācikāya kuṭiṁ kattukāmo assāmikaṁ attuddesaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, I want to build a hut by means of begging, without a sponsoring owner and intended for myself. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.4
Pali: Sohaṁ, bhante, saṅghaṁ kuṭivatthuolokanaṁ yācāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I request the Sangha to inspect the site for that hut.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.5
Pali: Dutiyampi yācitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): He should make his request a second 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.6
Pali: Tatiyampi yācitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): and a third time. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.7
Pali: Sace sabbo saṅgho ussahati kuṭivatthuṁ oloketuṁ, sabbena saṅghena oloketabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If the whole Sangha is able to inspect the site, they should all go. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.8
Pali: No ce sabbo saṅgho ussahati kuṭivatthuṁ oloketuṁ, ye tattha honti bhikkhū byattā paṭibalā sārambhaṁ anārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ aparikkamanaṁ jānituṁ te yācitvā sammannitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): If the whole Sangha is unable to inspect the site, then those monks there who are competent and capable—who know where harm will be done and where no harm will be done, who know what is meant by space on all sides and a lack of space on all sides—should be asked and then appointed. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.9
Pali: “Evañca pana, bhikkhave, sammannitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, they should be appointed like this. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.10
Pali: Byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena saṅgho ñāpetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): A competent and capable monk should inform the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.11
Pali: ‘Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.12
Pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmo bhikkhu saññācikāya kuṭiṁ kattukāmo assāmikaṁ attuddesaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Monk so-and-so wants to build a hut by means of begging, without a sponsoring owner and intended for himself. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.13
Pali: So saṅghaṁ kuṭivatthuolokanaṁ yācati. 
English (brahmali, published): He is requesting the Sangha to inspect the site for that hut. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.14
Pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmañca itthannāmañca bhikkhū sammanneyya itthannāmassa bhikkhuno kuṭivatthuṁ oloketuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If the Sangha is ready, it should appoint monks so-and-so and so-and-so to inspect the site for the hut of monk so-and-so. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.15
Pali: Esā ñatti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the motion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.16
Pali: Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.17
Pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmo bhikkhu saññācikāya kuṭiṁ kattukāmo assāmikaṁ attuddesaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Monk so-and-so wants to build a hut by means of begging, without a sponsoring owner and intended for himself. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.18
Pali: So saṅghaṁ kuṭivatthuolokanaṁ yācati. 
English (brahmali, published): He is requesting the Sangha to inspect the site for that hut. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.19
Pali: Saṅgho itthannāmañca itthannāmañca bhikkhū sammannati itthannāmassa bhikkhuno kuṭivatthuṁ oloketuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha appoints monks so-and-so and so-and-so to inspect the site for the hut of monk so-and-so. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.20
Pali: Yassāyasmato khamati itthannāmassa ca itthannāmassa ca bhikkhūnaṁ sammuti itthannāmassa bhikkhuno kuṭivatthuṁ oloketuṁ, so tuṇhassa; 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who approves of appointing monks so-and-so and so-and-so to inspect the site for the hut of monk so-and-so should remain silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.21
Pali: yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.22
Pali: Sammatā saṅghena itthannāmo ca itthannāmo ca bhikkhū itthannāmassa bhikkhuno kuṭivatthuṁ oloketuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha has appointed monks so-and-so and so-and-so to inspect the site for the hut of monk so-and-so. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.23
Pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.24
Pali: evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I will remember it thus.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.25
Pali: Tehi sammatehi bhikkhūhi tattha gantvā kuṭivatthu oloketabbaṁ, sārambhaṁ anārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ aparikkamanaṁ jānitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The appointed monks should go and inspect the site for the hut to find out if any harm will be done and if it has space on all sides. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.26
Pali: Sace sārambhaṁ hoti aparikkamanaṁ, “mā idha karī”ti vattabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): If harm will be done or it lacks space on all sides, they should say, ‘Don’t build here.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.27
Pali: Sace anārambhaṁ hoti saparikkamanaṁ, saṅghassa ārocetabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): If no harm will be done and it has space on all sides, they should inform the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.28
Pali: “anārambhaṁ saparikkamanan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘No harm will be done and it has space on all sides.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.29
Pali: Tena kuṭikārakena bhikkhunā saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā vuḍḍhānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ pāde vanditvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): The monk who wants to build the hut should then approach the Sangha, arrange his upper robe over one shoulder, pay respect at the feet of the senior monks, squat on his heels, raise his joined palms, and say: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.30
Pali: “ahaṁ, bhante, saññācikāya kuṭiṁ kattukāmo assāmikaṁ attuddesaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Venerables, I want to build a hut by means of begging, without a sponsoring owner and intended for myself. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.31
Pali: Sohaṁ, bhante, saṅghaṁ kuṭivatthudesanaṁ yācāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I request the Sangha to approve the site for the hut.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.32
Pali: Dutiyampi yācitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): He should make his request a second 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.33
Pali: Tatiyampi yācitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): and a third time. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.34
Pali: “Byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena saṅgho ñāpetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): A competent and capable monk should then inform the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.35
Pali: ‘Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.36
Pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmo bhikkhu saññācikāya kuṭiṁ kattukāmo assāmikaṁ attuddesaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Monk so-and-so wants to build a hut by means of begging, without a sponsoring owner and intended for himself. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.37
Pali: So saṅghaṁ kuṭivatthudesanaṁ yācati. 
English (brahmali, published): He is requesting the Sangha to approve the site for that hut. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.38
Pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmassa bhikkhuno kuṭivatthuṁ deseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): If the Sangha is ready, it should approve the site for the hut of monk so-and-so. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.39
Pali: Esā ñatti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the motion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.40
Pali: Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.41
Pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmo bhikkhu saññācikāya kuṭiṁ kattukāmo assāmikaṁ attuddesaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Monk so-and-so wants to build a hut by means of begging, without a sponsoring owner and intended for himself. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.42
Pali: So saṅghaṁ kuṭivatthudesanaṁ yācati. 
English (brahmali, published): He is requesting the Sangha to approve the site for that hut. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.43
Pali: Saṅgho itthannāmassa bhikkhuno kuṭivatthuṁ deseti. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha approves the site for the hut of monk so-and-so. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.44
Pali: Yassāyasmato khamati itthannāmassa bhikkhuno kuṭivatthussa desanā, so tuṇhassa; 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who agrees to approving the site for the hut of monk so-and-so should remain silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.45
Pali: yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who doesn’t agree should speak up. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.46
Pali: Desitaṁ saṅghena itthannāmassa bhikkhuno kuṭivatthu. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha has approved the site for the hut of monk so-and-so. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.47
Pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.2.48
Pali: evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I will remember it thus.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.3.1
Pali: Sārambhaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Where harm will be done: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.3.2
Pali: kipillikānaṁ vā āsayo hoti, upacikānaṁ vā āsayo hoti, undūrānaṁ vā āsayo hoti, ahīnaṁ vā āsayo hoti, vicchikānaṁ vā āsayo hoti, satapadīnaṁ vā āsayo hoti, hatthīnaṁ vā āsayo hoti, assānaṁ vā āsayo hoti, sīhānaṁ vā āsayo hoti, byagghānaṁ vā āsayo hoti, dīpīnaṁ vā āsayo hoti, acchānaṁ vā āsayo hoti, taracchānaṁ vā āsayo hoti, yesaṁ kesañci tiracchānagatānaṁ pāṇānaṁ āsayo hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): it is the abode of ants, termites, rats, snakes, scorpions, centipedes, elephants, horses, lions, tigers, leopards, bears, or hyenas, or any other animal; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.3.3
Pali: pubbaṇṇanissitaṁ vā hoti, aparaṇṇanissitaṁ vā hoti, abbhāghātanissitaṁ vā hoti, āghātananissitaṁ vā hoti, susānanissitaṁ vā hoti, uyyānanissitaṁ vā hoti, rājavatthunissitaṁ vā hoti, hatthisālānissitaṁ vā hoti, assasālānissitaṁ vā hoti, bandhanāgāranissitaṁ vā hoti, pānāgāranissitaṁ vā hoti, sūnanissitaṁ vā hoti, racchānissitaṁ vā hoti, caccaranissitaṁ vā hoti, sabhānissitaṁ vā hoti, saṁsaraṇanissitaṁ vā hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): or it is bordering on a field of grain, a field of vegetables, a place of slaughter, a place of execution, a charnel ground, a park, a royal property, an elephant stable, a horse stable, a prison, a bar, a slaughterhouse, a street, a crossroads, a public meeting hall, or a cul-de-sac—

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.3.4
Pali: Etaṁ sārambhaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): this is called “where harm will be done”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.3.5
Pali: Aparikkamanaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Which lacks space on all sides: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.3.6
Pali: na sakkā hoti yathāyuttena sakaṭena anuparigantuṁ samantā nisseṇiyā anuparigantuṁ. Etaṁ aparikkamanaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): it is not possible to go around it with a yoked cart, or to go all the way around it with a ladder—this is called “which lacks space on all sides”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.3.7
Pali: Anārambhaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Where no harm will be done: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.3.8
Pali: na kipillikānaṁ vā āsayo hoti, na upacikānaṁ vā āsayo hoti, na undūrānaṁ vā āsayo hoti, na ahīnaṁ vā āsayo hoti, na vicchikānaṁ vā āsayo hoti, na satapadīnaṁ vā āsayo hoti …pe… na saṁsaraṇanissitaṁ vā hoti. Etaṁ anārambhaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): it is not the abode of ants, termites, rats, snakes, scorpions, centipedes … it is not bordering on … a cul-de-sac—this is called “where no harm will be done”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.3.9
Pali: Saparikkamanaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Which has space on all sides: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.3.10
Pali: sakkā hoti yathāyuttena sakaṭena anuparigantuṁ, samantā nisseṇiyā anuparigantuṁ. Etaṁ saparikkamanaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): it is possible to go around it with a yoked cart, or to go all the way around it with a ladder—this is called “which has space on all sides”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.4.1
Pali: Saññācikā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): By means of begging: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.4.2
Pali: sayaṁ yācitvā purisampi purisatthakarampi …pe… mattikampi. 
English (brahmali, published): having himself begged for a man, a servant … clay. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.4.3
Pali: Kuṭi nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A hut: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.4.4
Pali: ullittā vā hoti avalittā vā ullittāvalittā vā. 
English (brahmali, published): plastered inside or plastered outside or plastered both inside and outside. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.4.5
Pali: Kāreyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Builds: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.4.6
Pali: karoti vā kārāpeti vā. 
English (brahmali, published): building it himself or having it built. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.4.7
Pali: Bhikkhū vā anabhineyya, vatthudesanāya pamāṇaṁ vā atikkāmeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Or he does not have monks approve the site, or he exceeds the right size: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.4.8
Pali: ñattidutiyena kammena kuṭivatthuṁ na desāpetvā, āyāmato vā vitthārato vā antamaso kesaggamattampi atikkāmetvā karoti vā kārāpeti vā, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if the site for the hut has not been approved through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements, or if he builds a hut or has one built that exceeds the allowable length or breadth even by the width of a hair, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.4.9
Pali: Ekaṁ piṇḍaṁ anāgate āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When there is one piece left to complete the hut, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.4.10
Pali: Tasmiṁ piṇḍe āgate āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When the last piece is finished, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.4.11
Pali: Saṅghādisesoti 
English (brahmali, published): He commits an offense entailing suspension: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:2.4.12
Pali: …pe… tenapi vuccati “saṅghādiseso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): … Therefore, too, it is called “an offense entailing suspension”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.1.1
Pali: Bhikkhu kuṭiṁ karoti adesitavatthukaṁ sārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ, āpatti saṅghādisesena dvinnaṁ dukkaṭānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk builds a hut whose site has not been approved, where harm will be done, and which lacks space on all sides, he commits one offense entailing suspension and two offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.1.2
Pali: Bhikkhu kuṭiṁ karoti adesitavatthukaṁ sārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ, āpatti saṅghādisesena dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk builds a hut whose site has not been approved, where harm will be done, but which has space on all sides, he commits one offense entailing suspension and one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.1.3
Pali: Bhikkhu kuṭiṁ karoti adesitavatthukaṁ anārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ, āpatti saṅghādisesena dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk builds a hut whose site has not been approved, where no harm will be done, but which lacks space on all sides, he commits one offense entailing suspension and one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.1.4
Pali: Bhikkhu kuṭiṁ karoti adesitavatthukaṁ anārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk builds a hut whose site has not been approved, where no harm will be done, and which has space on all sides, he commits one offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.1.5
Pali: Bhikkhu kuṭiṁ karoti desitavatthukaṁ sārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ, āpatti dvinnaṁ dukkaṭānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk builds a hut whose site has been approved, where harm will be done, and which lacks space on all sides, he commits two offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.1.6
Pali: Bhikkhu kuṭiṁ karoti desitavatthukaṁ sārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk builds a hut whose site has been approved, where harm will be done, but which has space on all sides, he commits one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.1.7
Pali: Bhikkhu kuṭiṁ karoti desitavatthukaṁ anārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk builds a hut whose site has been approved, where no harm will be done, but which lacks space on all sides, he commits one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.1.8
Pali: Bhikkhu kuṭiṁ karoti desitavatthukaṁ anārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk builds a hut whose site has been approved, where no harm will be done, and which has space on all sides, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.2.1
Pali: Bhikkhu kuṭiṁ karoti pamāṇātikkantaṁ sārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ, āpatti saṅghādisesena dvinnaṁ dukkaṭānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk builds a hut which exceeds the right size, where harm will be done, and which lacks space on all sides, he commits one offense entailing suspension and two offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.2.2
Pali: Bhikkhu kuṭiṁ karoti pamāṇātikkantaṁ sārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ, āpatti saṅghādisesena dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk builds a hut which exceeds the right size, where harm will be done, but which has space on all sides, he commits one offense entailing suspension and one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.2.3
Pali: Bhikkhu kuṭiṁ karoti pamāṇātikkantaṁ anārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ, āpatti saṅghādisesena dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk builds a hut which exceeds the right size, where no harm will be done, but which lacks space on all sides, he commits one offense entailing suspension and one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.2.4
Pali: Bhikkhu kuṭiṁ karoti pamāṇātikkantaṁ anārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk builds a hut which exceeds the right size, where no harm will be done, and which has space on all sides, he commits one offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.2.5
Pali: Bhikkhu kuṭiṁ karoti pamāṇikaṁ sārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ, āpatti dvinnaṁ dukkaṭānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk builds a hut which is the right size, where harm will be done, and which lacks space on all sides, he commits two offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.2.6
Pali: Bhikkhu kuṭiṁ karoti pamāṇikaṁ sārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk builds a hut which is the right size, where harm will be done, but which has space on all sides, he commits one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.2.7
Pali: Bhikkhu kuṭiṁ karoti pamāṇikaṁ anārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk builds a hut which is the right size, where no harm will be done, but which lacks space on all sides, he commits one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.2.8
Pali: Bhikkhu kuṭiṁ karoti pamāṇikaṁ anārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk builds a hut which is the right size, where no harm will be done, and which has space on all sides, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.3.1
Pali: Bhikkhu kuṭiṁ karoti adesitavatthukaṁ pamāṇātikkantaṁ sārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ, āpatti dvinnaṁ saṅghādisesena dvinnaṁ dukkaṭānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk builds a hut whose site has not been approved, which exceeds the right size, where harm will be done, and which lacks space on all sides, he commits two offenses entailing suspension and two offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.3.2
Pali: Bhikkhu kuṭiṁ karoti adesitavatthukaṁ pamāṇātikkantaṁ sārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ, āpatti dvinnaṁ saṅghādisesena dukkaṭassa.

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.3.3
Pali: Bhikkhu kuṭiṁ karoti adesitavatthukaṁ pamāṇātikkantaṁ anārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ, āpatti dvinnaṁ saṅghādisesena dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk builds a hut whose site has not been approved, which exceeds the right size, where no harm will be done, but which lacks space on all sides, he commits two offenses entailing suspension and one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.3.4
Pali: Bhikkhu kuṭiṁ karoti adesitavatthukaṁ pamāṇātikkantaṁ anārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ, āpatti dvinnaṁ saṅghādisesānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk builds a hut whose site has not been approved, which exceeds the right size, where no harm will be done, and which has space on all sides, he commits two offenses entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.4.1
Pali: Bhikkhu kuṭiṁ karoti desitavatthukaṁ pamāṇikaṁ sārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ, āpatti dvinnaṁ dukkaṭānaṁ.

Pali: Bhikkhu samādisati—
English (brahmali, published): A monk appoints someone

Pali: “kuṭiṁ me karothā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): to build him a hut.

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.6.1
Pali: Bhikkhu samādisitvā pakkamati— “kuṭiṁ me karothā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): A monk appoints someone to build him a hut. He then departs 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.6.2
Pali: Na ca samādisati— “desitavatthukā ca hotu anārambhā ca saparikkamanā cā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): without telling them to build one whose site has been approved, where no harm will be done, and which has space on all sides.

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.7.10
Pali: sārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ, āpatti dukkaṭassa …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): where harm will be done, but which has space on all sides, he commits one offense of wrong conduct. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.7.11
Pali: anārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ, āpatti dukkaṭassa …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): where no harm will be done, but which lacks space on all sides, he commits one offense of wrong conduct. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.7.12
Pali: anārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): where no harm will be done, and which has space on all sides, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.8.1
Pali: Bhikkhu samādisitvā pakkamati— “kuṭiṁ me karothā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): A monk appoints someone to build him a hut. He then departs 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.8.2
Pali: Na ca samādisati— “desitavatthukā ca hotu pamāṇikā ca anārambhā ca saparikkamanā cā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): without telling them to build one whose site has been approved, which is the right size, where no harm will be done, and which has space on all sides. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.8.3
Pali: Tassa kuṭiṁ karonti adesitavatthukaṁ pamāṇātikkantaṁ sārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ, āpatti dvinnaṁ saṅghādisesena dvinnaṁ dukkaṭānaṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If they build one whose site has not been approved, which exceeds the right size, where harm will be done, and which lacks space on all sides, he commits two offenses entailing suspension and two offenses of wrong conduct. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.8.4
Pali: sārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ, āpatti dvinnaṁ saṅghādisesena dukkaṭassa …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): where harm will be done, but which has space on all sides, he commits two offenses entailing suspension and one offense of wrong conduct. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.8.5
Pali: anārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ, āpatti dvinnaṁ saṅghādisesena dukkaṭassa …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): where no harm will be done, but which lacks space on all sides, he commits two offenses entailing suspension and one offense of wrong conduct. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.8.6
Pali: anārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ, āpatti dvinnaṁ saṅghādisesānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): where no harm will be done, and which has space on all sides, he commits two offenses entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.8.7
Pali: Bhikkhu samādisitvā pakkamati— “kuṭiṁ me karothā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): A monk appoints someone to build him a hut. He then departs

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.9.2
Pali: Samādisati ca— “desitavatthukā ca hotu anārambhā ca saparikkamanā cā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): telling them to build one whose site has been approved, where no harm will be done, and which has space on all sides, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.9.3
Pali: Tassa kuṭiṁ karonti adesitavatthukaṁ sārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): but they build one whose site has not been approved, where harm will be done, and which lacks space on all sides. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.9.4
Pali: So suṇāti—“kuṭi kira me kayirati adesitavatthukā sārambhā aparikkamanā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he hears about it, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.9.5
Pali: Tena bhikkhunā sāmaṁ vā gantabbaṁ dūto vā pāhetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): he must either go there himself or send a message, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.9.6
Pali: “desitavatthukā ca hotu anārambhā ca saparikkamanā cā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): telling them to build one whose site has been approved, where no harm will be done, and which has space on all sides. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.9.7
Pali: No ce sāmaṁ vā gaccheyya dūtaṁ vā pahiṇeyya, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he neither goes himself nor sends a message, he commits an offense of wrong conduct.

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.11.11
Pali: Bhikkhu samādisitvā pakkamati— “kuṭiṁ me karothā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): A monk appoints someone to build him a hut. He then departs, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.11.12
Pali: Samādisati ca— “desitavatthukā ca hotu pamāṇikā ca anārambhā ca saparikkamanā cā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): telling them to build one whose site has been approved, which is the right size, where no harm will be done, and which has space on all sides, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.11.13
Pali: Tassa kuṭiṁ karonti desitavatthukaṁ pamāṇikaṁ sārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): but they build one whose site has been approved, which is the right size, where harm will be done, and which lacks space on all sides. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.11.14
Pali: So suṇāti—“kuṭi kira me kayirati desitavatthukā pamāṇikā sārambhā aparikkamanā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he hears about it, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.11.15
Pali: Tena bhikkhunā sāmaṁ vā gantabbaṁ dūto vā pāhetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): he must either go there himself or send a message, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.11.16
Pali: “anārambhā ca hotu saparikkamanā cā”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): telling them to build one where no harm will be done and which has space on all sides. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.11.17
Pali: “anārambhā hotū”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): telling them to build one where no harm will be done. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.11.18
Pali: “saparikkamanā hotū”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): telling them to build one which has space on all sides. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.11.19
Pali: anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.12.1
Pali: Bhikkhu samādisitvā pakkamati— “kuṭiṁ me karothā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): A monk appoints someone to build him a hut. He then departs, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.12.2
Pali: Samādisati ca— “desitavatthukā ca hotu anārambhā ca saparikkamanā cā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): telling them to build one whose site has been approved, where no harm will be done, and which has space on all sides. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.12.3
Pali: Tassa kuṭiṁ karonti adesitavatthukaṁ sārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ, āpatti kārukānaṁ tiṇṇaṁ dukkaṭānaṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If they build one whose site has not been approved, where harm will be done, and which lacks space on all sides, the builders commit three offenses of wrong conduct. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.12.4
Pali: sārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ, āpatti kārukānaṁ dvinnaṁ dukkaṭānaṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): where harm will be done, but which has space on all sides, the builders commit two offenses of wrong conduct. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.12.5
Pali: anārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ, āpatti kārukānaṁ dvinnaṁ dukkaṭānaṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): where no harm will be done, but which lacks space on all sides, the builders commit two offenses of wrong conduct. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.12.6
Pali: anārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ, āpatti kārukānaṁ dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): where no harm will be done, and which has space on all sides, the builders commit one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.12.7
Pali: Bhikkhu samādisitvā pakkamati— “kuṭiṁ me karothā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): A monk appoints someone to build him a hut. He then departs,

Pali: Tassa kuṭiṁ karonti desitavatthukaṁ sārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ, āpatti kārukānaṁ dvinnaṁ dukkaṭānaṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If they build one whose site has been approved, where harm will be done, and which lacks space on all sides, the builders commit two offenses of wrong conduct. …

English (brahmali, published): where no harm will be done, but which lacks space on all sides, the builders commit one offense of wrong conduct. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.12.12
Pali: anārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): where no harm will be done, and which has space on all sides, there is no offense.

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.14.1
Pali: Bhikkhu samādisitvā pakkamati— “kuṭiṁ me karothā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): A monk appoints someone to build him a hut and then departs. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.14.2
Pali: Tassa kuṭiṁ karonti adesitavatthukaṁ sārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They build one whose site has not been approved, where harm will be done, and which lacks space on all sides. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.14.3
Pali: So ce vippakate āgacchati, tena bhikkhunā sā kuṭi aññassa vā dātabbā bhinditvā vā puna kātabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is unfinished when he returns, that hut is to be given to someone else, or it is to be demolished and rebuilt. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.14.4
Pali: No ce aññassa vā dadeyya bhinditvā vā puna kāreyya, āpatti saṅghādisesena dvinnaṁ dukkaṭānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he neither gives it to someone else, nor demolishes and rebuilds it, he commits one offense entailing suspension and two offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.14.5
Pali: Bhikkhu samādisitvā pakkamati— “kuṭiṁ me karothā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): A monk appoints someone to build him a hut and then departs. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.14.6
Pali: Tassa kuṭiṁ karonti adesitavatthukaṁ sārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They build one whose site has not been approved, where harm will be done, but which has space on all sides. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.14.7
Pali: So ce vippakate āgacchati, tena bhikkhunā sā kuṭi aññassa vā dātabbā bhinditvā vā puna kātabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is unfinished when he returns, that hut is to be given to someone else, or it is to be demolished and rebuilt.

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.14.42
Pali: So ce vippakate āgacchati, tena bhikkhunā sā kuṭi aññassa vā dātabbā bhinditvā vā puna kātabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is unfinished when he returns, that hut is to be given to someone else, or it is to be demolished and rebuilt. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.14.43
Pali: No ce aññassa vā dadeyya bhinditvā vā puna kāreyya, āpatti dvinnaṁ dukkaṭānaṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If he neither gives it to someone else, nor demolishes and rebuilds it, he commits two offenses of wrong conduct. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.14.44
Pali: sārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ, āpatti dukkaṭassa …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): where harm will be done, but which has space on all sides … he commits one offense of wrong conduct. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.14.45
Pali: anārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): where no harm will be done, but which lacks space on all sides … he commits one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.14.46
Pali: Bhikkhu samādisitvā pakkamati— “kuṭiṁ me karothā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): A monk appoints someone to build him a hut and then departs. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.14.47
Pali: Tassa kuṭiṁ karonti desitavatthukaṁ pamāṇikaṁ anārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): They build one whose site has been approved, which is the right size, where no harm will be done, and which has space on all sides. There is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.15.1
Pali: Attanā vippakataṁ attanā pariyosāpeti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he finishes what he began himself, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.15.2
Pali: Attanā vippakataṁ parehi pariyosāpeti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has others finish what he began himself, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.15.3
Pali: Parehi vippakataṁ attanā pariyosāpeti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he finishes himself what was begun by others, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.15.4
Pali: Parehi vippakataṁ parehi pariyosāpeti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has others finish what was begun by others, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.16.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.16.2
Pali: leṇe 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a shelter; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.16.3
Pali: guhāya 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a cave; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.16.4
Pali: tiṇakuṭikāya 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a grass hut; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.16.5
Pali: aññassatthāya 
English (brahmali, published): if it is built for someone else; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.16.6
Pali: vāsāgāraṁ ṭhapetvā sabbattha anāpatti 
English (brahmali, published): if it is anything apart from a dwelling; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.16.7
Pali: ummattakassa 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.16.8
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss6:3.16.9
Pali: Kuṭikārasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ chaṭṭhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on building a hut inappropriately, the sixth, is finished.
