pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:0.3
Pali: Saṅghādisesakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing suspension 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:0.4
Pali: 7. Vihārakārasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 7. Building a dwelling inappropriately 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā kosambiyaṁ viharati ghositārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Kosambī in Ghosita’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmato channassa upaṭṭhāko gahapati āyasmantaṁ channaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): a householder who was a supporter of Venerable Channa said to him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:1.3
Pali: “vihāravatthuṁ, bhante, jānāhi ayyassa vihāraṁ kārāpessāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I’ll have a dwelling built for you, venerable, if you would find a site for it.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:1.4
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā channo vihāravatthuṁ sodhento aññataraṁ cetiyarukkhaṁ chedāpesi gāmapūjitaṁ nigamapūjitaṁ nagarapūjitaṁ janapadapūjitaṁ raṭṭhapūjitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): While Venerable Channa was clearing a site for that dwelling, he felled a tree that served as a shrine and was revered by village, town, district, and kingdom. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:1.5
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:1.6
Pali: “kathañhi nāma samaṇā sakyaputtiyā cetiyarukkhaṁ chedāpessanti gāmapūjitaṁ nigamapūjitaṁ nagarapūjitaṁ janapadapūjitaṁ raṭṭhapūjitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the Sakyan monastics fell a tree that serves as a shrine and is revered by village, town, district, and kingdom? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:1.7
Pali: Ekindriyaṁ samaṇā sakyaputtiyā jīvaṁ viheṭhentī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): They are hurting one-sensed life.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:1.8
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the criticism of those people, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:1.9
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:1.10
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā channo cetiyarukkhaṁ chedāpessati gāmapūjitaṁ …pe… raṭṭhapūjitan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Channa in the same way. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:1.11
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ channaṁ anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking Venerable Channa in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned Channa: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:1.12
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, channa, cetiyarukkhaṁ chedāpesi gāmapūjitaṁ …pe… raṭṭhapūjitan”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Channa, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:1.13
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:1.14
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:1.15
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, cetiyarukkhaṁ chedāpessasi gāmapūjitaṁ nigamapūjitaṁ nagarapūjitaṁ janapadapūjitaṁ raṭṭhapūjitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:1.16
Pali: Jīvasaññino hi, moghapurisa, manussā rukkhasmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): People perceive trees as conscious. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:1.17
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:1.18
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:1.19.1
Pali: “Mahallakaṁ pana bhikkhunā vihāraṁ kārayamānena sassāmikaṁ attuddesaṁ bhikkhū abhinetabbā vatthudesanāya. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘When a monk builds a large dwelling with a sponsoring owner and intended for himself, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:1.20
Pali: Tehi bhikkhūhi vatthu desetabbaṁ anārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): he must have monks approve a site where no harm will be done and which has space on all sides. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:1.21
Pali: Sārambhe ce bhikkhu vatthusmiṁ aparikkamane mahallakaṁ vihāraṁ kāreyya bhikkhū vā anabhineyya vatthudesanāya, saṅghādiseso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk builds a large dwelling on a site where harm will be done and which lacks space on all sides, or he does not have monks approve the site, he commits an offense entailing suspension.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.1
Pali: Mahallako nāma vihāro 
English (brahmali, published): A large dwelling: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.2
Pali: sassāmiko vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): one with a sponsoring owner is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.3
Pali: Vihāro nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Dwelling: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.4
Pali: ullitto vā hoti avalitto vā ullittāvalitto vā. 
English (brahmali, published): plastered inside or plastered outside or plastered both inside and outside. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.5
Pali: Kārayamānenāti 
English (brahmali, published): Builds: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.6
Pali: karonto vā kārāpento vā. 
English (brahmali, published): building it himself or having it built. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.7
Pali: Sassāmikanti 
English (brahmali, published): With a sponsoring owner: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.8
Pali: añño koci sāmiko hoti itthī vā puriso vā gahaṭṭho vā pabbajito vā. 
English (brahmali, published): there is another owner, either a woman or a man, either a householder or a monastic. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.9
Pali: Attuddesanti 
English (brahmali, published): Intended for himself: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.10
Pali: attano atthāya. 
English (brahmali, published): for his own use. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.11
Pali: Bhikkhū abhinetabbā vatthudesanāyāti 
English (brahmali, published): He must have monks approve a site: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.12
Pali: tena vihārakārakena bhikkhunā vihāravatthuṁ sodhetvā saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā vuḍḍhānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ pāde vanditvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): the monk who wants to build a dwelling should clear a site. He should then approach the Sangha, arrange his upper robe over one shoulder, pay respect at the feet of the senior monks, squat on his heels, raise his joined palms, and say: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.13
Pali: “ahaṁ, bhante, mahallakaṁ vihāraṁ kattukāmo sassāmikaṁ attuddesaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, I want to build a large dwelling with a sponsoring owner and intended for myself. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.14
Pali: Sohaṁ, bhante, saṅghaṁ vihāravatthuolokanaṁ yācāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I request the Sangha to inspect the site for the dwelling.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.15
Pali: Dutiyampi yācitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): He should make his request a second 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.16
Pali: Tatiyampi yācitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): and a third time. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.17
Pali: Sace sabbo saṅgho ussahati vihāravatthuṁ oloketuṁ sabbena saṅghena oloketabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If the whole Sangha is able to inspect the site, they should all go. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.18
Pali: No ce sabbo saṅgho ussahati vihāravatthuṁ oloketuṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): If the whole Sangha is unable to inspect the site, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.19
Pali: ye tattha honti bhikkhū byattā paṭibalā sārambhaṁ anārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ aparikkamanaṁ jānituṁ te yācitvā sammannitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): then those monks there who are competent and capable—who know where harm will be done and where no harm will be done, who know what is meant by space on all sides and a lack of space on all sides—should be asked and then appointed. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.20
Pali: “Evañca pana, bhikkhave, sammannitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, they should be appointed like this. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.21
Pali: Byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena saṅgho ñāpetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): A competent and capable monk should inform the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.22
Pali: ‘Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.23
Pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmo bhikkhu mahallakaṁ vihāraṁ kattukāmo sassāmikaṁ attuddesaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Monk so-and-so wants to build a large dwelling with a sponsoring owner and intended for himself. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.24
Pali: So saṅghaṁ vihāravatthuolokanaṁ yācati. 
English (brahmali, published): He is requesting the Sangha to inspect the site for that dwelling. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.25
Pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmañca itthannāmañca bhikkhū sammanneyya itthannāmassa bhikkhuno vihāravatthuṁ oloketuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If the Sangha is ready, it should appoint monks so-and-so and so-and-so to inspect the site for the dwelling of monk so-and-so. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.26
Pali: Esā ñatti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the motion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.27
Pali: Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.28
Pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmo bhikkhu mahallakaṁ vihāraṁ kattukāmo sassāmikaṁ attuddesaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Monk so-and-so wants to build a large dwelling with a sponsoring owner and intended for himself. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.29
Pali: So saṅghaṁ vihāravatthuolokanaṁ yācati. 
English (brahmali, published): He is requesting the Sangha to inspect the site for that dwelling. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.30
Pali: Saṅgho itthannāmañca itthannāmañca bhikkhū sammannati itthannāmassa bhikkhuno vihāravatthuṁ oloketuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha appoints monks so-and-so and so-and-so to inspect the site for the dwelling of monk so-and-so. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.31
Pali: Yassāyasmato khamati itthannāmassa ca itthannāmassa ca bhikkhūnaṁ sammuti itthannāmassa bhikkhuno vihāravatthuṁ oloketuṁ, so tuṇhassa; 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who approves of appointing monks so-and-so and so-and-so to inspect the site for the dwelling of monk so-and-so should remain silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.32
Pali: yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.33
Pali: Sammatā saṅghena itthannāmo ca itthannāmo ca bhikkhū itthannāmassa bhikkhuno vihāravatthuṁ oloketuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha has appointed monks so-and-so and so-and-so to inspect the site for the dwelling of monk so-and-so. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.34
Pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.35
Pali: evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I will remember it thus.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.36
Pali: Tehi sammatehi bhikkhūhi tattha gantvā vihāravatthu oloketabbaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): The appointed monks should go and inspect the site for the dwelling 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.37
Pali: sārambhaṁ anārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ aparikkamanaṁ jānitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): to find out if any harm will be done and if it has space on all sides. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.38
Pali: Sace sārambhaṁ hoti aparikkamanaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): If harm will be done or it lacks space on all sides, they should say, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.39
Pali: “māyidha karī”ti vattabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Don’t build here.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.40
Pali: Sace anārambhaṁ hoti saparikkamanaṁ, saṅghassa ārocetabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): If no harm will be done and it has space on all sides, they should inform the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.41
Pali: “anārambhaṁ saparikkamanan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘No harm will be done and it has space on all sides.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.42
Pali: Tena vihārakārakena bhikkhunā saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā vuḍḍhānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ pāde vanditvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): The monk who wants to build the dwelling should then approach the Sangha, arrange his upper robe over one shoulder, pay respect at the feet of the senior monks, squat on his heels, raise his joined palms, and say: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.43
Pali: “ahaṁ, bhante, mahallakaṁ vihāraṁ kattukāmo sassāmikaṁ attuddesaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Venerables, I want to build a large dwelling with a sponsoring owner and intended for myself. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.44
Pali: Sohaṁ, bhante, saṅghaṁ vihāravatthudesanaṁ yācāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I request the Sangha to approve the site for the dwelling.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.45
Pali: Dutiyampi yācitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): He should make his request a second 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.46
Pali: Tatiyampi yācitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): and a third time. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.47
Pali: Byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena saṅgho ñāpetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): A competent and capable monk should then inform the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.48
Pali: “Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.49
Pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmo bhikkhu mahallakaṁ vihāraṁ kattukāmo sassāmikaṁ attuddesaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Monk so-and-so wants to build a large dwelling with a sponsoring owner and intended for himself. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.50
Pali: So saṅghaṁ vihāravatthudesanaṁ yācati. 
English (brahmali, published): He is requesting the Sangha to approve the site for that dwelling. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.51
Pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmassa bhikkhuno vihāravatthuṁ deseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): If the Sangha is ready, it should approve the site for the dwelling of monk so-and-so. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.52
Pali: Esā ñatti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the motion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.53
Pali: Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.54
Pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmo bhikkhu mahallakaṁ vihāraṁ kattukāmo sassāmikaṁ attuddesaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Monk so-and-so wants to build a large dwelling with a sponsoring owner and intended for himself. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.55
Pali: So saṅghaṁ vihāravatthudesanaṁ yācati. 
English (brahmali, published): He is requesting the Sangha to approve the site for that dwelling. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.56
Pali: Saṅgho itthannāmassa bhikkhuno vihāravatthuṁ deseti. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha approves the site for the dwelling of monk so-and-so. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.57
Pali: Yassāyasmato khamati itthannāmassa bhikkhuno vihāravatthussa desanā, so tuṇhassa; 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who agrees to approving the site for the dwelling of monk so-and-so should remain silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.58
Pali: yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who doesn’t agree should speak up. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.59
Pali: Desitaṁ saṅghena itthannāmassa bhikkhuno vihāravatthu. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha has approved the site for the dwelling of monk so-and-so. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.60
Pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.61
Pali: evametaṁ dhārayāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I will remember it thus.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.62
Pali: Sārambhaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Where harm will be done: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.63
Pali: kipillikānaṁ vā āsayo hoti, upacikānaṁ vā āsayo hoti, undūrānaṁ vā …pe… ahīnaṁ vā … vicchikānaṁ vā … satapadīnaṁ vā … hatthīnaṁ vā … assānaṁ vā … sīhānaṁ vā … byagghānaṁ vā … dīpīnaṁ vā … acchānaṁ vā … taracchānaṁ vā āsayo hoti, yesaṁ kesañci tiracchānagatānaṁ pāṇānaṁ āsayo hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): it is the abode of ants, termites, rats, snakes, scorpions, centipedes, elephants, horses, lions, tigers, leopards, bears, or hyenas, or any other animal; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.64
Pali: pubbaṇṇanissitaṁ vā hoti, aparaṇṇanissitaṁ vā hoti, abbhāghātanissitaṁ vā hoti, āghātananissitaṁ vā hoti, susānanissitaṁ vā hoti, uyyānanissitaṁ vā hoti, rājavatthunissitaṁ vā hoti, hatthisālānissitaṁ vā hoti, assasālānissitaṁ vā hoti, bandhanāgāranissitaṁ vā hoti, pānāgāranissitaṁ vā hoti, sūnanissitaṁ vā hoti, racchānissitaṁ vā hoti, caccaranissitaṁ vā hoti, sabhānissitaṁ vā hoti, saṁsaraṇanissitaṁ vā hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): or it is bordering on a field of grain, a field of vegetables, a place of slaughter, a place of execution, a charnel ground, a park, a royal property, an elephant stable, a horse stable, a prison, a bar, a slaughterhouse, a street, a crossroads, a public meeting hall, or a cul-de-sac—

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.65
Pali: Etaṁ sārambhaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): this is called “where harm will be done”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.66
Pali: Aparikkamanaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Which lacks space on all sides: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.67
Pali: na sakkā hoti yathāyuttena sakaṭena anuparigantuṁ, samantā nisseṇiyā anuparigantuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): it is not possible to go around it with a yoked cart, or to go all the way around it with a ladder—

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.68
Pali: Etaṁ aparikkamanaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): this is called “which lacks space on all sides”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.69
Pali: Anārambhaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Where no harm will be done: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.70
Pali: na kipillikānaṁ vā āsayo hoti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): it is not the abode of ants … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.71
Pali: na saṁsaraṇanissitaṁ vā hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): it is not bordering on … a cul-de-sac—

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.72
Pali: Etaṁ anārambhaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): this is called “where no harm will be done”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.73
Pali: Saparikkamanaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Which has space on all sides: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.74
Pali: sakkā hoti yathāyuttena sakaṭena anuparigantuṁ, samantā nisseṇiyā anuparigantuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): it is possible to go around it with a yoked cart, or to go all the way around it with a ladder—

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.75
Pali: Etaṁ saparikkamanaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): this is called “which has space on all sides”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.76
Pali: Mahallako nāma vihāro 
English (brahmali, published): A large dwelling: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.77
Pali: sassāmiko vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): one with a sponsoring owner is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.78
Pali: Vihāro nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Dwelling: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.79
Pali: ullitto vā hoti avalitto vā ullittāvalitto vā. 
English (brahmali, published): plastered inside or plastered outside or plastered both inside and outside. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.80
Pali: Kāreyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Builds: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.81
Pali: karoti vā kārāpeti vā. 
English (brahmali, published): building it himself or having it built. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.82
Pali: Bhikkhū vā anabhineyya vatthudesanāyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Or he does not have monks approve the site: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.83
Pali: ñattidutiyena kammena vihāravatthuṁ na desāpetvā karoti vā kārāpeti vā, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if the site has not been approved through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements, and he then builds a dwelling or has one built, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.84
Pali: Ekaṁ piṇḍaṁ anāgate, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When there is one piece left to complete the dwelling, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.85
Pali: Tasmiṁ piṇḍe āgate, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When the last piece is finished, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.86
Pali: Saṅghādisesoti 
English (brahmali, published): He commits an offense entailing suspension: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:2.87
Pali: …pe… tenapi vuccati— “saṅghādiseso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): … Therefore, too, it is called “an offense entailing suspension”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.1.1
Pali: Bhikkhu vihāraṁ karoti adesitavatthukaṁ sārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ, āpatti saṅghādisesena dvinnaṁ dukkaṭānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk builds a dwelling whose site has not been approved, where harm will be done, and which lacks space on all sides, he commits one offense entailing suspension and two offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.1.2
Pali: Bhikkhu vihāraṁ karoti adesitavatthukaṁ sārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ, āpatti saṅghādisesena dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk builds a dwelling whose site has not been approved, where harm will be done, but which has space on all sides, he commits one offense entailing suspension and one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.1.3
Pali: Bhikkhu vihāraṁ karoti adesitavatthukaṁ anārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ, āpatti saṅghādisesena dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk builds a dwelling whose site has not been approved, where no harm will be done, but which lacks space on all sides, he commits one offense entailing suspension and one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.1.4
Pali: Bhikkhu vihāraṁ karoti adesitavatthukaṁ anārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk builds a dwelling whose site has not been approved, where no harm will be done, and which has space on all sides, he commits one offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.1.5
Pali: Bhikkhu vihāraṁ karoti desitavatthukaṁ sārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ, āpatti dvinnaṁ dukkaṭānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk builds a dwelling whose site has been approved, where harm will be done, and which lacks space on all sides, he commits two offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.1.6
Pali: Bhikkhu vihāraṁ karoti desitavatthukaṁ sārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk builds a dwelling whose site has been approved, where harm will be done, but which has space on all sides, he commits one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.1.7
Pali: Bhikkhu vihāraṁ karoti desitavatthukaṁ anārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk builds a dwelling whose site has been approved, where no harm will be done, but which lacks space on all sides, he commits one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.1.8
Pali: Bhikkhu vihāraṁ karoti desitavatthukaṁ anārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk builds a dwelling whose site has been approved, where no harm will be done, and which has space on all sides, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.2.1
Pali: Bhikkhu samādisati—
English (brahmali, published): A monk appoints someone 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.2.2
Pali: “vihāraṁ me karothā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): to build him a dwelling. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.2.3
Pali: Tassa vihāraṁ karonti adesitavatthukaṁ sārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ, āpatti saṅghādisesena dvinnaṁ dukkaṭānaṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If they build a dwelling whose site has not been approved, where harm will be done, and which lacks space on all sides, he commits one offense entailing suspension and two offenses of wrong conduct. …

English (brahmali, published): where no harm will be done, but which lacks space on all sides, the builders commit one offense of wrong conduct. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.5.12
Pali: anārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): where no harm will be done, and which has space on all sides, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.6.1
Pali: Bhikkhu samādisitvā pakkamati— “vihāraṁ me karothā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): A monk appoints someone to build him a dwelling and then departs. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.6.2
Pali: Tassa vihāraṁ karonti adesitavatthukaṁ sārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They build a dwelling whose site has not been approved, where harm will be done, and which lacks space on all sides. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.6.3
Pali: So ce vippakate āgacchati, tena bhikkhunā so vihāro aññassa vā dātabbo bhinditvā vā puna kātabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is unfinished when he returns, that dwelling is to be given to someone else, or it is to be demolished and rebuilt. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.6.4
Pali: No ce aññassa vā dadeyya bhinditvā vā puna kāreyya, āpatti saṅghādisesena dvinnaṁ dukkaṭānaṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If he neither gives it to someone else, nor demolishes and rebuilds it, he commits one offense entailing suspension and two offenses of wrong conduct. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.6.5
Pali: sārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ, āpatti saṅghādisesena dukkaṭassa …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): where harm will be done, but which has space on all sides … he commits one offense entailing suspension and one offense of wrong conduct. …

English (brahmali, published): where no harm will be done, but which lacks space on all sides … he commits one offense entailing suspension and one offense of wrong conduct. …

English (brahmali, published): where no harm will be done, and which has space on all sides … he commits one offense entailing suspension.

English (brahmali, published): A monk appoints someone to build him a dwelling and then departs. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.6.9
Pali: Tassa vihāraṁ karonti desitavatthukaṁ sārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They build a dwelling whose site has been approved, where harm will be done, and which lacks space on all sides. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.6.10
Pali: So ce vippakate āgacchati, tena bhikkhunā so vihāro aññassa vā dātabbo bhinditvā vā puna kātabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is unfinished when he returns, that dwelling is to be given to someone else, or it is to be demolished and rebuilt. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.6.11
Pali: No ce aññassa vā dadeyya bhinditvā vā puna kāreyya, āpatti dvinnaṁ dukkaṭānaṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): If he neither gives it to someone else, nor demolishes and rebuilds it, he commits two offenses of wrong conduct. …

Pali: sārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ, āpatti dukkaṭassa …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): where harm will be done, but which has space on all sides … he commits one offense of wrong conduct. …

English (brahmali, published): where no harm will be done, but which lacks space on all sides … he commits one offense of wrong conduct. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.6.14
Pali: anārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): where no harm will be done, and which has space on all sides … there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.7.1
Pali: Attanā vippakataṁ attanā pariyosāpeti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he finishes what he began himself, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.7.2
Pali: Attanā vippakataṁ parehi pariyosāpeti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has others finish what he began himself, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.7.3
Pali: Parehi vippakataṁ attanā pariyosāpeti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he finishes himself what was begun by others, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.7.4
Pali: Parehi vippakataṁ parehi pariyosāpeti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he has others finish what was begun by others, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.7.5.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.7.6
Pali: leṇe guhāya tiṇakuṭikāya 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a shelter, a cave, or a grass hut; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.7.7
Pali: aññassatthāya 
English (brahmali, published): if it is built for someone else; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.7.8
Pali: vāsāgāraṁ ṭhapetvā sabbattha anāpatti 
English (brahmali, published): if it is anything apart from a dwelling; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.7.9
Pali: ummattakassa 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.7.10
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss7:3.7.11
Pali: Vihārakārasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ sattamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on building a dwelling inappropriately, the seventh, is finished.

English (brahmali, published): 8. Making a groundless accusation out of anger 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Rājagaha in the Bamboo Grove, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmatā dabbena mallaputtena jātiyā sattavassena arahattaṁ sacchikataṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Dabba the Mallian realized perfection at the age of seven. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.1.3
Pali: Yaṁ kiñci sāvakena pattabbaṁ sabbaṁ tena anuppattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): He had achieved all there is to achieve by a disciple 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.1.4
Pali: Natthi cassa kiñci uttari karaṇīyaṁ, katassa vā paticayo. 
English (brahmali, published): and had nothing further to do. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.1.5
Pali: Atha kho āyasmato dabbassa mallaputtassa rahogatassa paṭisallīnassa evaṁ cetaso parivitakko udapādi—
English (brahmali, published): Then, while reflecting in private, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.1.6
Pali: “mayā kho jātiyā sattavassena arahattaṁ sacchikataṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.1.7
Pali: Yaṁ kiñci sāvakena pattabbaṁ sabbaṁ mayā anuppattaṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.1.8
Pali: Natthi ca me kiñci uttari karaṇīyaṁ, katassa vā paticayo. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.1.9
Pali: Kiṁ nu kho ahaṁ saṅghassa veyyāvaccaṁ kareyyan”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): he thought, “How can I be of service to the Sangha? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.1.10
Pali: Atha kho āyasmato dabbassa mallaputtassa etadahosi—
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.1.11
Pali: “yannūnāhaṁ saṅghassa senāsanañca paññapeyyaṁ bhattāni ca uddiseyyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Why don’t I assign the dwellings and designate the meals?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.2.1
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā dabbo mallaputto sāyanhasamayaṁ paṭisallānā vuṭṭhito yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): In the evening Dabba came out of seclusion and went to the Buddha. He bowed, sat down, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.2.2
Pali: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā dabbo mallaputto bhagavantaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.2.3
Pali: “idha mayhaṁ, bhante, rahogatassa paṭisallīnassa evaṁ cetaso parivitakko udapādi mayā kho jātiyā sattavassena arahattaṁ sacchikataṁ, yaṁ kiñci sāvakena pattabbaṁ, sabbaṁ mayā anupattaṁ, natthi ca me kiñci uttari karaṇīyaṁ, katassa vā paticayo, kiṁ nu kho ahaṁ saṅghassa veyyāvaccaṁ kareyyanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, while I was reflecting in private, it occurred to me that I’ve achieved all there is to achieve by a disciple, and I was wondering how I could be of service to the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.2.4
Pali: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhante, etadahosi—
English (brahmali, published): I thought, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.2.5
Pali: ‘yannūnāhaṁ saṅghassa senāsanañca paññapeyyaṁ bhattāni ca uddiseyyan’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Why don’t I assign the dwellings and designate the meals?’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.2.6
Pali: Icchāmahaṁ, bhante, saṅghassa senāsanañca paññapetuṁ bhattāni ca uddisitun”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.2.7
Pali: “Sādhu sādhu, dabba. 
English (brahmali, published): “Good, good, Dabba, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.2.8
Pali: Tena hi tvaṁ, dabba, saṅghassa senāsanañca paññapehi bhattāni ca uddisā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): please do so.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.2.9
Pali: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho āyasmā dabbo mallaputto bhagavato paccassosi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.3.1
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.3.2
Pali: “tena hi, bhikkhave, saṅgho dabbaṁ mallaputtaṁ senāsanapaññāpakañca bhattuddesakañca sammannatu. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, the Sangha should appoint Dabba the Mallian as the assigner of dwellings and the designator of meals. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.3.3
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, sammannitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): And he should be appointed like this. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.3.4
Pali: Paṭhamaṁ dabbo mallaputto yācitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): First Dabba should be asked. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.3.5
Pali: Yācitvā byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena saṅgho ñāpetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): A competent and capable monk should then inform the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.3.6
Pali: ‘Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.3.7
Pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ saṅgho āyasmantaṁ dabbaṁ mallaputtaṁ senāsanapaññāpakañca bhattuddesakañca sammanneyya. 
English (brahmali, published): If the Sangha is ready, it should appoint Venerable Dabba the Mallian as the assigner of dwellings and the designator of meals. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.3.8
Pali: Esā ñatti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the motion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.3.9
Pali: Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.3.10
Pali: Saṅgho āyasmantaṁ dabbaṁ mallaputtaṁ senāsanapaññāpakañca bhattuddesakañca sammannati. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha appoints Venerable Dabba the Mallian as the assigner of dwellings and the designator of meals. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.3.11
Pali: Yassāyasmato khamati āyasmato dabbassa mallaputtassa senāsanapaññāpakassa ca bhattuddesakassa ca sammuti, so tuṇhassa; 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who approves of appointing Venerable Dabba as the assigner of dwellings and the designator of meals should remain silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.3.12
Pali: yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.3.13
Pali: Sammato saṅghena āyasmā dabbo mallaputto senāsanapaññāpako ca bhattuddesako ca. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha has appointed Venerable Dabba the Mallian as the assigner of dwellings and the designator of meals. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.3.14
Pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.3.15
Pali: evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I will remember it thus.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.4.1
Pali: Sammato ca panāyasmā dabbo mallaputto sabhāgānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ ekajjhaṁ senāsanaṁ paññapeti. 
English (brahmali, published): Dabba assigned dwellings to the monks according to their character. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.4.2
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū suttantikā tesaṁ ekajjhaṁ senāsanaṁ paññapeti—
English (brahmali, published): He assigned dwellings in the same place to those monks who were experts on the discourses, thinking, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.4.3
Pali: “te aññamaññaṁ suttantaṁ saṅgāyissantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “They’ll recite the discourses to one another.”

English (brahmali, published): And he did likewise for the experts on the Monastic Law, thinking, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.4.5

English (brahmali, published): “They’ll discuss the Monastic Law;”

Pali: Ye te bhikkhū dhammakathikā tesaṁ ekajjhaṁ senāsanaṁ paññapeti—
English (brahmali, published): for the expounders of the Teaching, thinking, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.4.7
Pali: “te aññamaññaṁ dhammaṁ sākacchissantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “They’ll discuss the Teaching;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.4.8
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū jhāyino tesaṁ ekajjhaṁ senāsanaṁ paññapeti—
English (brahmali, published): for the meditators, thinking, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.4.9
Pali: “te aññamaññaṁ na byābādhissantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “They won’t disturb one another;” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.4.10
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū tiracchānakathikā kāyadaḷhibahulā viharanti tesampi ekajjhaṁ senāsanaṁ paññapeti—
English (brahmali, published): and for the gossips and the bodybuilders, thinking, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.4.11
Pali: “imāyapime āyasmanto ratiyā acchissantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “In this way even these venerables will be happy.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.4.12
Pali: Yepi te bhikkhū vikāle āgacchanti tesampi tejodhātuṁ samāpajjitvā teneva ālokena senāsanaṁ paññapeti. 
English (brahmali, published): When monks arrived at night, he entered the fire element and assigned dwellings with the help of that light. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.4.13
Pali: Apisu bhikkhū sañcicca vikāle āgacchanti—
English (brahmali, published): Monks even arrived late on purpose, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.4.14
Pali: “mayaṁ āyasmato dabbassa mallaputtassa iddhipāṭihāriyaṁ passissāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): hoping to see the marvel of Dabba’s supernormal powers. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.4.15
Pali: Te āyasmantaṁ dabbaṁ mallaputtaṁ upasaṅkamitvā evaṁ vadanti—
English (brahmali, published): They would approach Dabba and say,

Pali: “amhākaṁ, āvuso dabba, senāsanaṁ paññapehī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable Dabba, please assign us a dwelling.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.4.17

English:

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.4.31
Pali: Tesaṁ āyasmā dabbo mallaputto tejodhātuṁ samāpajjitvā aṅguliyā jalamānāya purato purato gacchati.

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.4.32
Pali: Tepi teneva ālokena āyasmato dabbassa mallaputtassa piṭṭhito piṭṭhito gacchanti.

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.4.33
Pali: Tesaṁ āyasmā dabbo mallaputto evaṁ senāsanaṁ paññapeti—
English (brahmali, published): And he would assign them dwellings: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.4.34
Pali: “ayaṁ mañco, idaṁ pīṭhaṁ, ayaṁ bhisi, idaṁ bibbohanaṁ, idaṁ vaccaṭṭhānaṁ, idaṁ passāvaṭṭhānaṁ, idaṁ pānīyaṁ, idaṁ paribhojanīyaṁ, ayaṁ kattaradaṇḍo, 
English (brahmali, published): “This is the bed, this the bench, this the mattress, this the pillow, this the place for defecating, this the place for urinating, this the water for drinking, this the water for washing, this the walking stick; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.4.35
Pali: idaṁ saṅghassa katikasaṇṭhānaṁ, imaṁ kālaṁ pavisitabbaṁ, imaṁ kālaṁ nikkhamitabban”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): these are the Sangha’s agreements concerning the right time to enter and the right time to leave.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.4.36
Pali: Tesaṁ āyasmā dabbo mallaputto evaṁ senāsanaṁ paññapetvā punadeva veḷuvanaṁ paccāgacchati. 
English (brahmali, published): Dabba then returned to the Bamboo Grove. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.5.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena mettiyabhūmajakā bhikkhū navakā ceva honti appapuññā ca. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the monks Mettiya and Bhūmajaka were only newly ordained. They had little merit, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.5.2
Pali: Yāni saṅghassa lāmakāni senāsanāni tāni tesaṁ pāpuṇanti lāmakāni ca bhattāni. 
English (brahmali, published): getting inferior dwellings and meals. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.5.3
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena rājagahe manussā icchanti therānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ abhisaṅkhārikaṁ piṇḍapātaṁ dātuṁ sappimpi telampi uttaribhaṅgampi. 
English (brahmali, published): The people of Rājagaha were keen to give specially prepared almsfood to the senior monks—ghee, oil, and special curries—

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.5.4
Pali: Mettiyabhūmajakānaṁ pana bhikkhūnaṁ pākatikaṁ denti yathārandhaṁ kaṇājakaṁ bilaṅgadutiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): but to the monks Mettiya and Bhūmajaka they gave ordinary food of porridge and broken rice. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.5.5
Pali: Te pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkantā there bhikkhū pucchanti—
English (brahmali, published): When they had eaten their meal and returned from almsround, they asked the senior monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.5.6
Pali: “tumhākaṁ, āvuso, bhattagge kiṁ ahosi? Tumhākaṁ, āvuso, bhattagge kiṁ ahosī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What did you get at the dining hall?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.5.7
Pali: Ekacce therā evaṁ vadanti—
English (brahmali, published): Some said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.5.8
Pali: “amhākaṁ, āvuso, sappi ahosi telaṁ ahosi uttaribhaṅgaṁ ahosī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “We got ghee, oil, and special curries.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.5.9
Pali: Mettiyabhūmajakā pana bhikkhū evaṁ vadanti—
English (brahmali, published): But the monks Mettiya and Bhūmajaka said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.5.10
Pali: “amhākaṁ, āvuso, na kiñci ahosi, pākatikaṁ yathārandhaṁ kaṇājakaṁ bilaṅgadutiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “We didn’t get anything except ordinary food of porridge and broken rice.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.6.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena kalyāṇabhattiko gahapati saṅghassa catukkabhattaṁ deti niccabhattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time there was a householder who gave a regular meal of fine food to four monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.6.2
Pali: So bhattagge saputtadāro upatiṭṭhitvā parivisati. 
English (brahmali, published): He made his offering in the dining hall together with his wives and children. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.6.3
Pali: Aññe odanena pucchanti, aññe sūpena pucchanti, aññe telena pucchanti, aññe uttaribhaṅgena pucchanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Some of them offered rice, some bean curry, some oil, and some special curries. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.6.4
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena kalyāṇabhattikassa gahapatino bhattaṁ svātanāya mettiyabhūmajakānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ uddiṭṭhaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion the meal to be given by this householder on the following day had been designated to the monks Mettiya and Bhūmajaka. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.6.5
Pali: Atha kho kalyāṇabhattiko gahapati ārāmaṁ agamāsi kenacideva karaṇīyena. 
English (brahmali, published): Just then that householder went to the monastery on some business. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.6.6
Pali: So yenāyasmā dabbo mallaputto tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ dabbaṁ mallaputtaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): He approached Dabba, bowed, and sat down. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.6.7
Pali: Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho kalyāṇabhattikaṁ gahapatiṁ āyasmā dabbo mallaputto dhammiyā kathāya sandassesi samādapesi samuttejesi sampahaṁsesi. 
English (brahmali, published): Dabba instructed, inspired, and gladdened him with a teaching. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.6.8
Pali: Atha kho kalyāṇabhattiko gahapati āyasmatā dabbena mallaputtena dhammiyā kathāya sandassito samādapito samuttejito sampahaṁsito āyasmantaṁ dabbaṁ mallaputtaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): After the talk, he asked Dabba, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.6.9
Pali: “kassa, bhante, amhākaṁ ghare svātanāya bhattaṁ uddiṭṭhan”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, who has been designated to receive tomorrow’s meal in our house?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.6.10
Pali: “Mettiyabhūmajakānaṁ kho, gahapati, bhikkhūnaṁ tumhākaṁ ghare svātanāya bhattaṁ uddiṭṭhan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Mettiya and Bhūmajaka.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.6.11
Pali: Atha kho kalyāṇabhattiko gahapati anattamano ahosi—
English (brahmali, published): He was disappointed, and thought, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.6.12
Pali: “kathañhi nāma pāpabhikkhū amhākaṁ ghare bhuñjissantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Why should bad monks eat in our house?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.6.13
Pali: Gharaṁ gantvā dāsiṁ āṇāpesi—
English (brahmali, published): After returning to his house, he told a female slave, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.6.14
Pali: “ye, je, sve bhattikā āgacchanti te koṭṭhake āsanaṁ paññapetvā kaṇājakena bilaṅgadutiyena parivisā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “For those who are coming for tomorrow’s meal, prepare seats in the gatehouse and serve them broken rice and porridge.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.6.15
Pali: “Evaṁ, ayyā”ti kho sā dāsī kalyāṇabhattikassa gahapatino paccassosi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.7.1
Pali: Atha kho mettiyabhūmajakā bhikkhū—
English (brahmali, published): The monks Mettiya and Bhūmajaka said to each other, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.7.2
Pali: “hiyyo kho, āvuso, amhākaṁ kalyāṇabhattikassa gahapatino bhattaṁ uddiṭṭhaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): “Yesterday we were designated a meal from that householder who offers fine food. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.7.3
Pali: sve amhe kalyāṇabhattiko gahapati saputtadāro upatiṭṭhitvā parivisissati; 
English (brahmali, published): Tomorrow he’ll serve us together with his wives and children. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.7.4
Pali: aññe odanena pucchissanti, aññe sūpena pucchissanti, aññe telena pucchissanti, aññe uttaribhaṅgena pucchissantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Some of them will offer us rice, some bean curry, some oil, and some special curries.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.7.5
Pali: Te teneva somanassena na cittarūpaṁ rattiyā supiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): And because they were excited, they did not sleep properly that night. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.7.6
Pali: Atha kho mettiyabhūmajakā bhikkhū pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaraṁ ādāya yena kalyāṇabhattikassa gahapatino nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkamiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): The following morning they robed up, took their bowls and robes, and went to the house of that householder. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.7.7
Pali: Addasā kho sā dāsī mettiyabhūmajake bhikkhū dūratova āgacchante. Disvāna koṭṭhake āsanaṁ paññapetvā mettiyabhūmajake bhikkhū etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): When the female slave saw them coming, she prepared seats in the gatehouse and said to them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.7.8
Pali: “nisīdatha, bhante”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please sit, venerables.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.7.9
Pali: Atha kho mettiyabhūmajakānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ etadahosi—
English (brahmali, published): They thought, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.7.10
Pali: “nissaṁsayaṁ kho na tāva bhattaṁ siddhaṁ bhavissati. Yathā mayaṁ koṭṭhake nisīdeyyāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “The meal can’t be ready, since we’re given seats in the gatehouse.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.7.11
Pali: Atha kho sā dāsī kaṇājakena bilaṅgadutiyena upagacchi—
English (brahmali, published): She then brought them broken rice and porridge, and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.7.12
Pali: “bhuñjatha, bhante”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Eat, sirs.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.7.13
Pali: “Mayaṁ kho, bhagini, niccabhattikā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “But, sister, we’ve come for the regular meal.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.7.14
Pali: “Jānāmi, ayyā, niccabhattikāti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I know. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.7.15
Pali: Api cāhaṁ hiyyova gahapatinā āṇattā— ‘ye, je, sve bhattikā āgacchanti te koṭṭhake āsanaṁ paññapetvā kaṇājakena bilaṅgadutiyena parivisā’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): But yesterday I was told by the head of the household to serve you like this. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.7.16
Pali: Bhuñjatha, bhante”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Please eat.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.7.17
Pali: Atha kho mettiyabhūmajakā bhikkhū—
English (brahmali, published): They said to each other, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.7.18
Pali: “hiyyo kho, āvuso, kalyāṇabhattiko gahapati ārāmaṁ agamāsi dabbassa mallaputtassa santike. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yesterday this householder came to the monastery and spoke with Dabba. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.7.19
Pali: Nissaṁsayaṁ kho mayaṁ dabbena mallaputtena gahapatino antare paribhinnā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Dabba must be responsible for this split between the householder and us.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.7.20
Pali: Te teneva domanassena na cittarūpaṁ bhuñjiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): And because they were dejected, they did not eat as much as they had intended. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.7.21
Pali: Atha kho mettiyabhūmajakā bhikkhū pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkantā ārāmaṁ gantvā pattacīvaraṁ paṭisāmetvā bahārāmakoṭṭhake saṅghāṭipallatthikāya nisīdiṁsu tuṇhībhūtā maṅkubhūtā pattakkhandhā adhomukhā pajjhāyantā appaṭibhānā. 
English (brahmali, published): When they had eaten their meal and returned from almsround, they put their bowls and robes away and squatted on their heels outside the monastery gatehouse, using their upper robes as a back-and-knee strap. They were silent and humiliated, with shoulders drooping and heads down, glum and speechless. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.8.1
Pali: Atha kho mettiyā bhikkhunī yena mettiyabhūmajakā bhikkhū tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā mettiyabhūmajake bhikkhū etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Just then the nun Mettiyā came to them and said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.8.2
Pali: “vandāmi, ayyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “My respectful greetings to you, venerables.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.8.3
Pali: Evaṁ vutte, mettiyabhūmajakā bhikkhū nālapiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): But they did not respond. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.8.4
Pali: Dutiyampi kho …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): A second time 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.8.5
Pali: tatiyampi kho mettiyā bhikkhunī mettiyabhūmajake bhikkhū etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): and a third time she said 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.8.6
Pali: “vandāmi, ayyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): the same thing, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.8.7
Pali: Tatiyampi kho mettiyabhūmajakā bhikkhū nālapiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): but they still did not respond. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.8.8
Pali: “Kyāhaṁ ayyānaṁ aparajjhāmi? 
English (brahmali, published): “Have I done something wrong? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.8.9
Pali: Kissa maṁ ayyā nālapantī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): Why don’t you respond?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.8.10
Pali: “Tathā hi pana tvaṁ, bhagini, amhe dabbena mallaputtena viheṭhīyamāne ajjhupekkhasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s because we’ve been badly treated by Dabba the Mallian, and you’re not taking an interest.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.8.11
Pali: “Kyāhaṁ, ayyā, karomī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “But what can I do?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.8.12
Pali: “Sace kho tvaṁ, bhagini, iccheyyāsi ajjeva bhagavā dabbaṁ mallaputtaṁ nāsāpeyyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “If you like, you could make the Buddha expel Dabba.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.8.13
Pali: “Kyāhaṁ, ayyā, karomi, kiṁ mayā sakkā kātun”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “And how can I do that?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.8.14
Pali: “Ehi tvaṁ, bhagini, yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkama; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ evaṁ vadehi—
English (brahmali, published): “Go to the Buddha and say, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.8.15
Pali: ‘idaṁ, bhante, nacchannaṁ nappatirūpaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Sir, this isn’t proper or appropriate. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.8.16
Pali: Yāyaṁ, bhante, disā abhayā anītikā anupaddavā sāyaṁ disā sabhayā saītikā saupaddavā. 
English (brahmali, published): There’s fear, distress, and oppression in this district, where none of these should exist. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.8.17
Pali: Yato nivātaṁ tato savātaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): It’s windy where it should be calm. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.8.18
Pali: Udakaṁ maññe ādittaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): It’s as if water is burning. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.8.19
Pali: Ayyenamhi dabbena mallaputtena dūsitā’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Dabba the Mallian has raped me.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.8.20
Pali: “Evaṁ, ayyā”ti kho mettiyā bhikkhunī mettiyabhūmajakānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ paṭissutvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): Saying, “Alright, venerables,” she went to the Buddha, bowed, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.8.21
Pali: Ekamantaṁ ṭhitā kho sā mettiyā bhikkhunī bhagavantaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): and repeated what she had been told to say. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.8.22
Pali: “idaṁ, bhante, nacchannaṁ nappatirūpaṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.8.23
Pali: Yāyaṁ, bhante, disā abhayā anītikā anupaddavā sāyaṁ disā sabhayā saītikā saupaddavā. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.8.24
Pali: Yato nivātaṁ tato savātaṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.8.25
Pali: Udakaṁ maññe ādittaṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.8.26
Pali: Ayyenamhi dabbena mallaputtena dūsitā”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.1
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe bhikkhusaṅghaṁ sannipātāpetvā āyasmantaṁ dabbaṁ mallaputtaṁ paṭipucchi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha had the Sangha gathered and questioned Dabba: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.2
Pali: “sarasi tvaṁ, dabba, evarūpaṁ kattā yathāyaṁ bhikkhunī āhā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Dabba, do you remember doing as the nun Mettiyā says?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.3
Pali: “Yathā maṁ, bhante, bhagavā jānātī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, you know what I’m like.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.4
Pali: Dutiyampi kho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): A second and

English (brahmali, published): a third time the Buddha asked

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.7

English (brahmali, published): and got the same response. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.8
Pali: “Na kho, dabba, dabbā evaṁ nibbeṭhenti. 
English (brahmali, published): He then said, “Dabba, the Dabbas don’t give such evasive answers. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.9
Pali: Sace tayā kataṁ katanti vadehi, 
English (brahmali, published): If it was done by you, say so; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.10
Pali: sace tayā akataṁ akatanti vadehī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): if it wasn’t, then say that.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.11
Pali: “Yato ahaṁ, bhante, jāto nābhijānāmi supinantenapi methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevitā, pageva jāgaro”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Since I was born, sir, I don’t recall having sexual intercourse even in a dream, let alone when awake.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.12
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.13
Pali: “tena hi, bhikkhave, mettiyaṁ bhikkhuniṁ nāsetha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Well then, monks, expel the nun Mettiyā, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.14
Pali: Ime ca bhikkhū anuyuñjathā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): and call these monks to account.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.15
Pali: Idaṁ vatvā bhagavā uṭṭhāyāsanā vihāraṁ pāvisi. 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha then got up from his seat and entered his dwelling. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.16
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū mettiyaṁ bhikkhuniṁ nāsesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): When the monks had expelled the nun Mettiyā, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.17
Pali: Atha kho mettiyabhūmajakā bhikkhū te bhikkhū etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): the monks Mettiya and Bhūmajaka said to them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.18
Pali: “māvuso, mettiyaṁ bhikkhuniṁ nāsetha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Don’t expel the nun Mettiyā; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.19
Pali: Na sā kiñci aparajjhati. 
English (brahmali, published): she hasn’t done anything wrong. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.20
Pali: Amhehi sā ussāhitā kupitehi anattamanehi cāvanādhippāyehī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): She was urged on by us. We were angry and displeased, and trying to make Dabba give up the monastic life.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.21
Pali: “Kiṁ pana tumhe, āvuso, āyasmantaṁ dabbaṁ mallaputtaṁ amūlakena pārājikena dhammena anuddhaṁsethā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “But did you groundlessly charge Venerable Dabba with an offense entailing expulsion?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.22
Pali: “Evamāvuso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.23
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires … complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.24
Pali: “kathañhi nāma mettiyabhūmajakā bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ dabbaṁ mallaputtaṁ amūlakena pārājikena dhammena anuddhaṁsessantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the monks Mettiya and Bhūmajaka groundlessly charge Venerable Dabba with an offense entailing expulsion?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.25
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū mettiyabhūmajake bhikkhū anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): They rebuked those monks in many ways and then told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned those monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.26
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, dabbaṁ mallaputtaṁ amūlakena pārājikena dhammena anuddhaṁsethā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that you did this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.27
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.28
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.29
Pali: “kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, dabbaṁ mallaputtaṁ amūlakena pārājikena dhammena anuddhaṁsessatha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish men, how could you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.30
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisā, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.31
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.32.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhuṁ duṭṭho doso appatīto amūlakena pārājikena dhammena anuddhaṁseyya—‘appeva nāma naṁ imamhā brahmacariyā cāveyyan’ti, 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk who is angry and displeased groundlessly charges a monk with an offense entailing expulsion, aiming to make him leave the monastic life, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:1.9.33
Pali: tato aparena samayena samanuggāhīyamāno vā asamanuggāhīyamāno vā amūlakañceva taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ hoti bhikkhu ca dosaṁ patiṭṭhāti, saṅghādiseso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): and then after some time, whether he is questioned or not, it is clear that the legal issue is groundless, and he admits to his ill will, he commits an offense entailing suspension.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.5
Pali: Bhikkhunti 
English (brahmali, published): A monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.6
Pali: aññaṁ bhikkhuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): another monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.7
Pali: Duṭṭho dosoti 
English (brahmali, published): Angry: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.8
Pali: kupito anattamano anabhiraddho āhatacitto khilajāto. 
English (brahmali, published): upset, dissatisfied, discontent, having hatred, hostile. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.9
Pali: Appatītoti 
English (brahmali, published): Displeased: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.10
Pali: tena ca kopena tena ca dosena tāya ca anattamanatāya tāya ca anabhiraddhiyā appatīto hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): because of that upset, that ill will, that dissatisfaction, and that discontent, he is displeased. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.11
Pali: Amūlakaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Groundlessly: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.12
Pali: adiṭṭhaṁ asutaṁ aparisaṅkitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): not seen, not heard, not suspected. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.13
Pali: Pārājikena dhammenāti 
English (brahmali, published): With an offense entailing expulsion: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.14
Pali: catunnaṁ aññatarena. 
English (brahmali, published): with one of the four. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.15
Pali: Anuddhaṁseyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Charges: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.16
Pali: codeti vā codāpeti vā. 
English (brahmali, published): accuses him or has him accused. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.17
Pali: Appeva nāma naṁ imamhā brahmacariyā cāveyyanti 
English (brahmali, published): To make him leave the monastic life: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.18
Pali: bhikkhubhāvā cāveyyaṁ, samaṇadhammā cāveyyaṁ, sīlakkhandhā cāveyyaṁ, tapoguṇā cāveyyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): to make him leave the monkhood, leave the state of a monastic, leave his morality, leave the virtue of monasticism. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.19
Pali: Tato aparena samayenāti 
English (brahmali, published): And then after some time: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.20
Pali: yasmiṁ khaṇe anuddhaṁsito hoti taṁ khaṇaṁ taṁ layaṁ taṁ muhuttaṁ vītivatte. 
English (brahmali, published): the moment, the instant, the second after he has laid the charge. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.21
Pali: Samanuggāhīyamānoti 
English (brahmali, published): He is questioned: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.22
Pali: yena vatthunā anuddhaṁsito hoti tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ samanuggāhīyamāno. 
English (brahmali, published): he is questioned about the grounds of his charge. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.23
Pali: Asamanuggāhīyamānoti 
English (brahmali, published): Not: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.24
Pali: na kenaci vuccamāno. 
English (brahmali, published): he is not spoken to by anyone. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.25
Pali: Adhikaraṇaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The legal issue: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.26
Pali: cattāri adhikaraṇāni— vivādādhikaraṇaṁ, anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ, āpattādhikaraṇaṁ, kiccādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): there are four kinds of legal issues: legal issues arising from disputes, legal issues arising from accusations, legal issues arising from offenses, legal issues arising from business. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.27
Pali: Bhikkhu ca dosaṁ patiṭṭhātīti 
English (brahmali, published): And he admits to his ill will: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.28
Pali: tucchakaṁ mayā bhaṇitaṁ, musā mayā bhaṇitaṁ, abhūtaṁ mayā bhaṇitaṁ, ajānantena mayā bhaṇitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “What I said was empty,” “What I said was false,” “What I said was unreal,” “I said it without knowing.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.29
Pali: Saṅghādisesoti 
English (brahmali, published): He commits an offense entailing suspension: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:2.30
Pali: …pe… tenapi vuccati saṅghādisesoti. 
English (brahmali, published): … Therefore, too, it is called “an offense entailing suspension”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:3.1.1
Pali: Adiṭṭhassa hoti pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ ajjhāpajjanto. 
English (brahmali, published): Although he has not seen it, he accuses someone of having committed an offense entailing expulsion: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:3.1.2
Pali: Tañce codeti—“diṭṭho mayā, pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ ajjhāpannosi, 
English (brahmali, published): “I’ve seen that you’ve committed an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:3.1.3
Pali: assamaṇosi, asakyaputtiyosi, 
English (brahmali, published): You’re not an ascetic, not a Sakyan monastic. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:3.1.4
Pali: natthi tayā saddhiṁ uposatho vā pavāraṇā vā saṅghakammaṁ vā”ti, 
English (brahmali, published): You’re excluded from the observance-day ceremony, from the invitation ceremony, and from the legal procedures of the Sangha.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:3.1.5
Pali: āpatti vācāya, vācāya saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): For each statement, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:3.1.6
Pali: Asutassa hoti—
English (brahmali, published): Although he has not heard it, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:3.1.7
Pali: “pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ ajjhāpanno”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): he accuses someone of having committed an offense entailing expulsion:

Pali: Tañce codeti—“suto mayā, pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ ajjhāpannosi, 
English (brahmali, published): “I’ve heard that you’ve committed an offense entailing expulsion.

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:3.4.2
Pali: Diṭṭhe vematiko diṭṭhaṁ no kappeti diṭṭhaṁ nassarati diṭṭhaṁ pamuṭṭho hoti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): but he is unsure of what he has seen, he does not believe what he has seen, he does not remember what he has seen, he is confused about what he has seen … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:3.4.3
Pali: sute vematiko sutaṁ no kappeti sutaṁ nassarati sutaṁ pamuṭṭho hoti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): he is unsure of what he has heard, he does not believe what he has heard, he does not remember what he has heard, he is confused about what he has heard … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:3.4.4
Pali: parisaṅkite vematiko parisaṅkitaṁ no kappeti parisaṅkitaṁ nassarati parisaṅkitaṁ pamuṭṭho hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): he is unsure of what he suspects, he does not believe what he suspects, he does not remember what he suspects, he is confused about what he suspects. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:3.4.5
Pali: Tañce codeti—
English (brahmali, published): If he then accuses him like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:3.4.6
Pali: “parisaṅkito mayā diṭṭho ca …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “I suspect and I’ve seen …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:3.4.7
Pali: parisaṅkito mayā suto ca …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “I suspect and I’ve heard …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:3.4.8
Pali: parisaṅkito mayā diṭṭho ca suto ca, pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ ajjhāpannosi, 
English (brahmali, published): “I suspect and I’ve seen and I’ve heard that you’ve committed an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:3.4.9
Pali: assamaṇosi, asakyaputtiyosi, 
English (brahmali, published): You’re not an ascetic, not a Sakyan monastic. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:3.4.10
Pali: natthi tayā saddhiṁ uposatho vā pavāraṇā vā saṅghakammaṁ vā”ti, 
English (brahmali, published): You’re excluded from the observance-day ceremony, from the invitation ceremony, and from the legal procedures of the Sangha.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:3.4.11
Pali: āpatti vācāya, vācāya saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): For each statement, he commits an offense entailing suspension.

Pali: Adiṭṭhassa hoti pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ ajjhāpajjanto.

English (brahmali, published): For each statement, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:4.1.1
Pali: Asuddhe suddhadiṭṭhi, 
English (brahmali, published): Someone is impure, but viewed as pure; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:4.1.2
Pali: suddhe asuddhadiṭṭhi, 
English (brahmali, published): someone is pure, but viewed as impure; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:4.1.3
Pali: asuddhe asuddhadiṭṭhi, 
English (brahmali, published): someone is impure and viewed as impure; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:4.1.4
Pali: suddhe suddhadiṭṭhi. 
English (brahmali, published): someone is pure and viewed as pure. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:4.2.1
Pali: Asuddho hoti puggalo aññataraṁ pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ ajjhāpanno. 
English (brahmali, published): An impure person has committed an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:4.2.2
Pali: Tañce suddhadiṭṭhi samāno anokāsaṁ kārāpetvā cāvanādhippāyo vadeti, āpatti saṅghādisesena dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one views him as pure, but then, without having gotten his permission, speaks with the aim of making him leave the monastic life, one commits one offense entailing suspension and one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:4.2.3
Pali: Asuddho hoti puggalo aññataraṁ pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ ajjhāpanno. 
English (brahmali, published): An impure person has committed an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:4.2.4
Pali: Tañce suddhadiṭṭhi samāno okāsaṁ kārāpetvā cāvanādhippāyo vadeti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one views him as pure, but then, having gotten his permission, speaks with the aim of making him leave the monastic life, one commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:4.2.5
Pali: Asuddho hoti puggalo aññataraṁ pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ ajjhāpanno. 
English (brahmali, published): An impure person has committed an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:4.2.6
Pali: Tañce suddhadiṭṭhi samāno anokāsaṁ kārāpetvā akkosādhippāyo vadeti, āpatti omasavādena dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one views him as pure, but then, without having gotten his permission, speaks with the aim of abusing him, one commits one offense for abusive speech and one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:4.2.7
Pali: Asuddho hoti puggalo aññataraṁ pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ ajjhāpanno. 
English (brahmali, published): An impure person has committed an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:4.2.8
Pali: Tañce suddhadiṭṭhi samāno okāsaṁ kārāpetvā akkosādhippāyo vadeti, āpatti omasavādassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one views him as pure, but then, having gotten his permission, speaks with the aim of abusing him, one commits an offense for abusive speech. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:4.3.1
Pali: Suddho hoti puggalo aññataraṁ pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ anajjhāpanno. 
English (brahmali, published): A pure person has not committed an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:4.3.2
Pali: Tañce asuddhadiṭṭhi samāno anokāsaṁ kārāpetvā cāvanādhippāyo vadeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one views him as impure, and then, without having gotten his permission, speaks with the aim of making him leave the monastic life, one commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:4.3.3
Pali: Suddho hoti puggalo aññataraṁ pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ anajjhāpanno. 
English (brahmali, published): A pure person has not committed an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:4.3.4
Pali: Tañce asuddhadiṭṭhi samāno okāsaṁ kārāpetvā cāvanādhippāyo vadeti, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If one views him as impure, and then, having gotten his permission, speaks with the aim of making him leave the monastic life, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:4.3.5
Pali: Suddho hoti puggalo aññataraṁ pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ anajjhāpanno.

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:4.5.4
Pali: Tañce suddhadiṭṭhi samāno okāsaṁ kārāpetvā cāvanādhippāyo vadeti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one views him as pure, but then, having gotten his permission, speaks with the aim of making him leave the monastic life, one commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:4.5.5
Pali: Suddho hoti puggalo aññataraṁ pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ anajjhāpanno. 
English (brahmali, published): A pure person has not committed an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:4.5.6
Pali: Tañce suddhadiṭṭhi samāno anokāsaṁ kārāpetvā akkosādhippāyo vadeti, āpatti omasavādena dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If one views him as pure, but then, without having gotten his permission, speaks with the aim of abusing him, one commits one offense for abusive speech and one offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:4.5.7
Pali: Suddho hoti puggalo aññataraṁ pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ anajjhāpanno tañce suddhadiṭṭhi samāno okāsaṁ kārāpetvā akkosādhippāyo vadeti, āpatti omasavādassa. 
English (brahmali, published): A pure person has not committed an offense entailing expulsion. If one views him as pure, but then, having gotten his permission, speaks with the aim of abusing him, one commits an offense for abusive speech. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:4.5.8.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:4.5.9
Pali: suddhe asuddhadiṭṭhissa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he views a pure person as impure; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:4.5.10
Pali: asuddhe asuddhadiṭṭhissa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he views an impure person as impure; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:4.5.11
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:4.5.12
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss8:4.5.13
Pali: Amūlakasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ aṭṭhamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on making a groundless accusation out of anger, the eighth, is finished.

English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk who is angry and displeased, uses an unrelated legal issue as a pretext to charge a monk with an offense entailing expulsion, aiming to make him leave the monastic life, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:1.2.15

English (brahmali, published): and then after some time, whether he is questioned or not, it is clear that the legal issue is unrelated and was used as a pretext, and he admits to his ill will, he commits an offense entailing suspension.’”

English (brahmali, published): How is a legal issue unrelated to a legal issue? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.2.4

English (brahmali, published): A legal issue arising from a dispute is unrelated to a legal issue arising from an accusation, a legal issue arising from an offense, and a legal issue arising from business.

English (brahmali, published): A legal issue arising from an offense is unrelated to a legal issue arising from business, a legal issue arising from a dispute, and a legal issue arising from an accusation. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.2.7

English (brahmali, published): A legal issue arising from business is unrelated to a legal issue arising from a dispute, a legal issue arising from an accusation, and a legal issue arising from an offense.

English (brahmali, published): It is in this way that a legal issue is unrelated to a legal issue.

English (brahmali, published): How is a legal issue related to a legal issue?

Pali: Anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ anuvādādhikaraṇassa tabbhāgiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): A legal issue arising from an accusation is related to a legal issue arising from an accusation. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.2.12

English (brahmali, published): A legal issue arising from an offense may be either related or unrelated to a legal issue arising from an offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.2.13

English (brahmali, published): How is a legal issue arising from an offense unrelated to a legal issue arising from an offense? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.2.14
Pali: Methunadhammapārājikāpatti adinnādānapārājikāpattiyā manussaviggahapārājikāpattiyā uttarimanussadhammapārājikāpattiyā aññabhāgiyā. 
English (brahmali, published): An offense entailing expulsion in regard to sexual intercourse is unrelated to an offense entailing expulsion in regard to stealing, an offense entailing expulsion in regard to a human being, and an offense entailing expulsion in regard to a superhuman quality. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.2.15
Pali: Adinnādānapārājikāpatti manussaviggahapārājikāpattiyā uttarimanussadhammapārājikāpattiyā methunadhammapārājikāpattiyā aññabhāgiyā. 
English (brahmali, published): An offense entailing expulsion in regard to stealing is unrelated to an offense entailing expulsion in regard to a human being, an offense entailing expulsion in regard to a superhuman quality, and an offense entailing expulsion in regard to sexual intercourse. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.2.16
Pali: Manussaviggahapārājikāpatti uttarimanussadhammapārājikāpattiyā methunadhammapārājikāpattiyā adinnādānapārājikāpattiyā aññabhāgiyā. 
English (brahmali, published): An offense entailing expulsion in regard to a human being is unrelated to an offense entailing expulsion in regard to a superhuman quality, an offense entailing expulsion in regard to sexual intercourse, and an offense entailing expulsion in regard to stealing. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.2.17
Pali: Uttarimanussadhammapārājikāpatti methunadhammapārājikāpattiyā adinnādānapārājikāpattiyā manussaviggahapārājikāpattiyā aññabhāgiyā. 
English (brahmali, published): An offense entailing expulsion in regard to a superhuman quality is unrelated to an offense entailing expulsion in regard to sexual intercourse, an offense entailing expulsion in regard to stealing, and an offense entailing expulsion in regard to a human being. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.2.18

English (brahmali, published): It is in this way that a legal issue arising from an offense is unrelated to a legal issue arising from an offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.2.19

English (brahmali, published): How is a legal issue arising from an offense related to a legal issue arising from an offense? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.2.20

English (brahmali, published): An offense entailing expulsion in regard to sexual intercourse is related to an offense entailing expulsion in regard to sexual intercourse. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.2.21

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.2.23

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.2.24
Pali: Evaṁ āpattādhikaraṇaṁ āpattādhikaraṇassa tabbhāgiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): It is in this way that a legal issue arising from an offense is related to a legal issue arising from an offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.2.25
Pali: Kiccādhikaraṇaṁ kiccādhikaraṇassa tabbhāgiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): A legal issue arising from business is related to a legal issue arising from business. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.2.26
Pali: Evaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ adhikaraṇassa tabbhāgiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): It is in this way that a legal issue is related to a legal issue. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.3.1
Pali: Kiñci desaṁ lesamattaṁ upādāyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Uses as a pretext: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.3.2
Pali: leso nāma dasa lesā— jātileso, nāmaleso, gottaleso, liṅgaleso, āpattileso, pattaleso, cīvaraleso, upajjhāyaleso, ācariyaleso, senāsanaleso. 
English (brahmali, published): A pretext: there are ten kinds of pretext—the pretext of caste, the pretext of name, the pretext of family, the pretext of characteristic, the pretext of offense, the pretext of almsbowl, the pretext of robe, the pretext of preceptor, the pretext of teacher, the pretext of dwelling. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.3.3
Pali: Jātileso nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The pretext of caste: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.3.4
Pali: khattiyo diṭṭho hoti pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ ajjhāpajjanto. 
English (brahmali, published): a monk sees an aristocrat committing an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.3.5
Pali: Aññaṁ khattiyaṁ passitvā codeti—
English (brahmali, published): If he then accuses another aristocrat, saying, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.3.6
Pali: “khattiyo mayā diṭṭho. 
English (brahmali, published): “I’ve seen an aristocrat. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.3.7
Pali: Pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ ajjhāpannosi, 
English (brahmali, published): You’ve committed an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.3.8
Pali: assamaṇosi, asakyaputtiyosi, 
English (brahmali, published): You’re not an ascetic, not a Sakyan monastic. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.3.9
Pali: natthi tayā saddhiṁ uposatho vā pavāraṇā vā saṅghakammaṁ vā”ti, 
English (brahmali, published): You’re excluded from the observance-day ceremony, from the invitation ceremony, and from the legal procedures of the Sangha,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.3.10
Pali: āpatti vācāya, vācāya saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): he commits an offense entailing suspension for each statement.
