pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.4.1
Pali: Pārājikena dhammenāti 
English (brahmali, published): With an offense entailing expulsion: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.4.2
Pali: catunnaṁ aññatarena. 
English (brahmali, published): with one of the four. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.4.3
Pali: Anuddhaṁseyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): To charge: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.4.4
Pali: codeti vā codāpeti vā. 
English (brahmali, published): accuses him or has him accused. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.4.5
Pali: Appeva nāma naṁ imamhā brahmacariyā cāveyyanti 
English (brahmali, published): To make him leave the monastic life: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.4.6
Pali: bhikkhubhāvā cāveyyaṁ, samaṇadhammā cāveyyaṁ, sīlakkhandhā cāveyyaṁ, tapoguṇā cāveyyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): to make him leave the monkhood, leave the state of a monastic, leave his morality, leave the virtue of monasticism. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.4.7
Pali: Tato aparena samayenāti 
English (brahmali, published): And then after some time: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.4.8
Pali: yasmiṁ khaṇe anuddhaṁsito hoti, taṁ khaṇaṁ taṁ layaṁ taṁ muhuttaṁ vītivatte. 
English (brahmali, published): the moment, the instant, the second after he has laid the charge. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.4.9
Pali: Samanuggāhīyamānoti 
English (brahmali, published): He is questioned: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.4.10
Pali: yena vatthunā anuddhaṁsito hoti tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ samanuggāhīyamāno. 
English (brahmali, published): he is questioned about the grounds of his charge. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.4.11
Pali: Asamanuggāhīyamānoti 
English (brahmali, published): Not: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.4.12
Pali: na kenaci vuccamāno. 
English (brahmali, published): he is not spoken to by anyone. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.4.13
Pali: Adhikaraṇaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The legal issue: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.4.14
Pali: cattāri adhikaraṇāni— vivādādhikaraṇaṁ, anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ, āpattādhikaraṇaṁ, kiccādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): there are four kinds of legal issues: legal issues arising from disputes, legal issues arising from accusations, legal issues arising from offenses, legal issues arising from business. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.4.15
Pali: Koci deso lesamatto upādinnoti 
English (brahmali, published): Was used as a pretext: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.4.16
Pali: tesaṁ lesānaṁ aññataro leso upādinno hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): he has used a certain pretext among those listed above. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.4.17
Pali: Bhikkhu ca dosaṁ patiṭṭhātīti 
English (brahmali, published): And he admits to his ill will: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.4.18
Pali: tucchakaṁ mayā bhaṇitaṁ, musā mayā bhaṇitaṁ, abhūtaṁ mayā bhaṇitaṁ, ajānantena mayā bhaṇitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “What I said was empty,” “What I said was false,” “What I said was unreal,” “I said it without knowing.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.4.19
Pali: Saṅghādisesoti 
English (brahmali, published): He commits an offense entailing suspension: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:2.4.20
Pali: …pe… tenapi vuccati “saṅghādiseso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): … Therefore, too, it is called “an offense entailing suspension”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:3.1.1
Pali: Bhikkhu saṅghādisesaṁ ajjhāpajjanto diṭṭho hoti, saṅghādisese saṅghādisesadiṭṭhi hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): A monk sees another monk committing an offense entailing suspension, and the first monk regards it as an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:3.1.2
Pali: Tañce pārājikena codeti—
English (brahmali, published): If he then accuses him of an offense entailing expulsion, saying, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:3.1.3
Pali: “assamaṇosi, asakyaputtiyosi, 
English (brahmali, published): “You’re not an ascetic, not a Sakyan monastic. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:3.1.4
Pali: natthi tayā saddhiṁ uposatho vā pavāraṇā vā saṅghakammaṁ vā”ti, 
English (brahmali, published): You’re excluded from the observance-day ceremony, from the invitation ceremony, and from the legal procedures of the Sangha,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:3.1.5
Pali: evampi āpattaññabhāgiyaṁ hoti leso ca upādinno, āpatti vācāya, vācāya saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): thus using an unrelated offense as a pretext, he commits an offense entailing suspension for each statement. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:3.1.6
Pali: Bhikkhu saṅghādisesaṁ ajjhāpajjanto diṭṭho hoti, saṅghādisese thullaccayadiṭṭhi hoti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): A monk sees another monk committing an offense entailing suspension, but the first monk regards it as a serious offense … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:3.1.7
Pali: pācittiyadiṭṭhi hoti … 
English (brahmali, published): but the first monk regards it as an offense entailing confession … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:3.1.8
Pali: pāṭidesanīyadiṭṭhi hoti … 
English (brahmali, published): but the first monk regards it as an offense entailing acknowledgment … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:3.1.9
Pali: dukkaṭadiṭṭhi hoti … 
English (brahmali, published): but the first monk regards it as an offense of wrong conduct … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss9:3.1.10
Pali: dubbhāsitadiṭṭhi hoti.

English (brahmali, published): The training rule on making a groundless accusation out of anger, using a pretext, the ninth, is finished.

English (brahmali, published): 10. Causing a schism in the Sangha 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Rājagaha in the Bamboo Grove, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.1.2
Pali: Atha kho devadatto yena kokāliko kaṭamodakatissako khaṇḍadeviyā putto samuddadatto tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā kokālikaṁ kaṭamodakatissakaṁ khaṇḍadeviyā puttaṁ samuddadattaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Devadatta went to Kokālika, Kaṭamodakatissaka, Khaṇḍadeviyāputta, and Samuddadatta. He said to them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.1.3
Pali: “etha mayaṁ, āvuso, samaṇassa gotamassa saṅghabhedaṁ karissāma cakkabhedan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Let’s cause a schism in the Sangha of the ascetic Gotama. Let’s break its authority.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.1.4
Pali: Evaṁ vutte, kokāliko devadattaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Kokālika said to Devadatta,

Pali: “samaṇo kho, āvuso, gotamo mahiddhiko mahānubhāvo. 
English (brahmali, published): “The ascetic Gotama is powerful and mighty. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.1.6
Pali: Kathaṁ mayaṁ samaṇassa gotamassa saṅghabhedaṁ karissāma cakkabhedan”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): How can we do this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.1.7
Pali: “Etha mayaṁ, āvuso, samaṇaṁ gotamaṁ upasaṅkamitvā pañca vatthūni yācissāma—
English (brahmali, published): “Well, let’s go to the ascetic Gotama and request five things: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.1.8
Pali: ‘bhagavā, bhante, anekapariyāyena appicchassa santuṭṭhassa sallekhassa dhutassa pāsādikassa apacayassa vīriyārambhassa vaṇṇavādī. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘In many ways, sir, you praise fewness of wishes, contentment, self-effacement, ascetic practices, being inspiring, the reduction in things, and being energetic. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.1.9
Pali: Imāni, bhante, pañca vatthūni anekapariyāyena appicchatāya santuṭṭhiyā sallekhāya dhutatāya pāsādikatāya apacayāya vīriyārambhāya saṁvattanti. 
English (brahmali, published): And there are five things that lead to just that: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.1.10
Pali: Sādhu, bhante, bhikkhū 
English (brahmali, published): It would be good, sir, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.1.11
Pali: yāvajīvaṁ āraññikā assu; 
English (brahmali, published): if the monks stayed in the wilderness for life, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.1.12
Pali: yo gāmantaṁ osareyya, vajjaṁ naṁ phuseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): and whoever stayed near an inhabited area would commit an offense 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.1.13
Pali: Yāvajīvaṁ piṇḍapātikā assu; 
English (brahmali, published): if they ate only almsfood for life,

English (brahmali, published): if they were rag-robe wearers for life, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.1.16
Pali: yo gahapaticīvaraṁ sādiyeyya, vajjaṁ naṁ phuseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): and whoever accepted robe-cloth from a householder would commit an offense 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.1.17
Pali: Yāvajīvaṁ rukkhamūlikā assu;

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.1.18

English (brahmali, published): and whoever took shelter would commit an offense

Pali: Yāvajīvaṁ macchamaṁsaṁ na khādeyyuṁ;

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.1.20
Pali: yo macchamaṁsaṁ khādeyya, vajjaṁ naṁ phuseyyā’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): and whoever did would commit an offense.’ 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.1.21
Pali: Imāni samaṇo gotamo nānujānissati. 
English (brahmali, published): The ascetic Gotama won’t allow this. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.1.22
Pali: Te mayaṁ imehi pañcahi vatthūhi janaṁ saññāpessāmāti. 
English (brahmali, published): We’ll then be able to win people over with these five points.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.1.23
Pali: Sakkā kho, āvuso, imehi pañcahi vatthūhi samaṇassa gotamassa saṅghabhedo kātuṁ cakkabhedo.

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.1.24

English (brahmali, published): for people have confidence in austerity.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.2.10

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.2.11
Pali: yo icchati āraññiko hotu, yo icchati gāmante viharatu; 
English (brahmali, published): Those who wish may stay in the wilderness, and those who wish may live near inhabited areas.

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.2.14
Pali: Aṭṭhamāse kho mayā, devadatta, rukkhamūlasenāsanaṁ anuññātaṁ, tikoṭiparisuddhaṁ macchamaṁsaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): I have allowed the foot of a tree as a resting place for eight months of the year, as well as fish and meat that are pure in three respects: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.2.15
Pali: adiṭṭhaṁ asutaṁ aparisaṅkitan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): one hasn’t seen, heard, or suspected that the animal was specifically killed to feed a monastic.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.2.16
Pali: Atha kho devadatto—
English (brahmali, published): Devadatta thought, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.2.17
Pali: “na bhagavā imāni pañca vatthūni anujānātī”ti haṭṭho udaggo sapariso uṭṭhāyāsanā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): “The Buddha doesn’t allow the five points.” Glad and elated, he got up from his seat, bowed down, circumambulated the Buddha with his right side toward him, and left with his followers. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.2.18
Pali: Atha kho devadatto sapariso rājagahaṁ pavisitvā pañcahi vatthūhi janaṁ saññāpesi—
English (brahmali, published): Devadatta then entered Rājagaha and won people over with the five points, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.2.19
Pali: “mayaṁ, āvuso, samaṇaṁ gotamaṁ upasaṅkamitvā pañca vatthūni yācimhā—
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.2.20
Pali: ‘bhagavā, bhante, anekapariyāyena appicchassa santuṭṭhassa sallekhassa dhutassa pāsādikassa apacayassa vīriyārambhassa vaṇṇavādī. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.2.21
Pali: Imāni, bhante, pañca vatthūni anekapariyāyena appicchatāya santuṭṭhiyā sallekhāya dhutatāya pāsādikatāya apacayāya vīriyārambhāya saṁvattanti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.2.22
Pali: Sādhu, bhante, bhikkhū yāvajīvaṁ āraññikā assu; 
English:

English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.2.24
Pali: Yāvajīvaṁ piṇḍapātikā assu; 
English: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.2.25

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.2.32
Pali: Imāni samaṇo gotamo nānujānāti. 
English (brahmali, published): saying, “The ascetic Gotama doesn’t agree to them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.2.33
Pali: Te mayaṁ imehi pañcahi vatthūhi samādāya vattāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): but we practice in accordance with them.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.3.1
Pali: Tattha ye te manussā assaddhā appasannā dubbuddhino te evamāhaṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): The foolish people with little faith and confidence said, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.3.2
Pali: “ime kho samaṇā sakyaputtiyā dhutā sallekhavuttino, 
English (brahmali, published): “These Sakyan monastics are practicing asceticism and living with the aim of self-effacement. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.3.3
Pali: samaṇo pana gotamo bāhulliko bāhullāya cetetī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): But the ascetic Gotama is extravagant and has chosen a life of indulgence.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.3.4
Pali: Ye pana te manussā saddhā pasannā paṇḍitā byattā buddhimanto te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): But the wise people who had faith and confidence complained and criticized Devadatta, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.3.5
Pali: “kathañhi nāma devadatto bhagavato saṅghabhedāya parakkamissati cakkabhedāyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can Devadatta pursue schism in the Sangha of the Buddha? How can he break its authority?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.3.6
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhū tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks heard the criticism of those people, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.3.7
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the monks of few desires complained and criticized him 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.3.8
Pali: “kathañhi nāma devadatto saṅghabhedāya parakkamissati cakkabhedāyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): in the same way. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.3.9
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū devadattaṁ anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking Devadatta in many ways, they told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned Devadatta: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.3.10
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, devadatta, saṅghabhedāya parakkamasi cakkabhedāyā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, Devadatta, that you are doing this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.3.11
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.3.12
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.3.13
Pali: kathañhi nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, saṅghabhedāya parakkamissasi cakkabhedāya. 
English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, how can you do this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.3.14
Pali: Netaṁ, moghapurisa, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.3.15
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.3.16.1
Pali: “Yo pana bhikkhu samaggassa saṅghassa bhedāya parakkameyya, bhedanasaṁvattanikaṁ vā adhikaraṇaṁ samādāya paggayha tiṭṭheyya, so bhikkhu bhikkhūhi evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk pursues schism in a united Sangha or persists in taking up a legal issue conducive to schism, the monks should correct him like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.3.17
Pali: ‘māyasmā samaggassa saṅghassa bhedāya parakkami, bhedanasaṁvattanikaṁ vā adhikaraṇaṁ samādāya paggayha aṭṭhāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, don’t pursue schism in the united Sangha or persist in taking up a legal issue conducive to schism. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.3.18
Pali: Sametāyasmā saṅghena. Samaggo hi saṅgho sammodamāno avivadamāno ekuddeso phāsu viharatī’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Stay with the Sangha, for a united Sangha—in concord, in harmony, having a joint recitation—is at ease.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.3.19
Pali: Evañca so bhikkhu bhikkhūhi vuccamāno tatheva paggaṇheyya, so bhikkhu bhikkhūhi yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsitabbo tassa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): If that monk continues as before, the monks should press him up to three times to make him stop. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.3.20
Pali: Yāvatatiyañce samanubhāsiyamāno taṁ paṭinissajjeyya, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): If he then stops, all is well. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:1.3.21
Pali: no ce paṭinissajjeyya, saṅghādiseso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not stop, he commits an offense entailing suspension.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.1
Pali: Yo panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.2
Pali: yo yādiso …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.3
Pali: bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Monk: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippeto bhikkhūti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.5
Pali: Samaggo nāma saṅgho 
English (brahmali, published): A united Sangha: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.6
Pali: samānasaṁvāsako samānasīmāyaṁ ṭhito. 
English (brahmali, published): those belonging to the same Buddhist sect and staying within the same monastery zone. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.7
Pali: Bhedāya parakkameyyāti—
English (brahmali, published): Pursues schism: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.8
Pali: “kathaṁ ime nānā assu, vinā assu, vaggā assū”ti pakkhaṁ pariyesati, gaṇaṁ bandhati. 
English (brahmali, published): thinking, “What can I do to split, separate, and divide them?” he searches for a faction and puts together a group. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.9
Pali: Bhedanasaṁvattanikaṁ vā adhikaraṇanti 
English (brahmali, published): A legal issue conducive to schism: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.10
Pali: aṭṭhārasabhedakaravatthūni. 
English (brahmali, published): the eighteen grounds for schism. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.11
Pali: Samādāyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Taking up: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.12
Pali: ādāya. 
English (brahmali, published): having adopted. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.13
Pali: Paggayhāti 
English (brahmali, published): Taking up: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.14
Pali: dīpeyya. 
English (brahmali, published): he proclaims. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.15
Pali: Tiṭṭheyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): If he persists in: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.16
Pali: na paṭinissajjeyya. 
English (brahmali, published): if he does not stop. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.17
Pali: So bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Him: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.18
Pali: yo so saṅghabhedako bhikkhu. 
English (brahmali, published): the monk who is pursuing schism in the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.19
Pali: Bhikkhūhīti

English (brahmali, published): other monks, those who see it or hear it. They should correct him like this:

English (brahmali, published): And they should correct him a second

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.38

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.39
Pali: Byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena saṅgho ñāpetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): A competent and capable monk should inform the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.40
Pali: ‘Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.41
Pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmo bhikkhu samaggassa saṅghassa bhedāya parakkamati. 
English (brahmali, published): The monk so-and-so is pursuing schism in the united Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.42
Pali: So taṁ vatthuṁ na paṭinissajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): And he keeps on doing it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.43
Pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuṁ samanubhāseyya tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): If the Sangha is ready, it should press monk so-and-so to make him stop. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.44
Pali: Esā ñatti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the motion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.45
Pali: Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.46
Pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmo bhikkhu samaggassa saṅghassa bhedāya parakkamati. 
English (brahmali, published): The monk so-and-so is pursuing schism in the united Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.47
Pali: So taṁ vatthuṁ na paṭinissajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): And he keeps on doing it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.48
Pali: Saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuṁ samanubhāsati tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha presses monk so-and-so to make him stop. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.49
Pali: Yassāyasmato khamati itthannāmassa bhikkhuno samanubhāsanā tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya, so tuṇhassa; 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who approves of pressing monk so-and-so to make him stop should remain silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.50
Pali: yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.51
Pali: Dutiyampi etamatthaṁ vadāmi …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): For the second time, I speak on this matter. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.52

English (brahmali, published): For the third time, I speak on this matter. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.53
Pali: suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.54
Pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmo bhikkhu samaggassa saṅghassa bhedāya parakkamati. 
English (brahmali, published): The monk so-and-so is pursuing schism in the united Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.55
Pali: So taṁ vatthuṁ na paṭinissajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): And he keeps on doing it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.56

English (brahmali, published): The Sangha presses monk so-and-so to make him stop. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.57
Pali: Yassāyasmato khamati itthannāmassa bhikkhuno samanubhāsanā tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya, so tuṇhassa; 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who approves of pressing monk so-and-so to make him stop should remain silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.58
Pali: yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.59
Pali: Samanubhaṭṭho saṅghena itthannāmo bhikkhu tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha has pressed monk so-and-so to make him stop. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.60
Pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.61
Pali: evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I will remember it thus.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.62
Pali: Ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): After the motion, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.63
Pali: dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā, 
English (brahmali, published): After each of the first two announcements, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.64
Pali: kammavācāpariyosāne āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When the last announcement is finished, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.65
Pali: Saṅghādisesaṁ ajjhāpajjantassa ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ, dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā paṭippassambhanti. 
English (brahmali, published): For one who commits the offense entailing suspension, the offense of wrong conduct and the serious offenses are annulled. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.66
Pali: Saṅghādisesoti 
English (brahmali, published): He commits an offense entailing suspension: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:2.67
Pali: …pe… tenapi vuccati saṅghādisesoti. 
English (brahmali, published): … Therefore, too, it is called “an offense entailing suspension”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:3.1.1
Pali: Dhammakamme dhammakammasaññī na paṭinissajjati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and he perceives it as such, and he does not stop, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:3.1.2
Pali: Dhammakamme vematiko na paṭinissajjati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but he is unsure of it, and he does not stop, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:3.1.3
Pali: Dhammakamme adhammakammasaññī na paṭinissajjati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but he perceives it as illegitimate, and he does not stop, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:3.1.4
Pali: Adhammakamme dhammakammasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but he perceives it as legitimate, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:3.1.5
Pali: Adhammakamme vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:3.1.6
Pali: Adhammakamme adhammakammasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and he perceives it as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:3.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:3.2.2
Pali: asamanubhāsantassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he has not been pressed; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:3.2.3
Pali: paṭinissajjantassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he stops; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:3.2.4
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:3.2.5
Pali: khittacittassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is deranged; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:3.2.6
Pali: vedanāṭṭassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is overwhelmed by pain; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:3.2.7
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss10:3.2.8
Pali: Saṅghabhedasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ dasamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on causing a schism in the Sangha, the tenth, is finished.

English (brahmali, published): 11. Siding with a schismatic 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Rājagaha in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrel sanctuary. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena devadatto saṅghabhedāya parakkamati cakkabhedāya. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time Devadatta was pursuing schism in the Sangha, a break in authority. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:1.3
Pali: Bhikkhū evamāhaṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): The monks were saying, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:1.4
Pali: “adhammavādī devadatto, avinayavādī devadatto. 
English (brahmali, published): “Devadatta speaks contrary to the Teaching and the training. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:1.5
Pali: Kathañhi nāma devadatto saṅghabhedāya parakkamissati cakkabhedāyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): How can he pursue schism in the Sangha?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:1.6
Pali: Evaṁ vutte, kokāliko kaṭamodakatissako khaṇḍadeviyā putto samuddadatto te bhikkhū etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): But Kokālika, Kaṭamodakatissaka, Khaṇḍadeviyāputta, and Samuddadatta said to those monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:1.7
Pali: “māyasmanto evaṁ avacuttha. 
English (brahmali, published): “No, venerables, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:1.8
Pali: Dhammavādī devadatto, vinayavādī devadatto. 
English (brahmali, published): Devadatta speaks in accordance with the Teaching and the training. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:1.9
Pali: Amhākañca devadatto chandañca ruciñca ādāya voharati jānāti no bhāsati amhākampetaṁ khamatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): And he speaks with our consent and approval. He knows about us and speaks for us, and we approve of this.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:1.10
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:1.11
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhū devadattassa saṅghabhedāya parakkamantassa anuvattakā bhavissanti vaggavādakā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can these monks support Devadatta’s pursuit of schism in the Sangha?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:1.12
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū te anuvattake bhikkhū anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): They rebuked those monks in many ways and then told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:1.13

English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that there are monks who support this?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:1.14
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:1.15
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:1.16
Pali: kathañhi nāma te, bhikkhave, moghapurisā devadattassa saṅghabhedāya parakkamantassa anuvattakā bhavissanti vaggavādakā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, how can those foolish men support this? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:1.17
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:1.18
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:1.19.1
Pali: “Tasseva kho pana bhikkhussa bhikkhū honti anuvattakā vaggavādakā eko vā dve vā tayo vā. Te evaṁ vadeyyuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): ‘That monk may have one, two, or three monks who side with him and support him, and they may say, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:1.20
Pali: ‘māyasmanto etaṁ bhikkhuṁ kiñci avacuttha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, don’t correct this monk. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:1.21
Pali: Dhammavādī ceso bhikkhu, vinayavādī ceso bhikkhu. 
English (brahmali, published): He speaks in accordance with the Teaching and the training. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:1.22
Pali: Amhākañceso bhikkhu chandañca ruciñca ādāya voharati jānāti no bhāsati amhākampetaṁ khamatī’ti, 
English (brahmali, published): And he speaks with our consent and approval. He knows about us and speaks for us, and we approve of this.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:1.23
Pali: te bhikkhū bhikkhūhi evamassu vacanīyā—
English (brahmali, published): The monks should correct those monks like this:

English (brahmali, published): “No, venerables, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:1.25
Pali: na ceso bhikkhu dhammavādī, na ceso bhikkhu vinayavādī, 
English (brahmali, published): this monk speaks contrary to the Teaching and the training. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:1.26
Pali: māyasmantānampi saṅghabhedo ruccittha, 
English (brahmali, published): And don’t consent to schism in the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:1.27
Pali: sametāyasmantānaṁ saṅghena, samaggo hi saṅgho sammodamāno avivadamāno ekuddeso phāsu viharatī’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Stay with the Sangha, for a united Sangha—in concord, in harmony, having a joint recitation—is at ease.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:1.28
Pali: Evañca te bhikkhū bhikkhūhi vuccamānā tatheva paggaṇheyyuṁ, te bhikkhū bhikkhūhi yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsitabbā tassa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): If those monks continue as before, the monks should press them up to three times to make them stop. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:1.29
Pali: Yāvatatiyañce samanubhāsiyamānā taṁ paṭinissajjeyyuṁ, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): If they then stop, all is well. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:1.30
Pali: no ce paṭinissajjeyyuṁ, saṅghādiseso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If they do not stop, they commit an offense entailing suspension.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:2.1

Pali: Byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena saṅgho ñāpetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): A competent and capable monk should inform the Sangha:

Pali: ‘Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:2.41
Pali: Itthannāmo ca itthanāmo ca bhikkhū itthannāmassa bhikkhuno saṅghabhedāya parakkamantassa anuvattakā vaggavādakā. 
English (brahmali, published): Monks so-and-so and so-and-so are siding with and supporting monk so-and-so who is pursuing schism in the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:2.42
Pali: Te taṁ vatthuṁ na paṭinissajjanti. 
English (brahmali, published): And they keep on doing it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:2.43
Pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmañca itthannāmañca bhikkhū samanubhāseyya tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): If the Sangha is ready, it should press monks so-and-so and so-and-so to make them stop. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:2.44
Pali: Esā ñatti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the motion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:2.45
Pali: Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:2.46
Pali: Itthannāmo ca itthannāmo ca bhikkhū itthannāmassa bhikkhuno saṅghabhedāya parakkamantassa anuvattakā vaggavādakā. 
English (brahmali, published): Monks so-and-so and so-and-so are siding with and supporting monk so-and-so who is pursuing schism in the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:2.47
Pali: Te taṁ vatthuṁ na paṭinissajjanti. 
English (brahmali, published): And they keep on doing it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:2.48
Pali: Saṅgho itthannāmañca itthannāmañca bhikkhū samanubhāsati tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha presses monks so-and-so and so-and-so to make them stop. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:2.49
Pali: Yassāyasmato khamati itthannāmassa ca itthannāmassa ca bhikkhūnaṁ samanubhāsanā tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya, so tuṇhassa; 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who approves of pressing monks so-and-so and so-and-so to make them stop should remain silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:2.50
Pali: yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:2.51

English (brahmali, published): For the second time, I speak on this matter. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:2.52

English (brahmali, published): For the third time, I speak on this matter. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:2.53
Pali: suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:2.54
Pali: Itthannāmo ca itthannāmo ca bhikkhū itthannāmassa bhikkhuno saṅghabhedāya parakkamantassa anuvattakā vaggavādakā.

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:2.55
Pali: Te taṁ vatthuṁ na paṭinissajjanti. 
English (brahmali, published): And they keep on doing it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:2.56
Pali: Saṅgho itthannāmañca itthannāmañca bhikkhū samanubhāsati tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha presses monks so-and-so and so-and-so to make them stop. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:2.57
Pali: Yassāyasmato khamati itthannāmassa ca itthannāmassa ca bhikkhūnaṁ samanubhāsanā tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya, so tuṇhassa; 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who approves of pressing monks so-and-so and so-and-so to make them stop should remain silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:2.58
Pali: yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:2.59
Pali: Samanubhaṭṭhā saṅghena itthannāmo ca itthannāmo ca bhikkhū tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha has pressed monks so-and-so and so-and-so to make them stop. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:2.60
Pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:2.61
Pali: evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I will remember it thus.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:2.62
Pali: Ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): After the motion, they commit an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:2.63
Pali: dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā, 
English (brahmali, published): After each of the first two announcements, they commit a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:2.64
Pali: kammavācāpariyosāne āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When the last announcement is finished, they commit an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:2.65
Pali: Saṅghādisesaṁ ajjhāpajjantānaṁ ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ, dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā paṭippassambhanti. 
English (brahmali, published): For those who commit the offense entailing suspension, the offense of wrong conduct and the serious offenses are annulled. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:2.66
Pali: Dve tayo ekato samanubhāsitabbā, taduttari na samanubhāsitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): Two or three may be pressed together, but not more than that. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:2.67
Pali: Saṅghādisesoti 
English (brahmali, published): They commit an offense entailing suspension: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:2.68
Pali: …pe… tenapi vuccati “saṅghādiseso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): … Therefore, too, it is called “an offense entailing suspension”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:3.1.1
Pali: Dhammakamme dhammakammasaññī na paṭinissajjanti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and they perceive it as such, and they do not stop, they commit an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:3.1.2
Pali: Dhammakamme vematikā na paṭinissajjanti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but they are unsure of it, and they do not stop, they commit an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:3.1.3
Pali: Dhammakamme adhammakammasaññī na paṭinissajjanti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but they perceive it as illegitimate, and they do not stop, they commit an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:3.1.4
Pali: Adhammakamme dhammakammasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but they perceive it as legitimate, they commit an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:3.1.5
Pali: Adhammakamme vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but they are unsure of it, they commit an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:3.1.6
Pali: Adhammakamme adhammakammasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and they perceive it as such, they commit an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:3.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:3.2.2
Pali: asamanubhāsantānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if they have not been pressed; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:3.2.3
Pali: paṭinissajjantānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if they stop; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:3.2.4
Pali: ummattakānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if they are insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:3.2.5
Pali: khittacittānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if they are deranged; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:3.2.6
Pali: vedanāṭṭānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if they are overwhelmed by pain; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:3.2.7
Pali: ādikammikānanti. 
English (brahmali, published): if they are the first offenders. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss11:3.2.8
Pali: Bhedānuvattakasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ ekādasamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on siding with a schismatic, the eleventh, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā kosambiyaṁ viharati ghositārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Kosambī in Ghosita’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā channo anācāraṁ ācarati. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Channa was misbehaving. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:1.3
Pali: Bhikkhū evamāhaṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): The monks would tell him,

English (brahmali, published): it’s not allowable,”

Pali: So evaṁ vadeti—
English (brahmali, published): and he would reply,

Pali: “kiṁ nu kho nāma tumhe, āvuso, maṁ vattabbaṁ maññatha? 
English (brahmali, published): “Who are you to correct me? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:1.8
Pali: Ahaṁ kho nāma tumhe vadeyyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): I should correct you! 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:1.9
Pali: Amhākaṁ buddho amhākaṁ dhammo 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha is mine; the Teaching is mine. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:1.10
Pali: amhākaṁ ayyaputtena dhammo abhisamito. 
English (brahmali, published): The Master realized the Truth because of me. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:1.11
Pali: Seyyathāpi nāma mahāvāto vāyanto tiṇakaṭṭhapaṇṇasaṭaṁ ekato ussāreyya, seyyathā vā pana nadī pabbateyyā saṅkhasevālapaṇakaṁ ekato ussāreyya; 
English (brahmali, published): Just as grass, sticks, and fallen leaves are whirled up by a strong wind all at once, just as various water plants are whirled up by a mountain stream all at once, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:1.12

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:1.14

English (brahmali, published): I should correct you! 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:1.15
Pali: Amhākaṁ buddho amhākaṁ dhammo 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha is mine; the Teaching is mine. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:1.16
Pali: amhākaṁ ayyaputtena dhammo abhisamito”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The Master realized the Truth because of me.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:1.17
Pali: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks of few desires complained and criticized him, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:1.18
Pali: “kathañhi nāma āyasmā channo bhikkhūhi sahadhammikaṁ vuccamāno attānaṁ avacanīyaṁ karissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can Venerable Channa make himself incorrigible when he’s legitimately corrected by the monks?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:1.19

English (brahmali, published): They rebuked Channa in many ways and then told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned Channa: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:1.20
Pali: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, channa, bhikkhūhi sahadhammikaṁ vuccamāno attānaṁ avacanīyaṁ karosī”ti?

English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked him … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:1.23

English (brahmali, published): “Foolish man, how can you do this?

English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:1.25
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:1.26.1
Pali: “Bhikkhu paneva dubbacajātiko hoti uddesapariyāpannesu sikkhāpadesu bhikkhūhi sahadhammikaṁ vuccamāno attānaṁ avacanīyaṁ karoti—
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a monk is difficult to correct, and he makes himself incorrigible when legitimately corrected by the monks concerning the training rules that are recited, saying, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:1.27
Pali: ‘mā maṁ āyasmanto kiñci avacuttha kalyāṇaṁ vā pāpakaṁ vā, ahampāyasmante na kiñci vakkhāmi kalyāṇaṁ vā pāpakaṁ vā, 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, don’t say anything to me, either good or bad, and I won’t say anything to you, either good or bad. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:1.28
Pali: viramathāyasmanto mama vacanāyā’ti, 
English (brahmali, published): Please refrain from correcting me,” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:1.29
Pali: so bhikkhu bhikkhūhi evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): then the monks should correct him like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:1.30
Pali: ‘māyasmā attānaṁ avacanīyaṁ akāsi, vacanīyamevāyasmā attānaṁ karotu, 
English (brahmali, published): “Be easy to correct, venerable, not incorrigible. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:1.31
Pali: āyasmāpi bhikkhū vadetu sahadhammena, bhikkhūpi āyasmantaṁ vakkhanti sahadhammena. 
English (brahmali, published): And please give legitimate correction to the monks, and the monks will do the same to you. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:1.32
Pali: Evaṁ saṁvaddhā hi tassa bhagavato parisā yadidaṁ aññamaññavacanena aññamaññavuṭṭhāpanenā’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): For it’s in this way that the Buddha’s community has grown, that is, through mutual correction and mutual clearing of offenses.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:1.33
Pali: Evañca so bhikkhu bhikkhūhi vuccamāno tatheva paggaṇheyya, so bhikkhu bhikkhūhi yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsitabbo tassa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): If that monk continues as before, the monks should press him up to three times to make him stop. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:1.34
Pali: Yāvatatiyañce samanubhāsiyamāno taṁ paṭinissajjeyya, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): If he then stops, all is well. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:1.35
Pali: no ce paṭinissajjeyya, saṅghādiseso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If he does not stop, he commits an offense entailing suspension.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.1
Pali: Bhikkhū paneva dubbacajātiko hotīti 
English (brahmali, published): If a monk is difficult to correct: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.2
Pali: dubbaco hoti dovacassakaraṇehi dhammehi samannāgato akkhamo appadakkhiṇaggāhī anusāsaniṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is hard to correct, endowed with qualities that make him hard to correct, resistant, not receiving instructions respectfully. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.3

English (brahmali, published): Concerning the training rules that are recited:

Pali: yaṁ bhagavatā paññattaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ, etaṁ sahadhammikaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): the training rules laid down by the Buddha—this is called “legitimately”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.9
Pali: Tena vuccamāno attānaṁ avacanīyaṁ karoti—
English (brahmali, published): When corrected in regard to this, he makes himself incorrigible, saying, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.10

English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, don’t say anything to me, either good or bad, and I won’t say anything to you, either good or bad. Please refrain from correcting me.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.11
Pali: So bhikkhūti 
English (brahmali, published): Him:

Pali: yo so dubbacajātiko bhikkhu. 
English (brahmali, published): the monk who is difficult to correct.

Pali: Bhikkhūhīti 
English (brahmali, published): The monks:

English (brahmali, published): other monks, those who see it or hear about it. They should correct him like this: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.15
Pali: “māyasmā attānaṁ avacanīyaṁ akāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Be easy to correct, venerable, not incorrigible.

Pali: Vacanīyameva āyasmā attānaṁ karotu.

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.17

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.18

English (brahmali, published): For it’s in this way that the Buddha’s community has grown, that is, through mutual correction and mutual clearing of offenses.”

English (brahmali, published): And they should correct him a second

English (brahmali, published): If he does not stop, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.23
Pali: Sutvā na vadanti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If those who hear about it do not say anything, they commit an offense of wrong conduct.

Pali: So bhikkhu saṅghamajjhampi ākaḍḍhitvā vattabbo—
English (brahmali, published): That monk, even if he has to be pulled into the Sangha, should be corrected like this:

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.36
Pali: So taṁ vatthuṁ na paṭinissajjati.

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.38
Pali: Esā ñatti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the motion. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.39

English (brahmali, published): Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

Pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmo bhikkhu bhikkhūhi sahadhammikaṁ vuccamāno attānaṁ avacanīyaṁ karoti. 
English (brahmali, published): The monk so-and-so makes himself incorrigible when legitimately corrected by the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.41
Pali: So taṁ vatthuṁ na paṭinissajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): And he keeps on doing it. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.42
Pali: Saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuṁ samanubhāsati tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha presses monk so-and-so to make him stop. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.43
Pali: Yassāyasmato khamati itthannāmassa bhikkhuno samanubhāsanā tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya, so tuṇhassa; 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who approves of pressing monk so-and-so to make him stop should remain silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.44
Pali: yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.45

English (brahmali, published): For the second time, I speak on this matter. … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.46

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.47
Pali: suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.48
Pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmo bhikkhu bhikkhūhi sahadhammikaṁ vuccamāno attānaṁ avacanīyaṁ karoti. 
English (brahmali, published): The monk so-and-so makes himself incorrigible when legitimately corrected by the monks. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.49
Pali: So taṁ vatthuṁ na paṭinissajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): And he keeps on doing it.

Pali: Saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuṁ samanubhāsati tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha presses monk so-and-so to make him stop. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.51
Pali: Yassāyasmato khamati itthannāmassa bhikkhuno samanubhāsanā tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya, so tuṇhassa; 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who approves of pressing monk so-and-so to make him stop should remain silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.52
Pali: yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.53
Pali: Samanubhaṭṭho saṅghena itthannāmo bhikkhu tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha has pressed monk so-and-so to make him stop. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.54
Pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.55
Pali: evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I will remember it thus.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.56
Pali: Ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): After the motion, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.57
Pali: dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā, 
English (brahmali, published): After each of the first two announcements, he commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.58
Pali: kammavācāpariyosāne āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When the last announcement is finished, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.59
Pali: Saṅghādisesaṁ ajjhāpajjantassa ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ, dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā paṭippassambhanti. 
English (brahmali, published): For one who commits the offense entailing suspension, the offense of wrong conduct and the serious offenses are annulled. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.60
Pali: Saṅghādisesoti 
English (brahmali, published): He commits an offense entailing suspension: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:2.61
Pali: …pe… tenapi vuccati “saṅghādiseso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): … Therefore, too, it is called “an offense entailing suspension”. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:3.1.1
Pali: Dhammakamme dhammakammasaññī na paṭinissajjati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and he perceives it as such, but he does not stop, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:3.1.2
Pali: Dhammakamme vematiko na paṭinissajjati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but he is unsure of it, and he does not stop, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:3.1.3
Pali: Dhammakamme adhammakammasaññī na paṭinissajjati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but he perceives it as illegitimate, and he does not stop, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:3.1.4
Pali: Adhammakamme dhammakammasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but he perceives it as legitimate, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:3.1.5
Pali: Adhammakamme vematiko, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but he is unsure of it, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:3.1.6
Pali: Adhammakamme adhammakammasaññī, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and he perceives it as such, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:3.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:3.2.2
Pali: asamanubhāsantassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he has not been pressed; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:3.2.3
Pali: paṭinissajjantassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he stops; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:3.2.4
Pali: ummattakassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if he is insane; 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:3.2.5
Pali: ādikammikassāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if he is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss12:3.2.6
Pali: Dubbacasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ dvādasamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule on being difficult to correct, the twelfth, is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:0.2
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Great Analysis 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:0.3
Pali: Saṅghādisesakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing suspension 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:0.4
Pali: 13. Kuladūsakasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 13. Being a corrupter of families 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena assajipunabbasukā nāma kīṭāgirismiṁ āvāsikā honti alajjino pāpabhikkhū. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the bad and shameless monks Assaji and Punabbasuka were staying at Kīṭāgiri. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.1.3
Pali: Te evarūpaṁ anācāraṁ ācaranti—
English (brahmali, published): They were misbehaving in many ways. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.1.4
Pali: mālāvacchaṁ ropentipi ropāpentipi, siñcantipi siñcāpentipi, ocinantipi ocināpentipi, ganthentipi ganthāpentipi, 
English (brahmali, published): They planted flowering trees, watered and plucked them, and then tied the flowers together. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.1.5
Pali: ekatovaṇṭikamālaṁ karontipi kārāpentipi, ubhatovaṇṭikamālaṁ karontipi kārāpentipi, 
English (brahmali, published): They made the flowers into garlands, garlands with stalks on one side and garlands with stalks on both sides. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.1.6
Pali: mañjarikaṁ karontipi kārāpentipi, vidhūtikaṁ karontipi kārāpentipi, vaṭaṁsakaṁ karontipi kārāpentipi, āveḷaṁ karontipi kārāpentipi, uracchadaṁ karontipi kārāpentipi. 
English (brahmali, published): They made flower arrangements, wreaths, ornaments for the head, ornaments for the ears, and ornaments for the chest. And they had others do the same. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.1.7
Pali: Te kulitthīnaṁ kuladhītānaṁ kulakumārīnaṁ kulasuṇhānaṁ kuladāsīnaṁ ekatovaṇṭikamālaṁ harantipi harāpentipi, ubhatovaṇṭikamālaṁ harantipi harāpentipi, mañjarikaṁ harantipi harāpentipi, vidhūtikaṁ harantipi harāpentipi, vaṭaṁsakaṁ harantipi harāpentipi, āveḷaṁ harantipi harāpentipi, uracchadaṁ harantipi harāpentipi. 
English (brahmali, published): They then took these things, or sent them, to the women, the daughters, the girls, the daughters-in-law, and the female slaves of respectable families. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.1.8
Pali: Te kulitthīhi kuladhītāhi kulakumārīhi kulasuṇhāhi kuladāsīhi saddhiṁ ekabhājanepi bhuñjanti, ekathālakepi pivanti, 
English (brahmali, published): They ate from the same plates as these women and drank from the same vessels. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.1.9
Pali: ekāsanepi nisīdanti, ekamañcepi tuvaṭṭenti, ekattharaṇāpi tuvaṭṭenti, ekapāvuraṇāpi tuvaṭṭenti, ekattharaṇapāvuraṇāpi tuvaṭṭenti, 
English (brahmali, published): They sat on the same seats as them, and they lay down on the same beds, on the same sheets, under the same covers, and both on the same sheets and under the same covers. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.1.10
Pali: vikālepi bhuñjanti, majjampi pivanti, mālāgandhavilepanampi dhārenti, 
English (brahmali, published): They ate at the wrong time, drank alcohol, and wore garlands, perfumes, and cosmetics. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.1.11
Pali: naccantipi gāyantipi vādentipi lāsentipi, 
English (brahmali, published): They danced, sang, played instruments, and performed. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.1.12
Pali: naccantiyāpi naccanti, naccantiyāpi gāyanti, naccantiyāpi vādenti, naccantiyāpi lāsenti, gāyantiyāpi naccanti, gāyantiyāpi gāyanti, gāyantiyāpi vādenti, gāyantiyāpi lāsenti, vādentiyāpi naccanti, vādentiyāpi gāyanti, vādentiyāpi vādenti, vādentiyāpi lāsenti, lāsentiyāpi naccanti, lāsentiyāpi gāyanti, lāsentiyāpi vādenti, lāsentiyāpi lāsenti; 
English (brahmali, published): While the women were dancing, singing, playing instruments, and performing, so would they. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.2.1
Pali: aṭṭhapadepi kīḷanti, dasapadepi kīḷanti, ākāsepi kīḷanti, parihārapathepi kīḷanti, santikāyapi kīḷanti, khalikāyapi kīḷanti, ghaṭikāyapi kīḷanti, salākahatthenapi kīḷanti, akkhenapi kīḷanti, paṅgacīrenapi kīḷanti, vaṅkakenapi kīḷanti, mokkhacikāyapi kīḷanti, ciṅgulakenapi kīḷanti, pattāḷhakenapi kīḷanti, rathakenapi kīḷanti, dhanukenapi kīḷanti, akkharikāyapi kīḷanti, manesikāyapi kīḷanti, yathāvajjenapi kīḷanti. 
English (brahmali, published): They played various games: eight-row checkers, ten-row checkers, imaginary checkers, hopscotch, pick-up-sticks, board games, tipcat, painting with the hand, dice, leaf flutes, toy plows, somersaults, pinwheels, toy measures, toy carriages, toy bows, guessing from syllables, thought guessing, mimicking deformities. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.2.2
Pali: Hatthismimpi sikkhanti, assasmimpi sikkhanti, rathasmimpi sikkhanti, dhanusmimpi sikkhanti, tharusmimpi sikkhanti, 
English (brahmali, published): They trained in elephant riding, in horsemanship, in carriage riding, in archery, in swordsmanship. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.2.3
Pali: hatthissapi purato dhāvanti, assassapi purato dhāvanti, rathassapi purato dhāvantipi ādhāvantipi, 
English (brahmali, published): And they ran in front of elephants, horses, and carriages, and they ran backward and forward. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.2.4
Pali: usseḷentipi, apphoṭentipi, nibbujjhantipi, muṭṭhīhipi yujjhanti, 
English (brahmali, published): They whistled, clapped their hands, wrestled, and boxed. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.2.5
Pali: raṅgamajjhepi saṅghāṭiṁ pattharitvā naccakiṁ evaṁ vadanti—“idha, bhagini, naccassū”ti, nalāṭikampi denti, vividhampi anācāraṁ ācaranti. 
English (brahmali, published): They spread their outer robe on a stage and said to the dancing girls, “Dance here, sister,” and they made gestures of approval. And they misbehaved in a variety of ways. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.3.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu kāsīsu vassaṁvuṭṭho sāvatthiṁ gacchanto bhagavantaṁ dassanāya, yena kīṭāgiri tadavasari. 
English (brahmali, published): Just then a monk who had completed the rainy-season residence in Kāsi was on his way to visit the Buddha at Sāvatthī when he arrived at Kīṭāgiri. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.3.2
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya kīṭāgiriṁ piṇḍāya pāvisi 
English (brahmali, published): In the morning he robed up, took his bowl and robe, and entered Kīṭāgiri to collect almsfood. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.3.3
Pali: pāsādikena abhikkantena paṭikkantena ālokitena vilokitena samiñjitena pasāritena okkhittacakkhu iriyāpathasampanno. 
English (brahmali, published): He was pleasing in his conduct: in going out and coming back, in looking ahead and looking aside, in bending and stretching his arms. His eyes were lowered, and he was perfect in deportment. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.3.4
Pali: Manussā taṁ bhikkhuṁ passitvā evamāhaṁsu—“kvāyaṁ abalabalo viya mandamando viya bhākuṭikabhākuṭiko viya? Ko imassa upagatassa piṇḍakaṁ dassati? Amhākaṁ pana ayyā assajipunabbasukā saṇhā sakhilā sukhasambhāsā mihitapubbaṅgamā ehisvāgatavādino abbhākuṭikā uttānamukhā pubbabhāsino. Tesaṁ kho nāma piṇḍo dātabbo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): When people saw him, they said, “Who’s this, acting like a moron and always frowning? Who’s gonna give almsfood to him? Almsfood should be given to our Venerables Assaji and Punabbasuka, for they are gentle, congenial, pleasant to speak with, greeting one with a smile, welcoming, friendly, open, the first to speak.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.3.5
Pali: Addasā kho aññataro upāsako taṁ bhikkhuṁ kīṭāgirismiṁ piṇḍāya carantaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): A certain lay follower saw that monk walking for alms in Kīṭāgiri. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.3.6
Pali: Disvāna yena so bhikkhu tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ bhikkhuṁ abhivādetvā etadavoca—“api, bhante, piṇḍo labbhatī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): He approached him, bowed, and said, “Venerable, have you received any almsfood?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.3.7
Pali: “Na kho, āvuso, piṇḍo labbhatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “No, I haven’t.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.3.8
Pali: “Ehi, bhante, gharaṁ gamissāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Come, let’s go to my house.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.4.1
Pali: Atha kho so upāsako taṁ bhikkhuṁ gharaṁ netvā bhojetvā etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): He took that monk to his house and gave him a meal. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.4.2
Pali: “kahaṁ, bhante, ayyo gamissatī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): He then said, “Where are you going, venerable?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.4.3
Pali: “Sāvatthiṁ kho ahaṁ, āvuso, gamissāmi bhagavantaṁ dassanāyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I’m going to Sāvatthī to see the Buddha.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.4.4
Pali: “Tena hi, bhante, mama vacanena bhagavato pāde sirasā vanda, evañca vadehi—
English (brahmali, published): “Well then, would you please pay respect at the Buddha’s feet in my name and say, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.4.5
Pali: ‘duṭṭho, bhante, kīṭāgirismiṁ āvāso. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Sir, the monastery at Kīṭāgiri has been corrupted. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.4.6
Pali: Assajipunabbasukā nāma kīṭāgirismiṁ āvāsikā alajjino pāpabhikkhū. 
English (brahmali, published): The bad and shameless monks Assaji and Punabbasuka are staying there. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.4.7
Pali: Te evarūpaṁ anācāraṁ ācaranti—
English (brahmali, published): And they’re misbehaving in many ways. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.4.8
Pali: mālāvacchaṁ ropentipi ropāpentipi, siñcantipi siñcāpentipi …pe… vividhampi anācāraṁ ācaranti. 
English (brahmali, published): They plant flowering trees, water them … And they misbehave in a variety of ways. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.4.9
Pali: Yepi te, bhante, manussā pubbe saddhā ahesuṁ pasannā tepi etarahi assaddhā appasannā. 
English (brahmali, published): Those who previously had faith and confidence have now lost it, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.4.10
Pali: Yānipi tāni saṅghassa pubbe dānapathāni tānipi etarahi upacchinnāni. 
English (brahmali, published): and there’s no longer any support for the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.4.11
Pali: Riñcanti pesalā bhikkhū, nivasanti pāpabhikkhū. 
English (brahmali, published): The good monks have left and the bad monks are staying on. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.4.12
Pali: Sādhu, bhante, bhagavā kīṭāgiriṁ bhikkhū pahiṇeyya yathāyaṁ kīṭāgirismiṁ āvāso saṇṭhaheyyā’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Sir, please send monks to stay at the monastery at Kīṭāgiri.’” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.5.1
Pali: “Evamāvuso”ti kho so bhikkhu tassa upāsakassa paṭissutvā yena sāvatthi tena pakkāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): The monk consented, got up, and set out for Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.5.2
Pali: Anupubbena yena sāvatthi jetavanaṁ anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāmo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): When he eventually arrived, he went to the Buddha in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. He bowed to the Buddha and sat down. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.5.3
Pali: Āciṇṇaṁ kho panetaṁ buddhānaṁ bhagavantānaṁ āgantukehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ paṭisammodituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Since it is the custom for Buddhas to greet newly-arrived monks, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.5.4
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): the Buddha said to him,

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.5.7
Pali: kuto ca tvaṁ, bhikkhu, āgacchasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): And where have you come from?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.5.8

English (brahmali, published): “I’m keeping well, sir, I’m getting by. 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.5.9
Pali: Appakilamathena cāhaṁ, bhante, addhānaṁ āgato. 
English (brahmali, published): I’m not tired from traveling.”

English (brahmali, published): He then told the Buddha all that had happened at Kīṭāgiri,

English (brahmali, published): adding, “That’s where I’ve come from, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.6.1
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe bhikkhusaṅghaṁ sannipātāpetvā bhikkhū paṭipucchi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks: 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.6.2

Pali: yepi te, bhikkhave, manussā pubbe saddhā ahesuṁ pasannā tepi etarahi assaddhā appasannā; 
English (brahmali, published): And is it true that those people who previously had faith and confidence have now lost it, 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.6.5

Pali: riñcanti pesalā bhikkhū nivasanti pāpabhikkhū”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): and that the good monks have left and the bad monks are staying on?” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.6.7
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.6.8
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.6.9

English (brahmali, published): “Monks, how can those foolish men misbehave in this way? 

pli-tv-bu-vb-ss13:1.6.10
